### hearing Bhagavatam, separation, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NM8YmOZcjo4?t=397
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavatam trains you, that you simply submissively hear the Bhagavatam from the pure devotee regularly, you keep on hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, your eternal relation with Krishna is awakened, and then you become mad after Krishna, that is the result of hearing."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of submissively hearing the Bhagavatam from pure devotees to awaken our eternal relationship with Krishna. It also describes the reaction of the female relatives in Hastinapura upon Krishna's departure, highlighting their intense feelings of separation and their attempts to control their tears.

Prabhupada says here, Bhagavatam trains you, that you simply submissively hear the Bhagavatam from the pure devotee regularly, you keep on hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, your eternal relation with Krishna is awakened, and then you become mad after Krishna, that is the result of hearing. The fourteenth verse, it is said in this same chapter in the Bhagavatam, Nyarundhan Udgalad Bhashpam, Outkantyad Devaki Sute, Niryati Agarano Abhadram, Itishyad Bandhavas Triyaha. So, the meaning of this verse is, the female relatives, whose eyes were flooded with tears out of anxiety for Krishna, came out of the palace. They could not stop their tears, but they did so with great difficulty. They feared, why did they stop their tears? Because they feared that tears would cause misfortune at the time of departure. So, this is the description of the female relatives who were present in that palace of Hastinapura. How they felt separation from Krishna, that they began to shed tears, and with only great difficulty they could stop their tears from flowing.

---
### material vs. spiritual, detachment, bewilderment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NM8YmOZcjo4?t=514
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These material feelings, material relationships, material attachments, no value other than simply make us bewildered, that's all."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the temporary and superficial nature of material relationships with the eternal and transcendental relationship with Krishna. It explains that material attachments ultimately lead to bewilderment and entanglement in the material world.

So, Prabhupada explains that, Prabhupada explains that a very important point here. Please, hear carefully. There were hundreds of ladies in the palace of Hastinapura. All of them were affectionate to Krishna. How did they become affectionate to Krishna? All of them were relatives also. When they saw Krishna was going away from the palace for his native place, they were very anxious for him. And, as usual, tears began to roll down their cheeks because there was so much attraction. So, they were very anxious for Krishna. They had so much affection for him, Krishna. They thought at the same time that tears at the time of Krishna's departure might be a cause of misfortune for Krishna. Therefore, they wanted to check them. But that was very difficult for them because the tears could not be checked. Now, it is explained here that because it was difficult to stop their tears from flowing from their eyes, so they simply smeared their tears in their eyes and their hearts were throbbing with that intense feeling of separation from Krishna. Now, these descriptions are significant because we can compare this with the kind of material relationships and attachments we have in this world, which is completely temporary. It is superficial. It is conditional. It is only a maximum. It will exist only as long as the body exists, and it is bewildering for the soul. All such material relationships are just bewilderment for the soul, for the conditioned soul. So, there is no real value for that attachment or that relationship or some feelings. There is no value. These material feelings, material relationships, material attachments, no value other than simply make us bewildered, that's all. Entangle us in this material world. Chase some illusory object. That's all it can do.

---
### transcendental relationship, hearing and chanting, affection for Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NM8YmOZcjo4?t=659
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, directly or indirectly, anyone who thinks of Krishna, anyone who talks about Krishna, anyone who worships Krishna becomes attached to Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the transcendental nature of Krishna and our eternal relationship with Him. It explains how even without direct contact, one can develop intense affection for Krishna by hearing about Him, talking about Him, and discussing His qualities and pastimes.

Whereas, Krishna is a transcendental personality. He is the transcendental personality. We are all spiritual beings. We have an eternal relationship with him, and in that eternal relationship, we are intimately attached and connected very intensely with Krishna. Now, Prabhupada says, the ladies who were the wives and daughters-in-law of those who died in the battlefield never came in direct contact with Krishna. That was the culture those days. Women, they don't directly contact men except their own husband or their father like that, other than them. Other men, they will not directly contact. So, how is it that without direct contact with Krishna, they had so much of attachment for Krishna? How is it possible? That is explained here by Srila Prabhupada. All of them heard of Krishna and his great activities, and thus, they thought of him. They talked about him. They discussed about his name, fame, qualities, activities, past tense, etc., and in that way, they developed affection for him, like the people who are in direct contact with Krishna. To the same degree, they developed intense feeling of love for Krishna, just like those who are in direct touch with Krishna developed that feeling. So, it is said here. Therefore, directly or indirectly, anyone who thinks of Krishna, anyone who talks about Krishna, anyone who worships Krishna becomes attached to Krishna. This is a fact.

---
### bhakti, chanting, absolute nature of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NM8YmOZcjo4?t=794
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by hearing about Krishna, simply by talking of Krishna, about Krishna, simply by reading about Krishna in the Bhagavatam, simply by thinking of Krishna, simply by remembering his past times, we can revive our relationship of Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment advises against practicing bhakti mechanically and emphasizes the absolute nature of Krishna, where there is no difference between His name, form, qualities, and pastimes. It highlights the power of hearing, talking, reading, thinking, and remembering Krishna to revive our relationship with Him.

So, don't practice bhakti as some mechanical thing, as some mechanical activity. Just like when we chant Hare Krishna on a daily basis, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Don't do it so mechanically. It comes in the way of our progress in developing, re-establishing our relationship with Krishna. It comes in the way. So, Krishna is absolute. There is no difference between his name, form, quality, past times, etc. No difference. That's the meaning of absolute. So, therefore, simply by hearing about Krishna, simply by talking of Krishna, about Krishna, simply by reading about Krishna in the Bhagavatam, simply by thinking of Krishna, simply by remembering his past times, we can revive our relationship of Krishna. Our relationship with Krishna is revived that way. It's a very, very powerful technique, and the best method that is recommended in this age.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.10.15 | 03-06-2022

### Krishna's Departure, Royal Customs, Honoring the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GHxEhqfE3jU?t=55
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.15
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So when the Lord was departing from the palace of Hastinapura, he was given a very nice send-off."
**Summary:** This segment describes the grand send-off Lord Krishna received from Yudhishthira Maharaj and the residents of Hastinapura as he departed for Dwaraka, including the playing of various musical instruments to honor him.

In this session, we will be discussing the 15th verse of the 10th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, while the Lord was departing from the palace of Hastinapura, different types of drums like the mridanga, dhol, nagra, dhundhuri and dhundubhi and flutes of different types, the veena, gomukha and bheri all sounded together to show him honor. So as the title of the chapter suggests, this is the description of the departure of Lord Krishna for Dwaraka from Hastinapura. So when the Lord was departing from the palace of Hastinapura, he was given a very nice send-off. So it is a royal custom in the palace that all these wonderful instruments are played like a band to show him honor. He is the supreme Lord. Not only that, he was visiting as a guest of Yudhishthira Maharaj. So Yudhishthira Maharaj was seeing off the Lord, Lord Krishna. He was very pleased to arrange this band for showing the honor to Krishna.

---
### Royal Ladies, Shyness, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GHxEhqfE3jU?t=154
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.16
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Out of a loving desire to see the Lord, the royal ladies of the Kurus got up on top of the palace, and smiling with affection and shyness, they showered flowers upon the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on the royal ladies of Hastinapura who, out of love for Krishna, went to the top of the palace to shower him with flowers, highlighting their shyness and the cultural context of the time.

The next verse, 16th verse. The meaning of this 16th verse is, out of a loving desire to see the Lord, the royal ladies of the Kurus got up on top of the palace, and smiling with affection and shyness, they showered flowers upon the Lord. So Krishna was seen off by all the residents of the palace, and the other citizens also, when he was leaving Hastinapura to return to Dwaraka. So particularly mention is made here in this verse, of the royal ladies of the palace of Hastinapura, they got up on top of the palace, because in those days there was no free mixing of men and women. A very highly cultured society was present then. And these ladies, to see the Lord, they went on top of the palace, and from there, while smiling with affection and shyness, these ladies showered flowers upon the Lord. So this particular quality of the ladies shyness, particularly this shyness, is explained by Prabhupada very nicely in the purport. So let us see. So let us hear Prabhupada. What does he say about the shyness?

---
### Shyness, Respect, Vedic Culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GHxEhqfE3jU?t=278
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shyness is a particular extra-natural beauty of the fair sex, and it commands respect from the opposite sex."
**Summary:** This segment presents Srila Prabhupada's explanation of shyness as a natural beauty in women that commands respect, contrasting it with modern societal norms and emphasizing its importance in Vedic culture.

Prabhupada says, shyness is a particular extra-natural beauty of the fair sex, and it commands respect from the opposite sex. Women naturally have got this shyness, and it is a quality that deserves to be respected by the men. So it is said here by Srila Prabhupada further, this custom was observed even during the days of the Mahabharata. It is more than 5000 years ago. Only the less intelligent persons not well-versed in the history of the world say that observance of separation of female from male is an introduction of the Mohammedan period in India. This is not correct. That the separation of female from males, some people say that it is an introduction of the Mughal or Mohammedan period in India. That is not correct. As is evident from this record in the Srimad Bhagavatam and the Mahabharata, it is very clear that even in the days 5000 years back, there was this separation of the men and women. There was no free mixing. And it is a very important point to be noted. So Srila Prabhupada says, this incident from the Mahabharata period proves definitely, that the ladies of the palace observed strict parda, restricted association with men. And instead of coming down in the open air where Lord Krishna and others were assembled, the ladies of the palace went up on top of the palace and from there paid their respects to Lord Krishna by showers of flowers. So they showed their respect. They maintained the distance. At the same time, the respect that was being given was very evident by their action, showering flowers upon the Lord. It is definitely stated here in this verse, that the ladies were smiling there on the top of the palace, checked by shyness. Both are mentioned. Prema vrida smitekshanaha. Now prema is out of affection and love. Vrida is glancing with shyness. So Prabhupada particularly comments about this shyness, that this shyness is a gift of nature to the fair sex, and it enhances their beauty and prestige, even if they are of a less important family, or even if they are less attractive. It's a very important point.

---
### Shyness, Respect, Material Beauty, Illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GHxEhqfE3jU?t=491
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Women generally are more attractive than men, but in case, in some instances, women are not so attractive, but still shyness enhances their beauty."
**Summary:** This segment continues Prabhupada's explanation, using the example of a sweeper woman commanding respect due to her shyness, contrasting it with the lack of respect for immodest women, and then delves into the illusory nature of material beauty and the importance of spiritual understanding.

Women generally are more attractive than men, but in case, in some instances, women are not so attractive, but still shyness enhances their beauty. And even their prestige. So, Srila Prabhupada explains further, we have practical experience of this fact. And what is that experience that Prabhupada is narrating here? A sweeper woman commanded the respect of many respectable gentlemen simply by manifesting a lady's shyness. Prabhupada is talking of his times, say about 80 years back. The sweeper woman, who is not very, not a high class lady, but still, because of her shyness, she was commanding the respect of even big gentlemen in the society. On the other hand, Prabhupada comments, half naked ladies in the street do not command any respect, but a shy sweeper's wife commands respect from all. So shyness is such an important quality for a woman. And it is very, very important. Human civilization as conceived of by the sages of India is to help one free himself from the clutches of illusion. Everyone here in this material world, men, women of whichever class, whichever standard, are all in the clutches of maya, illusion, as conditioned souls. But, the sages are instructing us to lead a particular lifestyle to become free from these clutches of the illusory energy. The material beauty of a woman is an illusion because actually the body is made of earth, water, fire, air, etc. But because there is association of the living spark with matter, this material body appears to be beautiful. Kindly note this carefully, that a woman appears attractive only because the material body is actually in contact with the living spark.

---
### Spiritual Beauty, Illusion, Restricted Mixing, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GHxEhqfE3jU?t=664
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The real beauty is within and the real beauty belongs to the soul, not to the body."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that true beauty lies within the spirit soul, not the material body, and explains how Vedic civilization restricts the mixing of men and women to avoid being trapped by the illusion of material beauty.

The life due to the spirit soul within the body. And Prabhupada gives an example to illustrate this. No one is attracted by an earthen doll even if it is the most perfectly prepared doll to attract the attention of others. You have seen mannequins in stores which sell, let's say, some garments. They dress up a doll of a woman very nicely. Now, everyone knows this doll is actually lifeless and nobody is attracted to such a doll. So, therefore, the dead body has no beauty because no one will accept the dead body of a so-called beautiful woman. Therefore, the conclusion is that the spirit spark is beautiful and because of the soul's beauty, one is attracted by the beauty of the outward body. So, the real beauty is within and the real beauty belongs to the soul, not to the body. Therefore, the lesson to be learnt is the Vedic wisdom forbids us to be attracted by false beauty. Don't be attracted by material beauty. Don't be attracted by external beauty. The real beauty is within. But because we are now in the darkness of ignorance, the Vedic civilization allows very restricted mixing of men and women. This restriction is necessary so that we don't become trapped by maya. This is a trap for both men and women. It's not only for men. It's for both.

---
### Shyness, Maya, Ephemeral Beauty, Vedic Wisdom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GHxEhqfE3jU?t=796
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A woman's shyness stops this unrestricted mixing of men and women and thereby saves people from becoming trapped by the illusory presentation of maya as something external, as beautiful and attractive."
**Summary:** This segment concludes the discussion by comparing women to fire and men to butter, emphasizing the importance of shyness in preventing unrestricted mixing and protecting individuals from the illusion of external beauty, highlighting the fleeting nature of bodily beauty and the wisdom of Vedic restrictions.

So, the sages of the Vedic civilization, they say that the woman is considered to be like fire and the man is considered to be butter. Butter must melt in the association of fire. Therefore, they may be brought together only when it is necessary. Men and women are brought together only when absolutely essential. And shyness, is a check to the unrestricted mixing. A woman's shyness stops this unrestricted mixing of men and women and thereby saves people from becoming trapped by the illusory presentation of maya as something external, as beautiful and attractive. The real beauty is inside, within, of the spirit, not of the body. So, don't be attracted by bodily beauty. And we all know this bodily beauty is highly flickering. It is very, very short-lived. Like somebody, even if however beautiful they are, when they don't take bath, they are not dressed very nicely, neatly, or they are completely fatigued, or they become old, they have wrinkles, or they get injury on their face, then where is the beauty? Beauty is all gone. So, this is ephemeral beauty. This is not real beauty. And to not get trapped by this ephemeral beauty or false beauty, there is restriction in mixing of men and women. And also, this restriction is automatically there when women utilize the characteristic quality of theirs called shyness, and by which the restricted, unrestricted mixing is prevented. And thereby, we don't become trapped by maya. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

Sitaatapatram Jagraha Muktadam Vibhushitam Ratnadandam Gudakeshah Priyapriyatamasyah. ### Civilization, Materialism, Nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=107
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When man does not waste his valuable time in producing unwanted things in the name of necessities is a contrast between the civilization that was prevailing 5000 years back and nowadays in the name of necessity so many unwanted things are produced in factories mass produced and people are busy improving upon this mass production artificial things not exactly nature's gift."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the natural gifts utilized in ancient times with the artificial products of modern industrial civilization, highlighting the waste of resources and time on unnecessary items.

So Krishna is leaving the palace of Hastinapura. He is proceeding to Dwaraka. So many different residents of the palace and other citizens have come to see off Krishna. So Arjuna, the intimate friend of Krishna, he is seeing off Krishna. How is he seeing off? He is described here as the great warrior and conqueror of sleep, Gudakeshah. I would say they had such a marriage with Krishna, which is why others have written and are grateful to her. So this is also about the life of Krishna. And then he says here, were used in the luxurious royal ceremonies in those days. Of course, they are all nature's gifts and are produced by the hills, oceans, etc. by the order of the Lord. When man does not waste his valuable time in producing unwanted things in the name of necessities is a contrast between the civilization that was prevailing 5000 years back and nowadays in the name of necessity so many unwanted things are produced in factories mass produced and people are busy improving upon this mass production artificial things not exactly nature's gift. So contrast this, the nature's gifts that were utilized about 5000 years back and what we are producing in the name of advancement of civilization.

---
### Materialism, Degradation, Artificiality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=244
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, all the metal wear has vanished or gradually vanishing or affordable only by rich people. Now, they don't even have brass and copper vessels. Forget about silver and gold."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the decline in the quality of materials used in modern society, contrasting the past use of gold, silver, brass, and copper with the present reliance on cheaper, less durable materials like plastic and margarine.

So Prabhupada says by so-called development of industrial enterprises they are now using parts of, describe it in a different way. They don't use metal in manufacturing they are using some metal like brass copper copper copper copper in the past they were called Doch. Instead of metals like gold, silver, brass and copper, some material, plastic like material, gatta parcha. Of course, this is not so commonly understood nowadays. But during Prabhupada's time, it is said gatta parcha they used to use because that is cheaper and it is accessible to more people. So, all the metal wear has vanished or gradually vanishing or affordable only by rich people. Now, they don't even have brass and copper vessels. Forget about silver and gold. And nowadays, they have just some stainless steel which is iron. And that also is becoming expensive and they have instead all kinds of metal. And that also is becoming expensive and they have instead all kinds of plastic wear. Exactly what Prabhupada is telling here which are worthless. And Prabhupada also says they are using margarine instead of purified butter. Nobody can afford pure butter or pure ghee which is not even easily available because only the adulterated substitutes are available. That is what is described as margarine.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=371
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This is what they call advancement of civilization. What have they done? They have displaced people from the city. **Summary:** This segment critiques modern civilization's displacement of people from rural prosperity to urban slums, highlighting the lack of shelter and the rise of slums as a consequence of so-called advancement.

And they even say that pure ghee does not have trans fat. I was reading in one of the descriptions in encyclopedia that margarine has trans fat. Purified ghee has no trans fat. So, purified ghee, pure ghee is a healthy alternative to margarine. So, they can neither afford pure ghee nor is margarine the substitute for ghee very healthy. So, this is the civilization we are living in now and Prabhupada also says one-fourth of the city population has no shelter. This is what they call advancement of civilization. What have they done? They have displaced people from the city. They have displaced people from peacefully living in villages in prosperity to cities and in cities when they come seeking a job or trying to find some employment in factories or all kinds of horrible places then they don't have proper shelter. They don't have shelter. They stay in slums. The slum phenomena is typical of so called advanced civilization. The slum phenomena is typical of so called advanced civilization in modern cities. The slum phenomena is typical of so called advanced civilization in modern cities. In the former times there was no slums at all.

### Simplicity, Rural Life, Vrindavan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=461
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Poor people used to live in villages in nice cottages built with whatever wood and other leaves, etc. was available very easily. Plenty of open space. Fresh air. Nice water. They grow grains and they eat first class food. Even the poorest man."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the slum conditions with the simple, healthy life of the poor in villages, particularly in Vrindavan, where they have access to fresh food, open space, and natural resources.

In the former times there was no slums at all. Doesn't mean there were no poor people. Poor people used to live in villages in nice cottages built with whatever wood and other leaves, etc. was available very easily. And they would live in a farm as laborers. Plenty of open space. Fresh air. Nice water. They grow grains and they eat first class food. Even the poorest man. For that matter if you can go to Vrindavan even today. You see those poor people staying in some cottages in the outskirts of the towns and in the villages. And in the villages they prepare always fresh food they never eat stale food they prepare fresh food even the poorest person will have little wheat flour which they will mix and make a dough and nicely bake a roti hot fresh roti the the housewife will prepare and serve hot hot to her husband the poor husband the poorest of the poor also eat hot chapatis nicely made on fire.

### Economic Development, Nature, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=651
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The basic principle of economic development is centered on land and cows. The necessities of human society are food grains, fruits, milk, minerals, clothing, wood, etc. And these are supplied. During the regime of Maharaja Yudhishthira, all over the world there were regulated rainfalls."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of land and cows for economic development, highlighting how regulated rainfalls, influenced by devotion to Krishna, lead to abundant natural resources and a prosperous society.

People should learn to live by nature's gifts. And how is it that the nature's gifts are becoming so very scarce? So, Prabhupada explains elsewhere. The basic principle of economic development is centered on land and cows. The necessities of human society are food grains, fruits, milk, minerals, clothing, wood, etc. And these are supplied. How are these supplied? Prabhupada explains during the regime of Maharaja Yudhishthira, all over the world there were regulated rainfalls. Very, very important point. Rainfalls are not in the control of human beings. They are regulated by the heavenly king Indradeva. And he is a servant of the supreme lord Krishna. So, when the king and the citizens obey the supreme lord Krishna, then under the king's administration, there will be regulated rainfalls all over the planet earth. And these rains are the causes of varieties of production on the land. Plenty of grains, plenty of vegetables, plenty of fruits. Not only do regulated rains help ample production of grains and fruits, but when they combine with astronomical influences, there is ample production of valuable stones and jewels. And pearls, see, this is also dependent on nature. You cannot manufacture valuable stones, precious stones like diamond and rubies and pearls. You cannot manufacture that. That is produced by nature under certain favorable conditions. And that is explained here by Prabhupada. Regulated rainfall combined with astronomical influences. Result in production of, ample production of valuable stones and pearls. So, if there is enough milk, enough grains, enough fruits, enough cotton, enough silk.

---
### Krishna Consciousness, Harmony, Exploitation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=781
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "People can lead a happy and healthy and prosperous life peacefully and actually cultivate Krishna consciousness, rather than work like donkeys for getting some very little artificial produce."
**Summary:** This segment advocates for a return to a Krishna conscious life in harmony with nature, emphasizing the futility of excessive labor for artificial products and the importance of devotional service.

People can lead a happy and healthy and prosperous life peacefully and actually cultivate Krishna consciousness, rather than work like donkeys for getting some very little artificial produce by paying huge amounts of money, which doesn't anyway have value. What kind of civilization is this? So, Prabhupada is suggesting that we return to land and cows and lead a Krishna conscious life. So, then there will be plenty of supply by the order of the Supreme Lord by material nature. We cannot order nature to produce. But nature will produce because nature is controlled by Krishna. And nature will produce plenty when we please the Supreme Lord by engaging in devotional service and living a harmonious life in harmony with nature. Not exploiting nature, killing poor animals, poisoning the soil by putting all kinds of chemicals into the soil. No, that's not the way. So, human beings should actually depend on the production of material nature.

---
### Dharma, Nature, Devotional Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NT6C9v8g6oY?t=883
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Human beings should actually depend on the production of material nature by following Dharma and by shifting from dependence on factories and artificial production to natural produce of land and crops and cows and regulated rainfall and plenty of all these supplies by nature under the order of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by reiterating the need to follow Dharma, shift from artificial production to natural produce, and depend on nature's abundance under the Supreme Lord's order, as advised by Srila Prabhupada.

Human beings should actually depend on the production of material nature by following Dharma and by shifting from dependence on factories and artificial production. Anartha. To natural produce of land and crops and cows and regulated rainfall and plenty of all these supplies by nature under the order of the Supreme Lord. So, this is the advice we get from Srila Prabhupada in this particular purport. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

The meaning of this verse is it was being heard here and there that the benedictions being paid to Krishna were neither befitting nor unbefitting because they were all for the absolute who was now playing the part of a human being. ### Krishna's role, relativity, devotion

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the context of the verse, explaining that brahmanas were offering benedictions to Krishna as he departed Hastinapura. He clarifies Prabhupada's explanation that the benedictions were fitting because Krishna was playing the part of a human being and a guest of Yudhishthira.

...there were brahmanas who were glorifying Krishna by chanting Vedic hymns and offering benediction to Krishna. Now, it is described in this verse that the benedictions that were being offered to Krishna were neither befitting nor unbefitting, so something like a contradiction. It appears as a contradictory statement here. So Prabhupada explains this in the purport very nicely. Why is it being said it was neither befitting nor unbefitting? So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, 'At places there were sounds of Vedic benediction at the personality of God is Shri Krishna. The benedictions were fitting in the sense that the Lord was playing the part of a human being as if a cousin of Maharaj Yudhishthira. Krishna appeared as the son of Vasudeva who was the brother of Kuntidevi and Kuntidevi's son was Yudhishthira. So Yudhishthira and Krishna were cousins in terms of relationship but here it is said because Krishna was playing the part of a human being though he is not a human being. He is the Supreme Lord always under all circumstances but he is playing the part of a human being. Therefore the benedictions were fitting because Yudhishthira is the elder brother of Krishna. So the elder brother arranges to offer benedictions by the qualified brahmanas and the Vedic chanting is accepted which will be offered through chanting of various hymns to the important guest. Krishna has come to the Hastinapura palace as the guest of Yudhishthira. So in that sense there it is befitting because Krishna was playing the part of a human being...

This absolute personality of God. It doesn't mean when he describes Nirguna that he has no qualities at all. He has no material qualities but he is full of transcendental qualities. Whereas in the relative world, the world we are staying, it's called relative world. And the transcendental world, the spiritual world where Krishna resides with his pure devotees, that is called the spiritual world, the transcendental world, the absolute world. There are no relativities. And there is nothing contradictory. But in this relative world everything has got its opposite. For example, in this world white is the opposite of conception of black. But in the transcendental world there is no distinction between white and black.

**Key Quote:** "Lord Sri Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the maintainer of the world, became pleased after re-establishing Maharaj Yudhishthira in his own kingdom and after restoring the Kuru dynasty, which had been exhausted by the bamboo fire of anger."
**Summary:** Suta Goswami replies to Shaunaka Rishi's question about how Yudhishthira Maharaj ruled, explaining that Krishna was pleased to re-establish Yudhishthira in his kingdom and restore the Kuru dynasty after the battle.

In this session, we will be discussing from the second verse of the tenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Suta Vacha Vamsham Kuroor Vamsha Dabhagni Nirhritam, Samroha Itva Bhavabhavano Harihi, Nivesha Itva Nijarajya Ishvaro, Yudhishthiram Preetamana Babhu Vaha. The meaning of this verse is: Suta Goswami said. Suta Goswami is responding to Shaunaka Rishi's question. The first verse was the question by Shaunaka Rishi: How did Yudhishthira Maharaj rule? And Suta Goswami is now going to reply. Lord Sri Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the maintainer of the world, became pleased after re-establishing Maharaj Yudhishthira in his own kingdom and after restoring the Kuru dynasty, which had been exhausted by the bamboo fire of anger. So the description is now being given that Krishna was pleased when Yudhishthira was re-established in his own kingdom.

---
### Material world, Anger, Forest fire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GGFNzt2HdnY?t=116
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This world is compared to a forest fire caused by the cohesion of bamboo bushes in the forest."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Prabhupada's purport that the material world is like a forest fire caused by the friction of bamboo, representing the anger and competition of those who want to lord over material nature.

So the description is now being given that Krishna was pleased when Yudhishthira was re-established in his own kingdom. It was his rightful claim, but he was denied by Duryodhana and party. So after the battle of Kurukshetra, the Kuru dynasty had been exhausted by the bamboo fire of anger. So Prabhupada explains in the purport, this world is compared to a forest fire caused by the cohesion of bamboo bushes in the forest. When there is rubbing of dry bamboo, especially in summer, then the spark ignites the dry wood, and the fire of the bamboo bush, and it spreads very quickly. It is typically called a forest fire. It spreads very quickly, and a whole forest can be devoured. All the dry bamboo will be completely devoured by the forest fire. Such a forest fire takes place automatically, for bamboo cohesion occurs without external cause. Nobody goes and sets fire to a bamboo bush. The bamboo in the forest, it happens without external cause. The natural reason for the fire is the nature of the material world. Similarly, in the material world, the wrath, wrath means anger, anger of those who want to lord it over material nature interacts, and the fire of the wild takes place, exhausting the unwanted population. In the material world, there are so many people who want to lord over material nature or lord over the world. There is competition for supremacy, though the supremacy is actually belonging only to one person, the Supreme Lord Krishna.

---
### Krishna's Lordship, Materialistic ambition, Natural calamities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GGFNzt2HdnY?t=247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the Lord, Krishna is the master, Krishna is the controller, Krishna is the provider, Krishna is the maintainer, Krishna is the well-wisher."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Krishna's supreme control and contrasts it with the foolish ambition of materialistic people who seek to dominate the world, leading to wars and natural calamities.

Krishna is the Lord, Krishna is the master, Krishna is the controller, Krishna is the provider, Krishna is the maintainer, Krishna is the well-wisher. But these foolish people under the influence of illusion wrongly think, I am the lord of all I survey. They think we are independent, they are Lords, they have power, or if they don't have enough power, I will amass power by my strength, by my influence, by my intelligence, by my political diplomacy. I will amass power and then rule over the whole world. This is the ambition of materialistic people. But when two such people both foolishly want to lord over, then there is the war that takes place between them. One will fight against the other, and in this way, the war exhausts the unwanted population. Natural calamities in this world are actually due to the fight between such foolish blind materialistic lords, and then the war is one natural calamity. Like that, there are so many different natural calamities which exhaust the unwanted population. Such fires or wars take place, and the Lord has nothing to do with them. The Lord has given us the laws, and if we abide by the laws, we will be able to understand the purpose of this creation.

---
### Purpose of creation, Demoniac mentality, Self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GGFNzt2HdnY?t=379
**Verse References:** BG 16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Foolish people, without understanding the real purpose of this creation, they think that there is no God in control; this is demonic mentality."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the purpose of creation, which is to understand and follow God's laws, contrasting this with the demonic mentality that denies God's control and emphasizes individual power.

The Lord has given us the laws, and if we abide by the laws, we will be able to understand the purpose of this creation, and we can live here as long as we live here. We can execute the purpose and prepare ourselves to go back to Godhead. But foolish people, without understanding the real purpose of this creation, they think that there is no God in control. This is demonic mentality. In the 16th chapter of the Bhagavad-gita, that is described, the demoniac thing: there is no God in control. If at all there is any God, I am that God. Ishwaroham ahambhogi siddhoham balavansukhi. This is demonic mentality, blind to reality. This is demonic nature. But Krishna still, even though there are foolish people and they falsely declared themselves to be the Lords, Krishna nevertheless is in control, because the Lord wants to maintain the creation. He desires the mass of people to follow the right path of self-realization, which enables the living beings to enter into the kingdom of God.

---
### Religious principles, Dharma, Suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GGFNzt2HdnY?t=463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Our suffering is only because we don't follow the religious principles; it's the only cause of our suffering in this material world."
**Summary:** Krishna provides the right path for self-realization, but people deviate from religious principles, leading to suffering. The Lord intervenes to re-establish Dharma and guide people back to Godhead.

Krishna has given us the right path to follow. Yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata dharmasya glani means people don't follow the right path of self-realization. They neglect or they violate the laws of God, and thereby the Lord has to intervene when there is dharmasya glani. tadatmanam srujamyaham. So, when Krishna comes or he sends his representative paritranaya sadhunam vinashaya ca dushkritam dharmasam sthapanartham dharma, he establishes the real religious principles, re-establishes because that was established in the beginning of creation, and time to time there is deterioration in following the religious principles. Kala influence of Kala. The people desiring to Lord over material nature. So the deterioration in practice of Dharma, and the Lord corrects it from time to time because Lord is the well-wisher of everyone. He doesn't want anyone to suffer. Our suffering is only because we don't follow the religious principles; it's the only cause of our suffering in this material world. Otherwise the Lord is provided. Everything is re-established. That's also important. That is our mission here, which is to realize is perfectly controlled by the Lord, we simply have to follow the religious principles and everything will be alright.

---
### Kingdom of God, Atheism, Faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GGFNzt2HdnY?t=578
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "This place is not meant for our permanent residence. No. We are meant to understand we belong to Krishna. We belong to Krishna's personal abode."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the goal is to return to the kingdom of God by following the right path of self-realization, warning against the misleading propaganda of atheists and materialists.

As Prabhupada says here, when people follow the right path of self-realization as given by God, then the living beings can enter into the kingdom of God. This place is not meant for our permanent residence. No. We are meant to understand we belong to Krishna. We belong to Krishna's personal abode. And we are meant to prepare ourselves to enter into the kingdom of God after following the right path of self-realization. The Lord wants the suffering human beings to come back. Welcome back to home back to him, and he wants to stop the suffering, threefold material banks. The people who are suffering, He wants to stop their suffering, and he wants these suffering beings, human beings to come back to his personal abode. So foolish people don't even know about the kingdom of God, and they are misled by atheists and agnostics and materialists who say, oh, this is all mythology, kingdom of God, this is all mythology. They have given a term to spoil the faith, to make people more materialistic and demoniac like themselves, so one should not be misled by such propaganda by the godless atheists. The whole plan of creation is made in that way.

---
### Laws of God, Maya, Threefold miseries
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GGFNzt2HdnY?t=692
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "You have to follow the laws of God; everyone has to follow the laws of God; that is the proper way to actually end our suffering."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of following God's laws, warning that deviating from them leads to suffering inflicted by Maya and the threefold miseries.

The whole plan of creation is made in that way, which way that people can follow the religious principles as given by God, not self-manufactured religious system. That is another mistake that sometimes people do. I follow my own religious principles. Now, you cannot have your own religious principles; it's not going to help you; it's not going to work. Everything is going on according to the laws of God, the laws of God. So you have to follow the laws of God; everyone has to follow the laws of God; that is the proper way to actually end our suffering. One who does not come to his senses suffers in the material world by pangs inflicted by the illusory energy of the Lord. Anybody who deviates, there is a system that Maya, the agent of Krishna in this material world, she will inflict suffering upon those who violate the laws of God and the laws of God. Durga Devi, she has got ten hands, and each of the ten hands she is having one weapon. Ten weapons she has. She is riding on a lion, and one particular weapon she has got is called trishu, trident. That trishu, you can see one picture, she is killing one demon with a trident. The trident represents the three prongs of the trident represents the threefold miseries: Adi Daivika Klesha, Adhyatmika Klesha, Adi Bhautika Klesha, miseries due to our own body and mind, miseries due to other living beings, and miseries due to higher forces of nature. So these threefold miseries are inflicted upon those who don't follow the religious principles given by God.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.10.20 | 06-06-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's qualities, Vedic hymns, attraction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gvBEg13rCQ0?t=61
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This talk was more attractive than the hymns of the Vedas."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse describing the ladies of Hastinapura discussing Krishna's transcendental qualities, which was more attractive than Vedic hymns.

In this session, we will be discussing the 20th verse of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, absorbed in the thought of the transcendental qualities of the Lord, whose glorified in select poetry, the ladies on the roofs of all the houses of Hastinapura began to talk of Him. This talk was more attractive than the hymns of the Vedas. This is a very interesting verse in this particular chapter, which describes the departure of Lord Krishna for Dwarka from Hastinapura. So the ladies who were the relatives of the kings and the royalty in the palace of Hastinapura, they went on top of the roof of the houses, and they began to talk of Krishna because they were always absorbed in the thought of the transcendental qualities of the Lord. The word used is Uttamasloka. Krishna's another name is Uttama Shloka, meaning He is glorified in select poetry by great poets, by great devotees. In all the Vedas, He is glorified by select poetry, choicest poetry. So they began to talk of Him, of Krishna, and this talk is described here in this verse, was more attractive than the hymns of the Vedas.

---
### Vedic literature, Krishna consciousness, devotee characteristics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gvBEg13rCQ0?t=193
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By all the Vedas am I to be known."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that the goal of all Vedic literature is Krishna, and that devotees are always absorbed in thoughts of Krishna.

Srila Prabhupada explains the purport. In the Bhagavad Gita, it is said that in all the Vedic literatures, the goal is the personality of Godhead, Krishna. What does Bhagavad Gita say? Vedascha sarvair ahameva vedyo. By all the Vedas am I to be known. Krishna says this in the Bhagavad Gita. And Srila Prabhupada says further, factually the glories of the Lord are depicted in such literature as the Vedas, Ramayana, and Mahabharata. This is also mentioned in the scriptures. Vede Ramayane chaiva purane bharate tatha. Aadavantecha madhyecha hari sarvatra giyate. The meaning of this verse is, in the Vedic literature, including the Ramayana, Puranas, and Mahabharata, from the very beginning, Aadav, to the end, Antecha, as well as within the middle, Madhyecha, only Hari, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is explained, is glorified. And in the Bhagavatam, they are specifically mentioned in respect to the Supreme Lord. Bhagavatam is Krishna Katha. It is exclusive glorification of Krishna from the beginning till the end, throughout the Bhagavatam. Therefore, while the ladies on the tops of the houses in the capital of the kings of the kingdom, the Kuru dynasty, were talking about the Lord, their talk was more pleasing than the Vedic hymns. It's generally seen that when ladies gather, they have a tendency to talk amongst themselves about some subject. Now, the subject they choose depends on the level of their consciousness. Anybody, for that matter. Like Srila Prabhupada explains, when businessmen gather in one place, what will their subject matter be? If they are seriously absorbed in business, then their talk will be about business only. Similarly, if scholars of some particular subject, they gather in one place, they will discuss about that subject matter only. They will talk about that subject matter only. So like that, these ladies, they were not just talking about some ordinary topics. No. They were talking about Lord Krishna. Why? Because they were absorbed in thinking about the transcendental qualities of Krishna, which means that they are all devotees of Krishna. So this is one characteristic of a devotee. A devotee is always absorbed in thought of Krishna.

---
### devotional service, Krishna consciousness, language vs. subject matter
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gvBEg13rCQ0?t=386
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The language is immaterial, but the subject matter is important."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of thinking of Krishna, and how the subject matter of devotional songs is more important than the language they are written in, using the example of Narottam Dasa Thakur's Bengali songs.

So Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, if you really are serious about devotional service, about bhakti, you want to become Krishna's devotee, then you simply think of Krishna always. Think of Krishna always. Conversely, devotees of Krishna always think of Krishna. They are always thinking of Krishna. So therefore, when they talk, the subject matter for their talking is only Krishna and nothing else. So Srila Prabhupada explains that why is it said that the talk of these ladies about Krishna was more attractive than the hymns of the Vedas? So Srila Prabhupada explains, anything sung in the praise of the Lord is Shruti Mantra. Shruti means the Vedas, the original Vedas. There are songs of Thakur Narottam Dasa, one of the Acharyas in the Gaudiya Sampradaya, composed in simple Bengali language. Narottam Dasa Thakur is a great Acharya. He was a contemporary of Srila Jeeva Goswami, who was one of the chief disciples of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So he learnt this Bhagavatam under Srila Jeeva Goswami, and then he has written so many songs in simple Bengali language, explaining the same subject matter, so that it is easily understandable by common people. He was preaching in Bengal, so it is written in Bengali language. Now, another Acharya, Thakur Vishwanath Chakravarthi, who is a very learned Acharya of the same Sampradaya, Vishwanath Chakravarthi Thakur has approved the songs by Thakur Narottam Dasa to be as good as Vedic mantras, even though they are written in simple Bengali language, but the subject matter is simply describing Krishna. So therefore they are as good as Vedic mantras. And Prabhupada says, this is so because of the subject matter. The language is immaterial, but the subject matter is important.

---
### Vedic wisdom, grace of the Lord, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gvBEg13rCQ0?t=561
**Verse References:** BG 10.10-11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna enlightens such a devotee from within."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the subject matter is more important than the language, and that devotees develop Vedic wisdom through the grace of Krishna, who enlightens them from within.

Of course, the original Vedas are always in Sanskrit language, but even if something is written in simple Bengali, or for that matter in Karnataka, Purandara Dasa has written lakhs and lakhs of songs in simple Kannada language, but the subject matter is the subject matter of Krishna, the subject matter of the Vedas. So the subject matter is important, the language is immaterial. So it is said here, the ladies who are all absorbed in the thought and actions of the Lord, develop the consciousness of Vedic wisdom by the grace of the Lord. Now in the next few verses, there will be description of what were they, what was their subject of discussion? What were they talking? So it's all full of Vedic wisdom. Their talk is full of Vedic wisdom. So Srila Prabhupada explains, therefore, although such ladies might not have been very learned scholars in Sanskrit or otherwise, these ladies, they are just householders. They are not particularly meant to study the Vedas, go to Gurukul, in the Vedic culture at least. But that does not mean they are totally ignorant about Krishna, or they are totally ignorant about the Vedic conclusions. No. By absorbing their mind in thoughts and actions of the Supreme Lord, they develop the consciousness of Vedic wisdom. How? By the grace of the Supreme Lord Krishna. And how are they able to get that grace of the Supreme Lord? Because Krishna is seated on the heart of every one of us, every living being. So if somebody is absorbed in the thought of Krishna, Krishna enlightens such a devotee from within. TESHAM SATATA YUKTANAM BHAJATAM PRITI PURVAKAM DADAMI BUDDHI YOGAM TAM YENAMA MUPAYANTITE. TESHAM EVANUKAM PARTHAM AHAM AGNYANAJAM TAMAHA NASHAYAMI ATMABHAVASTO GYANADIPENABHASVATAM. Krishna enlightens from within the heart of His devotees. Therefore, Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, the most confidential instruction, become my devotee, then Krishna will enlighten. There is no need of specifically attempting to study the Vedas, which are very difficult, because the Vedic language is not easy. Even for Sanskrit scholars, Vedic language is not easily understood in the proper perspective. So therefore, Krishna says, you simply become my devotee. Think of me. Discuss about me. And then I will enlighten you from within your heart. All the Vedic conclusions will be revealed by Krishna in the heart of the devotee.

---
### Upanishads, Puranas, personal vs. impersonal
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gvBEg13rCQ0?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The discussions or the descriptions in the Puranas, Itihasas, about the personal feature of the Lord are more attractive and pleasing to hear than the descriptions in the Upanishads."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why the ladies' talks were more attractive than Vedic hymns, contrasting the indirect approach of the Upanishads with the direct, personal descriptions in the Puranas and Itihasas.

Whatever they spoke, these ladies of Hastinapura, was more attractive than the Vedic hymns. Why? Why is it like that? Prabhupada says, the Vedic hymns in the Upanishads are sometimes indirectly directed to the Supreme Lord. The Upanishads are actually the philosophical portions of the Vedas which describe the Absolute Truth. And the Absolute Truth is described both impersonal and personal. So the impersonal descriptions are very prominent in the Upanishads portions of the Vedas. Whereas, the personal aspect of the Absolute Truth, that is the personality of Godhead, Krishna, Rama, Narsimha, Varaha, all the incarnations are more nicely described in detail in the Puranas, Itihasas, like Ramayana, entirely about Lord Rama. Mahabharata, about Krishna and many other incarnations. Bhagavatam, fully about Krishna and his incarnations. So the Puranas are describing the personal feature of the Absolute Truth. Therefore, the discussions or the descriptions in the Puranas, Itihasas, about the personal feature of the Lord are more attractive and pleasing to hear than the descriptions in the Upanishads. Therefore, it is said the ladies' talks were more attractive than the Vedic hymns.

### Kṛṣṇa consciousness, devotional service, annihilation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ecBDTNJDpnM?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Instead of trying to study the Vedas and then try to understand what is the Absolute Truth, one should simply engage in devotional service by which automatically one becomes enlightened in the Vedic wisdom."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains that engaging in devotional service is superior to studying the Vedas for understanding the Absolute Truth, and describes the partial and complete annihilation of the universe at the end of Brahma's day and life, respectively.

We are in the tenth chapter of the first canto of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. savai-kilāyaṁ puruṣa-pūrātaṇo ya eka āsīd aviśeṣa ātmani agre guṇebhyo jagad ātmanīśvare nimīlita ātman nishi-sūpta-śaktiṣu. The meaning of this verse is, they said, the ladies of Sīnāpura who are standing on top of the palace, here he is, the original personality of Godhead, as we definitely remember him. He alone existed before the manifested creation of the modes of nature, and in him only, because he is the Supreme Lord, all living beings merge as if sleeping at night, their energy is suspended. The ladies of the royal palace of Sīnāpura, they are talking amongst themselves about Kṛṣṇa, and the level of their knowledge is so very advanced that Bhāgavatam presents this as the philosophy of Kṛṣṇa consciousness, their talks. That is because, as was explained earlier, a devotee of Kṛṣṇa, simply because the devotee is engaged in devotional service, which is as simple as chanting Hare Kṛṣṇa, worshipping Kṛṣṇa, or just having darshan of Kṛṣṇa's deity, one becomes enlightened from within by the grace of the Supreme Lord, who is seated in everyone's heart, is Paramātmā. So these ladies, they are able to discuss at this level, advanced level of Kṛṣṇa consciousness, simply because they are enlightened from within by Kṛṣṇa Himself. So therefore it is said, instead of trying to study the Vedas and then try to understand what is the Absolute Truth, one should simply engage in devotional service by which automatically one becomes enlightened in the Vedic wisdom, and one also becomes purified in the heart of all material contamination, one makes advancement in Kṛṣṇa consciousness, and ultimately one becomes reconnected with Kṛṣṇa in their original relationship with Kṛṣṇa. So bhakti is the only way to achieve ultimate perfection in our self-realization, in our spiritual development. The magnetism has definitely been learnt from my Angara which is known as отсurgency because through non-figure rests and water fish GMT whoever is clothed in water in the and when it is not visible it's called unmanifested so this manifested cosmic manifestation cosmos has two kinds of dissolution two kinds of destruction at the end of Brahma's day which is four billion three hundred and twenty million years the quanda our calculation that is just the day of Brahma when Brahma the one particular the Lord of one particular universe goes to sleep there is one annihilation it's called a partial destruction at the end of Brahma's life which takes place at the end of Brahma's 100 years. Brahma's 100 years and what is that calculation according to our calculation 8 billion 640 million years into 30 into 12 into 100 that comes to some 311 trillion years there is complete annihilation of the entire universe complete annihilation as I said Brahma's end of the day only partial destruction of the middle and lower planetary systems at the end of Brahma's life all the planets and universe are completely destroyed and in both the periods the material energy called the Mahat Tattva and the marginal energy called the Jeeva Tattva merge in the person of the Supreme Lord. ### creation, Mahavishnu, universes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ecBDTNJDpnM?t=487
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mahavishnu breathes out universes in very, very, very tiny seed-like form, but each of them develops into huge, gigantic universe with millions of planets."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains how the material creation is directed by the three modes of material nature, set in motion by the Lord, and describes the role of Mahavishnu in breathing out universes in seed-like form, which then develop into huge universes with millions of planets.

Sri La Pravapa explains further the material creation is directed by the interaction of the three modes of material nature set in action by the Lord and therefore it is said here that the Lord existed before the modes of material nature was set in motion so before this creation Lord existed as Krishna in his various forms all the forms Lord Krishna Rama Narasimha Vraha Narayana Vishnu they always exist in that particular eternal form where is we we have got a form now and after that creation but this form is not going to last it is going to be destroyed end of this life or in the end of the universe at the end of this manifestation when the dissolution takes place this will be destroyed so it is competency Informed in the Shruti Mantra, in the Vedas, original Vedas, that only Vishnu, the Supreme Lord, existed before the creation and there was no Brahma, no Shiva, no demigods. Vishnu means the Mahavishnu who is lying on the causal ocean. There is one causal ocean, Karana Jala. And there, one form of Vishnu, the first form of Vishnu, Mahavishnu, he is lying there and he is breathing and by his breathing, the universes are coming out of his body in the form of seeds. And these seed-like universes, very, very tiny, they develop gradually into huge universe. And within that universe, there will be universe. There will be innumerable planets in each and every universe. Now, how do they develop? Prabhupada gives a very nice example. Just like a very, very, very small banyan seed, seed of a banyan tree, very tiny, it's like a pinhead, it's very, very small. That, when planted in a fertile ground, it will develop into huge universe. A huge banyan tree that can spread over hundreds of square kilometers. That tiny seed. So, like that, Mahavishnu breathes out universes in very, very, very tiny seed-like form. But each of them develops into huge, gigantic universe with millions of planets.

### Mahavishnu, Krishna, Brahma's lifespan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ecBDTNJDpnM?t=660
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Try to understand how great is God. Just this alone can help you understand."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the relationship between Krishna and Mahavishnu, explaining that Mahavishnu is a plenary portion of Krishna, and describes the lifespan of Brahma in relation to the breathing cycle of Mahavishnu.

Each planet, millions of living entities. So, this Mahavishnu is described in the Brahma Samhita as a plenary portion of Krishna. In the Brahma Samhita it is said, Let me offer my respectful obeisances unto the original personality of Godhead, Govinda, whose plenary portion is Mahavishnu. All the Brahmas, the heads of the universes, live only for the period of his exhaling after universes are generated from the pores of his transcendental body. So, Krishna expands as Mahavishnu. That Mahavishnu breathes out universes in seed-like form. And they'll remain only as long as, after his breathing out, for some time, he is not inhaling. So, he, after he exhales, little time, then he inhales. So, that tiny duration between his exhalation and inhalation, that is the duration of the life of Brahma. Jeevan tiloma vilaja jagadanda natha That is lifetime of Brahma. For us, how long is that period? 311 trillion years. The lifespan of Brahma, according to our calculation. So, the interval between the exhalation, outgoing breath of Mahavishnu, and the inhaling, the incoming breath, is 311 trillion years. This is not even the complete breathing cycle of Mahavishnu. 311 trillion years. It is the intervening time between his exhalation and inhalation. So, try to understand how great is God. Just this alone can help you understand.

### Krishna, authority, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ecBDTNJDpnM?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To discover or awaken or come to know about our real identity, we have to engage in devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that Krishna is the original person and expands into Mahavishnu, emphasizing the importance of hearing from authoritative sources to understand transcendental subject matter, and highlighting that the real identity of the living being is spiritual and can be realized through devotional service.

So, that Krishna, what the ladies are telling, is actually the Mahavishnu. Krishna is the original person. He expands into different incarnations, different forms of incarnations, and they are all Him only. So, Mahavishnu, who is actually the origin of this universe, who breathes in and breathes out, creation and annihilation takes place. So, that Mahavishnu is none other than Krishna only. Therefore, they are saying, here is the original personality of Godhead. He alone existed before the manifested creation of the modes of nature. Therefore, they are saying that. The ladies talking about this Vedic truth must have heard it from authoritative source. Otherwise, how can they exactly describe this accurately? So, an authoritative source is the only means of knowing about transcendental subject matter definitely. There is no other alternative. Who can ever figure out this? That how is this creation taking place? The way it is described here, how can somebody figure out? Except by hearing from the proper authority or proper source. So, therefore, the scriptures say, hear from the Vedas. Hear from the original source, from Krishna, from the Acharyas, who receive this knowledge from Krishna in parampara. There is no other way of knowing this. Now, the merging of the living entities in the body of Mahavishnu or Garbhodakshaya Vishnu, does not mean the living being is the only being. Does not mean the living being is the only being. The living being is annihilated. The living being as spirit soul is eternally existent. But the body is annihilated. So, the real identity of the living being is spiritual. And that is, we are ultimately devotees of Krishna. And to discover or awaken or come to know about our real identity, we have to engage in devotional service. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 22nd verse of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is a continuation of the talk of the ladies of Hastinapura about Krishna. Sa eva bhuyo nija virya choditam Svajivamayam prakritim sisrikshatim Anama roopatmani roopanamani Viditsamano anusasara shastrakrit. The meaning of this verse is: The personality of Godhead, again desiring to give names and forms to his parts and parcels, the living entities, place them under the guidance of material nature. By his own potency, material nature is empowered to recreate. So in the talk, they are describing how this creation takes place by the intervention of Krishna.

### Krishna consciousness, purification, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aqmqW9DWZnw?t=60
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Here is the same Supreme Personality of Godhead whose transcendental form is experienced by the great devotees who are completely cleansed of material consciousness by dint of rigid devotional service and full control of life and the senses. And that is the only way to purify existence."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces verse 23 from the 10th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that the verse describes how the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Krishna, can be experienced by devotees who have purified themselves through devotional service and sense control. He emphasizes that this purification is the only way to improve our existence.

Savaayam Yat Padam Atra Surayo Jitendriya Nirjitam Atarishvanaha Pashyanti Bhakti Utkalitat Amalatmana Nanvesha Sattvam Parimastum Arhati. The meaning of this verse is, here is the same Supreme Personality of Godhead whose transcendental form is experienced by the great devotees who are completely cleansed of material consciousness by dint of rigid devotional service and full control of life and the senses. And that is the only way to purify existence. These are words spoken by the ladies of Hastinapura. They are discussing amongst themselves about the personality of Godhead Krishna. This is when Krishna is about to leave Hastinapura for traveling back to Dwaraka. So these ladies, they are not any great women. They are not that great, they are not very well educated. See, they have absolutely no knowledge of Krishna. They have no wisdom in Krishna and they have no knowledge of the great Godhead Krishna. Can't they even understand what Krishna is about? They are not aware of him, they are aware of the great Godhead Krishna who is about to leave Hastinapura. So, what is Krishna? What is Krishna's personality? What is the nature of Krishna? What is Krishna's identity, what does he mean by that? What is Krishna's personality? And of course, that hearing also results in purification of the heart in awakening their dormant Krishna consciousness, which is natural for everyone, every living being, every person.

### chanting, worship, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aqmqW9DWZnw?t=453
