He explains that consistent practice purifies the mind of material contamination accumulated over lifetimes, breaking the cycle of birth and death (samsara).

In the Bhagavad Gita also the same thing is explained. By rigid devotional service. Practice. Constant practice. Every day we have to chant and hear Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Every day. Every day we have to worship Krishna. Every day we have to take food offered to Krishna as prasadam. Every day we have to sing Krishna's glories. We have to hear the Srimad Bhagavatam or read the Bhagavatam or recollect the pastimes of Krishna which we may have heard earlier. This is required every day. Srimad Bhagavatam sevaya. Nitya means every day. Regularly. Constantly. Always. This way the mind can become purified of all material dust. This material dust we have accumulated over long association with material nature in past so many millions of years. Of lifetimes. We have been around in this material world changing bodies again and again and again and again and again. This is called repetition of birth and death. Samsara. We perform some activities in this life. And those activities result in some reactions, good or bad. And then to undergo those reactions we get another body and in the next life again we perform some more material activities and this repeats till we come in contact with a pure devotee of Krishna like Srila Prabhupada and after that we accept the process of rigid devotional self.

---
### human life, ignorance, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aqmqW9DWZnw?t=573
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And only in the human form of life do we have the developed intelligence to understand this. Who am I? Who is Krishna? What is my relationship with Krishna? What am I entitled to? As spirit soul I am entitled to eternal happiness in association with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the material activities that most people engage in with the opportunity for purification through devotional service. He emphasizes that only in the human form of life can we develop the intelligence to understand our true identity, our relationship with Krishna, and our potential for eternal happiness. He introduces the concept of 'Jita Indriya,' one who has full control over the senses, as explained by Srila Prabhupada.

This is the service. Until then our material existence continues life after life after life after life after life. You can see everywhere around you what are people engaged in. They are simply engaged in material activities whether good or bad. So they have no chance of purifying their body. Mind. So they remain in ignorance. Darkness of ignorance. And only in the human form of life do we have the developed intelligence to understand this. Who am I? Who is Krishna? What is my relationship with Krishna? What am I entitled to? As spirit soul I am entitled to eternal happiness in association with Krishna. That is explained by Srila Prabhupada. So Srila Prabhupada explains further in this purport. Jita Indriya means one who has full control over the senses. Jita Indriya. One who has conquered his senses. Normally we are dictated by our senses. Our senses demand and we try to fulfill those demands. With the hope that I will be able to fulfill those demands. I will become happy if I satisfy my senses. This is ignorance actually. The reality is that these senses if they are controlled and engaged in devotional service only then can we become really happy. Experience real happiness.

---
### sense control, bhakti, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aqmqW9DWZnw?t=693
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Engage the senses to fulfill himself the eyes and seeing the beautiful form of Krishna, engage the tongue in tasting wonderful Krishna Prasadam, engage the ears in hearing nice Krishna Katha or nice Krishna Bhajans, like that and your senses automatically become controlled and when they are purified you can see Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts 'Jita Indriya' with 'Ajita Indriya,' those who are controlled by their senses. He explains that true sense control is not achieved through artificial suppression, but through engaging the senses in devotional service (bhakti). He gives examples of how to engage the senses in Krishna consciousness, such as seeing Krishna's form, tasting Krishna prasadam, and hearing Krishna katha and bhajans, leading to purification and the ability to see Krishna.

If we are Jita Indriya. Those who are simply trying to satisfy the demands of the senses they are called Ajita Indriya. Ajita Indriya. Most people they are just having these uncontrolled senses and their life is only simply satisfying the demands of the senses all the time. All the time. And the center of all these senses is the mind so it's the mind which is driving the senses and the senses are driving our organs of action and is simply engaged in different activities with the single aim of satisfying the senses and the mind with a hope that we can become happy but it is explained here that sense control is not possible by stopping all activities of the senses and it is even explained here for Srila Prabhupada the artificial means of the yogic processes to make the senses inactive has proved to be abject failure trying to stop the senses by even the hatha yoga or the ashtanga yoga process is not a guaranteed method of controlling the senses the example is given of Vishwamitra Muni a very famous example he controlled his senses by yogic trance but when he happened to meet Menaka an absurd trauma he became a victim of sex and artificial way of controlling the senses failed what is this artificial way you try to suppress the senses we say no no no no to demands of the senses but the devotee simply engages all the senses and that is exactly what is bhakti rishikeshya sevannam bhakti ruchyate engage the senses to fulfill himself the eyes and seeing the beautiful form of Krishna, engage the tongue in tasting wonderful Krishna Prasadam, engage the ears in hearing nice Krishna Katha or nice Krishna Bhajans, like that and your senses automatically become controlled and when they are purified you can see Krishna. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Krishna's nature, transcendence, Leela
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cc7Gtm392rY?t=70
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.24
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is Krishna only who is the supreme personality of Godhead who creates, maintains, and annihilates the material world, and he remains unaffected."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces verse 24 from the 10th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that Krishna, though appearing as a king in Dwaraka, is eternally engaged in his Leela, aiming to liberate conditioned souls from material suffering.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 24th verse of the 10th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Savaayam Sakhi Anugeeta Sakkatho Vedeshu Guhyejucha Guhyavadibhihi Ya Eka Eesho Jagad Atma Leelaya Srijati Avati Atti Natatra Sajjate. The meaning of this verse is: O dear friends, here is that very personality of Godhead whose attractive and confidential pastimes are described in the confidential parts of Vedic literature by his great devotees. It is he only who creates, maintains, and annihilates the material world, and yet remains unaffected. These are words spoken by the ladies of Ashthinapura. They know the transcendental nature of Krishna's personality. They are not mistaking him to be another ordinary King. Even though he is playing the role of the King of Dwaraka, it is actually his eternal Leela in the spiritual world which he is displaying in the material world to attract us, the conditioned souls who are trapped here. He wants us to get out of this material existence, put an end to all our suffering. That's Krishna's mission, and that's the mission of all Krishna's devotees also. So it is Krishna only who is the supreme personality of Godhead who creates, maintains, and unleashes the material world, and he remains unaffected. That cannot be said of the Devatas or anybody else who is engaged in different activities in this material world.

---
### Vedic literature, confidential knowledge, devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cc7Gtm392rY?t=198
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you really want to hear the most attractive personal feature of the absolute truth, then you have to hear the most confidential part of the Vedic literature which describes those confidentialities of the Vedas and the personal features of the Vedas, confidential pastimes, and they are described only by great devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while the Vedas generally describe the impersonal and localized features of the Absolute Truth (Brahman and Paramatma), the personal feature (Bhagavan) is found in the confidential parts of Vedic literature, especially Srimad Bhagavatam, and is revealed by great devotees.

You can see that in the material world everyone else becomes bound up by their material actions, whereas Krishna remains completely transcendental under all circumstances. Here it's also said that Krishna's pastimes are confidential, described in the confidential parts of the Vedic literature by his great devotees. Ordinarily, the Vedas describe the absolute truth in terms of the impersonal feature and the localized feature, the Brahman feature and the Paramahatma feature. The descriptions of the Bhagawan feature, the personal feature, is very rarely found in the original Vedas. It is there in the original Vedas, but it is not so much described. The bulk of the Vedas consists of the descriptions of the interactions to the three gunas, trai guna vishaya Veda, and the conclusion of the Vedas given as Vedanta philosophy describes mostly the impersonal feature or the Brahman feature of the absolute truth. So if you really want to hear the most attractive personal feature of the absolute truth, Krishna, Rama, Narsimha, Brahma, Narayana, all of these activities of the personality of Godhead in his various incarnations, and especially his activities in his original form as Krishna, then you have to hear the most confidential part of the Vedic literature which describes those confidentialities of the Vedas and the personal features of the Vedas, confidential pastimes, and they are described only by great devotees. They are described only by great devotees.

---
### Srimad Bhagavatam, devotees, confidential pastimes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cc7Gtm392rY?t=350
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavatam is special literature...the most confidential pastimes described in the Bhagavatam are very special, and they are described by confidential devotee Sukhadeva Goswami."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of Srimad Bhagavatam as a special literature containing the most confidential pastimes of Krishna, described by the confidential devotee Sukhadeva Goswami, contrasting it with the Vedanta sutras and Upanishads which only hint at these pastimes.

So in the purport from the process as stated in the Bhagavad Gita, all the Vedic literature is glorified greatness of Lord Sri Krishna. Everywhere in all the Vedic literature, they are simply glorifying the greatness of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna. Here it is confirmed in the Bhagavatam also. The Vedas are expanded by many branches and sub-branches by great devotees and empowered incarnations of the Lord like Vyasa, Narada, Sukhadeva Goswami, the Kumaras, Kapila, Prahlada, Janaka, Bali, and Yamaraj. All of these personalities are Mahajanas, Mahatmas, pure devotees, great personalities, but in the Srimad Bhagavatam, especially the confidential parts of his activities are described by the confidential devotee Sukhadeva Goswami. So Bhagavatam is special literature. There are so many Vedic literatures, but among all of them, Nigamakalpatarorgalitamphalam, the most confidential pastimes described in the Bhagavatam are very special, and they are described by confidential devotee Sukhadeva Goswami. In the Vedanta sutras or the Upanishads, there is only a hint of the confidential parts of his pastimes.

---
### spiritual nature, Krishna's form, understanding Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cc7Gtm392rY?t=478
**Verse References:** BG 2
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He looks like an ordinary person, but he is not an ordinary person. He looks like a limited person, but he is unlimited personality."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna's identity is fully spiritual, distinct from material conceptions. He emphasizes that Krishna's form, name, qualities, and paraphernalia are transcendental, and that understanding Krishna requires hearing from authorized sources like Sukhadeva Goswami or his representatives such as Srila Prabhupada.

In such Vedic literatures as the Upanishads, the Lord has explained to us that the Lord has expressively been distinguished from the mundane conception of his existence. The Upanishads are describing the transcendental nature of the absolute truth completely separated from the material nature. In the Bhagavad Gita in the second chapter, elaborately it is explained in the second chapter that the soul is not the soul itself is non-material, the spirit, and so is Krishna, the personality of Godhead, is completely a spiritual personality, no tinge of any materiality in his personality. He looks like an ordinary person, but he is not an ordinary person. He looks like a limited person, but he is unlimited personality. He looks like he is incapable when he's chastised by mother Yashoda, and he becomes afraid of her punishment. It appears like if a child is afraid of the mother, then that child is not any great personality, but Krishna is performing his Leela in his activities in relationship of love with mother Yashoda. This we can understand if we hear the Bhagavatam from Sukhadeva Goswami or from a representative of Sukhadeva Goswami like Srila Prabhupada, or from a representative of Sukhadeva Goswami like Srila Prabhupada.

---
### spiritual qualities, Krishna's name, attraction to Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cc7Gtm392rY?t=595
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you say Krishna, it's not a name which is indicating Krishna, the name Krishna is Krishna, is the person Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the spiritual nature of Krishna's form, name, and qualities, explaining that Krishna is fully present in his name and that his attractive qualities are transcendental, leading to detachment from material attractions.

They know the confidential nature of such activities of Krishna. So the nature of Krishna's personality has to be understood carefully from the right source. So further Srila Prabhupada says his identity being fully spiritual, his form, name, quality, and paraphernalia have been elaborately distinguished from matter. They are not material. This form is not material. Our form is completely material. His name is not a material name. That means there are no material qualities. When we designate this object as a table, it is entitled actually is nominated as the author of a census, which means it has been talked about in materialist literature all the way to the recent decades when it is designation of the object itself, it is not the object itself. The name table or the sound table is not the object table. Where is Krishna? When you say Krishna, it's not a name which is indicating Krishna, the name Krishna is Krishna, is the person Krishna. So Krishna is fully present in that sound Krishna. So when you say Krishna, Krishna appears in the form, the sound, and he purifies you. If you touch him by your consciousness, your consciousness becomes completely surcharged with devotion, with bhakti, with love for Krishna. So that is name of Krishna, Krishna namah. His qualities, all his qualities are spiritual, transcendental. That means if Krishna is attractive, that attractive feature of his personality is a transcendental feature. That means once we become attracted to Krishna, nothing else in this material world will be attractive to us. All other attractions will pale into insignificance when we get attracted by Krishna.

### attraction, qualities of Krishna, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cc7Gtm392rY?t=762
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is all attractive means he attracts everyone and how does he do so by his unlimited attractive qualities."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's explanation of the Atmarama verse, highlighting Krishna's unlimited attractive qualities and how different individuals are drawn to different aspects of his personality, ultimately leading to the uncovering of the heart and attraction to Krishna through bhakti.

This is very nicely explained by Sri Chaitanya Maha Prabhu in the 61 meanings of the Atmarama verse of the Bhagavatam. There is one verse in the Bhagavatam which describes the all attractive feature of Krishna's personality. This verse explains Krishna is all attractive means he attracts everyone and how does he do so by his unlimited attractive qualities. He is a reservoir of all wonderful qualities and each person is attracted by one particular quality of Krishna because that is their nature. Somebody is attracted by Krishna's beauty, somebody is Krishna attracted by Krishna's wealth, somebody is attracted by Krishna's kindness, somebody is attracted by Krishna's love. Of course, love is a general thing. Everybody is attracted by Krishna's love because everyone has a loving relationship with Krishna, but different qualities of Krishna attract different persons. And how many qualities that Krishna possess? Unlimited qualities. The sum total of all qualities which are possessed by anybody or all the people those are the reservoir of qualities in Krishna. All good qualities Krishna possesses and he attracts. So right now our condition is that we are covered so we are unable to experience that attraction of Krishna. So when we uncover our heart, immediately we get attracted to Krishna. That is actually the process of bhakti. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Incarnation, Dharma, Mercy

**Verse References:** SB 1.10.25

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the 25th verse of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that Krishna incarnates to show mercy, protect devotees, and destroy those in the lowest modes of existence.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 25th verse of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam: Yadahi Adharmena Tamodhiyo Nripa Jeevanti Tatreshahi Satvatah Kila Dhatte Bhagam Satyam Ritam Dayam Yasho Bhavaya Rupani Dadhat Yuge Yuge. The meaning of this verse is: Whenever there are kings and administrators living like animals in the lowest modes of existence, the Lord in his transcendental form manifests his supreme power, the truth positive, shows special mercy to the faithful, performs wonderful activities, and manifests various transcendental forms as is necessary in different periods and ages.

---
### Property, Entitlement, Responsibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lSrS5LVxN_U?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This world we think it belongs to us but actually it belongs to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the cosmic creation belongs to Krishna, and we are entitled to a portion of its resources only to execute our prescribed duties, emphasizing that no one should encroach upon the property of the Supreme Lord.

So this is the understanding of the pure devotees that Krishna incarnates in every yuga to show his mercy towards the faithful devotees, to protect them, and to destroy all these animal-like kings and administrators in the lowest modes of existence and to also most importantly perform his wonderful activities to attract the conditioned souls to go back to Godhead, to go back to his personal abode with him, live with him eternally, a life of bliss and knowledge and eternal life. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, as mentioned above, the cosmic creation is the property of the Supreme Lord. This world we think it belongs to us but actually it belongs to Krishna. This is the basic philosophy of the issue punishment, the first to punish it among all the punishments, issue punishment, there it is explained. Every one of us is a part of the Supreme Lord and we are all part of the Supreme Lord, everything is the property of the Supreme being Isha vasya idam sarvam yatkincha jagattyam jagat. So therefore no one should encroach upon the property of the Supreme Lord, tena tyaktena bunjitha maa grudakasya svidhanam, one should accept only what is kindly awarded by him. Therefore, the earth or any other planet or universe is the absolute property of the Supreme Lord Krishna. The living beings are certainly his parts and parcels or sons, and thus every one of them has a right to live at the mercy of the Lord to execute his prescribed work. It belongs to Krishna does not mean we are not entitled to any portion of any resource in this world. It doesn't mean that we have an entitlement as his son, as his child, as his amsha, but we have a right to only a tiny portion which is our actual requirement for executing the prescribed duties.

---
### Governance, Responsibility, Exploitation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lSrS5LVxN_U?t=290
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The authority given to a king to rule over the citizens or for that matter a government to rule over the people is for the sake of executing the will of the Lord that everybody gets their due share to execute their prescribed duty."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the role of kings and administrators as representatives of the Lord, emphasizing their responsibility to ensure everyone receives their due share and contrasting this with irresponsible leaders who exploit resources for personal gain.

No one therefore can encroach upon the right of another individual man or animal without being so sanctioned by the Lord. The Lord sanction is required if we have to encroach upon others. We cannot encroach, and if we do so unlawfully, we are punishable by the laws of Krishna called the laws of material nature, by the laws of Krishna called the laws of material nature, laws of God. The king or the administrator is a representative of the Lord to look after the management of the Lord's will. The authority given to a king to rule over the citizens or for that matter a government to rule over the people is for the sake of executing the will of the Lord that everybody gets their due share to execute their prescribed duty. The king therefore must be a recognized person like Maharaj Yudhishtira or Maharaj Pariksit. Such kings have full responsibility and knowledge from authorities about the administration of the world. They were responsible kings, they were the ideal kings. They ensured that everybody had their quota. There was no scarcity of any material resource. So everybody was happy, everybody was living peacefully, and they were executing their duties very nicely and everything was very nice in this world. But at times due to the influence of the ignorance mode of material nature, tamo guna, the lowest of the material modes, kings and administrators come into power without knowledge and responsibility. And such foolish administrators live like animals for the sake of their own personal interest. Irresponsible kings, irresponsible government heads. They live a lavish life. They think they have captured power by dint of their own prowess and they exploit the resources of material nature. They exploit people. They exploit the wealth which they gather as taxes. And they enjoy and lead a luxurious life without any sense of responsibility.

---
### Ignorance, Kali Yuga, Leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lSrS5LVxN_U?t=456
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because foolish people think, my only need is to satisfy my senses."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the consequences of ignorant leadership, including anarchy, vicious elements, and societal problems like nepotism and famine, particularly prevalent in Kali Yuga, where people elect leaders based on promises of sense enjoyment rather than spiritual understanding.

The result is that the whole atmosphere becomes surcharged with anarchy and vicious elements. Naturally such kings cannot rule properly when they don't have the sense of responsibility, when they don't have the proper knowledge, don't have proper understanding. Then in ignorance, darkness of ignorance, they are not able to rule properly. Nepotism, bribery, cheating, aggression and therefore famine, epidemic, war and similar other disturbing features become prominent in human society. We see this, especially in this age. As Kali Yuga progresses, we will see more of this because the people are unable to understand what kind of ruler will actually be the ideal administrator. They elect somebody who promises some sense enjoyment because foolish people think, my only need is to satisfy my senses. They don't understand the responsibility in the human form of life is to engage in activities of self-realization and thereby make an all-around solution to all problems in this world. That people do not know. So they elect the wrong type of leader and as a result, they suffer due to ignorance. And gradually the situation becomes goes from bad to worse. That's what happens.

---
### Incarnation, Dharma, Transcendental
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lSrS5LVxN_U?t=580
**Verse References:** BG 4.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these symptoms indicate the time of an incarnation of the Lord to reestablish the principles of religion and to vanquish the maladministrators."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the persecution of devotees and the rise of irreligion signal the time for the Lord's incarnation to re-establish Dharma, referencing the Bhagavad Gita and emphasizing the transcendental nature of the Lord's forms.

And as regards the devotees of the Lord, or the faithful, they are persecuted by such leaders who are in the mode of ignorance. All these symptoms indicate the time of an incarnation of the Lord to reestablish the principles of religion and to vanquish the maladministrators. This is also confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita: yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata abhyuttanam adharmasya. When irreligion becomes too prominent and people neglect the real religious principles, the codes of God, then yadatmanamsrujamyaham. Krishna says, I appear then as an incarnation. The Lord then appears in his transcendental form without any tinge of material qualities. Whichever the form, Narsimha, Varaha or Krishna, Rama, all are transcendental forms of the Lord. No tinge of material quality. There is no material quality at all. Completely spiritual qualities. He descends just to keep the state of his creation in normal condition. And it becomes abnormal. Krishna restores normalcy.

---
### Provision, Duties, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lSrS5LVxN_U?t=672
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The normal condition is that the Lord has provided each and every planet with all the needs of the native living beings."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's provision for all beings, emphasizing the importance of executing prescribed duties to attain liberation and explaining that the material world is created to satisfy the desires of conditioned souls while also providing a path to liberation through following the shastras.

The normal condition is that the Lord has provided each and every planet with all the needs of the native living beings. If we are meant to stay on the earth planet, Krishna has provided on this earth planet everything necessary for our living comfortably and mainly for executing our prescribed duties to prepare ourselves to get out of material entanglement and go back to Godhead. They can live happily and execute their predestined occupations and attain salvation at the end following the rules and regulations mentioned in the revealed scriptures. Everything is given by Krishna. The shastras are giving us the knowledge, information about who we are, what are our duties, what are our necessities, what is the way of securing those necessities, how should we perform our duties, and how we can prepare ourselves to attain salvation or liberation at the end of this life, especially human beings. The material world is created to satisfy the whims of the Nityabhadra or everlasting conditioned souls. We are called eternally conditioned because we cannot trace the beginning of our material existence. But nevertheless, Krishna has created this world in such a way that we can try to satisfy our material desires. At the same time, when we follow the shastras, we can also prepare ourselves to attain ultimate liberation. Both are, both the purposes are there in creating this world. Actually, because we have material desires for sense enjoyment, Krishna has created this world, otherwise there would be no need of creating this world if we didn't have material desires.

---
### Intoxication, Exploitation, Punishment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lSrS5LVxN_U?t=797
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When people become intoxicated with the power of material science to exploit the resources unlawfully without the sanction of the Lord and that also only for sense gratification, there is necessity of the Lord's incarnation to chastise the rebellious and to protect the faithful."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining that the Lord incarnates when people become intoxicated with material power and exploit resources unlawfully for sense gratification, emphasizing the Lord's role in chastising the rebellious, protecting the faithful, and re-establishing Dharma, citing examples like Ravana, Hiranyakashipu, and Kamsa.

When people become intoxicated with the power of material science to exploit the resources unlawfully without the sanction of the Lord and that also only for sense gratification, there is necessity of the Lord's incarnation to chastise the rebellious and to protect the faithful. So here Prabhupada is explaining when does the Lord incarnate? When people become too much intoxicated by some material power. They go on exploiting and a stage comes when they become intoxicated with the power, we can do whatever we like, we have amassed so much of wealth, we have so much of technology, we can do whatever we want, but actually the stringent laws of nature are always working and these foolish people cannot understand how the laws are working. So the Lord incarnates at such times, so when He descends, He exhibits His superhuman acts just to prove His supreme right and materialists like Ravana, Hiranyakashipu and Kamsa are sufficiently punished. The Lord punishes all these miscreants and He protects His devotees and re-establishes Dharma. So this happens periodically that is what is being explained in this verse. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Krishna's appearance, internal potency, conditioned soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MaInYq5698k?t=237
**Verse References:** BG 4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord appears in a particular family or place by his inconceivable potency."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing Krishna's transcendental appearance through his internal potency, contrasting it with the conditioned soul's birth through external energy.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, in the Bhagavad Gita, the personality of Godhead, Sri Krishna, has expressively given a description of his transcendental appearance, disappearance, and activities. Particularly in the fourth chapter, Krishna describes that even though he is unborn, he definitely does appear in this material world by his internal potency. All of us in this material world, we appear or we take birth by the force of the external energy, the illusory energy of Krishna. But Krishna appears by his internal potency, spiritual potency. So there is a very, very, very big difference between the two. There is a big difference between Krishna's appearance and our taking birth. There is a big difference. And therefore, his birth is specifically called appearance. And his, Krishna does not die. So it's called disappearance. When Krishna is out. When Krishna is out of our vision, then it is described as disappearance, and both of them are transcendental, and even his activities are completely transcendental, completely spiritual. Janma Karma Chame Divyam Evam Yo Vetthi Tatvatah. This is described by Krishna in the fourth chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. That is being cited by Srila Prabhupada. He will show us this story in this purport. Further, Srila Prabhupada says, the Lord appears in a particular family or place by his inconceivable potency. That is his internal potency, his spiritual potency, his personal potency. And it is inconceivable how that internal energy is working. How that internal energy is working. He does not take his birth as a conditioned soul, quits his body and acts as another body. Conditioned soul, this has been used a few times earlier.

---
### conditioned soul, material energy, transcendental form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MaInYq5698k?t=391
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna never accepts any material body. Krishna never comes in contact with the material energy. This is the mystery, how he appears to take birth like another human being, but he is not taking birth."
**Summary:** The speaker defines 'conditioned soul' and contrasts it with Krishna's unchanging, transcendental form, emphasizing that Krishna never comes into contact with material energy.

Conditioned means completely under the control of the conditions of this material world, imposed by the external energy. And we are all spirit soul, but our spiritual nature is covered by this conditioning of the material nature. So we take birth after we quit one body. We are forced to accept another material. Krishna never accepts any material body. Krishna never comes in contact with the material energy. This is the mystery, how he appears to take birth like another human being, but he is not taking birth. Ajop is an avyayatma. His transcendental form never deteriorates. Our form, this form, it is constant. It is constantly changing. It is constantly changing. Krishna's form never changes. Krishna is always in his transcendental form, eternally. The same supreme personality of Godhead without any change in position, without any change in his qualities, without any change in his opulences. He always is the supreme lord, always is the owner, enjoyer, controller of all the opulences in existence. So that unique position of Krishna is unchanged. Therefore it is said here, he does not take his birth like a conditioned soul. His birth is like the appearance and disappearance of the sun. Srila Prabhupada is giving one example.

---
### analogy, sun, Krishna's appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MaInYq5698k?t=527
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, the Lord appears in this universe like the sun and again leaves our sight at another time. He exists at all times at every place."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of the sun's rising and setting to explain Krishna's appearance and disappearance, emphasizing that Krishna is always present, but becomes visible at certain times due to his causeless mercy.

The sun, which we can relate with, because we have experience of seeing the sun rise and set every single day. The sun arises from the eastern horizon, but that does not mean that the eastern horizon is the parent of the sun. Is it that the sun is being generated out of the eastern horizon? No. The sun exists. Even before it appears on the eastern horizon, the sun is existing. In its position, relative to our vision, the sun becomes visible in the eastern horizon at a certain time. And about 12 hours later, the sun sets on the western horizon. The sun is existent in every part of the solar system. But he becomes visible at a scheduled time. And so also becomes invisible at another scheduled time. Sunrise, sunset. Becomes visible at sunrise. And the sun becomes invisible at sunset. So this is a very good example to help us understand. Similarly, the Lord appears in this universe like the sun and again leaves our sight at another time. He exists at all times at every place. Krishna is present everywhere. And always He is present. But by His causeless mercy, when He appears before us, we take it for granted that He has taken His birth. Janma karma chame divyam. Anyone who can understand this truth in terms of the statements of revealed scriptures certainly becomes liberated just after quitting the present body. That is told by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita.

---
### liberation, knowledge, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MaInYq5698k?t=667
**Verse References:** BG 4.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But simply by knowing in truth about the Lord's transcendental births and activities, one can get liberation at once."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that understanding Krishna's transcendental birth and activities leads to liberation, contrasting it with the difficult path of knowledge and renunciation over many lifetimes.

Tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti. When quitting the body, the present body, one who understands the truth in terms of the statements of the shastras, the revealed scriptures, such a person does not take his birth again in this material world. Instead, returns to Krishna loka, Krishna's personal abode in the spiritual world. So liberation is obtainable after many births and after great endeavor in patience and perseverance in knowledge and renunciation. Generally, liberation is not easy. The scriptures describe liberation is after many, many, many births, millions of lifetimes. And that also, after great endeavor, what is that endeavor? In cultivating spiritual knowledge and developing material detachment. These two are very important factors for liberation. But simply by knowing in truth about the Lord's transcendental births and activities, one can get liberation at once. Therefore, the importance of hearing the glories of Krishna's appearance and activities from the Srimad Bhagavatam, from the Bhagavad Gita. Of course, Bhagavatam contains much more details about Krishna's appearance and activities in his various incarnations, especially in his incarnation as Krishna. This is what is explained in the Bhagavad Gita, so far whatever Prabhupada has said.

---
### ignorance, atheism, fortunate souls
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MaInYq5698k?t=794
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is simply by knowing the transcendental nature of birth and activities of Lord Krishna, one can get liberation easily."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by contrasting those in ignorance who see Krishna as an ordinary being with the fortunate souls who enjoyed Krishna's company, emphasizing the ease of liberation through understanding Krishna's transcendental nature.

But those who are in the darkness of ignorance conclude that the Lord's birth and activities in the material world are similar to those of ordinary living beings, atheists and materialistic persons. They think Krishna is just another human being, which is completely wrong. Such imperfect conclusions cannot give anyone liberation. So they are unable to get liberation by coming to such wrong conclusions. His birth, therefore, in the family of King Yadu as the son of King Vasudeva and his transfer into the family of Nanda Maharaj in the land of Mathura are all transcendental arrangements made by the internal potency of the Lord. The internal potency is working to make this happen. The fortunes of the Yadu dynasty and that of the inhabitants of the land of Mathura cannot be materially estimated. How very, very fortunate they are. It is simply by knowing the transcendental nature of birth and activities of Lord Krishna, one can get liberation easily. We can just imagine what is in store for those who actually enjoyed the company of the Lord in person as a family member or as a neighbor. Krishna appeared and there were so many fortunate souls who were accompanying Krishna in his Leela in Mathura, in the Yadu dynasty, in Vrindavan. Very, very, very fortunate. So there is no doubt about their great fortune and therefore the ladies of Hastinapur are glorifying the Yadu dynasty and the land of Mathura. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Dwaraka, Krishna's Descent, Transcendental Abode
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1GtmDOd5CGg?t=82
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When Krishna descends, Krishna brings with him his transcendental abode."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the glories of Dwaraka, emphasizing that it surpasses even the heavenly planets because Krishna descended there, bringing his transcendental abode and associates with him.

The meaning of this verse is, undoubtedly, it is wonderful that Dwaraka has defeated the glories of the heavenly planets and has enhanced the celebrity of the earth. The inhabitants of Dwaraka are always seeing the soul of all living beings, Krishna, in his loving feature. He glances at them and favors them with sweet smiles. So, this is another description by the ladies of Hastinapura about the good fortune of the residents of Dwaraka, that they are always able to see Krishna, because Krishna lives in Dwaraka, resides in Dwaraka. Glories of Dwaraka are many, many times greater than the glories of the heavenly planets. For somebody who is a resident of the earth, heavenly planets are many times superior. But, for residents of Dwaraka, even though they are on the earth planet, Dwaraka is not like any other ordinary place on the earth. No, it is a very special place, because Krishna has descended. So, when Krishna descends, Krishna brings with him his transcendental abode. This is the special thing about Krishna's appearance on this world. He brings with him his transcendental abode. His personal associates of the spiritual world come with him. His opulences are manifest when he comes here, so in so many ways his appearance is very, very special.

### Heavenly Planets, Demigods, Indra's Power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1GtmDOd5CGg?t=211
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Indra controls the rainfall on all the planets in the entire universe, so he is not a small person."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the heavenly planets and their inhabitants, the demigods, focusing on Indra's immense power to control rainfall throughout the universe, contrasting this knowledge with the limited understanding of modern science.

Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, the heavenly planets are inhabited by demigods like Indra, Chandra, Varuna, and Vayu, and the pious souls reach there after performance of many virtuous acts on earth. So modern day people in general do not know what the heavenly planets are. For galoka, they know there are many different planets which we see in the night sky, but they do not know the living conditions because they are misinformed by the so-called scientists that there is no life on other planets. That's totally wrong. There is life on every planet in the entire universe, and there are different grades of living beings with different kinds of bodies suitable for living in each of the planets. So the heavenly planets are inhabited by devatas, Indra, Chandra, Varuna. Now, Indra, what is his power? What is his position? Please understand, he controls the rainfall on all the planets in the entire universe, so he is not a small person. He's not comparable to somebody on this earth planet. No, he is in a very, very, very powerful position. In one of the pastimes of Krishna described in the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam, it is mentioned that Indra summoned samvarthaka clouds to pour down devastating rainfall in Vrindavan. So this is the power of Indra. He has the power to summon different clouds to pour rain in different parts of this earth planet or even the other planets. So there are a set of clouds called samvarthaka clouds. They are summoned by Indra to pour down devastating rainfall.

---
### Scriptural Knowledge, Heavenly Planets, Time and Enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1GtmDOd5CGg?t=375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "From the revealed scriptures we get so much of knowledge about the heavenly planets, about the still higher planetary systems, Brahma loka, and even the spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the limited knowledge of modern science with the extensive knowledge available in revealed scriptures about the heavenly planets, their time scales, and the enhanced facilities for enjoyment.

At the end of Brahma's day, the middle and lower planetary systems are submerged by the waters of devastation. These are all unknown to people of the modern age who are not taking information from the revealed scriptures. They limit their knowledge to whatever they study in schools and colleges and whatever little research of the so-called scientists and other philosophers reveals to them. Their knowledge is very, very poor. Poor fund of knowledge, Prabhupada says. But from the scriptures, revealed scriptures, we get so much of knowledge about the heavenly planets, about the still higher planetary systems, Brahma loka, and even the spiritual world. That is special thing about the revealed scriptures. Now, to reach these heavenly planets, you cannot force yourself by trying to manufacture one space capsule or some space vehicle. What is that? Lunar Explorer, Mars Explorer? No, you cannot go to those planets which are presented as revealed scriptures planets by those vehicles. It is only possible to reach there after performance of pious activities as described in the scriptures. There is a qualification required to go. Modern scientists agree that the timing arrangement in the higher planetary system is different from that of the earth time is related. So therefore, from the revealed scriptures we understand the duration of life on the heavenly planets is 10,000 years by our calculation. That's the duration of life of the inhabitants of the heavenly planets. How is that? Six months on earth is equal to one day on the heavenly planets and similar duration is the night. So if you consider one day and night on the heavenly planets, it amounts to one complete solar year on the earth. Facilities of enjoyment are also similarly enhanced and the beauty of the inhabitants is there.

### Krishna's Leela, Holy Places, Devotee's Experience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1GtmDOd5CGg?t=662
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these three places where Krishna performed his leela, his pastimes, are very, very, very special."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the significance of Mathura, Vrindavan, and Dwaraka as the places where Krishna performed his pastimes, emphasizing their perpetual sanctity and the devotees' ability to relish Krishna's presence even now.

Because they spread Democratic, nor do dairy. So Kṛṣṇa was born in Mathura, He was transferred to Vṛndāvana and after some 12 years or so in Vṛndāvana, He went back to Mathura and from Mathura he went to Dvārakā. So all these three places where Krishna performed his leela, his pastimes, are very, very, very special. They are more important than the most famous planets of this entire universe. These places are perpetually sanctified because whenever the Lord descends on earth, he displays his transcendental activities, particularly in these three places. Krishna chooses where he wants to perform his leela when he appears on this earth. These are the three places. They are perpetually the holy lands of the Lord, and the inhabitants still take advantage of the holy places, even though the Lord is now out of their sight. Even now there are devotees in Dwarka, in Mathura, in Vrindavan, who are relishing the Lord's pastimes by remembering his leelas, by meditating on his transcendental form, by hearing his glories from bona fide devotees like that. The Lord is the soul of all living beings, and he desires always to have, to be a part of the living beings in their swaroopa, in their constitutional position to participate in transcendental life, in his association. What is our actual position?

### Eternal Position, Relationship with Krishna, Spiritual World
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1GtmDOd5CGg?t=784
**Verse References:** BG 8.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our actual position is in relationship with Krishna, in his association."
**Summary:** The speaker defines our true position as being in relationship with Krishna, free from material defects, and emphasizes that going to Krishna's abode ensures no return to the material world, unlike the heavenly planets.

It's not the position we have now, or not the positions we aspire in our ordinary material life. No, that's not our actual position. Our actual position is in relationship with Krishna, in his association. It is a spiritual position, free from all the shackles of the world. Free from all these material defects, material inebriaties, material insufficiencies. So we have got what is called as our eternal position in relationship with Krishna called svaroopa, that's our original position, and in that position we are with Krishna transcendental pastimes and experiencing the highest bliss, eternal life full of knowledge, his attractive features are sweet, very, very, very nice and his sweet smiles go deep into the heart of everyone and once it is so done, the living being is admitted into the kingdom of God. Just one glance of Krishna with his sweet smile on anyone will attract that person to actually go to the kingdom of Krishna in the spiritual world. And having gone there, Bhagavad Gita says one never returns to this material world ever again. This is confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita. The heavenly planets may be very famous for offering better facilities of material enjoyment, but we learn that in the Bhagavad Gita, it is said, if one goes to the heavenly planets, one comes back to this earth planet after exhausting this pious credits. But once having gone to the abode of the Lord one never returns. That's the big difference between the heavenly planets and the abode of the Lord. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### devotion, purification, penance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/atipl2O96aE?t=52
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.28
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lot of tapasya they have done to get that position."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the glorification of Krishna's wives and the gopis of Vrindavan, emphasizing the severe penances they underwent to attain their intimate relationship with Krishna.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 28th verse of the 10th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, "O friends, just think of his wives whose hands he had to bear to marry her. How they must have undergone vows, baths, fire sacrifices and perfect worship of the Lord of the universe to constantly relish now the nectar from his lips by kissing." The damsels of Raja Bhoomi would often faint just by expecting such favors. So among the inhabitants of Dwara, particularly the ladies of Hastinapur are glorifying the wives of Krishna and the gopis of Vrindavan. They are very intimately associated with Krishna. They are the intimate devotees of Krishna. And they are being described here that they do not get that position simply by desiring to have that position. They did not get that position simply by desiring to have that position, no. They must have undergone vows, baths, fire sacrifices and perfect worship of Krishna who is the Lord of the universe. Lot of tapasya they have done to get that position.

---
### purification, devotional service, karma vs bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/atipl2O96aE?t=174
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is a lot of difference between bhakti and karma. Bhakti is completely spiritual. Karma is material."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's purport is discussed, explaining that religious rites purify mundane qualities, leading to transcendental service to Krishna. Bhakti is distinguished from karma, emphasizing that devotional service requires purification through scriptural processes.

Now Sri La Pravapada explains in the purport. Religious rites prescribed in the scriptures are meant to purify the mundane qualities of the conditioned souls. Mundane qualities means material qualities. And these material qualities are to be purified. Because we are spirit soul, we are spiritual by nature, we have spiritual qualities which are covered by material body, by material mind, covered with material qualities. So the religious rituals described in the scriptures are meant to purify the mundane qualities of the conditioned souls. By these mundane qualities, these material qualities. And what will happen if we get purified? We are gradually promoted to the stage of rendering transcendental service to the Supreme Lord Krishna. So this purification is necessary for elevating us gradually to the stage of being able to serve Krishna, to be able to recognize. Render devotional service to Krishna. So devotional service is not an ordinary activity. And not everybody can take to devotional service simply by desiring or deciding to do so. No. There is a lot of difference between bhakti and karma. Bhakti is completely spiritual. Karma is material. Karma may be performed by any human being. But bhakti is only for those who are purified by the right process described in the scriptures, the religious rituals for purification. And only then one can actually begin devotional service to Krishna. And only then one can actually begin devotional service to Krishna.

---
### swarupa, liberation, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/atipl2O96aE?t=320
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Liberation means renovation of this stage of Swarupa."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with Srila Prabhupada's explanation of Swarupa, the factual identity of the living being and its relationship with Krishna. True liberation is defined as the renovation of this Swarupa, not merely freedom from suffering or attainment of mystic powers. The five phases of loving service are introduced.

Further Srila Prabhupada says, "Attainment of this stage of pure spiritual life is the highest perfection." And this stage is called Swarupa. This was mentioned in the previous verse also by Prabhupada. Swarupa. The factual, what is Swarupa? The factual identity. Of the living being. Each one of us, we have got a spiritual identity. The spiritual identity is called Swarupa. It's our original position in our relationship with Krishna. In the spiritual world. In his association. It is a perfection. Highest perfection to be attained. Liberation means renovation of this stage of Swarupa. This is what Bhagavatam describes. Sometimes people think liberation means going to the heavenly planets. Sometimes they think liberation means merging into some void. Sometimes they think liberation means getting some mystic powers. But that is not liberation. Even though they may be desired by somebody who is suffering in this material world. As freedom from suffering through these methods is possible to some extent. But ultimately, real liberation is not just freedom from suffering. Of course, those who are really liberated are free from all suffering. But beyond that, they are established in their Swarupa, in their relationship, loving relationship with Krishna. In that, they are free from suffering. And in that perfect stage of Swarupa, the living being is established in five phases of loving service. Five phases of loving service. This is described in detail in the Bhakti Samrita Sindhu, which describes what is Bhakti? What is the kind of relationship in Bhakti? What are the different kinds of relationships? What is the relationship between the two? Five kinds of relationships, depending on the five kinds of service that one renders to Krishna. Shanta, Das, Chesav, Kya, Vatsalya, Madhurya.

---
### Krishna's perfection, devotee's love, conjugal love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/atipl2O96aE?t=475
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is a devotees loving service to Krishna that Krishna agrees to become the master of the loving servant the husband of the loving wife"
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is always perfect and full, not lacking anything. He becomes a master, friend, son, or husband to fulfill the intense love of his devotees. Two classes of devotees in conjugal love are mentioned: the queens of Dwaraka and the gopis of Vrindavan.

Of these five, the stage of Madhurya Rasa or the humor of conjugal love is one of the five and it is the topmost. The Lord is always perfect in himself. And thus he is perfect in himself. There is no hankering for himself. It is not that Krishna wants some enjoyment in association with the gopis or the queens, the most beautiful personalities, because he is lacking in any way. No, Krishna is never lacking. Krishna is always full in himself. Purna, he is completely full in himself. That is the most beautiful personality. He is the who's the greatest. and he's independent because he's full he's independent so he is not hankering as we the souls of this material world are always incomplete imperfect therefore we have a hankering we want so we are lacking therefore we want Krishna is never lacking never eternally he is full perfect completely full Krishna however in spite of being full in himself he becomes a master a friend a son or a husband to fulfill the intense love of the devotee concern it is a devotees loving service to Krishna that Krishna agrees to become the master of the loving servant the husband of the loving wife the son of the loving mother or loving father the friend of another loving friend end so Krishna agrees out of his causeless mercy out of his kindness so in this particular description of this words two classes of devotees of the Lord are mentioned in the stage of conjugal love the Queens and the Gopis in Vrindavan the Queens in Dwaraka and Gopis in Vrindavan two classes of devotees who are situated in Madhuriya-rasa relationship one is called Swakhiya and other is called Parakhiya this is very important to understand

The queens of Dwaraka had a Swakhiya relationship, while the gopis of Vrindavan had a Parakhiya relationship. Both underwent severe penances to attain their positions.

both of them are in conjugal love with Krishna but there is a difference the Queens at Dwaraka were Swakhiya relationship or duly married wives of Dwaraka. that means there is a relationship because of being married Krishna married 16,108 princesses and they became after marriage his Queens in Dwaraka whereas the damsels of Vraja the Gopis young Gopis the young friends of Lord were his friends in Vrindavan when he was unmarried when he was a boy the Lord stayed at Vrindavan till the age of 16 and his friendly relations with the neighboring girls were in terms of Parakhiya. Parakhiya means it is not a relationship of conjugal love conjugal love means love between opposite genders male and female. so this relationship is not with not due to marriage now these girls in Vrindavan the Gopis young Gopis as well as the Queens underwent severe penances by taking vows bathing and offering sacrifices in the fire as prescribed in the scriptures how did they reach that position of becoming the beloved of Krishna. as the queen or as the Gopis it is by undergoing severe penances that is being described in this verse. now the rights as they are not an end in themselves what is described in this Shastra as the tapasya or some rata or some yagna, some dana or some yagna.

### swarupa, transcendental love, material vs spiritual
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/atipl2O96aE?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should understand carefully that the kissing of the Lord, either by his wives or the gopis, is not a material act or a material quality. No."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that rituals are means to attain Swarupa, not ends in themselves. Jnana, karma, and siddhi ultimately lead to transcendental loving service. The kissing of the Lord by his wives or the gopis is not a material act but a transcendental position attained after severe penances.

All those things are not end in themselves, but they are means to attain the highest stage of Swaroopa, including the descriptions of different pious activities, different types of cultivation of knowledge, transcendental knowledge or even perfect knowledge. Perfection in mystic powers, getting siddhis. So, jnana, karma and siddhi, all these are not end in themselves. The ultimate perfection of these attainments is to reach the highest stage of Swaroopa. To render constitutional transcendentalism. To render transcendental loving service to the Lord. That is the goal. So, we should understand carefully that the kissing of the Lord, either by his wives or the gopis, is not a material act or a material quality. No. If it is material, then it is not a material act. If it is material, then it is not a material act. Then Shukadeva Goswami would never have even cared to mention it in the 10th canto of the Bhagavad Gita. Because Shukadeva Goswami is a very very strict brahmachari. And he is completely free from all material desires. So, he has no interest in any material activities. He is only interested in spiritual activities. Therefore, Shukadeva Goswami elaborately described Krishna's pastimes. Therefore, Shukadeva Goswami elaborately described Krishna's pastimes. Therefore, Shukadeva Goswami elaborately described Krishna's pastimes. And even Chaitanya Mahaprabhu participated in this subject matter, even though he was a very strict sannyasi. And even Chaitanya Mahaprabhu participated in this subject matter, even though he was a very strict sannyasi. So, therefore, we should understand this stage is attained only after severe penances of many many many lifetimes. And it is a transcendental position. It is not a material position. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Krishna's queens, Glories of Krishna

**Verse References:** SB 1.10.29

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse and explains that the ladies of Hastinapura are glorifying Krishna's children and queens in Dwarka.

In this session we will be discussing from the 29th verse of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is the children of these ladies are Pradyumna, Samba, etc. Ladies like Rukmini, Satyabhama and Jambavati were forcibly taken away by him from their Swayamvara ceremonies after he defeated many powerful kings headed by Shishupala and other ladies were also forcibly taken away by him after he killed Bhomasura and thousands of his assistants. All of these ladies are glorious. So the ladies of Hastinapura are glorifying the children of Krishna when he was in Dwarka.

### Swayamvara, Vedic culture, Kshatriya tradition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0zIoNyR_fc?t=130
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the Vedic culture generally the parents of the girl who is to be married will make the selection of the boy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the Swayamvara tradition in Vedic culture, where qualified daughters of powerful kings could choose their own husbands through open competition, contrasting it with the general practice of parents selecting the groom.

He was married to so many queens and even the queens they are very glorious because Krishna had to fight against very powerful Kshatriyas in order to take them away in Swayamvara ceremony. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, exceptionally qualified daughters of powerful kings were allowed to make a choice of their own bridegrooms in open competition and such ceremonies were called Swayamvara or selection of the bridegroom. In the Vedic culture generally the parents of the girl who is to be married will make the selection of the boy. The girls are not given a free hand in selecting their own. The boy whom they want to marry they can select on their own. But an exception is when the qualified daughter of a powerful king is to be married then she will marry the most competent or chivalrous Kshatriya by selecting one who is victorious in a competition. Just like even Lord Ramachandra he married the most powerful Kshatriya in Swayamvara ceremony. Janaka Maharaj had organized that the very very heavy and big bow that had to be strung after lifting it and nobody was able to do that except Lord Ramachandra. So therefore Sita was married to Lord Ramachandra. Similarly Draupadi also, she was married in the Swayamvara ceremony to Arjuna and later on to all the Pandavas like that even Krishna.

---
### Krishna's pastimes, Kshatriya spirit, Devotee protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0zIoNyR_fc?t=310
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For them, that chivalrous spirit is more important than marriage."
**Summary:** The speaker continues discussing Swayamvara, highlighting Krishna's defeat of Shishupala and the chivalrous spirit of Kshatriyas who were willing to lay down their lives in competition.

He defeated Shishupala and others when Lord Ramachandra were to be smoothed out. Rukmini had actually requested Krishna to actually take her away from being married to Shishupala. It was not exactly a swayamvara but Krishna had to fight against Shishupala and other kings to take away Rukmini. So, like this, this selection of bridegroom for exceptionally qualified daughters of powerful kings. Because this swayamvara was an open competition between the rival and valiant princes, such princes were invited by the father of the princes and usually there were regular fights between the invited princely order in a sporting spirit. So, generally Kshatriyas, they like to fight when challenged or when there is a competition and it is not done so much in an inimical spirit, not out of enmity, much out of a sporting spirit, much in a sporting spirit. But it so happened. So, sometimes the belligerent princes were killed in such marriage fighting and the victorious princes offered the trophy princes for whom so many princes died. The Kshatriyas are ready to lay down their lives. They don't hesitate. For them, that chivalrous spirit is more important than marriage. So, they are ready to lay down their lives for the sake of protecting their own life. So, that is Kshatriya.

Such Kshatriyas are very rarely seen in this Kali Yuga. Of course, we have many people who enlist themselves in the army or navy or air force, defense forces. And they are ready to lay down their lives for the sake of protection. So, they are ready to lay down their lives for the sake of protecting the country. There are people even now. But, by and large, the Kshatriyas who can actually follow the Vedic principles and rule over a state or a kingdom or a province or a country, that is not to be found now. Such Kshatriyas are not available now. Who are trained up. Trained up in the military science according to their aptitude. Such training doesn't happen. There is no such training. There is no such training available now.

### Krishna's power, Supreme Personality of Godhead, Maya's influence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/v0zIoNyR_fc?t=630
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, unlike the living entities, who bewildered by the material influence, lose their strength or power or knowledge or their opulence or wealth, and the living entities are totally helpless under the clutches of maya, that is not the position of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Krishna's unlimited power and distinguishes Him from ordinary living entities who are subject to the influence of Maya and lose their strength and knowledge.

Which is his, the unlimited power, which only belongs to the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna, he is never without his powers, he is always the same personality with all his powers, with all his opulences, with all his energies, with all his strength, knowledge, everything, complete, 100% complete, so, unlike the living entities, who bewildered by the material influence, lose their strength or power or knowledge or their opulence or wealth, and the living entities are totally helpless under the clutches of maya, that is not the position of the Supreme Lord, even though he may appear to be a son of another Kshatriya like Vasudeva, but he is not a person of this world, he is not a jiva, he is not a living entity like a spirit soul, he is a Supreme Personality, he is a Supreme Soul, he is always with all his powers, so, there is nothing surprising or astonishing in the descriptions which are given in the shastras about his prowess, by which he defeats. There are very very very powerful demons or powerful kings or powerful personalities or powerful devatas, after all these devatas or kings or demons, they are under the complete control of material nature, in this material world, they are completely controlled by material nature.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshuranmeelitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gurdhadhara Sreevasaadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet.
