### leadership, responsibility, enlightenment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aEnrjFjXt6U?t=53
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Yudhishtira after being enlightened by what was spoken by Bhishma Deva and Lord Sri Krishna the infallible engaged himself in matters of perfect knowledge because all his misgivings were eradicated thus he ruled over the earth and seas and was followed by his younger brothers."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Srimad Bhagavatam 1.10.3, explaining how Yudhishtira Maharaj, after being enlightened by Bhishma Deva and Lord Krishna, overcame his grief and ruled the kingdom with perfect knowledge and the assistance of his righteous brothers.

In this session we will be discussing from the 3rd verse of the 10th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Nishamya Bhishmoktam Athachyutoktam Pravritta Vijnana Vidhuta Vibhramaha Shashasa Gaam Indra Iwajitashrayaha Paridhyu Paantam Anujanu Vartitaha. The meaning of this verse is Maharaj Yudhishtira after being enlightened by what was spoken by Bhishma Deva and Lord Sri Krishna the infallible engaged himself in matters of perfect knowledge because all his misgivings were eradicated thus he ruled over the earth and seas and was followed by his younger brothers. So this verse Sruti Goswami is describing that just after the battle of Kurukshetra when Yudhishtira saw that such a huge massacre had taken place, 640 million Kshatriyas were killed in that battle, it is a world war. So Yudhishtira Maharaj thought for me to be enthroned as the emperor so many people had to be killed. He was very much mortified, he was aggrieved by the mass killing. So then he was enlightened by Bhishma Deva and also the Supreme Lord Krishna and when his misgivings were all gone then he engaged himself in matters of perfect knowledge. He properly understood what is his responsibility, how he has to rule the kingdom, how he has to guide the citizens, he ruled over the earth and the seas and was followed by his younger brothers. He had four younger brothers, all of them were righteous like him and all of them followed in his footsteps in perfectly assisting him in ruling the entire world.

---
### leadership, devotion, scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aEnrjFjXt6U?t=187
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything belongs to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's explanation, highlighting that Yudhishtira ruled not for personal enjoyment but as Krishna's representative, following scriptural directions, and understanding that everything belongs to Krishna.

So Srila Prabhupada explains elaborately in this purport the modern English law of primogeniture or the law of inheritance by the first born was also prevalent in those days when Maharaj Yudhishtira ruled the earth and the seas. In those days the king of Hastinapura which is now part of New Delhi was the emperor of the world including the seas up to the time of Maharaj Pariksit the grandson of Maharaj Yudhishtira. There was only one king, there was one flag, there was one religion until the time of Maharaj Pariksit at least as we have information from the records, historical records in the Puranas and the Tehasas. Maharaj Yudhishtira's younger brothers were acting as his ministers and commanders of state and there was full cooperation between the perfectly religious brothers of the king. So there was no fight for supremacy because primarily their devotees, they accept Krishna as the supreme lord, supreme controller and if Yudhishtira is sitting on the throne he is sitting as the representative of Krishna, he is ruling exactly according to the directions given in the shastras by Krishna himself and therefore there is no question of any one person enjoying as was mentioned in the first verse. Yudhishtira Maharaj could not have enjoyed freely with unrestricted consciousness, oh this kingdom I have won, now I can enjoy all the power and the wealth and the resources, natural resources, no that was not his mood. That is never the mood of a devotee, that is never the mood of a religious or righteous person. One should accept only what is necessary for his own subsistence understanding that everything belongs to Krishna. So nobody can claim proprietorship over what is actually rightfully belonging to the supreme personality of Godhead who is referred to as Isavasya in the Isha Upanishad, the first of the Upanishads. That Upanishad explains everything belongs to God, everything belongs to Krishna. So Yudhishtira Maharaj never thought oh this whole kingdom is mine, I can enjoy, no. Everything belongs to Krishna, as a representative Krishna I should rule according to the codes of direction given in Dharmashastras by Krishna. Scriptures are ultimately given by God, given by Krishna.

---
### knowledge, parampara, leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aEnrjFjXt6U?t=371
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna directly impregnated Vedic knowledge into the heart of Brahma and Brahma disseminated that knowledge to his sons and disciples and that way the knowledge is coming down in Parampara."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the origin of scriptures, stating they are given by God, Krishna, at the beginning of creation, imparted to Brahma, and disseminated through parampara. He compares Yudhishtira to King Indra, both ruling according to shastric principles as representatives of the Lord.

Nobody sits and manufactures scriptures as foolishly sometimes people think that Krishna is God, no. It is given by God at the beginning of creation, Tene Brahma Hrudaya Adikavaye Muhyantiya Tsuraya. In the beginning of creation the first born creature, Lord Brahma was instructed in the Vedic knowledge, he was impregnated with Vedic knowledge through the medium of his heart because in the beginning there was only Brahma. So Krishna directly impregnated Vedic knowledge into the heart of Brahma and Brahma disseminated that knowledge to his sons and disciples and that way the knowledge is coming down in Parampara. Maharaj Yudhishtira was the ideal king or representative of Lord Krishna to rule over the kingdom of earth and was comparable to King Indra, the representative ruler of the heavenly planets. Just as the Deva, just as the Devatas have Indra as their king who rules exactly according to the principles of the Shastra, same way Yudhishtira was ruling the kingdom of the earth as the representative of Lord Sri Krishna. Prabhupada explains the demigods like Indra, Chandra, Surya, Varuna and Vayu are representative kings of different planets to the universe. They are their own planets and they are rulers but they don't rule whimsically and they don't try to enjoy some material power or material wealth, no. Similarly Maharaj Yudhishtira was also one of them ruling over the kingdom of the earth.

---
### leadership, self-realization, democracy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aEnrjFjXt6U?t=495
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The goal is self realization in order to prepare oneself to return to the kingdom of God to enter into the kingdom of God."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Maharaj Yudhishtira with modern political leaders, emphasizing that Yudhishtira was enlightened and focused on the ultimate goal of self-realization and returning to the kingdom of God, a goal often missing in modern democratic systems.

Now an important point about Maharaj Yudhishtira, Srila Prabhupada says Maharaj Yudhishtira was not a typically unenlightened political leader of modern democracy. It is noteworthy that in the modern system of government, the democratic system of government, somebody who gathers enough votes such a person becomes the elected leader of the people but he is not enlightened in the scriptural directions how to rule the kingdom or how to rule the country because there is a ultimate goal of life that is to be pursued by all human beings under the able leadership of a king like Yudhishtira. What is that goal? The goal is self realization in order to prepare oneself to return to the kingdom of God to enter into the kingdom of God. That goal you can understand now is completely missing in the life of people, neither the elected leaders are aware of this goal nor the people themselves are able to ascertain this goal. They do make enquiries what is the goal of life but they think that life in this world is limited to the duration of time between birth and death. This is all the understanding they have, they don't have understanding of the spiritual reality that each living being is spirit soul and one is changing bodies life after life but this change of body is actually a botheration and this material existence is a place of suffering due to the very nature of material existence and one is meant to become self realized specially in the human form of life and prepare oneself to enter into the kingdom of God where there is no suffering, there is no death, there is no disease. You have heard this many times in earlier sessions that the kingdom of God is the ideal place of inhabitation for all of us and we should prepare ourselves to go. So that is being explained by Prabhupada.

---
### authority, scripture, leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aEnrjFjXt6U?t=668
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "See this is the way to learn from the proper source from the proper authority and when one becomes enlightened then one can properly execute the principles given in the scriptures and give benefit to people which is tangible."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of learning from proper authorities and following scriptural principles, contrasting this with the limitations of modern democratic leaders who lack kingly power and are bound by constitutions.

Maharaj Yudhishthira was instructed by Bhishma Deva and the infallible Lord also therefore he had full knowledge of everything in perfection. See this is the way to learn from the proper source from the proper authority and when one becomes enlightened then one can properly execute the principles given in the scriptures and give benefit to people which is tangible. Now Prabhupada says about the modern democratic system, the modern elected executive head of a state is just like a puppet because he has no kingly power even if he is enlightened like Maharaj Yudhishthira he cannot do anything out of his own good will due to his constitutional position. The powers of the elected leader are limited or defined by the constitution therefore there are so many states over the earth quarrelling because of ideological differences or other selfish motives. Somebody claims socialist system is best system, somebody claims communist system is best, somebody claims capitalist system is best and they keep on fighting. So this is not the way.

---
### authority, devotion, scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aEnrjFjXt6U?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira simply has to follow the perfect laws given in the authorized scriptures as instructed by the infallible lord."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that Yudhishtira followed the instructions of the infallible Lord and Bhishma Deva, adhering to perfect laws in the scriptures, and emphasizing that success in ruling comes from following perfect authority without personal motives or manufactured ideologies.

A king like Maharaj Yudhishthira has no ideology of his own. Yudhishthira is not telling I have got my way of ruling, no. He had but to follow the instructions of the infallible lord and the lord's representative and the authorized agent Bhishma Deva. Yudhishthira simply has to follow the perfect laws given in the authorized scriptures as instructed by the infallible lord. Infallible means the lord never falls down from his position, he is always the supreme lord, always full of knowledge, full of all opulences, Bhagawan, the supreme personality of Godhead and Bhishma Deva is the perfect representative of Krishna. Such a perfect representative instructed Yudhishthira in what is Dharma, how to rule, how to ensure that citizens well being is provided. See it is told in the shastras that the king should follow the great authority and the infallible lord without any personal motive and manufactured ideology. Nobody should manufacture their own laws or their own principles, they should follow the perfect authority then there is possibility of success in ruling the kingdom properly. I will stop here, thank you very much, Hare Krishna.

### devotion, Krishna's independence, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M-zxPx9dtoc?t=106
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only through devotion we can attract Krishna's attention."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that Krishna is self-satisfied and doesn't need anything, but he accepts the loving service of his devotees, attracted by their devotion, not by material achievements or skills.

...like Krishna is a henpecked husband, but actually Krishna is just reciprocating the devotional service of his devotees. That's all Krishna is doing because Krishna has no need for anyone's service. He is full in himself, Atma Rama. He is completely self-satisfied. So it is the devotees who, out of their own devotion to Krishna, their love for Krishna, they render service to him. And the mood of the devotees, pure devotees also, is that we simply want to please Krishna. Reciprocally, Krishna also accepts the service and in return for the service, Krishna wants to please his devotees. So the description here is that the queens, they always were rendering very, very nice service to Krishna in the palace. Now these queens were provided by Krishna with each queen one palace and so many thousands of servants to attend to every single need of the king and the queen, of Krishna and his queen. But in spite of that, the queens would personally serve Krishna. They would not depend on, they would not take the help of any of the other servants. Personally, they would tend to every need of Krishna. That is described in detail in the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam. And Krishna very kindly accepted their service simply because it was offered with devotion. So only through devotion we can attract Krishna's attention. We cannot attract Krishna's attention by any other way, not by knowledge, not by our skill, not by our perfection in some yoga practice, siddhis, mystic perfections. No.

### purification, devotional service, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M-zxPx9dtoc?t=331
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service itself is purifying."
**Summary:** This segment explains that devotees are purified souls, and even in the beginning stages of devotional service, the process itself is purifying. Mixed devotional service is accepted by Krishna only when offered through a pure devotee spiritual master.

So in the Purpose, Srila Prabhupada explains, the devotees of the Lord are purified souls. Devotee means completely purified of all material contamination. Of course, in the beginning stage, when we begin devotional service, in the neophyte stage, beginning stage, we may not be completely pure, we may be contaminated to whatever extent, but devotional service itself is purifying, itself is purifying. So we become purified as we keep on rendering devotional service according to rules and regulations, according to the principles of sadhana bhakti. But when we become completely purified, then our service comes to the level of pure devotional service. As long as there is contamination in the heart, our service is a mixed devotional service. It is not counted as pure devotional service. Nevertheless, Krishna accepts if such mixed devotional service is offered through a pure devotee spiritual master, only then Krishna accepts. If such mixed devotional service is offered through a pure devotee spiritual master, mixed devotional service is never accepted by Krishna if somebody tries to do that independently. Therefore...

...in the beginning, we have to approach a bona fide spiritual master and render service to Krishna, begin rendering or continue to render service to Krishna through the spiritual master. Only then Krishna will accept it. Krishna accepts anything only from his pure devotees. So through the pure devotee, we can offer to Krishna some food we might have cooked. Otherwise, Krishna is not hungry or Krishna is not in need of any food. But when the pure devotee recommends that here is a sincere soul wanting to offer you some service, and Krishna very kindly accepts the seva of the neophyte devotee, even though it is mixed devotional service, he accepts it through the medium of the bona fide spiritual master who is a pure devotee.

### devotional service, independence, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M-zxPx9dtoc?t=486
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti is never, never, never possible to be independently practiced."
**Summary:** This segment stresses that offering seva to Krishna in the mixed devotional stage is only possible through a pure devotee, and warns against trying to perform devotional service independently, contrasting it with other practices like knowledge cultivation or yoga.

Only through a pure devotee, we can offer our seva to Krishna in the mixed devotional stage, mixed devotional service stage. Otherwise, it is not possible. Therefore, don't try to do any service, devotional service independently. Independently, somebody may cultivate knowledge. Independently, somebody may practice. Independently, somebody may practice ashtanga yoga. Independently, somebody may try to perform some rituals. But that is impersonal kind of thing. You see, it doesn't depend on who the person is. They are just governed by some laws. That's all. Laws in this material world. And somebody may make progress also independently, trying to do something, cultivation of knowledge or perfecting their yoga practices, asana, pranayama, pratyahara, they may do independently. Though even in those aspects, it is recommended that one should have a guide so that you don't waste time making mistakes and trying to correct yourself through trial and error. But somebody is somehow desirous of independently practicing or trying out the practice itself. It may work, but not bhakti. Bhakti is never, never, never possible to be independently practiced, to make progress on the path of pure devotional service, it's never possible.

### spiritual master, guidance, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M-zxPx9dtoc?t=605
**Verse References:** BG 14.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as the devotees surrender unto the lotus feet of the Lord sincerely, the Lord accepts them and thus the devotees at once become free from all material contaminations."
**Summary:** This segment highlights the importance of remaining under the guidance of a pure devotee spiritual master to achieve perfection and return to Godhead, contrasting it with those who abandon such guidance and become lost. It also mentions that even the most sinful person can achieve perfection through devotional service under proper guidance.

Therefore, we see people who take to devotional service, but give up the shelter, the guidance, the teachings of the pure devotee spiritual master, they are lost in this material world. They cannot achieve perfection. Never. Perfection is never achieved that way. Whereas those who are carefully under the guidance of the pure devotee spiritual master and strictly follow his instructions without even one millimeter of deviation, they are the ones who make rapid progress. And Srila Prabhupada says, in this lifetime you can go back to Godhead. You can perfect yourself in this lifetime, go back to Godhead, irrespective of what your background had been before taking to devotional service. Even the most sinful person among sinners can take to devotional service under the guidance of a pure devotee spiritual master and perfect himself very quickly. So therefore, Prabhupada says, as soon as the devotees surrender unto the lotus feet of the Lord sincerely, the Lord accepts them and thus the devotees at once become free from all material contaminations. Such devotees are above the three modes of material nature. They are not on the platform of the three gunas. Krishna says that in Bhagavad Gita: mam chayo avyabhicharena bhakti-yogena sevate sagunan samatityetan brahma bhuyaya kalpate. One comes to the spiritual platform as soon as one renders pure devotional service to Krishna...

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M-zxPx9dtoc?t=733

**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of the Ganges river to illustrate that devotional service purifies regardless of one's background, just as the Ganges maintains its spiritual potency even when mixed with drain water.

...then such a service is above the three gunas. So therefore, Prabhupada says, there is no bodily disqualification of a devotee, whether he is from a Brahminical background, or a Kshatriya background, or a Vaishya background, or a Shudra background, or a Chandala background, or a sinful background, antyaja, it doesn't matter when it comes to pure devotional service. Just as the example is given by Srila Prabhupada, there is no qualitative difference between the Ganges water and the Fildi drain water when they are amalgamated. Now the Ganga river is flowing. When it comes from the Gangotri Himalayas to Haridwar, it is crystal clear water. But as it flows down the Gangetic plains through different towns, villages, cities, till it joins the Bay of Bengal, so many drain water may join the Ganga river flow. But that doesn't mean after the drain water is joining the Ganga, Ganga does not lose any potency, spiritual potency. Ganga does not lose any spiritual potency. And even it is proven that, let us say, a person who is infected with some disease, he's got germs of the disease on his body, and such a person goes and takes bath in the Ganga river, then, immediately if you take that water, where the person has taken a bath, that water will actually have those germs. But within three hours of taking that water and keeping it aside, that water, Ganga Jal, which had those germs, all those germs will be destroyed. This has been verified by medical scientists back in the British era. That these Hindus, they claim Ganga water is very pure always. How is it possible? So they have proven that actually. It's a fact. But we are more interested in understanding that the spiritual potency of Ganga is not reduced because some drain water is mixing with the river Ganga when it flows in different parts of this, North India. Similarly...

### Krishna's Protection, Devotion, Affection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/64JQqiJnuKw?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.31, SB 1.10.32
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira Maharaj did this because of the enemy and also out of affection for the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces and explains verses 31 and 32 of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the ladies of Hastinapura greeting Krishna and Yudhishthira's arrangements for Krishna's protection out of affection.

In this session we will be discussing from verses 31 to 32 of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. So the 31st verse, the meaning of this verse is: While the ladies of the capital of Hastinapura were greeting him and talking in this way, the Lord smiling accepted their good greetings and casting the grace of his glance over them, he departed from the city. So the past few verses we heard the glorification of Krishna by the ladies of Hastinapura in their talking amongst themselves when they had gone to the rooftop to bid farewell to Krishna. So it is explained here Krishna accepted their good greetings and he cast his merciful glance on them that way blessing them and he departed from the city. The 32nd verse: Ajatashatrupritanam gopi thaya madhudvishah Parabhyah shankitathnehat prayum tacctur anginim. The meaning of this verse is: Maharaj Yudhishthira, although no one's enemy, engaged four divisions of defense horse elephant chariot and army to accompany Lord Krishna the enemy of the asuras or demons. The Maharaj Yudhishthira did this because of the enemy and also out of affection for the Lord.

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Yudhishthira's motivations and the composition of the defense forces, then discusses ancient warfare, including the training of horses and elephants and the power of arrows.

So Yudhishthira Maharaj, he is sending off Krishna and even though there is no need for giving any special protection to Krishna, Yudhishthira knows Krishna is supreme personality of God but out of affection for him, he arranged four divisions of defense forces. Those days there used to be warriors on horseback, warriors on elephant back, warriors on chariots and warriors on foot, foot soldiers. So four divisions, horse, elephant, chariot and army, what is called? Horse infantry. They were to accompany Krishna till he reached Dwaraka. So it is explained that Yudhishthira Maharaj did this out of affection for Lord Krishna because Yudhishthira Maharaj is a pure devotee of Krishna. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, Natural defensive measures are horses and elephants combined with chariots, horses and men, horses and elephants are trained to move to any part of the hills or forests and plains. These horses and elephants are trained up to engage in fighting in a battle by the warriors or Kshatriyas. The charioteers could fight with many horses and elephants by the strength of powerful arrows even up to the standard of the Brahmastra similar to modern atomic weapons.

### Krishna's Pastimes, Devotee Relationship, Transcendental Bliss
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/64JQqiJnuKw?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord plays the part of a subordinate in his transcendental sporting."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of Krishna accepting Yudhishthira's arrangements and Krishna's childhood pastimes with Yashoda to illustrate how the Lord plays different roles to reciprocate with his devotees and provide them with transcendental bliss.

There are different形 Grinville kusama will take it as a demoniac because he could not disobey the king who was his elder cousin. Krishna when he plays the part of being a relative of a devotee, he plays the role perfectly. So he was a younger cousin of Yudhishthira. So therefore, Krishna played that part, I am younger than Yudhishthira, I cannot refuse. That is the etiquette. So I cannot refuse when Yudhishthira arranges bodyguards for my travel, I cannot refuse it. So Prabhupada says, the Lord plays the part of a subordinate in his transcendental sporting. And thus sometimes he puts himself in the care of Yashoda Mata for his protection in his so-called helplessness of childhood. Small child is after all helpless, totally dependent on the mother. When it is just born, what is the condition of the child? The infant, we do not even say child, we say infant, very, very, very small, just come out of the mother's womb, totally helpless. So we should not mistakenly think that Krishna is helpless when he is a small child. He puts himself under the care of his mother Yashoda or Devaki as if he is a small child. But actually he remains the Supreme Lord with all his powers, even as a small child. So therefore Prabhupada explains that is the transcendental lela or pastime of the lord. Simply to give pleasure to his devotees, Krishna enacts these pastimes. The basic principle for all transcendental exchanges between the lord and his devotees, is exhibited to enjoy a transcendental bliss. So, Krishna keeps enjoying these transcendental pleasures, Krishna is full of transcendental blessings. transcendental bliss and there is no comparison with this bliss even to the level of Brahmananda. Krishna shares that transcendental bliss with his devotees when he engages in transcendental pastimes with his devotees. So both Krishna and the devotees are always in transcendental bliss.

### Separation, Devotion, Constant Remembrance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/64JQqiJnuKw?t=733

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces verse 33 and explains how the Pandavas, being pure devotees, accompanied Krishna a considerable distance due to their deep affection and inability to bear separation, highlighting the importance of constant remembrance of Krishna.

The thirty-third verse, atha duragataan shaurihi, kauravaan viraha aturan, sannivartya drdham snigdhan, praya svanagarim priyaihi. The meaning is out of profound affection for Lord Krishna, the Pandavas who were of the Kuru dynasty accompanied him a considerable distance to see him off. They were overwhelmed with the thought of Krishna. The Lord, however, persuaded them to return home and he proceeded towards Dwaraka with his dear companions. Yudhishthira Maharaj has made arrangements for bodyguards, four divisions of defense forces to accompany Krishna till Dwaraka. But even the Pandavas themselves, Yudhishthira, Bhima, Arjuna, Manakula, Sahadeva also could not think of separation from Krishna because they are pure devotees. All pure devotees are so. They are very intimately connected with Krishna and therefore continuously thinking of Krishna only. Continuously. Such continuous thinking of Krishna is a commandment of qualities of special prestige. Krishna is also the развит 좋은 is a characteristic of devotional service to krishna bhakti for krishna.

### historical context, geography, Krishna's travels

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the description is Krishna has started from Hastināpura and he is going to proceed towards Dvārakā crossing the different parts of the land in between Hastināpura and Dvārakā."
**Summary:** Suta Goswami describes Krishna's journey from Hastināpura to Dvārakā, mentioning the various provinces he traversed.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from verses 34 to 36, the last three verses of this tenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: 34th verse. Kurujaangala Paanchalaan Shurasenaan Sayamunaan Brahmaavartam Kurukshetram Matsyaan Saraswataan Athah Marudhanvam Atikratham Saviraviravirayoparan Anartanbhardavo Paagat Shantavaho Managvibhu. This is actually the 34th and the 35th verses. Together they have been given here, of this tenth chapter of the first canto. So the translation is given together. This is spoken by Suta Goswami when he is narrating these past times to the sages of Naimisharanya, headed by Shounaka, and addressing who is the representative of all the sages in Naimisharanya. O ShTomaka the lord proceeded towards Kuru Jangla, Panchala, Shurasena, the land on the bank of the river Yamuna, Brahmavarta, Kurukshetra, Matsya, Saraswata, the province of the desert and the land of scanty water. After crossing these provinces he gradually reached the Savveera and Abheera provinces, then west of these reached Dvārakā at last. So the description is Krishna has started from Hastināpura and he is going to proceed towards Dvārakā crossing the different parts of the land in between Hastināpura and Dvārakā.

### historical accuracy, Bhagavatam, modern research
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eN7YuPC5xdY?t=172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They are real happenings. Just because it does not fit with our logical thinking, we should not reject them as mythology or something unacceptable, unbelievable."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that the events described in the Bhagavatam are historical incidents, not imaginary stories, and challenges modern researchers' theories about the desert's recent development.

Śrīla Prabhupāda explains in the purport about this different lands being described in these two verses. The provinces passed over by the Lord in those days were differently named, but the direction given is sufficient to indicate that he travelled through Delhi, Punjab, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Saurashtra, and Gujarat and at last reached his home province at Dvārakā. So Prabhupāda is trying to explain these are all historical incidents and they are not imaginary stories described in the Bhāgavatam, the Pūrāṇas, the itihāsas. They are not imaginary fiction. They are not fiction. They are real happenings. Just because it does not fit with our logical thinking, we should not reject them as mythology or something unacceptable, unbelievable. It may appear unbelievable but actually it is a fact. We do not gain any profit simply by researching the analogous provinces of those days up to now. But it appears that the desert of Rajasthan and the provinces of scanty water like Madhya Pradesh were present even 5000 years ago. Some characteristics of certain land are the same or were the same 5000 years back as is indicated by the statements in the Bhāgavatam. So these are factual statements. These are not some imaginary things. The theory of soil experts that the desert developed in recent years is not supported by the statements of the Bhagavatam. Some speculation modern researchers indulge in because they have limited resources. They have limited brains and they have limited access to knowledge, so they speculate, they theorize that deserts developed in recent years. It is not there in ancient times proposes no even five thousand years back as given in the bhagavatam the the desert portion and the the the part of the land where there was scanty water existed 5,000 years back Krishna passed through that while proceeding from Hastinapura to Dwaraka.

### Vedic culture, karma, Krishna's actions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eN7YuPC5xdY?t=524
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is never under the laws of karma. Krishna is the master of all the laws of material nature."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna, though acting as a Kshatriya and following Vedic injunctions, is not bound by karma, unlike ordinary beings.

The meaning of this verse is, on his journey through these provinces, he was welcomed, worshipped and given various presentations. In the evening in all places, the Lord suspended his journey to perform evening rites. This was regularly observed after sunset. If you remember, the Lord is playing the part of a Kshatriya. So they belong to the Vedic culture. So they follow certain practices. At least the Brahmanal Kshatriyas and Vaishyas in the Vedic culture, they follow certain practices as described in the, prescribed in the Shastras, in the Vedic scriptures. So Prabhupada says in the purport, it is said here that the Lord observed the religious principles regularly while he was on the journey. There are certain philosophical speculations that even the Lord is under the obligations of fruitive action. Karma. Some people say Krishna is under the laws of karma. That is wrong. Krishna is never under the laws of karma. But actually this is not the case. Krishna does not follow the laws of karma. Krishna does not depend on the action of any good or bad work. Since the Lord is absolute, everything done by him is good for everyone. Unlike us, we do some action, it is judged according to the laws of karma as pious or impious. Pious actions have got pious reactions, good reactions, and impious activities, sinful activities have got sinful reactions. So we accordingly either enjoy or suffer the results of our own activities which are under the laws of karma. Krishna is never under the laws of karma. Krishna is the master of all the laws of material nature.

---
### Vedic injunctions, imitation, example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eN7YuPC5xdY?t=673
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna also teaches by his own example how to follow the Vedic injunctions."
**Summary:** Krishna acts to protect devotees and annihilate the impious, teaching by example how to follow Vedic injunctions, but not all his actions are meant for imitation.

He is the master of material nature. He is the supreme controller of material nature. So he is never under the control. But still it is explained here, when Krishna descends on earth, he acts for the protection of the devotees and for the annihilation of the impious non-devotees. Although he has no obligatory duty, still he does everything so that others may follow. Krishna also teaches by his own example how to follow the Vedic injunctions. So if he plays the part of a Kshatriya, he follows the Vedic injunctions which are prescribed for a Kshatriya. That is the way, a factual teaching. One must act properly himself and teach the others how to follow the Vedic injunctions by his own actions. Otherwise no one will accept one's blind teaching. So Krishna himself is the awarder of fructive results. He is self-sufficient. Yet he acts according to the rulings of the revealed scripture in order to teach us the process of following the Vedas. If he does not do so, the common man may go wrong. But a warning is given here. In the advanced stage, when one can understand the transcendental nature of Lord Krishna, one does not try to imitate him. This is not possible. So every action of Krishna is not meant for teaching us. Some actions are meant for teaching us. As Prabhupada explains in further. The Lord in human society does what is the duty of everyone. But sometimes he does something extraordinary and not to be imitated by the living being. His acts of evening prayer as stated herein must be followed by the living beings. But it is not possible to follow his mountain lifting or dancing with the gopis. So what we do? So what we should follow and what we should not imitate. This distinction must be there when we hear about or read or try to examine the activities performed by Krishna in his entire manifestation on this earth.

---
### imitation, spiritual master, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eN7YuPC5xdY?t=820
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, one should be guided by the bona fide spiritual master to distinguish between what we should follow among the activities of Krishna and which actions of Krishna are not to be imitated."
**Summary:** The speaker uses analogies of the sun and Shiva to illustrate the dangers of imitating powerful figures and emphasizes the need for guidance from a bona fide spiritual master to discern which of Krishna's actions to follow.

One cannot imitate the sun which can exhaust water even from a filthy place. Example again is given of the sun. The sun is so powerful. Even the sun evaporates some filthy water from a dirty place. The sun doesn't become polluted. Ludicrous to think that sun will become contaminated. No. So the principle is the most powerful can do something which is all good. But our imitation of such acts will put us into endless difficulty. So we should not imitate the most powerful. The most powerful personalities, the controllers, what they do. Shiva drank an ocean of poison. But Shiva's followers try to imitate Shiva and they get into so much of problems. So they say Shiva smokes ganja so we can also smoke ganja. But Shiva smokes ganja for a different purpose. He has no attraction or addiction. He has no desire to enjoy smoking ganja. He does that just to give association for living entities who are in the mode of ignorance, Tamsic living entities, bhutas, pretas, pishachas, etc. Like that, Krishna performs some activities to protect his devotees, mountain lifting, govardhaniya. But we cannot imitate. We should never try to imitate that. Therefore, one should be guided by the bona fide spiritual master. Therefore, one should be guided by the bona fide spiritual master. Therefore, one should be guided by the bona fide spiritual master to distinguish between what we should follow among the activities of Krishna and which actions of Krishna are not to be imitated, the spiritual master should be the guide for all such practices of our own, our own following the practice of bhakti. We should be guided by the spiritual master. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna. Thank you very much.

Should not a king or executive head protect the lives of the poor animals who are unable to defend themselves? Is this humanity? Are not the animals of a country citizens also? Praja, the word Praja is used. Praja means those who take birth in a particular land. So, even the animals they also require protection. They also require protection. Why are the innocent animals allowed to be butchered in organized slaughterhouses? **Summary:** The speaker explains that Maharaj Yudhishthira ruled the entire world, emphasizing Krishna as the seed-giving father and material nature as the mother of all living beings, stressing the right to live and the abundance provided when we don't interfere with Krishna's arrangement.

Because Maharaj Yudhishthira was perfectly ruling as the king of the entire world. Maharaj Yudhishthira was just the ruler of the entire world. He rules the всего, and he rules the world. We are all creatures of material city, but we have to be aware of the fact that we are not the same kind of people. We are all creatures of material nature. In the Bhagavad Gita, it is said that Lord himself is the seed-giving father and material nature is the mother of all living beings on all shapes. Sarva yonishu kaunteya murtaya sambhavanti yaha tasam brahma maha dyonir aham bija pradapita. Krishna says, I am the seed-giving father and material nature is the source of birth for all living beings. Mother nature. So this is the way the world is created. So Krishna is the father. Mother nature is the mother of all creatures. Everyone has a right to live. We should respect their right to live under the protection of the Supreme Lord as father and material nature as mother in this material world. Thus, mother, material nature is enough foodstuff for both animals and for men by the grace of the Almighty Father Sri Krishna. We don't have to produce anything. We simply should not interfere with Krishna's arrangement. That's all. Then there'd be plenty for everyone, animals and humans. Everyone will have plenty.

### Taxation, Governance, Prosperity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gkVcCFwxs90?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This verse is talking about rivers paying their tax quota to the king and they were paying in profusion, plenty, there was plenty of water in the rivers of all kinds of valuables in the ocean in the hills in the mountains."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the initial verses of the chapter, focusing on King Yudhishthira's rule and the concept of nature providing taxes in abundance, highlighting the prosperity of the kingdom.

The departure of Lord Krishna for Dwarka is described before actually describing the departure in this chapter. It is described the rule of King Yudhishthira after Yudhishthira assumed the post of the emperor of the world after the Battle of Kurukshetra. Then how was he ruling, that's being described here in the initial portion of this chapter. The fifth verse says, 'Nadia samadra giraya.' The meaning of this verse is religious oceans, hills, mountains, and falls of the oceans, mountains, and falls of the mountains, and falls of the mountains, forest creepers, and active drugs in every season paid their tax quota to the king in profusion. This is an interesting concept here. This verse is talking about rivers paying their tax quota to the king and they were paying in profusion, plenty. There was plenty of water in the rivers of all kinds of valuables in the ocean in the hills in the mountains. That's being explained in the purport by Srila Prabhupada.

### Varnashrama, Social Order, Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gkVcCFwxs90?t=146
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The shudras are never given money, they are given all the necessities, they are taken care of by their masters because they always have to be under the protection of a brahmana and a shudra, a kshatriya or a vaisya."
**Summary:** This segment explains the varnashrama system, focusing on the roles and responsibilities of different classes, particularly the vaisyas who pay taxes, and the shudras who are provided for but not given money due to a perceived lack of developed intelligence.

So it's not just the citizens alone who pay the tax and even among the citizens in the Vedic culture only the vaisyas pay tax. The kshatriyas are rulers and the brahmanas they voluntarily accept to lead a very simple life. They don't accumulate money, they don't even accumulate goods and the shudras are never given money, they are given all the necessities, they are taken care of by their masters because they always have to be under the protection of a brahmana and a shudra, a kshatriya or a vaisya. So they're taken care completely. They are never given money because there is a possibility that they may misuse the money for wrong purposes. They don't have sufficiently developed intelligence to understand the consequences of spending money for let's say smoking, drinking, gambling, etc. Whereas what about the vices who have money in their hands or the kshatriyas? The vices and kshatriyas are responsible. They are sufficiently engaged in their duties. They are very strictly, there are strict followers of the Vedic injunctions. So they are not only responsible but they are also responsible for the.

### Divine Protection, Success, Supreme Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gkVcCFwxs90?t=371
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The secret to success is to take refuge under the protection of the supreme lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the king receives gifts of nature due to ruling according to dharma and being under the protection of Krishna (Ajita). It emphasizes that the secret to success is taking refuge in the Supreme Lord, whose sanction is necessary for all endeavors, contrasting this with modern economic development relying solely on tools and machinery.

The king would also receive the gifts of nature in profusion because he was ruling according to the principles of dharma that will be explained here in the parpo. Shila Prabhupada says, since Maharaj Yudhishthira was under the protection of the Ajita. Ajita is another name for Krishna, the infallible Lord, as above mentioned. The properties of the Lord, namely the rivers, the oceans, the hills, the forests, etc. were all pleased and they used to supply the respective quota of taxes to the king. Whose property is it? Rivers, oceans, hills, etc. They are the property of the supreme Lord. So Shila Prabhupada says, the secret to success is to take refuge under the protection of the supreme Lord. That is the secret of success for the king to properly rule the kingdom without experiencing any scarcity, without the supreme Lord's success, without the supreme Lord's sanction, nothing can be possible. Ultimately for every single thing the supreme Lord's sanction is absolutely required to make economic development by our own endeavors on the strength of tools and machinery is not all like it is happening in the modern times. The sanction of the supreme Lord must be there otherwise despite all instrumental arrangements everything will be unsuccessful. The ultimate cause of success is daiva the supreme.

### Daiva, Action, Super Soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gkVcCFwxs90?t=483
**Verse References:** BG 18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without his sanction, no action can be successfully accomplished."
**Summary:** This segment references the Bhagavad Gita, explaining that Krishna outlines five factors for successful action, with 'daivam' (the Supersoul) being the ultimate cause of success. While proper endeavor, instruments, place, and the doer's effort are important, the Lord's sanction is crucial, a factor often overlooked by non-devotees.

In the 18th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna explains five factors which are to be considered for successful accomplishment of every action in this material world. The four factors are in our hands when we do some action. It's in our hands, four factors, but the fifth factor is called daivam and daivam means the super soul, the personality of God, Krishna satan in our heart is paramatma. Ultimately the cause of success is daiva or the supreme Lord Krishna as paramatma in the heart. Without his sanction, no action can be successfully accomplished. Please remember this always, always the most important factor. Of course that does not mean that we neglect the other four factors which are in our hands, like they should be proper endeavor, they should be the proper instruments to be used, they should be the proper place or conditions for doing that action. There should be the effort on the part of the particular person, the doer. A doer is required for the accomplishment of any action. Nothing happens automatically without an effort or endeavor by a doer or a group of doers, a team effort under the guidance of a leader and without the proper instruments of action, no action can be accomplished. But this is not all. These four factors, daiva is very, very important factor and that generally people who are other non devotees, they do not know or they don't care for this fifth factor, most important factor, daiva.

---
### Welfare State, God Consciousness, Godly Communism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gkVcCFwxs90?t=616
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually the state belongs to the supreme lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the king's role as an agent of the Supreme Lord, emphasizing the concept of a welfare state. It highlights that the state and its resources belong to Krishna, and living beings are allowed to use them for His service. It contrasts this with the modern slogan of 'everything is for the people' and advocates for a return to God consciousness and 'godly communism' for the perfection of human life.

Kings like Maharaj Yudhishthira knew perfectly well that the king is the agent of the supreme Lord to look after the welfare of the mass of people. Here the concept of welfare state is coming. The king is ruling as the representative of Krishna. Ultimately Krishna is the supreme ruler, sarva loka maheshwaram. He is the supreme Lord who is the controller of all planets, all persons, all demigods, everyone. So if there is a king who is ruling a particular province or a state or a kingdom or a planet, that king should understand his position as an agent of the supreme Lord Krishna only. Actually the state belongs to the supreme Lord. The rivers, oceans, forests, hills, drugs, etc. are not creations of man. They are all creations of the supreme Lord and the living being is allowed to make use of the property of the Lord for the service of the Lord. Please understand this point. All these facilities exist for one purpose, that is human beings should utilize all the natural resources which are the property of the Lord and is provided for what, for service of the Lord. Today's slogan is that everything is for the people and therefore the government is for the people and by the people. But to produce a new species of humanity at the present moment on the basis of God consciousness and perfection of human life, the ideology of God, of godly communism, the world has to again follow in the footsteps of kings like Maharaj Yudhishtira or Parikshit Maharaj.

### Natural Resources, Godless Civilization, God Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gkVcCFwxs90?t=736
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply we have to become god conscious, that's all, nothing else is required."
**Summary:** This segment asserts that there is plenty of everything by the Lord's will and that proper use of natural resources can lead to a comfortable life without enmity. It contrasts this with modern, godless civilization, which focuses on artificial happiness through factories and tools, benefiting only a few at the expense of many and diverting attention from God realization. The solution is to become God conscious to get every necessity in plenty.

There is enough of everything by the will of the Lord, by the will of Krishna. There is plenty of all natural resources and we can make proper use of the things to live comfortably, both enmity between men or between animal and man or between man and nature. We can live comfortably, but we should understand the secret is that the control of the Lord is there everywhere and if the Lord is pleased, every part of nature will be pleased. The river will flow profusely to fertilize the land. The ocean will supply sufficient quantity of minerals, pearls, and jewels. This is practically unknown to modern man. What is the use of the ocean? The forest will supply wood, drugs, and vegetables and the seasonal changes will effectively help produce fruits and flowers in profuse quantity. There will be no scarcity for anyone of any of the basic needs. There will be plenty. On the other hand, Prabhupada is commenting about the modern civilization, a godless civilization, the artificial way of living which is the case in the modern civilization, depending on factories and tools can render so-called happiness only to a limited number at the cost of millions. See what is happening today. They are producing in plenty mobile phones and electronic goods because they somehow figured out a method of mass producing these at a very cheap price and selling them in the millions to the people and diverting their attention from the main business of human life which is God realization or service to Krishna and thereby people suffer. So to actually get every necessity in plenty, simply we have to become God conscious, that's all, nothing else is required. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### invocation, verse explanation, miseries of material existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CK4hyhTUISs?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because of the kings having no enemy, the living beings were not at any time disturbed by mental agonies, diseases or excessive heat or cold."
**Summary:** The speaker begins with invocations and then explains Srimad Bhagavatam verse 1.10.6, focusing on how the absence of enemies leads to freedom from various miseries.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananj Nishalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Dasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Prishtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunya Vadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhigaura Bhakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session, we will be discussing the 6th verse of the 10th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Nadhayo Vyadhaya Klesha Daiva Bhutatma Hetavaha Ajatashatra Vabhavan Jantu Unam Ragni Karhi Chit. The meaning of this verse is: Because of the kings having no enemy, the living beings were not at any time disturbed by mental agonies, diseases, or excessive heat or cold. This is a very interesting verse that our existence in this material world is always disturbed by three kinds of miseries.

---
### threefold miseries, Daiva, Bhuta, Atma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CK4hyhTUISs?t=86
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As spirit soul, we are actually free from all these three, but when we are covered by this body, we have to suffer."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the three types of miseries (Daiva, Bhuta, and Atma) that disturb our existence in the material world, explaining their sources and effects.

These are imposed by material nature upon all of us. So they are described here in this verse as Daiva. The disturbances or the miseries imposed by supernatural powers, the Devatas, who control the wind, who control the rain, who control the heat, the sunshine, etc. So we suffer due to excessive heat or excessive cold or excessive rain, or excessive wind, cyclone, tornado, hurricane, or earthquakes. Of course, epidemics doesn't come under Daiva. Earthquakes come under that. Then Bhuta, due to other living entities, enemies or mosquitoes, or some other living beings, some wild animals or some pests like that. Then Atma refers to our own body and mind. When the body becomes fatigued, body becomes injured, body becomes old and invalid, body becomes diseased, we suffer. Or the mind, mind is disturbed due to fear, anxiety, worry, stress, we suffer. So due to our own body and mind also, we suffer.

---
### Yudhishthira Maharaj, protection of all beings, violence against animals, demoniac mentality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CK4hyhTUISs?t=210
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To be non-violent to human beings and to be a killer or enemy of the poor animals is Saturn's philosophy. This is demoniac mentality; this is not divine nature."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the peaceful reign of Yudhishthira Maharaj, where all creatures were protected, with the current demoniac mentality of harming animals, especially in the context of meat consumption.

So these are imposed upon us. As spirit soul, we are actually free from all these three, but when we are covered by this body, we have to suffer. Now, in the case of the citizens of the world when Yudhishthira Maharaj was ruling as the emperor, it is described here the citizens, citizens includes not only the human beings but also the animals and all other creatures. Praja means those who are born in a particular land. So all those who have taken birth are entitled to be protected by the king, not just humans alone. So all of them were experiencing a very peaceful life, free from these three kinds of miseries, Klesha Daiva Bhutatma Hetavaha. So here it is said because Yudhishthira Maharaj is not having any enemy, Ajata Shatru. He for whom enemy is never born. Ajata, never born. Shatru means enemy. So therefore the living beings were not at any time disturbed by the enemy. They were not disturbed by mental agonies, diseases, or excessive heat or cold. They were not disturbed by any of these. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains: To be non-violent to human beings and to be a killer or enemy of the poor animals is Saturn's philosophy. This is demoniac mentality; this is not divine nature. Just like nowadays, they say we should not harm human beings, other human beings, but when it comes to animals, they don't hesitate to kill animals, especially in the western countries, and nowadays even in India, so many millions of animals are killed just because people want to eat animal flesh when there is plenty of animal flesh, plenty of vegetables, fruits, and vegetarian grains, dal available. Why should people kill poor animals? Why should they kill animals?

---

So this is not at all justified. It is showing enmity towards animals. So this is condemned in the scriptures, unnecessary killing of any creature, not only animals, even the birds, even the insects, even the worms. Everyone has got a right to live under the protection of the ruler or the king, as was explained in the previous verse. The king is ruling on behalf of the supreme lord, and all creatures are children of the supreme lord in this world. So this perspective, that everyone has the right to live, is the perspective of the Vedic scholars in the scriptures. Sarvejana Sukhino Bhavantu, or all creatures, may they be living happily, safely, nicely. This is the correct perspective for our own living in this world, not that we just think human beings should be not harmed, but it is okay to kill animals.

Ordinary civilians are attacked by the militants, and each group goes and, you know, attacks these civilians, apparently belonging to the opposite group. They don't indulge in fighting, but the militants, they want to enforce their own ideology or whatever that may be, but they are fighting against each other, and in the process, so many people are either injured or killed, and it has been going on for the last 11 years without any respite. They are indulging in killing animals.

### Yudhishthira's reign, united world, religious principles, scientific understanding, peace and prosperity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CK4hyhTUISs?t=786
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ultimately there is only one supreme lord, and he is in control, and he administers through nature justice for all kinds of unnecessary killing."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the current state of conflict with the unified and peaceful reign of Yudhishthira, emphasizing the importance of religious principles and a scientific understanding of God's control for achieving peace and prosperity.

Whereas here it is described in this particular verse that we are discussing, at the time of Maharaj Yudhishthira, there were no different nations. There were only eight states. Yudhishthira Maharaj was the emperor of the world. So there were many states. The whole world was united because there was one emperor, and the supreme head, being a trained king like Yudhishthira, kept all the inhabitants free from anxiety, diseases, and excessive heat and cold. That was about 5000 years back. They were not only economically well to do, as was described in the previous verse, nature used to supply plenty of all the natural resources, and they were also physically fit and undisturbed by supernatural power. They were also protected against enmity by other living beings, and they were also protected against bodily and mental agonies. So this is the result of the king being a God-conscious king, following the principles, the religious principles as described in the scriptures. This is nothing to do with any belief system or any kind of sectarian view of who they believe in or what they believe in now, but actually the scientific understanding that ultimately there is only one supreme lord, and he is in control, and he administers through nature justice for all kinds of unnecessary killing. This is the understanding. And so if the government or the head of state is conversant with these religious principles and rules according to the scriptural codes, then all the people will be peaceful, happy, and there will be plenty of supply. That is what is to be learnt from this particular verse. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Krishna's mission, Reducing the burden of the earth, Pacifying relatives
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=55
**Verse References:** SB 1.10.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In order for Krishna's mission of reducing the burden of this earth planet, which was overburdened by demoniac forces, to reduce these demoniac forces, Krishna had appeared when the Mother Earth and the demigods prayed to the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna resided in Hastinapura to pacify relatives and please Subhadra after the battle of Kurukshetra, which was part of Krishna's mission to reduce the burden of the earth caused by demoniac forces.

In this session, we will be discussing the seventh verse of the tenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, Shri Hari, Lord Shri Krishna, resided at Hastinapura for a few months to pacify his relatives and please his own sister Subhadra. So here is a description that after the battle of Kurukshetra, it was already described that Yudhishthira Maharaj was enthroned as the emperor of the world. Now Krishna had come to Hastinapura in order for arranging this battle of Kurukshetra in which Krishna's mission was to pacify his relatives and please his own sister Subhadra. In order for Krishna's mission of reducing the burden of this earth planet, which was overburdened by demoniac forces. To reduce these demoniac forces, Krishna had appeared when the Mother Earth and the demigods prayed to the Lord.

---
### Krishna's mercy, Liberation, Demons
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=143
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now, the benefit for these demoniac forces or demons is that when Krishna kills them, they are delivered, they are liberated."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how Krishna's actions, even killing demons, lead to their liberation, emphasizing his mercy and the necessity of dealing with those who harass devotees.

So the Lord appeared. Krishna appeared, the original form of the Lord appeared, Krishna appeared to reduce the burden. So, the reduction of the burden began when he was on his mother's lap, just a few days old infant. Puthana came to kill Krishna and Krishna instead of Puthana killing him, of course Krishna cannot be killed, so Krishna sucked out the life air and from there on these demons were delivered by Krishna when Krishna killed him. Puthana killed them one after another, one after another, one after another, but even after so much of killing, still some demons remained towards the end of Krishna's manifest pastimes. So, the remaining demons were assembled in the battle of Kurukshetra and Krishna did not personally kill them, but Krishna and Puthana killed them. Puthana arranged killing them through the Pandavas, his devotees. Now, the benefit for these demoniac forces or demons is that when Krishna kills them, they are delivered, they are liberated. Not only that, even when Krishna was present on the battlefield, even though they were not directly killed by Krishna, just because Krishna was present on the battlefield, because of the presence of Krishna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, all those who died in the presence of Krishna were liberated, were delivered. So, Krishna is merciful to everyone. But nevertheless, he had to take this action because the devotees are unnecessarily harassed by the demons, unnecessarily. So, when the harassment becomes too much, Krishna personally comes or empowers his agents to actually deal with these demons.

---
### Krishna's presence, Pacifying relatives, Subhadra's grief
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna resided at Hastinapura for a few months to pacify his relatives, because in this battle, so many were killed."
**Summary:** This segment explains why Krishna stayed in Hastinapura after the battle and Yudhishthira's enthronement: to pacify grieving relatives, especially Subhadra, who lost her son Abhimanyu.

So, after that battle of Kurukshetra, after Yudhishthira was enthroned as the emperor of the world, Krishna's work is over, he has to return to Dwaraka. So, here it is described, when did he go to Dwaraka? Departure of Lord Krishna of Dwaraka. Not immediately, even though his work was over, he stayed back a few months as is described in this seventh verse. Krishna resided at Hastinapura for a few months to pacify his relatives, because in this battle, so many were killed. So, many of them were related to Krishna. So, Krishna had to pacify the survivors, and also to please his own sister Subhadra. Subhadra was married to Arjuna and that was explained by Prabhupada in the purport. Why Krishna had to stay back to pacify Subhadra. So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, Krishna was to start for Dwaraka, his own kingdom after the battle of Kurukshetra and Yudhishthira's being enthroned. But to oblige the request of Maharaja Yudhishthira, and to show special mercy to Bhishma Deva, Lord Krishna stopped at Hastinapura, the capital of the Pandavas. Yudhishthira requested Krishna, please stay back for a few months, and Krishna accepted. But during that time, as is described in this verse, he also was pacifying his relatives, and he showed special mercy to Bhishma Deva as was described in the previous chapter. He was personally present and Bhishma was to go into the body, and blessed Bhishma Deva by his presence. So, it is further said by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. The Lord decided to stay, especially to pacify the aggrieved king, Yudhishthira, as well as to please Subhadra, the sister of Lord Krishna. Now, why was Subhadra aggrieved? That is because, Subhadra was especially to be pacified because she lost her only son Abhimanyu, who was just married.

---
### Mahabharata, Pandavas' exile, Duryodhana's plot
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=423
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because Duryodhana attempted to kill the Pandavas, just so that he could rule the kingdom out of his greed, though he is not entitled."
**Summary:** This segment recounts the story of Abhimanyu's parents, Arjuna and Subhadra, and the events leading to the battle of Kurukshetra, including the Pandavas' exile and Duryodhana's attempts to eliminate them.

Abhimanyu was the son of Subhadra and Arjuna. Subhadra was married to Arjuna. So, this Abhimanyu, he left his wife Uttara, who was the mother of Maharaja Pariksit. Now, it is described in the Mahabharata as well as the Bhagavata elsewhere, that when the Pandavas were in exile, the thirteenth year they spent in the kingdom of Virat Raj, Virat Maharaja, the kingdom of Virata, Virata Pradesh. So, they disguised themselves. Arjuna became a dancing teacher, in the court of Virat Raja. So, Virat Maharaja could not make out that this dance teacher who is seeking some engagement or employment is actually Arjuna. He could not make out because the Pandavas disguised themselves according to the agreement in the gambling match, which was a cheating, but nevertheless, because the Pandavas lost, Yudhishthira Maharaja agreed to go and exile and stay. Those accustomed to royal comforts, they have to stay in the forest, in exile, twelve years. And the thirteenth year, they have to hide themselves somewhere. And if Duryodhana discovers where they are hiding, then they have to again go twelve years for exile, twelve more years for exile. Because Duryodhana attempted to kill the Pandavas, just so that he could rule the kingdom out of his greed, though he is not entitled. It's not his rightful claim to become the emperor. But because he was greedy, somehow or the other, eliminate the Pandavas, who are the rightful claimants for the throne. So he tried to kill them, but because the Pandavas were always protected by Krishna, Duryodhana could not kill them.

---
### Cheating, Exile, Battle of Kurukshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So this Duryodhana and Shakuni plotted to send away the Pandavas to exile, by cheating the Pandavas in a gambling match."
**Summary:** The segment continues the story of Duryodhana's plot to cheat the Pandavas, their exile, Arjuna's disguise as a dance teacher, and the events leading to the inevitable battle of Kurukshetra.

Then somehow, by trick, by cheating, send them away to the forest and keep them in the forest forever. So this was the, this was the plot that was hatched by Duryodhana in consultation with his maternal uncle Shakuni, who was another wicked person. Same mentality as Duryodhana, demoniac mentality. Shakuni, Shakuni also. So this Duryodhana and Shakuni plotted to send away the Pandavas to exile, by cheating the Pandavas in a gambling match. And 13th year exile, what happened? That Arjuna disguised as a dancing teacher, and he became the dance teacher of Virat Maharaja's daughter, Uttara. So then, after the period of exile was over, one year is over, the 13th year, then the Pandavas revealed their identity, and they had to go back to Hastinapura to get back their kingdom, which they had lost during the gambling match, because they fulfilled the conditions. So when they went back, they were not given the kingdom back, and Duryodhana refused. So Duryodhana said, if you want the kingdom, you have to fight and defeat us, and then only take back the kingdom. So then the battle of Kurukshetra was inevitable.

---
### Marriage, Loss, Pacification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=677
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So to pacify, and Subhadra is Krishna's sister, so after the battle of Kurukshetra, because Subhadra lost her son, Krishna wanted to stay back and pacify Subhadra."
**Summary:** This segment describes the events following the Pandavas' return, including Virat Maharaja's offer to marry his daughter to Arjuna, Arjuna's refusal and arrangement of her marriage to Abhimanyu, and the tragic death of Abhimanyu in the battle of Kurukshetra, leading to Krishna's decision to stay and pacify Subhadra.

But still, when Virat Maharaj came to know after the 13th year of exile of the Pandavas, that they were the Pandavas, the great Pandavas, and Arjuna, the great bowman, the greatest bowman of his time, he was living in Kognito, and he was a dance teacher. So Virat Maharaj felt very, very bad, sorry for the Pandavas. In any case, he thought that now is the opportunity for me to marry my daughter to Arjuna, when he discovered Arjuna is the one who was the dance teacher. So when he offered to marry his daughter to Arjuna, because Kshatriyas, they can marry, Arjuna is already married to Draupadi, but Kshatriyas can marry more, have more than one wife, can have more than one queen. So when he was offered, he refused because by teaching Virat Maharaj's daughter, being the dance teacher, he was like, a teacher is on the level of the father. So I am like the father of your daughter, so I cannot marry your daughter. So Virat Maharaj was very disappointed. Then he said, okay, I cannot marry, but my son can marry your daughter. So Abhimanyu was just 16 years old, but still he was a prince, and Virat Maharaj's daughter was a princess. So it was a suitable match, therefore Arjuna offered Virat Maharaj that he can marry your daughter to my son. And Virat Maharaj was very pleased, so this Virat Maharaj's daughter Uttara was married to Abhimanyu, who was the son of Arjuna and Subhadra. But soon after that, what happened, the battle of Kurukshetra was declared, had to be fought, and the Pandavas along with their sons, Abhimanyu also went to fight, but this boy never returned alive, he was killed in the battle, in the fighting. So Subhadra lost her son, Uttara, the daughter-in-law of Arjuna, lost her husband Abhimanyu, Arjuna's son. So to pacify, and Subhadra is Krishna's sister, so after the battle of Kurukshetra, because Subhadra lost her son, Krishna wanted to stay back and pacify Subhadra.

---
### Devotion, Krishna's affection, Equality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QB-vnMB7VH0?t=851
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is actually affectionate to his devotees. And when he performs his Leela, his devotees assume the role of Krishna's relatives."
**Summary:** This segment clarifies that Krishna's affection is primarily for his devotees, who play the roles of his relatives in his pastimes, emphasizing his equality to all and special affection for devotees.

That is being described here. So that's the context in which it is said here, Krishna wanted to satisfy, pacify his sister. Now, it should be understood that Krishna has no particular affection for his relatives, like conditioned souls have some specific affection for their own relatives. No. Krishna is actually affectionate to his devotees. And when he performs his Leela, his devotees assume the role of Krishna's relatives. Just like Devaki and Vasudeva are devotees of Krishna, but they play the role of Krishna's father and mother. Or Subhadra is the devotee of Krishna, but she plays the role of Krishna's sister. Like that. So, Krishna is always pleased to satisfy his devotees. And therefore, he stayed back to pacify his sister, who is a devotee of Krishna. Not because she's sister. She's playing the part of sister, but she's devotee. And only the devotees can play the part of sister, the devotees can play the part of the Lord's relatives. And the Lord is always absolute. He's not particularly partial to his relatives, and he's neglectful about other people, indifferent to other people. No. He's always equal to everybody, but his devotees, he's got some special affection. So, I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
