### surrender, spiritual world, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kcyhwydg42Q?t=330
**Verse References:** SB 1.11.33
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the spiritual world everything is in plenty and merely by desiring anyone can get whatever they need because there are desiraries to supply everything in the spiritual world."
**Summary:** This segment delves into the meaning of the 33rd verse, explaining that even though Lord Krishna is always present, His presence is ever-new to the devotees. It contrasts the spiritual world, where everything is readily available, with the material world governed by karma, where one must strive for necessities and the goddess of fortune is always moving.

Then the next verse, the 33rd verse. The meaning of this 33rd verse is, although Lord Sri Krishna was constantly by their sides, as well as exclusively alone, His feet appeared to them to be newer and newer. The goddess of fortune, although by nature always restless and moving, could not quit the Lord's feet. So what woman can be detached from those feet having once taken shelter of them? So Srila Prabhupada explains this elaborately in the purport. The result of somebody completely surrendering to the Lord and taking shelter of his Lord's feet. What is the result of that? Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, conditioned living beings are always after the favour of the goddess of fortune, although by nature she is moving from one place to another. In this material world, everyone, all conditioned souls are living. It doesn't matter if you are saying that you are living or not, you are still living. You are still living. You are still living. You are still living. It is youratura, They are in a state of existence where they have to seek anything and everything that they want, that they need. That is not the state of existence in the spiritual world, in the association of the Lord. In the spiritual world everything is in plenty and merely by desiring anyone can get whatever they need because there are desiraries to supply everything in the spiritual world. But that's not the case in the material world.

---
### material nature, spiritual nature, fortune
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kcyhwydg42Q?t=471
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as the body is finished, everything is finished connected with the body."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the impermanence of material fortune and the eternal nature of spiritual existence, contrasting the fleeting nature of wealth and power in the material world with the constant, ever-fresh relationship with the Lord in the spiritual realm, as described in the Brahma Samhita.

In the material world there are material laws which are working, the laws of karma. And one has to work in order to even maintain oneself. So, such conditioned living beings, means those who are under the conditions of material nature, all the living beings within this material world who have come here for the sake of material enjoyment, independent material enjoyment, in forgetfulness of Krishna, they always seek the favor of the goddess of fortune. And the nature of the goddess of fortune is she keeps moving from one place to another. She is not fixed up except at the lotus feet of Krishna. And further Srila Prabhupada says, In the material world no one is permanently fortunate however clever one may be. Nobody is always fully fortunate, no. There have been so many big empires in different parts of the world. There have been so many powerful kings all over the world. And there have been so many fortunate men. But all of them have been liquidated gradually. Permanently nobody is having all the wealth or the power or the other kinds of fortunes. It's never permanent. This is the law of material nature. As soon as the body is finished, everything is finished connected with the body. But spiritually it is different. What is the difference? That is explained in the Brahma Samhita. According to Brahma Samhita, The Lord is served very respectfully by hundreds and thousands of goddesses of fortune. The statement in the Brahma Samhita is, They are always in a lonely place also with the Lord, the goddess of fortune. But still the association with the Lord is so inspiringly newer and newer that they cannot quit the Lord for a moment, even though they are by nature very restless and moving and are moving about. Another name for Lakshmi is Chanchala. She is restless. She always keeps moving around. But when it comes to the association of the Lord, Particularly His lotus feet appear always newer and newer. That is the spiritual nature. And that is the special thing about the Lord. Adhyam purana purusham na va yauvanam ca. In the Brahma Samhita. Na va yauvanam. He is freshly youthful all the ways. Even though he is the oldest personality. He is the youngest in the Brahma Samhita. He is the youngest in the Brahma Samhita. He is the youngest in the Brahma Samhita. He is the oldest personality. Purana purusham. Oldest personality. But his transcendental form never ages like this material forms. Never.

### spiritual relation, shelter, illusory energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kcyhwydg42Q?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Take shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord. All your problems will be solved."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the enlivening nature of a spiritual relationship with the Lord, encouraging listeners to seek shelter at His lotus feet to solve all problems and transcend the illusory attractions of the material world, which is described as a temporary interaction of the three gunas.

And the form always appears freshly youthful. The spiritual relation with the Lord is so enlivening and resourceful that no one can leave the company of the Lord once having taken shelter of him. So that's why the scriptures are directing us. Take shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord. All your problems will be solved. No more any attraction will remain in the material world. Actually material world is not really attractive. It is a presentation of the illusory energy to bewilder the living entities who are desirous of independent enjoyment. It is just an interaction of the three gunas, producing a temporary appearance of some attractive features. But spiritual beauty, spiritual world is not at all conducted by the three material modes. There is no touch of matter there. There are no material modes. There is no destructive kala. The time factor is conspicuous by its absence in the spiritual world. So such a spiritual world Krishna is inviting all of us. The living beings are by constitution feminine by nature. Prakriti. That's the way they are designated or explained in the Bhagavad Gita. The male or enjoyer is the Lord. And all manifestations of his different potencies are feminine by nature. The Lord is only male. Only Purusha. And all his potencies are prakriti. Feminine.

### potencies, surrender, satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kcyhwydg42Q?t=831
**Verse References:** BG 7.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavad Gita directs us surrender to the Lord. And in his association you will be able to satisfy your desires completely."
**Summary:** This segment explains the concept of living beings as paraprakriti (superior potency) and material elements as aparaprakriti (inferior potency), emphasizing that all potencies are meant for the Lord's enjoyment. It concludes by urging listeners to surrender to the Lord, promising complete satisfaction of desires in His association, which will be further elaborated in subsequent verses.

In the Bhagavad Gita the living beings are designated as paraprakriti. Apareyam itastu anyam prakritim vidhime param jivabhutam. The living beings are jivabhutam. They are param. Paraprakriti. Or superior potency. Spiritual potency. The material elements are aparaprakriti or inferior potency. They lack consciousness. Just like this table. This table doesn't have any consciousness. Any of the material items, objects, they don't have consciousness. Consciousness is only in the spiritual entities beginning with the living beings. Such potencies are always employed for the satisfaction of the employer or the enjoyer. All potencies are meant for the enjoyment of the Lord. He is the only enjoyer. He is the only Purusha. So therefore Bhagavad Gita directs us surrender to the Lord. And in his association you will be able to satisfy your desires completely. And that is what will be explained in the next few verses. How the devotees, they serve the Lord and seek and they are able to experience ecstasy, satisfaction, happiness, everything. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Srimad Bhagavatam, burden of the earth, divine plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=41
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He simply created hostility between the powerful administrators, and they fought amongst themselves."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and explains how the Lord pacifies the earth by creating hostility among burdensome, power-hungry rulers.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udeerayet. In this session, we will be discussing the 34th verse of the 11th chapter of the 1st Chant of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this 34th verse looks as the Lord was pacified after killing those kings who were burdensome to the earth. They were puffed up with military strength, horses, elephants, chariots, infantry, etc. He himself was not a party in the fight. He simply created hostility between the powerful administrators, and they fought amongst themselves. He was like the wind which causes friction between bamboos and so sparks a fire.

The living being becomes a false enjoyer under the dictation of the modes of nature, the gunas, sattva guna, rajya guna, tamo guna, the influence creates an influence such that I think every living being in this material world thinks I am an enjoyer.

### false ego, burden of the earth, dharmasya glani
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=163
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** Continuing the explanation, the speaker describes how the false sense of being God leads to increased material strength, making individuals a burden on the earth and creating an environment where sane people find it difficult to live, a state called dharmasya glani.

And that is a false enjoyer because it is explained why false enjoyer. Puffed up by such a false sense of becoming God. You see, God is God, nobody else can become God, but we try to become God. And what happens? The deluded living being increases material strength by so many activities and thus becomes the burden of the earth, so much so that the earth becomes completely uninhabitable by the same people. The earth is meant for all the living beings who are part and parcel of the Supreme Lord, but some people, they try to lord over others, and they become burdensome for the earth. That the sane people, the saintly people, the sober people, they find it difficult to live there. They find it difficult to live there. They find it difficult to live there. They find it difficult to live on the earth. This state of affairs is called dharmasya glani.

This is the term that is used in Bhagavad Gita. Yada yadahi dharmasya glani bhavati bharata. The fourth chapter, Krishna describes this. Or dharmasya glani is also described by Prabhupada here as misuse of the energy of the human being. When such misuse of human energy is prominent, the saner living beings become poor. The saner living beings become perturbed by the awkward situation created by the vicious administrators who are simply burdens of the earth. And the Lord appears by his internal potency just to save the saner section of humanity and to alleviate the burden due to the earthly administrators in different parts of the world.

The Lord comes to actually reduce this burden by eliminating. Eliminating means the Lord actually purifies them by killing them. He purifies them, or he delivers them, or he liberates them. Now the Supreme Lord does not favor either of the unwanted administrators, but by his potential power, he creates hostility between such unwanted administrators, as the air creates fire in the forest by the friction of the bamboos. ### divine plan, ideological conflict, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=404
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The hostility between different groups of politicians takes place by the unseen design of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the forest fire analogy, explaining that hostility among politicians is part of the Lord's unseen design. Only devotees are saved from the false prestige and ego that lead to such conflicts.

Now Srila Prabhupada explaining the example given. The fire in the forest takes place automatically by the force of the air. And similarly, that means nobody goes to set fire. No human being is required to set fire in a forest. The forest fire takes place, the air creating friction between the bamboos, creating a spark, and the spark ignites a fire. Dry bamboo catches fire, and it spreads. Similarly, the hostility between different groups of politicians takes place by the unseen design of the Lord. The unwanted administrators, puffed up by false power and military strength, thus became engaged in fighting amongst themselves over ideological conflicts and so exhaust themselves of all powers. They will kill each other because they cannot tolerate different ideologies. Each one has his own ideology. Nobody follows the ideal Supreme Lord. That is the nature of demoniac quality people. The history of the world reflects this factual will of the Lord, and it will continue to be enacted until the living beings are attached to the service of the Lord. Those who become devotees, they are saved from this false prestige.

### maya, surrender, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=538
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who take shelter in Krishna, they can cross over the gigantic ocean of material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita (7.14), explaining that the deluding energy (Maya) is difficult to overcome, but those who surrender to Krishna can easily cross the ocean of material existence.

In the Bhagavad Gita, this fact is very vividly described in the 7th chapter 14th verse. Daivi Hesha Gunamayi Mamamaya Duratyaya. The deluding energy is my potency, Mamamaya. And thus it is not possible for the dependent living beings to supersede the strength of the material modes, Duratyaya. It is not possible to supersede. But those who take shelter in me, Krishna is speaking these words, Mam evaye prapadyante. Those who take shelter in Krishna, they can cross over the gigantic ocean of material energy. Maya metam tarantite. The gigantic ocean of material existence.

### peace, surrender, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=597
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Independent attempts at establishing peace in this world are never going to be successful."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts that true peace and prosperity cannot be achieved through fruitive activities or speculative philosophy but only through surrender to the Supreme Lord and following His directions.

They are very easily able to cross it. This means that no one can establish peace and prosperity in the world by fruitive activities or by speculative philosophy or ideology. Independent attempts at establishing peace in this world are never going to be successful as long as people don't surrender to the Supreme Lord and follow the Lord's directions for establishing peace, for themselves becoming peaceful, and for bringing about peace in the entire world. Is never possible by human efforts, by people's speculation, by having their own ideologies. It's never going to work. The only way is to surrender unto the Supreme Lord and thus become free from the illusion of the deluding energy. This material energy which is acting, influencing, bewildering all the living beings, that influence one has to overcome by surrender to Krishna. There is no other way.

### demoniac nature, ignorance, surrender

This is the 15th verse of the 7th chapter. Namaham duskruti no mudha. Duskruti naha means engaged in vicious activities, destructive work. That's their nature. Demonic nature people or those who are lowest of mankind, those who are sinful, those who are atheistic, those who are materialistic, they are unable to surrender to the Lord. They are all now four categories Krishna has described in the Bhagavad Gita. Prabhupada is quoting that Bhagavad Gita verse, the 15th verse of the 7th chapter. They are all fools of the first order. Mudha. Mudha means they are compared to ass, donkey.

### ignorance, material energy, demoniac mentality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=741
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Always challenging the supreme power of the lord."
**Summary:** The speaker continues describing the four categories of people unable to surrender to Krishna: those who are the lowest of mankind, those whose knowledge is stolen by Maya, and those with a demoniac mentality who challenge the Lord's power.

And the second category, they are the lowest of the human species of life. Narathama, the lowest of mankind. The third category are the people who are Mayaya Apahrutagyana. They are robbed of their knowledge. Although apparently they seem to be academically educated, Apahrutagyana, Mayaya, by the material energy, illusory energy, their knowledge is stolen away. Though they have accumulated knowledge, they have studied, or they have academic achievements, all that knowledge is lost by the influence of Maya. And the last category of these Dushkrutina, they are all of the demoniac mentality, Asurambhavamashrita, always challenging the supreme power of the lord. They never surrender to Krishna. They always want to challenge. If there is god, I will defeat him. I will kill him. I will finish him off. I am powerful. I am supremely powerful. This is their mentality.

### materialism, ignorance, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zmRslIya4w8?t=830
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They only think in terms of body and the material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that materialistic people, focused on material power and strength, are fools because they lack knowledge of the living energy and the supreme spiritual science, which can only be understood through devotion to Krishna.

Those who are very materialistic, always hankering after material power and strength, are undoubtedly fools of the first order. They think that this material world, there is no controller, so let us take charge, let us take control. We will become powerful and defeat all the fellow humans and then control of the entire world. This is their plan, but they are fools. Why? Because they have no information of the living energy. They only think in terms of body and the material energy, and they are also ignorant of the supreme spiritual science. They are absorbed in material science. The end of the material body. Material science is like that. It has no understanding of what is spirit. They don't have understanding of the supreme spirit, individual spirit. They have no understanding of spirit at all, and neither they can really understand if they don't surrender to Krishna, if they don't take devotion and service. This will always remain unknown to them, and they will be always deluded by the material. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and explains that Krishna's pastimes may appear mundane, but He is eternally the controller and master, warning against misunderstanding His divine nature.

In this session we will be discussing from the 35th verse of the 11th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Saesha Naralokesmin Avatirina Swamayaya Ramesh Sri Ratnakootastho Bhagavan Prakruto Yatha. The meaning of this 35th verse is That Supreme Personality of Godhead Sri Krishna out of his causeless mercy appeared on this planet by its internal potency and enjoyed himself amongst competent women as if he were engaging in mundane affairs. Here is a warning given by Suta Goswami that the Lord's pastimes especially with his queens with his with the gopis appears like he is a mundane personality ordinary person appears like he is overpowered by their attractive features but actually He is Eternally the controller the Master, the enjoyer, the Lord, the um controller.

### spiritual vision, eternal relationship, pure love

**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of spiritual vision to understand Krishna's true nature and emphasizes that each individual has an eternal relationship of pure love with Krishna, distinct from material relationships.

Though when he incarnates, they are able to see him. But that seeing is not the spiritual vision by which they can see the spiritual form and understand the spiritual nature of the Lord. They cannot. They think he is another person of this world. But that is completely wrong understanding. When the Lord appears on different lokas or planets or on this planet of human beings, he displays his transcendental pastimes just to attract the conditioned souls to become his eternal servitors, friends, parents and lovers respectively in the transcendental world. Each one of us, we have got a relationship with Krishna, relationship of pureness, pure love, spiritual love. Now love is a word not to be mistaken with loving relationships in this material world. Material world or relationships are based on the body, on matter, on material qualities. A relationship with the Lord is based completely on spiritual attributes. The Lord is completely spiritual. We are also pure spirit souls. Irtundas. So when we become purified of all material contamination, then our actual spiritual form, spiritual identity is revealed. And in that spiritual identity, we can approach the Lord and actually reestablish the forgotten relationship, the spiritual relationship of spiritual love. That is what is being described here.

### foolishness, material relationships, temporary nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/te4YRXv9XEw?t=578
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker warns against the foolishness of comparing oneself to Krishna and criticizes material relationships as temporary and defective, contrasting them with the eternal relationship with the Lord.

But foolishly, people think that Krishna is an ordinary person. And if he can enjoy with so many women, why can't I enjoy? This is a foolishness. This is a foolishness of materialistic people who fail to understand the real nature of Krishna's personality which is revealed in the Bhagavatam and the Bhagavad Gita and all the Vedic scriptures. It is clearly revealed. Now as regards service in the material world, Prabhupada says, service is pervertedly represented in the material world and broken untimely, resulting in sad experiences. We may establish a nice relationship with another person of this world, but it is broken when the body is finished. This body, material body is temporary. And the relationship lasts only as long as the body is there. When the body is finished, relationship is finished. The illusion living being conditioned by material nature, cannot understand out of ignorance that all our relations here in the mundane world are temporary and full of inebriates. Inebriate means defects. Everything here is defective. Material relationships are defective. And they are also temporary. They don't last because the body itself is temporary.

### ignorance, illusion, permanent happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/te4YRXv9XEw?t=675
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker continues to discuss the state of ignorance in material conditioning, emphasizing that material relationships cannot provide permanent happiness and contrasting them with the eternal happiness found in a relationship with the Lord.

So relationships based on body will be temporary. And it is temporary. It is a state of ignorance. As long as we are materially conditioned, we are in ignorance that we cannot understand the effect of the illusion. We think whatever we are perceiving is reality. Material presentation by maya, we consider that as permanent, as eternal. It is actually temporal. And it is based on the fact that we are in a state of ignorance. And it is based on the actions and reactions of the three gunas. It is not a factual reality. What is presented, what appears like real but is not real. Such relations means, these material relations, cannot help us be happy perpetually. Some temporary happiness may be there in such material relations. But it cannot give us permanent happiness. If the same relation is established with the Lord, then we are transferred to the transcendental world after leaving this material body. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita.

### transcendence, detachment, divine qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mfyo29OEzIA?t=51
**Verse References:** SB 1.11.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Still, despite all their magical feats and attractions, they could not agitate the senses of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Srimad Bhagavatam 1.11.36, explaining how Krishna's queens, despite their beauty and power to attract even Cupid and Lord Shiva, could not agitate Krishna's senses, highlighting His transcendental nature.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 36th verse of the 11th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is the section which describes Krishna's relationship with his queens, and Suta Goswami clarifies a very, very important point in this connection. The verse is as follows. The meaning of this verse is, although the queen's beautiful smiles and furtive glances were all spotless and exciting, and although they could conquer Cupid himself by making him give up his bow in frustration, and although even the tolerant Shiva could fall victim to them, still, despite all their magical feats and attractions, they could not agitate the senses of the Lord.

---
### spiritual nature, detachment, divine qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mfyo29OEzIA?t=140
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He is un-agitated by their association, even though they are very, very beautiful."
**Summary:** Suta Goswami describes the transcendental quality of the Supreme Lord in his association with his 16,108 queens, emphasizing that Krishna remains unagitated by their beauty and attractions, contrasting with the reactions of Cupid and even Lord Shiva.

Now, Suta Goswami is describing the transcendental quality of the soul, of the Supreme Lord in his association with his 16,108 queens. What is the spiritual nature of the Lord? He is un-agitated by their association, even though they are very, very beautiful. They have beautiful smiles. They have very, very attractive glances. They can conquer Cupid himself. Cupid generally agitates the mind of materialistic people in this world to get attracted to material beauty, but Cupid himself becomes attracted by the beauty of these queens. And the Lord Shiva is known as a Dheera, but even Lord Shiva, he may fall victim to the attraction of the queens, but the queens can never agitate the senses of the Supreme Lord, which means that why did Upanishad interact with the queens as if he was engaged in a mundane love affair?

### devotion, liberation, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mfyo29OEzIA?t=231
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The path of salvation, that means the path of liberation from this material bondage, are a path going back to Godhead always forbids the association of women."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Srila Prabhupada's explanation of the path of salvation, which generally restricts association with women, particularly in the brahmachari, vanaprastha, and sannyas ashramas, contrasting this with Krishna's association with 16,108 queens, raising the question of how this can be understood.

That is because of their devotion that we explained in the later verse. So here it is just stated by Suta Goswami that they could not agitate the senses of the Lord. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport the path of salvation, that means the path of liberation from this material bondage, are a path going back to Godhead always forbids the association of women and a complete Sanatana Dharma or Vanashrama Dharma scheme forbids or restricts association of women. Srila Prabhupada explains elsewhere three of the four ashramas completely forbid association with women: brahmachari ashram, vanaprastha ashram, and sannyas ashram, no association of women. Only in the Grihastha ashrama there is permission to associate with women, that also under highly restricted conditions, only with one wife and that also for the sake of producing Krishna conscious children and becoming responsible raising children and becoming responsible raising children and becoming responsible raising a Krishna conscious family. All these are there. Grihastha ashrama, Grihastha Dharma, Grihastha Dharma. So Prabhupada raises this question, how then can one be accepted of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who is addicted to more than 16,000 wives? Krishna seemed to be very much attached to those Queens and He is one person and He has 16,108 Queens and is attached to them, so what is the proper understanding? This question may relevantly be raised by inquisitive persons really anxious to know about the transcendental nature of the Supreme Lord. And to answer such questions, the sages at Nainbi Sharanya have discussed the transcendental character of the Lord in this and in the following verses.

### material existence, suffering, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mfyo29OEzIA?t=505
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "See material existence means miseries; under illusion, we cannot understand that."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how marriage and family expand the illusory field of activities, leading to a false attraction for temporary material existence filled with miseries. He explains that we are always in the midst of miseries, even when we overlook them, and uses the analogy of climatic conditions to illustrate how we adjust to unfavorable conditions in our pursuit of enjoyment.

Man and woman marriage each other and then the family expands, children and relation and relatives and friends and home and wealth and accumulation of wealth, a society friendship community, all these things become the illusory field of activities and thus a false but indefatigable attraction for a temporary material existence which is full of miseries is manifest. See material existence means miseries; under illusion, we cannot understand that. We always think miseries are now and then coming and going, that's what we think, but actually we are always in the middle of miseries. Sometimes these miseries are overlooked, we are not cognizant of them or it doesn't bother us as much as it may bother somebody else, just like the climatic conditions are related for some people. A cold weather is very much related to the situation of the world. Some Ontarians say that the cold weather is pleasant for the people who are in the cold weather and they dislike the cold weather because the body, the body is pleasant for them because their body somehow they have made it acclimatized to the cold weather by generating lot of heat within and they like this cold weather but in summer they just can't tolerate even a little bit of heat they cannot tolerate and then the other people are not tolerant of them so that's a concept. Whose body is very warm they are not so uncomfortable in summer but in winter they can't tolerate little cold also. The fact is this is told by one Acharya Govinda Das Kaviraj. We are always harassed by four different material conditions, climatic conditions. Sheetha means cold, Vata. Sheetha-Atapa means heat and Vata means wind and varisolana-the rain.

---
### struggle for existence, salvation, devotee association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mfyo29OEzIA?t=679
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are therefore on the path of salvation for going back home back to Godhead are especially advised by all scriptural instructions to become free from the bondage of the world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that despite constant botheration from cold, heat, wind, and rain, we are determined to enjoy and adjust to unfavorable conditions, which is called material advancement. However, scriptures warn us that this is temporary and a hard struggle for existence. He emphasizes that those on the path of salvation are advised to become free from material bondage and attraction, which is possible through association with devotees (Mahatmas).

Cold, heat, wind, and rain, these are sometimes there in different times of the year and they always bother us. One or the other botheration is there, but we are so determined to enjoy that we'll somehow make adjustment and try to live amid such conditions, unfavorable conditions, and try to create a favorable atmosphere by adjustment. This is called material advancement of civilization, but the scriptures warn us and awaken us to the reality. It is temporary and you are simply engaged in a hard struggle for existence. So those who are therefore on the path of salvation for going back home back to Godhead are especially advised by all scriptural instructions to become free from the bondage of the world and become free from the bondage of the world and from such paraphernalia of material attraction because material attraction makes us forget the reality. We go deeper into illusion because of material attraction between male and female and how is this possible to become free from this paraphernalia of material attraction? It is possible only by association of the devotees of the Lord were called Mahatmas...Mahar Manas Tummampartha Day目. The Mahatmas are the pure devotees, they are always under the shelter of the internal potency of the Lord, the spiritual potency and they are always engaged in devotional service and such Mahatmas association will help us to become free from material attraction.

---
### material bondage, spiritual attraction, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mfyo29OEzIA?t=795
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is possible by positive spiritual attraction, and that positive spiritual attraction is by association of pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how Cupid's work keeps living entities in material bondage, but association with devotees helps us overcome this by developing spiritual attraction. This positive spiritual attraction is achieved through association with pure devotees, allowing us to situate ourselves on the spiritual platform and nullify material attractions, ultimately leading to attraction and attachment to Krishna in an eternal relationship of love.

Now Prabhupada explains elaborately that this material world is such that Cupid's work is going on to keep the living entities under material bondage, but association of devotees helps us to come out of this material bondage by losing attraction for all material things, by developing spiritual attraction to come out of this bondage. Krishna, it is not done in a void. It is possible by positive spiritual attraction, and that positive spiritual attraction is by association of pure devotees, association of pure devotees. So it is described here, just like the Lord is transcendentally situated because of which he is never agitated. He is never agitated by any feminine attractive features. Similarly, if we situate ourselves on the spiritual platform, all material attraction will be nullified, attractions will be nullified completely and we get attracted to Krishna, which is desirable, which is most desirable, which is the best. The safest and the topmost position of perfection for any living being to get attracted to Krishna and to become attached to Krishna, to become connected with Krishna in eternal relationship of love. That is the goal. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.11.37 | 26-07-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's Transcendence, Devotion, Reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=58
**Verse References:** SB 1.11.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But actually Krishna is only reciprocal."
**Summary:** This segment introduces verse 37 of the 11th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that Krishna's dealings may appear ordinary, but are actually a display of reciprocity with his devotees, and his supreme position remains unaffected.

In this session, we will be discussing the thirty-seventh verse of the eleventh chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tam ayam manyate loko yasangam api sanginam atmaupamyena manujam vyapruvanam yato abudhaha. The meaning of this verse is, the common materialistic conditioned souls speculate that the Lord is one of them. Out of their ignorance, they think that the Lord is affected by matter, although he is unattached. These are words spoken by Srila Suta Goswami. He is speaking to the sages of Naimisharanya. He is describing the Lord. He is describing the transcendental position of the Supreme Lord Krishna. To a common person, it may appear like Krishna is an ordinary man because his dealings look like his dealings with his queens in Dwaraka appears like he is a henpecked husband. But actually, Krishna is only reciprocal. He is reciprocating with them because they are his devotees, and he is only attracted by their devotional service. Accordingly, Krishna responds with every devotee, every pure devotee. Even while reciprocating with his devotees, even though he may appear to take a subordinate position, his supreme position remains unaffected because he is the Lord eternally, always, under all circumstances, in all times, at all places, in every situation, in his dealings with everyone. He is the Supreme Lord.

---
### Ignorance, Mundane Wranglers, Impersonalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=211
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Due to ignorance only, the foolish mundane wranglers misunderstand the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on Srila Prabhupada's explanation of the verse, particularly the word 'abudhaha,' meaning foolish due to ignorance, and describes 'mundane wranglers' who find fault with God and spiritual figures, promoting impersonal views and denying Krishna's supreme personality.

Srila Prabhupada explains this verse in the purport. The word abudhaha is significant here. Abudhaha means foolish because of ignorance. Due to ignorance only, the foolish mundane wranglers misunderstand the Supreme Lord. Srila Prabhupada uses this term, mundane wranglers. Mundane means somebody who is of this material world, is completely covered by ignorance due to the influence of the material nature. Such a person is called a mundane person. And wrangler means one who simply picks up quarrel for even trivial reasons. One is always trying to pick up a dispute. So, these foolish mundane wranglers are those who are always trying to find fault with God, Krishna, incarnations, spiritual persons, transcendentalists. And they themselves are actually very, very materialistic. So, they can never accept that the Absolute Truth is the Supreme Person. At the most, they may agree to accept some transcendental reality which is impersonal at the most, but even that they do not properly understand. So, Srila Prabhupada says here, the foolish mundane wranglers misunderstand the Supreme Lord. It's always the case with such people. Not only they misunderstand, they spread their foolish imaginations amongst innocent persons by propaganda. They are also engaged in propaganda, that God is impersonal or God does not exist or everyone is God, or God is ordinary person like that. The Supreme Lord Sri Krishna is the original primeval personality of Godhead. Primeval means he is the original person.

---
### Krishna's Potency, Divine Activities, Authenticity of Scriptures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=367
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you carefully analyze every action of Sri Krishna, as described in the authentic scriptures, you will definitely find his divine potency working."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes Krishna's divine potency displayed in his activities, citing the lifting of Govardhan Hill and subduing Kaliya as examples, and highlighting the importance of authentic scriptures in understanding Krishna's actions.

When he was personally present before the eyes of everyone, he displayed full-fledged divine potency in every field of activities. Now, if you carefully analyze every action of Sri Krishna, as described in the authentic scriptures, you will definitely find his divine potency working. Otherwise, how is it possible for a seven-year-old boy to lift Govardhan hill on his little left finger and hold it for seven days and nights continuously against a torrential rain, devastating rainfall sent by Indra? How is it possible? Only because he is unlimitedly powerful. That's the only explanation that can be given. Or he subdued that huge, powerful serpent Kalia who had 101 hoods and was very poisonous, spewing poison in the entire area of Yamuna lake. How could he subdue that Kalia? It's only because he is unlimitedly powerful. He is a supreme person. So, every action of Krishna is full of his divine potency.

---
### Krishna's Independence, Bliss and Knowledge, Transcendental Platform
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=466
**Verse References:** SB 1.1.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He may appear like an ordinary person, but his actions are full of bliss."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna is independent and his actions are full of bliss, knowledge, and eternity, differentiating them from ordinary activities, and emphasizing that Krishna's activities occur on the transcendental platform.

Therefore, he is the Lord under all circumstances. As we have already explained in the first verse of Srimad Bhagavatam, he is completely independent to act however he likes, but all his actions are full of bliss, knowledge, and eternity. This is the difference between Krishna's activities and our activities. He may appear like an ordinary person, but his actions are full of bliss. It gives pleasure to him and his devotees, always. Every action. So, that is the characteristic difference in the quality of the activity, the nature of the activity. And his activities are full of knowledge. Nothing is ignorance. Nothing is done ignorantly by Krishna, and eternity. Everything is on the spiritual platform, on the transcendental platform. Only the foolish mundaners misunderstand him, unaware of his eternal form of knowledge and bliss. Foolish mundaners. These foolish materialistic people. They don't understand Krishna's personality. Why? Because they don't want to understand. They simply want to deny God. Now, the nature of Krishna's personality as eternal form of knowledge and bliss.

---
### Scriptural Evidence, Krishna's Energies, Perfect Plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=573
**Verse References:** BG 10.12-13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He wills and things are carried out by his subordinate energies."
**Summary:** This segment cites the Bhagavad Gita and Upanishads to confirm Krishna's nature as eternal knowledge and bliss, explaining that Krishna's energies work according to his will, and that everything in the material world happens sequentially according to a perfect plan.

Is confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita and the Upanishads. Param Brahma Paramdham Apavitram Paramambhavan Purusham Shashvatam Divyam Adhidevam Ajam Vibhum. How much more do you require? Description. How much more description do you require? Bhagavad Gita. This is stated. Upanishads. Nityo Nityanam Chetanas Chetananam Eko Bahunam Yo Vidyati Kaman. Plenty of evidence is there in the scriptures about Krishna's personality. His different potencies work in a perfect plan of natural sequence. Parasthi Shaktir Vividhaiva Shuyate Swabhaviki Gyanabhila Kriyacha. All his energies are working exactly according to his will. He doesn't have to do anything. Then what are his activities meant for? It's simply meant for his pleasure pastimes. That's all. He doesn't have to do anything for the sake of maintaining this world or for the sake of controlling any particular thing. He doesn't have to do because his energies work according to his will. Sathya Sankalpa. Another name for Krishna. He wills and things are carried out by his subordinate energies, who are always engaged in serving his desires. And there is a perfect plan of natural sequence in this material world particularly. Everything is happening sequentially, very naturally, and according to a certain plan. Kala, Karma, Guna.

---
### Natural Laws, Karma, Krishna's Independence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=689
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is doing everything by the agency of his different potencies, while he remains eternally the supreme independent."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the perfect arrangement of the material world, with seasons changing, laws of nature working, and souls taking birth according to their karma, all arranged by Krishna through his potencies, while he remains independent and engaged in his pleasure pastimes.

So that is perfectly arranged by Krishna, that the seasons are changing, the sun is rising, the time is passing by, and systematically creation, maintenance, destruction is happening, and all the natural laws are working. Laws of nature are working perfectly. Every condition soars. Every soul is taking birth according to their karma in course of time. Changes are happening. Krishna doesn't have to involve himself in details. The details are already coded into the material nature. That is Krishna's wonderful creation, that the foolish scientists will never understand because they cannot decode that, because nature is working. They have their theories. Krishna is doing everything by the agency of his different potencies, while he remains eternally the supreme independent. He is aloof from everything because he is engaged in his pleasure pastimes with his devotees. When he descends from the material world by his causeless mercy to different living beings. When he descends from the material world by his own potency. In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, I appear by my internal potency.

---
### Internal Potency, Material Modes, Mayavadis, Parampara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eiA74rWzdOw?t=792
**Verse References:** BG 4.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The correct understanding can only get from pure devotees, from the parampara system."
**Summary:** This segment distinguishes between Krishna's internal and external potencies, explaining that Krishna uses his internal potency for his activities and is unaffected by material modes, contrasting this with the mental speculators (Mayavadis) who misunderstand Krishna and the importance of receiving correct understanding through the parampara system.

What is the difference between internal potency and external potency? External potency is the material energy under which we are all placed, due to which we are in illusion. But Krishna does not come by the agency of the material energy or illusory energy or external potency. He uses his internal potency or spiritual energy for all his activities, for his incarnation, for his appearance, for his activities, everything. He uses his internal potency. He is not subject to the conditions of the material modes of nature. We are all affected by something, sattva guna, raja guna, tamo guna, always acting on us. But Krishna is completely aloof from these three gunas, even when he is in the material world. Nirguna. He is called nirguna, unaffected by material nature. The mental speculators misunderstand him as the supreme person, and they consider his impersonal features as inexplicable, Brahman to be all. The mental speculators are the mayavadis. They always speculate. They don't take the scriptural statements to be fact. They misunderstand the scriptural statements, and they always argue that the impersonal feature is the highest truth, whereas in the Bhagavad Gita, Brahmano hi pratishtaham. It is clearly explained that the Brahman feature or the impersonal feature is subordinate to the supreme personality of Godhead. Yesya prabha prabhavato jagalanda koti. In the Brahmasamhita it is explained, the Brahman feature is the effulgence of the body of the transcendental form of Krishna. Very clearly it is explained everywhere. In the Vedanta Sutra also it is explained like that. So this. The correct understanding can only get from pure devotees, from the parampara system, from the proper explanation of the Vedic literature given by the acharyas, Vaishnava acharyas. Only then you can understand. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.11.38 | 27-07-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### divinity, material nature, transcendentalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kaq1QUSodbI?t=55
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The divinity of the personality of Godhead is never in contact with the qualities of material nature in any circumstances."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Lord and his devotees are unaffected by material qualities, referencing Vedic literature and the example of Krishna's birth and actions to address potential doubts about this concept.

In this session, we will be discussing the 38th verse of the 11th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, this is the divinity of the personality of Godhead. He is not affected by the qualities of material nature even though he is in contact with them. Similarly, the devotees who have taken shelter of the Lord do not become influenced by the material qualities. So both the Lord and his pure devotees are unaffected by material qualities, material nature. Srila Prabhupada explains this in the purport. In the Vedas and Vedic literatures, Shruti and Smriti, Shruti is the portion of the Vedas which is coming down through the oral tradition from the beginning of creation, whatever was revealed to Brahma by the Supreme Lord within the heart of Brahma. Tene Brahma Hrudaya Adhikavaye. The original Vedas and Smriti is the corollary literature in pursuance of the Vedas. The Vedas explained by saintly persons, great acharyas, great rishis, for understanding of the common people. The Smriti literature means the Upuranas, the Itihasas, Dharmashastras, like that. So all of them affirm that in the divinity there is nothing material. The Supreme Lord, Krishna, is transcendental only, nirguna, this word is used. The Supreme cognisant is always cognisant, is always full of consciousness and is supremely conscious person. Hari or the personality of God had a supreme transcendental person beyond the range of material affection. Hari nirgunasakshaa. This is the statement in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 10th canto. Hari, the supreme personality of Godhead, is a supreme transcendental person situated beyond the range of material affection. These statements are confirmed even by Acharya Shankara. Shankaracharya says, Narayana paro avyaktat. So he admits, Narayana, the supreme personality of Godhead is beyond this manifested and unmanifested matter. One may argue that his relation with the goddess of fortune may be transcendental, but what about his relation with the Yadu dynasty being born in that family? Or is killing the non-believers like Jarasandha and other asuras directly in contact with the mouths of material nature? Because Lakshmi is situated transcendentally. People may consider his relationship with Lakshmi to be also transcendental. But what about Krishna taking birth in the Yadu dynasty as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki while they were imprisoned? And as if Vasudeva and Devaki are afraid that Kamsa will kill this newborn child, Vasudeva takes that newborn child and hides him in Gokula. Vasudeva under the care of Yashoda and Nanda. It appears like Krishna is afraid or Vasudeva is afraid of Kamsa killing the child. So why should Vasudeva be afraid or why should Krishna be afraid? Because Krishna only directed Vasudeva, take me to Gokula. So why did Krishna do so? Why did Krishna do that? So therefore, his taking birth in the Yadu dynasty as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki under very very peculiar circumstances or his killing the non-believers like Jarasandha and other asuras directly in contact with the modes of nature. This Jarasandha and all these asuras, they are completely entrenched in the three gunas and they are killed by Krishna as if Krishna had no other means of subduing these people. So the answer is, Prabhupada gives the answer, that divinity of the personality of Godhead is never in contact with the qualities of material nature in any circumstances.

---
### divinity, mystic power, control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kaq1QUSodbI?t=368
**Verse References:** BG 9.11, BG 10.41
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He is in contact, appears to be in contact, but he is aloof, never in contact."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's transcendental position, explaining that while he appears to interact with material nature, he remains aloof and in control as the ultimate source of everything, possessing unlimited mystic powers.

That is the affirmation in these words. prakṛtiṣṭhopi tad gunaihi na yujyate. He is in contact, appears to be in contact, but he is aloof, never in contact. Actually, he is in contact with such qualities because he is the ultimate source of everything. ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo, janmadyasya yatah. Everything has ultimately got its source in Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He is the ultimate source of everything. So he is the source of the three gunas also. They are his energies. They are working according to his control and direction and his will. So, he being the controller, he will never come under the control of the materials. He will never come under the control of the material modes of nature. Please understand this point. He being the supreme controller always, eternally, why will he come under the control of material nature? He is known, therefore, as Yogeshwara. In the Bhagavad Gita, yatra yogeshwara krishnao yatra pārthodhanuddharah. Yogeshwara means he is the master of mystic power, or in other words, the all-powerful. A yogi in this material world, after practicing severe austerities, may get ashta siddhi, eight kinds of mystic powers, which will bewilder even the greatest of human beings. Everybody can be bewildered by the ashta siddhi displayed by some powerful yogis, perfect yogis. But Krishna is Yogeshwara, the master of all these yogis. And he only awards that ashta siddhi to the yogis. He has unlimited siddhis. Krishna has unlimited siddhis. A yogi can only have eight siddhis. Of course, eight major and eight minor. They have different kinds of siddhis. But maximum, eight major siddhis and eight minor siddhis, and four auxiliary siddhis. This is explained in detail in the eleventh canto of the Bhagavad Gita. But Krishna personally has unlimited mystic powers. Unlimited mystic powers. So we cannot even imagine what are the kinds of mystic powers Krishna has. Just like a yogi, to give an example. A yogi can expand into eight forms, at the most, ten forms. Krishna expanded into 16,108 forms when he married 16,108 princesses and was living with them in 16,100 palaces. So that is Krishna. Yogeshwara. Please understand this point.

---
### devotees, transcendence, behavior
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kaq1QUSodbI?t=595
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's also how their behavior is under different circumstances."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how Krishna's devotees are also unaffected by material qualities, using the example of the six Goswamis of Vrindavan, and emphasizes that true transcendence is demonstrated through behavior, not just outward appearance.

And Krishna's devotees, learned devotees are not affected by the influence of material mounts. What is being of Krishna? Even his pure devotees are unaffected by material kunas. The example is given. The great six Goswamis of Vrindavan, the direct disciples of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, who were in Vrindavan, living in a very austere condition, for doing devotional work, according to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's directions. They all came from greatly rich and aristocratic families. But when they adopted the life of mendicants of Vrindavan, superficially they appeared to be in a wretched condition of life. But factually, they were the richest of all in spiritual values. The Goswamis, the six Goswamis of Vrindavan, tyaktva, toornam, ashesha, mandala, pati, srenim, sada, tuchchavat. They gave up all aristocracy and all material opulences, and they adopted the renounced order, Goswamis. And they were living like beggars, just begging for a little food. Little food, not much. And from whom? From the devotees, from the brajavasis, little food. And then doing the work of writing extensively the scriptures, preparing the scriptures for us, for common people, explaining the transcendental subject matter. And then they were engaged in excavating lost places in Vrindavan, and they were engaged in preaching Krishna consciousness. They were engaged in chanting, performing Sankirtana, showing by their example how we should do. So, such Mahabhagavatas, or first great devotees, all the moving amongst men are not contaminated by honour, insult, hunger or satisfaction, sleep or wakefulness, which are all resultant actions of the three modes of material nature. So, we can understand how they are unaffected by the three gunas when we see their behaviour. They don't care for any honour. Somebody praises them, they are not elated. And somebody insults them, they don't feel hurt. They continue with their service unaffected by all these conditions. And the Goswamis were so controlled in their sense activities, they used to sleep for two hours or not even that. So, what were they doing? They were busy with writing books, with excavating lost places of Krishna's pastimes, and preaching and chanting and Sankirtana. Twenty-two hours they used to work, constantly engaged in such work, and with great internal pleasure, they used to do that, not as if it's a burden. Now, Prabhupada explains that some of them are engaged in worldly dealings yet are unaffected, the pure devotees. Unless these neutralities of life are there, one cannot be considered to be situated in transcendence. So, there is a test you can apply to actually understand whether somebody is actually transcendently situated or not. It's not just by dressing oneself in some saffron clothes or just remaining in some simple dress or staying in a forest. That alone is not the criterion. It's also how their behavior is under different circumstances. When they interact with ordinary people, do they become affected by anger or by insult? Or do they have any trace of material desires? Do they have any trace of envy? No. So, it is very, very evident and this is clearly proved by their interactions with ordinary people.

---
### divinity, internal potency, devotees

### simplicity, devotion, glories of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=64
**Verse References:** SB 1.11.39
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The simple and delicate women truly thought that Lord Sri Krishna, their beloved husband, followed them and was dominated by them."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces and translates the verse, explaining that the queens of Dwaraka, though devoted, were unaware of the full extent of Krishna's glories due to their simplicity.

This is the last verse of this chapter, 11th chapter. Tamme Nire Avala Moodha Strainam Chanu Vratam Rahaha Apramana Vido Bhartur Ishwaram Matayoyatha. The translation of this verse is, the simple and delicate women truly thought that Lord Sri Krishna, their beloved husband, followed them and was dominated by them. They were unaware of the extent of the glories of their husband, as the atheists are unaware of him as the supreme controller. So, the queens in Dwaraka, they were not aware of the glory of their husband. They were not aware of the glory of their husband. They were not fully aware of Krishna's glories, because Krishna's glories are unlimited. And they were very, very simple women. Devotees are simple by nature. They are not shrewd like materialistic persons. Devotees are very, very simple and straightforward in all their dealings.

---
### reciprocity, unlimited glories, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=142
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is only reciprocating because the pure devotees are engaged in his devotional service unconditionally."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's reciprocation with devotees, emphasizing that it is due to their unconditional devotional service. He uses the analogy of Lord Ananta to illustrate the unlimited nature of Krishna's glories.

And devotees sometimes think that Krishna is very nicely responding to all their actions or all their interactions. But actually the fact is Krishna is only reciprocating because the pure devotees are engaged in his devotional service unconditionally. That's the only reason Krishna reciprocates. And there is no other reason. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, even the transcendental wives of Lord Sri Krishna did not know completely the unfathomable glories of the Lord because his glories are unlimited. Nobody can estimate what is the extent of Krishna's glories. To give an example, Lord Ananta, Sesha, he has got millions and billions of hooves. Ananta means unlimited. So he is holding all the universes on his hooves. And how many universes are there? So many, millions and billions of universes. And he is glorifying Krishna by his millions of mouths. And he has not been able to reach the end of the glorification of Krishna. He's been glorifying since the beginning of creation. And he's been glorifying, glorifying. He keeps on glorifying till the end of the duration of this life of the universe. So which means to say that Krishna's glories are actually unlimited. Even Lord Ananta cannot find the end of his glories.

---
### Yogamaya, internal potency, transcendental relationships
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=264
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is an exchange of feelings between Krishna and his eternal associates which Yogamaya facilitates."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the ignorance of devotees about Krishna's glories is due to the action of Yogamaya, the internal potency, which facilitates the exchange of feelings in transcendental relationships. He gives the example of Arjuna ordering Krishna as his chariot driver.

So what to speak of it, anybody else? Now, the ignorance of the devotees, pure devotees, about Krishna's glories is not mundane. It's not due to material influence. It's because in such a case of the pure devotees, there is the action of the internal potency of the Lord, the spiritual potency, the internal potency, Yogamaya. Why is there this action of Yogamaya? Because there is an exchange of feelings between Krishna and his eternal associates which Yogamaya facilitates. The Lord exchanges transcendental relations in five ways as proprietor, master, friend, son, and lover. And in each of these pastimes, he plays fully by the potency of Yogamaya, the internal potency. He plays exactly like an equal friend with a covered voice, or even with friends like Arjuna. Arjuna orders Krishna, please take my child, please take my son, please take my wife, please take my son, please take my son, please take my wife, please take my son, please take my wife, please take my son, please take my wife, please take my wife. Please take my chariot between the two armies. And Krishna very nicely takes the chariot exactly according to the order of Arjuna.

---
### unaffected position, pleasure pastimes, reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=356
**Verse References:** BG 4.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply for the sake of pleasure the Lord reciprocates."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna's supreme position is unaffected by assuming subordinate roles in his pastimes. He highlights the reciprocal nature of the relationship between Krishna and his devotees, where both seek to please each other.

But Krishna's position is not compromised when we realize Arjuna is affected by seeing the relatives on the enemy's side. He becomes afflicted by confusion. Should I fight? Should I not fight? And Krishna instructs him in Bhagavad Gita. So from that we understand Krishna's supreme position is unaffected by Krishna assuming the position of a chariot driver subordinate to Arjuna. He plays, that is Lord Krishna, plays, exactly like a son in the presence of Yashoda Mata. He plays exactly like a lover in the presence of the cowherd damsels. And he plays exactly like a husband in the presence of the queens of Dwarka. Such devotees of the Lord never think of the Lord as a supreme. They don't think he is supreme. But think of him exactly as a common friend, a pet son, or a lover, or husband, very much dear to their heart and soul. That is the relation between the Lord and his transcendental devotees. It's a relationship. And these are called pleasure pastimes of the Lord. Simply for the sake of pleasure the Lord reciprocates. And the devotees also serve him simply to give him pleasure. Krishna wants to please his devotees. Devotees want to please Krishna. This is what is going on in the spiritual world, in all spiritual relationships. This is what is going on. Eternally this goes on.

---
### spiritual world, attraction, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=473
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When the Lord descends, he does so along with his own devotees. He gives them an entourage to display a complete picture of the transcendental world where pure love and devotion for the Lord prevail without any mundane tinge of lording it over the creation of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Lord descends with his devotees to give a glimpse of the spiritual world, attracting those entangled in the material world. He emphasizes the need to purify the heart through bhakti to join Krishna in his pleasure pastimes.

This is not understood by the materialistic persons, by atheists, by common people, by those who are under the influence of the illusory energy. They cannot understand this. When the Lord descends, he does so along with his own devotees. He gives them an entourage to display a complete picture of the transcendental world where pure love and devotion for the Lord prevail without any mundane tinge of lording it over the creation of the Lord. See, what is going on in the spiritual world eternally, always. The Lord, when he comes down, he wants to give us a sample. He wants to display a sample of what is going on there. Why? Just to attract us, who are stuck up in this material world with a misconception that I belong to this material world. I am this material body. So, to deliver us, to get us out of this entanglement, Krishna very mercifully, very kindly comes. And he comes with his associates. And he displays exactly what is going on there. So, to deliver us, so that we become attracted. Because Krishna is always engaged in pleasure pastimes with his pure devotees, we can also join Krishna in those pleasure pastimes provided we purify our material contaminated heart. That is important.

---
### liberation, transcendental bliss, understanding the difference
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=574
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In the spiritual world, or in contact with Krishna, in devotional service, there is always transcendental bliss. Naturally. Always it is there."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the process of bhakti as a means to become free from material miseries and attain transcendental bliss in Krishna's abode. He encourages listeners to understand the difference between material and spiritual happiness.

We should also purify by the process of bhakti. And we can also become completely free from material miseries, from material entanglement, from the repetition of birth and death. We can get out of this material existence, because Krishna will take us back to his own personal abode. Here, simply we are engaged in some lording over material nature, or engaged in some sense gratification, because we are always in want of some happiness. We are seeking happiness because our heart is dry without any natural experience of bliss. That is not our natural position in the material world, to always be blissful. In the spiritual world, or in contact with Krishna, in devotional service, there is always transcendental bliss. Naturally. Always it is there. So, you should carefully understand this difference from the descriptions in the Bhagavatam. You should understand the difference.

---
### liberated souls, pure consciousness, contamination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=660
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They are perfect representations of the marginal or internal potency in complete negation of the influence of the external potency."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the devotees who accompany the Lord are liberated souls with pure Krishna consciousness, unaffected by material energy, even when appearing to take birth in this world.

The devotees of the Lord who come with him when he comes to this world are all liberated souls. They are all liberated souls. They may appear to take birth like ordinary people. Like when Krishna appeared as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki in the Yadu dynasty. His associates appeared in Vrindavan, in Mathura. His associates appeared like that. Neither Krishna is actually under the clutches of Maya nor his pure devotees associated with the spiritual world are under the control of Maya, material, illusory energy. No. Even though they take birth, even though they seem to be performing some ordinary activities, they are completely aloof, because their consciousness is, the devotees consciousness is pure Krishna consciousness. Pure Krishna consciousness. They are perfect representations of the marginal or internal potency in complete negation of the influence of the external potency. See, originally all of us we are pure spirit. We are originally not contaminated by material energy. But in association with this material world, with a desire to lord over material nature, with a desire to enjoy materially, we become contaminated. Whereas the pure devotees, they are not contaminated. When they come to this material world, they remain uncontaminated, because they don't lord over material nature. They don't want to materially enjoy. Why should they materially enjoy, try to materially enjoy? When they have so much of spiritual bliss,

---
### spiritual pleasure, internal potency, exchange of love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WRBy8LTfe14?t=791
**Verse References:** BG 9.29
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The wives of lord Krishna were made to forget the immeasurable glories of the lord by the internal potency, so that there might not be any flaw of exchange."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the pure devotees' spiritual pleasure with the illusory happiness sought by those in the material world. He concludes by explaining that the wives of Krishna were made to forget his greatness by the internal potency to facilitate a free exchange of love.

we are the people who are starving for happiness. So we take whatever illusory happiness is available, or we try to squeeze out material happiness by sense gratification. That is not the way the transcendentalists, the pure devotees relish spiritual pleasure. No. The wives of lord Krishna were made to forget the immeasurable glories of the lord by the internal potency, so that there might not be any flaw of exchange. See, for the exchange to take place, between Krishna and his queens, if they become aware of Krishna's greatness, then they will not freely exchange their feelings of love for Krishna. Then they will be inhibited. So, to enable them to have a free exchange of their loving feelings with Krishna, the external potency will make them forget Krishna's real greatness. And they will think Krishna is like them, a person belonging to this world. Like that they will think. They take it for granted that Krishna is a henpecked husband, and they think that Krishna is always satisfying them, because he is henpecked. But actually he is satisfying, because he is pleased by their devotional service. That is the reality. So, we should understand this carefully from the Bhagavad Gita. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
