### Krishna, eternality, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WPoiixXPFCY?t=376
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the source of all bliss for everyone else."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Krishna's eternal existence, supreme position, and all-cognizant nature, contrasting it with the constantly changing condition of beings in the material world, and emphasizes that while we cannot merge into Krishna, He accepts offerings made with pure devotional service out of His well-wishing nature.

The entire spiritual world is full of bliss, the source of all bliss. He is eternally existent. Krishna is always existing as Krishna, as the Supreme Lord, as the supreme enjoyer, supreme proprietor, supreme master, supreme controller, supreme belvisher, supreme guardian, supreme protector. He is always in that same position. What is our condition? Constantly changing our position. At least in the material world, our position is constantly changing. Krishna is all cognizant. He is fully aware of everything. His consciousness is spread everywhere. His consciousness is universal consciousness. Now, sometimes people think that we can merge into Krishna and also become fully cognizant. No, that is never possible. One may try to merge into Krishna, but even after merging, the tiny particle of consciousness of the individual soul remains the tiny particle. The particle remains the particle. That is the law of identity. So, our identity as the tiny part of Krishna is eternally our identity. And Krishna as the complete whole is always the complete whole. He is never incomplete. We are never complete without our connection with Krishna. Krishna is also all blissful, total bliss. Krishna is the source of all bliss for everyone else. And Krishna himself is always blissful. Therefore, any presentation, however valuable it may be, is not needed by him. See, we become happy if somebody gives us a gift because we are in need. We are in need of the gift because we are incomplete. But Krishna is not in need of anything. Still, because he is the well-wisher for one and all, he accepts from everyone everything that is offered to him in pure devotional service. So, whenever you make an offering to Krishna,

---
### devotion, love, reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WPoiixXPFCY?t=549
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is all a sign of pure love and affection between the Lord and the devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that offerings to Krishna must be made with pure devotion, as Krishna is not in need of anything but accepts out of loving reciprocation, and emphasizes that in devotional service, there is no demand from either side, but rather pure love and affection.

We have to do it in pure devotional service. Yome bhaktya prayacchati. Whatever you may offer. Generally, devotees don't eat anything which is not offered to Krishna. So, they may prepare varieties of foodstuff for offering to Krishna. But Krishna will accept it only if it is offered with pure devotion. Not otherwise. Because he is not in need of anything. Still, he will accept when it is offered with pure devotion. That is his loving reciprocation with his devotees. It is not that Krishna is in want for such things. Because the things are themselves generated from his energy. Where are we getting all that we have, whatever we can offer to Krishna? We are getting it from Krishna only, through his energy. The comparison is made herein that making offerings to the Lord is something like offering a lamp in the worship of the sun god. Anything fiery and illuminating is but an emanation of the energy of the sun. And yet, to worship the sun god, it is necessary to offer him a lamp. Etiquette of worshipping any great personality in the Vedic culture. One of the offerings made is an offering of lamp, arati. So, in the worship of the sun, a lamp is offered to the sun, not because the sun is in need of illumination. The sun is source of illumination for all of us. But because it is a way of worshipping the sun god. Now, Prabhupada says, in the worship of the sun, there is some sort of demand made by the worshippers. They offer prayers, asking for something. But in the case of devotional service to the Lord, there is no question of demand from either side. Neither Krishna is in need of worship. Now, the devotees are worshipping the Lord. They are worshipping Krishna to ask something from Krishna. Because Krishna supplies everything that the devotees require completely. Devotees don't have to ask Krishna for anything. Krishna naturally, automatically, very lovingly supplies everything for the devotees.

---
### devotion, love, dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WPoiixXPFCY?t=697
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord does not supply to a pure devotee what is considered to be a barrier to the discharge of devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Prabhupada's teachings, stating that in devotional service there is no demand from either side, it is pure love, and that devotees should only ask Krishna for their needs, but Krishna will not supply anything that hinders their devotion.

So, Prabhupada says, in devotional service, there is no demand from either side. Neither devotees demand anything from Krishna, nor Krishna demands anything from the devotees. It is all a sign of pure love and affection between the Lord and the devotee. This is the wonderful quality of pure devotional service. It is pure love. There is no demand. So, it is free from any obligations. There is no obligation. The Lord is the supreme Father of all living beings. And therefore, those who are conscious of this vital relation with God can make filial demands from the Father. And the Father is pleased to supply the demands of such obedient sons without bargaining. Prabhupada explains, still, devotees, if at all, at any time there is any need for the devotees, they will only approach Krishna and only ask from Krishna. Though there is no need, but in case there is some need at any time, the devotees will only ask Krishna and nobody else. And Prabhupada says further, the Lord is just like a desire tree, and from him everyone can have everything by the causeless mercy of the Lord. But, Prabhupada explains one important point, for those who are practicing devotional service, whenever you need anything, you ask Krishna only and not anybody else. But, you should also remember, as a supreme Father, the Lord does not supply to a pure devotee what is considered to be a barrier to the discharge of devotional service. So, if we ask in our beginning stages of devotional service from Krishna, something which will block our devotion, then Krishna will not supply that. So, this has to be remembered.

---
### devotional service, attraction, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WPoiixXPFCY?t=837
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the heart is purified of all these material misconceptions, and our spiritual consciousness is awakened, then spontaneously we are attracted to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining that those engaged in devotional service can rise to unalloyed devotion, free from material desires, through Krishna's transcendental attraction, and that when the heart is purified, spontaneous attraction to Krishna awakens, leading to absorption in hearing, chanting, and remembering Him.

Those who are engaged in devotional service of the Lord can rise to the position of unalloyed devotional service. Unalloyed devotional service is pure devotional service, free from any mixture of karma, and material desires, free from all those mixtures. Pure devotional service, we can come to that position by his transcendental attraction. When he attracts us, spiritually. So, by pure devotional service, attraction for Krishna is awakened in the heart. Right on a material condition, we are attracted to so many different material things. That's because of ignorance. That's because of our heart lacking Krishna consciousness. But, when the heart is purified of all these material misconceptions, and our spiritual consciousness is awakened, then spontaneously we are attracted to Krishna, Krishna's name, Krishna's form, Krishna's qualities, Krishna's activities, Krishna's devotees, Krishna's abode, Krishna's descriptions. We are spontaneously attracted. And then we become absorbed in hearing, chanting, remembering, etc. That transcendental attraction is the way to elevate ourselves above these gunas, above the modes, and become completely situated in pure devotional service. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from two verses, the sixth verse and the seventh verses of the eleventh chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. These two verses are given separately, but they are related, so I will discuss in this one session both the verses. First I will discuss the sixth verse: Natas Mate Natas Sadangri Pankajam Virincha Vairincha Surendra Vanditam Parayanam Chemam Ihe Chathamparam Nayatrakala Prabhavet Paraprabhu.

### Bhakti, Knowledge, Detachment, Pure Heart
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=94
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti means knowledge. Bhakti means complete material detachment. Bhakti means pure heart. That is Bhakti."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the meaning of the verse, emphasizing that the citizens' devotion to Krishna is based on knowledge and understanding of His position, and that true bhakti involves knowledge, detachment, and a pure heart, not just sentiment.

This is one of the prayers offered by the citizens of Dwaraka to Krishna. The citizens said, O Lord you are worshipped by all demigods like Brahma, the four Sanas, Sanaka, Sanatana, Sanandana and Sanatkumara, and even the king of heaven, Surendra, the king of heaven Indra. You are the ultimate rest for those who are really aspiring to achieve the highest benefit of life. You are the supreme transcendental lord and inevitable time cannot exert its influence upon you. So these citizens are enlightened persons. They know what is Krishna's position there. Attachment or attraction for Krishna is not blind. They are not ignorant fools. They are completely knowledgeable. So one of the important points to remember is Bhakti means knowledge. Bhakti means complete material detachment. Bhakti means pure heart. That is Bhakti. Bhakti is not just a word of speech. Bhakti is not just another material sentiment. In a heart filled with material desires, in a heart full of ignorance, that is not Bhakti. That may appear like Bhakti.

---
### Devotion, Krishna's Supremacy, Scriptural Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=194
**Verse References:** BG 9.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The supreme means nobody is equal to him and nobody is greater than him. There is no competition between Krishna and the other Devatas."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to define devotion, emphasizing the need to progress beyond sentiment to actual devotion filled with knowledge and detachment. He quotes Prabhupada, stating that Krishna is confirmed as the Supreme Lord in Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Samhita, and other Vedic literatures, and that no one is equal to or greater than Him.

Or that may be a beginning of one's expression of devotion to Krishna, but one has to make progress from the stage of sentiment to the stage of actual devotion filled with knowledge, detachment, transcendental feelings, etc. So Prabhupada explains in the purport, the supreme lord is Shri Krishna, is confirmed in Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Samhita, and other authorized Vedic literatures. No one is equal to him and no one is greater than him, and that is a verdict of all this knowledge, all the scriptures. He is the supreme. The supreme means nobody is equal to him and nobody is greater than him. There is no competition between Krishna and the other Devatas. Between Devatas there is competition, though there is hierarchy among Devatas, though there is hierarchy among Devatas, but still their position is never permanent. The Devata's position is not permanent.

---
### Dependence, Karma, Subordination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=291
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living entities are predominated Brahman, we are spirit, we are spiritual beings and Lord is spirit, supreme spirit, supremely spiritual being. But we are predominated, we are controlled, we are subordinate to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the constitutional difference between Krishna and the living entities, emphasizing that while we are part and parcel of Krishna, we are dependent and subordinate to Him, whereas He is the predominating absolute.

Constitutionally the influence of time and space is exerted upon the dependent living entities. We are all dependent in this material world. Those are taken birth by force of karma. We depend on the material nature and there is Kala, powerful Kala which restricts our duration of life in this body which limits everything what we can do. Now we as living beings we are part and parcel of Krishna but what is our position? The living entities are predominated Brahman, we are spirit, we are spiritual beings and Lord is spirit, supreme spirit, supremely spiritual being. But we are predominated, we are controlled, we are subordinate to Krishna. Whereas the supreme Lord is a predominating absolute. He is always the Lord, the controller, the master, the enjoyer, the supremely powerful. That we have to remember.

---
### Illusion, Krishna Consciousness, Spiritual Nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=385
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We are in illusion because we forget who we are, we forget who is Krishna, we forget our spiritual nature. We think we are product of matter. We identify with the body. This is illusion."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the two states of existence: Krishna consciousness, where we are protected by Krishna, and illusion, where we are governed by the stringent laws of material nature. He explains that illusion arises from forgetting our true spiritual nature and identifying with the material body.

As soon as we forget this clear fact, we are at once in illusion. There are only two states of existence for every living being. Either we are in Krishna, Krishna consciousness, where we are properly situated under the full protection of Krishna, Krishna providing us with everything without even ever asking, or we are in the shelter of the illusory energy and the illusory energy is very stringent laws which are governing every single being. We are in illusion because we forget who we are, we forget who is Krishna, we forget our spiritual nature. We think we are product of matter. We identify with the body. This is illusion and threefold miseries constantly occur. We are constantly attacking the body, the mind. The clear consciousness of the cognizant living being is God consciousness. Cognizant means we are always full of consciousness, aware, we are always aware. That awareness is distinctly a quality of the living being as opposed to the body, matter. Matter doesn't have cognizant, doesn't have awareness, doesn't have consciousness.

---
### Krishna Consciousness, Subordination, Gratitude, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=493
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Eternally we are subordinate. Eternally we should be grateful to Krishna for everything."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that we are not matter but spiritual beings, and in Krishna consciousness, we bow down to Him in all circumstances, eternally grateful and subordinate. He then introduces the seventh verse, explaining that Krishna is everything for His devotees.

So we are not matter. We are not this material body. We are not product of material nature. No. In God consciousness or Krishna consciousness, one bows down unto Him, Him means Krishna, the Supreme Lord, in all circumstances. Eternally we are subordinate. Eternally we should be grateful to Krishna for everything. The seventh verse: tvameva mātā atha suhṛt patih pitā tvam sad-gurur naḥ paramam ca daivatam yasyān vṛtyākṛtino bhavu vimā. The meaning of this seventh verse is O creator of the universe, you are our mother, well-wisher, lord, father, spiritual master and worshipable deity. Krishna is everything for his devotees. So that is being highlighted here. By following in your footsteps, we have become successful in every respect. We pray therefore that you continue to bless us with your mercy.

---
### Krishna's Plan, Success, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Success in life, especially in human life, is not just about success. When we follow Krishna's instructions, as he has given us advice in the Bhagavad Gita."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada, explaining that Krishna, as the creator, plans for the good of all living beings and advises them to follow His good advice for success in all spheres of life. He emphasizes that success in human life comes from following Krishna's instructions in the Bhagavad Gita.

So Sri Lapa Rāpāda explains in the purport. The all-good personality of Godhead, being the creator of the universe, also plans for the good of all good living beings. See actually Krishna does not make a distinction between different living beings in this world as good and bad, but we become good or bad according to our good and bad, according to our attitude, according to our choice, the choice we make. So he has all good plans for us, Krishna. The good living beings are advised by the Lord to follow his good advice, and by doing so, they become successful in all spheres of life. Success in life, especially in human life, is not just about success. When we follow Krishna's instructions, as he has given us advice in the Bhagavad Gita.

---
### Krishna Worship, Demigods, Atheism, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=643
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the only worshipable Lord for everyone. Even the devatas worship Krishna as was explained in the previous verse."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that there is no need to worship any deity but Krishna, as even the demigods worship Him. He contrasts this with those who worship demigods for material desires or those who are atheistic and depend on their own abilities, failing to realize that everything comes from Krishna.

There is no need to worship any deity but the Lord. This material world is such a complex place. People have so many material desires and they sometimes think that the devatas can fulfill my material desires better than Krishna, or they are ignorant of Krishna, so therefore they don't even approach Krishna. They only go to demigods, or some of them who are completely atheistic, they don't even go to any demigods or Krishna. They simply depend upon their own personal strength, personal abilities, personal intelligence, without realizing all these personal things are given by Krishna only through material nature. So foolish people are like that. So Prabhupada says there is no need to worship any deity other than Krishna. Krishna is the only worshipable Lord for everyone. Even the devatas worship Krishna as was explained in the previous verse. Brahma, Shiva, Indra, the topmost devatas. Everyone, all the devatas are Vishnu Bhakta.

---
### Material and Spiritual Life, Krishna's Blessings, Eternal Relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=774
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Both materially and spiritually we are successful simply by worshipping Krishna, by depending on Krishna, by following Krishna's instructions even in material life."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights Prabhupada's point that Krishna is competent to bestow all kinds of blessings for both material and spiritual success. He emphasizes that there is no need for separate endeavors for material success, as both are achieved by worshipping Krishna and following His instructions. He also stresses the importance of understanding our eternal relationship with Krishna, which is not based on the body like material relationships.

Vishnu Bhakta Smruto Daiva Asuras Tad Viparyaya. Asuras are non-devotees. All the 33 crore devatas in this one universe are all devotees of Vishnu or Krishna. So if they are worshipping Krishna, why shouldn't we worship Krishna? They derive everything they have including their power from Krishna. So we should understand Krishna's position is absolute Lord, not relatively situated as Lord sometimes. No. The demigods are relatively situated. The Lord is all-powerful and if he is satisfied by our obedience unto his Lord's feet, he is competent to bestow upon us all kinds of blessings for the successful execution of both our material and spiritual lives. This is an important point Srila Prabhupada is making. For spiritual life, somebody may think I may have to worship Krishna, but for material life, they think they have to worship some devatas or they have to work hard in material existence. No. There is no need of any separate endeavor for success in material life either. Both materially and spiritually we are successful simply by worshipping Krishna, by depending on Krishna, by following Krishna's instructions even in material life. And it is important to remember for attaining spiritual existence, the human form is a chance for all of us to understand our eternal relation with God. We have an eternal relationship with Krishna. It is not like the material relationships which are based on this body. They end with the body. Material relationships end with the body. Spiritual relationship is with Krishna and that is eternal. That is never based on this body. It is actually the relationship between the soul and this Supreme Lord Krishna. It is eternal to Supreme Soul. That relationship. Because soul is eternal. Krishna is eternal. Therefore relationship is also eternal. Soul to soul. Our relation with Him is eternal. It can either be broken or vanquished.

---
### Eternal Relationship, Revival, Scriptures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VXAmI17rayg?t=873
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Material relationships are broken. Spiritual relationship with Krishna is never broken. It is just forgotten for the time being."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that material relationships are broken, but the spiritual relationship with Krishna is never broken, only forgotten. He explains that this relationship can be revived by the grace of the Lord if we follow His injunctions, which are revealed in the scriptures, and that the scriptures provide instructions on how to revive our memory of Krishna.

Material relationships are broken. Spiritual relationship with Krishna is never broken. It is just forgotten for the time being. Now somebody may be forgetful of Krishna, but it can be revived also by the grace of the Lord if we follow His injunctions. If we follow His instructions, it can be revived. And these injunctions are revealed in the scriptures of all times and all places. Shastra means that which gives us the instructions, how we can revive our memory of Krishna. That is what is actually explained in the Shastra. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.11.8 | 29-06-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### invocation, mantra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudireet"
**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and mantras, invoking blessings and paying respects to various deities and spiritual figures.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyanan Janishalakaya Chakshurun Miritam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadhadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudireet.

---
### darshan, devotion, transcendental form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=53
**Verse References:** SB 1.11.8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's form is completely transcendental and it can be seen only by pure devotees, those who have the vision to perceive transcendental form."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.11.8), explaining that it describes the residents of Dwaraka welcoming Krishna and expressing their good fortune at seeing Him; he contrasts this with the impersonalists who cannot perceive Krishna's transcendental form.

In this session we will be discussing from the 8th verse of the 11th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam: Aho Sanatha Bhavatasma Yadvayam Traivishta Panam Apidura Darshanam Prema Smita Snigdha Nireeksha Nananam Pashyema Rupam Tavasarva Saubhagam. The meaning of this verse is, Oh, it is our good luck that we have come again today under your protection, by your presence for your Lordship. The Lordship rarely visits even the denizens of heaven. Now it is possible for us to look into your smiling face which is full of affectionate glances. We can now see your transcendental form full of all auspiciousness. This is a verse spoken by the residents of Dwaraka who had come to receive Krishna when Krishna was entering Dwaraka after sounding his conch shell. All the saints were saying to Krishna, all the citizens who are waiting to have darshan of Krishna, they came running, leaving whatever they were doing. And they are so attached to Krishna is very evident from all these descriptions here. So what is their reaction to be able to see Krishna once again? They are telling it is our good luck that we have come again today under your protection, by your presence. So devotees are always under the protection of Krishna and they are able to directly see Krishna. That is special because Krishna's form is completely transcendental and it can be seen only by pure devotees, those who have the vision to perceive transcendental form.

---
### impersonalism, materialism, Krishna's form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The impersonalists and the materialists, when they see Krishna, when Krishna appears and gives darshan as an incarnation, as Krishna, Rama, Narasimha, Varaha, they see that form and they think that form is material. This is the biggest mistake made by non-devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of impersonalism, explaining that impersonalists believe the absolute truth has no form, qualities, or abode; they are ignorant and unable to see Krishna's transcendental form, even when He appears on Earth, mistakenly considering His form to be material.

There are transcendentalists who don't perceive spiritual form. They are called impersonalists because their conception of the absolute truth is that the absolute truth is impersonal. Impersonal means no form, no spiritual qualities, no spiritual form, no spiritual name, no spiritual paraphernalia, no spiritual abode. So they are totally impersonalists. They are totally ignorant and therefore unable to see the transcendental form of the Lord even when he appears on this earth planet. So does it mean they can't see Krishna at all as a person when he comes? No, Krishna becomes visible, but they think the form of Krishna is material. That's what they think. The impersonalists and the materialists, when they see Krishna, when Krishna appears and gives darshan as an incarnation, as Krishna, Rama, Narasimha, Varaha, they see that form and they think that form is material. This is the biggest mistake made by non-devotees.

---
### devotion, transcendental form, auspiciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=250
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just thinking of Krishna's transcendental form, seeing Krishna's transcendental form, remembering Krishna's transcendental form, hearing descriptions of Krishna's transcendental form, describing oneself, Krishna's transcendental form, all these are meant to bring all auspiciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the perception of devotees with that of impersonalists, stating that pure devotees know Krishna's form is not material; the residents of Dwaraka are pure devotees who understand Krishna's transcendental nature and appreciate His presence; Krishna's smiling face and affectionate glances demonstrate His loving relationship with His devotees, and His form is full of auspiciousness.

As far as devotees are concerned, pure devotees, they see Krishna, but they also know Krishna's form is not material. They know Krishna's form is not material. So therefore, they are telling here the residents of Dwaraka, they are all pure devotees, every one of them. So they are telling that Krishna never visits the denizens of heaven, but he stays in Dwaraka and just he had gone little time for settling things in Hastinapura. So the citizens were missing him. They were missing him. And what are they able to see now? They are able to see Krishna's smiling face. Krishna is very, very kind to his pure devotees. So therefore, when Krishna sees them, they are able to see Krishna's smiling face. Now, further it is said, Krishna's smiling face is full of affectionate glances. That is because the relationship between Krishna and each and every devotee is one of loving affection. Therefore, Krishna's glance upon his devotees is full of affection. And they are also telling, we can now see your transcendental form. Krishna's form is fully transcendental and this is only known to the pure devotees. Nobody else knows this. This transcendental form is described here as full of all auspiciousness. Sarva Saubhagam.

---
### devotional service, demigods, Shwetadvipa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=388
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By devotional service, a devotee directly sees Krishna, just like I see you or you are seeing me. Exactly like that, they are seeing Krishna face to face, which is impossible to be done even by the denizens of higher planets, the demigods, including Lord Brahma, the topmost demigod."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada, emphasizing that Krishna's eternal personal form can only be seen by pure devotees through devotional service; even demigods like Brahma cannot directly see Vishnu, who resides in Shwetadvipa, a spiritual planet within the material world, and must instead transmit their messages from the shore of the ocean of milk.

It is full of all auspiciousness. Just thinking of Krishna's transcendental form, seeing Krishna's transcendental form, remembering Krishna's transcendental form, hearing descriptions of Krishna's transcendental form, describing oneself, Krishna's transcendental form, all these are meant to bring all auspiciousness. In the Purport, Srila Prabhupada says, The Lord in his eternal personal form can be seen only by pure devotees. The Lord is never impersonal, but he is the supreme absolute personality of Godhead. Krishna is not impersonal. Krishna has an impersonal feature but that is subordinate to the personality. And Srila Prabhupada says, This absolute personality of Krishna is possible to be visited by devotional service face to face. By devotional service, a devotee directly sees Krishna, just like I see you or you are seeing me. Exactly like that, they are seeing Krishna face to face, which is impossible to be done even by the denizens of higher planets, the demigods, including Lord Brahma, the topmost demigod. It is described by Prabhupada here, that when Brahmaji and other demigods want to consult Lord Vishnu, the plenary portion of Lord Krishna, where does Vishnu stay? Vishnu resides in this material world in a special planet of his own, which is a completely spiritual planet, Shwetadvipa. And the demigods, when they have some problem, they visit Lord Vishnu in this personal planet, but they cannot enter that planet. They have to wait on the shore of the ocean of milk where Lord Vishnu is lying on Shwetadvipa. This ocean of milk and the Shwetadvipa planet are the replica of Vaikunthaloka within the universe. Vaikunthaloka is far beyond the spiritual sky even. But that Vaikunthaloka descends when Lord Vishnu comes into this world.

---
### pure devotees, material contamination, fructive activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=549
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotees means their heart is completely free from all material contamination. Material contamination is of two kinds. One is called material contamination of fructive activities."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that neither Brahma nor other demigods can enter Shwetadvipa, but pure devotees in Dwaraka can see the Lord face to face due to their lack of material contamination; he defines pure devotees as those whose hearts are free from material contamination, which includes fructive activities (desire for material benefits) and empirical philosophical speculation.

So, neither Brahmaji nor the demigods like Indra can enter into this island of Shwetadvipa. But they can stand on the shore of the ocean of milk and transmit their message to Lord Vishnu, who is known as Kshirodaka Shai Vishnu. Kshirodaka means milk ocean. Shai means he is lying down on that milk ocean, on Shesha Naga. So, they can transmit message standing on the shore of the milk ocean. So, when they look at the milk ocean, they can't see anything of the island on the milk ocean. They can't see that. They can just see the vast expanse of the milk ocean and they transmit their message to Lord Vishnu. Therefore, the Lord is rarely seen by them, but the inhabitants of Dwaraka, because of their being pure devotees, without any tinge of material contamination, of fructive activities and empirical philosophical speculation, can see the Lord face to face by the grace of the Lord. What's the meaning of this? Pure devotees means their heart is completely free from all material contamination. Material contamination is of two kinds. One is called material contamination of fructive activities. Fructive activities is the desire for doing material activities which will give some material benefit. That's called fructive activity. That's a contamination in the consciousness of a conditioned soul of this world. All of us, to different degrees, we have this fructive mentality. We will not do any action if it does not give us some material benefit in any form. We think that it is useless to do some work without expectation of any positive material gain.

---
### devotion, philosophical speculation, absolute truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=695
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as the heart is contaminated by material contamination, one can never know the absolute truth. It's impossible. It's impossible to know the absolute truth."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts devotees, who work exclusively to please Krishna, with those who engage in fructive activities; he then discusses empirical philosophical speculation, where people try to understand what they cannot perceive through imagination and material methods, emphasizing that the absolute truth cannot be realized through such contaminated methods.

Whereas, devotees, they perform actions, they work for Krishna, exclusively for pleasing Krishna. They don't do any material activities. Pure devotees never do any material activities. So therefore, their heart is described as free from the tinge of fructive action. And the second material contamination in the heart is the empirical philosophical speculation. Speculation, you understand, people try to guess or try to conjecture whatever they can't see, whatever they can't perceive, whatever they can't know by direct perception, or even they cannot conceive using the descriptions in the scriptures. They can't relate with it. Then they try to imagine in their mind, by their fertile brain. This is called speculation. And it is philosophical speculation is by taking help of the philosophical descriptions in the scriptures. Philosophical descriptions in the scriptures. And empiric is something which is verifiable by material methods. So impersonalists and some of the materialists and some philosophers, material philosophers, all of them indulge in this empiric philosophical speculation. And they think that they can realize the absolute truth or have a understanding of the absolute truth by this method. But the fact is the absolute truth can never be realized by any of such methods, material methods. As long as the heart is contaminated by material contamination, one can never know the absolute truth. It's impossible. It's impossible to know the absolute truth.

---
### bhakti, devotional service, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KHmQazc7XwI?t=833
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Reviving our natural constitutional position is only possible by bhakti, by practice of devotional service and no other way."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that the original state of living entities is to be pure in heart and see the Lord face to face; the current condition of forgetfulness is a deviation, and reviving our natural position is only possible through bhakti, or devotional service, under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master.

So the original state of the living entities is to be always pure in the heart and thereby be always be able to see the Lord face to face in his personal abode and reside there in loving relationship with the Lord. That's the normal condition. This present condition in the material world is a condition of forgiveness, condition of forgetfulness of Krishna. So, reviving our natural constitutional position is only possible by bhakti, by practice of devotional service and no other way. That is what Srila Prabhupada concludes this purport by telling, if you engage in pure devotional service under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master, you can revive your original position. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.11.9 | 02-07-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### separation from Krishna, attachment to Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oEQh4FPv9Aw?t=58
**Verse References:** SB 1.11.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every moment seemed like millions of years when Krishna was absent from Dwaraka."
**Summary:** The residents of Dwaraka express their feelings of missing Krishna, emphasizing their deep attachment and the perceived length of time during his absence.

In this session, we will be discussing the 9th verse of the 11th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. "Yari Ambujaksh Apasasarabho Bhavan Kurun Madhun Vatha Suhradidrikshaya Tatravdha Koti Pratimakshanobhavet Ravim Vinakshnor Ivanastavachyuta." The meaning of this verse is, "O lotus-eyed lord, whenever you go away to Mathura, Vrindavan, or Hastinapura to meet your friends and relatives, every moment of your absence seems like a million years. O infallible one, at that time our eyes become useless as if bereft of the sun." This is a very beautiful verse where the residents of Dwaraka, who have come to receive Krishna when Krishna arrived in Dwaraka from Hastinapura after a long period of absence, are expressing their feelings, how they were missing Krishna. Every moment seemed like millions of years when Krishna was absent from Dwaraka for the Dwaraka. That helps us understand how much they are attached to Krishna.

---
### senses, Krishna as the sun, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oEQh4FPv9Aw?t=148
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without him, all our knowledge is either false or partial."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that our senses are limited and dependent on Krishna, who is compared to the sun. Without Krishna, our knowledge is incomplete and we are in illusion.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, we are all proud of our material senses for making experiments to determine the existence of God, but we forget that our senses are not absolute by themselves. What is the meaning of this statement? Our senses are not absolute. This means that the senses can act only under certain conditions. Absolute means unconditional. For example, our eyes, as long as the sunshine is there, our eyes are not absolute. Our eyes are useful to a certain extent, but in the absence of sunshine, the eyes are useless. Lord Sri Krishna, being the primeval Lord, the supreme truth, is compared to the sun. Without him, all our knowledge is either false or partial. The opposite of the sun is darkness, and similarly, the opposite of Krishna is Maya, or illusion. The devotees of the Lord can see everything in true perspective due to the light disseminated by Krishna.

---
### Krishna consciousness, devotee, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oEQh4FPv9Aw?t=260
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotee means always situated in transcendental knowledge, never in ignorance."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the analogy of light and darkness, explaining that devotees in Krishna consciousness can see properly due to the light of Krishna, while those in illusion cannot. Pure devotees are always in transcendental knowledge and never in ignorance because they are always Krishna conscious.

So here is an example given by Prabhupada, rather the example given in the Bhagavatam itself, that just like the eyes are useless when there is no light, similarly, in the absence of Krishna, or in the absence of Krishna consciousness, when we are in illusion, when we are in mind, or in Maya, we are unable to see Krishna, or we are unable to see any things spiritual. Whereas the devotees who are always in Krishna consciousness, they are able to see everything properly due to the light disseminated by Lord Krishna. So, Srila Prabhupada explains, by the grace of the Lord, the pure devotee cannot be in the darkness of ignorance. Devotee, pure devotee means always situated in transcendental knowledge, never in ignorance. That is the characteristic of a pure devotee because the pure devotee is always Krishna conscious. Therefore, Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, "Man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru." Always think of me. If we always think of Krishna, we are always situated in Krishna consciousness, and in that way, we'll never be in ignorance. We'll always be situated in perfect knowledge.

---
### Krishna's energies, material energy, conditioned souls
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oEQh4FPv9Aw?t=388
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything in existence is only Krishna and his energies."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada continues to explain the importance of always being in the sight of Lord Krishna to see ourselves and the Lord with his energies. Everything in existence is Krishna and his energies, which are transcendental and spiritual. Material energy is Krishna's energy, but it appears material to conditioned souls who are forgetful of Krishna.

So, Srila Prabhupada continues to explain, therefore, it is necessary that we must always be in the sight of Lord Krishna so that we can see both ourselves and the Lord with his different energies. Elsewhere it is explained that everything in existence is only Krishna and his energies. Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead, and he has many varieties of energies. All his energies are transcendental, spiritual, because he is complete spirit. So since his energies emanate from him, they cannot be anything material. They are all spiritual. But what is described as material energy, which is Krishna's another type of energy, is only material for the conditioned souls, for those who are forgetful of Krishna, for those who are in the darkness of ignorance. For them, this material energy acts to put them in illusion. But factually, this material energy is also Krishna's own energy for the purpose of the conditioned souls. This we have to carefully understand.

---
### forgetfulness of Krishna, material contamination, independent enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oEQh4FPv9Aw?t=514
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as we are desirous of material enjoyment, as long as we want to be Krishna, we cannot know Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that in a state of forgetfulness of Krishna, we are placed in the material energy, which is a manifestation of Krishna's spiritual energy. This material world is Krishna's material energy, but when we forget Krishna or desire independent enjoyment, we become entangled. As long as we desire material enjoyment and want to be Krishna, we cannot know Krishna.

In the state of forgetfulness of Krishna, we are placed in the material energy of Krishna's spiritual energy, the internal potency, the spiritual world. So, this material world is Krishna's material energy. But when we forget Krishna, or we desire independent enjoyment, or we become because of our desire to seek independent enjoyment, independent of Krishna, we want to be Krishna. So, as long as we are desirous of material enjoyment, as long as we want to be Krishna, we cannot know Krishna. So, we have to come out of this contamination.

---

So, to remove the material contamination, to purify the heart of all material contamination, is the process of bhakti. Even the chanting of Hare Krishna is described by Krishna. We begin cleansing our heart of material contamination. Now, this material contamination is accumulated over millions of lifetimes. So, only when we take to Krishna consciousness and the heart of our heart is cleansing of all material activity of our heart is accumulated of material activity of our heart is accumulated of our heart is of material activity of material off the heart is our heart is accumulated of material activity of our heart is accumulated of the material variable of material of the reaction.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oEQh4FPv9Aw?t=799
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even now in your present condition within your heart you are situated as pure spirit in pure Krishna consciousness as a pure devotee of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of tolerating karma and understanding our spiritual identity. Even in our present condition, we are situated as pure spirit in Krishna consciousness within our hearts. By penetrating the layers of coverings and entering the core of our heart through bhakti, we can realize our spiritual existence. We must cleanse our heart before we can enter the inner heart and remember our spiritual realm. Just like the residents of Dwarka are always Krishna Conscious, similarly we should also become Krishna Conscious by constantly chanting Krishna's name, hearing about his glories, hearing the pastimes of Krishna from the Bhagavatam, like that performing more and more Krishna Conscious activities. And in this way, if we become Krishna Conscious, we can very quickly cleanse our heart and become situated on the spiritual platform even while in this material world we can be in spiritual consciousness and thereby in the spiritual world.

Are due to our own misdeeds in the past so learn to tolerate them while you are in the material world with a material body also it helps if you try to understand that you are not this material body you don't belong to the material world even now in your present condition within your heart you are situated as pure spirit in pure Krishna consciousness as a pure devotee of Krishna so you just have to penetrate the layers of coverings and enter your own inner heart in a portion of the heart the core of the heart and the process is the process of bhakti so in the beginning stages it is a process of cleansing the heart so till the heart is cleansed we cannot enter the inner heart of the heart and enter the inner heart of the heart and we cannot remember the core of our heart we cannot enter the spiritual realm within our heart our own spiritual existence we cannot get into so we have to wait for the cleansing in our body to happen so therefore we have to learn fro readiness in life. We have to come to Krishna Consciousness by the process of Bhakti and that is what is being explained by Prabhupada in this purport. Just like the residents of Dwarka are always Krishna Conscious, similarly we should also become Krishna Conscious by constantly chanting Krishna's name, hearing about his glories, hearing the pastimes of Krishna from the Bhagavatam, like that performing more and more Krishna Conscious activities. And in this way, if we become Krishna Conscious, we can very quickly cleanse our heart and become situated on the spiritual platform even while in this material world we can be in spiritual consciousness and thereby in the spiritual world. ## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.10-1 | 6-08-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Brahmastra, Krishna's Potency, Devatas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oTyiL8KO0dA?t=30
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That Brahmastra, the Lord vanquished as easily as the sun evaporates a drop of dew."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Krishna effortlessly vanquished the Brahmastra aimed at Pariksit, comparing it to the sun evaporating a dewdrop, and emphasizes Krishna's unlimited power compared to the limited power of the devatas.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namah Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Vanarottamam Deviim Saraswatiim Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 10th and 11th verses of the 12th chapter of 1st Canto Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is: The Lord was thus engaged in vanquishing the radiation of the Brahmastra, just as the sun evaporates a drop of dew. He was observed by the child who thought about who he was. While Pariksit is being given darshan by the Lord, what was the Lord doing? He was engaged in vanquishing the radiation of the Brahmastra. He was protecting child Pariksit from being destroyed by the Brahmastra. The Brahmastra, its power, is so immense. It can destroy one whole universe. That is the power of Brahmastra. That Brahmastra, the Lord vanquished as easily as the sun evaporates a drop of dew, one drop of water that is condensed on a blade of grass. When the sun rises in the morning and dispels the darkness and even the cold that's in winter, when water vapor condenses and a droplet is sitting on a blade of grass, and the sun rises, it can evaporate the drop of dew, not only one drop, but millions and billions and trillions of drops of water everywhere. The sun will immediately evaporate. So, like that, the sun will evaporate immediately. Like that for the all powerful lord, unlimited lord, it is very very insignificant to vanquish the radiation of the brahmastra because even though the brahmastra is very very very very powerful, it has got so much of power, destructive power, but it is limited and Krishna's strength or Krishna's potency potencies are unlimited. This difference always exists between Krishna and everyone else.

---
### Krishna's Supremacy, Mayavadi Philosophy, Qualitative vs. Quantitative
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oTyiL8KO0dA?t=297
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is mentioned herein as a supreme living being, unlimited by time and space, whereas every living being is limited by time and space."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the 11th verse, emphasizing Krishna's all-pervading nature and refuting the Mayavadi philosophy that equates all living beings with God, highlighting the difference between the Lord and individual souls in terms of limitations.

The 11th verse: the meaning of this 11th verse, while thus being observed by the child, the supreme lord, personality of godhead, the super soul of everyone and the protector of the righteous who stretches in all directions and who's unlimited by time and space disappeared at once. Krishna's work is over. He vanquished the brahmastra, saved Pariksit, he gave him darshan and immediately he disappeared. So Srila Prabhupada explains the description of the Lord means he is all-pervading super soul bhagavan, he is a supreme personality of godhead, the protector of the righteous, the supreme lord, all kinds of opulences including power, that supreme lord, while he was being observed by the child, he suddenly disappeared. So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, child Pariksit was not observing a living being who is limited by time and space. There is a gulf of difference between the Lord and the individual living being. This should particularly be noted by the mayavadi philosophers. They always think that every living being is ultimately god, brahman or supreme or absolute truth or atma, whatever term they may use, they think there is no difference between god and each one of us, but that is wrong. The Lord is mentioned herein as a supreme living being, unlimited by time and space, whereas every living being is limited by time and space. This one difference, if you remember, immediately we can understand the difference between the Supreme Lord Krishna and every other living being, in whatever position, whatever situation, whatever power, whatever position, no living being can compare with the Lord. Even though a living being is qualitatively one with the Lord, qualitatively one means in quality, we are spirit, in quality, the Lord is also completely spiritual. Quantitatively, there is a great difference between the Supreme Soul and the common individual soul. It's a very, very, very, very big difference. You cannot conceive what's the difference. Unlimited versus limited, the difference.

---
### All-Pervasiveness, Consciousness, Super Soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oTyiL8KO0dA?t=495
**Verse References:** BG 13.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has entered into my body, your body, everyone's body, everybody. That's the difference between Krishna and each one of us."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of all-pervasiveness, contrasting the limited consciousness of individual souls within their bodies with Krishna's unlimited consciousness that pervades all of space, time, and living beings.

In the Bhagavad Gita, both the living beings and the supreme being are said to be all-pervading. That is the description of the Lord. The living being is described as all-pervading, the spirit soul. But, there is a difference between these two. There is a difference between these two kinds of all-pervasiveness. What's the difference? A common living being or soul can be all-pervading within his own limited body. I am present throughout my body. That's why if an ant bites my toe, immediately I come to know. Or an ant bites somewhere in my forehead, I immediately come to know. Because, even though I am a very tiny spirit, even though I am a very tiny spirit soul within this body, by my consciousness, I am present everywhere. My consciousness is spread throughout my body. Your consciousness is spread throughout your body. Even Brahma's consciousness is spread throughout his body. But, what about Krishna? What about Krishna's awareness or consciousness? It is spread everywhere. In the spiritual world, in the material world, in every universe, in every planet, in every living being, in every space, in all time. No limitation. The supreme living being is all-pervading in all space and all time. You see, all space and all time. A common living being cannot extend its influence over another common living being by its all-pervasion. Very simple example. I am within this body by even using yogic power. I cannot extend my influence over another person's body. Not possible. But the supreme super-soul, the personality of God, is unlimitedly able to exert its influence over all places and all times and over all living beings. See, Krishna enters into the heart of every living being. I have entered into this body. I am situated in this body. Krishna has entered into my body, your body, everyone's body, everybody. That's the difference between Krishna and each one of us.

---
### Surrender, Protection, Righteousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oTyiL8KO0dA?t=690
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anyone who is a surrendered soul unto the Supreme is righteous, and he is specifically protected by the Lord in all circumstances."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna's all-pervasiveness allows him to appear anywhere, emphasizing the protection offered to surrendered souls and the indirect protection extended to the unrighteous through the rectification of their sins by Krishna's external energy.

Because he is all-pervasive, unlimited by time and space, he can appear even within the womb of the mother of child Parikshit. Try to understand this point. He can appear anywhere, wherever he pleases, wherever he wants to. We cannot do so. We are limited, always limited, where we can go, how much we can exert a certain influence. That also, empowered by Krishna. Empowered by Krishna. Now, the Lord is described as all-pervading through all time and space. He is also described here as the protector of the righteous, Dharmakut. Anyone who is a surrendered soul unto the Supreme is righteous, and he is specifically protected by the Lord in all circumstances. So, if we have surrendered to Krishna, we are protected by Krishna always, in all situations, all circumstances. Now, Srila Prabhupada says a very important point here. The Lord is the indirect protector of the unrighteous also. Directly he protects all those who are surrendered souls who are righteous, but he is also the indirect protector of the unrighteous, those who are not righteous. Now, for he rectifies their sins through his external energy. The external energy is controlling all the unrighteous souls to rectify their mentality of being rebellious to the Supreme, of their mentality of not surrendering to Krishna. So that, when they surrender, they become completely protected by the Lord. They become delivered from this material existence. They are free from all material miseries, material bondage.

---
### Ten Directions, Appearance and Disappearance, Darshan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oTyiL8KO0dA?t=831
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So when Krishna appears, he becomes visible to us right in that place where he appears. And when he disappears, he becomes invisible. He is there, present, because he is everywhere. But he becomes invisible. That's all."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining Krishna's presence in all ten directions, his ability to appear and disappear at will, and the concept of darshan, emphasizing that while Krishna is always present, he becomes visible only when he chooses to reveal himself.

The Lord is mentioned herein as one who is dressed in ten directions. Dasha ma sasya mishato dasha ma sasya, who is dressed in all ten directions. That means, he is present everywhere and can appear and disappear at his will. What are these ten directions? The eight directions you know, east, west, north, south, north-east, north-west, south-east, south-west, and up and down. So these are called the ten directions. Now he is present in everywhere, in all directions, in all ten directions. This means, dressed with garments on ten sides, up and down. He is present everywhere and can appear and disappear at his will from everywhere and anywhere. Now, his disappearance from the side of child Parikhit does not mean that he appeared on the spot from any other place. He was present there. And even after disappearance, he was present there, although invisible to the eyes of the child. So when Krishna appears, he becomes visible to us right in that place where he appears. And when he disappears, he becomes invisible. He is there, present, because he is everywhere. But he becomes invisible. That's all. This material covering of the effulgent firmament, firmament in the sky, is also something like a womb of the Mother Nature. And we are put into the womb by the Lord, the Father of all living beings. So he is present everywhere, even in the material womb of Mother Durga. And those who are deserving can see the Lord. He is present wherever we have our awareness in the material world also. He is present. But we can see him only when he gives darshan, when he becomes visible, not otherwise. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### devotee, birth, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/72dtwrrOxKI?t=58
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.12
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that we can also become pure devotees and perfect our lives very very easily simply by following in their footsteps."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of Emperor Pariksit's birth, emphasizing his inherent devotion and the importance of following the example of pure devotees to perfect one's own life.

In this session, we will be discussing from the twelfth verse of the twelfth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tata Sarva Gunodarke Sanukula Grahodaye Jagne Vamsadharapandur Bhuyapandur Ivaujasa. The meaning of this verse is, thereupon, when all the good signs of the zodiac gradually evolved, the heir apparent of Pandu, who would be exactly like him in prowess, took birth. So this is the chapter describing the birth of Emperor Pariksit. And his birth is very special because by birth he is a pure devotee. And that's the reason he was protected in the womb. He was protected in the womb by the love of his mother, by Krishna himself personally. And throughout his life, he was conducting himself as a pure devotee. Such pure devotees are directly coming from the spiritual world. They have got the purpose of life as the mission of the divine. And therefore, such personalities are described in Srimad Bhagavatam as ideal devotees whose footsteps we should all follow. So that we can also become pure devotees and perfect our lives very very easily simply by following in their footsteps.

---
### astrology, astronomy, science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/72dtwrrOxKI?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "These calculations as Srila Prabhupada says here are factual. They are not some theories based on some speculation which are some sentimental people's belief systems. No, these are cent percent true and they are all factual."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on the factual nature of astronomical calculations and astrology, emphasizing that they are based on a complete and perfect science called Jyotir Veda, not mere speculation.

Now Pariksit's birth is happening in a very very favorable astronomical situation. The birth itself is like that and Srila Prabhupada will explain the reason for the birth being very special. Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, astronomical calculations of stellar influences upon a living being are not suppositions, but are factual as confirmed in Srimad Bhagavatam. There is a lot of speculation about what is astronomy, what is astrology and what are the stars or the different heavenly bodies whose movements are basis of a calculation done in astronomy as well as in astrology. A chart is prepared and based on the chart a learned astrologer can read and actually tell many things about the person taking birth in a particular time, in a particular place under the influence of these stars or these heavenly bodies appearing in the sky. So, these calculations as Srila Prabhupada says here are factual. They are not some theories based on some speculation which are some sentimental people's belief systems. No, these are cent percent true and they are all factual. And it is based on a complete science. Jyotir Veda, complete science, perfect science.

---
### karma, free will, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/72dtwrrOxKI?t=335
**Verse References:** BG 3.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "It is foolish to think that we are free, independent. We can do whatever we like. No, we cannot."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that living beings are controlled by the laws of nature, and that every action produces a reaction, binding us to the material world, except for actions performed on behalf of Vishnu.

So, Srila Prabhupada says further, every living being is controlled by the laws of nature at every minute. It is foolish to think that we are free, independent. We can do whatever we like. No, we cannot. There is some degree of freedom, but we have to act responsibly because there is control by the laws of nature every minute, just as a citizen is controlled by the influence of the state. Just like state laws are working, and if somebody is belonging to a particular state, he has to obey the laws. The state laws are grossly observed, but the laws of material nature being subtle to our gross understanding cannot be experienced grossly. How these influences are working, the astronomical influences, we cannot see with our eyes manifest in gross terms. As stated in the Bhagavad Gita, third chapter, ninth verse, every action of life produces another reaction which is binding upon us. Every action that we perform, every action has got a reaction according to the laws of nature, and that reaction is binding us to this material world. It is tying us down. We cannot escape the reaction. And it is also told in the same verse in the Bhagavad Gita, only those who are acting on behalf of Yajna or Vishnu are not bound by reactions.

---
### devotion, karma, higher authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/72dtwrrOxKI?t=472
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Only foolish, ignorant, arrogant, less intelligent, atheistic, demoniac people say there is no higher control."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of acting on behalf of Vishnu, emphasizing that devotees are not bound by karmic reactions, and contrasts this with the foolishness of denying higher control and the existence of laws of nature.

So there is a special facility given to human beings who are performing actions on behalf of Vishnu. Yajna. Yajna arthat karma naha it is called. Karma done. Actions done on behalf of Yajna. Yajna arthat. Such people. Who are they? They are devotees. Devotees of Vishnu. Devotees of the Supreme Lord. They are not bound by reactions because they do all work on behalf of Vishnu. So our Srila Prabhupada further says, our actions are judged by the higher authorities who are the agents of the Lord, and thus we are awarded bodies according to our activities. So many living beings in so many varieties of bodies. What is the basis somebody is born in a particular type of body? The basis is the judgment of the higher authorities according to the work we have performed in the previous life. So there is a definite law working. There is a definite basis. There are enforcers of all these laws, and they are not visible to our eyes. They cannot be perceived with our gross senses. Only foolish, ignorant, arrogant, less intelligent, atheistic, demoniac people say there is no higher control. I don't believe in astronomy. I don't believe in astrology. I don't believe in scriptures. Only such foolish people talk nonsense, rubbish. And even as they are talking, they don't realize that even their movement of the tongue is controlled. Next moment such a foolish person gets a paralytic stroke. He can't talk. He can't move his limbs. He cannot even utilize his body at all. And he is simply admitted to some hospital or he lies in some place completely like a vegetable. And then he speaks all kinds of rubbish as long as he is able to vibrate his tongue. You see, this is the reality. And we see it all around us all the time. All the time. So, the fact is, the laws of nature are there. They are being enforced by the devatas, the agents of the Supreme Lord Krishna, and they are binding upon us. Nobody is exception. Nobody is exception.

---
### body, imprisonment, astrology, misuse of science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/72dtwrrOxKI?t=678
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We are very proud of the body that we have, but we don't realize that this body is a prison for the soul, the real I, real self, for me. It is a prison."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the material body as a prison for the soul, emphasizing the importance of scriptures in enlightening us about this reality, and concludes by discussing the validity of astrology while acknowledging its potential for misuse by unqualified individuals.

The laws of nature are so subtle that every part of our body is influenced by the respective stars. And a living being obtains his working body to fulfill his terms of imprisonment. We are very proud of the body that we have, but we don't realize that this body is a prison for the soul, the real I, real self, for me. It is a prison. It is limiting me in so many ways because we do not know as spirit-soul what is the kind of freedom I have. That I do not know. I am totally ignorant of that reality. So, therefore, the scriptures are enlightening us, educating us, informing us, warning us that you are imprisoned in your material body. And it has got a basis. Why? Why are you imprisoned? To fulfill the term of imprisonment imposed by the higher authorities according to a set of laws of nature which are working, perfectly working. The state laws may not be always imposed perfectly. Some, few people may escape the state law, but nobody can escape the laws of nature. So, a man's destiny is therefore ascertained by the bird-time constellation of stars, and a factual horoscope is made by a learned astrologer. It is a great science. This astrological science is a great science, and misuse of a science does not make it useless. In recent times, it has been misused, abused, and ignorant people sometimes do get cheated by bogus astrologers who do not know the science, and they make money out of misleading people. So, that doesn't mean the science is wrong or the science is not valid. It is the person who doesn't know the science who is cheating people. It is a quack who cheats people, not the learned, enlightened, authorized doctor. So, there is a difference between a quack and a doctor, a good doctor. Similarly, there is a difference between a learned astrologer and a bogus astrologer. There is a big difference and we have to learn that. So, I will stop here.

---

### invocation, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Go1XucXQF6k?t=0
**Verse References:** —

### purification, Vedic culture, samskaras
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Go1XucXQF6k?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual process is the purification of the material contaminations."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 13th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining the Vedic tradition of Jatakarma, a purificatory process performed on the birth of a child, emphasizing its role in removing material contamination and reviving one's spiritual position.

In this session we will be discussing from the 13th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Tasyapreetamana Raja Vipraya Dhaumya Kripadibihi Jatakam Karayamasa Vachayitvacha Mangalam. The meaning of this verse is King Yudhishthira who was very satisfied with the birth of Maharaj Pariksit had the purificatory process of birth performed. Learned Brahmanas headed by Dhaumya and Kripa recited auspicious hymns. So this is a Vedic tradition, Vedic culture. On the birth of a child there is something called Jatakarma which is performed. It is one of the samskaras, purificatory processes. Purification by cultural method. The life of a human being in this material world is contaminated in so many ways. Material contamination is there. But if one becomes purified of all material contaminations, one revives his or her spiritual position. The spiritual process is the purification of the material contaminations. So this is a great science, it is called the Spiritual Science. And such samskaras, cultural purificatory processes are employed for purifying the material contamination.

So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport there is a need for a good and intelligent class of brahmanas who are expert in performing the purificatory processes prescribed in the system of varnashrama dharma. Varnashrama dharma is a scientific method of purification, a system of practicing Dharma, religion, religious processes by which every human being can become purified of all material contamination and revive their spiritual position. Unless such purificatory processes are performed there is no possibility of good population. How can we have a good population? How can we have a good population? How can we have a good population? How can we have a population nice citizens, responsible, well-behaved with good qualities who know the aim of life who are progressive in their thinking? How can we get such a population by performing such purificatory processes under the able guidance of the Buddha, with the help of learned Brahmanes, with the help of learned Brahma.

### Kali Yuga, brahmanas, qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Go1XucXQF6k?t=286
**Verse References:** BG 18.42
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So there is an acute scarcity of qualified, learned, gentle Brahmanas."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the degraded state of the population in Kali Yuga due to the lack of purificatory processes and the scarcity of qualified Brahmanas, emphasizing that many so-called Brahmanas lack the necessary qualifications as described in the Bhagavad Gita, including control of mind and senses, austerity, cleanliness, tolerance, simplicity, knowledge, realization, and faith.

In the age of Kali the population all over the world is of Shudra quality or lower for want of this purificatory process. So in Kali yuga according to the scriptures kalava shudra sambhava aha in Kali yuga most of the population which is born from the in Kali yuga most of the population which is born from the human population is of the shudra quality, means they are not having cultural purification. Now shudra quality means they are not refined, they are not purified, why? Because there is no purificatory process for purifying the child which is born, why? Because there is this acute scarcity of learned brahmanas. So such learned brahmanas are not easily available in Kali Yuga. Some so called brahmanas by birth are there in Kali Yuga but they are all bogus brahmanas because they are not having qualification, neither they are learned in the Vedic sciences, nor their behavior, their conduct, their character is up to the standard of Brahmanical prescription in the Shastra. In the Bhagavad Gita, it is clearly said, a Brahmana is qualified in so many ways. At least nine distinct qualifications a Brahmana should have. Shamo, Damo, Tapaha, Shoucham, Shanti, Arjavam, Evacha, Gyanam, Vigyanam, Astikyam. Nine qualifications for a Brahmana mentioned in the Bhagavad Gita. Control of the mind, control of the senses, performing austerities, Tapasya, Shoucham, maintaining internal and external cleanliness, Shoucham, Shanti, tolerant, Arjavam is simple, is not duplicitous, straightforward, honest in all his dealings with everyone, Arjavam, Evacha, Gyanam is having proper knowledge, Vigyanam is having proper realization of that knowledge of the Vedas, and Astikyam has got faith in the Vedic scriptures, is not simply reciting some hymns or verses like a parrot.
