### Pancharatrika system, spiritual upliftment, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Go1XucXQF6k?t=490
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual upliftment is never conditioned by higher or lower parentage."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while reviving the Vedic purification process is difficult in Kali Yuga, the Pancharatrika system is recommended as an alternative, especially for the Shudra class, enabling spiritual upliftment regardless of birth or background, emphasizing that it is specifically for spiritual purification and upliftment.

No, he is learned, he knows the science, he is able to apply that science in his own life, guide others how to apply that science, he knows the reality, he knows the truth, and he has got faith, he is not faithless, he is not faithless. He is not a blind follower, he is not a foolish person who actually is ignorant but poses as a Brahmana by cheating innocent people. Not such a Brahmana. So there is an acute scarcity of qualified, learned, gentle Brahmanas. Such Brahmanas are not there in Kali Yuga. It is not possible, however, to revive the Vedic process of purification in this age of Kali, but there is the Pancharatrika system also recommended for this age. See, this Varnashram. To revive that is very difficult in Kali Yuga because there are no proper facilities and there are no good Brahmanas to revive this. But there is another system recommended in the scriptures that is called Pancharatrika system specially for Kali Yuga. The Pancharatrika system acts on the Shudra class of men. Subtlety. Supposedly the population of Kali Yuga. So Pancharatrika is specially meant for the Shudra class of people. Those who are born without cultural purification, without samskara, such people, they can take advantage of the Pancharatrika system. And in this Pancharatrika system there is a prescribed purificatory process suitable for this age and the present time. You see, this is the arrangement of the Supreme Lord Krishna for us in Kali Yuga when there is a scarcity of Brahmanas, when there is no cultural purification happening before the birth of a child. Such a purification or purificatory process as prescribed in the Pancharatrika system is allowed only for spiritual purification, for spiritual upliftment and not for any other purpose. See, the purpose is also very clearly explained in the scriptures. Why this Pancharatrika system is given when already the scriptures describe a Varanashrama Dharma system? Why? Because it is necessary for spiritual upliftment of the population through this Pancharatrika method, which is possible that anyone from any kind of background can be uplifted to the spiritual platform directly without going through the complicated procedures of the Varanashrama system. Spiritual upliftment is never conditioned by higher or lower parentage.

### samskaras, Garbhadana samskara, science of conception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Go1XucXQF6k?t=695
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's not by chance some child is born to some parents. No. It is a complete science."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights Srila Prabhupada's point that spiritual upliftment is not dependent on parentage and discusses various samskaras, including Garbhadana, emphasizing its importance in purifying the consciousness of parents before conception, which is largely missing in Kali Yuga, and highlighting the scientific approach to conceiving children with good qualities.

See, Srila Prabhupada explained a very, very, very important point. If somebody is born without samskara or samskruta, culturally not refined, but irrespective of what is the status of the parents or the family in which one takes birth, one can be uplifted spiritually by the Pancharatrika system. This is the method of purification. After the Garbhadana purificatory process, there are certain other samskaras like Seeman Tonnayana, Sardh Bhakshana, etc. During the period of pregnancy and when the child is born, the first purificatory process is Jatakarma. See, before a child is conceived, the very first samskara is called Garbhadana samskara. Or, the parents combine to conceive a child by themselves purifying their consciousness of material contamination by observing their child, by observing certain specific practices. Now, that is missing in Kali Yuga. Garbhadana samskara is missing. They do not even know about it, that they can have a child with a good background if they are able to perform this Garbhadana samskara. They themselves become purified before they combine for conceiving a child. It's not accidental. It's not by chance some child is born to some parents. No. It is a complete science. What kind of child you want, accordingly there is a prescription in the scriptures. How parents can actually have a child with a good background.

### charity, dharma, scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9i2cTPvQgh0?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.12.14) about King Yudhishthira giving charity upon the birth of his son, emphasizing the importance of knowing how, when, and to whom charity should be given.

In this session we will be discussing from the 14th verse of the 12th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Hiranyam Gaam Mahim Gramaan Hasti Ashwaan Nirupatir Varan Pradat Svannam Chavipre Bhyaha Prajaatirthesa Tirthavit. The meaning of this verse is: Upon the birth of his son, the king, who knew how, where and when charity should be done, should be given, gave gold, land, villages, elephants, horses and good food grains to the brahmanas. Particularly in this verse it is said Prajaatirthesa Tirthavit. On the occasion of giving in charity on the birth of his son, Yudhishthira Maharaj, he gave charity because he knew Tirthavit. He knew how to give charity, to whom charity should be given and when it should be given.

### brahmanas, charity, varnashrama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9i2cTPvQgh0?t=129
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In that regard, the brahmanas are giving the highest quality of service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's perspective on charity, stating that it is primarily meant for brahmanas and sannyasis who provide spiritual service to society, especially during important samskaras like birth, marriage, and death, because they give the highest quality of service in regard to the prime necessity of humankind.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport: Only the brahmanas and sannyasis are authorized to accept charity from the householders. Only the brahmanas and sannyasis are authorized to accept charity from the householders, especially from the householders. And only the brahmanas and sannyasis in the Varna Shtrama System can accept charity. It doesn't say scriptures don't prescribe poorer people should be given charity. No. That is not the correct understanding. Dana or charity or dakshina only given to brahmanas and sannyasis for the spiritual service they render to service spiritual service they render to the society free of charge the dakshina that is given or dana that is given to a brahmana or sannyasi is not a prescribed charge for the services rendered no there is no such prescription anywhere in the scriptures neither a brahmana will ever ask that you give me this much fees for some consultation or for some service I may render no never never so this is very culture you see the best of the service is regards giving advice giving guidance giving any helping in any way to conduct a sacrifice or to worship the Lord or to prepare an horoscope or to do any of these brahmanical activities or to teach the Vedas never will a brahmana charge anyone who seeks the services of a brahmana never never he will charge anyone who seeks the services of a brahmana never never he will charge anyone who seeks the services of a brahmana never never he will charge so then how do they maintain themselves this is the way they are given dakshina they are given dana by the householders without any consideration of how much the householders are benefiting how much service they're taking no, no such consideration so it is said here specifically In all the different occasions of samskaras, especially during the time of birth, marriage and death, wealth is distributed to the brahmanas because the brahmanas give the highest quality of service in regard to the prime necessity of humankind. What is the prime necessity of a human being? Spiritual upliftment, spiritual elevation, progress towards the ultimate goal of life. In that regard, the brahmanas are giving the highest quality of service. Because such service is not ordinarily possible to be obtained from anyone else other than qualified, learned brahmanas who are engaged in tapasya, who are engaged in constantly worshipping the Lord, in constantly studying the scriptures, in constantly performing welfare activity for the entire human society. Not only human society, for the entire world, of all living creatures. Foolish people don't understand this. So, such brahmanas deserve to get dakshina, dana, especially during three occasions, birth, marriage and death. It must be given.

### brahmanas, simplicity, welfare
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9i2cTPvQgh0?t=380
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They would simply devote themselves to the well-being of the entire society."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the qualities of qualified brahmanas, emphasizing their simple living, austerity, benevolence, and dedication to the welfare of society, made possible by the charity they receive, which frees them from the need to earn a livelihood.

The charity was substantial in the shape of gold, land, villages, horses, elephants and food grains, with other materials for cooking, complete foodstuff. So, the brahmanas were not, therefore, poor in the actual sense of the term. Proper qualified brahmanas were properly given charity. They were not exactly poor, though they would never make a show of wealth. They never made a show of wealth. They lived a simple life, even though they had, in the form of dakshina or dana, all these things which are given, but they never made a show of that, neither they exploited the system to become very, very rich and become extravagant in spending, or simply indulge in sense-enjoyment with dana or dakshina that they received. No, never. On the other hand, they'll be always austere in their mode of living. On the other hand, they'll be always austere in their mode of living. Tapasya. And they'll be simple and straightforward in all their dealings. And they'll be very benevolent to the general people in society. And they'll be very benevolent to the general people in society. Always willing to do welfare activity for everyone in the society. Always willing to do welfare activity for everyone in the society. Because they possessed gold, land, villages, horses, elephants and sufficient grains, they had nothing to earn for themselves. There was no need for them to earn some money for their own maintenance. They would simply devote themselves to the well-being of the entire society. See, so nice a system. That somebody is fully devoted for the welfare of the society. And they don't have to bother to find out some means of earning some money for their own maintenance. Their maintenance is taken care of. And they simply give very good service to the whole society, everyone.

### charity, scripture, goodness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9i2cTPvQgh0?t=542
**Verse References:** BG 17.20-22
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Charity has got a purpose."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the significance of the word 'Tirthavit' and explains that charity should be given purposefully, considering the place, time, and recipient, as explained in the scriptures and Bhagavad Gita, and that true brahmanas and sannyasis give their services free of charge.

Now, the word that is used here, Tirthavit, is significant in this verse. Because the king, that is Yudhishthira Maharaj, knew well where and when charity has to be given. Yudhishthira Maharaj was aware. He was enlightened. He was Raja Rishi. He was a saintly king. So he knew when and where charity has to be given. Charity is never unproductive or brutal. Charity is never unproductive or brutal. Charity is never unproductive or brutal. Charity has got a purpose. Giving charity is purposefully given. And it is given with due consideration of who is the person who is to be given charity, and when it is to be given, and where should it be given. The place, the time, the candidate for receiving charity, all these are very clearly explained in the scriptures. In the Bhagavad Gita, it is said that charity should be given in the mode of goodness. Mode of goodness means following the scriptures. It is regulated. Only brahmanas and sannyasis should be given charity. And these brahmanas and sannyasis do accept charity because they don't have any other means of livelihood for their maintenance. They have no other job or profession or work or some income or some wealth. They don't have anything like that. And whatever service they give, they give it free of charge, always. In this case, anyone who comes needs some service. They give it free of charge, totally free, 100% free. Never there is any consideration of charges. That is a true brahmana, a true sannyas.

### varnashrama, economics, taxes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9i2cTPvQgh0?t=678
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Brahmanas are never commercially oriented."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the Vedic culture, where brahmanas are not commercially oriented and are not taxed, with the current Kali Yuga, where professional fees and taxes are common, and explains the traditional taxation system where only Vaishyas are taxed by Kshatriyas for the welfare of society.

So, Kali Yuga we only know that people who are expert in some field of learning, they have fees, they charge fees, professional fees. And the government collects taxes on those fees. So, it has become what we can say as commercial, a commercial venture. But Vedic culture is, brahmanas are never commercially oriented. Neither the government or the king would charge any tax on the brahmanas. Never brahmanas are taxed. Kshatriyas will tax only Vaishyas who are business minded. Only Vaishyas are taxed. And Kshatriyas don't collect taxes from other Kshatriyas. And Kshatriyas don't collect any tax from brahmanas and sutras. So, only Vaishyas are the ones who give taxes. And only the Kshatriyas collect such taxes to engage in welfare activity for the whole society. For all the citizens, welfare of the citizens, they collect taxes. And in return, they give protection to the citizens. And they ensure that the society is properly maintained. The common facilities for the people in the state or the kingdom are properly maintained.

---
### charity, compassion, daridra narayana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9i2cTPvQgh0?t=800
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Narayana is never Daridra. And a Daridra can never become Narayana. So, this is completely wrong."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that charity should be offered to spiritually enlightened persons, not based on the misconception of 'Daridra Narayana,' and distinguishes between giving charity (Dana/Dakshina) and showing compassion (Daya) to the needy, emphasizing that the latter involves sharing excess resources.

So, Srila Prabhupada says further, In the Shastras, charity was offered to persons who deserve to accept charity, but didn't have spiritual enlightenment. You see, who can receive charity? One who is spiritually enlightened. Whether it's a brahmana or a sanyasi. Both of them are supposed to be spiritually enlightened. And nowadays, what goes on in the name of Daridra Narayana, Srila Prabhupada says, The so-called Daridra Narayana, a misconception of the Supreme Lord by unauthorized persons, is never to be found in the Shastras as the object of charity. Daridra means somebody who is poverty stricken. And a poverty stricken person sometimes is designated wrongly as Daridra Narayana. Narayana is never Daridra. And a Daridra can never become Narayana. So, this is completely wrong. Combining these two terms and designating some poor people as object of charity wrongly is a total misconception. Because the Supreme Lord is always having unlimited opulences. By definition, Bhagavan. Bhaga means opulences. Vaan means one who possesses. And to what extent? Unlimited. And a poverty stricken man is poverty stricken because of his sinful past. Because of his sinful past. So, never consider a poverty stricken man as Narayana. So, there is a difference between giving charity to spiritually enlightened persons and helping some poor people who may be needy. Who may be need. There is a difference between the two. A needy person should be shown compassion. Daya. Not Dana. Not Dakshina. What is called Daya. Daya means, you want to help somebody who is in need. Because you have the means to share. You have little excess. So, you share it with somebody who is in need. That is called Daya. Compassion. It is not Dana. Don't confuse the two. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.15-16 | 10-08-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### divine protection, lineage, purity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cbBSA1duS40?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.15, SB 1.12.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This potless son has been restored by the all-powerful and all-pervasive Lord Vishnu, the personality of Godhead, in order to oblige you."
**Summary:** The speaker explains verses 15 and 16 of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the Brahmanas' description of Parikshit's birth and Lord Vishnu's protection of him.

In this session, we will be discussing the 15th and 16th verses of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. To begin with the 15th verse: Tam Uchur Brahmanas Tushta Rajanam Prashrayanvitam Esha Hyasmin Prajatantau Purunam Paura Varshabha. The meaning of this 15th verse is: The learned Brahmanas who were very satisfied with the charities of the king addressed him as the chief amongst the Purus and informed him that his son was certainly in the line of descent from the Purus. So the learned Brahmanas are describing that the child which is born in this dynasty is actually a very, very fortunate child and deserving to take birth in this glorious dynasty, is going to only increase the glories of this dynasty in which great people had appeared earlier. So the next verse, 16th verse, daivena apratighatena shukle samstham upeyushi ratovo anugraharthaya vishtuna prabhavishtuna. The meaning of this verse is: the brahmanas said this spotless son has been restored by the all-powerful and all-pervasive Lord Vishnu, the personality of Godhead, in order to oblige you, brahmanas speaking to Yudhishthira. So in order to oblige Yudhishthira, this child has been protected by the Supreme Lord personally. He was saved when he was doomed to be destroyed by an irresistible supernatural weapon.

---

So specifically Prabhupada is citing two reasons why this child was saved. When it was attacked by that irresistible supernatural weapon, Brahmastra, the child was saved by the all-powerful and all-pervasive Vishnu. If this had not been provoked, it would certainly have died. But because the child was protected personally by Lord Vishnu the child did not die.

**Summary:** The speaker details the two reasons for Parikshit's salvation: his purity as a devotee and his role as the sole surviving male heir of the Puru dynasty, crucial for continuing the lineage of pious kings.

The reason is that the child in the womb of the mother was spotless due to his being a pure devotee of the Lord. So there is no ordinary child spotless pure because pure devotee of the Supreme Lord means there is no material contamination, there is no past karma, sinful reactions, nothing, spotless child. And that is described here, shukle samstha mupe yushi, shukle means pure, the child is pure, spotless. The second reason, there is a second reason why the child was saved by the Supreme God personally. The second reason is that the child was the only surviving male descendant of Puru, the pious forefather of the virtuous King Yudhishthira. In this dynasty where great personalities had appeared and they were doing excellent service to the society of protecting the citizens by ruling exactly according to scriptural directions and elevating the people to the spiritual platform, spiritual upliftment of the people in the society. They were doing that work very nicely. So in order to further the mission of the Supreme Lord in this material world, which is spiritual upliftment of the people, these kings in this Puru dynasty, all the forefathers of Yudhishthira and Yudhishthira himself, all had actually done excellent service in the mission of the Supreme Lord in this world. And this child was the only surviving male descendant because everyone else was killed in the battle of Kurukshetra. All of them were killed in the battle of Kurukshetra. All the other descendants were killed in the battle.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.17| 13-08-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### divine protection, pure devotion, qualities of a devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOINCg3TvZM?t=55
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When it comes to pure devotees, Krishna personally protects them."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Emperor Parikshit was protected by Krishna from birth because he was a pure devotee, and pure devotees receive personal protection from the Lord, unlike others who are protected through the Lord's potencies.

In this session, we will be discussing the 17th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, the birth of Emperor Parikshit. This verse is describing the reason why this child will be well known throughout the world. The translation of this verse: 'For this reason, the child will be well known in the world as one who is protected by the personality of Godhead.' That's the meaning of the word Vishnurata. O most fortunate one! The brahmanas are addressing King Yudhishthira. There is no doubt that this child will become a first-class devotee and will be qualified with all good qualities. Mahabhagavato, a first-class devotee, topmost devotee, pure devotee. In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains this point very elaborately. Srila Prabhupada says, 'The Lord gives protection to all living beings because he is their supreme leader.' The Vedic hymns confirm that the Lord is the supreme person amongst all personalities. Nithyao Nithyanaam Chetanas Chetananaam. The difference between the two living beings is that the one, the personality of Godhead, provides for all living beings. All other living beings and by knowing him, one can achieve eternal peace. Eko Bahunam Yo Viddhati Kaman, the one, only one, supreme personality of Godhead, he provides for everyone else. Anybody who knows this personality of Godhead, the provider of everyone, the chief, the topmost personality, the supreme person, will attain eternal peace. Such protection is given by his different potencies to different grades of living beings. There are various potencies of the Supreme Lord that are working, always working in his service, and they provide different grades. Different grades of living beings get different protection. But as far as his unalloyed devotees are concerned, unalloyed means pure devotees, the first-class devotees, he gives the protection personally. There is a difference. The living entities who are not pure devotees, they are given protection through arrangement of the Supreme Lord through his different potencies or energies. But when it comes to pure devotees, Krishna personally protects them. Therefore, Maharaj Pariksit is protected from the very beginning of his appearance in the womb of his mother. Krishna is always taken personally, and then they are always under the personal charge of his pure devotees. Always, they are under his personal protection. And because he is especially given protection by the Lord, the indication must be concluded that the child would be a first grade devotee of the Lord with all good qualities.

---
### devotee classification, kanishtha adhikari, madhyama adhikari
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOINCg3TvZM?t=330
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The greatest service one can do to one's family is simply to become a pure devotee of Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment describes the three grades of devotees: Kanishtha Adhikari (materialistic devotee), Madhyama Adhikari (intermediate devotee), and Mahabhagavata (topmost devotee), focusing on the characteristics and behaviors of the first two categories.

All the good qualities can only be found in the Supreme Lord and His pure devotees. There are three grades of devotees, namely the Mahabhagavata, Madhyama Adhikari, and the Kanishtha Adhikari, three grades. The topmost is the Mahabhagavata, intermediate level of devotion is the Madhyama Adhikari, intermediate level, and Kanishtha Adhikari, the lowest level, the neophyte devotee. Now Srila Prabhupada explains these three grades of devotees. Those who go to the temples of the Lord and offer worshipful respect to the devotees, the deity, without sufficient knowledge in the theological science, and therefore without any respect for the devotees of the Lord, are called materialistic devotees or Kanishtha Adhikari, the third grade devotees, the lowest, the third grade devotees. Prakrita Bhakta, this is Sanskrit, Prabhupada translates that in English as materialistic devotees. So their conception of God is God is only in the temple and in the form of a deity, and they offer respect, they don't have proper understanding of the science, theological science, science concerning God. They don't have sufficient knowledge, and therefore, they don't have any respect for the devotees of the Lord. Their attitude is like, I am there, God is there, that's all. And what about the others? They are also there, God is there, between them and God, between me and God, that's all. Nothing to do with interpersonal exchange between different persons. Secondly, the devotees who have developed, the second class of devotees, the devotees who have developed a mentality of genuine service to the Lord, and who thus make friendships only with similar devotees, show favor to the neophytes and avoid the atheists, are called the second grade, the second grade devotees, madhyama adhikari, intermediate level devotees, the second grade devotees, they recognize four classes of people in this world, the topmost, of course, four classes of personalities, I would say. First of all, they have the highest respect.

---
### devotee classification, mahabhagavata, god consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOINCg3TvZM?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They don't see just the creation, they see the creator."
**Summary:** This segment continues the description of the Madhyama Adhikari and then focuses on the Mahabhagavata, explaining their vision of seeing everything in relation to the Lord and using everything in His service, emphasizing God consciousness.

This is the second grade devotee. They have developed a genuine service mentality. That means they are properly engaged, under guidance, under clear guidance, in devotional service of the Supreme Lord, and they are in knowledge. They have proper knowledge of who is God, who is a devotee, who are the neophytes, who are the atheists. They have clear knowledge, clear understanding. Then, the first grade devotee. This is now described by Prabhupada. But those who see everything in the Lord, or everything of the Lord, everything in the Lord, everything of the Lord. Two different things. But this is the vision of a pure devotee. And also see in everything an eternal relation of the Lord. It may sound like difficult to understand, but the fact is, in the whole existence, the Lord is there, and His energies are there. And the Lord is the possessor, owner, and enjoyer, and controller of all His energies. So the energies belong to the Lord. But in another sense, the Lord and His energy are not different. So, because the Lord and His energy are not different, therefore, a pure devotee sees everything in the Lord, and sees everything belonging to the Lord. Everything belongs to the Lord. And also, anything we take, it has a specific relationship with the Lord. It has a specific relationship. Now, based on this understanding of relationship, the treatment for that thing will also be different. Each item, each thing. It's not the same treatment for everything. Now, such pure devotees, for them, there is nothing within their purview of sight except the Lord. They see the Lord everywhere. Now, seeing the Lord everywhere doesn't mean that everything is God. No. Everything is not God. God is God. Everything belongs to God. Everything is God's property, is God's proprietorship. So, everything has to be used in God's service. Everything belongs to God, so use everything in God's service. So, because they recognize everything belonging to God, therefore, they only see God. Very simple way to put it is, they don't see just the creation, they see the creator. Creation means there is a creator, and creation also means they can see the creator everywhere. It's a consciousness that is saturated with God. So, they are fully God conscious, fully Krishna conscious, and they are called the Mahabhagavatas, or the first great devotees of the Lord. Such first great devotees of the Lord are perfect in all respects.

---

And because Maharaj Pariksit took his birth in the family of Maharaj Yudhishtira, he is addressed here in this Mahabhagavata, the greatest of fortunates. The family in which a Mahabhagavata takes his birth is fortunate because due to the first great devotee taking birth in the family, in a particular family, the members of the family, past, present and future, up to 100 generations, become liberated by the grace of the Lord out of respect for his beloved devotees. So, that is the greatest thing that can happen to anybody. So, the greatest service one can do to one's family is simply to become a pure devotee of Krishna. That's all. I'll stop here. ### Srimad Bhagavatam, King Yudhishthira, Emperor Pariksit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Sqqw2JDxTfI?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O great souls, will he become as saintly a king, as pious in his very name, and as famous and glorified in his achievements as others who appeared in this great royal family?"
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the 18th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on King Yudhishthira's question to the Brahmins about the future of Emperor Pariksit.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Chai Vanarottamam, Devim Saraswatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing the 18th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The topic of this chapter is the birth of Emperor Pariksit. Shri Rajovacha Apyesha Vamsyaan Rajarsheen Punya Shlokan Mahatmanaha Anuvartitha Svithyashasa Sadhuvadena Sattamaha. This is the birth of Emperor Pariksit. This is a word spoken by King Yudhishthira to the Brahmins in response to what they said. Now he is asking a question. The good King Yudhishthira inquired, O great souls, will he become as saintly a king, as pious in his very name, and as famous and glorified in his achievements as others who appeared in this great royal family? So this verse is a question. This is a question by King Yudhishthira to the Brahmins.

---
### Rajarsheen, Saintly Kings, Spiritual Nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Sqqw2JDxTfI?t=113
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual nature does not change."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that King Yudhishthira's forefathers were great saintly kings, Rajarsheen, who were both kings and Rishis, and that their example is timeless because the spiritual nature of a person does not change with time or circumstances.

So Shri La Pravapara explains in the purport to this verse. The forefathers of King Yudhishthira were all great saintly kings. Rajarsheen. The term used is Rajarsheen. They are Raja, they are kings, but they are also Rishi, they are saintly, saintly kings. It's a rare combination. And all of them are pious and glorified by their great achievements. They are all pious because they strictly follow the scriptures, and they are glorified by their great achievements. Their achievements are noteworthy, even for kings. They are future generations. So they are all recorded in the Vedic scriptures. And they are glorified, and their example is cited again and again and again. They are the ideal for everyone else in the society, even in the future. So their example is a timeless example. They don't go out of relevance, because there may be some very ancient examples of a different time, of a different era, because the nature of a person is essentially spiritual, and that spiritual nature doesn't change with the change of time, change of era, change of yuga, change of place, change of circumstances. The spiritual nature does not change.

---
### Saintly Kings, Citizen Welfare, Prosperity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Sqqw2JDxTfI?t=266
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, all the members of the state, all the citizens, were happy, pious, well-behaved, prosperous, and spiritually enlightened."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada describes the qualities of saintly kings and the positive impact they have on their citizens, leading to happiness, piety, good behavior, prosperity, and spiritual enlightenment due to the king's perfect rule and the abundance provided by Mother Earth.

So further Srila Prabhupada says, they were all saints on the royal throne. They are sitting on the royal throne. They are ruling. They are powerful. And they can be very, very tough in dealing with miscreants. But they are saints. They have all the qualities of a sadhu, of a saintly person. And therefore, as a result of such people ruling, what is being described here, please hear. Therefore, all the members of the state, all the citizens, were happy, pious, well-behaved, prosperous, and spiritually enlightened. Five things Prabhupada says about the citizens who are under the care of great saintly kings, Rajarshis. First of all, people are happy. This is a primary characteristic of a saintly person ruling a kingdom, or a state, or a province, or a country, or even the whole world. All the people are happy. Why? Because of his perfect rule, there is plenty of supply of all necessities by Mother Earth. Plenty of supply. So there is no scarcity of anything, for anyone. So naturally people will be happy. And then people are also, they are pious.

---
### Scriptural Guidance, Social Harmony, Vedic Culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Sqqw2JDxTfI?t=379
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone is trained up in the Vedic culture."
**Summary:** The lecture explains that saintly kings enforce scriptural directions for the good of all, leading to harmony and peace. Citizens are well-behaved because they are trained in Vedic culture and perform their respective duties within the four social and spiritual classes.

Because the saintly kings enforce the scriptural directions to be followed by everybody for their own good, for the good of the whole country, for the good of the whole world. So there are no clashes between different sections of people in the society. People can live in harmony, peacefully. And because of the prosperity, they are all happy. Everyone is having everything they require. And people are well behaved. They are well behaved because they are all trained up to follow their respective duties, to perform their respective duties. Everyone is trained up in the Vedic culture. The whole society is divided into four social classes and four spiritual classes. So the four social classes are the Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras. So they have a certain nature, acquired nature. Accordingly, they are trained and they are engaged in occupational duties. So everyone contributes to the society positively.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the roles of the Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas in society, explaining their contributions to guidance, protection, and economic development, respectively, all for the public welfare.

And the others acknowledge, I may be inclined to work like a Brahmana. So I give my contribution to the society in terms of giving guidance to the people, in terms of educating them, in terms of teaching them how to worship God, in terms of giving them guidance about their own responsibilities, duties. Like that. So, I do my duty and the Kshatriyas, they do their duty giving protection to the citizens, collecting taxes, spending the tax money for the public welfare. Then the Vaishyas, they do economic development by trading, commerce, by growing food grains, by giving cow protection, so plenty of milk, milk products, plenty of grains, plenty of minerals, everything. The Vaishyas cultivate the land, they engage in business, trading the excess grains or excess silk or excess of metals, minerals, everything. So, the Vaishyas do their work, they give taxes to the government and the government spends it for public welfare.

### Shudras, Menial Service, Social Structure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Sqqw2JDxTfI?t=584
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They just are humble, they do whatever they can do as menial service and even for that they are trained up, even in that menial service they are trained up like carpenters, sculptors, the tillers in the land, or the workers who work in a Goshala or workers who work in the in the government, in the military."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the role of Shudras in society, explaining that they are engaged in menial service according to their proclivities and are trained to perform specific tasks without needing high intellectual development, and their needs are provided for by those who employ them.

The Shudras are engaged according to their proclivities to do a particular type of menial work because they cannot work as a Vaishya or a Kshatriya or a Brahmana. They don't have the necessary intellectual development. Their nature is not suited for using their intelligence in a Vaishya way or a Kshatriya way or a Brahmana way. So, they just are humble, they do whatever they can do as menial service and even for that they are trained up, even in that menial service they are trained up like carpenters, sculptors, the tillers in the land, or the workers who work in a Goshala or workers who work in the in the government, in the military. They are trained up to do one specific type of work without trying to use their intelligence in in in understanding some philosophy or some theory, nothing, they just follow. That is the level of the Shudra. And they are provided with all their needs, they are provided with all their needs by whoever engages them in a respective menial service.

---
### Social Order, Prosperity, Spiritual Enlightenment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Sqqw2JDxTfI?t=681
**Verse References:** BG 3.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker summarizes that people are well-behaved due to training and the presence of ideals, prosperous due to nature's abundance, and spiritually enlightened through systematic dissemination of spiritual knowledge, which the government should be concerned with.

So, in this way people are well behaved, people are well behaved because they are trained up, they are taught and they have ideals in the society, the saintly persons, the brahmanas, and the rishis, the sages. They have ideal examples to follow and people are prosperous because they have plenty of everything. There is no scarcity. Why? Because Mother Nature provides, Mother Earth provides. When there is a saintly person, a saintly king ruling, Mother Earth provides plenty. Everybody does their duty, therefore there is yajña, yajña karma samudbhava. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita. And, above all, people are spiritually enlightened. There is systematic dissemination of spiritual knowledge, which is most essential in human society. It cannot be like the government is not concerned with people's spiritual development. No. Government should be concerned. Should be concerned.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O son of Pritha, this child shall be exactly like King Ikshvaku, son of Manu, in maintaining all those who are born. And as for following the Brahmanical principles, especially in being true to his promise, he shall be exactly like Rama, the personality of Godhead, the son of Maharaj Dasaratha."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 19th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the birth of Emperor Pariksit and his predicted qualities, comparing him to King Ikshvaku and Lord Rama.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 19th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The topic of this chapter is birth of Emperor Pariksit. Brahmana Uchuhu Partha Prajavita Sakshad Ikshvakor Iva Manavaha Brahmanya Satya Sandhashya Ramo Dasarathir Yatha. The meaning of this verse is, the learned Brahmana said, O son of Pritha, this child shall be exactly like King Ikshvaku, son of Manu, in maintaining all those who are born. And as for following the Brahmanical principles, especially in being true to his promise, he shall be exactly like Rama, the personality of Godhead, the son of Maharaj Dasaratha. And as for following the Brahmanical principles, especially in being true to his promise, he shall be exactly like Ramachandra, Lord Rama. Because there are many Ramas in the past, so specifically this child will be like Dasarathi Rama, Lord Ramachandra, the personality of Godhead, who appeared as the son of Dasaratha Maharaj. Lord Ramachandra, the personality of Godhead, who appeared as the son of Dasaratha Maharaj, that Rama.

### Srila Prabhupada, Kuru dynasty, Soul, Material Desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bL4CspM0X30?t=136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually the living being has no birth and no death, but because of his separation from the service of the Lord and due to his desire to lord it over material nature, he is offered a suitable body to satisfy his material desires."
**Summary:** The speaker mentions Srila Prabhupada's elaborate purport on the verse, highlighting Pariksit Maharaja's promising birth as the sole surviving descendant of the Kuru dynasty and explaining the fundamental understanding of the soul's nature, its separation from the Lord, and the awarding of a material body to fulfill material desires.

So, in a very elaborate purport by Srila Prabhupada for this verse. Maharaj asked a question, will he be as glorious as his forefathers in his dynasty? So the Brahmanas replied, yes, he will have all the good qualities of all the great persons and that will be described in the next 10 to 15 verses of this chapter. So very, very promising child has taken birth as the grandson of Arjuna, the only surviving descendant of the Kuru dynasty. Everyone else has died in the battle of Kurukshetra. So a very qualified and exceptionally great personality has taken birth because this is a pure devotee taken birth and it's a lord's arrangement for the good of this entire world so srila prabhupada elaborately explains in the purport praja means the living being who has taken his birth in this material world. Actually the living being has no birth and no death, but because of his separation from the service of the Lord and due to his desire to lord it over material nature, he is offered a suitable body to satisfy his material desires. So a very fundamental understanding of who we are and we are actually spirit soul. Spirit soul does not have birth, does not have death. The soul itself is not born or does not die, but it is the body. Body has got birth and body has got death and this body is awarded to us, to the spirit soul, particular spirit soul, to satisfy the material desires of the spirit soul. The soul.

---
### Material World, Spiritual World, Transmigration, Karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bL4CspM0X30?t=279
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Otherwise if you don't do devotional service we keep changing our body according to our work in different species in different places or planets."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the soul's misplaced presence in the material world due to its desire for material enjoyment, emphasizing the necessity of a material body for existence in this realm and the conditioning by material nature. He explains that our actions determine our next body, and without devotional service, we transmigrate through 8,400,000 species of life.

Soul and material desires don't go together actually, but because we are separated from the service of the Lord, why are we separated from the service of the Lord? Because we want to desire to lord over material nature, we want to enjoy materially. So this is the reason we are placed in the material world where we don't belong. Spirit soul does not belong to material world spirit soul belongs to the spiritual world, but then in this material world if a spirit soul has to live in the material world spirit soul has to get a material body only then we can live in the material world. We can attempt to lord over material nature we can attempt to satisfy material desires. So therefore shloka prabha says the spirit soul the actual person the living being is offered a suitable body so the varieties of bodies are there in doing so one becomes conditioned by the laws of material nature and the material body is changed in terms of his own work. Condition means we are subject to the control the influence of material nature we cannot act in any way different from the influence and whatever work we do exactly according to that work we get our next body. So if our work is possible we can move it we can change it and we can change the world in terms of devotional service we get a spiritual body we revive our spiritual form, otherwise if you don't do devotional service we keep changing our body according to our work in different species in different places or planets in different the living entity thus transmigrates from one body to another in 8 million 400 thousand species of life transmigration.

---
### Transmigration, Body, Change
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bL4CspM0X30?t=436
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Same person, yes, but not the same body. It's not the same body. It's a similar body."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of transmigration, comparing it to the continuous changes within our own body from childhood to old age. He clarifies that although we perceive ourselves as the same person, the body is constantly changing, with old cells being replaced by new ones, and that we are giving up an old body to accept a new body.

Transmigration that means we are changing body but we don't appear to be changing body especially when in this very body in this very life what we understand is life between birth and death in this life we are changing body from childhood to youth to old age but we are not thinking that our body is changing we think our body is growing we think our body is the same body which is become bigger or which has become older but that's not a fact the body is material and matter doesn't grow for example this table will this table ever grow no never nothing material ever grows so therefore this body appears to grow but because it is material it will not grow it doesn't grow then what happens I was a child now I have somebody has a body of a youth and I had a body of old man so what happened actually the old body we give up to accept a new body and this happens in such a way that we don't realize that continuously changes are taking place in this body. After all, this body is made up of so many cells, right? And some cells are dying and they are replaced with new cells. Old cells are replaced with new cells. Continuously, this is happening. And it's so very gradual. If you are continuously in touch with another person, we don't notice how the person is changing body. We don't notice that. And as long as there is a resemblance between one body, the previous state of my body and the next stage of my body, because there is a resemblance, we think, oh, the same person, same body. Same person, yes, but not the same body. It's not the same body. It's a similar body. It's a similar body. So, therefore, this is called transmigration. Dehantara. In the Bhagavad Gita, it is explained. Dehino samsara.

---
### Desire, Species, Material Enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bL4CspM0X30?t=624
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everybody doesn't have the same desire to enjoy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that transmigration from one body to another occurs in different species of life to accommodate different types of desires for enjoyment, giving examples of pigs, tigers, and birds to illustrate how specific bodies cater to specific desires.

So, this transmigration from one body to another happens in different species of life to accommodate different types of desires for enjoyment. Everybody doesn't have the same desire to enjoy. Just like in the human body, nobody enjoys eating stool. Rubbish. But there is a particular body for enjoyment. For those who have such desires, the pig's body, the hog's body, somebody likes to enjoy eating, drinking fresh blood. Then they have to get the body of a tiger. A tiger pounces on its prey, kills it, and sucks the blood and eats the flesh. So, there is a suitable body. There is a body for that. The body of a tiger. Or the body of a lion. So, there are varieties of bodies. There are some birds which eat stone. So, somebody has a desire to eat stone. They have to get that particular type of body for which it's food and it is relishable. So, nature has actually fashioned 8,400,000 types of bodies. They are called species of life. And every person gets a suitable body according to the desires the person has to enjoy.

But due to his being the part and parcel of the Lord, every soul, every living being, you, I, every living being, is actually part and parcel of God. This many times it has been mentioned in Srila Prabhupada's books and writings, but please understand part means we are a tiny part, a tiny amsa of God, of Krishna. Krishna is a complete whole, we are a tiny part and we are very, very, very, very, very small. It's just like an atom compared to an entire universe, entire universe, compared to the universe, whole universe as a whole, compared to that what is an atom in that universe, like that. So, Krishna is the whole creation and we are just one insignificant tiny atomic particle of spirit. Part and parcel. Parcel means we cannot be disconnected from Krishna. We cannot be independent of Krishna. We are always connected through the relationship of we are maintained and Krishna is the maintainer. Krishna maintains us. Krishna provides for us. we cannot independently get our necessities independent of Krishna supply we cannot get in necessities so to think that somebody other than Krishna is my maintainer or provider or my master is actually a mistake is a mistake under the illusory energy mistake under illusion Krishna only is the provider Krishna only is the maintainer of everyone there is a statement in the Kathopanishad Eko Bahunam Yo Vidhati Kama so therefore because we are part and parcel of the Lord we are maintained by the Lord with all necessities of life and we are also protected by the Lord and his representatives the saintly kings.

**Summary:** The speaker explains why we take birth in the material world, describing the body as a prison house that limits the spirit soul's freedom, and how saintly kings protect living beings to fulfill their duties and terms of imprisonment according to their karma.

And Prabhupada explained why we take birth in the material world. What does it mean to take birth in the material world and how a saintly king as a representative of Krishna, who is the maintainer of everyone, a saintly king as a representative of Krishna gives protection to all those born in the land where he is ruling. So Sri Laprabha is explaining about the saintly kings because Yudhishthira Maharaj made a reference to saintly kings of the past, and it is foretold that this Pariksit, who has just taken birth, will also be a saintly king like the forefathers of this dynasty. So Prabhupada says, these saintly kings give protection to all the Prajas, or living beings, to live and to fulfill their duties. So Prabhupada says, these saintly kings give protection to all the Prajas, or living beings, to live and to fulfill their duties. But, in fact, they are both priests who issue their terms of imprisonment. But, in fact, they are both priests who issue their terms of imprisonment. This body is actually a prison house because this body is imposing so many limitations on this spirit soul, which has got freedom, just like every spirit soul is free to travel across hemos cre grantos, and repel spiders and dubies, at will in any part of the Lord's creation, every part of the Lord's creation, we have to remember that we areżeniae of this body, but we should remember that moment we are inside this body, iber, my body imposes limitation on my mode of travel and where I can go and where I can survive, what atmosphere I can survive in, in this body. See the soul as far as I am concerned, as spirit soul, I have no death, but then if I go to a place which is not suited for my body, then the body will die, body will perish, body cannot survive. So therefore there is a need for giving protection to living beings, all the prajas, to fulfill their terms of imprisonment. When I get this body, there is a certain duration I have to live in this body according to my karma of the past. Only then my desire to enjoy in a particular way, materially enjoy, will be fulfilled in this body.

### protection, animals, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/l14fgF83Gbw?t=298
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is a scientific fact that every living being has a right to live."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the duty of saintly kings to protect all living beings, including cows and trees, and criticizes the modern understanding that only human beings need protection, highlighting the spiritual understanding that all beings have a spirit soul and a right to live.

So the saintly king, they gave this. They gave protection to all living beings, without exception, to live, not that unnecessarily somebody is killed and the king is a mute spectator, just like nowadays, so many cows are being killed and they have a right to live. Why are they being killed? So therefore, cows also require protection. Trees are being cut indiscriminately. So that is also against the principle of the scriptures that even the trees require protection to be given by the king or the ruler or the government. The trees also, they have to live within the tree body for whatever their duration. So this is very very important point, that the saintly kings used to give protection to all varieties of living beings in their kingdom, not just only human beings. Today the understanding is only human beings have to be protected. For the government, concern is protect only human beings. What about animals? What about the trees? They also require protection. That's why spiritually enlightened people understand that there is spirit soul in the cow's body. There is spirit soul in the tree body. There is also spirit soul in the human body. Some religious faiths, they say, oh, there is no soul in animal body. There is only soul in human body. This is wrong. This understanding is completely wrong. From the scriptures we understand there are different varieties of bodies, different varieties of species of life. This is the correct understanding. This is the scientific fact. So, Srila Prabhupada explains, Maharaj Pariksit was actually an ideal saintly king because while touring his kingdom, he happened to see that a poor cow was about to be killed by the person. He was about to be killed by the personified Kali whom he at once took to task as a murderer. See, Prabhupada is giving an example. This will be explained later, how Pariksit Maharaj dealt with Kali Purusha, the personification of this age, we are living in Kali Yuga. Kali Purusha, he was about to kill a cow. When Pariksit Maharaj reached that spot, immediately he took out his sword and said, how dare you harm a cow. I am going to kill you for even attempting to kill a cow. So, Srila Prabhupada says, this means that even animals were given protection by the saintly administrators.

### protection, rights, Vedic literature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/l14fgF83Gbw?t=490
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's only in the Vedic literature you find the facts explained about who am I? What is this world?"
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that animals were protected not out of sentimentality, but because all living beings have a right to live, and violating that right is punishable. He emphasizes the importance of Vedic literature in understanding the nature of all creatures and their right to live and feel pain.

Not from any sentimental point of view, but because those who have taken their birth in the material world have the right to live. You see, that way some people have sentiment, no? Oh, poor dog, poor cat, poor mouse, or poor some creature. Different people have different sentiments towards different creatures. But this is not a king who sentimentally feels for some other birds or animals, no. It is a scientific fact that every living being has a right to live, has a right to live, having taken birth in this world by the arrangement of the Supreme Lord. They have a right to live. And if their right is violated, then those who violate that are punishable. If they are not punished by the king, then they are going to suffer punishment in the future by the laws of nature. By the laws of nature they will be suffering. So therefore, it is said here, animals are also given protection by the saintly administrators because the animals also have a right to live. All the saintly kings, beginning from the king of the sun globe down to the king of the earth, are so inclined by the influence of Vedic literature. See, this Vedic literature is very important. It's only in the Vedic literature you find the facts explained about who am I? What is this world? What is the nature or constitution of all the other creatures? Those who are also living, the animals, the birds, the insects, the reptiles, the plants, the trees. Who are they? They are also spirit souls. They also have a right to live. They also feel pain when they are hurt. Of course, we can hear them cry and when they are harmed, when they are injured, they also bleed. But there may be some creatures who don't show such symptoms of pain. But there have been experiments done on some trees or some plants that they also respond to different kinds of treatment and they show symptoms. They show symptoms of pain. They show symptoms of life by which they respond to different kinds of treatment. So, according to the Vedic literature, every living being has feelings that originally belongs to whom? To the spirit soul within the body. The body may not be having the facility.

### pain, suffering, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/l14fgF83Gbw?t=697
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If I give pain to somebody else, I'll be punished by the laws of nature that I also have to suffer similar pain in future."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a child unable to express pain to explain that plants also feel pain even though they cannot cry, and criticizes modern civilization for being unaware that inflicting pain on others leads to future suffering, emphasizing the king's role in punishing wrongdoers to teach them not to inflict pain.

To express, to express. Just like a child. If it is having stomach pain, it cannot exactly tell its mother, my stomach is paining. So what will the child do? Just cry. That's all. So the mother has to figure out why is the child crying. Is it crying because it's hungry? Is it crying because it has stomach pain? Is it crying because the aunt is biting its toe? The mother has to figure out. The child cannot express. So does it mean the child cannot express therefore it doesn't have any specific requirement? No, it has, but it cannot express. Similarly, the plants, they feel pain, but just because they cannot cry, so does it mean they don't feel the pain? So this is the mistake of the modern civilization, who are uneducated, who don't have proper knowledge, and who are unaware that if I give pain to somebody else, I'll be punished by the laws of nature that I also have to suffer similar pain in future. Therefore, when the king gives punishment, then the king is doing a great favor to that soul or the person who is inflicting the pain. He gets the lesson that I should not unnecessarily give pain to others because then I will be punished. Then I have to suffer pain. Atmavat sarva bhuteshu. If I suffer pain and I don't want to suffer, similarly others also suffer pain, they should not be given pain. They should not inflict suffering on anyone else, any other living creature. So this is the philosophy of life.

**Verse References:** BG 4.1

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Live and let live."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates that Vedic literatures educate us in the philosophy of 'live and let live' and explains how lessons are transferred through disciplic succession, citing the example of Krishna teaching the Bhagavad Gita to the Sun God Vivasvan, who then taught it to his son Manu, and from Manu to Ikshvaku, the first king on Earth, emphasizing that Pariksit will be a saintly king like Ikshvaku.

This is the philosophy of life. The Vedic literatures educate us in this philosophy of life. Live and let live. Live and let live. The Vedic literatures have taught in higher planets also, as there is reference in the Bhagavad Gita about the teachings to Sun God Vivasvan by the Lord, Supreme Lord Krishna. And such lessons are transferred by disciplic succession as it was done by the Sun God to his son Manu. Imam vivasvate yogam proktavanam abhyayam vivasvan manave praha. Vivasvan, the Sun God, he has a son by name Manu, vaivasvata Manu. That vaivasvata Manu is ruling in this vanvantara, the human beings. And he was taught, first of all Krishna says he taught the Bhagavad Gita to the Sun God. Sun God means the the controller of the Sun planet, the ruler of the Sun planet. And the Sun God taught it to his son Manu, vaivasvata Manu. The same lessons which Krishna taught to Vivasvan, Sun God, same lessons Vivasvan taught it to vaivasvata Manu. And from Manu to Ikshvaku, Ikshvaku, Ikshvaku is the first king in the in this earth planet, in this vanvantara, Ikshvaku. So Ikshvaku's name is mentioned here. Like Ikshvaku, this child will be maintaining everyone perfectly whoever has taken birth in the land, like Ikshvaku. Ikshvaku was famous. As a saintly king, like him, this Pariksit also will be a saintly king. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.19 Part 3 | 17-08-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Prophecy, Comparison, Lineage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yAxB7WrxnMA?t=30
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.19
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The learned Brahmana said, O son of Pritha, this child shall be exactly like King Ikshvaku, son of Manu, in maintaining all those who are born."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 19th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that Pariksit will be like King Ikshvaku and Lord Rama.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Chaiva Narottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session, we will be discussing the 19th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is a continuation from the previous two sessions where we discussed the same verse. Today is the final session on this verse. Just to refresh your memory, the verse: Brahmana Uchuhu Parthap Prajavita Sakshad Ikshvakur Iva Manavaha Brahmanya Satya Sandhascha Ramo Dasara Thirya Tha. The meaning of this 19th verse is, the learned Brahmana said, O son of Pritha, this child shall be exactly like King Ikshvaku, son of Manu, in maintaining all those who are born. And as for following the Brahmanical principles, especially in being true to his promise, he shall be exactly like Rama, the personality of Godhead, the son of Maharaj Dasaratha.

### Protection, Parampara, Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yAxB7WrxnMA?t=107
**Verse References:** BG 4.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone has the right to live."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the role of saintly kings in protecting all living beings and traces the lineage of Bhagavad Gita's knowledge from Krishna to Vivasvan, Vaivasvata Manu, and Maharaj Ikshwaku.

So in the purport, we were discussing that saintly kings, they give protection to all the prajas, all the living beings who are taken birth in a particular land or the kingdom, irrespective of whether that living being is a cow, or a tree, or a human being. So they don't discriminate between giving protection for humans and non-humans. No, there is no such discrimination. Everyone has the right to live. So it was explained in the past two sessions, how the kings in the former times, they were trained up to actually represent Krishna, who is the ultimate protector and maintainer of everyone, to actually give protection to all the creatures who are all living beings. So we had discussed up to the point that Bhagavad Gita was first spoken by Krishna to Vivaswan, the sun god, millions of years back. And the sun god, he spoke that same science of yoga, the Bhagavad Gita, to Vaivasvata Manu. Vaivasvata Manu is the current Manu who is ruling the human being. And his name is Vaivasvata Manu, his name is Shraddha Deva actually. And this Vaivasvata Manu's son, one Ikshwaku, Maharaj Ikshwaku, Maharaj Ikshwaku was the emperor of this earth planet at the beginning of this Manvantara. In a day of Brahma, there are 14 Manus. And the Manu referred here is the seventh Manu, the one who is the present Manu. So Shraddha Prabhupada says this Vaivasvata Manu had 10 sons. And Maharaj Ikshwaku is one of the 10 sons of Vaivasvata Manu. Maharaj Ikshwaku learned Bhakti Yoga as taught in Bhagavad Gita from his father, Vaivasvata Manu. Now this Vaivasvata Manu learned it from his father, Vivasvan. And Vivasvan learned it directly from Krishna, when Krishna instructed Vivasvan. So the teaching of Bhagavad Gita is coming down in parampara, in disciplic succession, in the parampara of Rajarshis, the saintly kings.

---
### Parampara, Vedic Knowledge, Kingship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yAxB7WrxnMA?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the Vedic literatures are current from the very beginning of creation of the material world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how the parampara of Rajarshis was broken, necessitating Krishna to speak the Bhagavad Gita again to Arjuna, and emphasizes the eternal nature of Vedic literatures originating from Krishna.

In course of time, this parampara of Rajarshis, where the knowledge of Bhagavad Gita is transmitted without change from one king to the next king, that chain was broken. And therefore, again Krishna had to speak the same Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. So, Srila Prabhupada explains, all the Vedic literatures are current from the very beginning of creation of the material world. And thus the Vedic literatures are known as Apavrushaya, not made by any man, not creation of any created being of this world. No, it is coming from Krishna to Brahma down. And the Vedic knowledge... Krishna is first heard at the very beginning of creation by Brahma, the first created being within the universe, directly from Krishna. Then, about Maharaj Ikshwaku, Srila Prabhupada says, he is one of the sons of Vaivasvata Manu, as it was mentioned earlier. Vaivasvata Manu had ten sons, so one of them was Maharaj Ikshwaku. And Maharaj Ikshwaku had... He had one hundred sons. He prohibited meat-eating, and his son, Shashada, became the next king after his death.

### Kingship, Protection, Time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yAxB7WrxnMA?t=390
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maintaining those who are born, the king is able, capable, it's possible for him to maintain all those who are born in a particular land in his kingdom, if he is receiving the knowledge in the parampara, and he is a strict follower of those principles."
**Summary:** The speaker connects the qualities of King Ikshwaku to Pariksit, emphasizing the importance of parampara and adherence to scriptural principles for a king to be a proper protector, and discusses the lineage of Manu and the creation process.

So, since the reference is made in this verse, that this child, Pariksit, shall be exactly like King Ikshwaku in maintaining all those who are born. So, maintaining those who are born, the king is able, capable, it's possible for him to maintain all those who are born in a particular land in his kingdom, if he is receiving the knowledge in the parampara, and he is a strict follower of those principles, explained in the authentic scriptures and that way the king will be a perfect representative of the Supreme Lord who is the ultimate maintainer of all living beings taking birth in this material world so for the king to be a perfect representative and therefore a proper protector of the Praja he has to be qualified he has to be trained he has to be knowledgeable he has to be having the specific qualifications so that is being maintained mentioned here so about Manu itself Prabhupada says in the purport the Manu mentioned in this verse as the father of Ikshwaku is the seventh Manu in the present day of Brahma and his name is Vaivasvata Manu so this Vaivasvata Manu is receiving the knowledge of Bhagavad Gita from his father from his father Vivasvan to whom Krishna spoke Bhagavad Gita millions of years back this Vaivasvata Manu. But in the beginning of the creation, only Brahma was there. Brahma created the Prajapatis. The Prajapatis created the first of each species. And then the species multiplied and the population increased. The whole universe was populated. All the plants were populated with different varieties of species of living beings.

### Incarnation, Time, Bhakti Yoga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yAxB7WrxnMA?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pariksit will be like Ikshwaku in maintaining everyone perfectly."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the Matsya Avatar during Vaivasvata Manu's reign, the concept of Mahayugas, and how Vaivasvata Manu learned and passed down the principles of Bhakti Yoga, emphasizing that Pariksit will be like Ikshwaku in maintaining everyone perfectly.

So it is explained here, the Lord's incarnation of Matsya Avatara, the gigantic fish, was invented during the beginning of Vaivasvata Manu's reign. Vaivasvata Manu began ruling as the Manu, the mankind, at the beginning of the creation of Brahma. At the beginning of his term, the seventh Manvantara, of which now 28 Mahayugas have already exhausted. In one Manvantara, there are 71 Mahayugas. What is the meaning of Mahayuga? You have heard of Satyavyuga, Treta Yuga, Dwapar Yuga, Kali Yuga, each of different durations. So a cycle of these four yugas on the earth together is called a Mahayuga. So one Mahayuga, like that 28 Mahayugas are already over in this Manvantara. 28 Mahayugas are already over. Totally, a Manvantara consists of 71 Mahayugas. 71 Mahayugas. So the beginning of this Vaivasvata Manu's reign, that is 28 Mahayugas before the present time, the Lord incarnated as the gigantic fish, Matsya Avatar. And the Lord, in the Matsya Avatar form, he instructed this Vaivasvata Manu in principles of Bhakti Yoga. And this Vaivasvata Manu also learned the principles of Bhakti Yoga from his father, Vivasvan, who was taught the principles of Bhakti Yoga by Krishna himself. And it is further said, that this Vaivasvata Manu, he instructed the same Bhagavad Gita, the science of Bhakti Yoga, to his son Ikshwaku. And the Ikshwaku was perfectly ruling in maintaining all those who are born in the land. So Pariksit will be like Ikshwaku in maintaining everyone perfectly. That is what is being foretold here.

**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the comparison with Lord Rama, emphasizing his adherence to Brahmanical principles and truthfulness, and describes Rama's appearance as an incarnation of the Supreme Lord who protected the sages and killed demons.

Now the next comparison is made to Lord Rama. How this Pariksit will be especially following the Brahmanical principles in being true to his promise. Like Lord Rama, who ruled millions of years back, the son of Maharaj Dasharatha. So Srila Prabhupada in the purport describes briefly about Lord Rama. Lord Rama appeared as the son of his pure devotee. Lord Rama is the Supreme Lord himself. Sakshat Bhagavan from the spiritual world is coming and descending and incarnating as Rama, son of Maharaj Dasharatha. When the Lord comes in this world, he appears as the son of his devotee or appears in an extraordinary way. Extraordinary way. So his birth is not ordinary or his appearance is not ordinary. It is extraordinary appearance. He is not bound by any laws of material nature. He does not accept the material body. He comes in a spiritual form. And his form is completely transcendental to all material modes of nature. So Rama appeared along with his plenary portions who appeared as his younger brothers. Rama had three younger brothers: Lakshmana, Bharata, Shatrughna. And all of them are the personality of Godhead. Different plenary portions. Different plenary portions. Just like Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha appeared as Rama, Lakshmana, Bharata, Shatrughna. That's what is explained elsewhere in the Bhagavatam. When this Rama was a young boy, he helped the great sage Vishwamitra by killing one Subahu, one big demon. And these demons were disturbing the sages who were performing yajna for the welfare of the people of the world. So Rama actually killed this Subahu and also he killed 14,000 Rakshasa associates of Ravana. And he specifically also killed Ravana on the request of the devatas. So Rama was known for giving perfect protection to all the people of Prajna. All the living beings in his kingdom. Similarly Parikshit also will give protection. That's what is told. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Eshadata Sharanyascha Yathahi Ausheenarashibihi Yasho Vitanitha Svanam Daushyantir Iva Yajvanam.
