### Vaman Dev, Bali Maharaj, charity, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H_NLM8da-zc?t=232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bali immediately washed the feet of this Brahmachari who is Lord himself as Vaman Dev and sprinkled that water that washes the feet of the Brahmachari on his head."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Vaman Dev's appearance as a Brahmachari in Bali Maharaj's sacrificial arena, his reception, and Bali Maharaj's offer of charity, highlighting Bali's respect and humility towards the Brahmachari, who is actually Lord Vaman Dev.

That is very nicely described here. So, Vaman Dev as soon as he appeared, he disguised himself as a small Brahmachari boy and visited the sacrificial arena where Bali Maharaj was performing sacrifices. Now, Vaman Dev appeared in the sacrificial arena of Bali Maharaj with his transcendentally effulgent form. Because he is the Supreme Lord, his form is full of transcendental effulgence called Brahmajyoti. So, normally the Lord in the material world will not display his Brahmajyoti. Then nobody will be able to see him. So, he partially displayed his transcendental effulgence and all the priests in that sacrificial arena they stood up from their seats and offered all respects and prayers to this Brahmachari. Of course, they didn't know it is Vaman Dev. So, they thought some very, very, very austere Brahmachari, some great personality. We do not know who he is. He has appeared with this effulgence which is very, very dazzling. And they stood up in respect and offered prayers to him. Now, so Vaman Dev was received very respectfully by Bali Maharaj as a Brahmachari boy. And Bali immediately washed the feet of this Brahmachari who is Lord himself as Vaman Dev and sprinkled that water that washes the feet of the Brahmachari on his head. And then Bali Maharaj inquired Vaman Dev that you may ask me for some dana, either you may ask for some money or jewels or anything else you might desire. Because I am the emperor of the world. So, you may ask me anything that you desire.

---
### Vaman Dev, Bali Maharaj, charity, desire, satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H_NLM8da-zc?t=371
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vaman Dev actually asked Bali Maharaj that I will be satisfied with just three paces of land measured by my footprint."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, focusing on Vaman Dev's request for only three paces of land, measured by his footprint, and Bali Maharaj's initial reaction, thinking the Brahmachari lacks experience in asking for boons, followed by Vaman Dev's explanation about the importance of being satisfied with minimal necessities.

Now, Vaman Dev praised Bali Maharaj. And Vaman Dev actually asked Bali Maharaj that I will be satisfied with just three paces of land measured by my footprint. A small Brahmachari boy, how much will his footprint be? So, Bali Maharaj thought this Brahmachari, even though he is so bright and effulgent and very, very, very austere, he does not have sufficient experience what to ask. So, Bali Maharaj said I can give you something much more than that. And he offered to give more. But Vaman Dev described to Bali Maharaj that one should be satisfied with the minimum necessities. Otherwise, there is no end to one's desire for possessing more and more and more. So, then Bali Maharaj hearing this enlightening words from this Brahmachari, he became very impressed and said, Oh, you are very, very learned and even realized. Okay, according to your desire, whatever you want, I am ready to give you, offer you in charity. I will give you three steps of land measured by your footprint.

---
### Bali Maharaj, Shukracharya, guru, promise, truthfulness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H_NLM8da-zc?t=473
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bali Maharaj thought it is the duty of a householder to maintain the principles of religion and he thought it improper to withdraw his promise to the Brahmachari, even if this Brahmachari is Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates how Shukracharya, Bali Maharaj's guru, intervenes and warns him about Vaman Dev's true identity and intentions, but Bali Maharaj, valuing truthfulness and his promise, decides to proceed with the charity despite the potential consequences.

So, then when he is about to accept to give, agree to give, immediately Shukracharya stopped him. Stop. My dear King, please stop. What is the matter? So, then Shukracharya said, You do not know who this Brahmachari boy is. He is actually Vishnu disguised as a Brahmachari boy. He has come here on behalf of the devatas, our enemies. And he has come to take away your entire kingdom on the plea of taking three steps of land in charity, he is going to take away your entire kingdom. So, beware, do not give what he is asking for in charity, because he has come to cheat you. So, then Bali Maharaj heard Shukracharya and he considered that Bali Maharaj thought it is the duty of a householder to maintain the principles of religion. And he thought it improper to withdraw his promise to the Brahmachari, even if this Brahmachari is Vishnu. And even if he is going to take away everything I possess still, I do not want to go back on my promise, because lying is a sinful activity. So, I should not go back on my promise. And therefore, I want to give this Brahmachari whatever I have promised, this three steps of land.

---
### Vaman Dev, Bali Maharaj, universal form, devotion, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H_NLM8da-zc?t=593
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vaman Dev expanded himself into a universal body, because he is the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how Bali Maharaj, against his guru's advice, decides to give the three steps of land, and how Vaman Dev then reveals his universal form, covering the entire universe with two steps, leaving no place for the third.

So, Bali Maharaj finally against the instruction of his guru Shukracharya, he decided to nevertheless give away three steps of land as Vaman Dev in the form of that Brahmachari had asked in charity. So, then what did Vaman Dev do? He immediately after getting that accent, ascent, ascent of the, of Bali Maharaj. Vaman Dev expanded himself into a universal body, because he is the Supreme Lord. He has got a form called the universal form. So, he displayed that universal form of his, means as big as the whole universe. And Bali Maharaj could see that the Lord is all pervading and everything resting in his body. And then Bali Maharaj could also see that this gigantic form the Brahmachari is displaying is actually the form of Lord Vishnu, because Vishnu's mark of Srivatsa and his Kaustubha jewel and his crown and his ornaments and flower garland and everything that is decorating his body, Bali Maharaj could clearly see. So, what did Vaman Dev do after expanding himself into a universal body? He covered the entire surface of the world by extending his body and covered the entire sky. So, with one step he covered the earth and all the lower planets completely. And then with his second footstep he covered the entire upper planetary system and there was no vacant place where he could place his third footstep. Then what happened? The Devatas headed by Brahma they certainly were very much attracted by the wonderful form of the Lord and his wonderful pastime of covering everything in the universe by his two footsteps. So, Brahma accompanied by great sages offered humble prayers to worship the Supreme Lord. They could recognize here is the Supreme Lord and he has appeared for the sake of favoring Indra.

---
### Bali Maharaj, Vaman Dev, patience, surrender, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H_NLM8da-zc?t=783
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Look at the patience of Bali Maharaj. He is completely bereft of all his possessions and still he is offering his own self."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story, describing how Bali Maharaj, having lost everything, offers his own head as the place for Vaman Dev's third step, pleasing the Lord and demonstrating his ultimate surrender and devotion, highlighting Bali Maharaj's extraordinary patience, which is the quality being compared to Pariksit.

So, then when Bali Maharaj was deprived of all his possessions then all the associates of Bali Maharaj became angry at this Brahmachari boy who cheated Bali and they wanted to attack this Brahmachari boy who is now in this form, universal form. And they attempted but immediately Vishnu's associates defeated all the associates of Bali Maharaj and sent them to the lower planets. And then Bali Maharaj was arrested by the ropes of Varuna by Garuda the carrier of Lord Vishnu. But when Lord Vishnu said to Bali Maharaj you have not fulfilled your promise to give me three steps of land. Where do I place my third step? So, then Bali Maharaj, he explained to Lord Vamana, Lord Vishnu that I have given away whatever is my possession, but I am still there. So, I will give my head in charity as the place for Lord Vishnu's third step. Now, that was very very pleasing to Lord Vishnu. Look at the patience of Bali Maharaj. He is completely bereft of all his possessions. He has been taken, all his possessions have been taken away and he is nothing and still he is offering his own self, Atma Nivedanam. Of course, Bali Maharaj is a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. So, the Lord performed this pastime to glorify Bali Maharaj and show to the whole world that my devotees are so very dedicated to my seva that they are willing to give up everything just to please me, to satisfy me, to serve me. In this way, Bali Maharaj proved to be very very successful in his conduct as giving charity. So, his patience was tested by Lord Vamana Dev and he became successful. So, similarly Pariksit also will be very very patient. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### devotion, opposition, education
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mn9k_EBop_o?t=122
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada says a perfect devotee of Lord Krishna, his father, Prahlad's father, Hiranyakashyapu, chastised him severely when he was only five years old, for his becoming an unalloyed devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to narrate the history of Prahlad Maharaj, focusing on his devotion to Lord Krishna despite the opposition from his demoniac father, Hiranyakashipu, and his initial education under Shanda and Amarka.

he gives a brief description of the history of Prahlad Maharaj. Prabhupada says a perfect devotee of Lord Krishna, his father, Prahlad's father, Hiranyakashyapu, chastised him severely when he was only five years old, for his becoming an unalloyed devotee of the Lord. This is the history of Prahlad Maharaj. So, the details of Prahlad Maharaj's history is given in the seventh canto of the Bhagavatam. So, I will try to narrate in brief some more details about the history of Prahlad Maharaj. It is said in the seventh canto that by severe austerities, this demon Hiranyakashipu satisfied Lord Brahma and obtained the benedictions he desired. Nonetheless, he continued to be envious of Lord Vishnu. He conquered the three worlds. He brought all living entities, both demigods and asuras under his control. All the demigods except Lord Vishnu, Lord Brahma and Lord Shiva came under his control and began serving him. Practically, Hiranyakashipu terrorized the whole universe. Since Hiranyakashipu was the oppressor of all the demigods and envious of the Supreme Lord, he would naturally be killed. Hiranyakashipu had four sons of whom the eldest Prahlad was a Mahabhagavata. Hiranyakashipu's spiritual master Shukracharya had two sons, Shanda and Amarka. Now, Prahlad Maharaj was entrusted to Shanda and Amarka for education.

---
### devotion, teaching, conflict
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mn9k_EBop_o?t=256
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prahlad taught the teachers that the mentality of ownership is false and that he was therefore trying to become an unalloyed devotee of the Lord Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, explaining how Prahlad Maharaj resisted the material education of his teachers and instead remained a pure devotee, even teaching them about the falsity of material ownership and the importance of devotion to Lord Vishnu.

Although the teachers tried to educate the boy Prahlad Maharaj, in politics, economics and other material activities, he did not care for their instructions. Instead, Prahlad continued to be a pure devotee. Prahlad never liked the idea of discriminating between one's friends and enemies because he was spiritually inclined, he was equal to everyone. Once upon a time, Hiranyakashipu inquired from his son Prahlad, what was the best thing that Prahlad had learned from the teachers. So, Prahlad Maharaj replied that a man engrossed in material consciousness of duality, thinking this is mine and something else belongs to an enemy, such a person should give up his householder life and go to the forest to worship the Supreme Personality of Godhead. When Hiranyakashipu heard this from his son, speaking about devotion and service, Hiranyakashipu decided this small boy has been polluted by some friend in school. So, he advised the teacher, to take care of the boy so that he would not become a devotee of Krishna. However, when the teachers inquired from Prahlad, why he was going against their teachings, Prahlad taught the teachers that the mentality of ownership is false and that he was therefore trying to become an unalloyed devotee of the Lord Vishnu.

---
### devotion, punishment, teachings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mn9k_EBop_o?t=375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prahlad explained that the duty of every man is to take shelter of a pure devotee and thus become eligible to understand Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The narrative progresses with the teachers chastising Prahlad and bringing him before Hiranyakashipu. Prahlad continues to glorify devotional service, angering his father further. Prahlad emphasizes the importance of taking shelter of a pure devotee to understand Krishna consciousness.

The teachers being very angry, chastised and threatened the boy with many fearful conditions. They taught him to the best of their ability and then brought him before his father. So, Hiranyakashipu affectionately took his son Prahlad on his lap and then inquired from him what was the best thing that he had learned from his teachers. As usual, Prahlad Maharaj began praising the nine processes of devotional service such as Shravanam and Kirtanam, Vishnu. The king of the demons, Hiranyakashipu, being extremely angry, chastised the teachers, Shanda and Amarka, for having wrongly trained Prahlad Maharaj. The so-called teachers informed King Hiranyakashipu, that Prahlad Maharaj was automatically a devotee and did not listen to their instructions at all. When they proved themselves innocent, Hiranyakashipu inquired from Prahlad, where have you learned about Vishnu Bhakti? Prahlad Maharaj's reply was very profound. Prahlad Maharaj said, those who are attached to family life do not develop Krishna consciousness, either personally or collectively. Instead, they suffer repeated birth and death in the material world and continue simply chewing the chewed. Prahlad explained that the duty of every man is to take shelter of a pure devotee and thus become eligible to understand Krishna consciousness.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Since Prahlad was so treacherous that he had become a devotee of Vishnu, who had killed his uncle Hiranyakashipu, Hiranyakashipu asked his assistants to kill him."
**Summary:** Hiranyakashipu, enraged by Prahlad's devotion, orders his assistants to kill him. Despite numerous attempts, Prahlad remains unharmed due to the protection of the Supreme Lord, a point Hiranyakashipu fails to grasp.

Hiranyakashipu became angry. He was very, very angry hearing this answer. He threw this small boy down from his lap. Since Prahlad was so treacherous that he had become a devotee of Vishnu, who had killed his uncle Hiranyakashipu, Hiranyakashipu asked his assistants to kill him. Now the assistants of Hiranyakashipu struck Prahlad with sharp weapons, threw him under the feet of elephants, subjected him to hellish conditions, threw him from the peak of a mountain, tried to kill him in thousands of other ways, but were always unsuccessful. Hiranyakashipu therefore became increasingly afraid of his son Prahlad and arrested him. Can you imagine? A small five-year-old boy, he is just innocently practicing bhakti, Vishnu bhakti, and his father is so angry, he is trying to kill the boy. And even after being unsuccessful, nothing is striking him that this boy is completely protected. Nobody can do anything to the boy because he is protected by the Supreme Lord. Hiranyakashipu is not understanding this point.

**Key Quote:** "Prahlad began to preach Krishna Consciousness to the classmates. And following the instructions of Prahlad, all the sons of the demons who were Prahlad's classmates became attached to Lord Vishnu."
**Summary:** The teachers attempt to change Prahlad's mind, but he begins preaching Krishna consciousness to his classmates, leading them to become devoted to Lord Vishnu. This causes fear and concern among the teachers, who then inform Hiranyakashipu.

But the sons of Shukracharya, Chandana Marka, or the teachers of Prahlad, they pacified Hiranyakashipu and told, we will still try to change his mind. He is after all a small boy. He is speaking based on whatever he knows from the past. So we will try to change his mind as he grows up. Don't worry. So while the teachers were absent from the classroom, Prahlad began to preach Krishna Consciousness to the classmates. And following the instructions of Prahlad, all the sons of the demons who were Prahlad's classmates became attached to Lord Vishnu. When this attachment became pronounced, Shanda and Amarka were very afraid that the boys would become more and more devoted to Lord Vishnu. In a helpless condition, they approached Hiranyakashipu and described in detail the effect of Prahlad's preaching.

**Summary:** Hiranyakashipu decides to kill Prahlad himself. Prahlad's attempts to pacify his father fail, and Hiranyakashipu proclaims himself greater than Vishnu. Prahlad challenges this, asserting Vishnu's all-pervasiveness, leading Hiranyakashipu to question if God exists within the palace columns.

After hearing of this, Hiranyakashipu decided to kill his son Prahlad personally. Hiranyakashipu was so angry that Prahlad Maharaj fell down at his feet and said things like, just to pacify him. But Prahlad was unsuccessful in satisfying his father. Hiranyakashipu as a typical demon began to advertise himself as being greater than the Supreme Personality of Godhead Vishnu. But Prahlad Maharaj challenged him saying that Hiranyakashipu was not God. And Prahlad began to glorify the Supreme Personality of Godhead Lord Vishnu, declaring that Lord Vishnu is all-pervading. Everything is under his control. No one is equal to or greater than Lord Vishnu. Thus he requested his father, please be submissive to the Omnipotent Supreme Lord. The more Prahlad glorified Vishnu, the more angry and agitated became Hiranyakashipu. Hiranyakashipu asked his Vaishnava son whether God existed within the columns of the palace. And Prahlad said yes. He was everywhere, so he is also in the pillar. When Hiranyakashipu heard this,

### divine intervention, protection, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mn9k_EBop_o?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Suddenly there issued a tumultuous sound and the Supreme Lord in his wonderful form of Narasimha, half man, half lion, came out of the pillar."
**Summary:** Hiranyakashipu strikes a pillar, and Lord Narasimha emerges, signifying divine intervention. Narasimha engages in a pastime of fighting with Hiranyakashipu before killing him, liberating the universe from his oppressive rule.

he struck his fist with all force against the pillar. Let me see your Lord in this pillar. Suddenly there issued a tumultuous sound and the Supreme Lord in his wonderful form of Narasimha, half man, half lion, came out of the pillar. And Hiranyakashipu could understand that this form of the personality who had appeared out of the pillar was actually meant for his death. And he began to fight with Lord Narasimha. The Lord performed some pastime of fighting with the demon for some time. And in the evening, the border between day and night, the Supreme Lord captured the demon, threw him on his lap and killed him by piercing his abdomen with his nails and tearing apart his body into pieces. Then the associates of Hiranyakashipu came to attack Lord Narasimha. But Lord Narasimha easily killed every one of them. When there was no one else to fight, the Supreme Lord sat on Hiranyakashipu's throne and then the whole universe was relieved of the rule of Hiranyakashipu and everyone was jubilant with transcendental bliss.

### wisdom, devotion, kali yuga, cow protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uiOtHfMjHOk?t=87
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The wisest man in the world is a devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's explanation emphasizes that the wisest man is a devotee of the Lord, and that wise kings are needed to control the wise men in the state. The lecture then discusses Maharaja Pariksit and his chastisement of Kali Purusha for attempting to kill a cow, highlighting the importance of cow protection and the symptoms of Kali Yuga: wine, women, gambling, and slaughterhouses.

The wisest man in the world is a devotee of the Lord. Ye Krishna bhaje se bada chatura. Also Krishna says, Tesham satata yuktaanam bhajatam pritipurvakam dadami buddhi yogam tam. So the wisest man is always a devotee of the Supreme Lord. The wisest man is always a devotee of the Supreme Lord. The sages are called wise men and there are different types of wise men for different branches of knowledge. Unless therefore the king or the head of the state is the wisest man, he cannot control all types of wise men in the state. So Prabhupada is explaining what is the necessity for the king to be the wisest because he is to control different types of wise people in the state. In the line of royal succession in the family of Maharaja Yudhishthira, all the kings without exception were the wisest men of their times. And so also it is foretold about Maharaja Pariksit and his son Maharaja Janamejaya who was yet to be born. Such wise kings can become chastisers of upstarts and uprooters of kali or quarrelsome. As will be clear in the chapters ahead, the later chapters of this canto, Maharaja Pariksit wanted to kill the personified kali who was attempting to kill a cow, the emblem of peace and religion. Kali Purusha, personified kali, was trying to enter the kingdom of Pariksit Maharaj. When Pariksit Maharaj is ruling, that is going to be described in the next few chapters. And this Kali Purusha was chastised severely by Pariksit Maharaj. Why? Because this Kali Purusha was trying to kill a cow. It was unheard of, unknown during Pariksit Maharaj's time. Nobody would even hurt a cow in any minor way. Forget about killing a cow, that was unknown. But in such a place, at such a time, Kali Purusha attempting to kill a cow was intolerable for Maharaja Pariksit. And Srila Prabhupada says further, the symptoms of kali are wine, women, gambling and slaughterhouses. Four symptoms of kali yuga. What are they? Wine, women, gambling, slaughterhouses.

### maharaja pariksit, morality, kali yuga, hedonism

It emphasizes that while meat-eating existed in the past, it was restricted, unlike the rampant and openly advertised hedonistic practices of today, which are ruining society. The solution is to adopt the principles followed by Maharaja Pariksit and create an atmosphere of peace and prosperity through Srila Prabhupada's suggested methods.

Therefore, Srila Prabhupada says wise rulers of all states should take lessons from Maharaj Pariksit in how to maintain peace and morality by subduing the upstarts and quarrelsome people who indulge in wine, illicit connection with women, gambling and meat eating supplied by regularly maintained slaughterhouses. This has to stop. It's not that there was no meat eating in the former times, but it was restricted, highly restricted, and there was no mass killing of animals, open sale of meat or liquor. No, this was never there in the former times. So some few people would indulge in these very, very a hidden way. So that was the situation in the former times. But now it has become very much rampant in the society and openly they're selling, advertising, making propaganda. Come, enjoy, eat, drink, be merry. The hedonistic philosophy, which is going to ruin the whole society. So therefore we should adopt the principles followed by Maharaj Pariksit and thereby create a good atmosphere of peace and prosperity through the method suggested here by Srila Prabhupada. The meaning of this verse is: After hearing about his life, his death, which will be caused by the bite of a snake bird sent by a son of a brahmana, he will get himself freed from all material attachment and surrender unto the personality of Godhead taking shelter of him. and he was always searching, where is this person or this beautiful personality I have seen? I want to see him. I am searching for him. Searching, searching. So he became known as the examiner Pariksit. That's how he got that name, Pariksit, examiner. He is examining everybody. Are you the person I have seen? Are you the person I have seen? Are you the person I have seen? So he is searching for Krishna, because Krishna is so attractive. So, being in the womb of the Supreme Lord, being a examiner, always he was Krishna conscious. So he was always continuously Krishna conscious and therefore under the shelter of Lord Krishna, even before he got notice of death. But when he got the warning of his death within seven days, that was the end of his life. That curse of the Brahmana proved to be a boon to him, to enable him to prepare himself to go back to Godhead.

### karma, mistake, anger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/id1OH3UT09Y?t=462
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story

**Key Quote:** "Pariksit became angry. He took one dead snake that was just lying outside the ashram of that Shamika Rishi, garlanded the sage who was sitting in meditation room with that dead snake and walked away from that place."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to narrate the events leading to Parikshit Maharaj's curse, describing his hunting trip, his thirst, his encounter with Shamika Rishi, and his disrespectful act of garlanding the sage with a dead snake.

Now, briefly I will just mention what happened. As regards the curse by the Brahmana boy, Pariksit as the emperor of the world, he was ruling from his capital city, Hastinapura. He once went hunting, because the Kshatriyas had to practice killing to keep themselves fit for giving protection to the citizens. So he went hunting and he became thirsty. He started searching for water. He came across the ashram of one Shamika Rishi. He went inside that ashram and he saw that there was a sage sitting there, half-closed eyes. The sage was in meditation. So the sage was not conscious of his externals. But Pariksit, being very thirsty, not finding water, not being welcomed by this sage, he mistook the sage to be feigning to be meditating in order to avoid receiving the king. So Pariksit became angry. He took one dead snake that was just lying outside the ashram of that Shamika Rishi, garlanded the sage who was sitting in meditation room with that dead snake and walked away from that place.

As he was going away, Pariksit reflected on what he did. Then he began to consider, maybe the sage was actually in trance. He was not externally conscious. So how could he have received me properly? How could he have even noticed me entering his ashram? And why did I insult the sage in that way? He doesn't deserve to be insulted in that way at all. So reflecting on his action, Pariksit began to feel very, very repentant for having insulted that great sage. And he began to think, may the sage's wrath or anger burn me and my entire kingdom to ashes immediately now. I deserve the worst kind of punishment for having misbehaved in this way.

sent word to Pariksit that Pariksit has been cursed by this immature, a foolish boy misusing his paraminical power. In this session, we will be discussing from the 28th verse of the 12th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Jigna Sitaatam, Yatharthyo Muner Vyasa Sutadasau, Hithvedam Rupa Gangayam, Yasyati Adha Akutobhayam. The meaning of this verse is: After inquiring about proper self-knowledge from the son of Vyasa Deva, who will be a great philosopher, he will renounce all material attachments and achieve a life of fearlessness, Akutobhayam. So Srila Prabhupada explains this is the foretelling about what will happen when he is cursed to die within seven days.

aší dot accumulations of the leadership powers to create a spiritual nature. ### anxiety, material existence, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RA-ZR6IJrJ4?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually the fearfulness in this material world is called anxiety; everyone in material life in all species and varieties of life is full of anxiety."
**Summary:** This segment describes the material world as a prison house filled with anxiety, even for those who claim to be fearless, and highlights the common desire for sense gratification as a source of anxiety.

Its prisoners are always fearful as within a prison house. This material world has been discovered as a prison house. Just like in a prison house, no one can violate the jail rules and regulations, and if they do violate, that means another term for extension of prison life. Similarly, in this material existence, we are always fearful. Somebody may say I'm not afraid of death, I'm not afraid of anything, I'm a bold person, courageous person. Somebody may say that, but as soon as there is some danger to his life, immediately he starts becoming fearful. Till there is no danger or there's no apparent difficulty or problem, people may speak so many things, but they stop speaking all this rubbish when there is actual danger for their life. Actually the fearfulness in this material world is called anxiety; everyone in material life in all species and varieties of life is full of anxiety. Either by breaking or without breaking the laws of nature, some people they break the laws of nature because they are sinful, and some people are avoiding sin, so they adhere to the laws as far as possible, but both the categories of people have got one common desire for sense gratification, material enjoyment. So such people anyway are always in anxiety.

### devotional service, anxiety, Shukadeva Goswami

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment explains that relief from anxiety is possible only through devotional service to Krishna, emphasizing the unique guidance provided by Shukadeva Goswami in preparing for death and achieving perfection.

And this material body is always subject to disease, old age, death, misery, suffering. So always in anxiety, always in anxiety. So how is it possible to get relief from all anxieties? That is possible only when the anxiety is changed to devotional service of the Supreme Lord. We should not simply try to counteract anxiety by some material methods, which we generally do. No, that's not going to be successful. We should turn to devotional service of Krishna. Only way to become completely free from all anxieties. So Srila Prabhupada says, Srimad Bhagavatam gives us the chance to change the quality of anxiety from matter to spirit, to become the true self. So this is possible only when we are not enlightened about our real self. How is this done? This is done in the association of a learned philosopher like the self-realized Shukadeva Goswami, the great son of Sri Vyasadeva. Shukadeva Goswami instructed Parikshit Maharaj what should Parikshit actually do to prepare for death and achieve perfection, going back to Godhead after quitting the body. Other saintly persons could not give this assurance to the entire world.

What is the difference to Parikshit? That means all other methods, other than the methods suggested by Shukadeva Goswami, are not effective in helping us to prepare for death in such a way that at the end of this life, we can go back to Godhead. So Shukadeva Goswami recommended to Parikshit Maharaj that you become Krishna conscious by hearing about Krishna, by hearing about Krishna's activities, Krishna's names, Krishna's forms, Krishna's pastimes, Krishna's devotees, Krishna's paraphernalia, Krishna's creation. Everything in connection with Krishna. Krishna Katha. You hear Krishna Katha. Absorb your mind in Krishna Katha and become completely transcendental to everything material. And in that way, if you are situated at the time of death, then you straight go back to Godhead.

And that's exactly what Parikshit did. This is Parikshit Maharaj's good fortune that he was very anxious when he was sitting for, preparing for death. He was very anxious to somehow hear about Krishna, think about Krishna, talk about Krishna, engage in devotional service to Krishna. And when he got the association of Shukadeva Goswami, he took full advantage of hearing about Krishna and getting to know about Krishna properly, and thereby becoming attached to Krishna, automatically detached from everything material. And he developed that attraction for Krishna by which he straight went back to Godhead after quitting the present body.

### Srimad Bhagavatam, hearing, nityam Bhagavata Sevaya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RA-ZR6IJrJ4?t=850
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should not only hear seven days but we should hear every day for rest of the life, the Srimad Bhagavatam."
**Summary:** This segment warns against the ineffective imitation of Shukadeva Goswami's seven-day discourse and emphasizes the importance of hearing Srimad Bhagavatam daily for the rest of one's life to prepare for returning to Godhead.

Srila Prabhupada warns that because Shukadeva Goswami spoke for seven days the stream of Bhagavatam to Parikshit Maharaj, other people are imitating Shukadeva Goswami by speaking Bhagavatam for only one week. They call it Bhagavata Saptaha, but this Saptaha is not at all effective. We can clearly see. So, for us to be successful in preparing ourselves to go back to Godhead, we should not only hear seven days but we should hear every day for rest of the life, the Srimad Bhagavatam. That is the recommendation of Bhagavatam, Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya. And in that way we can prepare ourselves to go back to Godhead. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.29 part 1 | 09-09-2022

### suffering, material existence, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eSUCM6nqK1M?t=265
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We cannot be completely free from all suffering in material existence, simply by performing pious activities, materially pious activities."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the four fundamental miseries and three kinds of other miseries imposed upon everyone in the material creation, emphasizing that even pious activities cannot completely free one from suffering.

Implicated because of which we can lead a happy, healthy, prosperous life in this world, while that is partially correct, it is not the complete truth. Under the influence of illusion, we don't take note of the fact that there are four fundamental miseries and three kinds of fundamental other miseries which are imposed upon everyone in the whole material creation, beginning from Brahma down to the most insignificant creature. Brahma, situated in the highest mode of goodness, even he is afraid of death, even he is in anxiety. Even Hiranyakashipu terrorized the whole universe, so nobody is freed from fear, anxiety. Asad grahat, you accept material existence via this material body and you're forced to suffer four fundamental miseries: janma, mrutyu, jara, vyadhi, dukkha, dosha, anudarshanam. I mentioned this several times in the past, and also the three impositions of material nature: adhi daivika klesha, adhyatmika klesha, adhibhautika klesha. Everyone suffers this throughout the universe, so we cannot be completely free from all suffering in material existence, simply by performing pious activities, materially pious activities.

---
### spiritual knowledge, self-realization, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eSUCM6nqK1M?t=383
**Verse References:** BG 2.0
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Vedas instruct us, especially Bhagavad Gita, which gives the essence of the Vedic wisdom, the spiritual wisdom in the Vedas, that you are spirit soul."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the importance of spiritual knowledge from the Vedas, particularly the Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing self-realization as a spirit soul and attaining spiritual bliss through Krishna consciousness.

So, that is particularly highlighted in the spiritual knowledge given in this book, in the Vedas, while people are engrossed in the material knowledge, either given by modern books of knowledge, or given by the Vedas, they forget the ultimate reality of material existence, which in essence is simply suffering. All those who have born, have to die. In the Bhagavad Gita, second chapter, Krishna says, of one who has taken birth in this material world, it is certain that such person will have to die, and the example is in the Bhagavatam, Hiranyakashipu who wanted to be immortal in that very body that he had taken birth, and Brahma said, no, you cannot be immortal in this body, nobody can be immortal, even Brahma cannot be immortal in the same body in which he has taken birth, even he has to leave his body. Therefore, the Vedas instruct us, especially Bhagavad Gita, which gives the essence of the Vedic wisdom, the spiritual wisdom in the Vedas, that you are spirit soul, and as spirit soul, you are entitled to spiritual bliss, eternal life of spiritual bliss and spiritual knowledge, Satchidananda, state of existence, and that is possible by realizing yourself as spiritual by nature, same quality as Krishna, the Supreme, and such realization is perfected by Krishna consciousness, by bhakti yoga, not by any other method.

### Vedas, spiritual salvation, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eSUCM6nqK1M?t=533
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Merely becoming free from all material bondage without realizing one's own spiritual self is imperfect because as spirit soul, we are always active."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's view that the Vedas guide civilized man towards freedom from material miseries and spiritual salvation, emphasizing that liberation without self-realization is imperfect because the spirit soul is always active.

Therefore, Srila Prabhupada says, the knowledge of the Vedas ultimately aims at perfection of self-realization. In other words, Srila Prabhupada says, the Vedas are the guides for the civilized man in every respect, since human life is the opportunity to get free from all material miseries, it is properly guided by the knowledge of the Vedas in the matters of both material needs and spiritual salvation. Material needs and spiritual salvation. Material necessities are there of the body, but that is very easy, taken care of by following the Vedic directions and most importantly if we follow the Vedas then we can also achieve spiritual salvation, spiritual liberation. There is a material conception of liberation where one merely thinks that let me become free from all material problems or material miseries or material bondage, but merely becoming free from all material bondage without realizing one's own spiritual self is imperfect because as spirit soul, we are always active. We cannot be inactive, we cannot be without activity as a spirit soul. So those who become free from longevity and physical liberation because they are free from the mind, mental appetite, they can innately become free from their inner activity, they can become free from everything, they feel free from death and all physical and material self. It is one of the most important aspects that life libretto lives are Graveос in mining and efflores advisory services a month prior to their mark from material bondage by any process other than bhakti but do not realize their spiritual self.

### devotional service, spiritual existence, bhakti yoga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eSUCM6nqK1M?t=679
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotional service to Krishna is not only freedom from material bondage but also engagement in the spiritual state of existence."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that those who attain freedom from material bondage without realizing their spiritual self cannot sustain that freedom, emphasizing that pure devotional service to Krishna leads to both liberation and engagement in the spiritual state of existence through bhakti yoga.

They actually do not sustain their state of freedom from material bondage because they do not know about the activities after becoming free from material bondage if they are not engaged in pure devotional service to Krishna. So pure devotional service to Krishna is not only freedom from material bondage but also engagement in the spiritual state of existence, active service to Krishna, spiritual state of existence. So therefore we need not only to fulfill the demands of this body, the material needs, but also work for attaining the spiritual state of existence beyond this material existence, and that is possible only by bhakti, bhakti yoga.

**Key Quote:** "By birth everybody is ignorant."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of Vedic knowledge and brahmanical culture, explaining that everyone is born ignorant and needs purification through samskaras, even if born into a brahmana family.

Further Srila Prabhupada says the specific intelligent class of men who were devoted particularly to the knowledge of the vedas were called the vipras or the graduates of the vedic knowledge. There are four stages of this development in terms of the brahmanical culture. What do the scriptures say? Janmana jayate shudra, by birth everybody is ignorant. Shudra here means one is born ignorant. Please explain in the Bhagavad-gita also, icha dvesha samuthena dvandva mohena bharata sarva bhutani sammoham sargayanti parantapa, everyone is born into ignorance in this material world, even if one takes birth in a brahmana family with good parents, very nice cultured family, very nice one may have a very nice upbringing but it is necessary to actually undergo samskara purification, cultural purification, even after taking birth in a brahmana family and that's what exactly Yudhishthira Maharaj is doing, he is doing this samskara called jatakarma for the child Parikshit which is performed by qualified brahmanas and the vedas further say janmana jayate shudra samskara vijam ucyate, one gets a second birth, the first birth is by the parents, the mother and father who conceive a child.

---
### samskara, spiritual progress, upanayana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eSUCM6nqK1M?t=883
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The particular samskara which actually gives one the status of being twice born, second birth is called upanayana, the sacred thread ceremony."
**Summary:** The speaker continues discussing samskaras, explaining that cultural purification gives one a second birth, with upanayana (sacred thread ceremony) being the samskara that confers the status of being twice-born, which is required for spiritual progress.

The second birth is by cultural purification, samskara. There are ten kinds of samskara, dashavida samskara, and jatakarma is one of them. So samskara vijam ucyate, the particular samskara which actually gives one the status of being twice born, second birth is called upanayana, the sacred thread ceremony. So this samskara is required for advancement or progress on the spiritual path in the human form of life. So I'll stop here, we'll discuss more in the next session.

In this session we will be discussing from the third verse of the twelfth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter describes birth of Emperor Pariksit. Tad idam srotum ichchamo gaditum yadimanyase bruhina shraddadhananam yasyagyanam adachukaha. The meaning of this verse is: We all respectfully want to hear about him, that is Maharaj Pariksit, to whom Sukadev Goswami imparted transcendental knowledge, please speak on this matter. And Maharaj Pariksit heard him properly, just like an ardent student. The effect of such a bona fide hearing and chanting of Srimad Bhagavatam was equally shared by both the hearer and the chanter. Both of them were benefited.

Elsewhere it is explained that there are nine different ways of executing devotional service, Navavidha Bhakti, and nine different personalities, they attained perfection of self-realization simply by adopting one of the nine ways of doing self-realization. And in that description it is said Pariksit Maharaj attained perfection simply by hearing properly the Srimad Bhagavatam as was spoken to him by Sukadev Goswami. Then it is said Sukadev Goswami attained perfection of self-realization simply by speaking Srimad Bhagavatam to Pariksit Maharaj and all the assembled saintly persons. So the benefit of speaking Bhagavatam was shared by the hearer and the chanter. Hearer was Pariksit, chanter was Sukadev Goswami. So Srila Prabhupada says both of them were benefited. Pariksit Maharaj as well as Sukadev Goswami.

### devotional service, hearing, chanting, false gurus, criticism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MNtsWlWJ0Dk?t=242
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Transcendental realization is attained by such serious hearing and chanting and not otherwise."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada, emphasizing the importance of serious hearing and chanting for transcendental realization. He then criticizes bogus spiritual practices involving physical touch and electric current, warning against such misleading advertisements.

Out of the nine different transcendental means of devotional service to the Lord prescribed in the Bhagavatam, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu, Asmaranam, Padusevanam, Archanam, Vandanam, Dasyam, Sukhyam, Atmanivedanam, either all of them, or some of them, or even one of them, were benefited, equally beneficial if properly discharged. Maharaj Pariksit and Shukadev Goswami were serious performers of the first two items namely the process of chanting and the process of hearing and therefore both of them were successful in their laudable attempt. Srila Prabhupada is now going to tell a very very important point for all of us to note regarding devotional service. Transcendental realization is attained by such serious hearing and chanting and not otherwise. There is a type of spiritual visualization that has made perfect sense to all of us and to wish master and disciple much advertised in this age of Kali. It is said that the master injects spiritual force into the disciple by an electric current generated by the master and the disciple begins to feel the shock. He becomes unconscious and the master weeps for his exhausting his store of so-called spiritual assets. Such bogus advertisement is going on in this age and the poor common man is becoming the victim of such advertisement.

### false gurus, criticism, scriptural authority, cheating
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MNtsWlWJ0Dk?t=365
**Verse References:** —

So, Srila Prabhupada is talking about the bogus methods adopted by some bogus spiritualists or spiritual masters or spiritual learners. Both are bogus because nowhere in the scriptures you can find any description that the master is touching the disciple, the spiritual master is touching the disciple and some spiritual force is injected. All this is bogus. This is rubbish, nonsense. There is no such thing in the entire Vedic literature. And further you see the description here of this bogus people that some by the touch some electrical current is generated and the disciple feels some kind of shock. And suddenly the disciple becomes unconscious because of the shock. And this bogus spiritual master, he begins to cry. Oh, by touching you, my spiritual asset is completely become nil. I have lost all the knowledge that I had. This kind of advertisement is going on and it's being glorified. This is a great enlightenment of the disciple by the touch of the master. This is totally bogus.

### scriptural authority, hearing, questioning, false gurus, criticism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MNtsWlWJ0Dk?t=481
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "On the other hand, we find in every Upanishad, in every Purana, we find they are sitting and hearing, asking intelligent questions and the spiritual master is speaking coherently, very, very specifically answering questions."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the bogus practices with the authentic Vedic tradition of sitting, hearing, and asking intelligent questions. He emphasizes that true spiritual knowledge is gained through coherent teaching and learning, not through physical touch or sensational experiences.

Nowhere in the entire Vedic, authentic Vedic literature you find that somebody is touching and then he becomes bereft of all his transcendental knowledge and the disciple is enlightened and the disciple is enlightened. The disciple feels shock. All these bogus things are never there in any authentic scriptures. On the other hand, we find in every Upanishad, in every Purana, we find they are sitting and hearing, asking intelligent questions and the spiritual master is speaking coherently, very, very specifically answering questions. The spiritual master is answering the question to the point without any of this kind of touching and shock and unconscious experience. None of these bogus things are there in the scriptures. So Srila Prabhupada is warning us, please don't become victim of such false propaganda. These are all for... Innocent, ignorant, poor people who do not know what is spiritual life and they are somehow attracted by some advertisement and they are cheated, completely cheated. Nothing is going to happen by somebody touching that some touch will make somebody enlightened. No such thing. No such thing. No such thing is there.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses to be discussed and explains how Pariksit was raised with great care by his grandfathers after the foretelling of the brahmanas.

In this session we will be discussing three verses, verses 31, 32 and 33 of the twelfth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The thirty-first verse is as follows. The meaning of this verse is as the moon in its waxing fortnight develops day after day, so the royal prince Pariksit very soon developed luxuriantly under the care and full facilities of his guardian grandfathers. So after the foretelling of the brahmanas, the child was raised. Pariksit was raised very nicely by the grandfathers, the five Pandava brothers, the Pandavas, headed by Yudhishthira. The only surviving descendant was this Pariksit. So therefore this Pariksit was raised very nicely and he had good care and attention of all the grandfathers, all the five grandfathers.

### sacrifice, guilt, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I9l26DM7yNM?t=131
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.32
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, in order to become free from that guilt, he heard instructions of Bhishma Deva."
**Summary:** The speaker explains verse 32, detailing Yudhishthira's desire to perform a horse sacrifice to atone for the sins of the Kurukshetra war and his lack of funds, as well as Bhishma Deva's instructions.

The thirty-second verse. Yatsyama. Yatsyama no ashwamedhena nyati droha jihasaya raja labdh dhano dadyau nan yatra karadandayoh. The meaning of this verse is just at this time, King Yudhishthira was considering performing a horse sacrifice to get freed from sins incurred from fighting with kinsmen, but he became anxious to get some wealth for there were no surplus funds, funds outside of fines and tax collection. So, Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, as the brahmanas and viparas had a right to be subsidized by the state, the state executive head had the right to collect taxes and fines from the citizens. After the battle of Kurukshetra, the state treasury was exhausted and therefore there was no surplus fund except the fund from tax collections and fines. Such funds were sufficient only for the state budget and having no excess fund, the king was anxious to get more wealth in some other way in order to perform the horse sacrifice. Maharaja Yudhishthira wanted to perform the sacrifice under the instruction of Bhishma Deva. So, the history is that after the battle of Kurukshetra, Yudhishthira was thinking that he was responsible for the massacre of such a huge number of Kshatriyas who died in the battle. So, he felt very guilty. Just for me to become the emperor, so many had to be killed. So, in order to become free from that guilt, he heard instructions of Bhishma Deva. And Bhishma Deva instructed him that you are not at all at fault because it is the dharma yudha, and even if you feel that you want to, you have become sinful somehow, you can become free from those residual sins if any, by simply performing this aswam idaiyajna. So, Yudhishthira Maharaj wanted to perform this because Bhishma Deva instructed and he had no surplus funds outside of what he considered the– Whatever taxes he was collecting and fines he was imposing on the citizens and that was just enough for running the state, day to day state administration. So how will he get extra wealth? That is described in the next verse.

---
### divine guidance, sacrifice, wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I9l26DM7yNM?t=300
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.33, BG 2.47
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is of course the Supreme Lord, who is all-knowing."
**Summary:** The speaker explains verse 33, describing how Yudhishthira's brothers, guided by Krishna, went to collect riches from the north to facilitate the Ashwamedha yajna, emphasizing the importance of sacrifice for pleasing Lord Vishnu.

The 33rd verse, tad abhipretam alaksya, brātaro achyuta choditāha, dhanam prahīnam ājahror, udīcchyām diṣi bhūriṣaḥ. The meaning of this 33rd verse is, understanding the hearty wishes of the king, his brothers, as advised by the infallible Lord Krishna, collected sufficient riches from the north left by king. So Yudhishthira sent his brothers, four brothers, Bhima, Arjuna, Nakula, Sahadeva, to search for wealth and collect that extra wealth and bring it to Yudhishthira so that Yudhishthira can perform this Ashwamedha yajña. Such sacrifices are performed and by kings only because it requires huge arrangements, huge amount of wealth and that is only possible to be organized or arranged by a king. And the emperor, when he performs at the scale of befitting his position as emperor of the whole world, so then he has to organize in a very big way. So for Yudhishthira to organize in a big way the Ashwamedha yajña, his four brothers went to collect riches from the north. Now they were advised by the Supreme Lord Krishna as to where they can find the wealth. Krishna is of course the Supreme Lord, who is all-knowing. But still Krishna wanted to encourage Yudhishthira to perform that sacrifice. And ultimately sacrifices are meant for pleasing Lord Vishnu. So Yudhishthira went to Yudhishthira and told him, Yajñārthāt karmaṇo anyatra lokayam karma-bandhanah tadartham karma kaunteya mukta-saṅga-samācarāt. So tadartham, the sacrifice ultimately is meant for pleasing Lord Vishnu, the act of sacrifice. So Yudhishthira Maharaj wanted to perform this sacrifice and he required wealth for arranging it. And the four brothers whom he sent for fetching wealth, they were guided by Krishna.

### sacrifice, wealth, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I9l26DM7yNM?t=475
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He was so powerful an emperor that at the end of the day's end of sacrifice, the demigods from the other planets like Indra, Chandra and Brihaspati used to visit his palace."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the history of Maruta Maharaj, a predecessor king of Yudhishthira, highlighting his devotion, wealth, and the grand scale of his sacrifices, which attracted demigods from other planets.

So Prabhupāda describes the history of this Maruta Maharaj, who was a predecessor king of Yudhishthira. So Mahārāja Maruta, one of the great emperors of the world, Yudhishthira Maharaj was an emperor, so many millions of years before, Maruta Maharaj was the emperor of the world. He reigned over the world before the reign of Mahārāja Yudhishthira. He was the son of Mahārāja Avikṣit and was a great devotee of the son of the sun god known as Yamarāja. So this Mahārāja Maruta was the devotee of Yamarāja. His brother Sambhartha was a rival priest of the great Brihaspati, the learned priest of the demigods. He conducted one sacrifice called Shankara Yagna by which the Lord was so satisfied that he was pleased to hand over to him the charge of a mountain peak of gold. This peak of gold is somewhere in the Himalaya mountains and modern adventurers may try to find it there. It is there in the Himalayas, this peak of gold. He was so powerful an emperor that at the end of the day's end of sacrifice, the demigods from the other planets like Indra, Chandra and Brihaspati used to visit his palace. And the demigods generally don't come to the earth, but in exceptional cases where the emperor is a very powerful emperor or very qualified emperor or very pious Rajarishi, they visit the earth and become guests of such great emperors or great personalities on this earth. And because he had the gold peak at his disposal, he had sufficient gold in his possession, the canopy of the sacrificial altar was completely made of gold. In his daily performances of the sacrificial ceremonies, some of the inhabitants of the Vayu loka, airy planets, were invited to expedite the cooking work of the ceremony. So these kings were so powerful that they were sumptuously feeding and giving gifts to all the Brahmanas who would help them to perform the sacrifice and they would invite even the devatas to be their guests. And in order to expedite the cooking work, because so many people would assemble, they had to prepare food for all of them, they would expedite the cooking work by taking help of the inhabitants of Vayu loka, the wind god, their assistants. They would help in spiritual work.

---

And the assembly of the demigods in the ceremony was led by Vishwadeva. Different categories of demigods were different powers. But it is prestigious that a king is able to get on the earth, is able to get as his guests and get assistance from demigods from the higher planets, who are expert in performing sacrifices, who are expert in making arrangements. Those people helped this Maruth Maharaj was able to perform very nice sacrifices. By his constant pious work, he was able to drive out all kinds of diseases from the jurisdiction of his kingdom. All the inhabitants of higher planets like Deva loka and Pitra loka were pleased with him for his great sacrificial ceremonies. Every day he used to give in charity to the learned brahmanas such things as bedding, seats, conveyances and sufficient quantities of gold, because he had a whole peak of gold at his disposal. Because of munificent charities and performance of innumerable sacrifices, the king of heaven Indra Deva was fully satisfied with him and always wished for his welfare. Due to his pious activities, he was able to take care of thousands of men throughout his life and reigned over the world for one thousand years, surrounded by satisfied subjects, ministers, legitimate wife, sons and brothers. Even Lord Shri Krishna praised his spirit of pious activities. He handed over his only daughter to Maharishi Angira and by his good blessings he was elevated to the kingdom of heaven. He was blessed with a great wealth of wealth. First of all, he wanted to offer the priesthood of his sacrifices to learn his prahaspati, but the demigod refused to accept the post because of the king's being a human being, a man of this earth. He was very sorry for this, but on the advice of Narada, he appointed Sambhartha to the post and he was successful in this mission. Now,

### sacrifice, kali yuga, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I9l26DM7yNM?t=820
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Anyone can chant Hare Krishna without any previous qualification and can achieve the highest perfection of ultimate liberation and complete self-realization simply by chanting Hare Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the success of a sacrifice depends on the priest and that in Kali Yuga, Sankirtan Yajna, chanting Hare Krishna, is the recommended sacrifice due to the lack of qualified priests.

Prabhupada explains a very important point. The success of a particular type of sacrifice completely depends on the priest in charge. The priest had to chant proper mantras in a fire sacrifice. Now, if they are not qualified to chant the mantras effectively, the sacrifice will not be successful, the fire sacrifice. Therefore, Prabhupada says that in this age, Kali Yuga, all kinds of sacrifices are forbidden because there is no learned priest amongst the so-called brahmanas who go by the false notion of becoming sons of brahmanas without brahminical qualifications. That's what is happening now. Somebody claims I am a brahmana because he is born in a brahmana family, whether he has the qualification, whether he is trained up, whether he can chant mantras effectively to perform fire sacrifices, all that is not considered at all. In this age of Kali, therefore, only one kind of sacrifice is recommended, Sankirtan Yajna, as inaugurated by Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. The only recommended sacrifice due to lack of qualified priests is Sankirtan Yajna. And Sankirtan Yajna does not require any qualification for anybody to perform by chanting Hare Krishna. Anyone can chant Hare Krishna without any previous qualification and can achieve the highest perfection of ultimate liberation and complete self-realization simply by chanting Hare Krishna. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.34 part 1 | 13-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### sacrifice, sinful reactions, responsibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tKrJn_PZQs?t=51
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira Maharaj is very, very conscientious. He is not comfortable with his acquired position because it involved a killing, mass killing of so many millions of Kshatriyas soldiers."
**Summary:** This segment explains Yudhishthira Maharaj's fear and desire to perform sacrifices to atone for the sins incurred during the Kurukshetra war.

In this session, we will be discussing the 34th verse of the 12th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We will be covering this verse in two parts, so this session will be part 1 of this 34th verse. The verse is as follows. The meaning of this verse is, "By those riches, the king could procure the ingredients for three horse sacrifices. Thus the pious king Yudhishthira, who was very fearful after the battle of Kurukshetra, pleased Lord Hari, the personality of Godhead." In the previous verse, it was described how Yudhishthira Maharaj sent his brothers to fetch wealth for performing this Ashwamedha sacrifice. They were directed by Krishna to bring some gold, gold which was left behind by the brahmanas who were engaged in sacrifice by a former emperor, Maruth Maharaj. So that gold, Arjuna brought it for Yudhishthira Maharaj to procure ingredients for performing three horse sacrifices. However good they want to crucify Yudhishthira and take away their wealth through a battle of Kurukshetra, Kanpaksara's desires became as strong as their Council. Ripaaprasadana introduced this 2004 shloka here, that Maharaja Yudhishthira was that ideal and celebrated pious king of the world and still he was greatly afraid after the execution of a battle of Kurukshetra because of the mass killing in the fight, all of which was done only to install him on the throne. Yudhishthira Maharaj is very, very conscientious. He is not comfortable with his acquired position because it involved a killing, mass killing of so many millions of Kshatriyas soldiers. So he was very, very much afraid of the sinful reactions incurred due to such a massacre. He therefore took all the responsibility for sins committed in the warfare and to get rid of all these sins, he wanted to perform three sacrifices in which horses are offered at the altar. Ashwamedha Yagna or Vajimedha Yagna. Such a sacrifice is very costly.

---
### sacrifice, detachment, spiritual goal
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tKrJn_PZQs?t=247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sometimes the wealth is considered an impediment in spiritual progress."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the cost of sacrifices, the detachment of people in former times from material wealth, and the importance of a spiritual aim in life.

Even Maharaj Yudhishthira had to collect the necessary heaps of gold left by Maharaj Marutta and the brahmanas who were given gold in charity by King Marutta. The learned brahmanas could not take away all the loads of gold, and therefore they left behind a major portion of the gift. And Maharaj Marutta also did not again collect such heaps of gold given away in charity. Once it is given in charity, it should not be taken back. Besides that, all the golden plates and utensils which are used in the sacrifice were also thrown in the dustbins and all such heaps of gold remained unclaimed property for a long time till Maharaj Yudhishthira collected them for his own purposes. You see how people were, Prabhupada will explain that, people were satisfied with the basic needs because they had a higher goal in life. The modern civilization, people are just having some immediate goal of living their life for satisfaction of the senses. But in the former times, as directed by the Vedic scriptures, people had the spiritual aim of life. And for attaining that ultimate spiritual goal of life, you don't require to have a lot of wealth. Sometimes the wealth is considered an impediment in spiritual progress. Sometimes. Of course, all wealth should be utilized for yajna, for Vishnu, for the supreme personality of Godhead.

---
### sacrifice, sense gratification, material bondage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tKrJn_PZQs?t=396
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The defect of the modern civilization is that people simply know one thing in life, sense gratification."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Yudhishthira Maharaj utilized the gold for sacrifices, the concept of unclaimed property belonging to the king, and the contrast between ancient and modern attitudes towards wealth and sense gratification.

And that's what we will see Yudhishthira Maharaj utilizing this gold which was collected by Arjuna. It was Yudhishthira Maharaj who utilized all that gold for performing three hard sacrifices. Lord Sri Krishna advised the brothers of Maharaj Yudhishthira to collect the unclaimed property because it belonged to the king. This is the understanding, when the king is ruling a certain tract of land, then that tract of land belongs to the king. The king will also utilize it properly for the welfare of the state, the citizens. So Srila Prabhupada explains here, the more astonishing thing is that no subject of the state also collected such unclaimed gold for industrial enterprise or anything like that. Nowadays people are so busy mining for the sake of developing industry. But formerly, even if there was unclaimed gold left behind somewhere, nobody would go and touch that. How is that people were not attracted to go and fetch or take away such gold? Because they know that something that does not belong to me, if I take it, I am going to incur reactions, sinful reactions, for stealing, for taking away something that is not lawfully mine, something not properly earned by me, I cannot take that. This means that the state citizens were completely satisfied with all necessities of life and therefore not inclined to accept unnecessary productive enterprises for sense gratification. The defect of the modern civilization is that people simply know one thing in life, sense gratification. And their entire life is simply meant for such sense gratification, which is implicating them in material bondage. So,

---
### karma, sacrifice, unintentional sins
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tKrJn_PZQs?t=571
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One should take lessons from the acts of Maharaj Yudhishthira. He was afraid of sins committed on the battlefield, and therefore he wanted to satisfy the supreme authority."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of respect, caring, and love, drawing lessons from Yudhishthira Maharaj's fear of sins and his desire to satisfy the Supreme Authority, even for unintentional sins.

Aspirants must know the truth, precwrights should know the truth, that is, respect, respect, caring, love, respect. One should take lessons from the acts of Maharaj Yudhishthira. He was afraid of sins committed on the battlefield, and therefore he wanted to satisfy the supreme authority. Maharaj Yudhishthira is a very, very pious king. He is son of Dharmaraja, Yamaraja. And he is perfectly aware of all the laws of karma. So in spite of being pious, in spite of being very conscientious, still he was afraid of sins committed on the battlefield, unintentionally, unknowingly. Unknowingly he will never do it. Intentionally he will never have an intention of killing anybody unnecessarily, never. But unknowingly, accidentally, unintentionally something might have happened in a battle. So he is afraid of that. This indicates that unintentional sins are also committed in our daily occupational discharges. And to counteract even such unintentional crimes, one must perform sacrifices as they are recommended in the revealed scriptures. So Shastra says, if you have committed some sin unintentionally, unknowingly, still you have to perform recommended sacrifices for becoming free from the sinful reactions.

---
### sacrifice, karma, satisfaction of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tKrJn_PZQs?t=692
**Verse References:** BG 3.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, the main purpose of performing sacrifices is to satisfy the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Hari."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of performing sacrifices to get rid of unauthorized work and unintentional crimes, emphasizing that the ultimate aim is to satisfy the Supreme Lord Hari.

The Lord says in Bhagavad Gita, yajñārthāt karmano anyatra loko yam karma-bandhanah. In the third chapter, ninth verse, Krishna says this. That one must perform sacrifices recommended in the scriptures in order to get rid of commitments of all unauthorized work or even unintentional crimes which we are apt to commit. Therefore, if one performs the recommended sacrifices, then they are freed from sinful reactions. And those who do not do so, those who do not perform sacrifices, but work for self-interest or sense gratification, such people have to undergo all tribulations accrued from committed sins. The laws of nature are very subtle and they are working perfectly and there is an enforcing authority. Material nature itself being directed by the Supreme Lord Krishna. Mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ śūyateśaccharācaram. So, intelligent people understand that any person suffering in this world is only because that person has committed sinful activities for which he is suffering the reactions in the form of miseries, troubles and dangers, diseases and all kinds of, any kind of suffering. Therefore, the main purpose of performing sacrifices is to satisfy the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Hari. The process of performing sacrifices may be different in terms of different times, places and persons. Kāla, Deśa and Pātra. These are Sanskrit words. Time, place and person. But the aim of such sacrifices is one and the same at all times and in all circumstances. Namely, satisfaction of the Supreme Lord Hari. Only one aim of performing sacrifices as given in the scriptures. Simply to satisfy the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Hari. That is the way of pious life and that is the way of peace and prosperity in the world at large. So, by performing sacrifice for the pleasure of the Supreme Lord Hari, we not only are engaged in pious activity but we are also able to attain peace and prosperity by such performance sacrifices. So, I will stop here.

A proper specifically says here in Kali Yuga people do not have a way of becoming free from the sinful reactions. The platform says therefore that the prime duty of the human being is to satisfy the Supreme Lord by the performance of one's occupational duty. Samsiddhi means the highest perfection, is simply possible by pleasing the Lord Hari. So, should I proper further explain? Let any man of any place or community, caste or creed be engaged in any sort of occupational duty, but he must agree to perform sacrifices as it is recommended in the scriptures for the particular place, time, and place. ### sacrifice, economy, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the expensive horse sacrifices of the past with the inexpensive and effective method of chanting Hare Krishna recommended by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, highlighting its suitability for Kali Yuga.

Maharaj Yudhishthira had to collect heaps of gold to secure the paraphernalia for the horse sacrifice Yagnas in days of sufficiency. Those days people had lot of natural resources and all their desires easily fulfilled by the Supreme Lord they are all pious. But in other cases somebody may not be able to offer sacrifices to the Supreme Lord the way Yudhishthira Maharaj performed that is not possible. So people of this age can hardly think of such performance of Yagnas in the days in these days of insufficiency and complete scarcity of gold where will they get gold from so much of gold that Yudhishthira Maharaj spent so it is not practically possible it is not practically possible. And Prabhupada makes a remark about the modern system of economy at the present moment we have heaps of papers and promises of their being converted into gold by economic development of modern civilization and still there is no possibility of spending riches like Maharaj Yudhishthira either individually or collectively or by state patronization. State never promotes and finances such Yagnas but even people cannot spend the kind of money individually or collectively which Yudhishthira Maharaj spent. So Srila Prabhupada says just suitable for the age is the method recommended by Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in terms of the Shastra. Now what Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is recommending is not his own recommendation though it is Supreme Lord disguised as a devotee but he is not recommending anything which is not in the scriptures. Such a method requires no expenditure at all. What is the expenditure in chanting Hare Krishna and yet can award more benefit than other expensive methods of Yagna performance.
