### chanting, sacrifice, animal sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zTj7R9bpezY?t=720
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The perfect process of performing Yagna or sacrifice to please the Supreme Lord Hari is to follow the ways of Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in this dark age of quarrel and dissension."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that chanting Hare Krishna is more beneficial than other methods of sacrifice and clarifies that animal sacrifices in Vedic times were not mere killings but involved rejuvenation through mantras, a practice no longer possible.

Somebody takes up the method of meditation cultivating knowledge etc then we cannot get more benefit in this way than what is obtained by by chanting Hare Krishna in the association of devotees. The horse sacrifice Yagna or cow sacrifice Yagna performed by the Vedic regulations should not be misunderstood as a process of killing animals. Sometimes meat eaters they say that you tell us not to eat meat but then formerly even the pious people used to perform animal sacrifices so isn't it very difficult to eat meat and I mean people they always took sin as soul to kill animals and eat their flesh when plenty of fruits vegetables and all vegetarian food stuff is available so therefore the proper. Now, Prabhupada says, animals offered for the yajna were rejuvenated to a new span of life by the transcendental power of chanting the Vedic hymns, the brahmanas who chant the mantras. And by the power of the mantra, the animal which was sacrificed or killed in a horse sacrifice, that animal would be revived when the priest in the sacrifice would actually chant mantras, suitable mantras. So therefore, the misunderstanding that formerly they were killing animals for the sake of performing sacrifice, now they are killing animals for the sake of feeding the slaughterhouse. But that is not the real proper understanding. The Veda mantras are all practical and the proof is rejuvenation of the sacrificed animal. So when an animal is killed for a sacrifice then the animal is revived. But reviving such an animal requires the power of the mantra to be chanted by duly qualified brahmanas. And Srila Prabhupada says, there is no possibility of such methodical chanting of the Vedic hymns by the so-called brahmanas who preach of the present age. So therefore, it is recommended to chant the holy name of Supreme Lord Hari and easy, inexpensive, effective way of doing sacrifice in this age. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

And such unqualified brahmanas are actually equated with shudras. Somebody is not qualified such a person cannot be engaged for performing some yajna or some religious ritual it's not possible. Lord Shri Krishna was entrusted to look after this arrangement and perfect as he is he caused the yajnas to be performed by the bona fide twice-born brahmanas for successful execution. ### sacrifice, priests, Vedic rituals
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Z-IDm2-ju98?t=270
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To perform a sacrifice successfully, at least four expert priests are required."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 36th verse and explains the importance of engaging qualified brahmanas to perform Vedic sacrifices, detailing the four types of expert priests required: Hota, Utgata, Advaryu, and Brahma.

Under the guidance of qualified brahmanas the next verse the 36th verse. Tato ragnabhya anudhyatah krishnaya sorry. Tato ragnabhya anudhyatah krishnaya sahabandhubhihi yayau dvaravateem brahman sarjuno yarubhir vrittah. The meaning of this 36th verse is O Saunaka, thereafter the Lord, having bade farewell to King Yudhishthira, Draupadi and other relatives, started for the city of Dwaraka, accompanied by Arjuna and other members of the Yadu dynasty. So, we have to understand the importance of engaging qualified brahmanas to perform a Vedic sacrifice. So, elsewhere Srila Prabhupada explains, to perform a sacrifice successfully, at least four expert priests are required. Who are the four expert priests who should be present for successfully executing a sacrifice? One who can offer, is called Hota. One who can chant, Utgata. One who can kindle the sacrificial fire without the aid of separate fire, Advaryu. And one who can supervise, Brahma. So, Hota, Utgata, Advaryu, Brahma. Four kinds of priests are required for performing a Vedic sacrifice.

### Krishna, association, charity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Z-IDm2-ju98?t=450
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "O earthly gods, kindly hear us, who are ordered by Lord Krishna and Lord Balarama."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story about Krishna and Balarama sending covered boys to ask brahmanas performing a Vedic sacrifice for food, illustrating the importance of charity and the boys' knowledge due to their association with Krishna and Balarama.

And to chant the Hare Krishna Mantra does not require previous qualification. Another place, Prabhupada says that once Krishna had gone with some covered boys. Krishna and Balarama had gone with some covered boys early morning, taking the calves for grazing. And the boys, after some time, became very, very hungry. So, they told Krishna, please arrange for some foodstuff. Krishna then told the boys that nearby this place, wherever they were, there are some brahmanas who are performing a Vedic sacrifice. So, Krishna told the boys, go to those brahmanas and ask for some charity, some dana. Then those covered boys went to those brahmanas and approached them with folded hands and fell down to the ground to offer them respect. So, what did the covered boys say? They addressed the brahmanas and they said, O earthly gods, kindly hear us, who are ordered by Lord Krishna and Lord Balarama. We hope you know them both very well, and we wish you all good fortune. Krishna and Balarama are tending cows nearby, and we have accompanied them. We have come to ask for some food from you. You are all brahmanas. You are knowers of religious principles. And if you think that you should give us charity, then give us some food, and we shall all eat along with Krishna and Balarama. The boys continued to speak to those brahmanas. You are the most respectable brahmanas within the human society, and you are expected to know all the principles of religious procedure. So, this is what the boys said to those brahmanas.

### association, enlightenment, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Z-IDm2-ju98?t=624
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you associate with Krishna or his pure devotee, then automatically you are able to get enlightenment simply by performing devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that associating with Krishna or his pure devotee automatically leads to enlightenment and understanding of religious principles through devotional service, as demonstrated by the covered boys' knowledge.

Now, Srila Prabhupada explains, although the boys were village boys and were not expected to be learned in the Vedic principles of religion, they hinted that because of their association with Krishna and Balarama, they knew all those principles. If you associate with Krishna or his pure devotee, then automatically you are able to get enlightenment simply by performing devotional service in understanding all the religious principles, especially those which are meant to enliven us, enlighten us, without separate endeavour at studying or doing some other practice. Simply by association with the right people, you become enlightened, you become enlivened. Now, these boys, covered boys, because they were associating with Krishna and Balarama, they expressed the point of view that when the supreme personality of Godhead Krishna and Balarama were asking for food, the brahmana should immediately deliver some without hesitation and they should actually perform yajna only for the satisfaction of Vishnu.

---
### food offering, Vaishnava, ceremonial rituals
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Z-IDm2-ju98?t=758
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although the companions of Krishna and Balarama were so very simple in their habits, still, they were in a position to dictate to high-class brahmanas engaged in Vedic rituals of sacrifice."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, explaining that the boys emphasized the urgency of delivering food to Krishna and Balarama, highlighting the Vaishnava understanding of food offering and the boys' ability to instruct even high-class brahmanas due to their association with Krishna.

Now, the boys continued to speak to the brahmanas. They said, Lord Vishnu, as Krishna and Balarama is standing waiting, and you should immediately deliver whatever food you have in your stock. So, the Vedic culture is no food is refrigerated, but the food may be kept, fresh food may be kept, to actually feed some guests or feed some extra people like that. Now, these covered boys, they also explained to those brahmanas that when food is to be accepted, and when food is not supposed to be accepted. The Vaishnavas generally don't perform ordinary sacrificial ceremonies, but still the devotees know very well about all the ceremonial rituals in connection with offering to Krishna. So, they began to speak to the brahmanas that we can take your food at the present stage of your ceremony. For now, it will not be prohibited. So, you can deliver us the food. We'll take it to Krishna and Balarama. Although the companions of Krishna and Balarama were so very simple in their habits, still, they were in a position to dictate to high-class brahmanas engaged in Vedic rituals of sacrifice.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story by noting that the brahmanas, being fruitive-minded, missed the opportunity to serve Krishna, emphasizing that Krishna accepts offerings made with devotion, either directly or through a devotee, and ends the session with gratitude and the Hare Krishna mantra.

That is because of their association with these with Krishna and Balarama. Simply because of associating Krishna and Balarama they were able to dictate even to the high-class brahmanas. But the brahmanas being very very fruity-minded, they ignored this request of these covered boys and the boys went back disappointed. So, the brahmanas lost that opportunity for rendering pure devotion service to Krishna and therefore, we should be careful in understanding that Krishna accepts either directly from the devotee or through the medium of another devotee provided it is offered with devotion. ### leadership, Krishna consciousness, world peace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CgoBer_VitE?t=50

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and emphasizes Srila Prabhupada's vision of spreading Krishna consciousness for the benefit of the entire world, urging leaders to embrace this science for the good of society.

In this session, we will be discussing from the fourth verse of the twelfth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Suta Uvacha Apipalad Dharma Rajaha Pitruvad Rajayan Prajaha Nispraha Sarvakamebhya Krishna Padanu Sevaya. The meaning of this verse is Shri Suta Goswami said Emperor Yudhishthira administered generously to everyone during his reign. He was exactly like his father. He had no personal ambition and was freed from all sorts of sense gratification because of his continuous service unto the lotus feet of the Lord Shri Krishna. So Suta Goswami is beginning to answer the point raised by Shaunaka Rishi on behalf of the sages of Naimishaharanya. Srila Prabhupada explains this elaborately in the purport. As many of you know, in our introduction, there is a need for the science of Krishna in human society for all the suffering humanity of the world. And we simply request the leading personalities of all nations to take to the science of Krishna for their own good, for the good of society, and for the good of all the people of the world. This is the vision of the Acharya. Srila Prabhupada's vision is meant for doing good to the entire world.

So just like Krishna thinks of the entire world, every living being, similarly the Acharyas, and in our present times, Srila Prabhupada as the Acharya, is thinking about the well-being of every living entity in this entire world. Of every living entity in this entire world. Therefore he is suggesting, based on the Srimad Bhagavatam's recommendation, that everyone should simply take to the science of Krishna. ### ideal governance, protection of all beings, Maharaj Yudhisthira
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CgoBer_VitE?t=246
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Ramachandra was the ideal king and all other kings or emperors in India controlled the destiny of the world for the prosperity of every living being who took birth on the earth."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of Maharaj Yudhishthira and Ramachandra as ideal kings who ensured prosperity for all living beings, emphasizing that 'Praja' encompasses all species, not just humans, and that a true king represents and protects all living beings.

So it is confirmed herein by the example of Maharaj Yudhishtira. Ramachandra was the ideal king and all other kings or emperors in India controlled the destiny of the world for the prosperity of every living being who took birth on the earth. You see, Ramaraja is important because Lord Ramachandra ruled in such a way that there was prosperity for every living being. And Srila Prabhupada now particularly explains the word Praja. Herein, the word Praja is significant. The etymological import of the word is that which is born, Praja, that which is born. On the earth, there are many species of life from aquatics up to the perfect human beings and all are known as Prajas. Praja doesn't mean only human beings. Praja means all living beings who take birth in this world. Lord Brahma, the creator of this particular universe is known as the Prajapati because he is the grandfather of all who have taken birth. Thus, Praja is used in a broader sense than it is now used. The king represents all living beings. The aquatics, the plants, trees, reptiles, birds, animals, and man.

### demoniac administration, sense gratification, serving the whole
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CgoBer_VitE?t=672
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Modern day administrators they rule the world based on giving sense gratification to the people and that is demoniac."
**Summary:** The speaker criticizes modern administrations for prioritizing sense gratification, leading to degradation and selfishness, and contrasts this with serving Krishna, the complete whole, which automatically benefits all living beings, using the analogy of watering the root of a tree.

And not on the Rakshasi demoniac principle of sense gratification. Modern day administrators they rule the world based on giving sense gratification to the people and that is demoniac. It makes people degraded. It makes people selfish. It makes people cruel. It makes people envious. So that is not the proper way to rule the world or the nation or the kingdom. As an ideal king, he had no personal ambition. Nisriha Sarvakamyaipya. Personally. He had no desire for material enjoyment. And there was no place for sense gratification because all his senses at all times were engaged in the loving service of the Supreme Lord which includes the partial service to the living beings who form the parts and parcels of the complete whole. So when Yudhishthira Maharaj is serving the Supreme Lord Krishna, that means he is also engaged in serving the living beings who are part of Krishna. Those who are busy rendering service to the parts and parcels, leaving aside the whole, only spoil time and energy as one does when watering the leaves of a tree without watering the root. This is the example given in the Bhagavatam.

---
### serving Krishna, perfect management, safeguarding the next life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CgoBer_VitE?t=775
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "When one renders service to Krishna who is the complete whole, then all the parts and parcels, the living entities, they are automatically served."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the analogy of watering the root to emphasize that serving Krishna automatically serves all living entities, highlighting that Yudhishthira Maharaj's administration not only provided comforts in this life but also ensured progress in the next, concluding with gratitude and the Hare Krishna mantra.

As when watering the root of a very big tree, all the branches, the leaves, the twigs, every other part of the tree is completely nourished by the watering of the root of the tree. Exactly like that. When one renders service to Krishna who is the complete whole, then all the parts and parcels, the living entities, they are automatically served by this one action of serving Krishna. If water is poured on the root of the tree, the leaves are enlivened perfectly and automatically. But if water is poured on the leaves only, the whole energy is spoiled. Maharaj Yudhishthira therefore was constantly engaged in the service of the Lord and thus the parts and parcels of the Lord, the living beings, under his careful administration, were perfectly attended with all comforts in this life and all progress in the next. That is the way of perfect management of state administration. See, not only this life, when Yudhishthira Maharaj was doing administration, their next life was also safeguarded. It was the best preparation for the people for the next life by rendering service to Lord in this life. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Maharaj Yudhishthira's proper performance of duties led to earthly abundance, contrasting this with the futility of forceful entrance into upper planets via spacecraft, emphasizing that such attempts are unsuccessful and that they cannot land anywhere suitable.

Yanti deva vrata deva. Now Maharaj Yudhishtira, he was carrying out his duties as given in the shastra. But the result of Yudhishtira Maharaj doing his duties properly is that he was able to cultivate the earth in such a way that the earth was providing everything in plenty. That was described in the earlier chapter. So this is possible not because of forceful entrance into the upper planets by means of some space vehicle or some kind of spacecraft. It's not going to work. As Prabhupada says, they may go some few thousand miles into outer space, but they cannot land anywhere. That's why they are always coming back. If they really were to go to upper planets where standard of living is millions of times better, why will they come back to this earth planet which is filled with misery? Why will they come back to this situation? It's because they are not going anywhere into outer space. Neither they are actually able to land in any particular place, suitable place. They are simply engaged in sense gratification.

---

Giving trouble to lower animals and committing so many sinful activities, especially killing the cow. It's the worst kind of sin, the greatest sin. What do the shastras say about cow killing? ## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.6 | 3-08-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotional service, detachment, constitutional position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/hQ0Zcu7JbpI?t=51
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This means that the living being is constitutionally a servitor."
**Summary:** This segment explains that a pure devotee is not bewildered by material opulence because their mind is absorbed in devotional service to Krishna, highlighting the constitutional position of the living being as a servant.

In this session, we will be discussing the 6th verse of the 12th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, O Brahmanas, the opulence of the king was so enchanting that the denizens of heaven aspired for it. But because he, the king, Yudhishthira, was absorbed in the service of the king, nothing could satisfy him except the Lord's service. So this clearly explains the position of a pure devotee, even though he may be emperor of this world, with opulences which are able to bewilder even the heavenly residents, even such opulences cannot bewilder a pure devotee, because the pure devotee's mind is fully absorbed in devotional service to Krishna. So Srila Prabhupada explains very nicely in the purport, there are two things in the world which can satisfy living beings in this world. When one is materially engrossed, he is satisfied only by sense gratification. But when one is liberated from the conditions of the material modes, he is satisfied only by rendering loving service for the satisfaction of the Lord. This means that the living being is constitutionally a servitor. Constitutionally means the way we are made. We are meant to simply serve, serve, serve.

---
### illusion, servitude, spiritual knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/hQ0Zcu7JbpI?t=178
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Happiness in the service of Krishna is real happiness."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how everyone is a servant, either to Krishna or to their senses, and that true happiness lies in serving Krishna, not in material pursuits.

We are never the one who is served. Krishna is the only one who is always served by everyone else. Everyone else is a servant, a servitor. Being illusioned by the conditions of the external energy, material energy, illusory energy, maya, one falsely thinks himself to be the served, but actually he is not served. He is servant of the senses like lust, desire, anger, avarice, pride, madness, and intolerance. Even people who are very, very powerful, very rich, very nicely educated, very influential, but ultimately they are all servant of their senses. When it comes to them becoming victim of lust, desire, anger, pride, madness, intolerance, they cannot control themselves. When one is in his proper senses by attainment of spiritual knowledge, he realizes that he is not the master of the material world, but is only a servant of the senses. To realize this requires spiritual understanding. At that time, that is when one comes to understand his position as a servant, as a saint. He begs for the service of the Lord, and thus becomes happy, without being illusioned by so called material happiness. Material happiness is not real happiness. Happiness in the service of Krishna is real happiness.

---
### devotee, material desire, spiritual hunger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/hQ0Zcu7JbpI?t=309
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The whole material world is full of hungry living beings."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Maharaj Yudhishthira was a liberated soul, untouched by material pleasures, and that the world's dissatisfaction stems from a hunger for spiritual fulfillment, not just material needs like food and shelter.

Maharaj Yudhishthira was one of the liberated souls. How is that? Because, pure devotee and therefore for him there is no pleasure in a vast kingdom, good wife, obedient brothers, happy subjects, and prosperous world. None of these things could give him real happiness. These blessings automatically follow for a pure devotee even though the devotee does not aspire for them. That means a devotee may be blessed with a vast kingdom, any material opulences, he does not aspire for them. The example set herein is exactly suitable. What are the example? It is said that one who is hungry is never satisfied by anything other than food. Can hunger be satisfied by anything else other than food? Nourishing, relishable, palatable food is the only thing that can satisfy hunger. Similarly, a devotee's heart is only satisfied by devotional service to Krishna and not by anything else. Now Srila Prabhupada explains a very, very important point that we should carefully note: the whole material world is full of hungry living beings. There is no exception to this reality, fact of life. The whole material world is full of hungry living beings. The hunger is not for good food, is not for shelter, is not for sense gratitude. The hunger is for the spiritual atmosphere, why? Because we are spirit soul, we are not the body. We are not a material product. Due to ignorance only they think that the world is dissatisfied because there is not sufficient food. Due to ignorance only people think the world is dissatisfied. The world is dissatisfied because there is no sufficient food or no sufficient shelter, defense, and objects of sense gratification. Ahara, nidra, bhaya, maithu, namcha. These are four demands of the body. Every living entity's body demands these four things.

---
### illusion, spiritual hunger, material satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/hQ0Zcu7JbpI?t=477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even the people who are most sumptuously, materially satisfied are still hungry."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the perceived lack of material necessities is an illusion, and the true hunger is for spiritual satisfaction, which even the materially wealthy still crave.

And people think there is some scarcity of one or more of these four things. That's why people are dissatisfied. But that's not the real fact. This is called illusion. To think that people are dissatisfied because of the scarcity of one of these four things, ahara, nidra, bhaya, maithu, namcha, is actually due to illusion. When the living being is hungry for spiritual satisfaction, he is misrepresented by material hunger. But the foolish leaders cannot see that even the people who are most sumptuously, materially satisfied are still hungry. Even the people in rich nations, in rich households, in very powerful, influential, opulent positions, even they are hungry. So hunger is not for any of those four material needs. And what is their hunger and poverty? This Prabhupada explains. This hunger is actually for spiritual food, for spiritual shelter, for spiritual defense, and spiritual sense gratification. There are two things. There is something called material hunger, material food, material shelter, material defense, material sense gratification. This we all know. We have experience of this. But there is something else we do not know. What is that? Spiritual food, spiritual shelter, spiritual defense, spiritual sense gratification. This is something we have to learn from the Bhagavatam. These can be obtained in the association of the Supreme Spirit, Lord Sri Krishna. And therefore, one who has them cannot be attracted by so-called food, shelter, defense, and sense gratification of the material world.

---
### spiritual atmosphere, eternity, material limitations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/hQ0Zcu7JbpI?t=638
**Verse References:** BG 8.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Come to eternity."
**Summary:** This segment explains that even in the highest material planets like Brahmaloka, with immensely long lifespans, one cannot find true satisfaction because the hunger is for spiritual fulfillment and eternity, which can only be attained in the spiritual sky.

Even if such material food, shelter, defense, and sense gratification are relished by the denizens of the heavenly planet. We may see that somebody else in a relatively higher material position may be relishing something, apparently, better than what we have with us in terms of material food or material facilities. But the reality is all living beings in every position in this material world are hungry for spiritual atmosphere, for spiritual food, spiritual shelter, spiritual defense, spiritual sense gratification. Therefore, in the Bhagavad Gita 8.16, it is said by the Lord that even in the topmost planet of the universe, namely the Brahmaloka, what is the situation in Brahmaloka? In Brahmaloka, the duration of life is multiplied by millions of years by earth calculation. Sahasra yuga pariyantam aharyat brahmano vidhu. That twelve hours of Brahma, Brahma loka, the residence of Brahma loka, where Brahma lives, is equal to one thousand maha yugas of this world. This earth planet. That comes to some four billion years of our calculation. Twelve hours of Brahma, half a day of Brahma, is four billion solar years on the earth. So, in that Brahma loka also, one cannot satisfy his hunger by any material food, material arrangements, no. Such hunger can be satisfied only when the living being is situated in immortality. Asatoma saggamaya. This is one of the statements in the original Vedas. Don't remain in asat, in material existence. Come to spiritual existence. Satgamaya. Satgamaya. Sat. Eternity. Come to eternity. Come to eternality.

---
### liberation, devotional service, Lord Mukunda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/hQ0Zcu7JbpI?t=798
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He gives liberation to those who are engaged in his devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by stating that liberation from material existence is only granted by Lord Vishnu (Mukunda) to those engaged in devotional service, not by any other demigods or material activities.

This immortality is attained in the spiritual sky, far, far above Brahma loka, in the association of Lord Mukunda. The Lord, who awards his devotees, the transcendental pleasure of liberation. Lord Mukunda. There are so many verses that describe. Mukunda means the giver of liberation. Mukti pradata sarveshaam vishnur eva na samshayah. Lord Shiva says that, Mukti, if you want liberation from this material existence, then that it can be only given by Vishnu or Lord Krishna or Mukunda, one who can give liberation, one who gives liberation, one who factually gives liberation. Even Lord Shiva, Lord Brahma, any devata, anybody belonging to this material world cannot give you liberation from material existence. So this we have to always remember. And, to whom does the Lord give liberation? He gives liberation to those who are engaged in his devotional service. And not to anybody else who may be doing so many other things other than devotional service. They don't get liberation. They are only given relative material facilities. That's all.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.12.7-9 | 5-08-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### protection, devotion, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/63vN2Tzrlm0?t=31
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Pariksit while in the womb of his mother was struck by the Brahmastra released by Ashwatthama, and he was feeling the burning heat."
**Summary:** This segment explains verse seven, focusing on Parikshit Maharaj's experience in the womb when attacked by Ashwatthama's Brahmastra and the Lord's appearance to protect him due to Parikshit's devotion.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Chayiva Narottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing the three verses of the twelfth chapter of the first canto of Bhagavatam, verses seven, eight, and nine. To begin with, verse number seven, Matur Garbha Gato Veeraha Satadhaa Bhrugunandana Dadarsha Purusham Kanchit Dasyamano Astratejasaa. The translation of this verse is as follows. O son of Bhrugu, Suthu Goswami is addressing the sage Naimisharanya headed by Shaunaka Rishi. He is addressed as Bhrugunandana, son of Bhrugu. When the child Pariksit, the great fighter, was in the womb of his mother Uttara and was suffering from the burning heat of the Brahmastra thrown by Ashwathama, he could observe the Supreme Lord coming to him. Something extraordinary happened at the time when Pariksit was in his mother's womb and Ashwathama attempted to kill this child by releasing a Brahmastra targeted at this child only. Unlike modern weapons, the mantras which are used as weapons, mantra, astra, they can be directed towards a very specific target, and they will go searching for the target wherever the target may be and destroy the target, attack the target. So that is explained by Prabhupada in the purport. Death generally involves remaining in trance for seven months. A living being according to his own action is allowed to enter into the womb of a mother by the vehicle of a father's Samina, semen, and thus he develops his desired body. This is the law of birth in specific bodies according to one's past actions. When he is awake from trance, he feels the inconvenience of being confined within the womb. After seven months, a child in the womb of a mother comes to external consciousness, awakens from something like sleep. When the child enters the womb as a soul, transfer from one body to the next body, a human body, then he enters a human mother's womb. And then seven months the child is lying as if unconscious or as if sleeping. But he awakens after the seventh month. And seventh month when he awakens he is like in a mother's womb, in an airtight bag, in a very uncomfortable position, feels the inconvenience of being confined in that airtight bag called the womb of the mother, and also experiences the surroundings are full of all kinds of secretions in the mother's womb, all kinds of urine, pus, mucus, blood, stool, all kinds of obnoxious things. And thus he wants to come out of the mother's womb. And sometimes fortunately such a soul prays to the Lord for such liberation. I want to get out, please, my dear Lord, help me. Maharaj Pariksit while in the womb of his mother was struck by the Brahmastra released by Ashwatthama, and he was feeling the burning heat. But Maharaj Pariksit is no ordinary soul. He is described here because he was a devotee of the Lord. The Lord at once appeared himself within the womb by his all-powerful energy, and the child could see that someone else had come to save him. Pariksit is bold enough to face the Brahmastra, but still, even though he is bold, because he is a devotee, Krishna personally entered the womb of his mother to save the child. Krishna has a personal interest in that devotee, and Pariksit could see someone else has come into the womb, into this place, even at helpless condition. The child Pariksit endured the unbearable temperature due to his being a great fighter by nature, and for this reason the word Viraha has been used. Pariksit has been described as Matur Garbhagato Viraha. Viraha is a very powerful fighter, brave, bold, able to tolerate the extraordinary heat of the Brahmastra released by Ashwatthama, and then he saw Krishna entering the womb of his mother in order to save him. Then in the eighth verse, it is described.

---
### divine form, protection, omnipresence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/63vN2Tzrlm0?t=412
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.8, SB 1.12.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord was enriched with four hands, earrings of molten gold, and eyes blood red with fury."
**Summary:** This segment details verses eight and nine, describing the form of the Lord as seen by Parikshit in the womb, emphasizing His transcendental appearance, four arms, golden earrings, and the club He wielded.

It is described, Angushta matram amalam, Spurat puratam aulinam, Apivya darshanam shyamam, Tadidvasasam achyutam. This verse exactly describes what kind of form of the Lord did Pariksit see in his mother's womb when the Lord appeared. So the meaning of this eighth verse describing the form of the Lord that Pariksit saw in his mother's womb, He, the Lord, was only thumb high, but he was all transcendental. He had a very, very beautiful blackish infallible body, Shyamam, and he wore a dress of lightning yellow, Tadidvasasam, lightning yellow, and a helmet of blazing gold, Puratam aulinam, golden helmet. Thus he was seen by the child. The Lord had entered, and the Lord was seen by the child in a beautiful form, blackish form, golden helmet, shining beautifully. The dress is like lightning yellow, brilliantly glowing. Then in the ninth verse, it is further described, Srimadirgha chaturbahum, Taptakanchana kundalam, Shatajaksham gadapanim, Atmanasarvatodisham, Paribhramantam, Ulkabham, Bhramayantam, Gadammuhu. The meaning of this ninth verse, the Lord was enriched with four hands, Chaturbahum, earrings of molten gold, Kanchana kundalam, and eyes blood red with fury, Chatajaksham, and as he loitered about, his club constantly encircled him like a shooting star. Krishna was carrying the club in his hand, Gadapanim, Bhramayantam, holding his gada, he was just encircling like a shooting star, Ulkabham.

---
### omnipresence, devotee protection, divine power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/63vN2Tzrlm0?t=588
**Verse References:** BG 9.31
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If he can enter into every material atom, he enters into every universe, he enters into the heart of every living being, why can't he enter the womb of mother Uttara for the sake of his devotee to save his devotee from the attack of the Brahma's throne?"
**Summary:** This segment explains the Lord's omnipresence and His ability to protect devotees, referencing Brahma Samhita and Bhagavad Gita to illustrate how the Lord can enter the universe, atoms, and the hearts of living beings, thus emphasizing His power to save Parikshit in the womb.

Srila Prabhupada says in the purport that the Supreme Lord Govinda, by his one plenary portion, enters into the hallow of the universe and distributes himself as Paramatma of the Supersoul, not only within the heart of every living being, but also within every atom of the material elements. Andantarastaparamanuchayantarastam. He enters into the Anda. Anda means the egg, egg-shaped universe, universal shell. This is the beginning of creation, that Lord Maha Vishnu, he lies on the Karna ocean. He breathes out universes. They come in seed-like form, but that seed-like form of the universe, into that, Maha Vishnu expands as Garbhodakshayi Vishnu and enters into it. Vishnu means one who enters. So as Vishnu he enters, first of all the material dormant material energy, and then that material energy develops into universes, seed-like form. Into each seed-like form of the universe, Lord expands and enters the second Vishnu, Garbhodakshayi Vishnu, and it begins to develop. So entering into that universe, it is described in the universe, he enters, and then it develops, and he also, as the development happens, and living entities are produced. The whole material world is created. Every atom of the universe, material atom also, he enters his Paramatma, Antantarasta, Paramanu chayantarastam. Paramanu means the material atoms, the whole universe. Thus the Lord is all-pervading by his inconceivable energy. Who can conceive the Lord? In his form as Paramatma, has entered the hollow of the universe. He has entered into the heart of every living being. Every living being is entered into the inside every material atom. Who can conceive this? It's inconceivable, achintya. And he also entered the womb of mother Uttara to save his beloved devotee Maharaj Parikshit. If he can enter into every material atom, he enters into every universe, he enters into the heart of every living being, why can't he enter the womb of mother Uttara for the sake of his devotee to save his devotee from the attack of the Brahma's throne? So in the Bhagavad Gita again, Srila Prabhupada is quoting the Bhagavad Gita 9.31, 9th chapter 31st verse, the Lord assured everyone that his devotees are never to be vanquished.

---
### devotee protection, divine power, omnipresence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/63vN2Tzrlm0?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is all powerful and therefore he can both save and kill as he likes."
**Summary:** This segment reinforces the idea that the Lord protects His devotees and can manifest in any size or form, emphasizing His unlimited power and mercy, as demonstrated by saving Parikshit Maharaj.

Kaunteya pratijaanihi na me bhakta-pranasyati, my devotee, mad-bhakta na pranasyati, is never vanquished. So no one can kill a devotee of the Lord because he is protected by the Lord, and no one can save a person whom the Lord desires to kill. Maare Krishna rakshake, rakshake Krishna maare ke. If Krishna wants to protect a devotee, nobody can kill that devotee, and if Krishna wants to kill some Asura or demon or some miscreant, nobody can protect that miscreant. And that's what happened to Ravana. He was a devotee of Shiva, and he is praying to Shiva, kindly protect me when Rama came to attack Ravana. And Shiva said, Rama, the Supreme Personality of God wants to kill you, who can protect you? Nobody can save. And if Krishna wants to protect Parikshit Maharaj, no brahmastra, no person, no weapon, nothing can destroy the Pariksit Maharaj devotee. The Lord is all powerful and therefore he can both save and kill as he likes. He became visible to his devotee Maharaj Pariksit, even in that awkward position in the womb of his mother, in a shape just suitable for his vision. Lord gave him darshan. And Srila Prabhupada explains, the Lord can become bigger than thousands of universes, Mahavishnu. He is lying on the causal ocean and within his body there are millions of universes, and he can become smaller than an atom at the same time and enter into every material atom. Merciful as he is, he becomes just suitable to the vision of the limited living being. He is unlimited. He is not limited by any measurement of our calculation, but he can become bigger than we can think of and he can become smaller than what we can conceive. But in all circumstances he is the same all-powerful Supreme Lord. His position, his power does not change. Unlimited power is always with him. So, the Lord mercifully gave darshan to Pariksit and also saved him. That's his mercy upon his pure devotee. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.1-2 | 17-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Vidura, Yamaraj, Curse, Mahabharata
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/p5Oq5KN4TRA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And this Shudra incarnation of Yamaraj was known as Vidura, the Shudra brother of Dhritarashtra and Maharaj Pandu."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the chapter and verse, then delves into the history of Vidura, explaining his origin as an incarnation of Yamaraj due to a curse by Mandukamuni, and his role in the Mahabharata.

Om Agyana Timira Andhrasya Gyananj Nishalakaya Chakshur Unmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Svamin Nithinamine. Namaste Sarasvate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pachatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Sarasvati Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from the first and second verses of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. So with this session we are beginning a new chapter in the first canto, the 13th chapter. The 13th chapter is entitled Vritrashtra Quits Home. And here is the first verse. Suta Vacha Viduras Tirthayatrayam Maitreyad Atmanogatim Nyatvagat Hastinapuram Taya Vapt Vividsitaha. The meaning of this first verse is Shri Suta Goswami said While travelling on a pilgrimage Vidura received knowledge of the destination of the self from the great sage Maitreya and then returned to Hastinapuram. He became as well versed in the subject as he desired. Who was Vidura? Shrilairati Amarath Shilaprabha explains in the purport. He gives a brief history of the personality of Vidura. In this purport Shrilaprabha says One of the prominent figures in the history of the Mahabharata. He is describing Vidura. Vidura was conceived by Vyasa Deva in the 14th century. He took a sweet, light and gentle way of living. So Vidura quitted his old Soyine form. He became the central figure of the Mahabharata. In this way he is the leader of the Mahabharata. He has been a leader for two centuries. He was a great figure and the most experienced in the Mahabharata of this time around because he was a great person. He was a great warlord. Yasadeva in the womb of the maidservant of Ambika who was the mother of Maharaj Pandu. He is the incarnation of Yamaraj. Being cursed by Mandukamuni, he was to become a Shudra. The story is narrated as follows. Prabhupada's story. How Yamaraj had to take birth after being cursed as Vidura, the son of a maidservant. Once upon a time, the state police caught some thieves who had concealed themselves in the hermitage of Mandukamuni. The police constables, as usual, arrested all the thieves and Mandukamuni along with them. The magistrates specifically punished the Muni to death by being pierced with a lance. When he was just to be pierced, the news reached the king, and he at once stopped the act on consideration of his being a great Muni. The king personally begged the Muni's pardon for the mistake of his men. And the saint, that is Mandukamuni, at once went to Yamaraj who prescribes the destiny of the living beings. Yamaraj being questioned by the Muni, the king replied that the Muni in his childhood pierced an ant with a sharpened straw and for that reason he was put into difficulty. The Muni thought it unwise on the part of Yamaraj that he was punished for his childish innocence. And thus the Muni cursed Yamaraj to become a Shudra. And this Shudra incarnation of Yamaraj was known as Vidura, the Shudra brother of Dhritarashtra and Maharaj Pandu. But this Sudra son of the Kuru dynasty was equally treated by Bhishma Deva along with his other nephews and in due course Vidura was married with a girl who was also born in the womb of a Sudrani by a Brahmana. Although Vidura did not inherit the property of his father, that is the brother of Bhishma Deva, still he was given sufficient state property by Dhritarashtra, the elder brother of Vidura. Vidura was very much attached to his elder brother Dhritarashtra, and all along he tried to guide him on the right path. During the fratricidal war of Kurukshetra, Vidura repeatedly implored his elder brother Dhritarashtra to do justice to the sons of Pandu. But Duryodhana did not like such interference by his uncle Vidura, and he refused to do so. And thus Duryodhana practically insulted Vidura. This resulted in Vidura's leaving home for pilgrimage and taking instructions from Maitreya.

### Transcendental Loving Service, Krishna, Maitreya, Govinda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/p5Oq5KN4TRA?t=404
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The goal of all preliminaries in practicing religious principles is basically to come to the point of becoming established in transcendental loving service of the supreme lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains the meaning of the second verse, emphasizing that the ultimate goal of all religious practices is to become established in transcendental loving service to Lord Krishna, who is Govinda.

This resulted in Vidura's leaving home for pilgrimage and taking instructions from Maitreya. The meaning of this second verse is after asking various questions and becoming established in the transcendental loving service of Lord Krishna, Vidura retired from putting questions to Maitreya Muni. That means he kept asking questions till he was properly established in transcendental loving service of Krishna, so which means you can understand the goal of all preliminaries in practicing religious principles is basically to come to the point of becoming established in transcendental loving service of the supreme lord. In the purport Srila Prabhupada says Vidura retired from putting questions before Maitreya Muni when he was convinced by Maitreya Rishi that the samambonam of life is to be finally situated in the transcendental loving service of Lord Sri Krishna who is Govinda or one who satisfies his devotees in all respects.

---
### Sense Gratification, Spiritual Master, Vedic Literature, Karma, Jnana, Yoga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/p5Oq5KN4TRA?t=511
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Like Vidura, an inquisitive conditioned soul must approach a bona fide spiritual master like Maitreya and by intelligent inquiries must try to know everything about karma, fructive activities, about jnana, philosophical research for the supreme truth, and about yoga, the linking process of spiritual realization."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the conditioned soul's search for happiness through sense gratification and philosophical speculation, highlighting the importance of approaching a bona fide spiritual master like Maitreya to understand karma, jnana, and yoga, ultimately leading to the supreme truth.

The conditioned soul, the living being in material existence, seeks happiness by employing his senses in the modes of materialism, but that cannot give him satisfaction. Under illusion we think I can be happy, satisfied in every respect if I somehow engage in sense gratification. He then searches after the supreme truth by the empiric philosophical speculative method and intellectual feats, but if he does not find the ultimate truth, ultimate goal, he again goes down to material activities and engages himself in various philanthropic and altruistic works which all fail to give him satisfaction. So neither fruiting activities nor dry philosophical speculation can give one satisfaction because by nature a living being is the eternal servitor of Vishnu, and all the Vedic literatures give him direction towards that ultimate end. The Bhagavad-gita confirms the statement Krishna says from all the Vedas I am to be known. Like Vidura, an inquisitive conditioned soul must approach a bona fide spiritual master like Maitreya and get like Vidura inquisitive conditioned soul must approach a bona fide spiritual master like Maitreya and by intelligent inquiries must try to know everything about karma, fructive activities, about jnana, philosophical research for the supreme truth, and about yoga, the linking process of spiritual realization.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.11-13 | 26-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### invocation, mantra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjani Shalakaya Chaksharun Miritam Yena Tasvai Shri Gurave Namaha"
**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and mantras, including the Hare Krishna maha-mantra.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjani Shalakaya Chaksharun Miritam Yena Tasvai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasali Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare.

---
### devotion, inquiry
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=42
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You might have seen them or heard about them, are they all living happily in their abodes so this was the inquiry by Yudhishthira."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the verses to be discussed from Srimad Bhagavatam 1.13.11-13, focusing on Yudhishthira's inquiry to Vidura about the well-being of the Yadavas in Dwaraka.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. In this session we will be discussing from the eleventh, twelfth and thirteenth verses of the thirteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter is entitled Dhritarashtra Quits Home. The eleventh verse Apinaha Suhrudastata Bandhava Krishna Devataha Drishta Shrutava Yadavaha Svapuriyam Sukhamasya. This is an inquiry made by Yudhishthira to Vidura. The meaning of this verse is, My uncle you must have visited Dwaraka. In that holy place are our friends and well-wishers, the descendants of Yadu who were always wrapped in the service of the Lord, Sri Krishna. You might have seen them or heard about them, are they all living happily in their abodes so this was the inquiry by Yudhishthira.

---
### Krishna consciousness, devotion, favorability
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=123
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The real qualification of a pure devotee is to become wrapped in the thought of Krishna favorably."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the term 'Krishna Devata,' emphasizing that true devotion involves being favorably absorbed in the thought of Krishna, recognizing Him as the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

So in the purport points out the particular word Krishna Devata, that is those who are always wrapped in the service of Lord Krishna is significant. The Yadavas and the Pandavas were always wrapped in the thought of the Lord Krishna and his different transcendental activities were all pure devotees of the Lord like Vidura. Not only Vidura was a pure devotee, even the Yadavas and Pandavas even while staying in a palace or staying at home, they were always wrapped in the thought of Lord Krishna and his different transcendental activities and this way they are also counted among pure devotees of the Lord. Vidura left home in order to devote himself completely to the service of the Lord but the Pandavas and the Yadavas were always wrapped in the thought of Lord Krishna thus there is no difference in their pure devotional qualities either remaining at home or leaving home. The real qualification of a pure devotee is to become wrapped in the thought of Krishna favorably. This is very important to continuously absorb oneself in thinking of Krishna favorably, that is what is the meaning of favorably becoming absorbed in the thought of Krishna, knowing well that Lord Krishna is the absolute personality of Godhead.

---
### Krishna consciousness, devotion, unfavorable
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=289
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore Kamsa and Shishupala are not on the same level as pure devotees like Vidura, the Pandavas and the Yadavas."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts favorable Krishna consciousness with unfavorable absorption in Krishna, exemplified by demons like Kamsa and Shishupala, who do not recognize Krishna's supreme position.

So favorably being absorbed in thought of Krishna is called Krishna consciousness. Unfavorably always thinking of Krishna like Kamsa, that is not Krishna consciousness. Now these demons they were absorbed in thought of Krishna unfavorably or thinking him to be a powerful man only. See demons they don't recognize Krishna in their thoughts. They don't recognize Krishna to be the Supreme Personality of Godhead. They don't want to accept or they think that they can compete with Krishna. This is a very very big mistake. Therefore Kamsa and Shishupala are not on the same level as pure devotees like Vidura, the Pandavas and the Yadavas. Simply by thinking of Krishna always anyone can get salvation but to revive our dormant devotion to Krishna and to be transferred to his personal abode as his associate and engage in loving service is possible only by the divine grace of Lord Krishna. This is only for those who are absorbed in thinking of Krishna favorably, not unfavorably.

---
### devotion, association, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=387
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The art of constantly thinking of Krishna has to be learnt from Srila Prabhupad by following his instructions by reading his books, by associating with his devotees, by engaging in devotional service, in varieties of ways recommended in the scriptures."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of thinking of Krishna in loving devotion, drawing parallels with Yudhishthira's constant thought of Krishna and highlighting the path to the spiritual world through Krishna consciousness, association with devotees, and devotional service as taught by Srila Prabhupada.

Therefore to think of Krishna in Krishna consciousness. It is described in the Bhagavad Gita that always thinking of Krishna in loving devotion in thought of Krishna in loving devotion that is actually favorable and that is that immediately qualifies one to actually be transferred to the spiritual world in personal association of Krishna. Maharaja Yudhishthira was also always wrapped in the thought of Lord Krishna and his associates in Dwaraka. Otherwise he could not have asked all about them from Vidura. Maharaja Yudhishthira was therefore on the same level of devotion as Vidura although engaged in the state affairs of the kingdom of the world. This is an instruction for us that we may not be traveling to Holy places, may not be living in a temple but even living at home if we are able to absorb our mind in Krishna consciousness through chanting Hare Krishna as far as possible and gradually progressing to come to the stage of constantly thinking of Krishna in so many different ways. Maharaja Yudhishthira was also always wrapped in the thought of Lord Krishna in so many different ways. The art of constantly thinking of Krishna has to be learnt from Srila Prabhupad by following his instructions by reading his books, by associating with his devotees, by engaging in devotional service, in varieties of ways recommended in the scriptures we can be always thinking of Krishna and become attracted to Krishna and Krishna consciousness.

---
### compassion, truth, distress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=527
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.12, SB 1.13.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Compassionate Mahatma Vidura could not stand to see the Pandavas distressed at any time therefore he did not disclose this unpalatable and unbearable incident."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to verses 12 and 13, explaining why Vidura withheld the news of the Yadu dynasty's annihilation from the Pandavas, citing compassion and the principle of not speaking unpalatable truths that cause distress.

Promote ourselves to the kingdom of Krishna in the spiritual world at any time we can do that we can immediately transfer ourselves to the spiritual world the twelfth verse the meaning of this verse is thus being questioned by Maharaj Yudhishthira Mahatma Vidura gradually described everything he had personally experienced except the news of the annihilation of the Yadu dynasty Vidura told everything except that the Yadu dynasty was completely annihilated that he did not tell why in the 13th verse that is explained why so 13th verse the meaning of this 13th verse says compassionate Mahatma Vidura could not stand to see the Pandavas distressed at any time therefore he did not disclose this unpalatable and unbearable incident because calamities come of their own accord so in this 13th verse it's explained that Vidura did not disclose this unpalatable and unbearable incident of the annihilation of the Yadu dynasty to the Pandavas now Srita Prabhupada says in the purport according to Niti Shastra civic laws one should not speak an unpalatable truth to cause distress to others the Niti Shastra says satyam bhruyat priyam bhruyat na bhruyat satyam apriyam apriyam is unpalatable even though it may be the truth apriyam satyam unpalatable truth one should not speak because that will cause distress to others. See how refined is Vedic culture that you must speak the truth but only that which is palatable.

### compassion, detachment, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WGhmbvNuKoM?t=677
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Distress comes upon us in its own way by the laws of nature so one should not aggravate it by propaganda."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a forest fire to illustrate how distress arises naturally and should not be aggravated by spreading unpalatable truths, emphasizing Vidura's compassion in withholding the news of the Yadu dynasty's destruction from the Pandavas.

Why why is it like this because distress comes upon us in its own way by the laws of nature see nobody can prevent natural calamities they come of their own accord just like the example is given of a forest fire nobody goes to the forest to set fire to the trees but when there is sometimes in blazing summer heat when there is a wind blowing and there is friction between two dry bamboo pieces and there is a spark that spark can ignite a fire in the dry bamboo in the blazing sun and that fire will spread throughout the forest a dense forest filled with dry bamboo plants it can spread very quickly and the fire will even aid the spreading of the fire and a big portion of the forest can be entirely echeloned by this fire to collect out fire wherever they may be forest can be entirely burnt away by the forest fire and great destruction can happen now this is not due to somebody going and setting fire to the dry bamboo no it happens of its own accord by arrangement of providence therefore it is said here distress comes upon us in its own way by the laws of nature so one should not aggravate it by propaganda there is no need to speak of unpalatable truths if we want to not cause distress for people who are already distressed by natural calamities so this is so one should not aggravate the distress by propaganda further for a compassionate soul like Vidura especially in his dealings with the beloved Pandavas it was almost impossible to disclose an unpalatable piece of news like the annihilation of the Yadu dynasty the Pandavas and the Yadavas they were very closely related both by family ties and because both were devotees of pure devotees of Krishna so they always discussed about Krishna in absorbed in thought of Krishna as you said in the previous verse so therefore Vidura did not disclose this news of the annihilation Yadu dynasty purposely refrained from it and except that he told everything else about his travels to the Pandavas I'll stop here thank you very much Hari Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.14 | 27-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Vidura's purpose, Spiritual responsibility, Material absorption
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZBkgnr32SiA?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He stayed there just to rectify the mentality of his eldest brother Dhritarashtra."
**Summary:** This segment explains Vidura's purpose in visiting the palace of Yudhishthira, which was to rectify the mentality of Dhritarashtra and to remind householders of their spiritual responsibilities.

In this session, we will be discussing the 14th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is thus: Mahatma Vidura, being treated just like a godly person by his kinsmen, remained there for a certain period just to rectify his kinsmen, to rectify the mentality of his eldest brother, and in this way bring happiness to all the others. So this being said of the purpose of Vidura's visit to the palace of Yudhishthira, the Pandavas, after the battle of Kurukshetra. Why did Vidura come to the palace? So he has got a very, very important work. He stayed there just to rectify the mentality of his eldest brother Dhritarashtra. This was Vidura's purpose. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport very elaborately the significance of the visit of Vidura to this palace, Hastinapura, and similarly the significance of the travel and the visit of saintly persons of the caliber of Vidura to the homes of householders who are generally forgetful of their spiritual responsibility in their ordinary dealings. They become absorbed materially. So for such people, it's a very important instruction here in this verse.

---
### Devatas, Spiritual culture, Interplanetary travel
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZBkgnr32SiA?t=193
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is only the spiritual culture of the people concerned that makes interplanetary travel possible even in the present body."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how saintly persons like Vidura should be treated, the visits of devatas to pious homes in the past, and the possibility of interplanetary travel through spiritual culture, referencing Srila Prabhupada's book 'Easy Journey to Other Planets'.

So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, saintly persons like Vidura must be treated as well as a denizen from heaven. What's the meaning of this? In those days, denizens of heavenly planets used to visit homes like that of Maharaj Yudhishthira, and sometimes persons like Arjuna and others used to visit higher planets. Of course, nowadays we cannot see the devatas even if they visit the earth planet. First of all, devatas don't visit the earth planets in our present days because we are not engaged so much in pious activities in Kali Yuga. Most people are not engaged in pious activities. They are busy with sense gratification for selfish interest. So devatas are not interested in visiting such a place. And also in former times, some persons like Arjuna could visit higher planets. Arjuna visited the heavenly planet because he was exceptionally qualified for traveling like that. Then Srila Prabhupada gives the example of Narada. Srila Prabhupada says Narada is a spaceman who can travel unrestrictedly not only within the material universes but also in the spiritual universes. So Narada is a pure devotee. He has got all the mystic powers of a perfected yogi. A pure devotee has all the siddhis of a perfected yogi. So Narada can travel without the aid of a spacecraft everywhere within this universe, and he can even travel beyond the material universes to the spiritual world, to the personal abode of the Supreme Personality of Godhead in Vaikuntha and even in Goloka Brindavana. Narada can travel because he is a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Krishna. Therefore he can travel. Now about Narada, Srila Prabhupada says even Narada used to visit the palace of Maharaja Yudhishthira, and what to speak of other celestial demigods. So devatas would visit Yudhishthira because Yudhishthira was a very, very pious person, and not only that, he was himself a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. So Srila Prabhupada remarks here, it is only the spiritual culture of the people concerned that makes interplanetary travel possible even in the present body. Srila Prabhupada has written a book called Easy Journey to Other Planets.

---
### Interplanetary travel, Ashta Siddhi, Pure Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZBkgnr32SiA?t=389
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only pure devotees can go even to Krishna's personal abode from this earth planet or from anywhere they can go."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the possibility of interplanetary travel, emphasizing that pure devotees can travel to Krishna's abode, while those practicing Ashtanga Yoga can achieve Ashta Siddhi and travel to higher planets, but not beyond the coverings of the universe.

In that book, he says that in this present body, human beings who are not on the level of pure devotees, they cannot travel to upper planets in this earthly body. Not possible. They have to prepare themselves and change their bodies to actually transfer themselves to heavenly planets. Of course, if somebody practices Ashtanga Yoga and perfects the meditation process to achieve eight kinds of siddhi, Ashta Siddhi, then they can travel to the higher planets. But even Ashta Siddhi does not qualify a yogi to go beyond the coverings of the universe. To go beyond the coverings of the universe to the spiritual sky or the spiritual planets requires that one be a pure devotee of Krishna. Only pure devotees can go even to Krishna's personal abode from this earth planet or from anywhere they can go. So they can freely travel, pure devotees. So Narada, being a pure devotee, he can travel anywhere.

---
### Renunciation, Mercy, Deliverance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZBkgnr32SiA?t=483
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The purpose of living in the palace was to deliver his elder brother Dhritarashtra."
**Summary:** This segment explains why Yudhishthira received Vidura like a demigod, and emphasizes that sannyasis should not visit householders for material comforts but to deliver them, highlighting Vidura's purpose in staying at the palace to deliver Dhritarashtra.

Maharaja Yudhishthira therefore received Vidura in the manner of reception offered to the devatas or demigods. Further, Srila Prabhupada says Mahatma Vidura had already adopted the renounced order of life, and therefore he did not return to his paternal palace to enjoy some material comforts. This is a very important point that those who are in the renounced order of life, sannyasis, they should never visit any householder for enjoying a nice reception, for enjoying the comforts that the householders may offer to a saintly person or a sannyasi. No, never. So then why should they visit at all? It is explained here, Vidura accepted out of his own mercy what was offered to him by Maharaja Yudhishthira. But the purpose of living in the palace was to deliver his elder brother Dhritarashtra. This is the real purpose. Why should a sannyasi or a saintly person visit a householder? Just to deliver the householders who are otherwise absorbed in material activities. So here Srila Prabhupada says the purpose of Vidura visiting the palace and living there was to deliver his elder brother Dhritarashtra who was too much materially attached.

---
### Material Attachment, Compassion, Krishna's Plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZBkgnr32SiA?t=590
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's plan ultimately is that his representatives who are devotees who properly represent the Supreme Lord are meant to administer and rule this earth planet or any other planet for that matter."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Dhritarashtra's unhappiness after the Kurukshetra war, Krishna's plan for devotees to administer the earth, and Dhritarashtra's material attachment despite losing everything, contrasting it with Yudhishthira's compassion.

Dhritarashtra somehow, he was very unhappy that his sons could not succeed him. He was the caretaker king of the Hastinapura, the throne in Hastinapura, the royal throne. He was sitting in the in Hastinapura as the emperor of the world. But after the battle of Kurukshetra in which all his sons died in the battle, he was very unhappy that his sons could not succeed him, though by unfair means Duryodhana tried to kill the Pandavas, but Krishna protected the Pandavas, and Krishna's plan was something different than what Duryodhana planned or Dhritarashtra desired. So Krishna's plan ultimately is that his representatives who are devotees who properly represent the Supreme Lord are meant to administer and rule this earth planet or any other planet for that matter. So Srila Prabhupada further explains how Dhritarashtra became materially attached. Dhritarashtra lost all his state. Dhritarashtra lost all his state and descendants in the fight with Maharaj Yudhishthira, and still due to his sense of helplessness he did not feel ashamed to accept the charity and hospitality of Maharaj Yudhishthira.

---
### Hospitality, Enlightenment, Duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZBkgnr32SiA?t=712
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is a duty of enlightened souls to deliver the fallen ones and Vidura came for that reason."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts Yudhishthira's appropriate hospitality towards Dhritarashtra with Dhritarashtra's undesirable acceptance of it, highlighting Vidura's purpose in enlightening Dhritarashtra and guiding him towards spiritual cognition in his last days.

See Maharaj Yudhishthira is very, very conscientious that he felt compassion for Dhritarashtra, that Dhritarashtra had lost all his sons and he was in that aggrieved state. Yudhishthira was particularly careful to comfort Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, his uncle and aunt, his father's brother and sister-in-law, to offer Dhritarashtra and Gandhari very nice facilities in the palace. So Srila Prabhupada says on the part of Maharaj Yudhishthira it was quite in order to maintain his uncle in a befitting manner. Yudhishthira was right in doing that. But acceptance of such magnanimous hospitality by Dhritarashtra was not at all desirable. Even though Yudhishthira offered Dhritarashtra very nice facility to stay very comfortably, but for Dhritarashtra it was not proper. He accepted it because he thought there was no alternative. Dhritarashtra thought there was, what else do I have? But Vidura particularly came to enlighten Dhritarashtra and to give him a lift to the higher status of spiritual cognition. Vidura came to enlighten Dhritarashtra that his last days he should not spend comfortably living in a palace accepting all the kind hospitality of Yudhishthira. No. So Srila Prabhupada says it is a duty of enlightened souls to deliver the fallen ones and Vidura came for that reason. So Vidura came for the purpose of enlightening Dhritarashtra and thereby delivering him.

---
### Spiritual Enlightenment, Spiritual Talks, Krishna Consciousness
