**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If we simply hear the spiritual messages from the Bhagavatam and the Bhagavad Gita, it will work on our heart and our heart will get transformed and purified so that we can become Krishna conscious and prepare ourselves to go back to God."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by stating that Vidura was successful in enlightening Dhritarashtra, emphasizing the refreshing nature of spiritual talks and their ability to attract and enliven everyone, ultimately leading to Krishna consciousness and the preparation to return to God.

And it will be explained in this chapter that Vidura was successful in impressing upon Dhritarashtra the importance of preparing himself to quit his body by becoming spiritually enlightened. But Srila Prabhupada further says, but talks of spiritual enlightenment are so refreshing that while instructing Dhritarashtra, Vidura attracted the attention of all the members of the family, and all of them took pleasure in hearing him patiently. Spiritual talks are always enlivening for the soul. Spiritual talks even though may be intended for particular people, but it can be enlivening for everyone. Even the younger ones or those otherwise engaged can also become attracted by spiritually enlivening talks. This is the way of spiritual realization. The message should be heard attentively, and if spoken by a realized soul, it will act on the dormant heart of the conditioned soul. If we simply hear the spiritual messages from the Bhagavatam and the Bhagavad Gita, it will work on our heart and our heart will get transformed and purified so that we can become Krishna conscious and prepare ourselves to go back to God. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.15 | 28-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Varnashrama, Transcendental Qualification, Vidura's Status
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gz918Ho68hY?t=76
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So always the scriptures say, irrespective of birth, if somebody is trained up and has got the qualities of a Brahmana or a Kshatriya or even a Vaisya, even though he may be born in a Shudra family or a Shudra woman, still he is accepted as a Brahmana, Kshatriya or Vaisya according to qualification, training, the qualities he displays, etc."
**Summary:** This segment explains Vidura's birth as a Shudra and how, despite this, he was qualified to be a spiritual preceptor due to his training, renunciation, and transcendental knowledge.

The meaning of this verse is, as long as Vitura played the part of a Shudra, being cursed by Manduka Muni, Arhyama officiated at the post of Yamaraj to punish those who committed sinful acts. So in the Purport, Shila Prabhupada explains, already it has been told that Vitura was actually Yamaraj. Yamaraj was a Shudra. Yamaraj was cursed due to a small mistake, a slight mistake he did, in carrying out his official responsibility. And he was cursed by Manduka Muni. And therefore, he was born as Vitura in the womb of a Shudra woman. But actually, Prabhupada explains, the real reason why it was actually a higher arrangement by the Supreme Lord, that Yamaraj actually was cursed to take birth like that. So the hidden meaning of this particular incident is very nicely explained by Prabhupada in the Purport. So Shila Prabhupada says, Vitura, born in the womb of a Shudra woman, was forbidden even to be a party of royal heritage, along with his brothers Dhritarashtra and Pandu. If I may refresh your memory about who is Vitura, in terms of his relationship with Dhritarashtra and Pandu, all the three are sons of Vichitravirya, but they are posthumous children. They were born after Vichitravirya's death. And how were they born after Vichitravirya's death? This is called Deva-rena-suton-patti. That means, if a person is unable to conceive children, whatever may be the reason, why such a person, such a person is unable to conceive a child. The emperor of the world, he has to have sons who can succeed him. Qualified, capable, trained up sons. But Vichitravirya died untimely. So then there was nobody to ascend the throne. So then the mother of Vichitravirya, Satyavati, the wife of Shantanu, Vichitravirya's father, Satyavati, she called Vyasadeva and asked for a solution to this problem. So Vyasadeva agreed to impregnate the wives of Vichitravirya, Ampika and Ambalika, two queens. But it so happened that Vyasadeva, even though Vyasadeva was in perfect consciousness, but because he was undergoing severe austerities before he came to impregnate these wives of his brother, Deva and Sutrapati, means conceiving a child by the brother in the womb of one's own wife. So Vyasadeva was the son of Satyavati before her marriage with Shantanu. So Vyasadeva was called upon to impregnate these two queens so that they'll be good progeny for later on occupying the throne. So Dhritarashtra and Pandu were conceived by the two queens, but both of them were not conceived properly. So therefore, in order to conceive a third child, and one of the queens was asked to conceive a third child, but she, instead of conceiving one more child, she asked her maid to go in her place to conceive a child. And that royal maid actually was in very proper consciousness, and therefore, Vyasadeva conceived in that womb of the maiden, a maid servant, Yamaraj, who was cursed to be born as the son of a maid servant. So that was Vidura. So Vidura is actually a step-brother of Dhritarashtra and Pandu. So Dhritarashtra and Pandu were the two sons conceived in the queen. Even though they are conceived by Vyasadeva, they are considered sons of Vichitramirya. All the three are considered sons of Vichitramirya. But the son of the maid servant doesn't have the same position in terms of inheritance like the sons of the queen. So Dhritarashtra and Pandu inherited whatever the royal opulence, but entitled to inherit, but Vidura, as the son of a maid servant, was not entitled for that. So therefore, Srila Prabhupada is saying, Vidura, born in the womb of a Shudra woman, was forbidden even to be a party of royal heritage along with his brothers, Dhritarashtra and Pandu. So the question is being raised, how could Vidura occupy the post of a preacher to instruct such learned kings and Kshatriyas as Dhritarashtra and Maharaj Yudhishtira? If he is the son of a maid servant and he doesn't even have any inheritance, what is his authority to instruct and preach such learned Kshatriyas as Dhritarashtra and Maharaj Yudhishtira? So Srila Prabhupada gives the answer. The first answer is that even though it is accepted that Vidura was a Shudra by birth, because he renounced the word for spiritual enlightenment, by the authority of Rishi Maitreya, and was thoroughly educated by him in transcendental knowledge, he was quite competent to occupy the post of an Acharya, spiritual preceptor. So always the scriptures say, irrespective of birth, if somebody is trained up and has got the qualities of a Brahmana or a Kshatriya or even a Vaisya, even though he may be born in a Shudra family or a Shudra woman, still he is accepted as a Brahmana, Kshatriya or Vaisya according to qualification, training, the qualities he displays, etc. So Vidura was actually Yamaraja himself and even though he was born in the womb of a Shudra woman, he had all the good qualities because of which he was in a position to renounce and seek spiritual enlightenment by Maitreya Rishi and he was also thoroughly educated by Maitreya Rishi in transcendental knowledge. Therefore he was competent to occupy the post of an Acharya or a spiritual preceptor.

---
### Krishna Consciousness, Spiritual Master, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gz918Ho68hY?t=612
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "According to Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, anyone who is conversant in the transcendental knowledge or the science of Godhead, be he a Brahmana or a Shudra, a householder or a Sannyasi, he Krishna tattva veta, he is a Brahman."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's view that anyone with knowledge of Krishna consciousness, regardless of birth or ashram, is qualified to be a spiritual master, and also references Chanakya Pandit's similar view.

Now Srila Prabhupada percourses Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. According to Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, anyone who is conversant in the transcendental knowledge or the science of Godhead, be he a Brahmana or a Shudra, a householder or a Sannyasi, kiba vipra kiba nyasi, shudra kenanaya, he Krishna tattva veta, he is a Brahman. If a person is born as a Shudra or if he is a Brahmana or in terms of ashrama, in terms of varana, whether he is a Brahmana or a Shudra doesn't matter. In terms of ashrama, whether he is a householder or a Sannyasi doesn't matter. As long as he is conversant with transcendental knowledge or the science of Krishna consciousness, he is a Brahmana. In terms of Akshara, he is a Brahmana of Krishna consciousness, he is eligible to become a spiritual master. ये कृष्ण तत्व वेव्तकृ। One who knows in truth that science of Krishna consciousness, such a person, से गुरु होये, he is qualified to occupy the post of a spiritual master, a spiritual preceptor, a guru, a qualified guru. Now Prabhupada also quotes Chanakya Pandit. Even in the ordinary moral quotes mentioned by Chanakya Pandit, the great politician and moralist, there is no harm in taking lessons from a person who may be by birth less than a Shudra, provided that person is qualified to impart lessons. So this birth is not important at all. That's what the Acharyas have taught us, that is what the scriptures teach us. That's what even Chanakya Pandit says. This is one part of the answer that Vidura is qualified. He has undergone thorough training by Maitreya Rishi. He has renounced the world for the sake of gaining spiritual enlightenment. So exceptionally qualified. Even though he has taken birth in the womb of a maidservant.

---
### Yamaraj, Mahajana, Pure Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Gz918Ho68hY?t=755
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, therefore, he is the most authorized and competent and most appropriate person to preach to anyone in the whole universe what to speak of preaching to Dhritarashtra."
**Summary:** The segment explains that Vidura was actually Yamaraj, one of the twelve Mahajanas, and therefore a pure devotee transcendental to varna and ashrama, making him supremely qualified to preach.

Next is the other part of the reply for that question that was raised. How is he competent to instruct somebody like the Dhrashtra or Maharaja Yudhishthira? The second part of the answer is Vidura was not actually a Shudra. He was to play the part of a so-called Shudra for one hundred years being cursed by Manduka Muni. He was the incarnation of Yamaraj. And who is Yamaraj? Yamaraj is one of the twelve Mahajanas. Mahajanas means they are the authorities on Dharma. In the whole universe there are twelve Mahajanas. So Yamaraj is one of the twelve Mahajanas on the level with such exalted personalities as the other Mahajanas, Brahma, Narada, Shiva, Kapila, Bhishma, Prahlada, etc. So all these Mahajanas are the most exalted personalities in the whole universe. They are authorities on Dharma. So being a Mahajana, it is the duty of Yamaraj to preach the cult of devotion to the people of the world as Narada, Brahma and other Mahajanas do. All the Mahajanas are first-class Mahabhagavatas. Top-class Vaishnavas. And they preach the science of Krishna Consciousness to everyone. They are the most competent preachers of Krishna Consciousness. So, Prabhupada explains further. But Yamaraj is always busy in his platonic kingdom punishing the doers of sinful acts. Yamaraj has got a duty. Even though he is a Mahajana, he is qualified. But he has got this duty of punishing the wrongdoers. So, he generally doesn't have much time to take leave from his responsible office of punishing the wrongdoers. Therefore, Yamaraj wanted to preach the glories of the Lord. By the will of the Lord, he was cursed to be born as a son of a Shudra. But he took birth at a time when Krishna had appeared as an incarnation. So, he participated in Krishna Leela. And also, he is an exalted devotee. Even though he is cursed, his consciousness doesn't become affected by the birth because his activities are always those of a devotee in any body, in any situation, in any position. He is always a pure devotee, Mahabhagavata, fixed up in his position as a pure devotee. So, such a devotee is never a Shudra, or a Brahmana, or a householder, or a sannyasi. He is transcendental to all divisions of varna and ashrama. So, therefore, he is the most authorized and competent and most appropriate person to preach to anyone in the whole universe what to speak of preaching to Dhritarashtra. So, this is the explanation given. We should always understand him, Vidura, to be greater than the purest Brahmana because he is a pure devotee. He is also a Mahajan. Hare Krishna. I will stop here. Thank you very much.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.16 | 29-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### duty, self-realization, instruction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=75
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura has come to instruct Dhritharashtra, to save him from missing the opportunity for self-realization in his human form of life."
**Summary:** This segment explains the context of Vidura's visit to Yudhishthira, focusing on his intention to instruct Dhritharashtra and guide him towards self-realization.

So, the meaning of this verse is: Having won his kingdom and observed the birth of one grandson competent to continue the noble tradition of his family, Maharaj Yudhishthira reigned peacefully and enjoyed uncommon opulence in cooperation with his younger brothers who were all expert administrators to the common people. So this verse is describing Yudhishthira's position when Vidura is visiting. Vidura's purpose is not to enjoy any nice hospitality. Rather, he has come to instruct Dhritharashtra, to rescue Dhritharashtra from his so-called comfortable living towards the end of his life. Instead of preparing for putting the body through self-realization, Dhritharashtra is completely neglectful of his spiritual responsibility. So Vidura has come to instruct Dhritharashtra, to save him from missing the opportunity for self-realization in his human form of life.

---
### devotion, duty, Kurukshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=157
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So even after the term of exile is over, Duryodhana refused to give back the share of the kingdom."
**Summary:** This segment describes Yudhishthira's peaceful rule, his unhappiness with the Kurukshetra war, and Duryodhana's refusal to return the Pandavas' kingdom, leading to the war.

So what about Yudhishthira? So that is being explained by Prabhupada in this purport. Yudhishthira was, the verse says, ruling very nicely, assisted by his younger brothers who were all expert administrators. And he had a competent grandson because as soon as Parikshit was born, the learned astrologist told he is a competent successor for Yudhishthira. When he grows up, he will have excellent qualities. So Yudhishthira was very peacefully ruling, waiting for his grandson to grow up and take charge. Till then Yudhishthira was accepting to rule the whole world. Now Srila Prabhupada says something important about Yudhishthira, that both Maharaj Yudhishthira and Arjuna were unhappy from the beginning of the battle of Kurukshetra. Why were they unhappy? They were not very comfortable fighting against their own kinsmen, simply for the sake of getting their share of the kingdom to rule. Duryodhana cleverly cheated the Pandavas and sent them away for exile, 13 years. So even after the term of exile is over, Duryodhana refused to give back the share of the kingdom. So then Kshatriya code of conduct is, if a Kshatriya challenges, then another Kshatriya cannot refuse the challenge. So Yudhishthira could not refuse the challenge offered by Duryodhana. So Yudhishthira had to engage in fighting the battle of Kurukshetra because Duryodhana challenged.

---
### devotion, duty, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=277
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna wants the world to be ruled by his representative who is a devotee."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Yudhishthira and Arjuna fought out of duty, as it was Krishna's plan for a devotee to rule the kingdom and guide people towards spiritual life and Krishna consciousness.

So even Arjuna, even though he was very very powerful, even Yudhishthira was very powerful and strong and not afraid of fighting, but he did not like to fight for the sake of gaining some kingdom, because by and heart he is a pure devotee. Both Yudhishthira and Arjuna are pure devotees. Therefore it is described by Prabhupada here that they were unhappy from the beginning. Even though they were unwilling to kill their own men in the fight, it had to be done as a matter of duty. Why? Because it was planned by the supreme will of Lord Krishna that somebody like Yudhishthira should rule the kingdom and not be corrupt, non-devotional Duryodhana. Krishna wants the world to be ruled by his representative who is a devotee. Such a devotee is the perfect person to rule, because people have to not only be taken care of in terms of their material necessities, but they should also be directed and protected for practicing spiritual life to make their human form of life successful by preparing themselves to go back to Godhead. So that's why it is so important that the ruler not only be competent in terms of material qualifications to rule the kingdom, but also be spiritually competent to guide the citizens how they can side by side, apart from doing their material duties, also practice Krishna consciousness to actually prepare themselves for going back to Godhead.

---
### devotion, duty, lineage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=405
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Practically there was none to continue the Kuru dynasty after them, the Pandavas."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Krishna's desire for Yudhishthira to rule, the aftermath of the Kurukshetra war, the attempted assassination of Parikshit in the womb, and Yudhishthira's relief at the survival of his grandson.

So, because Krishna wanted Yudhishthira to go home and Duryodhana was not a devotee, so he was not giving any spiritual guidance to the people. He himself was not a devotee. So where would he give guidance to others to become devotees? So that is the reason Krishna wanted it, therefore as a duty they had to fight in the battle. After the battle, Maharaj Yudhishthira was unhappy over such mass killings. Huge massacre happened. It was a world war. So in that world war so many people died. Practically there was none to continue the Kuru dynasty after them, the Pandavas. Pandavas took charge of ruling. But there were no descendants in the Kuru dynasty. The only remaining hope was a child in the womb of Yudhishthira's daughter, Lavuktha Rao. And that child in the womb was attacked by Ashwatthama. Even the child in the womb was attacked by Ashwatthama. The last surviving descendant of the entire Kuru dynasty was attacked by Ashwatthama. But by the grace of the Supreme Lord Krishna the child was saved in the womb of the mother. The child was saved by Krishna. This has already been explained in an earlier chapter. So after the settlement of all disturbing conditions and re-establishment of the peaceful order of the state and after seeing the surviving child Parikshit well satisfied Maharaj Yudhishthira felt some relief as a human being. After the massacre Yudhishthira was very disturbed, but he was instructed by Bhishma that he has to do his duty and not become afflicted by his sorrow over the big killing that has happened in the battle. So he was pacified by Bhishma. Yudhishthira was pacified by Bhishma. And he was even more happy rather even more peaceful when this Parikshit was saved and Parikshit took birth as a competent descendant to ascend the throne after Yudhishthira Maharaj. So things were settled and he felt some relief as a human being.

---
### detachment, illusion, material happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=565
**Verse References:** BG 5.22
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Material happiness is always illusory and temporary."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Yudhishthira, as a pure devotee, had no attraction for material happiness because it is illusory and temporary, being based on sense contact which is ultimately a source of misery.

Although he had very little attraction for material happiness which is always illusory and temporary. See Prabhupada is mentioning a very important point that material happiness which can be had by having a nice kingdom growing peacefully, having a competent successor and having very able administrators as younger brothers, younger brothers as administrators. In spite of all this Yudhishthira as a pure devotee had no attraction for material happiness. Why? Because as Prabhupada says here material happiness is always illusory and temporary. Two characters only. Illusory because material happiness is the happiness due to sense contact. And what is the description of material happiness due to sense contact? In the Bhagavad Gita Krishna says in the fifth chapter: yehi samsparsha ja bhoga dukha yonaya evade aadyantamant kaunteya na teshu ramate buddhah. Intelligent people who are totally enlightened do not take pleasure in happiness arising from sense contact because they know all such happiness is simply a temporary sensation due to the sense contact and it is always a source of misery in the material world. So what we think as a source of happiness is actually a disguised source of misery in the future.

---
### illusion, sense gratification, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=681
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "All material happiness is like this. It is promising to give us great amount of happiness, but that is just a false promise."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of cold water in winter and summer to illustrate how material happiness is based on illusion and favorable sense contact, which is temporary and unreliable.

All material happiness is like this. It is promising to give us great amount of happiness, but that is just a false promise. It is due to illusion. Due to illusion we consider some type of sense contact as favorable and another type of sense contact as unfavorable. So when the sense contact is favorable we think it is happiness. And when the same sense contact is unfavorable we think it is distress. Prabhupada gives a simple example to help us understand. It is like cold water. In winter in the severe cold cold water is very unpleasant, very harsh for the sense of touch. The same cold water at the same temperature in summer it is very very pleasing for the sense of touch. In fact we like to take a cold water bath in summer and it is very hot outside. So it is just the contact which is due to some particular situation or condition it is either interpreted as happiness or distress based on whether it is favorable contact or unfavorable contact. This is the nature of all kinds of sense contact. We have five primary senses, external senses, the eyes, ears, nose, tongue, sense of touch, skin and we have one sense within the mind. Now including the mind with these six senses we are trying to enjoy happiness, material happiness of this world. But our only avenue for material happiness through these senses is sense contact. And that sense contact simply is a concoction in our mind and we are trying to enjoy something that is not so nice or something that is painful or something that is pleasurable. Just like another example.

---
### illusion, likes and dislikes, material happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rGdn_0Yg94Q?t=823
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So neither happiness nor distress is actually the reality. Reality is it is illusion."
**Summary:** Using the example of edible items, this segment explains how our pre-conditioned likes and dislikes influence our perception of pleasure and pain, highlighting the illusory nature of material happiness and distress.

If you are given some edible which you like to eat you will think oh it is so nice I am having this for eating because you like the taste, the contact of that edible with your sense of taste, oh so nice. The same thing for somebody else who does not like it. They when they look at it oh I don't like it I don't want it. So even if it is edible even if it is to the liking of somebody some other person for me it is not likeable so I don't consider it as a pleasurable experience to eat such an edible. So the same thing according to our concoction of likes and dislikes we are born into this material world with certain set of likes and dislikes, material likes and material dislikes and exactly according to these likes and dislikes we consider something as pleasurable and something as not pleasurable. So this is the basis of all material happiness and distress. So neither happiness nor distress is actually the reality. Reality is it is illusion. It is illusion. But we in ignorance we seek after material happiness and try to avoid material distress. And we are always in this way under illusion. So that is being explained. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.17 | 1-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### time, illusion, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HZXg0W7no-0?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every second of human life is meant for making an ultimate solution to the problems of life."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains Suta Goswami's narration about Dhritarashtra wasting time despite being cared for by Yudhishthira, emphasizing the importance of utilizing time for eternal benefit and contrasting materialistic thinking with spiritual awareness.

In this session, we will be discussing the 17th verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is: insurmountable eternal time imperceptibly overcomes those who are too much attached to family affairs and are always engrossed in their thought. So this is Sotu Goswami narrating to the sages of Naimisharanya, the reality about the time being wasted by Dhritarashtra, even though he is very nicely taken care of by Yudhishthira. He is spending his last few days without utilizing it properly for eternal benefit. So that is going to be explained. And that is the statement made by Sotu Goswami. What is that? Insurmountable eternal time. Time is eternal, but it is continuously passing, passing, passing, passing. And nobody can overcome it. The effect of time, the influence of time, nobody can overcome it. No materialist can overcome it. So imperceptibly this time overtakes us, particularly those who are too much attached to family affairs and engrossed in thought of the family, its well-being, and children and the bonding of affection between the different family members. Like that, time just passes away, and human form of life is wasted without any eternal benefit. So that is going to be explained. Srila Prabhupada says, "I am now happy. I have everything in order. My bank balance is quite enough. I can now give my children their first aid. I am now successful. The poor beggar sannyasis depend on God, but they come to me for begging from me. Therefore, I am more than the Supreme Father." Prabhupada says, "These are some of the thoughts which engross the insanely attached householder who is blind to the passing of eternal time."

---
### time, spiritual practice, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HZXg0W7no-0?t=418
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every second of human life is meant for making an ultimate solution to the problems of life."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami emphasizes the irretrievable nature of time and the importance of utilizing every second for spiritual progress, highlighting the cycle of birth and death and the unique opportunity presented by human life to address fundamental problems through spiritual practice.

Supposing I lose time, I cannot regain that lost time by any means. It is lost forever. So, why is Prabhupada telling this? Every second of human life is meant for making an ultimate solution to the problems of life, the fundamental problems of life, which we cannot solve except by spiritual method. If we don't make a solution, then we have the opportunity for spiritual practice in the human form of life. Then we simply are going to repeat the cycle of birth and death. And when repeating the cycle of birth and death, we may take birth in lower species. And in the lower species, if we take birth, there is no opportunity for somebody in the lower species, a soul in the lower species, to inquire about spiritual life, to take up the practice of spiritual life in lower life forms. It is not possible, generally. So, they are losing the birth and death opportunity for eternal life full of bliss and knowledge by not practicing spiritual life. They spend away all their time in material pursuits, and time lost is lost forever. So, the ignorant people, spiritually ignorant people, they don't understand a fundamental fact about this material body. What is that? This material body is subject to birth, death, disease, old age. And these four fundamental problems in the material body are the cause of all sufferings of the living being. Otherwise, the living being himself is eternal and never born, never dies, never has to face old age or disease in this spiritual body, in this spiritual form. Never has to face.

---
### illusion, devotional service, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HZXg0W7no-0?t=592
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such illusion cannot affect a person who is engaged in pure devotional service."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains that foolish people forget the miseries of the material body and their potential for eternal bliss, emphasizing that illusion cannot affect those engaged in pure devotional service, using Yudhishthira Maharaj and the Pandavas as examples of detachment from illusory happiness.

Even if some people take note of these problems, miseries which crop up from time to time, they do not know how to solve these miseries in such a way that we come out of the miseries entirely. They do not know that they become engrossed in the temporary family affairs, not knowing that they are losing valuable time in human form of life. So, this is called illusion. Such illusion cannot affect a person who is engaged in pure devotional service. So, Yudhishthira Maharaj and the Pandavas, they were all engaged in service of Lord Krishna. So, they had very little attraction for illusory happiness because they know what is the value of this illusory happiness. You know, it is worthless. And they also know they have to prepare for eternal life by practicing devotional service. So, they were engaged in Krishna consciousness. And therefore, they were always attracted to spiritual perfection, achieving spiritual perfection at the end of life by practicing spirituality now in the human form when we got an opportunity. So, Maharaj Yudhishthira was always fixed in the service of Lord Mukunda or Krishna. And therefore, there was no point in seeking the happiness of life as it was in the kingdom of heaven and what to speak of the happiness available on the earth because he knows all these are illusory, all these are temporal. So, therefore, happiness, being attached to family life, they do not realize that insurmountable eternal time is overtaking them and they are losing valuable time, wasting it in some material pursuits. So they don't realize this doesn't apply to Yudhishthira Maharaj, it doesn't apply to Pandavas, it doesn't apply to devotees, it applies to Dhritarashtra who was actually out of attachment for his body, for his wife, for the comforts, material comforts that Yudhishthira was offering as a courtesy. He was lost in that and he was completely lost.

---
### detachment, spiritual enlightenment, absolute happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HZXg0W7no-0?t=775
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who is factually learned, he knows that material happiness is not important, it's not worthwhile."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami concludes by emphasizing Vidura's role in enlightening Dhritarashtra, contrasting material happiness with Brahma Sukha (absolute happiness), and highlighting the importance of spiritual enlightenment over material comforts.

Neglecting spiritual life. So this verse is applicable to Dhritarashtra and Vidura had come exactly to shake up Dhritarashtra out of the ignorance and to instruct him how he can save himself before that comes. So Srila Prabhupada says that one who is factually learned, he knows that material happiness is not important, it's not worthwhile. There is something called Brahma Sukha or absolute happiness, spiritual happiness. That Brahma Sukha or spiritual happiness is eternal and it is always unconditionally experienced by those engaged in devotional service. So such devotees, they have no attraction for any material, including material happiness or material comforts. So Maharaj Yudhishthira actually was a true devotee of Yudhishthira. He was a true devotee of Yudhishthira. He was spiritual, enlightened, was spiritually alert. But Dhritarashtra was not. So it was actually for the sake of Dhritarashtra that Vidura had come to enlightened Dhritarashtra and that is why we will hear how Dhritarashtra strongly instructs Dhritarashtra to come out of this illusion. I will stop here. Thank you very much.

**Summary:** Vidura visits Dhritarashtra to instruct him on his neglected spiritual duty, as Dhritarashtra is engrossed in material concerns.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 18th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is Mahatma Vidura knew all this and therefore he addressed Dhritarashtra saying, "My dear king, please get out of here immediately. Do not delay. Just see how fear has overtaken you." So the purpose for Mahatma Vidura to visit the palace of Hastinapura was to actually instruct Dhritarashtra about his prime duty, spiritual duty, which he had completely neglected. He was engrossed in securing his bodily comforts, ensuring that his sons will inherit the kingdom against their rightful claim, etc. So now Vidura has come specifically to instruct Dhritarashtra and now he is addressing Dhritarashtra.

### spiritual instruction, death, equality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Pz3NNsvCyTs?t=133
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Cruel death cares for no one, be he Dhritarashtra or even Maharaj Yudhishthira; therefore, spiritual instruction as was given to old Dhritarashtra was equally applicable to younger Maharaj Yudhishthira."
**Summary:** Prabhupada explains that Vidura's instruction is urgent because fear has overtaken Dhritarashtra, and that spiritual instruction is equally applicable to both old Dhritarashtra and young Yudhishthira because death is impartial.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains the reason why Vidura is telling Dhritarashtra, "Get out of here immediately. Do not delay." It's urgent that he has to leave the palace, and one of the reasons he gives is because fear has overtaken you now. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains this: cruel death cares for no one, be he Dhritarashtra or even Maharaj Yudhishthira; therefore, spiritual instruction as was given to old Dhritarashtra was equally applicable to younger Maharaj Yudhishthira. Dhritarashtra is the uncle of Maharaj Yudhishthira, his father's brother. So Dhritarashtra is old and Yudhishthira is younger than Dhritarashtra, but this instruction which Vidura is giving is equally applicable to both Dhritarashtra and Yudhishthira.

### qualification, duty, intrigue
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Pz3NNsvCyTs?t=348
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Not just because, I am the eldest son, so I should ascend the throne, regardless of whether I am blind, I am unqualified. No, it's never like that."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why Dhritarashtra is addressed as 'Rajan', despite being disqualified from the throne due to his blindness, and how he became caretaker king after Pandu's death, harboring ambitions to become the factual king.

The word Rājan is especially addressed to Dhritarashtra. Why is the word Rājan applicable to Dhritarashtra, particularly? It's because Dhritarashtra was the eldest son of his father, and therefore, according to law, he was to be installed on the throne of Hastinapura. But because he was blind from birth, he was disqualified from his rightful claim. See how, in the ancient times, they were so conscious of exercising their claim based on their qualification. Not just because, I am the eldest son, so I should ascend the throne, regardless of whether I am blind, I am unqualified. No, it's never like that. So, he was disqualified because he was blind by birth. But he could not forget. Dhritarashtra could not forget the bereavement. And his disappointment was somewhat compensated after the death of his younger brother, Pandu. Pandu died untimely, when he was very young. So, Dhritarashtra was made the caretaker king, because there was no one to ascend the throne after Pandu's death, because the children were all... His younger brother left behind him some minor children, and Dhritarashtra became the natural guardian of them. But at heart, he wanted to become the factual king and hand the kingdom over to his own sons headed by Duryodhana. With all these imperial ambitions, Dhritarashtra wanted to become a king, and he contrived all sorts of intrigues in consultation with his brother-in-law. And he contrived all sorts of intrigues in consultation with his brother-in-law, Shakuni.

She was married to a blind king. So, Shakuni was very angry. Why my sister, who is so qualified, so beautiful, so chaste, so very good in all qualities, why is she being married to a blind person? So, he decided to destroy the Kuru dynasty. To destroy the Kuru dynasty. So, this was Shakuni's agenda. And when Dhritarashtra, he contrived all sorts of intrigues, Shakuni actually became very much instrumental in trying to, in Dhritarashtra's attempt at somehow trying to destroy the Pandavas, because Shakuni's agenda was somehow the Kuru dynasty should be destroyed. But everything failed by the will of the Lord. Dhritarashtra was not successful. Shakuni was not successful. Everything failed by the will of the Lord.

### aging, disease, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Pz3NNsvCyTs?t=695
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura as a saint and as the duty-bound affectionate youngest brother of Dhritarashtra wanted to awaken Dhritarashtra from his slumber of disease and old age."
**Summary:** Vidura, as a saintly and affectionate brother, wants to awaken Dhritarashtra from his slumber of old age and disease, as his body is failing.

Now Vidura as a saint and as the duty-bound affectionate youngest brother of Dhritarashtra wanted to awaken Dhritarashtra from his slumber of disease and old age. Now what is Dhritarashtra's condition? He had become old. So invalidity due to old age has set him. That will be described by Vidura later on. That means as the body ages, generally the senses become weaker and weaker and weaker. And internally the organs start failing. The digestion becomes poor. The liver doesn't function properly. The heart becomes weak. So many changes in the body. And that will be described later on. These are all changes which are natural, consequence of the aging of the body. And nobody can prevent it. It's natural for everyone to experience this if they live beyond a certain stage of this body. When old age sets in, if somebody is still living, then in the old age, even though they may be happy that I am still living, but their body may not be in the best of conditions. So Dhritarashtra was facing the invalidity due to old age and also the disease that accompanies sometimes the aging of the body.

### time, devotion, control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Pz3NNsvCyTs?t=812
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone is the servant of eternal time... Nobody can overcome Kala except a pure devotee who transcends the material conditions, the material modes, and even Kala."
**Summary:** Vidura sarcastically addresses Dhritarashtra as king, emphasizing that everyone is a servant of time (Kala), which represents the Supreme Lord, and only pure devotees can transcend it, highlighting Vidura's mission to help Dhritarashtra save himself.

Vidura therefore sarcastically addressed Dhritarashtra as the king, which he actually was not. Dhritarashtra was not the king. Then why is Vidura addressing him as the king? Rajan. The word says Rajan nirgam yatham shigram, "My dear king, please leave this place immediately." So it was a sarcastic address. Everyone is the servant of eternal time. You see Kala reigns supreme in this material world, because it is a representation of the Supreme Lord Paramatma. Nobody can overcome Kala except a pure devotee who transcends the material conditions, the material modes, and even Kala. Only a pure devotee can transcend Kala. But those who are not taken to devotional service, they cannot transcend Kala. So therefore, no one can be king in this material world, in the real sense of the term. Nobody is like the controller. Everyone is controlled. So therefore, this situation which Dhritarashtra was in, Vidura had come to get him out of this situation, to help him save himself. That was Vidura's mission. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Om Ajnana Timira Andhasya Jnananjani Shalakaya Chakshur Unmilitam Jena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. In this session, we will be discussing from the 19th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Pratikriyana yasyeha kutashchit karhichit prabho saesha bhagavan kalaha sarveshamna samagatah. The meaning of this verse is, this frightful situation cannot be remedied by any person in this material world. My Lord, it is the Supreme Personality of Godhead as eternal time or Kala that has approached us all.

### death, Hiranyakashipu, immortality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ocBAQPlHq1k?t=666
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone beginning from Brahma down to the most insignificant creature in this material world who has taken birth is subject to death."
**Summary:** The speaker gives the example of Hiranyakashipu, who wanted to be immortal and underwent severe penance, but Brahma could not grant him immortality because Brahma himself is subject to death, illustrating that everyone who has taken birth is subject to death.

So Srila Prabhupada gives an example here. Death cannot be checked by anyone or from any source within the material world. And Prabhupada gives the example here. Hiranyakashipu wanted to be immortal and underwent a severe type of penance by which the whole universe trembled. Hiranyakashipu amassed a lot of power by performing severe austerities and asking for boons from Brahma. And Brahma himself approached Hiranyakashipu to dissuade Hiranyakashipu from such a severe type of penance. So Hiranyakashipu's plan was to become immortal. But Brahma wanted to dissuade him from this penance because it is futile to think that I will do tapasya so that I will become immortal. Material type of tapasya. Hiranyakashipu asked Brahma to award him the blessings of immortality. But Brahma said that he himself was subject to death, even in the top. So how could Brahma award Hiranyakashipu the benediction of immortality? Brahma himself is going to die. Then how can Brahma give anybody else immortality when he himself is subject to death? So the lesson to be learnt is everyone beginning from Brahma down to the most insignificant creature in this material world who has taken birth is subject to death.

But still Brahma lives for some 311 trillion years measured in our time scale. But even Brahma has to die. Brahma has to die. When that 311 trillion years is measured in our time scale. When that 311 trillion years is measured in our time scale. So 311 trillion years are over. So Srila Prabhupada concludes by telling wherever there is influence of eternal time there is this set of tribulations namely birth, disease, old age and death. And all of them are invincible. You cannot conquer any of these. Birth, birth means rebirth after death, rebirth. Then followed by further rebirth by a disease old age and death so this cannot be overcome by any material means any material methods so the only way is to work for spiritual realization of our spiritual nature eternal spiritual nature so that is what the scriptures advice and that's what exactly Vidura is going to advise Dhritarashtra I'll stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

---

### time, death, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=39
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whoever is under the influence of supreme Kala or eternal time must surrender his most dear life, and what to speak of other things such as wealth, honour, children, land and home."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 20th verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that everyone is subject to the influence of time (Kala) and must surrender everything at the time of death.

In this session, we are discussing two verses, the 20th and the 21st verses of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavata, one verse at a time. First, the 20th verse: Yena Chaivabhipannoyam Pranai Priyatamair Api Janasadyo Viyujeta Kimutanyair Dhanadibhi. Now, Vidura is explaining to Dhritarashtra that whoever is under the influence of supreme Kala or eternal time must surrender his most dear life, and what to speak of other things such as wealth, honour, children, land and home. Dhanadibhi. Dhana is wealth, and all the things that we think wealth can buy for us: some land, some home, some material possessions, assets, some status, material status in society. All these things, none of these things can be protected or saved when death comes, supreme Kala. Kala means synonymous with death. That time we surrender most dear life, and what to speak of other things. So Srila Prabhupada explains.

---
### death, detachment, examples
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=146
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.20
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually being ignorant of the fact that death always comes suddenly."
**Summary:** The speaker provides examples from Srila Prabhupada's teachings and historical events to illustrate the inevitability of death and the surrender of material possessions, emphasizing that death comes suddenly and affects everyone.

A great Indian scientist busy in the plan making business was suddenly called by invincible eternal time while going to attend a very important meeting of the planning commission, and he had to surrender his life, wife, children, house, land, wealth, etc. One of the examples Srila Prabhupada is giving, we always actually witness around us, that somebody they say suddenly died, actually being ignorant of the fact that death always comes suddenly. So somebody is, the example being given by Srila Prabhupada, a great Indian scientist busy in plan making business, he was in the planning commission and very important meeting he was going to attend, suddenly death came and he collapsed while going for the meeting. And death means surrender his life, and along with his life he has to surrender everything connected with that body in which there was life till he was having his life span, everything he has to surrender. Another example Srila Prabhupada gives, during the political upsurge in India and his division into Pakistan and Hindustan at the time of independence, political independence India got, so many rich and influential Indians had to surrender life, property and honour due to the influence of time. When they had to die, they had to give up everything that they had accumulated, they had amassed by being so called successful in business or in becoming rich, everything they had to surrender. And there are hundreds and thousands of examples like that all over the world, all over the universe, which are all effects of the influence of time.

---
### time, dissolution, devastation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=289
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no powerful living being within the universe who can overcome the influence of time."
**Summary:** The speaker expands on the influence of time, explaining that even demigods (devatas) must relinquish their positions and possessions during the partial dissolution of the universe, and that no one can overcome the influence of time.

It's not just the earth people, it's not just the earth planet, it's not just the human beings, there are even devatas. When the partial dissolution of the universe happens, all the devatas, they have to give up their positions, they have to give up their life, they have to give up their wealth, they have to give up their power, everything they have to give up. The influence of time, therefore the conclusion is that there is no powerful living being within the universe who can overcome the influence of time, Kala. If we are conscious of Kala, we realize that the influence of time is insurmountable, we cannot overcome the influence of time, whatever position one might be in, even in the upper planets, including Brahma's position in the topmost planet. Many poets have written verses lamenting the influence of time, many devastations have taken place over the universes due to the influence of time. See, when devastation takes place, everything is destroyed. Of course, when there is partial dissolution of the universe, the planets are not exactly destroyed, but there is a flood, and water covers every planet in the middle and lower planetary systems. Everyone in the middle and lower planetary systems has to quit his body, and the planet itself is covered by water, the water of devastation.

---
### time, suffering, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=493
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So many things come and go in which we have no hand, but we have to suffer or tolerate them without remedial measure; that is the result of time."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the 21st verse, highlighting the effects of time in daily life and introducing Dhritarashtra's current situation.

Even in our daily life, so many things come and go in which we have no hand, but we have to suffer or tolerate them without remedial measure; that is the result of time, Kala. That Kala is being described by Vidura as insurmountable. 21st verse: pitra bhratra suhratputra hataste vigatam vayam aatmacha jarayagrastaha paragyeham upasase. What is Dhritarashtra's actual situation now that Vidura is telling him: get out of the place. Why? So some of the reasons are stated in this verse.

---
### karma, suffering, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=525
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are carrying a load of karma with us when we take birth, and Kala will decide in this particular body, during our life span, which of those reactions which are not yet fructified in our heart, in our subtle body, which of those will actually give us the result."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Dhritarashtra's situation, explaining that Vidura is reminding him of his precarious condition influenced by cruel time and the unfolding of karma.

Vidura is telling: your father, brother, well wishers and sons are all dead and passed away. You yourself have expended the major portion of your life. Your body is now overtaken by invalidity, and you are living in the home of another. So these are all the facts which Vidura is bringing to the notice of Dhritarashtra. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says the king is reminded of his precarious condition influenced by cruel time. What does Kala do? It unfolds our karma. We are carrying a load of karma with us when we take birth, and Kala will decide in this particular body, during our life span, which of those reactions which are not yet fructified in our heart, in our subtle body, which of those will actually give us the result. It could be either enjoyment or suffering. Of course, enjoyment, there is no real enjoyment, there is no real happiness, it is illusory happiness, but even if somebody settles for illusory happiness, there is also the suffering due to the bad reactions, the sinful reactions. So all this will unfold, that is what is called as the influence of cruel time. And Dhritarashtra by his past experience, he should have been more influenced and intelligent to see what was going to happen to his own life. We think that I am not going to die, though I see everyone else around me either as already dead or they are going to die, but I think I am not going to die. Each person thinks like that. So the warning given by Vidura is explained by Prabhupada.

---
### loss, enmity, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=673
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Despite all these reverses, he thought that you prolong his life more and more, as long as I live, let me live comfortably."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts the many losses Dhritarashtra has suffered, including the deaths of his father, brother, sons, and well-wishers, and points out that he is now living at the mercy of his nephew, Yudhishthira, whom he and his son Duryodhana considered an enemy.

That Dhritarashtra's father, Vichitravirya, died long ago when he and his younger brothers were all little children, and it was due to the care and kindness of Bhishma Deva that they were properly brought up. Bhishma Deva was the elder brother of Vichitravirya, so Bhishma Deva took responsibility for raising these small children when their father died untimely. Then again his brother, Dhritarashtra's brother Pandu, died also. Pandu died untimely. Then in the battlefield of Kurukshetra, his 100 sons and all his grandsons died. So Dhritarashtra lost all his sons, he had 100 sons. See his ill-fated destiny, that not even one son survived that battle of Kurukshetra, all of them died, and his grandsons also, they also died. And even his other well-wishers like Bhishma Deva, Dronacharya, Karna, many other kings and friends, all of them died in the battle of Kurukshetra. It is told elsewhere that only the Pandavas were specially protected by Krishna, they survived that ghastly war, the battle of Kurukshetra, everybody else died. So Dhritarashtra lost all men and money, and now he was living at the mercy of his nephew whom he had put into troubles of various types. With whom is Dhritarashtra living? With his enemy. Now Yudhishthira did not consider Dhritarashtra or Duryodhana as his enemy, but Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana were always eyeing the Pandavas as their enemy. Why? Because Pandavas were eligible, and it was their rightful claim to the kingdom of Hastinapura, and Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana had to give that kingdom to Yudhishthira when Yudhishthira grew up, became a major adult, but Dhritarashtra did not do that, and Duryodhana was always plotting and planning to kill Yudhishthira, and that way Duryodhana developed enmity, Dhritarashtra developed enmity towards Yudhishthira. So now where is Dhritarashtra living? In the house of his enemy. Is it a very honorable life, living in the house of the enemy is not at all honorable. Despite all these reverses, he thought that you prolong his life more and more, as long as I live, let me live comfortably. That was Dhritarashtra's plan.

---
### protection, duty, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TF8xQ8XiaUE?t=860
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "No friend, no children, no father, no brother, no state, and no one else can protect a person who is not protected by the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing that true protection comes from the Supreme Lord and that one should seek His protection, as the human form of life is meant for this purpose. He mentions that Vidura is warning Dhritarashtra of his precarious conditions, which will be further elaborated in the following verses.

Vidura wanted to point out to Dhritarashtra that everyone has to protect himself by his action and the grace of the Lord. There is a suitable way of protecting oneself by Krishna's mercy and your spiritual activities, only that can protect you. One has to execute his duty faithfully depending on the Supreme Lord for the result. Result is in the hands of the Lord. No friend, no children, no father, no brother, no state, and no one else can protect a person who is not protected by the Supreme Lord. So the only protector is the Supreme Lord and nobody else. One should therefore seek the protection of the Supreme Lord, for the human form of life is meant for seeking this particular type of protection. So Dhritarashtra was being warned of his precarious conditions more and more by the following words. In the next few verses, we will hear more about Dhritarashtra's precarious condition being warned by Vidura. I will stop here and thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Om Agyana Timiran Dhasya Gyananj Nishalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. In this session we will be discussing from two verses, verse number 22 and 23 of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam.

---
### old age, death, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=69
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.22
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura is actually alerting Dhritarashtra."
**Summary:** The speaker explains verse 22, where Vidura points out Dhritarashtra's failing health as a sign of approaching death, urging him to take action.

This chapter is entitled Dhritarashtra Quits Home. The verse number 22: Andhapuraiva Vadhiro Mandaprajnyascha Sampratam Vishirna Danto Mandagni Saraga Kapham Udbhahan. The meaning of this verse is: You have been blind from your very birth and recently you have become hard of hearing. Your memory is shortened and your intelligence is disturbed. Your teeth are loose, your liver is defective, and you are coughing up mucus. These are words spoken by Vidura to Dhritarashtra, and Vidura is pointing out that the symptoms of invalidity due to old age are very much prominent in the person of Dhritarashtra, which means that it is notice of death, which is fast approaching. So what are you doing about Dhritarashtra facing death? So Vidura is actually alerting Dhritarashtra.

---
### material attachment, illusion, impermanence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is the power of illusion in this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining how Dhritarashtra's symptoms are warnings of death, and how foolish people cling to temporary material possessions and relationships, forgetting the impermanence of the body and the cycle of birth and death.

So in the Purport Shila Prabhupada explains the symptoms of old age, which had already developed in Dhritarashtra, were all one after another pointed out to him as warning that death was nearing very quickly, and still he was foolishly carefree about his future. The signs pointed out by Vidura in the body of Dhritarashtra were signs of Apakshaya, or dwindling of the material body before the last stroke of death. The body is born, it develops, stays, creates other organs, other bodies dwindles and then vanishes. This is called Shadvikara, six different changes that take place in the body from birth to death. So body is born, body is going to die, but in between four stages: body develops, then it stays for some time, produces by-products and dwindles. So these are described in the Shadvikara. So these are described in the scriptures as Shadvikara six changes that happen in the body irrespective of whether it is the body of a small insignificant creature, or the body of a human being, or the body of an animal or a bird, or a demigod, these six changes take place. But foolish men want to make a permanent settlement of the perishable body and think that their estate, their children, their society, country, etc., will give them protection. With such foolish ideas, they become overtaken by such temporary engagements and forget altogether that they must give up this temporary body and take a new one, again to arrange for another term of society, friendship and love, again to perish ultimately. This is the power of illusion in this material world.

---
### spiritual identity, false gurus, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=294
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We are spirit soul, we belong to Krishna, we have eternal life."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how the desire for material enjoyment leads to illusion, causing people to forget their true spiritual identity and become entangled in temporary pursuits. He contrasts genuine saints with 'show bottle sadhus' who cater to sense gratification.

Because we have got a deep-rooted desire for material enjoyment, we have hope that we can make it in this material world by some adjustment, some arrangement, some wealth, some friendship, some so-called love of near and dear ones, but actually none of these can really save us ultimately from death. Everything is taken away, everything material is taken away from us at the time of death. They forget their permanent identity. What is our permanent identity? We are spirit soul, we belong to Krishna, we have eternal life. We belong to Krishna, we have eternal relationship of love with Krishna. That we forget and become foolishly active for impermanent occupations, forgetting altogether their prime duty. Therefore, saints and sages like Vidura approach such foolish men to awaken them to their real situation, but they take such sadhus and saints as parasites of society, and almost all of them refuse to hear the words of such sadhus and saints, although they welcome show bottle sadhus and so-called saints who can satisfy their senses.

---
### genuine sadhu, truth, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=395
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A genuine sadhu will never promise any sense gratification in exchange for some spiritual life."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why people are often cheated by false gurus, because they offer sense gratification. He emphasizes that a genuine sadhu speaks the truth and does not offer material rewards for spiritual practice, as Vidura did with Dhritarashtra.

Sometimes we ask, why do people get cheated by bogus sadhus? Because these bogus sadhus know how to cheat people by satisfying their senses. The same business is going on even otherwise without any saintly person, so-called saintly person, trying to satisfy their senses. People are simply busy with material enjoyment, and for the sake of material enjoyment, they are seeking satisfaction of their senses. So these show bottle sadhus who somehow give some license for sense gratification in the name of so-called religious practices. A genuine sadhu will never promise any sense gratification in exchange for some spiritual life, never. Vidura was not a sadhu to satisfy the ill-gotten sentiment of Dhritarashtra. He is correctly pointing out the real situation of life and how one can save oneself from such catastrophes.

---
### harsh words, truth, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=475
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A sadhu should never flatter kings or rich men to live comfortably at their cost."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces verse 23, where Vidura uses strong language to shake Dhritarashtra out of his illusion, comparing him to a household dog dependent on Bhima. He explains that a true sadhu speaks the naked truth, even if harsh, to awaken people to the precariousness of material existence.

The 23rd verse: Aho mahiyasi jantor Jivitasha Yatha bhavan Bhima pavarjitam Pindam adatte Grihapalavat. These are very strong words spoken by Vidura to shake up Dhritarashtra out of his illusory situation. The meaning of this 23rd verse is: Alas, how powerful are the hopes of a living being to continue his life. Verily, you are living just like a household dog and are eating remnants of food given by Bhima. These are very strong words spoken by Vidura. So the purport Shila Prabhupada explains why is Vidura speaking so harshly. Prabhupada says, a sadhu should never flatter kings or rich men to live comfortably at their cost. A sadhu is to speak to the householders about the naked truth of life so that they may come to their senses about the precarious life in material existence. This is a genuine sadhu.

---
### attachment, humiliation, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=549
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhima is particularly pointed out because he killed these two pet sons."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why Vidura's words are so harsh, highlighting Dhritarashtra's attachment to comfort despite his humiliation and dependence on the Pandavas, especially Bhima, who killed his sons. He also explains the context of Bhima's anger and Yudhishthira's forbearance.

Dhritarashtra is a typical example of an attached old man in household life. He had become a pauper in the true sense, yet he wanted to live comfortably in the house of the Pandavas of whom Bhima especially is mentioned here. Why is Bhima mentioned especially among the five Pandavas? Because personally it was Bhima who killed two prominent sons of Dhritarashtra, namely Duryodhana and Dushyasana. These two sons were very much dear to Dhritarashtra for their notorious and nefarious activities. Bhima is particularly pointed out because he killed these two pet sons. Actually Bhima killed not only these two pet sons, Bhima killed each of the 100 sons of Dhritarashtra. Bhima was angry because Ghervravas took undue advantage of Yudhishthira's righteous, upright behavior, even in trying circumstances in gambling match, which was rigged. And the Pandavas were cheated just because Yudhishthira agreed to follow the rules of the game, even though he was cheated, Duryodhana and Dushasana took undue advantage of Yudhishthira's good conduct, righteous conduct, and simply insulted Draupadi, made Pandavas slaves, and practically insulted all of them, so Bhima was very angry at this particular time, but he restrained himself because Yudhishthira did not want any of the Pandavas to protest. Now Yudhishthira is actually a pure devotee, so such pure devotees, they depend on Krishna under all circumstances.

---
### devotion, Krishna's protection, humiliation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=699
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura therefore was astonished how powerful is the urge to continue life this sense of continuing one's life indicates that a living being is eternally a living entity and does not want to change his bodily habitation."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that pure devotees depend on Krishna's will, even in suffering, and that Krishna ultimately protected the Pandavas and ensured Yudhishthira's victory. He reiterates that Dhritarashtra's desire to live comfortably despite humiliation astonished Vidura.

If it is the Lord's will that we have to undergo suffering, we will suffer, but we will never give up our righteous conduct in order to avoid some suffering, no. The Lord has got his own higher reason why he wants devotees to suffer in certain circumstances. There is a higher plan, and devotees have this full faith in the Supreme Lord, and as it turns out, that in the battle of Kurukshetra, against all odds the Pandavas were protected by Krishna and the Kauravas were completely destroyed in that battle because it is Krishna's will that the Pandavas must win this battle and Yudhishthira must be enthroned as the emperor of the world against all odds so therefore it is specifically mentioned here that Bhima who was angry at the Kauravas he got an opportunity to properly fight in this battle and personally kill each of the 100 sons of Dhritarashtra why was Dhritarashtra living there at the house of the Pandavas because he wanted to continue his life comfortably even at the risk of all humiliation. Vidura therefore was astonished how powerful is the urge to continue life this sense of continuing one's life indicates that a living being is eternally a living entity and does not want to change his bodily habitation.

---
### self-realization, human form of life, Vidura's task
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kMnHa7LYvdc?t=813
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The human body is awarded after many many births and deaths as a chance for self-realization to go back home back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that the human body is a rare opportunity for self-realization and returning to Godhead, but people like Dhritarashtra waste it by seeking material comfort. He emphasizes Vidura's responsibility to awaken people to this truth.

The foolish man does not know that a particular term of imprisonment, a particular term of bodily existence is awarded to him to undergo a total term of imprisonment and the human body is awarded after many many births and deaths as a chance for self-realization to go back home back to Godhead but persons like Dhritarashtra try to make plans to live here in this material world comfortably they do not see things as they are Dhritarashtra is blind and continues to hope to live comfortably in the midst of all kinds of reverses of life but as all that is celebrated in the event there may be some collective PTSD goal especially in the human form of life so that is the responsible task of Vidura I'll stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.24 - 25 | 8-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### degradation, atrocities, dignity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nwSUMw4yGqc?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.24
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura is pointing out to Dhritarashtra how Dhritarashtra had done so much of atrocities, committed so many atrocities against the Pandavas and now living in their house is a degraded life."
**Summary:** Vidura criticizes Dhritarashtra for his past atrocities against the Pandavas and his current degraded state of living off their charity.

In this session, we will be discussing from two verses, the 24th and the 25th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 24th verse: Agnir Nisrishto Dattascha Garodarascha Dushitaha, Hritham Kshetram Dhanam Yesham Tad Dattair Asubhi Kiyat. The meaning of this verse is, there is no need to live a degraded life and subsist on the charity of those whom you tried to kill by arson and poisoning. You also insulted one of their wives and usurped their kingdom and wealth. Vidura is pointing out to Dhritarashtra how Dhritarashtra had done so much of atrocities, committed so many atrocities against the Pandavas and now living in their house is a degraded life. This is a degraded life for Dhritarashtra.

---
### varnashrama, self-realization, degradation
