**Verse References:** SB 1.13.24
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To stick to family life to the end of one's human life is the grossest type of degradation and there is an absolute need for the Viduras to educate such Dhritarashtras even at the present moment."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that the Varnashrama system sets aside time for self-realization, contrasting this with Dhritarashtra's degraded state of accepting charity from enemies, and highlighting the need for guidance even today.

So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains some very important points. The system of Varnashrama religion sets aside a part of one's life completely for the purpose of self-realization and attainment of salvation in the human form of life. This is how the human life is. The human life is distinguished from animal life, that there is a special provision that you can set aside a part of your life exclusively for self-realization, which is the main goal of the human form of life and which is only possible in the human form of life. That is a routine division of life. But persons like Dhritarashtra, even at their very ripened age, want to stay home, even in a degraded condition of accepting charity from enemies. Vidura wanted to point this out and impressed upon him that it was better to die like his sons than accept such humiliating charity. It was not a dignified way that Dhritarashtra was living in the house of the Pandavas. So Vidura is pointing out if you have any consideration for living a dignified way you should immediately leave this place. This is not a suitable place for you. Five thousand years ago there was one Dhritarashtra but at the present moment there are Dhritarashtras in every home. People's life has become very degraded in this present time because they live in spite of all kinds of difficulties and dangers. They don't make any preparation for facing death like a proper human being.

---
### degradation, materialism, human life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nwSUMw4yGqc?t=247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is very much relevant now especially in modern times it's necessary for saintly persons to wake up the materialistic persons who are lost engrossed in sense gratification and missing the opportunity of the valuable human form of life."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the relevance of Dhritarashtra's situation in modern times, particularly among politicians and those engrossed in sense gratification, stressing the need for saintly persons to awaken them to the value of human life.

So that's why Prabhupada says now it's not just one rare Dhritarashtra who is living a degraded condition of life. There are many many many such people living a very degraded condition of life and Prabhupada specially mentions here politicians especially do not retire from political activities unless they're dragged by the cruel hand of death or killed by some opposing element. We see this all around us. This is the reality. To stick to family life to the end of one's human life is the grossest type of degradation and there is an absolute need for the Viduras to educate such Dhritarashtras even at the present moment. So don't take this episode as some historical incident which is not relevant now. It is very much relevant now especially in modern times it's necessary for saintly persons to wake up the materialistic persons who are lost engrossed in sense gratification and missing the opportunity of the valuable human form of life.

---
### death, material body, kripana, brahmana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nwSUMw4yGqc?t=345
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.25
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Kripana or mizerly man has no estimation of his material body but the Brahmana has a true estimation of himself and material body."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the 25th verse, highlighting the inevitable deterioration of the body and Prabhupada's explanation of the two classes of men: the Kripana (miserly) and the Brahmana, based on their understanding of the material body.

The meaning of this 25th verse is despite your unwillingness to die and your desire to live even at the cost of honor and prestige your miserly body will certainly dwindle and deteriorate like an old garment. Somebody may think that I will make some adjustment and somehow live but nature does not allow you to live in the same way you have been living. Your body deteriorates and it will give you more and more trouble as the body ages. It will only deteriorate further. That's the law of nature. So in the purport, Prabhupada says the words Kripanasya Jiji Vishoho are significant, two words Kripana and Jiji Vishoho. So Prabhupada explains there are two classes of men this is described in the scriptures in the Upanishads. This is described. That are the... One is called Kripana and the other is called Brahmana. The Kripana or mizerly man has no estimation of his material body but the Brahmana has a true estimation of himself and material body. So Kripana and Brahmana. Kripana does not know the value of this human form of body. The Brahmana knows the value.

---
### sense gratification, death, human life, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nwSUMw4yGqc?t=464
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Human life is meant for finishing all kinds of miseries of material existence. It's only in the human form of life that this is possible."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the difference between the Kripana and Brahmana, emphasizing the Kripana's desire for sense gratification even in old age, and Vidura's attempt to awaken Dhritarashtra to prepare for death, highlighting the unique opportunity of human life to end material miseries.

The Kripana, having a wrong estimation of his material body, wants to enjoy sense gratification with his utmost strength. And even in old age, he wants to become a young man by medical treatment or otherwise. We see this all around us, that old people somehow want to maintain themselves for sense gratification, living a life of sense gratification. But that's not possible. That's really not possible. Dhritarashtra is addressed herein as a Kripana because without any estimation of his material body, he wants to live at any cost, somehow live a life of sense gratification. Vidura is trying to open his eyes to see that he cannot live more than his term and that he must prepare for death. The symptoms are indicative of fast approaching death and Dhritarashtra must prepare to face death like a human being and not miss the opportunity like an animal. Since death is inevitable, why should he accept such a humiliating position for living? It is better to take the right path even at the risk of death. Human life is meant for finishing all kinds of miseries of material existence. It's only in the human form of life that this is possible. It's not possible in animal form of life, bird form of life, reptile form of life. The form of life is not possible. The form of life of lower creatures, this elevated consciousness does not exist.

---
### consciousness, human life, spiritual assets, material assets
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nwSUMw4yGqc?t=581
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only spiritual assets are eternally with us. We can carry forward and utilize it always. Material assets are not going to remain with us. They will be completely lost at the time of death."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the consciousness of lower life forms with the developed consciousness of human beings, which allows for preparing for death and ending material miseries, emphasizing the importance of regulating life to achieve the goal of returning to Godhead and highlighting the eternal nature of spiritual assets versus the impermanence of material assets.

There is consciousness in every form of life. But that consciousness is mainly simply used for eating, sleeping, mating, defending, just maintaining the body as far as possible, subsisting in this material existence. But human life, we have got developed consciousness. For what? To realize that I can prepare for death to put an end to all kinds of miseries of material existence. Life should be so regulated that one can achieve the desired goal of going back home, back to Godhead. Dhritarashtra, due to his wrong conception of life, had already spoiled 80% of his achieved energy. So it behooved him to utilize the remaining days of his miserly life for the ultimate good. Such a life is called miserly because one cannot properly utilize the assets of the human form of life. One who does not know the value of this body will waste this human energy simply for sense gratification. And at the end, left with nothing, which is like an asset which one can utilize in the future, can carry forward to the next life. So only spiritual assets are eternally with us. We can carry forward and utilize it always. Material assets are not going to remain with us. They will be completely lost at the time of death.

---
### self-realization, ignorance, spiritual practice, chanting, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nwSUMw4yGqc?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Simply by vibrating the tongue and hearing the sound of Hare Krishna, you can prepare for facing death in such a way that you keep on vibrating your tongue, chanting and hearing Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare and you actually are able to go back to Godhead at the end of this life."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that meeting a self-realized soul like Vidura can dispel ignorance and provide instructions on preparing for death through spiritual culture, particularly simplified in this age by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu through the chanting of the Hare Krishna mantra, which transforms consciousness and allows one to return to Godhead.

Only by good luck does such a miserly life, miserly man, meet a self-realized soul like Vidura. And by his instruction, by Vidura's instruction, Dhritarashtra can get rid of the nescience of material existence. What is nescience? Nescience means the ignorance, the darkness in which one is living. Material existence means there is this ignorance about who am I? What is my actual goal of life? How to solve all the problems of material existence once and for all? Make a permanent solution for these problems, not some temporary patchwork solutions. No. So that is possible by meeting a self-realized soul like Vidura and taking instructions from him how to actually prepare oneself, especially towards the end of one's life, how to prepare for facing death by spiritual culture, by spiritual practice, by spiritual enlightenment. And especially in this age, it has been simplified to the maximum extent possible by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, who very mercifully is pointing out that you can very easily vibrate your tongue in any condition. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare and simply by vibrating the tongue and chanting this Hare Krishna mantra and also hearing this mantra, so then the consciousness becomes transformed into spiritual consciousness, Krishna consciousness, and thereby preparing oneself to face death in touch with Krishnanama, the holy name, the merciful incarnation of the Supreme Lord Krishna in the form of his holy name. Simply by vibrating the tongue and hearing the sound of Hare Krishna, you can prepare for facing death in such a way that you keep on vibrating your tongue, chanting and hearing Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare and you actually are able to go back to Godhead at the end of this life. Simply by doing this and nothing more. That is Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's mercy. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### renunciation, detachment, death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fRmqWBXt3n0?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He is called undisturbed, Dheera, who goes to an unknown remote place and, freed from all obligations, quits his material body when it has become useless."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 26th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that it describes the condition of Dhritarashtra and Vidura's advice on how to face death by renouncing material attachments and going to a remote place.

In this session, we will be discussing two verses, the 26th and the 27th verses of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. The 26th verse: Gata Swartham Imam Deham Virakto Mukta Bandhanaha Avignyata Gatir Jahyat Savay Dheera Udah Hruthaha. So far, Vidura has been describing the condition of Dhritarashtra, which is a miserable condition, a degraded life he is leading, not preparing himself for death. Now he is giving a practical solution: what should Dhritarashtra do? He has already told him to quit home, leave home immediately, and do what? That is going to come in the next few verses. So this 26th verse, the meaning is he is called undisturbed, Dheera, who goes to an unknown remote place and, freed from all obligations, quits his material body when it has become useless.

---
### devotional service, human life, renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fRmqWBXt3n0?t=72
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who has spoiled his life without such cultural activities is advised to leave home without knowledge of friends and relatives, and being thus freed from all obligations of family, society, country, etc., give up the body at some unknown destination so that others may not know where and how he has met his death."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport on the verse, emphasizing that the human body is meant for cultivating devotion and service to the Lord. He discusses Narottam Das Thakur's song about wasting life and the recommendation for those who haven't engaged in devotional service to leave home and give up their body in an unknown place.

So far, Vidura has been describing the condition of the Trashtra, which is a miserable condition, a degraded life is leading, not preparing himself for death. Now he is giving a practical solution, what should the Trashtra do. He has already told him quit home, leave home immediately and do what, that is going to come in the next few verses. So this 26th verse the meaning is he is called undisturbed, undisturbed means Dheera, who goes to an unknown remote place and freed from all obligations, quits his material body when it has become useless. So what is the meaning of this, going to a remote unknown place and quitting the material body when one has, when the body has become useless, that is explained by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. Narottam Das Thakur, a great devotee and acharya of the Gaudiya Vaishnava sect, has sung, Narottam Das Thakur, my lord, I have simply wasted my life, having obtained the human body, I have neglected to worship your lordship, and therefore I have willingly drunk poison. This is a part of a song written by Srila Narottam Das Thakur, one of the previous acharyas. So Srila Prabhupada explains, in other words, the human body is especially meant for cultivating knowledge of devotion and service to the Lord, without which life becomes full of anxieties and miserable conditions. Therefore, one who has spoiled his life without such cultural activities is advised to leave home without knowledge of friends and relatives, and being thus freed from all obligations of family, society, country, etc., give up the body at some unknown destination so that others may not know where and how he has met his destiny, where and how he has met his death.

---
### devotional service, death, renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fRmqWBXt3n0?t=204
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Quit home is possible only for a dhira. Dhira means undisturbed. This is however the path of renunciation based on a frustrated life is for those who are unable to take to devotional service and are experiencing the miserable conditions especially in old age."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that engaging in devotional service before death is the best remedy. He clarifies that leaving home is for those who haven't practiced devotional service and are experiencing miserable conditions, and that 'dhira' means undisturbed, even in the face of provocation.

This is the recommendation for somebody who has not taken up devotional service, to fully engage in devotional service at the time of old age, before death comes, is the best remedy for facing death and achieving perfection of the human form of life. But if somebody, let us say, is in the middle of death, has not practiced, not prepared that way, then better to quit home without knowledge of friends and relatives, in such a way that one may give up the body in some unknown place and without the knowledge of anyone else. So Srila Prabhupada further explains, dhira means one who is not disturbed even when there is sufficient provocation. One cannot give up a comfortable family life due to his affectionate relationship with wife and children, self-realization is obstructed by such undue affection for family, and if anyone is at all able to forget such a relation, he is called undisturbed or dhira, to be able to forget that bond of affection for family members, and thereby Quit home is possible only for a dhira. Dhira means undisturbed. This is however the path of renunciation based on a frustrated life is for those who are unable to take to devotional service and are experiencing the miserable conditions especially in old age.

---
### devotional service, association, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fRmqWBXt3n0?t=323
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sincere surrender unto the lotus feet of the Lord is possible by awakening the transcendental sense of service."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's explanation, stating that stabilization in renunciation requires association with bona fide saints and engagement in devotional service. He highlights the importance of awakening the transcendental sense of service through association with pure devotees and emphasizes Dhritarashtra's fortune in having Vidura's association.

But Srila Prabhupada says this is a recommendation of accepting the path of renunciation based on frustrated life. But stabilization of such renunciation is possible only by association with bona fide saints and self-realized souls by which one can be engaged in the loving devotional service of the Lord. So what is Prabhupada saying here? That you may accept the path of renunciation giving up the miserable condition of material existence based on experiencing frustration living such a miserable life. But you cannot become stable and fixed up in that renounced life simply by going away from home and remaining undisturbed. No. You have to positively engage in pure devotional service under the guidance of pure devotees like Vidura. Sincere surrender unto the lotus feet of the Lord is possible by awakening the transcendental sense of service. This is called awakening of the divine sense of service. This is made possible by association with pure devotees of the Lord. Sadhu Sangha. Dhritarashtra was lucky enough to have a brother whose very association was a source of liberation for his frustrated life. So Dhritarashtra was very very fortunate because Vidura specially came to the palace of Hastinapura to deliver Vidura, to deliver Dhritarashtra. In the twenty-seventh verse further instructions are being given by Vidura to Dhritarashtra.

### instruction, detachment, time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aMGk2KrbQoE?t=53
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.28
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Please therefore leave for the north immediately, without letting your relatives know, for soon that time will approach which will diminish the good qualities of men."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the context of the lecture, focusing on Vidura's instructions to Dhritarashtra to leave home due to the approaching age of Kali and its deteriorating effects on human qualities.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 28th verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We are discussing Dhritarashtra quitting home on the instruction of Vidura. Vidura has come to the palace of Yudhishthira in order to instruct Dhritarashtra. So Vidura is speaking to Dhritarashtra. This 28th verse also is spoken by Vidura to Dhritarashtra, continuing his instructions. Atho Deechim Disham Yathu Svair Adnyata Gathir Bhavan Itorvak Prayashak Kalah. Pumsam Gunavikar Shanah. The meaning of this 28th verse is as follows: Please therefore leave for the north immediately. Please therefore leave for the north immediately, without letting your relatives know, for soon that time will approach which will diminish the good qualities of men. So Srila Prabhupada explains all these terms.

---
### Kali Yuga, degradation, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aMGk2KrbQoE?t=126
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Dhritarashtra was advised to leave home before Kali Yuga approached because the atmosphere which is created by Vidura, his valuable instructions on the facts of life, would fade away due to the influence of the age which was fast approaching."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's explanation of the verse, emphasizing the degradation caused by material association and the specific dangers of Kali Yuga, where good qualities deteriorate to the lowest standard. Dhritarashtra was advised to leave before the full onset of Kali Yuga.

What is the meaning of leave for the north, and what is the time being referred to here? So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says one can compensate for a life of frustration by becoming a Dheera, or leaving home for good without communicating with relatives. And Vidura advised his eldest brother to adopt this way without delay because very quickly the age of Kali was approaching. The age referred to here is the age of Kali, which is fast approaching. Dwapar Yuga is going to come to an end, and Kali Yuga is going to begin. And what is the characteristic of the age of Kali? And that is told in the verse. The verse it said, Pumsam Gunavikar Shanah, that which will diminish the good qualities of men, that which will deteriorate the good qualities of people in general. So Srila Prabhupada says regarding this age of Kali fast approaching, Srila Prabhupada says a conditioned soul is already degraded by the material association, whether Satya Yuga or Dwapar Yuga, or Treta Yuga or Kali Yuga, all Yugas. This material association is already degrading our consciousness by the power of illusion, the power of the three Gunas. And still in the Kali Yuga, the good qualities of a man will deteriorate to the lowest standard. Satya Yuga was the best age, and then little degradation when Treta Yuga set in, and even more little degradation when Dwapar Yuga set in, but Kali Yuga is the lowest. The maximum deterioration happens in Kali Yuga. Dhritarashtra was advised to leave home before Kali Yuga approached because the atmosphere which is created by Vidura, his valuable instructions on the facts of life, would fade away due to the influence of the age which was fast approaching.

---
### Kali Yuga, aim of life, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aMGk2KrbQoE?t=287
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "People are so busy with sense gratification that they completely forget about self-realization."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the symptoms of Kali Yuga, including decreased lifespan, loss of memory and finer sentiments, and the widespread forgetting of the true aim of life, which is self-realization, due to excessive focus on sense gratification.

Some symptoms of Kali Yuga are mentioned by Srila Prabhupada in another purport, in the beginning of the Bhagavatam, first canto first chapter. Srila Prabhupada says about the age of Kali, the age of Kali is very dangerous for the human being. Human life is simply meant for self realization, but due to this dangerous age, men have completely forgotten the aim of life. It's characteristic of Kali Yuga. We see everywhere around us, people do not know what is the aim of life. Most people lead a life without any particular aim. Of course, there is always a default aim, that is sense enjoyment, sense gratification. That's common for all living beings throughout the creation, at all times, in all places, in all circumstances. That's common, sense enjoyment. But in Kali Yuga, even this sense enjoyment, they are not cautious to exercise certain restrained. Unrestricted sense gratification is the norm in Kali Yuga. So it is been explained in the scriptures, in this age of Kali, the life span will gradually decrease, people will gradually lose their memory, they will lose their finer sentiments, they will lose their strength, and they will lose their better qualities. A list of the anomalies of this age is given in the 12th canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam. And so this age is very difficult for those who want to utilize this life for self-realization. The scriptures say there is only one aim of the human form of life, that is self-realization. But in general, people in the material world are inclined to sense gratification, and they don't take the aim of life as self-realization very seriously, especially in the age of Kali. They almost completely forget that the aim of life is self-realization. So people are so busy with sense gratification that they completely forget about self-realization. Out of madness, they frankly say there is no need for self-realization because they do not realize that this brief life is but a moment on our great journey towards self-realization.

---
### education, self-realization, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aMGk2KrbQoE?t=474
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The whole system of education in Kali Yuga is geared to sense gratification."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the current education system, geared towards sense gratification, with the traditional gurukula system, which emphasized self-realization. He advises learned individuals to be cautious of Kali Yuga and to seek guidance from saintly persons and scriptures like the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam to cross the ocean of material existence.

Generally self-realization takes many lifetimes. So as we progress in self-realization, life after life, life after life, life after life, then each duration of life, life in a particular body, is a brief moment. That's all. Made little progress, some more progress, some more progress, like that. The whole system of education in Kali Yuga is geared to sense gratification. And if a learned man thinks it over, he sees that the children of this age are being intentionally sent to the slaughterhouses of so-called education. This is not the case in the former ages. Children were educated in a gurukula, and in the gurukula, the primary education that was given was that this human form of life is very precious. It should be utilized only for self-realization. And of course, there was also the teaching of how to earn one's livelihood by different prescribed methods. The brahmanas, the kshatriyas, the vaishyas, they used to undergo training in the gurukula. So they were trained in the prime necessity of human life, that is self-realization, and they were also trained in how to earn their livelihood. Both training was given. But in this age of Kali, there is no training about self-realization at all, even in the so-called schools and educational institutions. It's almost completely forgotten. Learned men therefore must be cautious of this age, and if they at all want to cross over the dangerous ocean of Kali, they must follow the footsteps of great saintly persons like the sages Naimisharanya. And they should accept, like the sages Naimisharanya accepted Suta Goswami, they should accept Suta Goswami or his bona fide representative as the captain of the ship. What is a ship? Like in an ocean, to cross an ocean you take help of a ship. Similarly, the ship for crossing the ocean of Kali is described here as the message of Lord Sri Krishna in the shape of Bhagavad Gita, Shreemad Bhagavatam. So the Bhagavatam suggests that hear the message of Krishna in the Shreemad Bhagavatam or the Bhagavad Gita, and thereby prepare oneself to cross this ocean of material existence or the ocean of the age of Kali.

---
### detachment, self-realization, stages of devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aMGk2KrbQoE?t=663
**Verse References:** BG 7.28
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Dhritarashtra is advised by Vibhira to at least become a Dheera."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's interpretation of Vidura's advice to Dhritarashtra, emphasizing the importance of detachment and self-realization. He outlines three stages: Dheera (the least one can do), Sanyasi, and Narottama (the best), explaining that even becoming a Dheera is a crucial step towards spiritual progress, especially in Kali Yuga. He concludes by summarizing Vidura's advice to Dhritarashtra, urging him to leave home and prepare himself for self-realization.

Now Srila Prabhupada explains that Vidura is giving specific instruction to Dhritarashtra considering Dhritarashtra's current position. What is his situation? So Srila Prabhupada explains the best is to become a Narottama, or a first class human being, depending completely on the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna. Now this is not possible for any ordinary man to become a Narottama, or a first class human being, a first class personality is not possible for a human being in which one completely depends on the Supreme Lord. Such a situation is not possible for an ordinary human being. Why is it not possible for an ordinary human being? It is stated in the Bhagavad Gita in the 7th chapter and 28th verse that a person who is completely relieved of all taints of sinful acts can alone depend on the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna, the personality of Godhead. Ye shantu antagatam papam, Jananam punyakarmanam, Tedvam dhamoha nirmukta, Bhajan temam dhridam vrataham. This is not possible for ordinary people. Dhritarashtra is advised by Vibhira to at least become a Dheera. Three stages: Dheera, a Sanyasi, and Narottama. So Dheera is the lowest, the least one can do to prepare oneself for death, for Self Realization. So Dhritarashtra advised Vibhira at least to become a Dheera in the beginning, if it were impossible for him to become a Sanyasi or a Narottama. Lowest is Dheera, better than that is Sanyasi, better than that, best is Narottama. Persistently endeavoring on the line of Self Realization helps a person to rise to the conditions of a Narottama from the stage of a Dheera. Gradually rise to the top most platform. The Dheera stage is attained after prolonged practice of the Yoga system, but by the grace of Vidura, one can attain this stage immediately simply by willing to adopt the means of the Dheera stage, which is the preparatory stage for Sanyas. The Sanyas stage is the preparatory stage of Paramahamsa or the first great devotee of the Lord, also called Narottama. So this is the advice of Vidura for Dhritarashtra. Consider Dhritarashtra's situation. He was completely entangled, so to pull him out, extricate him from the entanglement, at least leave home and go to a secluded place and prepare yourself by being undisturbed. So that is the advice Vidura is giving to Dhritarashtra. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xOQ_PT_-FRw?t=59
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of verse 29, focusing on how Vidura's instructions led Dhritarashtra to detachment and the path of liberation.

So far we have heard Vidura instructing Dhritarashtra. Now the result of such instructions given by Vidura will now be explained in this particular verse, 29th verse. Evam Raja Vidurena Anujena Prajna Chakshur Bodhita Aaja Meedha Chitva Sveshu Sneha Pashaan Dhridhimno Nishchakrama Bhratra Sandarshita Adhva. The meaning of this verse is, Das Maharaj Dhritarashtra, this kind of the family of Ajameedha, firmly convinced by introspective knowledge, Prajna, broke at once the strong network of familial affection by his resolute determination. Thus he immediately left home to set out on the path of liberation as directed by his younger brother Vidura. Vidurena Anujena Prajna Chakshur Bodhita Aaja Meedha Chitva Sveshu Sneha Pashaan Dhritarashtra. Vidurena Anujena Prajna Chakshur Bodhita Aaja Meedha Chitva Sveshu Sneha Pashaan Dhridhimno Nishchakrama Vidura. Vidurena, bran Supreme Guru Anujaena. In these words Anuja means the younger brother. So the strong words of Vidura had a very nice impact on the heart of Dhritarashtra. He was able to cut off all the strong familial ties and he became determined. He immediately left home. Vidura was not delivered. collect вжеi ki Bopamanna Varasi Tal. Immediately left home. And that is the wonderful effect of the association of a pure devotee Vidura.

### association, devotee, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xOQ_PT_-FRw?t=173
**Verse References:** BG 9.30, SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has said that even a moment's association with a pure devotee can help one achieve all perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of associating with pure devotees (sadhus), quoting Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and explaining that even a brief moment of association can lead to perfection.

So, Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the great preacher of the principles of Srimad Bhagavatam, has stressed the importance of association with sadhus or pure devotees of the Lord. This is explained in the Bhagavad Gita. Sadhu means a pure devotee. Not one with a long beard or one with a particular type of tilak or talak, or one with a particular type of robes. No, this is not sadhu. Sadhu means a pure devotee. So, sadhu is bhajate mam ananyabhak. One who is engaged in pure devotional service without even a little deviation. So, Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has said that even a moment's association with a pure devotee can help one achieve all perfection. What is the verse Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says? Sadhu Sangha Sadhu Sangha Sarva Shastra Koi. Lava Matra Sadhu Sanghe Sarva Siddhi Hoi. The meaning of this verse in the Chaitanya Charitamrita. This is said by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. The verdict of all revealed scriptures is that, by even a moment's association with a pure devotee, one can achieve all perfection. The verdict of all revealed scriptures is that, by even a moment's association with a pure devotee, one can achieve all perfection. Lava Matra. Lava is just one moment. Actually, Shri Lapa Prabhupada says, this Lava Matra Sadhu Sangha, a brief moment. What is the duration of that brief moment? According to astronomical calculation, Lava means one eleventh of a second. Lava means one eleventh of a second. It is less than one second. It is one eleventh of a second. So, so little association is very very valuable. So, so little association is very very valuable. There is one more verse.

### association, devotee, Srila Prabhupada, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xOQ_PT_-FRw?t=313
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without seeing him at that opportune moment, he said, we could have become a very great business magnate, but never would we have been able to walk the path of liberation and be engaged in the factual service of the Lord under the instructions of His Divine Grace, Srila Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes another verse from the Bhagavatam about the value of association with devotees and then discusses Srila Prabhupada's experience of meeting his spiritual master, emphasizing how that brief meeting transformed his life.

In the Bhagavatam, it is said... tulayama lave naapi na svaryam na punar bhavam... tulayama lave naapi na svaryam na punar bhavam... Bhagavat Sanghi Sanghasya... Bhagavat Sanghi Sanghasya... martyanaam kim utashishah... lava also the same duration of time is being used. The meaning of this Bhagavatam verse is the value of a moment's association with a devotee of the Lord cannot be compared even to the attainment of heavenly planets or liberation from matter and what to speak of worldly benedictions in the form of material prosperity which is for those who are meant for death. So it's very very very very valuable to have even a brief association with a pure devotee of the supreme law that is the that is the teaching of chetanimahabha. So Srila Prabhupada says about his own life in this purport he says we are not ashamed to admit this fact that we are not ashamed to admit this fact that we are not ashamed to admit this fact that that we experienced this particular teaching of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in our practical life. Srila Prabhupada experienced this. Were we not favoured by His Divine Grace, Sri Madbhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj, by our first meeting for a few minutes only, it would not have been possible for us to accept this mighty task of describing Sri Madbhagavatam in English. Srila Prabhupada, while writing this purport, in the first canto, he is recollecting his first meeting with his spiritual master, Sri Srimadbhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj. Without seeing him at that opportune moment, he said, we could have become a very great business magnate, but never would we have been able to walk the path of liberation and be engaged in the factual service of the Lord under the instructions of His Divine Grace, Srila Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur. So that meeting was a turning point in the life of Srila Prabhupada. Completely everything changed.

**Summary:** The speaker returns to the example of Dhritarashtra and Vidura, highlighting how Vidura's words, like those of a sadhu, can cut through material attachments and lead to self-realization.

Though, we should not disregard the fact that Srila Prabhupada was born to a pure devotee. His father was a pure devotee, Gaur Mohan Dey. He was a pure devotee. And Prabhupada was trained in devotional service right from his infancy by his father. That is explained in Prabhupada's biography. Nevertheless, what is the example? Here is another practical example by the action of Vidura's association with Dhritarashtra. What is the effect of Vidura coming to the palace and instructing Dhritarashtra? He just spoke some words, but those words pierced the heart of Dhritarashtra and made such an impact that Dhritarashtra was able to cut off all material attachments and immediately leave home and proceed towards the north for self-realization. So, it is also told that a sadhu's words are meant for cutting off the material bonds of attachment.

### sadhu, detachment, Krishna, dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xOQ_PT_-FRw?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For maintaining himself, a sadhu is fully dependent on Krishna and he is confident that Krishna will protect him under all circumstances and provide him with the basic needs."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the qualities of a true sadhu, emphasizing their detachment from material gain, dependence on Krishna, and purpose in enlightening householders.

And it is also told in the Shastra that a sadhu should not flatter. A sadhu should not flatter householders or people who are attached simply for the sake of getting some dakshina or some viksha. No. For maintaining himself, a sadhu is fully dependent on Krishna and he is confident that Krishna will protect him under all circumstances and provide him with the basic needs. So, why does a sadhu go begging? It is an excuse to enter a householder's garden, in order to enlighten the householder, like Vidura is doing. So, Dhritarashtra was tightly bound in a network of material affinities related to politics, economy and family attachment. And Dhritarashtra, he did everything in his power to achieve so-called success in his planned projects, because he could not lay a claim for ascending the throne, even though he was the eldest son of his father. He could not ascend the throne because he was blind by birth. But, he had the desire and when his younger brother Pandu, after ascending the throne, died untimely, immediately Dhritarashtra got the opportunity to sit on the throne, at least as a caretaker king. Nobody else was there. So, he was the caretaker king. Then, he started planning how I can retain this throne for myself and my eldest son, Duryodhana. So, he made so many plans. But, he was frustrated from the beginning to the end, so far as his material activities were concerned. He could not continue. But, when he was still on the throne, he lost the post of the emperor when, in the battle of Kurukshetra, all his sons were defeated and killed in the battle. And he himself completely lost everything. All his well-wishers and his position and his sons and his wealth, everything he lost. And yet, despite his life of failure, materially speaking, it was a life of failure, he achieved the greatest of all success in self-realization by the forceful instructions of a pure devotee of the Lord, who is the typical emblem of a sadhu. A pure devotee of the Lord. A pure devotee of the Lord. A pure devotee is a true sadhu. He is a true saintly person.

---
### compassion, association, detachment, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xOQ_PT_-FRw?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This material world is a world of great illusion, and only by such association of pure devotees or genuine saints can we actually cut off our attachments."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of associating with sadhus to cut off material attachments and progress on the path of self-realization, drawing parallels with Shukadeva Goswami's compassion and Dhritarashtra's detachment.

His words can penetrate the heart through the layers of ignorance and instantly cleanse the heart. That's why as a sadhu, he goes door to door and speaks. In the Bhagavatam also, it is said, Shukadeva Goswami, Suta Goswami, glorified Shukadeva Goswami, that Shukadeva Goswami, out of compassion for the suffering humanity, spoke the Bhagavatam. Karunaya aha purana guhyam. Samsarinam karunaya aha purana guhyam. Out of compassion for the suffering souls of this world, Shukadeva Goswami spoke the Bhagavatam. So, same thing. Vidura spoke the words of enlightenment and Dhritarashtra became completely detached and left home and middle. The scriptures enjoin, therefore, that one should associate with sadhus only, rejecting all other kinds of association. Asasangatyagye Vaishnavachar. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, reject association of materialistic persons, even though in their association, somebody may enjoy some material, sense gratification. But that's not important. That's available in all species of life. Even to those in hell, sense gratification is available. What to speak of others? So, that's not important. What is important is the association of sadhus or pure devotees, who can cut the bonds of material affection and free us from this material bondage and direct us from this bondage, and direct us for self-realization. This material world is a world of great illusion. And only by such association of pure devotees or genuine saints can we actually cut off our attachments, by hearing them attentively and by taking their words very seriously, understanding them to be the best well-wishers and taking their words seriously and cutting off this bondage and following their instructions how to proceed on the path of self-realization. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.3-4 Part 1 | 18-09-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Vidura, Hastinapura, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=49
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura left the palace and went away not because exactly that he was insulted by Duryodhana, but because he was not interested in any material comforts or materialistic way of life."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verses to be discussed, focusing on Vidura's return to Hastinapura and the reasons for his initial departure, emphasizing his detachment from material life and his search for spiritual knowledge.

In this session, we will be discussing the third and fourth verses of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Since there is a long purport for these two verses given by Srila Prabhupada, we will discuss this in two parts, so this session will be discussing part one of the purport. The third and fourth verses are given together. I shall read them together. So the third and fourth verses are given together, their translation: When they saw Vidura return to the palace, all the inhabitants, Maharaj Yudhishthira, his younger brothers, Dhritarashtra, Satyaki, Sanjaya, Kripacharya, Kunti, Gandhari, Draupadi, Subhadra, Uttara, Kripi, many other wives of the Kauravas, and other ladies with children all hurried to him in great delight. It so appeared that they had regained their consciousness after a long period. This indicates how dear Vidura was to all the inhabitants of the palace of Hastinapura. Foolishly, Duryodhana insulted Vidura, though Vidura was a well-wisher of Dhritarashtra and all the Kauravas, but Duryodhana, he very foolishly in a narrow-minded way, he thought somehow that Vidura was against his real welfare, while the opposite was true. Vidura was actually advising Dhritarashtra for the well-being of Dhritarashtra and their entire family members, but Duryodhana thought otherwise. And when Duryodhana, out of foolishness, he insulted Vidura, Vidura left the palace and went away not because exactly that he was insulted by Duryodhana, but because he was not interested in any material comforts or materialistic way of life. His interest was in Krishna consciousness, and he thought it wise to go on a pilgrimage and travel to holy places searching for a suitable pure devotee of the Lord to hear Krishna katha and inquire about spirituality from such a bonafide proper devotee. So it was described in the previous words that Vidura was on pilgrimage. He actually went searching for a pure devotee. He came in contact with Maitriya Muni. He inquired from Maitriya Muni, and after he got conviction that the ultimate goal is to be established in transcendental loving service of the Supreme Lord Krishna, after that he stopped putting questions to Maitriya and returned to Hastinapura. Now what are the purpose of return to Hastinapura that will be made clear in the next few verses.

---
### Hastinapura, Family, Joy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=302
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So they all felt great delight when Vidura returned and they went to receive him and they were appearing to have regained their consciousness after a long time."
**Summary:** This segment describes the joyful reception Vidura received upon his return to Hastinapura, highlighting his importance to the family and setting the stage for Srila Prabhupada's description of key personalities mentioned in the verses.

But here, when he returned, all the inhabitants of Hastinapura were not the same as he had been in the past, also that palace, Maharaj Yudhishthira, the brothers of Yudhishthira, Kunti, Gandhari, Draupadi, and so many other ladies with children, all of them, they were very, very joyful to receive him back after he had left and gone. Means they were not happy that he left. Duryodhana foolishly insulted Vidura, but Vidura was actually a real well-wisher, and he was very dear to all the other members of the family. So they all felt great delight when Vidura returned and they went to receive him and they were appearing to have regained their consciousness after a long time. It appeared like that. They were so happy, so joyful. So Srila Prabhupada in the Purport describes the four different personalities mentioned in this verse, the brief history of Gandhari, Kunti, Draupadi, and Subhadra. These four personalities history, brief history is given by Srila Prabhupada in the Purport.

---
### Gandhari, Chastity, Family
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "She was the most beautiful girl of her time and she was equally qualified by her womanly qualities which endeared every member of the Kaurava court."
**Summary:** This segment begins Srila Prabhupada's description of Gandhari, emphasizing her chastity, devotion to her husband Dhritarashtra, and her role as the mother of the Kauravas, including her initial envy of Kunti.

So first of all, Prabhupada describes the history of Gandhari. Gandhari is the mother of the Kauravas. She is the wife of Dhritarashtra, and she is being described here by Srila Prabhupada, the ideal chaste lady in the history of the world. Such a glorious description of Gandhari, ideal chaste lady. Now why she is described in this way will be very clear in the later parts of this description. She was the daughter of Maharaj Subala, the king of Gandhara. Now this Gandhara is known as Kandahar in Kabul, Afghanistan. And in her maiden state, Gandhari worshipped Lord Shiva. Lord Shiva is generally worshipped by Hindu maidens to get a good husband. Gandhari satisfied Lord Shiva, and by his benediction to obtain 100 sons, she was betrothed to Dhritarashtra despite his being blind forever. Dhritarashtra was very powerful Kshatriya, but he had that blindness from birth. He was born blind. When Gandhari came to know that her would-be husband was a blind man, to follow her life companion, she decided to become voluntarily blind. These are the characteristics of a chaste lady, a chaste wife. She voluntarily decided to become blind. So what did she do? She wrapped up her eyes with many silk linens, and she was married to Dhritarashtra under the guidance of her elder brother Shakuni. She was the most beautiful girl of her time, and she was equally qualified by her womanly qualities which endeared every member of the Kaurava court. Despite all her good qualities, she had the natural frailties of a woman, and she was envious of Kunti.

---
### Gandhari, Kurukshetra, Family
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=538
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the intrigue of the battle of Kurukshetra was going on, she was not in favor of fighting with the Pandavas, rather she blamed Dhritarashtra, her husband, for such a fratricidal war."
**Summary:** This segment continues the story of Gandhari, detailing her reaction to Kunti giving birth to a male child first, the birth of her 100 sons, her opposition to the Kurukshetra war, and her grief over the loss of her sons.

When the latter gave birth to a male child, both the queens were pregnant, but Kunti first gave birth to a male child. Thus, Gandhari became angry and gave a blow to her own abdomen. As a result, she gave birth to a lump of flesh only, but since she was a devotee of Vyasadeva, by the instruction of Vyasadeva, the lump was divided into 100 parts, and each part gradually developed to become a male child. Thus, her ambition to become the mother of 100 sons was fulfilled, and she began to nourish all the children according to her exalted position. When the intrigue of the battle of Kurukshetra was going on, she was not in favor of fighting with the Pandavas, rather she blamed Dhritarashtra, her husband, for such a fratricidal war. She desired that the state be divided into two parts from the sons of Pandu and her own sons. She was very affected by this when all her sons died in the battle of Kurukshetra, and she wanted to curse Bhishmadeva and Yudhishthira, but she was checked by Vyasadeva. So you can understand, she is a very chaste lady. She is very faithful to her husband, but still she advised based on proper religious principles, and she actually felt grief.

---
### Gandhari, Krishna, Death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Always in the Vedic culture, when somebody is agreed due to the death of a near and dear one, they are pacified by transcendental messages from the Vedic literature."
**Summary:** This segment concludes the description of Gandhari's life, including her mourning over the death of her sons, her pacification by Lord Krishna and Vyasadeva, and her eventual death in the Himalayas, followed by Yudhishthira performing her death ceremony.

When she lost all her 100 sons in the battle of Kurukshetra, her mourning over the death of Duryodhana and Dushasana before Lord Krishna was very pitiful, and Lord Krishna pacified her by transcendental messages. Always in the Vedic culture, when somebody is agreed due to the death of a near and dear one, they are pacified by transcendental messages from the Vedic literature. That is the only way one can become pacified. Further, it is described by Srila Prabhupada, she was equally agreed on the death of Karna, and she described to Lord Krishna the lamentation of Karna's wife. She was pacified by Shri Vyasadeva when he showed her dead sons, then promoted to the heavenly kingdom. She died along with her husband in the jungles of the Himalayas near the mouth of the Ganges. She burned in a forest fire. Maharaj Yudhishthira performed the death ceremony of his uncle and aunt.

---
### Kunti, Devotee, Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=760
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In former times people were very pious that the pious people of the earth were very strict in following all the rules and regulations, so they were visited by the devatas and devatas were visible on this earth planet whenever they used to visit here because people were pious."
**Summary:** This segment begins the description of Kunti Devi, focusing on her birth, adoption, and her service to visiting heavenly denizens and the sage Durvasa Muni.

Then Srila Prabhupada describes Kunti Devi's brief history. Kunti Devi was born as the daughter of Maharaj Shurasena, and she was a sister of Vasudeva. Vasudeva was Lord Krishna's father. Later, Kunti was adopted by Maharaj Kunti Bhoja, and hence she is known as Kunti. She was born as the daughter of Maharaj Shurasena, but she was adopted by Kunti Bhoja, and therefore she is called Kunti. She is the incarnation of the success potency of the personality. You got it. The heavenly denizens used from the upper planets used to visit the palace of King Kunti Bhoja, and Kunti was engaged for their reception. In former times people were very pious that the pious people of the earth were very strict in following all the rules and regulations, so they were visited by the devatas and devatas were visible on this earth planet whenever they used to visit here because people were pious. In Kali Yuga because of the sinful activities having increased too much, we cannot see the devatas even when they come to this material world. Now whenever these heavenly denizens used to visit King Kunti Bhoja's palace, Kunti was engaged for their reception. She also happened to serve the great mystic sage Durvasa Muni.

---
### Kunti, Karna, Pandavas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/58fbM0TAqxU?t=882
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So by the grace of the sun, she again turned into a virgin girl, but being afraid of her parents, she quitted the newly born child Karna."
**Summary:** This segment continues the story of Kunti, detailing how Durvasa Muni gave her a mantra to call upon any devata, leading to the birth of Karna before her marriage, and later, her use of the mantra to bear children for Pandu due to his curse.

Being satisfied by her faithful service, Durvasa Muni gave her a mantra by which it was possible for her to call any devata she pleased. As a matter of inquisitiveness, she at once called for the sun god, Suryadeva. Now this Suryadeva, when he was called, he immediately desired couplement with her, but she declined, but the sun god assured her that she would be not adulterated, her virginity would not be adulterated, that she would be given immunity from the adulteration of her virginity. So she agreed to that proposal. As a result of this couplement, she became pregnant, and Karna was born by her. This is before her marriage to Pandu. So by the grace of the sun, she again turned into a virgin girl, but being afraid of her parents, she quitted the newly born child Karna. After that, when she actually selected her own husband, she preferred Pandu to be her husband. Now Pandu was under a curse that he could not conceive children from his wife Kunti. So therefore, Kunti had to use that mantra to call upon devatas to bear children for Pandu, and that's how the five Pandavas, sorry the three, first three Pandavas were born from Kunti, and two other Pandavas were born from Madri. So there is more description which we will continue to discuss in the next session. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananj Nishalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namo Mishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Ganadhara Shri Vasali Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udeerayet.

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Vidura's Return
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=51
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.3, SB 1.13.4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It so appeared that they had regained their consciousness after a long period."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses to be discussed, verses 3 and 4 of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, and briefly explains their meaning: the joyous reception of Vidura upon his return to the palace.

In this session we will be discussing from verses 3 and 4 of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This will be the part 2 of the same verses. In the previous session we discussed part 1. Now we are going to discuss part 2 of the purport. So the 2 verses are given together. I will chant them once again: Tam Bandhum Agatam Drishtva Dharma Putra Sahaj Dhanu Jah Dhritarashtro Yuyutsushcha Suta Sharadvata Pritha Gandhari Draupadi Brahman Subhadra Chottara Kripi Anyascha Jamaya Pandor Nyataya Sasutas Triya. These two verses, the meaning is given together. When they saw Vidura return to the palace all the inhabitants of the palace, Maharaj Yudhishthira, his younger brothers, Dhritarashtra, Satyaki, Sanjaya, Kripacharya, Kunti, Gandhari, Draupadi, Subhadra, Uttara, Kripi, many other wives of the Kauravas and other ladies with children all hurried to him in great delight. It so appeared that they had regained their consciousness after a long period.

### Kunti Devi, Vedic Culture, Self-Realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=146
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, the Vedic culture, everybody follows the Vedic scriptures as explained and endorsed by the great sages who are well versed in this Vedic literature."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with the history of Kunti Devi, focusing on her role in the birth of the Pandavas, Madri's sati ritual, and the endorsement of these actions by great sages, highlighting the importance of Vedic scriptures and self-realization in civilized human life.

So, we continue to discuss the purport from the previous session. We were discussing the history given by Srila Prabhupada about Kunti Devi. So, continuing to describe the history of Kunti Devi from where it was stopped in the previous session. So, continuing to describe the history of Kunti Devi. It was described that Kunti Devi after marrying Pandu, because Pandu could not beget any children in his wives, so Kunti Devi was requested to call upon the Devatas by using the mantra given to her by Durvasamuni and she called upon three different personalities: Yamaraj, Vayudeva, and Indrava and begot Yudhishthira, Bhima, and Arjuna. Then, Madri also wanted to begot children, so Kunti Devi taught her the mantra and she begot twins, Nakula and Sahadeva by calling upon the Ashwini Kumaras and in this way, Kunti Devi was called upon by Durvasamuni and Indra. This way, Pandu had five sons through his two wives. Then, it is described that Maharaj Pandu died at an early age for which Kunti was so aggrieved that she fainted. The two co-wives namely Kunti and Madri decided that Kunti should live for the maintenance of the five minor children, the Pandavas, and Madri should accept the Sati rituals by meeting voluntary death along with her husband Pandu. This agreement was endorsed by great sages like Shatashringa and others present on the occasion. This is the meaning of chaste wife or chaste wives. One of them decided to undergo Sati rituals and the other one decided to stay back as a widow for taking care of the five minor children and this was approved by great sages like Shatashringa. So, the Vedic culture, everybody follows the Vedic scriptures as explained and endorsed by the great sages who are well versed in this Vedic literature. So, it is a perfect society that is based on the principles given in the Vedas. How civilized human beings must live in this world with the ultimate aim of self-realization.

---
### Human Life, Self-Realization, Warning
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=335
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Don't waste the human form of life simply in sense gratification. You may not get this opportunity again."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the higher purpose of human life, which is self-realization and attaining eternal life, contrasting it with the modern tendency to live solely for sense gratification, and warns against wasting this rare opportunity.

This human life is not simply meant for enjoying some sense gratification while life is there in the body. No. That is the modern understanding. But the Vedas give us the higher purpose of life that we can become self-realized and attain eternal life in the spiritual abode of the Supreme Lord. Go back to Him to associate with Him and live with Him eternally full of bliss and knowledge. That is the special purpose, the special prerogative of the human form of life. People do not know this in the modern age and in ignorance they are simply living like animals. So, therefore Srila Prabhupada and all the Acharyas and the scriptures and the great Rishis, they are warning us: Don't waste the human form of life simply in sense gratification. You may not get this opportunity again though you will take birth again in this world. You may take birth in a lower species and again to evolve the consciousness from the lower species to human form takes millions of years. So don't miss this chance otherwise you have to wait millions of years to again get this chance for eternal life free from all miseries, all kinds of ignorance and suffering. This is the opportunity. So, all these great personalities that are described in the Bhagavatam, in the Puranas, the great devotees, the great saintly persons, they lived a life based on the scriptural directions and they attained perfection.

---
### Kunti Devi, Pandavas, Draupadi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=464
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But by order of Kunti, all the five Pandava brothers became equally the husbands of Panchali or Draupadi."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the narration of Kunti Devi's life, including her support of the Pandavas during their exile, the story of Hidimba and Bhima, the killing of Bakasura, and Kunti's role in Draupadi's marriage to all five Pandava brothers.

So, the description continues. Later on when the Pandavas were banished from the kingdom by the intrigues of Duryodhana, Kunti followed her sons and she equally faced all sorts of difficulties during those days in exile. During the forest life one demon girl, Hidimba, wanted Bhima as her husband. Now this is Kshatriya Dharma. If some woman or girl approaches a Kshatriya and begs for shelter, protection, because women have to be protected and if they don't have protection they can approach a Kshatriya and beg for protection. Bhima refused this girl. But when the girl approached Kunti and Yudhishthira, they ordered Bhima to accept her proposal and give her a son. As a result of this combination between Bhima and this Hidimba, Ghatotkaja was born and he fought very valiantly with his father against the Kauravas in the battle of Kurukshetra. In their forest life the Pandavas along with Kunti Devi, they lived with a Brahmana family that was in trouble because of one Bakasura demon and Kunti ordered Bhima to kill the Bakasura demon to protect the Brahmana family against troubles created by the demon. She advised Yudhishthira to start for Panchala Desha. Draupadi was gained in this Panchala Desha by Arjuna, but by order of Kunti all the five Pandava brothers became equally the husbands of Panchali or Draupadi. She was married with the five Pandavas in the presence of Vyasadeva.

---
### Draupadi, Chastity, Kunti's Order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=579
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So people sometimes wonder how can Draupadi be considered as a chaste lady when she had five husbands?"
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the question of Draupadi's chastity, explaining that her marriage to five husbands was due to Kunti's order and was approved by Vyasadeva, emphasizing that Draupadi is an extraordinary, transcendental personality.

Again, Draupadi accepted five husbands is not the norm. A chaste woman accepts only one husband. So people sometimes wonder how can Draupadi be considered as a chaste lady when she had five husbands? So it is explained that on the order of Kunti, though Draupadi actually was gained in this Swayamvara of Draupadi only by Arjuna. So she should have married Arjuna alone. But Kunti Devi ordered Draupadi to accept all the five brothers as her husband and this was approved by Vyasadeva, the great sage, based on the scriptures. So she is an extraordinary personality. Draupadi is described elsewhere by Srila Prabhupada as a partial incarnation of Lakshmi. So Draupadi is a transcendental personality. She is not an ordinary woman. So therefore we can't view this Draupadi having five husbands as unchaste on her part to have more than one husband.

---
### Kunti Devi, Karna, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Her prayers for the Lord after the battle of Kurukshetra, when Lord Krishna was going back home, she excellently explained. She is a pure devotee of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Kunti Devi's remembrance of Karna, her prayers to Lord Krishna after the battle of Kurukshetra, and her severe penance in the forest with Gandhari, highlighting her devotion and self-realization.

Kunti Devi never forgot her first child Karna who was born before Kunti's marriage. And after Karna's death in the battle of Kurukshetra she lamented and admitted before her other sons that Karna was her eldest son prior to her marriage with Maharaja Pandu. She admitted. Her prayers for the Lord after the battle of Kurukshetra, when Lord Krishna was going back home, she excellently explained. She is a pure devotee of Krishna. So she offered wonderful prayers and it has become immortalized in the Bhagavatam. So in those prayers we come to know what is the transcendental position of Lord Krishna very nicely explained by Kunti Devi. Later she went to the forest with Gandhari for severe penance. She used to take meals after 30 days. See this is proof that Kunti Devi is actually a great devotee. Because for the sake of self realization she underwent fasting taking meals only once in 30 days. Who can do this? Not possible for some ordinary people. She finally sat down in profound meditation and later burned to ashes in a forest fire.

---
### Subhadra, Krishna, Jagannath Temple
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=770
**Verse References:** SB 10
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Subhadra was very dear sister to both Krishna and Balarama."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Subhadra, the sister of Krishna and Balarama, mentioning her birth details from the 10th canto of the Bhagavatam and her representation in the Jagannath temple at Puri, linking it to the Lord's visit to Kurukshetra and the meeting of Radha and Krishna.

Then there is a description of Subhadra. Subhadra was the daughter of Vasudeva and sister of Lord Sri Krishna. In the 10th canto of the Bhagavatam it is described that Vasudeva and Devaki gave birth to 8 sons and 1 daughter is Subhadra and the 8th son is Krishna and 6 of her of their children were killed by Kamsa. Shadgarbha it is called and the 7th son was actually Balarama but he was transferred from Devaki's womb to Rohini's womb so when he was actually born he appeared as the son of Rohini but initially for 7 months he was in the womb of Devaki. So therefore it is described here Subhadra was very dear sister to both Krishna and Balarama. The two brothers and sisters are represented in the famous Jagannath temple at Puri and the temple is still visited by thousands of pilgrims daily Jagannath Baldev Subhadra Maharani Ki Jai the three are present in the deity form in this particular temple as the two brothers and sister. This temple is in remembrance of the Lord's visit at Kurukshetra during an occasion of solar eclipse and his subsequent meeting with the residents of Vrindavan. The meeting of Radha and Krishna during this occasion is a very pathetic story and Lord Sri Chaitanya in the ecstasy of Srimati Radharani always pined for Lord Sri Krishna Jagannath Puri.

---
### Subhadra, Arjuna, Abhimanyu, Pariksit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oYtHuYmKXds?t=891
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Arjuna kidnapping Subhadra on Krishna's advice, their marriage, the birth of Abhimanyu, and Subhadra's grief at Abhimanyu's death, concluding with her happiness at the birth of Pariksit.

While Arjuna was at Dwaraka he wanted to have Subhadra as his queen and he expressed his desire to Lord Krishna. Sri Krishna knew that his elder brother Lord Baladeva was arranging Subhadra's marriage elsewhere and since he did not dare to go against the arrangement of Baladeva, Krishna advised Arjuna to kidnap Subhadra. Krishna himself kidnapped Rukmini similarly Krishna advised Arjuna to kidnap Subhadra. So when all of them were on a pleasure trip on the Raivata hill, Arjuna managed to kidnap Subhadra. Krishna Baladeva was very angry at Arjuna and he wanted to kill Arjuna but Lord Krishna implored his brother to excuse Arjuna then Subhadra was duly married to Arjuna and Abhimanyu was born of Subhadra and Arjuna. At the premature death of Abhimanyu in the battle of Kurukshetra Subhadra was very mortified but on the birth of Pariksit she was happy and solaced. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
