### renunciation, devotion, Himalayas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=47
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.30
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is a very poetic, beautiful language used by Suta Goswami to describe."
**Summary:** Suta Goswami describes Gandhari following Dhritarashtra to the Himalayas, highlighting her chastity and devotion, and the Himalayas as an ideal place for self-realization.

In this session we will be discussing from the 30th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We have already heard how Dhritarashtra cut off his attachment after hearing the strong words spoken by Vidura and left home and went towards north. Now, the further description is being given here. It is Suta Goswami who is describing this to the sages of Naimisharanya. Patim Prayantam Subalasya Putri Pativratacha Anujagama Sadhvi Himalayam Nyasta Dhanda Praharsham Manasvinam Ivasat Samprahara. The meaning of this verse is, the gentle and chaste Gandhari, who was the daughter of King Subala of Kandahar or Gandhara, followed her husband, seeing that he was going to the Himalaya mountains, which are the delightful mountains, which are the delight of those who have accepted the staff of the renounced order, like fighters who have accepted a good lashing from the enemy. It is a very poetic, beautiful language used by Suta Goswami to describe.

---
### chastity, devotion, Vedic culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=126
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.30
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And Sadhvi means she is the most chaste lady. And Pativrata means she is devoted to her husband."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Gandhari's qualities as a Sadhvi and Pativrata, emphasizing her chastity and devotion to her husband, and highlighting the importance of these qualities in Vedic civilization.

Along with Dhritarashtra, Gandhari also followed her husband, seeing that he was going to the Himalayas. So, he proceeded north, means he went in at least the Indian subcontinent. When somebody proceeds north for a spiritual purpose, they are going to the Himalayas. And that's the ideal place for those who want to go to a secluded place and live in seclusion, exclusively for the purpose of self-realization. So, you see Gandhari is described very beautifully here as Sadhvi, Pativrata. And Sadhvi means she is the most chaste lady. And Pativrata means she is devoted to her husband.

---
### example, sacrifice, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=202
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But as a chaste and gentle lady, she followed her husband without consideration."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's explanation of Gandhari and Sita Devi's devotion to their husbands is presented, emphasizing their voluntary choice to follow them into difficult circumstances, highlighting their chastity and selflessness.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, Sovalini or Gandhari, daughter of King Subala and wife of King Dhritarashtra, was ideal as a wife devoted to her husband. Pativrata, Sadhvi. The Vedic civilization especially prepares chaste and devoted wives, of whom Gandhari is one amongst many mentioned in history. Whenever there is an example given in the scriptures about historical personalities who were ideal, chaste and devoted wives, Gandhari's name is one of the foremost. Similarly, Srila Prabhupada says, Lakshmiji, Sita Devi, was also a daughter of a great king. She followed her husband Lord Ramachandra into the forest. Sita, she was a daughter of King Janaka. She was a princess. She was married to the Prince Rama. But, when Rama was ordered by his father to go to the forest, Sita need not have gone. So, it is said here, just like Sita voluntarily chose to follow her husband and go to the forest with him, similarly Gandhari, she voluntarily accepted to follow her husband and go with him to the Himalayas. So, Srila Prabhupada says, similarly as a woman, Gandhari could have remained at home or at her father's house. But as a chaste and gentle lady, she followed her husband without consideration. Life in Himalayas, that too for practicing Self Realization, is a very difficult life, especially for a woman. So, in spite of knowing the difficulties, Gandhari did not hesitate even a little bit. She simply followed her husband.

---
### renunciation, determination, Vanaprastha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=340
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is real chastity. This is a sign of chastity."
**Summary:** The lecture discusses Dhritarashtra's renunciation and Gandhari's unwavering support, emphasizing that her decision was voluntary and highlighting the concept of Vanaprastha as a stage of life.

Instructions for the renounced order of life were imparted to Dhritarashtra by Vidura. And Gandhari was by the side of her husband, hearing Vidura, while Vidura was instructing Dhritarashtra. So, Vidura did not ask Gandhari to follow her husband. Or, Dhritarashtra did not ask Gandhari that you follow me. No. Because, Dhritarashtra was at that time fully determined, like a great warrior who faces all kinds of dangers in the battlefield. The example is given, Manasvinam Ivasat Samprahara. Manasvinam. Those who are great warriors, they are ready to face any danger in the battlefield, but they will not give up fighting for the just cause, or for fighting in a Dharma Yudha. They are never afraid of facing any situation. So, even if they are injured or even if they are killed, they don't mind. But they will fight for justice. Similarly, Dhritarashtra was determined to renounce and leave the palace and go away to the Himalayas. So, he did not ask her. Are you going to come with me? What are you going to do? He did not ask her. So, Srila Prabhupada says, Dhritarashtra was no longer attracted to so-called wife or relatives, and he decided to start all alone. But, as a chaste lady, Gandhari decided to follow her husband till the last moment. It will be explained in the later verses. And Srila Prabhupada is writing in the purport, Gandhari decided, I am going to be with my husband till the last moment of his life. I am not going to bother about my comforts, especially in old age. This is real chastity. This is a sign of chastity. As a chaste lady, Gandhari decided to follow her husband till the last moment. Maharaj Dhritarashtra accepted the order of Vanaprastha.

---
### Varna Ashrama, Sanyas, renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=499
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sanyas itself is not recommended in Kali Yuga."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the Varna Ashrama system, emphasizing the stages of Brahmacharya, Grahastha, Vanaprastha, and Sanyas, and discusses the rules and restrictions associated with each stage, particularly regarding the relationship between husband and wife, and the general prohibition of Sanyas in Kali Yuga.

It is not that after Grahastha, one directly accepts Sanyas. No. Directly accepting Sanyas is possible only for a Brahmachari who decides not to enter the Grahastha Ashrama. Such a Brahmachari can directly accept Sanyas. But a Grahastha, one who has been trained as a Brahmachari and enters Grahastha Ashrama, should next accept Vanaprastha and then accept Sanyas. So, Brahmacharya, Grahastha, Vanaprastha, Sanyas. These are the progressive stages of spiritual development in the life of a civilized, cultured human being following the Varna Ashrama system. But, Dhritarashtra, because he had accepted the Grahastha Ashrama, he next accepted the Vanaprastha order. Now, Srila Prabhupada says, because Dhritarashtra accepted the order of Vanaprastha, at this stage, the wife is allowed to remain as a voluntary servitor of the husband. It is allowed for the wife to remain with the husband. As a servitor of the husband. But in the Sanyas stage, no wife can stay with her former husband. So, Sanyas is that particular stage of renunciation or the spiritual order where a Grahastha who renounces home, goes away to the forest or a holy place or to the Himalayas, can take his wife only as long as he is in Vanaprastha order. But Sanyas order, he cannot be with his wife. He has to be away from his wife. Completely away from his wife. Why? Srila Prabhupada explains, according to the scriptures, a Sanyasi is considered to be a dead man civilly. It is declared like he is no longer present for the wife. Therefore, the wife becomes a civil widow without connection with her former husband. These are all based on a very advanced culture and civilization in the Vedic culture. Of course, nowadays it is not practical. Neither it is recommended. Sanyas itself is not recommended in Kali Yuga.

---
### Sanyas, preaching, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=671
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sanyas is only for those who want to dedicate themselves for traveling and preaching without any other responsibility."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the question of why he accepted Sanyas, clarifying that while Sanyas is generally not recommended in Kali Yuga, it may be accepted by those who dedicate themselves to traveling and preaching without other responsibilities.

Somebody may ask, why did I accept Sanyas? Now, Sanyas in Kali Yuga in general is not recommended for everyone. But some people who want to preach in this society very vigorously, travel and preach, for them, Sanyas may be accepted. May be accepted simply for the sake of dedicating oneself for traveling and preaching. Without any encumbrances of other responsibilities and duties in the society. So, Sanyas is only for those who want to dedicate themselves for traveling and preaching without any other responsibility.

---
### Kali Yuga, detachment, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=733
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "She is following her husband at her own risk."
**Summary:** The speaker mentions the prohibitions in Kali Yuga, including Sanyas, and emphasizes that Gandhari followed her husband at her own risk, while Dhritarashtra remained detached and focused on self-realization.

Otherwise, Sanyas in Kali Yuga is forbidden. Five things are forbidden in Kali Yuga. Ashwamedham, Gavalambham, Sanyasam, Palapaitrikam, Devarayana, Suthopati, Kalopancha, Vibharjayet. So, Sanyas is forbidden in Kali Yuga. Maharaj Dhritarashtra did not deny his faithful wife and she followed her husband at her own risk. It doesn't mean that husband because the wife is following him, he takes some responsibility for protecting her, maintaining her. No. She is following her husband at her own risk. And the husband is still completely detached from the wife and he is determined to practice. He is determined to practice self realization without any other incomprehenses.

---
### Sanyas, Vaishnava, Mayavadi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uel1SCMryu4?t=788
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vaishnava Sanyasis generally go to a holy place connected with the pastimes of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The lecture concludes with a discussion on the Sanyas Danda, differentiating between Ekadanda (Mayavadi) and Tridanda (Vaishnava) Sanyasis, their preferences for locations (Himalayas vs. Vrindavan/Puri), and their respective approaches to dependence on Krishna.

Now, regarding Sanyas, Srila Prabhupada says, briefly, the Sanyasis accept a rod as the sign of the renounced rod of the Danda. Sanyas Danda. Like, I am having this Danda here. So, Sanyasis accept this Danda. What is the significance of this Danda? This Danda is accepted by Sanyasis to signify that they have accepted the renounced daughter of the fly, Sanyas. There are two types of Sanyasis. Those who follow the Mayavadi philosophy headed by Sri Pachankaracharya accept only one rod or Ekadanda, Sanyas. But those who follow the Vaishnava philosophy accept three combined rods called Tridanda. Inside this, three Dandas. There are three rods. The Mayavadi Sanyasis are Ekadandi Swamis. Whereas the Vaishnava Sanyasis are Tridandi Swamis. More distinctly, they are called Tridandi Goswamis. In order to be distinguished from the Mayavadi philosophers. The Ekadandi Swamis are mostly fond of the Himalayas. But the Vaishnava Sanyasis are fond of Vrindavan and Puri. Vaishnava Sanyasis generally go to a holy place connected with the pastimes of the Supreme Lord. Whereas the Mayavadi Sanyasis they generally go to the Himalayas. The Vaishnava Sanyasis are Narottamas. Whereas the Mayavadi Sanyasis are Dheeras. The Vaishnava Sanyasis they from the beginning they learn to depend on Krishna. The Supreme Personality of God. So therefore, specially in Sanyas, they are completely dependent on Krishna only. Where therefore, they are eligible for this Narottama stage of renunciation. The highest stage. Whereas the Mayavadi Sanyasis because they don't have any conception or understanding of the personality of Godhead. So they are mostly Dheeras. Undisturbed by material circumstances and detached from all material comforts. So they are mostly Dheeras. So they are mostly Dheeras. Dhritarashtra was advised to follow the Dheeras because at that stage it was difficult for him to become a Narottama. Given the circumstances in which it was he was situated. He was advised to become a Dheera. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.31 - 33 | 14-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### householder duties, Vedic culture, charity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fFT0FZ_5BBM?t=34
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.31
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Modern householders are practiced to different modes of life, namely to rise late, they don't rise early, and then take bed tea without any sort of cleanliness, and without any purificatory practices as mentioned above."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on verse 31, explaining Maharaj Yudhishthira's pious duties as a householder, contrasting it with modern practices, and emphasizing the importance of following Vedic culture and associating with devotees.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Saraswatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from three verses, 31, 32 and 33 of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter is dealing with the topic of Dhritarashtra quits home. So we have already discussed how Vidura instructed Dhritarashtra with very very strong words, and Dhritarashtra was able to cut off his attachments and leave the palace and immediately go to the Himalayas. And after that what happened? So that is being now described by Suta Goswami to the sages of Naimishaharanya. So the 31st verse, Ajatashatru krutamaitro hutagnir Viprannatva tilago bhumi rukmaihi Graham pravishto guru vandanaya Naca apasyat pitarau saubhalimcha. The meaning of this verse is Maharaj Yudhishthira, whose enemy was never born, performed his daily morning duties by praying, offering fire sacrifice to the sun god, and offering obeisances, grains, cows, land and gold, to the brahmanas. He then entered the palace to pay respects to the elderly. However, he could not find his uncles or aunt, the daughter of King Subala. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada explains, Maharaj Yudhishthira was the most pious king because he personally practiced daily the pious duties for the householders. The householders are required to rise early in the morning, and after bathing they should offer respects to the deities at home by prayers, by offering fuel in the sacred fire, by giving the brahmanas in charity land, cows, grains, gold, etc., and at last offering to the elderly members due respects and obeisances. This is the Vedic culture, duties of a householder. If you study the 10th canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, you will find a chapter describing Lord Krishna's daily activities as a grahastha in Dwaraka. So Krishna is to do all this. Yudhishthira Maharaj was doing all his duties very nicely. So Srila Prabhupada comments, one who is not prepared to practice any duties, and is not prepared to practice injunctions prescribed in the shastras, cannot be a good man simply by book knowledge. Modern householders are practiced to different modes of life, namely to rise late, they don't rise early, and then take bed tea without any sort of cleanliness, and without any purificatory practices as mentioned above. A cultured householder should rise early in the morning. And the first business after rising early in the morning is to cleanse oneself completely. That is a very very very first thing one should do. But modern day householders do not do so. The household children are taken to practice what the parents practice. And therefore the whole generation glides towards hell. Nothing good can be expected from them unless they associate with sadhus. Sadhus means pure devotees. Like Dhritarashtra, the materialistic person may take lessons from a sadhu like Vidura, just like Vidura instructed Dhritarashtra. Similarly modern day householders should take lessons from devotees of the Lord who are following all these practices very nicely. So if the materialistic persons take proper lessons from sadhu like Vidura, and then they can be cleansed of the effects of modern life. Now Maharaj Yudhishthira, Srila Prabhupada says, however could not find in the palace his two uncles, namely Dhritarashtra and Vidura, along with Gandhari, the daughter of King Subala. Saubalini means the daughter of King Subala. So all the three of them had left the palace very silently, quietly, without informing anybody. So therefore the next morning when Yudhishthira came to offer respects to his uncles and his aunt, the elderly, he could find them. He was anxious to see them and therefore asked Sanjaya, the private secretary of Dhritarashtra.

---
### anxiety, compassion, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fFT0FZ_5BBM?t=391
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.32, SB 1.13.33
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Sanjaya, where is our uncle who is old and blind? Where is my well-wisher, uncle Vidura and mother Gandhari, who is very afflicted due to her son's demise?"
**Summary:** This segment covers verses 32 and 33, describing Yudhishthira's anxiety and questions to Sanjaya about the whereabouts of Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, and Vidura, highlighting his compassionate nature and concern for their well-being after the battle of Kurukshetra.

So the 32nd verse. The meaning of this 32nd verse. Maharaj Yudhishthira, full of anxiety, turned to Sanjaya who was sitting there and said, O Sanjaya, where is our uncle who is old and blind? The 33rd verse, Ambacha hata-putrarta pitruvyakva-gata-surat apimaiyakrita-prajne hata-bandhusya-bharyaya Asham samana shamalam Ganga aayam dukhito apatat. The meaning of this 33rd verse is, Where is my well-wisher, uncle Vidura and mother Gandhari, who is very afflicted due to her son's demise? My uncle Dhritarashtra was also very mortified due to the death of all his sons and grandsons. Undoubtedly, I am very ungrateful. Did he therefore take my offenses very seriously and along with his wife drown himself in the Ganges? See the reaction of Yudhishthira when he did not find his uncles and aunt. It is just befitting with Yudhishthira's good-natured behavior. So Srila Prabhupada explains, the Pandavas, especially Maharaj, Yudhishthira and Arjuna anticipated the after-effects of the battle of Kurukshetra. Both Yudhishthira and Arjuna did not actually want to fight the battle. They were just carrying out their duties according to what was prescribed for them as kshatriyas. Kshatriyas are duty-bound to fight for justice. So Yudhishthira and Arjuna were unwillingly engaged in the battle.

---
### devotee qualities, compassion, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fFT0FZ_5BBM?t=550
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira Maharaj, being the most saintly, compassionate, kind, considerate, good-natured, well-behaved, saintly king, he was always conscious of the miserable condition of his aunt and uncle."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the soft-hearted nature of devotees like Arjuna and Yudhishthira, who were unwilling participants in the battle of Kurukshetra, and highlights Yudhishthira's compassion and care for Dhritarashtra and Gandhari in their old age, contrasting it with modern societal trends of neglecting elderly parents.

So Srila Prabhupada points out, because both of them did not really like the fighting, therefore Arjuna declined to execute the fighting. You can read in the Bhagavad Gita, in the first chapter, how Arjuna tells Krishna, I won't fight. How can I kill my kinsmen just for the sake of me to enjoy a flourishing royal kingdom? I don't want to fight. So this is the soft-hearted nature of pure devotees. All the Pandavas are pure devotees. The fight was executed, however, by the will of the Lord. So even though the Pandavas did not want to fight, especially Yudhishthira and Arjuna did not want to fight against the Kauravas, but it was executed, the fighting was executed by the will of the Supreme Lord. Everyone is bound by the will of the Lord. Now after the battle, the effects of family agreement, as they had thought of it before, had come true. Whatever they had feared would be the aftermath of the massacre. Millions of Kshatriyas who were participating in the battle all died. Maharaj Yudhishthira was always conscious of the great plight of his uncle Dhritarashtra and aunt Gandhari, because they lost all their sons and grandsons. And they are old, and now they are bereft of their kingdom. Their sons and grandsons have died. Their well-wishers have passed away. So what do they do? But Yudhishthira Maharaj, being the most saintly, compassionate, kind, considerate, good-natured, well-behaved, saintly king, he was always conscious of the miserable condition of his aunt and uncle. Therefore, he took all possible care of them in their old age and aggrieved conditions. Care for the elderly. That is the culture, the Vedic culture. Nowadays, many, many children, when they are grown up, they go away from home, leaving their parents alone. They go for some career, some business, some opportunities for making a lot of money. In the western countries, especially from India, so many youngsters go away and leave their parents alone. Yudhishthira Maharaj is not like that. He was taking all possible care of Dhritarashtra and Gandhari in their old age.

---
### humility, self-reflection, devotee qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fFT0FZ_5BBM?t=792
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee never finds faults with others, but tries to find his own fault and rectify them as far as possible. This is a wonderful quality of a devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Yudhishthira's worries about Dhritarashtra and Gandhari's disappearance, his feelings of ingratitude, and his focus on his own misdeeds rather than blaming others, highlighting the qualities of a true devotee who seeks self-improvement.

When, therefore, he could not find his uncle and aunt in the palace, naturally, his doubts arose. He was worried, what happened to them? Where have they gone? Why are they missing from their place of residence? He conjectured that they had gone down to the water of the Ganges. Had they drowned themselves in the Ganga river? Because they thought their life is not worth living. He thought himself ungrateful because when the Pandavas were fatherless, Maharaj Dhritarashtra had given them all royal facilities to live. And in return, Yudhishthira had killed all Dhritarashtra's sons in the battle of Kurukshetra. See how Yudhishthira is thinking. Dhritarashtra gave me shelter when I was fatherless. And what did I do? I killed all his sons and grandsons in the battle of Kurukshetra. So, as a pious man, Maharaj Yudhishthira took into account all his unavoidable misdeeds. He never thought of the misdeeds of his uncle and company. Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana were always plotting to kill the Pandavas. So, the fact is, Dhritarashtra suffered the effects of his own misdeeds by the will of the Lord. But Maharaj Yudhishthira was thinking only of his own unavoidable misdeeds. This is the nature of a devotee of the Lord. A devotee never finds faults with others, but tries to find his own fault and rectify them as far as possible. This is a wonderful quality of a devotee of the Lord. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### family, protection, inquiry
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=56
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When my father Pandu fell down and we were all small children, these two uncles came to me and said, They gave us protection from all kinds of calamities. They were always our good well-wishers. Alas, where have they gone from here?"
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the context of the verses, focusing on Yudhishthira's search for his missing uncles and aunt, specifically explaining the meaning of the 34th verse where Yudhishthira expresses his concern and gratitude for the protection his uncles provided during his childhood.

The description is continuing about Yudhishthira searching for his missing uncles and aunt. The 34th verse: Pithari Uparate Pandav Sarvanas Surudas Shishun Arakshatam Vyasanataha Pitruvyav Kva Gatav Itaha. The meaning of this verse is, When my father Pandu fell down and we were all small children, these two uncles came to me and said, They gave us protection from all kinds of calamities. They were always our good well-wishers. Alas, where have they gone from here? This is spoken by Yudhishthira to Sanjaya while inquiring from Sanjaya about the uncles and aunt who are missing from the palace.

---
### compassion, sorrow, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=144
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sanjaya was fully compassionate toward Dhritarashtra because in the game of the battle of Kurukshetra, King Dhritarashtra had lost everything, men and money. And at last the king and the queen had to leave home in utter fright."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the 35th verse, focusing on Sanjaya's distress and inability to respond to Yudhishthira due to Dhritarashtra's sudden departure. Srila Prabhupada's purport is discussed, highlighting Sanjaya's compassion for Dhritarashtra and his lack of understanding regarding Dhritarashtra's inner awakening.

The 35th verse: Suta Vacha. We should remember that Suta Gosvami is speaking, narrating this, describing this in silence to the sage of Sunayamishadanya. Suta Vacha: Kripa Yaas Neha Vaikla Vyat Suto Viraha Karshitaha Atmeshwaram Achakshano Napratya Napratyaha Atipiditaha. The meaning of this verse is, Suta Goswami said, because of compassion and mental agitation, Sanjaya, not having seen his own master Dhritarashtra, was aggrieved and could not properly reply to Maharaj Yudhishthira. Immediately Sanjaya could not respond to Yudhishthira's inquiry. So in the Purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, Sanjaya was the personal assistant of Maharaj Dhritarashtra for a very long time and thus he had the opportunity to study the life of Dhritarashtra. And when he saw at last that Dhritarashtra had left home without his knowledge, his sorrows had no bound. He was extremely sorry that somebody whom he had served for a very long time had left home without informing Sanjaya. So Sanjaya was fully compassionate toward Dhritarashtra because in the game of the battle of Kurukshetra, King Dhritarashtra had lost everything, men and money. And at last the king and the queen had to leave home in utter fright. Dhritarashtra was so frustrated that he felt very sorry for the people who had left home without informing Sanjaya. He studied the situation in his own way because he did not know that the inner vision of Dhritarashtra has been awakened by Vidura. And that therefore he had lost his knowledge of Dhritarashtra. And that therefore he had lost his knowledge of Dhritarashtra.

---
### renunciation, detachment, spiritual awakening
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=288
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless one is convinced of a better life after renunciation of present life, one cannot stick to the renounced order of life simply by artificial dress or staying out of the home."
**Summary:** The speaker continues discussing Srila Prabhupada's explanation, emphasizing that true renunciation requires conviction in a better life beyond the material world, contrasting it with superficial or unsustainable forms of renunciation.

So Sanjaya left home in enthusiastic cheerfulness for a better life after departure from the dark well of home. So Srila Prabhupada says, Sanjaya doesn't know that Dhritarashtra's inner vision has been awakened by Vidura and he has cheerfully left the palace, not in frustration. So Srila Prabhupada says, unless one is convinced of a better life after renunciation of present life, one cannot stick to the renounced order of life simply by artificial dress or staying out of the home. Sometimes not. Nowadays people leave home and go away to some ashram. But such renunciation will not last. Unless they are properly fixed up in that renunciation by being convinced of a better life after quitting home. Being convinced of a better life.

### intelligence, mind control, spiritual guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=371
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.36, BG 3.42
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "First he slowly pacified his mind by intelligence. And wiping away his tears. And thinking of the feet of his master, Dhritarashtra. He began to reply to Maharaj Yudhishtira."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the 36th verse, explaining how Sanjaya composed himself by pacifying his mind with intelligence, wiping away tears, and remembering his master, Dhritarashtra, before replying to Yudhishthira. The speaker references the Bhagavad Gita to illustrate the hierarchy of senses, mind, intelligence, and soul, emphasizing the importance of intelligence in controlling the mind.

The 36th verse: Vimrgya ashroni paanibhyam vishtabhyatmanam atmana ajatashatrumpratyuche prabhopadavanusmaran. The meaning of this 36th verse: First he slowly pacified his mind by intelligence. And wiping away his tears. And thinking of the feet of his master, Dhritarashtra. He began to reply to Maharaj Yudhishtira. Sanjaya. He pacified his mind because he was disturbed on not finding Dhritarashtra in the palace. So he pacified his mind by his intelligence. In the Bhagavad Gita it is explained that the mind is superior to the senses. The intelligence is superior to the mind. The soul is even superior to the intelligence. So one can pacify the mind by intelligence. Because intelligence is higher. If one's intelligence is not working then one can get the mind pacified by taking instructions from somebody else who is more intelligent. Who is spiritually awakened. So Sanjaya pacified his mind by his intelligence. And wiping away his tears. Thinking of the feet of his master Dhritarashtra. He began to reply to Maharaj Yudhishtira.

---
### deception, respect, culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=479
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I have been cheated by those great souls."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 37th verse, where Sanjaya replies to Yudhishthira, stating he is unaware of Dhritarashtra, Gandhari and Vidura's whereabouts and feels 'cheated' by them. The speaker emphasizes the cultural aspect of respecting elders regardless of their actions.

What was his reply? That's the next verse. 37th verse: Sanjaya Vacha. Sanjaya is replying to Maharaj Yudhishtira. Naham Veda Vyavasitam Pitrorva Kulanandana Gandharya Vamahabaho Mushitosmi Mahatmabhi. This is the most important point here. In this verse. Mushitosmi Mahatmabhi. What's the meaning of this? The meaning of this verse. The 37th verse. Sanjaya said. He is speaking to Yudhishtira. Replying to Yudhishtira's enquiry. My dear descendant of the Kuru dynasty. Nandana. He is addressing Yudhishtira in that way. I have no information of the determination of your two uncles and Gandhari. I have no information. I do not know where they have gone. O King. I have been cheated by those great souls. Mushitosmi. Means I have been cheated. By whom? By those great souls. Mahatmabhi. This is Sanjaya's attitude towards Dhritarashtra, Vidura, and Gandhari. Elders. This is our culture. Regardless of what they may do or not do. The elders are to be offered proper respect by the younger ones. This is the culture. So.

---
### devotion, service, satisfaction of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=577
**Verse References:** BG 18.46
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The highest perfection of life is to satisfy the Lord by one's occupational duty."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain Srila Prabhupada's purport, addressing the apparent contradiction of great souls 'cheating' others. He clarifies that such actions are performed for a greater cause, citing examples where even Lord Krishna advised Yudhisthira to speak untruth for a higher purpose. The speaker emphasizes that satisfying the Lord is the ultimate goal and the highest perfection of life, referencing the Bhagavad Gita and Bhagavatam.

Sanjaya is addressing to Yudhishtira that I have been cheated by them because they never informed me where they are going. I was taking care of them, especially Dhritarashtra and Gandhari. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport that great souls cheat others may be astonishing to know. If they are great souls, why should they cheat? So Srila Prabhupada explains clearly in this purport, kindly hear attentively, that great souls cheat others may be astonishing to know, but it is a fact that great souls cheat others for a great cause. They have a solid reason to cheat others under very very very very special circumstances, rarest of rare cases. Such great souls, Mahatma, who are basically devotees, they sometimes for a greater cause, they do cheat others. So it is explained here by Srila Prabhupada. It is said that Lord Krishna also advised Yudhishtira only for Dronacharya. Yudhishtira is the greatest moralist of his time, but he was told by Krishna, tell a lie to Dronacharya, his own teacher, tell a lie. And it was also for a great cause. The Lord wanted it, therefore it was a great cause. Whatever the Lord wants, that is the greatest thing one can do. Satisfaction of the Lord is a criterion of one who is bona fide and the highest perfection of life is to satisfy the Lord by one's occupational duty. That is the verdict of Gita and Bhagavad. Both the Bhagavad Gita and Bhagavatam describe this.

---
### devotion, duty, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=731
**Verse References:** BG 18.46, SB 1.2.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all pervading, man can in the performance of his own duty attain perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita 18.46, explaining that perfection is attained by worshipping the Lord, who is the source of all beings, through the performance of one's prescribed duties. He then references the Bhagavatam, stating that the highest perfection is to please the Personality of Godhead, Lord Hari.

Where is it said in the Gita that the highest perfection of life is to satisfy the Supreme Lord by one's occupational duty? In the 18th chapter, the 46th verse says. Is the meaning of this verse: By worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all pervading, man can in the performance of his own duty attain perfection. So one attains perfection by worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all pervading, while a person performs his own duties as prescribed in the Shastra. One can please the Lord in this way. In the Bhagavatam it is said. It is therefore concluded that the highest perfection one can achieve by discharging the duties prescribed for one's own occupation according to caste divisions and orders of life is to please the personality of Godhead. Highest perfection, Sam Siddhi, is Haritoshanam. Satisfy or please Lord Hari.

---
### confidentiality, devotion, example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TgJFmDw9S5k?t=827
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To satisfy the Lord, anything is good, for it is in relation with the absolute truth."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that Dhritarashtra, Vidura, and Gandhari did not disclose their plans to Sanjaya because their actions were confidential and aimed at pleasing the Lord. He provides further examples of Sanatana Goswami and Raghunath Das Goswami 'cheating' to join Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, emphasizing that actions done to satisfy the Lord are aligned with the absolute truth. The lecture ends with gratitude and the Hare Krishna mantra.

So therefore Prabhupada says Dhritarashtra and Vidura, followed by Gandhari, did not disclose their determination to Sanjaya, although he was constantly with Dhritarashtra as his personal assistant. Sanjaya never thought that Dhritarashtra could perform any act without consulting him. But Dhritarashtra's going away from home was so confidential that it could not be disclosed even to Sanjaya. And Srila Prabhupada gives more examples. Sanatana Goswami also cheated the keeper of the prison house than Mahaprabhu. Sanatana was trying to get relief from the duties in the royal palace of the King Nawab Hussain Shah of Bengal. He was the minister. So he cheated the prison keeper and went away to join Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Raghunath Das Goswami also cheated his priest and left home to join Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So to satisfy the Lord, anything is good, for it is in relation with the absolute truth. That's the conclusion. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.38-39 | 16-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Narada, Divine Arrangement, Srimad Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=54
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.38
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, the Lord, unknowingly, arranged that Narada inform Yudhishthira about the whereabouts of Dhritarashtra and Gandhari and Vidura."
**Summary:** This segment introduces verse 38 of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining Narada's appearance and the Lord's arrangement for him to inform Yudhishthira about the whereabouts of Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, and Vidura.

In this session, we will be discussing two verses, the 38th and the 39th verses of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 38th verse is as follows: Athar Jagama Bhagavan Narada Sahatumburuho Pratyutthaya Abhivadhyaha Sanujo Abhyarchayan Munim. The meaning of this verse is, while Sanjaya was thus speaking, Shri Narada, the powerful devotee of the Lord, appeared on the scene carrying his tumburu. Maharaj Yudhishthira and his brothers received him properly by getting up from their seats and offering obeisances. So, this is the Lord's arrangement. When Yudhishthira is puzzled as to where his uncles and aunt have gone, Sanjaya is also unaware. So, the Lord, unknowingly, arranged that Narada inform Yudhishthira about the whereabouts of Dhritarashtra and Gandhari and Vidura.

---
### Devotee, Bhagavan, Preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=128
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The way of devotional service is not only to worship the Supreme Lord with all devotion, but also to worship the pure devotee spiritual master, the Acharya, on the same level as the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains why Narada is sometimes referred to as Bhagavan, emphasizing the importance of honoring pure devotees and Acharyas who dedicate themselves to preaching the glories of the Lord and converting non-devotees.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, Devarishi Narada is described herein as Bhagavan. Why? Why is he called Bhagavan? Is he Bhagavan? He is not Bhagavan. Krishna is Bhagavan. The Supreme Personality of God is Krishna. Krishna is Bhagavan. So, why is Devarishi Narada described as Bhagavan? Prabhupada explains it is because he is the most confidential devotee of the Lord. In rare cases, very exalted devotees who are most confidential devotees of the Lord are described, addressed as Bhagavan. So, Srila Prabhupada further says, The Lord and his very confidential devotees are treated on the same level by those who are actually engaged in the loving service of the Lord. The way of devotional service is not only to worship the Supreme Lord with all devotion, but also to worship the pure devotee spiritual master, the Acharya, on the same level as the Lord, because such Acharyas and pure devotees are very, very dear to the Lord. Such confidential devotees of the Lord are very much dear to the Lord because they travel everywhere to preach the glories of the Lord in different capacities and try their utmost to convert the non-devotees of the Lord into devotees in order to bring them to the platform of sanity. This material world is filled with non-devotees. And to convert these non-devotees into devotees, pure devotees like Narada travel and preach and try to convert non-devotees into devotees.

---
### Devotion, Preaching, Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=253
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's very interesting to note that actually a living being cannot be a non-devotee of the Lord because of his constitutional position."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the constitutional position of living beings as devotees of the Lord and emphasizes that those who preach to non-devotees and deliver them to the spiritual world render the best service.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains, it's very interesting to note that actually a living being cannot be a non-devotee of the Lord because of his constitutional position. What is our constitutional position? We are part and parcel of the Lord. We belong to the Lord. We are all devotees of the Lord. But, when one becomes a non-devotee, a non-believer, it is to be understood that the person concerned is not in a sound condition of life. So, if somebody is a non-devotee, that is not the perfect condition or sound condition of the life of such a non-devotee. The confidential devotees of the Lord treat such illusioned living beings and therefore they are most pleasing in the eyes of the Lord. There are so many devotees rendering different kinds of service to the Lord. Among all of them, who is rendering the best service or the topmost service? It is those who are engaged in preaching to the non-devotees and delivering such non-devotees to the spiritual world.

---
### Preaching, Respect, Pure Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=345
**Verse References:** SB 10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such personalities as Narada must be offered all due respects like those offered to the personality of Godhead Himself."
**Summary:** This segment highlights the importance of preaching, especially by pure devotees, and emphasizes that personalities like Narada should be respected as much as the Lord Himself, according to the shastras.

They are the best devotees. They are described in the Bhagavatam 10th canto in the prayers offered by the gopis to Krishna that bhuvigranantiye bhuridājana. Such people who, devotees, pure devotees, who preach the glories of the Lord among the non-devotees, they are actually the most munificent. Bhuridājana. They are very, very, very generous. The Lord says in the Bhagavad Gita that no one is dearer to Him than one who actually preaches the glories of the Lord to convert the non-believers and non-devotees. So the preaching is very, very much necessary and it is powerful and effective when it is done by pure devotees. Such personalities as Narada must be offered all due respects like those offered to the personality of Godhead Himself. sākṣād haritvena samasta-śāstrair uktas tathā bhāvyata eva sattvihī. The pure devotees are to be treated on the same level as the Lord. That is the verdict of the śāstras.

---
### Yudhishthira, Narada, Inquiry
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=445
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.39
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira along with his noble brothers were examples for others in receiving a pure devotee of the Lord like Narada who had no other business save and except singing the glories of the Lord along with his veena a musical stringed instrument."
**Summary:** This segment describes how Maharaj Yudhishthira and his brothers exemplify how to receive a pure devotee like Narada and introduces verse 39, where Yudhishthira inquires about the whereabouts of his uncles and aunt.

Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira along with his noble brothers were examples for others in receiving a pure devotee of the Lord like Narada who had no other business save and except singing the glories of the Lord along with his veena a musical stringed instrument. The 39th verse, Yudhiṣṭhira vāca. Now Yudhiṣṭhira is going to inquire from Narada. Narada is right there in the palace of Yudhiṣṭhira. Yudhiṣṭhira vāca nāham veda-gatiṁ pitro bhagavan kva-gatāv itaha ambā vā hata-putrārtha kva-gatā ca tapasvini. How much Yudhiṣṭhira is aggrieved because his uncle and aunt are missing. The translation of this verse, 39th verse, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira said O godly personality I do not know where my two uncles have gone nor can I find out my ascetic aunt who is grief-stricken by the loss of all her sons. Yudhiṣṭhira is now inquiring from Narada.

---
### Gandhari, Asceticism, Tolerance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=532
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Gandhari's personality is very much glorified in the Mahabharata because she was living the life of an ascetic even though she was living in a palace."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on Gandhari's character, highlighting her asceticism, tolerance, and devotion, even amidst palace life and great suffering.

So, in the purport Śrīla Prabhupāda explains Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira as a good soul and devotee of the lord was always conscious of the great loss of his aunt and her sufferings as an ascetic. So, Gandhari is an exceptional personality described in the history called Mahabharata. Gandhari's personality is very much glorified in the Mahabharata because she was living the life of an ascetic even though she was living in a palace. Because her husband was blind, Gandhari blindfolded herself voluntarily. This is called a chaste wife, an obedient wife, a devoted wife. And after the great loss of her sons, in the battle of Kurukshetra, Gandhari was living like an ascetic, tapasvini. Particularly, Yudhiṣṭhira is referring to Gandhari as tapasvini. And what is the characteristic of a tapasvini or ascetic? An ascetic is never disturbed by all kinds of sufferings. So, suffering is a part of material existence which is unavoidable even for great devotees, even for pious people, even for anybody who is willing to undergo the austerities to detach themselves from their body. For such people, any difficulty is tolerable.

---
### Gandhari, Tolerance, Devotional Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=686
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the material world, while we have taken up the path of devotional service, we have declared a war against Maya."
**Summary:** This segment continues to praise Gandhari's tolerance and explains that difficulties are expected on the path of devotional service, as it is a war against Maya, and one must tolerate these inconveniences to progress spiritually.

Queen Gandhari is a typical example of an ascetic because of her marvelous character in many trying situations. Gandhari has always displayed this tolerance because she was kind to Dhritarashtra who was plotting and planning to kill the Pandavas. Gandhari was not supporting her husband. She was giving him good counsel. But, the Kauravas would not listen to Gandhari nor would they listen to other great personalities. So, this is a demoniac nature. They will not listen to others if they think that is not so favorable. So, Queen Gandhari is a typical example of an ascetic because of her marvelous character in many trying situations. So, they teach us by their example these great personalities, great devotees, very, very tolerant. Srila Prabhupada says in the material world, while we have taken up the path of devotional service, we have declared a war against Maya. So, in a battle between Maya and people, there will be difficulties, there will be dangers, there will be some inconveniences, but one has to tolerate all of them in order to actually practice spiritual life and make progress to go back home, back to Godhead.

---
### Gandhari, Ideal Woman, Example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_1G4DBLZffM?t=804
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Gandhari is described as she was an ideal woman as mother, wife, and ascetic and in the history of the world such character in a woman is rarely found."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing Gandhari's exemplary qualities as a mother, wife, and ascetic, making her a rare and ideal woman whose character is rarely found in the world.

It is not some voluntary torture of the body or voluntarily denying something which is nicely enjoyable. No, that is not this proper understanding. So, Gandhari is described as she was an ideal woman as mother, wife, and ascetic and in the history of the world such character in a woman is rarely found. Gandhari has been glorified a lot in the Mahabharata and even in the Bhagavata as a great personality. So, she is setting a very good example for tapas we need. Even though her husband was not fair to the Pandavas, but she was always fair herself and she was requesting her husband to be fair to the Pandavas. She never approved the different nefarious activities of Duryodhana, of Dushasana, of Dhritarashtra, she never approved. And she was always advising them to refrain from giving trouble to the Pandavas. So, she is a marvelous personality and such character in a woman is rarely found in the world. She is a very rare ideal woman to be found very rarely. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Narada Muni, Dhritarashtra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=53
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.40
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thus addressed the godly personality Devarshi Narada, greatest of the philosopher devotees began to speak."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses to be discussed, SB 1.13.40 and 41, explaining the context of Dhritarashtra leaving the palace and Narada's appearance.

In this session we will be discussing from the 40th and the 41st verses of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 40th verse is as follows: Karanadhara Ivaapare Bhagavan Paradarsha Kaha Athababhase Bhagavan Narado Munisattamaha. The meaning of this verse is: You are like a captain of a ship in a great ocean and you can direct us to our destination. Thus addressed the godly personality Devarshi Narada, greatest of the philosopher devotees began to speak. So the background for this 40th verse is that Dhritarashtra has left the palace after hearing the strong instructions of Vidura.

---
### Dhritarashtra, Yudhishthira, Worry, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=103
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yudhishthira wanted to take care of his uncle and aunt that they should be comforted when they have undergone such a huge loss in the last days."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the story, describing Yudhishthira's concern for Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, and his worry about their disappearance, leading to Narada's arrival.

Vidura, Dhritarashtra and Gandhari all have left the palace. And Yudhishthira the next day morning is doing his daily morning duties as a pious householder. So he went to pay respects to Dhritarashtra and he didn't find Dhritarashtra in his usual place. Then immediately he inquired from Sanjaya, Dhritarashtra's secretary, 'Where is Dhritarashtra, my uncle?' So Sanjaya said, 'I don't know. I don't know where he's gone. I have no idea.' Then Yudhishthira began to worry whether because Dhritarashtra and Gandhari had lost all their sons and they were very much aggrieved because we were all here, aggrieved because of the great loss in the battle of Kurukshetra all their 100 sons were killed and their grandsons sons and grandsons everyone was killed in the battle of Kurukshetra so because he was so very much aggrieved did he leave the palace to go and drown himself in the river Ganga that was what Yudhishthira was so much concerned worried in fact Yudhishthira wanted to take care of his uncle and aunt that they should be comforted when they have undergone such a huge loss in the last days so he was taking good care actually but suddenly when they disappeared he began to worry what happened why did they where did they go and where have they gone so as he was worrying suddenly Narada appeared in the palace of Yudhishthira.

---
### Guru, Guidance, Analogy, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=241
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Only a captain who knows how to navigate the ship based on of course nowadays they have modern equipment but formerly they used to have maps and using the maps and the compass the direction which they should go they would actually steer the ship in the ocean."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Yudhishthira's description of Narada as a captain of a ship, using the analogy of a ship lost at sea needing a skilled navigator to reach its destination.

As soon as Narada came Yudhishthira began to inquire from Narada and he explained that my uncles and aunt have gone I do not know where they have gone kindly tell me what should I do now so Yudhishthira is describing Narada as a captain of a ship in a great ocean in this analogy is very important for us to understand that if somebody is I'm going in a ship and is in the middle of the coast the ocean if every side they look they'll only see water they'll see only water there'll be no sight of any land in the middle of the great ocean big ocean there will be no sight of any land then only the captain who knows how to navigate the ship based on of course nowadays they have modern equipment but formerly they used to have maps and using the maps and the compass the direction which they should go they would actually steer the ship in the ocean and take it from one place where they start shore of the ocean one place where they start shore of the ocean one particular land mark to another country or another place in the other side of the ocean so only a captain side of the ocean who's experienced who has got these maps who knows how to how to use the maps to steer the ship only such a person can help to reach the destination, the designated place they are supposed to go, they wish to go, they desire to go. Similarly, Yudhishthira is now lost. He doesn't know where his uncle and aunt are.

### Supreme Lord, Spiritual, Realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One has to first of all become spiritually situated to be able to see or perceive or realize what is spirit."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on Narada, explaining that the greatest philosophers are those who have seen the Personality of Godhead and surrendered to Him, emphasizing the need for spiritual realization to perceive the Supreme Spirit.

So in the purport Srila Prabhupada says something about Narada. There are different types of philosophers and the greatest of all of them are those who have seen the personality of Godhead and have surrendered themselves in the transcendental loving service of the Lord. And to see the Supreme Lord is not an ordinary thing. The Lord is completely spiritual. And not only is he a spiritual personality, but he is also the unlimited Supreme personality. So one has to first of all become spiritually situated to be able to see or perceive or realize what is spirit. And then go beyond this, beyond perceiving spirit, to be able to perceive or see the Supreme Spirit, the unlimited Supreme Lord. Both of these are distinct qualifications. Both of these require distinct qualifications. It's not that anybody and everybody can just either see spirit or ultimately be able to see even the Supreme Spirit.

---
### Surrender, Devotional Service, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=558
**Verse References:** BG 4.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This surrender is not for any material purpose. It is not for any other purpose other than to render loving devotional service to the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker references the Bhagavad Gita, explaining the process of surrendering to the Supreme Lord in transcendental loving service, emphasizing that this surrender is purely for devotional purposes.

That is the personality of Godhead. And after seeing the Supreme Lord, one has to surrender in transcendental loving service. This is what Krishna explains in the Bhagavad Gita. He says, tad viddhi pranipatena pariprasnena sevaya upadekshanti te jnanam jnaninah tatva-darshinah. Tatva-darshinah means one who has seen the Supreme Personality of Godhead. And jnaninah, one is full of knowledge about who is the Supreme Lord and what is the relationship, what is the relationship of everyone with the Supreme Lord, and how one can situate himself or herself in the proper position of one's relationship by surrendering to the Supreme Lord in transcendental loving service. This surrender is not for any material purpose. It is not for any other purpose other than to render loving devotional service to the Lord.

---
### Narada Muni, Devotee, Preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=638
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Narada Muni has now come to actually help Yudhishthira understand the destination or the future of his uncles and aunt."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Narada Muni's realization of the Lord and his subsequent mission to help others escape material existence, highlighting his role in assisting Yudhishthira.

That is our actual constitutional position. So Narada Muni is one such personality who has seen the Supreme Personality of Godhead. It is described in the earlier chapter how Narada had darshan of the Lord. Then he realized the Lord. Then he became situated as a totally surrendered devotee of the Lord. And after situating himself in that way, he is simply traveling and preaching to everyone to help them to get out of this material existence and reinstate themselves in their original position as pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. So Narada Muni has now come to actually help Yudhishthira understand the destination or the future of his uncles and aunt. So among all such pure devotees of the Lord, because Lord has many pure devotees like Narada, Devarishi Narada is the chief. Narada Munisattamaha. And therefore he has been described herein as the greatest of all philosopher devotees.

---
### Vedanta, Philosophy, Realization, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mXZ2vxA9aYA?t=732
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the goal of all Vedas, Vedic knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the qualifications of a philosopher devotee, emphasizing the importance of studying Vedanta philosophy from a bona fide spiritual master and realizing the conclusion of the Vedas, which is Krishna.

Now even among devotees there are different kinds of devotees. So who is a philosopher devotee? That Prabhupada explains, unless one has become a sufficiently learned philosopher by hearing the Vedanta philosophy from a bona fide spiritual master, one cannot be a learned philosopher devotee. So to know the philosophy of Vedanta. Vedanta means the conclusion of the Vedas. And what is the conclusion of the Vedas? Vedaischa sarvair ahameva vedyam. Krishna is the goal of all Vedas, Vedic knowledge. So Narada is such a a devotee who has become sufficiently learned philosopher by hearing the Vedanta philosophy from the Bhaktivedantas in his previous life. And he has become realized. He followed the instructions to realize that subject matter after hearing. And he had darshan of the Lord. And he became situated in his transcendental relationship as the pure devotee engaged in transcendental loving service of the Lord.

And then one has to become learned in the science of Krishna consciousness. Science of Bhakti. And one has to become renounced. Completely free from all material attachments. 100% pure. That's called 100% pure. Without these qualifications one cannot be a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. And such a pure devotee is qualified to help anybody cross this ocean of ignorance and reach the destination of the shelter of the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna. And it is also explained here by Prabhupada.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.41 | 18-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### control of the Supreme Lord, subordination, universal order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=51
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.41
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every living being either in this material world or in the spiritual world is under the control of the Supreme Lord, the Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** Narada Muni explains to Yudhishthira the general principle that all living beings are under the control of the Supreme Lord, Krishna, who is the ultimate controller.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 41st verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Narada Vacha Ma Kanchana Shucho Rajan Yadishvara Vasham Jagat. Loka Sapala Yasyeme Vahanti Balim Eeshituhu. Sa Samyunakti Bhutani Sa Eva Vinuyak Viyunakti Cha. The meaning of this verse is: Shri Narada said, O pious king, do not lament for anyone, for everyone is under the control of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, all living beings and their leaders carry on worship to be well protected. It is He only who brings them together and disperses them. So, Shri La Pravapada explains this very important point in the purport elaborately. What Narada Muni is telling as a reply to Yudhishthira. Yudhishthira is concerned, where are my uncles and aunt gone? So, Narada before directly telling where have they gone, he is first explaining the general principle, which all of us have to understand. Every living being either in this material world or in the spiritual world is under the control of the Supreme Lord, the Personality of Godhead. Now, why is Krishna called the Supreme Lord? Because Lord means controller, and Supreme means He is the ultimate controller of everyone. So, He is completely controlling everyone in the material world and in the spiritual world. Universally, everywhere He is controlled.

---
### control of the Supreme Lord, obedience, constitutional position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=156
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The constitutional position of the living being is subordination under the control of the Lord."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada clarifies that everyone, from Brahma to the most insignificant creature, abides by the Lord's order, either voluntarily (devotees) or by force (demons, atheists).

Pravapada further clarifies this. Beginning from Brahmaji, the leader of this universe, down to the insignificant, all are abiding by the order of the Supreme Lord. Brahma is the first created being in this universe. There are millions of planets in each universe, and in each planet there are millions of living entities, all of them beginning from Brahma, the topmost living being in this universe, and down to the most insignificant creature, everyone is abiding by the order of the Supreme Lord. The Supreme Lord's control is everywhere. And when it is said everyone is abiding by the order, not everyone is abiding by the order of the Lord voluntarily. It is only the devotees and the pious people, the followers of the Vedic injunctions or the scriptural directions given by God, only they are actually voluntarily following the order of the Supreme Lord. The others, demons, atheists, and agnostics, materialists, rebellious souls, all of them are forced to obey the order of the Supreme Lord. Thus, the constitutional position of the living being is subordination under the control of the Lord. Everyone is subordinate to the Lord. The Lord is the only supreme personality. He is the supreme master. Everyone is under His control. Ekala, Ishvara, Krishna are Sababhrithya. This is explained in the Chaitanya Charitamatha. Krishna is the only Ishvara, only control, ultimately.

---
### constitutional position, rebellion, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=284
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The foolish living being, especially man, artificially rebels against the law of the supreme God, and thus becomes chastised as an asura or law breaker."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that our constitutional position is to be subordinate to Krishna, who is the supreme controller. Foolish living beings rebel against this and are punished by the laws of nature, similar to how traffic police enforce traffic laws.

Constitutional position means the way we are made, the way we are constituted, the way we are eternally, is that we're subordinate. And the way Krishna is, what is Krishna's constitutional position? It is to always control, always be the master, the Lord, the supreme. And we are always subordinate, eternally we are subordinate to the supreme Lord Krishna. The foolish living being, especially man, artificially rebels against the law of the supreme God, the law of the supreme, and thus becomes chastised as an asura or law breaker when laws are operating and there's an enforcing authority. Anybody who breaks the law is immediately punished by the law enforcing authority, just like there is traffic police patrolling and anybody violates the traffic laws, immediately the traffic police will chastise, will actually stop the lawbreaker and correct the lawbreaker. So similarly, even in the whole creation, there is the law of the supreme that is operating and anybody who rebels against the law is chastised, is punished by the supreme Lord.

---
### control of the Supreme Lord, agents of the Lord, powerful figures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A living being is placed in a particular position by the order of the supreme lord and is again shifted from that place by the order of the supreme lord or his authorized agents."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Supreme Lord works through authorized agents like Brahma, Shiva, and even historical figures like Napoleon and Gandhi, to place and remove individuals from positions of power.

Now Sri La Prabhupada explains how does it work actually. In our practical experience, we don't see any God coming and chastising us. If at all we are chastised, it's by the lawmakers in our country or in our institutions. We don't see any God. Where is God? That's what an atheist or a law breaker may question in this way. So Prabhupada says a living being is placed in a particular position by the order of the supreme lord and is again shifted from that place by the order of the supreme lord or his authorized agents. We are all placed in some particular position and removed from that position by whom? By the order of Krishna or His authorized agents. So Prabhupada lists some names by which you can get an idea what is the extent of the control of the supreme lord: Brahma, Shiva, Brahma and Shiva, the greatest of demigods in the entire creation. Indra, Chandra, they are also very powerful demigods but subordinate to Brahma and Shiva. Even they, Maharaj Yudhishthira, even Maharaj Yudhishthira, he is under the control of the Lord. Or in modern history, Napoleon, Akbar, Alexander, Gandhi, Subhash, and Nehru, all are servants of the Lord. All these names mentioned are powerful persons of the Lord. All these names mentioned are powerful persons of the Lord. And they are placed in and they moved from their respective position by the supreme will of the Lord. Nobody is exception to the supreme. They rose to some very powerful positions in their own time, but they were placed in that powerful position by the Supreme Lord and they were also removed. They cannot remain forever. Nobody can remain forever in their position. They were also removed by the will of the Lord. None of them is independent.

---
### independence of Krishna, suffering, rebellious souls
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=554
**Verse References:** SB 1.1.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is only the rebellious souls who suffer."
**Summary:** Krishna is supremely independent, while everyone else is subject to the laws of material nature. Miseries are the enforcement of the Supreme Lord's authority on rebellious souls.

Nobody is independent. Only Krishna is supremely independent. There is this kind of the Bhagavatam in the beginning in the first canto, first chapter, first words: swarat janma dhyasya tyaha anvayat itirthas chartheshu abhigya swarat. Swarat means Krishna is supremely completely independent. Even though such men or leaders rebel so as not to recognize the supremacy of the Lord, they are put under still more rigorous laws of the material world by different miseries. Why do miseries come? They are enforcement of the authority of the Supreme Lord on lawbreakers, on rebellious souls. It is only the rebellious souls who suffer. It is only the rebellious souls who suffer.

---
### suffering, ignorance, past misdeeds
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=614
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The foolish man doesn't realize who is imposing miseries upon him."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a baby suffering from an ant bite in its diaper to illustrate how we suffer due to our past misdeeds, often without understanding the cause.

Nobody else who is fully obedient will have to suffer in this material world. Only the foolish man therefore says that there is no God. Foolish people, they say where is God? Who is God? There's no God. But the foolish man doesn't realize who is imposing miseries upon him. Whenever he suffers, right from the time he is placed in the womb of a particular mother. Inside the womb he has suffered so much and he forgets that. Everyone who enters the womb of a particular mother, what suffering they undergo, they forget after they come out of the womb because they get some relief and they say better don't remember that traumatic experience, better forget it. And then through their growing up, childhood, as a child, so many different types of sufferings. Even the rich man's child, the most cared for child also has to suffer. Supposing there an ant biting a child in the diaper, then how does the child express itself to its mother or nurse or caretaker? We had the best of people taking care, but how does the child express? The child simply is helpless in expressing. It can only cry. Now when the child cries, the mother or the caretaker or the nurse cannot understand why the child is crying. Of course, the mother or child caretaker can make out the child is having some discomfort. Okay, they'll try to comfort the child. Maybe they'll think it's hungry so they'll try to feed it. So feeding that will not stop the crying because the ant is biting in the diaper. And then they will try to make out the child is crying and then they will try to play with the child, try to give the child some air, maybe it's feeling suffocation or maybe it is feeling too cold, whatever different ways they speculate and try to comfort the child. But how will they understand the child is crying specifically because an ant is biting in the diaper? How will they figure that? They cannot. So this is a misery that the child has to suffer because of its own past misdeeds, own past misdeeds.

---
### suffering, surrender, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h5FYjyqVpKQ?t=776
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Intelligent people simply understand that we have to surrender to Krishna, the Supreme Lord, and we have to abide by His laws, orders, and pray to Him to get out of this entanglement of material existence."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that suffering is inevitable in the material world, and the only way to overcome it is to surrender to Krishna and abide by His laws, as explained in the Bhagavad-gita.

It's not very easy for the child to get relief from that. So only till that quota of suffering is given to that living entity in that situation, in that circumstance, till then it has to suffer. It has to cry and cry and cry and cry and then by the will of the Lord somehow they will figure out. Let's say it passes urine then to change the diaper. When they remove the diaper, oh they'll see oh there was a, they may or may not notice. Anyway with the diaper being removed and disposed the ant is also gone, the child gets relief. Or they notice the ant and then they understand the cause and then they remove that ant. So like that it is even those who are cared for by different material methods, there is no guarantee of freedom from miseries even those who are cared for. So therefore this world, everybody is by the Lord. Everybody is placed in a particular position and removed from that position by the will of the Lord, by His superior arrangement and those are called the laws of God and everybody is forced to abide by the laws. So intelligent people simply understand that we have to surrender to Krishna, the Supreme Lord, and we have to abide by His laws, orders, and pray to Him to get out of this entanglement of material existence. Only He can help us cross over this ocean of material existence. Daivihesha gunamayi mamma maya duratyaya ma mevaye prapadyante maya metham tarantite. In simple one verse in a simple way Krishna says in the Bhagavad-gita, you cannot overcome the stringent laws, this material nature which are the laws of Krishna only simply by your own methods and arrangements and intelligence and and and and all kinds of remedies, you cannot. But if you surrender to Krishna, Krishna will easily help you to cross over and that is what will be explained by Narada in the next verse. I'll stop here. Thank you very much.

---
