### divine law, control, vedic injunctions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=37
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.42
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Narad Muni is explaining how we are all controlled by the Supreme Lord through the Vedic injunctions."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.13.42) and explains that it discusses how all beings are controlled by the Supreme Lord through Vedic injunctions, like a cow controlled by a rope through its nose.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchaiva Narottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 42nd verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Yatha Gavo Nasiprotha Tantyam Baddhashchadamabhi Vaktantyam Namabhir Baddha Vahanti Balim Eeshituhu. The meaning of this verse is, as a cow bound through the nose by a long rope is conditioned, so also human beings are bound by different Vedic injunctions and are conditioned to obey the orders of the Supreme. Narad Muni is explaining how we are all controlled by the Supreme Lord through the Vedic injunctions.

---
### divine law, free will, supreme authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=90
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's laws nobody can neglect or evade or avoid."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that no one is truly free from the severe laws of the Lord, contrasting them with man-made laws that can be evaded. He stresses the impossibility of neglecting or evading Krishna's laws.

Now how does it work? Prabhupada explains this in the purport. Every living being whether a man or an animal or a bird thinks that he is free by himself, but actually no one is free from the severe laws of the Lord. This is the reality. This is the truth. Nobody is free. Nobody is independent. The laws of the Lord are said to be severe because they cannot be disobeyed in any circumstance. The man-made laws may be evaded by cunning outlaws, but in the codes of the Supreme Lawmaker there is not the slightest possibility of neglecting the laws. So Prabhupada is helping us distinguish between man-made laws, however stringently enforced or however powerful the garment may be or the law enforcing mechanism. There is a difference. Man-made laws are possible to be evaded by somebody who is cunning or more intelligent or is more powerful than the enforcing authority itself. That is possible. But in the codes of the Supreme, the Supreme Lawmaker there is not the slightest possibility of neglecting the laws. Krishna's laws nobody can neglect or evade or avoid.

---
### divine law, natural disasters, supreme authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=191
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "A slight change in the course of God-made law can bring about a massive danger to be faced by the law-breaker."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of a tsunami to illustrate the consequences of violating God's laws, emphasizing that even the most powerful individuals are helpless against natural calamities.

A slight change in the course of God-made law can bring about a massive danger to be faced by the law-breaker. We have experienced one, tsunami, and even the most powerful persons in this earth planet we are living in. When they have to face such calamities, natural calamities, then there is no question of anybody being powerful, or wealthy, or influential, or well-protected by some material arrangement. All that doesn't work. None of those things work. Such laws of the Supreme are generally known as the codes of religion under different conditions.

---
### religion, divine law, obedience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=250
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Namely, obey the orders of the Supreme God, that is the codes of religion."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the principle of religion is the same everywhere: to obey the orders of the Supreme God, and that authorized religious systems are given to help human beings obey God's laws for protection.

Codes of religion. God and God's laws as codes of religion, religious principles. But one should not think, oh, there are so many different religions, and so many different scriptures. So, is there any commonality? Prabhupada says, but the principle of religion everywhere is one and the same. Namely, obey the orders of the Supreme God, that is the codes of religion. Every scripture describes this. Authentic, bona fide scriptures we are talking about. We are not talking about some concocted religious systems. No. The authorized religious systems, they are given for the human beings to obey the laws of God, and thereby be under the proper protection, and safe, protection of the Supreme Lord only. That is the condition of material existence. Material existence means, we have to obey the codes of religion, which is the orders of the Supreme Lord.

---
### conditioned life, material nature, control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=339
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our body actually is controlled. It is controlled by material nature who is the agency of God's control in this material world, through this material body."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how living beings in the material world have taken the risk of conditioned life by their own choice, and uses the example of the body to illustrate that we are not ultimately in complete control, but rather controlled by material nature as an agency of God.

All living beings in the material world have taken up the risk of conditioned life by their own selection. Now, how is it that somebody comes under a particular set of laws? In this material world, the creation itself is made like that. For example, this body that I have got, I think that I am the controller of my body. But actually the reality is, I am not the real ultimate controller of the body. Though I have some degree of control, like I can lift my hand, okay, that is given to you, that control is given to you, limited control is given to you. I can digest the food I eat normally. But if I eat something which cannot be digested, or if I overeat, then I have to suffer indigestion, the consequences of indigestion. So, where is my control? Can I digest anything and everything, and whatever quantity I eat? No. So, can I keep on working without taking rest when the body becomes fatigued? No. When there is an infection that attacks me, then some germs invade my body, then I have to suffer the consequences of that infection unless it is treated. I cannot be living a normal, healthy life. I have to suffer the symptoms of that infection. So, like that, we are not ultimately under complete control of our body. Our body actually is controlled. It is controlled by material nature who is the agency of God's control in this material world, through this material body.

---
### material body, suffering, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=478
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are taking up the risk of conditioned life by our own selection, because we want to enjoy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that every created being has a material body, and suffering arises because we desire concocted enjoyment and violate God's laws, leading to punishment through the body by the agency of material nature (Durga Devi).

Now, who doesn't have a material body? Every created being has a material body. Every created being. Brahma, beginning from Brahma, the first created being, the topmost devata, up to the insignificant creatures, everyone has a material body. We don't have any suffering. There is no connection between the material body and the soul except that this body is given to us simply because we desire some concocted enjoyment. And we violate the laws and we are also given the punishment through this body only, by the agency of God, called material nature, Durga Devi. So, we are taking up the risk of conditioned life by our own selection, because we want to enjoy. We violate the laws and we come under the enforcement, law enforcement authority, forcing us to accept this body, so that we will be given suitable punishment through this body. We thus become entrapped by the laws of material nature. We are trapped, because even a slight violation, we have to suffer the consequences of that violation.

---

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The only way to get out of the entanglement is to agree to obey the Supreme."
**Summary:** The speaker states that the only way to escape entanglement in material nature is to obey the Supreme Lord, and that understanding this requires spiritual intelligence, which is awakened through spiritual instruction, practice, and guidance from scriptures and a spiritual master.

The only way to get out of the entanglement is to agree to obey the Supreme. Actually, it is very simple. How do I get out of this entanglement? First of all, to understand that I am entangled itself requires some a particular type of intelligence. It is called spiritual intelligence. It is different from material intelligence. Please try to understand this point. Material intelligence is for ordinary material subject matters. But spiritual intelligence is something else. It is only possible to exercise our spirit. Everybody has got spiritual intelligence because it is spirit soul. Everyone, every living being is spirit soul. But that spiritual intelligence is awakened and it is trained up to be utilized by spiritual instruction, by spiritual practice, by spiritual master, by spiritual direction given in the scriptures. So only that can awaken our spiritual intelligence. Spiritual intelligence is different. Like foolish people. Foolish people means they say there is no God. I don't believe in religion. Such foolish people, they don't, they are unable to awaken their spiritual intelligence because they don't follow the scriptural directions. They don't have any spiritual practices. They don't obey the order of the Supreme Lord. And so therefore they have to suffer.

---
### spiritual ignorance, suffering, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=692
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Those who have awakened their spiritual intelligence by properly following the laws of God. They are the wisest people in the whole creation."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts those who are spiritually ignorant, who suffer regardless of their material situation, with those who have awakened their spiritual intelligence by following God's laws, who are the wisest and are able to perceive God's hand in everything.

They suffer in ignorance, spiritual ignorance. They might be materially very qualified or unqualified. It doesn't matter. As long as one is spiritually ignorant, it doesn't matter what is their material situation or material position. Whether they are wealthy or they are poor. Whether they are old or young. Whether they are powerful or not so powerful. Materially, whatever their situation, if they are spiritually ignorant, they have to suffer. Suffer in ignorance. Because they violate and they don't awaken their spiritual intelligence. Those who have awakened their spiritual intelligence by properly following the laws of God. They are the wisest people in the whole creation. They are called devotees of the Supreme Lord. And very easily they understand how the laws are working. They are able to see the hand of God in every action they do. Everywhere. Everyone is under the control of God. So this requires spiritual intelligence to be able to understand, to be able to perceive, to be able to to be able to actually make a make a a very intelligent decision. How to get out of this forced suffering.

---
### surrender, illusion, designations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/w6zt4zxOjao?t=798
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Surrender to the Supreme Lord and agree to abide by His orders is the way to get out."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that surrendering to the Supreme Lord and obeying His orders is the path to liberation, contrasting this with becoming entangled in material designations and illusions. He uses examples of material titles and positions to illustrate the concept of 'Upadhi' (designation) and emphasizes that even powerful controllers are subject to God's laws.

But instead of becoming free from the clutches of Maya or illusion, foolish human beings become bound up by different nomenclatures being designated as Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, Shudra, Hindus, Mohammedans, Indians, Europeans, and many other designations like this. And thus they carry out the orders of the Supreme Lord under the influence with respect to scriptural or legislative injunctions. See these material designations. Upadhi. Everybody has got Upadhi. I am this, I am that. Beginning with I am the body. And later on the body becomes decorated with different titles. Somebody studies in a university and acquires degrees like Ph.D. or M.A. or whatever the university titles are. Or somebody becomes qualified as a doctor or a lawyer or a prime minister is elected or has won the popular vote and is in a very powerful position as the leader of a big nation. So, the leader of a big nation, great nation or even the post of a devata much higher. Indra, Chandra, Varuna, Vayu, all powerful controllers. You can imagine the control of the wind which is blowing all over the universe. Very powerful position. But even He is subject to the laws of God in this material world. So, surrender to the Supreme Lord and agree to abide by His orders is the way to get out. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Verse References:** SB 1.13.43
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the supreme will of the Lord brings men together and separates them."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.13.43) and explains that Narada Muni is teaching Yudhishthira about the principles governing the material world, specifically how the Lord's will brings people together and separates them.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naramcheiva Narottamam. Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naramcheiva Narottamam. In this session we will be discussing from the 43rd verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, Yatha Kredo Paskaraanam Samyoga Vigamavih Ichchaya Kreditu Syatam Tathai Veshechaya Dranam. The meaning of this verse is, as a player sets up and disperses his playthings according to his own sweet will. So the supreme will of the Lord brings men together and separates them. These are words spoken by Narada Muni. He is explaining to Yudhishthira when Yudhishthira questioned Narada Muni that I do not know where my uncles have gone away.

### karma, responsibility, Paramatma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3_uSQq75Ly4?t=160
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are held responsible for every action of ours, especially as human beings."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how the Lord's will works in conjunction with our past actions, emphasizing that we are responsible for our actions, especially as human beings, and are subject to the laws of karma, which are universally applicable, unlike state laws.

But how does it actually work? Some details are given by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. We must know for certain that the particular position in which we are now setup is an arrangement of the supreme will in terms of our own acts in the past. So we have to consider the supreme will of the supreme Lord and also our own actions in the past. So, it is not just the Lord alone who decides everything independently, there is also the consideration of our own actions in the past. What kind of actions? Our material activities in the past. We are held responsible for every action of ours, especially as human beings. So this we should not forget. If we don't act responsibly, we'll have to pay the price for irresponsibly acting because we are under the laws of the Supreme Lord God. These are called the laws of karma. So every action we are accountable, whether the state laws may or may not consider the consequence of every action of ours. The state laws work within the framework of some constitutional laws for people who are called citizens of this country. But the laws of karma are not responsible for our actions. The laws of God are working for every living being. And especially the human being is held responsible for every action of his.

---
### Paramatma, karma, divine justice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3_uSQq75Ly4?t=292
**Verse References:** BG 13.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is explaining, there is another transcendental enjoyer in this body apart from the living being who is a subordinate enjoyer, there is a transcendental enjoyer, he is called Paramatma, the Super Soul."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the role of the Paramatma (Supersoul) as the witnesser and sanctioner of our actions, referencing Bhagavad Gita (13.23). The Paramatma knows everything about our past, present, and future and rewards us accordingly, placing us in specific circumstances based on our past deeds.

Srila Prabhupada further explains, the Supreme Lord is present as the localized Paramatma in the heart of every living being, as it is said in the Bhagavad Gita in the 13th chapter in the 23rd verse. Upadrishta anumanam Svaramanta bharata bhoktamaheswaraha Paramatmeti capyukto dehesmin purushapparaah. Krishna is explaining, there is another transcendental enjoyer in this body apart from the living being who is a subordinate enjoyer, there is a transcendental enjoyer, he is called Paramatma, the Super Soul. Srila Prabhupada further explains, and he is the witnesser of every action of ours and he is the sanctioner of every action, both the witnesser and sanctioner, he is a supreme lord, he is a supreme controller, he is a supreme proprietor, Maheshwara, Bharta, he is a supreme enjoyer, he is a supreme maintainer, supreme provider, therefore Srila Prabhupada says he knows everything of our activities in every stage of our lives, he knows the past, present and future.

### karma, divine will, family
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3_uSQq75Ly4?t=444
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So we come together in a family, or in a society, in a community, in a workplace, in a family, in a country, in a foreign place, even or even in heavenly planets, we come together by the will of the Lord according to our karma."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a rich man's son born with a silver spoon to illustrate how the Paramatma rewards past actions. He emphasizes that the Lord guides every living being, especially humans, according to their desires and what they deserve, bringing them together and separating them in various relationships and situations.

Srila Prabhupada gives an example, a rich man gets his son born with a silver spoon in his mouth, but the child who came as the rich man's son deserved such a place and therefore he is placed there by the will of the Lord. Every one of us is spirit soul and we have got our reactions from the past. So the Lord places as Paramatma, he guides every living being, every living being, and particularly the human being is guided every moment for every action according to the will of the Lord to what he deserves and what he desires, both are taken into account and at a particular moment when the child has to be removed from that place, he is also carried by the will of the Supreme, even the child or the father does not wish to be separated from the happy relation. So we come together in a family, or in a society, in a community, in a workplace, in a family, in a country, in a foreign place, even or even in heavenly planets, we come together by the will of the Lord according to our karma.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.44 | 22-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### reality, philosophy, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FTvX9Jg1hfw?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavatam, and the followers of the Bhagavata philosophy, the pure devotees, they give the perfect understanding based on the complete knowledge about the reality."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and explains that it perfectly explains the reality of truth, contrasting it with the incomplete understanding of other philosophers.

In this session, we will be discussing the 44th verse of the 13th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is: O King, in all circumstances, whether you consider the soul to be an eternal principle, or the material body to be perishable, or everything to exist in the impersonal absolute truth, or everything to be an inexplicable combination of matter and spirit, feelings of separation are due only to illusory affection and nothing more. A technical verse, and it explains perfectly the philosophy of Krishna. As the perfect explanation of the reality of the truth in this world, about what is this world, or who we are, or what is actually going on, the basis for things to take place in this world. Different philosophers have different theories. Now, the Bhagavatam, and the followers of the Bhagavata philosophy, the pure devotees, they give the perfect understanding based on the complete knowledge about the reality.

---
### reality, materialism, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FTvX9Jg1hfw?t=160
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just like the materialists do not know anything about the soul. If they don't know anything about the soul, then they do not know anything about the supreme soul either."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the limitations of other philosophies, particularly materialism, which ignores the soul and spiritual reality, and highlights the varying degrees of understanding about matter among different philosophies and religions.

Because the other philosophers are not able to see the complete reality. They are ignorant about certain facts, or certain facets of reality. Just like the materialists do not know anything about the soul. If they don't know anything about the soul, then they do not know anything about the supreme soul either. So they don't take spiritual reality into consideration, which is always in the background of the material reality. Even the material reality, different philosophers have different degrees of understanding. Different philosophies have different degrees of understanding. Different philosophies are different. Different religions have different degrees of understanding. Different religions have different degrees of understanding of what is matter. Some people do not even know that matter means the five elements, gross matter, consists of five elements combined in different proportions. It consists of five elements combined in different proportions, and it comes into existence by the combination of the five elements in different proportions when it is created. It remains for some time, and then it is separated again back to the five elements. This is what is going on as far as all the material objects are concerned, and this is happening not by any person's endeavor, but by the actions and reactions of the material modes of nature.

---

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that feelings of separation are due to illusory affection, a key point from Narada Muni, and describes the pangs of separation and the desire for reunion.

This reality is not known to the modern-day scientists. They don't even know what are these five elements. So, here, Narada Muni is presenting the fact that whatever be one's understanding of the present reality, feelings of separation, which any two people experience who are separated, are only due to illusory affection. So, here, Narada Muni is presenting the fact that whatever be one's understanding of the present reality, feelings of separation, which any two people experience who are separated, are only due to illusory affection. So, here, Narada Muni is presenting the fact that whatever be one's understanding of the present reality, feelings of separation, are only due to illusory affection. We have an affection for some of our near and dear ones, and when we are separated from somebody whom we have got an affectionate relationship, we feel the pangs of separation, and we desire that we again are able to come together and stay as well-wishers of each other.

### soul, eternality, material body

**Summary:** The speaker begins to describe the four kinds of philosophies mentioned in the verse, starting with the concept of the soul as an eternal principle, contrasting it with the perishable material body and referencing the Bhagavad Gita's description of the soul's qualities.

The Maya that is working in this world, so what are the different philosophies just to cite the different philosophers mentioned here by an army, four kinds of philosophies are mentioned in our. So the first philosophy he mentions is those who say the soul to be an eternal principle. I am spirit soul, you are spirit soul, every living being is spirit soul, and as spirit soul we are all eternal beings. We don't die, we don't take birth. We don't deteriorate as soul. Soul has no deterioration. The wonderful qualities and properties of the soul are described in the second chapter of the Bhagavad Gita by Lord Krishna. So there it is clearly said cannot be burned by fire, cannot be withered by the wind, cannot be destroyed by any of the different kinds of weapons, is not subject to birth and death. It is only the body which is born.

### body, soul, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FTvX9Jg1hfw?t=434
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The absolute controller, the ultimate controller is Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker continues discussing the nature of the body and soul, emphasizing that the body undergoes changes while the soul remains eternal. He then introduces Krishna as the ultimate controller, beyond both the soul and matter.

Which undergoes changes, six kinds of changes, shad vikara: birth, growth, sustenance, producing by-products, dwindling, and finally vanishing. These six changes apply to the body or any material object, but it doesn't apply to the spirit soul. So the soul to be an eternal principle is a fact, but that is not the only eternal principle that is there in this world. There is a higher eternal principle of the absolute eternal principle that is the Supreme Lord Krishna above both this tiny spirits souls and matter itself for the material creation itself beyond both. The controller of both, the absolute controller, the ultimate controller is Krishna.

So this is just one aspect of the truth or the reality, the soul to be an eternal principle. Another theory says, a philosopher says, the material body is perishable. Many times even materialists say that, after all this body we are not going to have forever. So, why should we be so much worried about next life? One atheist by name Charvak Muni, he has said in the past, millions of years ago he has said, that this body is going to turn into ashes because ultimately according to our tradition, we either burn the body or bury the body or leave it for vultures to eat. It is going to be ultimately perish. So when the body is going to perish, where is the question of taking birth again? Now this is a philosophy which does not take into consideration within the body there is a spirit soul which is eternal. That Charvak Muni is not able to figure out. He is like the modern-day materialist, materialistic scientist that, after all, who are we? This body. So this body is, as long as it is there, enjoy life. ### rebirth, soul, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FTvX9Jg1hfw?t=629

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

So body is perishable. It is a fact that this body is perishable. But the body we are going to get next, after we quit this body, when it becomes no longer habitable, then we have to actually enter another body, forced to enter, and suffer in that body, undergo experiences in that body, whether we like it or not. So that Charvak Muni has no understanding. Neither the materialistic persons understand that. Change of body at death. By whom? By the person inside this body. The body is coming into existence and it's going to remain for some time, undergo some changes, and it's going to perish. Yes. But what about the person inside the body? The experiencer. The resident of the body. Dehi and Deha. This fundamental differentiation is made in the Bhagavad Gita in the beginning.

### impersonalism, illusion, liberation

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the third philosophy: everything exists in the impersonal absolute truth. He describes the Mayavadi viewpoint, which considers matter illusory and aims for liberation by merging with the impersonal Brahman.

Then, another philosophy says, everything is existing in the impersonal absolute truth. The Mayavadis. They say, there is only one impersonal Brahman. And, we are all that impersonal Brahman. This matter is simply some illusory, some illusory thing. That's all. It is there today. It is not there tomorrow. At the most, it will last till Pralaya. So, don't bother about this body. Don't take into consideration anything about matter. Try to understand matter is illusory. And, you are that impersonal absolute truth. And, you have to aim for liberation from this material bondage by developing detachment and becoming one with the supreme impersonal absolute. So, this is the Mayavada philosophy that Narada Muni is mentioning here.

And, yet another philosophy is, everything to be an inexplicable combination of matter and spirit. There are some philosophers who say there is something called spirit and something called matter. Two things are there, because they understand there is a distinct difference between a living person and dead matter, whether it is a dead body or dead object like a table. There is a distinct difference. And, this difference always exists. Always exists. Living body is never the same as a dead matter, and dead matter can never be showing any living symptoms. Though sometimes people claim that even in dead matter we can see some life symptoms, actually, it never is possible. There is a distinct difference between living person in this world I am talking and dead matter. There is always difference. So, they say ultimately there is some kind of combination of matter and spirit, because matter they recognize as completely different from the living person. And, living person, they kind of say is something spirit, which is not matter, which is not just dead matter. So, somehow they think there is some combination of the two, because we have got in this body hair and nails which is like dead matter. So, how is hair which is, when you cut the hair you don't feel any pain, and that hair is, it is just like matter. So, but, the hairs as long as it is attached to this body it keeps growing. **Key Quote:** "Feelings of separation is only due to illusory affection. That's all."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating Narada Muni's message that feelings of separation are due to illusory affection, regardless of one's philosophical understanding, and ends the session.

So, whatever be your philosophical understanding, Narada Muni says the truth you have to remember is that feelings of separation is only due to illusory affection. That's all. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.45-46 | 23-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### detachment, illusory affection, supreme will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7mH4uKshn4I?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.45
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All feelings of separation are due to illusory affection."
**Summary:** Narabunni advises Yudhishthira to give up his anxiety, explaining that everyone is a spirit soul controlled by the supreme will according to their past actions, and feelings of separation are due to illusory affection.

In this session, we will be discussing the 45th verse of the 13th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this 45th verse is, "Therefore, give up your anxiety due to ignorance of the self. You are now thinking of how they, who are helpless poor creatures, will exist without you." So, now Narabunni is coming to the point that he explained in the previous verse. All feelings of separation are due to illusory affection. The conclusion now is being drawn after explaining the philosophy, the truth, the reality, that give up your anxiety which is due to ignorance of the self. So, you are not considering the fact that you, your uncles, your aunt, and everybody for that matter, is spirit soul and their movements are controlled by the supreme will according to their own past actions. You are thinking, even though you know that, you might be aware that the supreme lord is the ultimate controller, but you're thinking, "How will they exist without your help? Because they have become old, they are helpless." That's your anxiety now, but give up your anxiety to the self. Give up that anxiety. And why he has to give up that anxiety, he'll explain in the next verse. Narabunni will explain the next verse to Yudhishthira.

---
### protection, duty, supreme control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7mH4uKshn4I?t=167
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everybody has to do their duties and always remember no one but the Supreme Lord can give protection to anyone else."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that while the Lord provides protection based on one's actions, we still have to perform our duties in our respective positions, depending on the Lord for protection.

But Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, a brief purport, "When we think of our kin and kin as being helpless and dependent on us, it is all due to ignorance." Why is Prabhupada saying this? Because every living creature is allowed all protection by the order of the supreme lord in terms of each one's action. A quiet position in the world, how much protection each person can have at any given time is dependent on the position in which one is placed by the Lord. The important point is to remember always the Lord is known as Buddha breath, means he is the one who gives protection to all living beings. He is not partial, he is impartial and is the only one who protects each and every living being. Therefore, understanding that there is the supreme control of the Supreme Lord and everybody is protected by the Lord, one should discharge his duties only. Does it mean the Supreme Lord is everybody's controller, so why should I bother to protect my children or why should I bother to give any protection to the people who are dependent on the Lord? No, that is not correct. We have to do our duty in the position in which we are placed presently. If I am in the position of a father, I have to do father's duties towards the children. If I am in the position of a husband, I have to do husband's duty. If somebody else is in the position of a mother, such a mother has to do her duty towards her children. So, everybody has to do their duties and always remember no one but the Supreme Lord can give protection to anyone else. You do your duties and depend on the Supreme Lord for your protection, for the protection of your children, the protection of your beloved loved ones, everybody, you depend on the Lord for protection. This is the perfect understanding as regards to the Lord and the way life is. Before lifts your hands and keeps you in mínima position, that is to say whatever time it is that you are here to make life safe, we need to do that first and foremost, but right now we have to sustain ourselves and we are only what is given to us the majority right now, you are being messed with by the 머리 and understanding however hard it is, it's not for us yet that we will do something for advert. Now how the Lord gives protection and how we have to simply discharge our duties is explained very nicely in the next verse.

### time, karma, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7mH4uKshn4I?t=337
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.46
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This gross material body made up of these five elements is already under the control of three different things."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the next verse, explaining that the material body is controlled by eternal time (kala), karma (action), and the three modes of material nature (guna), emphasizing the inevitability of death and the limitations of material protection.

The next verse, forty-sixth verse is, this is a very important verse. The meaning of this verse, this gross material body made up of these five elements is already under the control of three different things. What is that? It is already under control of eternal time, that is Kala, under the control of Karma or action, and under the control of Guna or the three moments. The three moments of material nature, Kala, Karma, Guna, eternal time, action, and the material moments. How then can it, being already in the jaws of the serpent, protect others? Kala is also called Kala Sarpa because time brings about death. As we all know, it is just a question of time. Somebody may die now, somebody may die some few hours later, somebody may die a few years later, somebody may die at the end of the duration of this universe, but Kala is going to ensure death for everybody. So, everybody is under the clutches of Kala. So, how can you... how can you avoid that particular death which is going to come? You cannot protect. Everybody is under Kala.

---
### material nature, modes of nature, conditioned living beings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7mH4uKshn4I?t=463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A conditioned living being is under the full control of material nature."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's purport explains that worldly efforts for freedom are futile because everyone is controlled by a superior power and the laws of karma, except for pure devotees who are transcendental.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, "The world's movements for freedom through political, economic, social, and cultural propaganda can do no benefit to anyone." This so-called freedom is actually really not beneficial. Why? Because everyone is controlled by the superior power. So, who can go against the supreme will of the Supreme Lord that is operating in this material world? No one can do it. A conditioned living being is under the full control of material nature. Conditioned means those who are under the laws of karma. So, everyone is under the laws of karma, except pure devotees, who are transcendental to the laws of karma, because of being directly under the Supreme Lord's internal potency, the spiritual potency, Yoga Maya. So, except for them, everyone else is under the full control of material nature. Everyone else is a conditioned living being, full control of material nature, represented by three things, control of material nature. Control of material nature means it is represented by eternal time. It is represented by activities under the direction of the material modes of nature, which are always working in this world. So, there are three material modes of nature, namely, goodness, passion, ignorance. Goodness means the sattva guna. Passion means rajo guna. Ignorance means tamo guna. Unless one is situated in the mode of goodness, or sattva guna, one cannot see things as they are. So, Prabhupada says, the passionate and the ignorant, that means the rajasic and tamasic people, they cannot even see things as they are. They are unable to see this control of Kala, Karma, Guna. They cannot see. They cannot perceive. They cannot understand.

---
### modes of nature, protection, bhakti yoga, kala
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7mH4uKshn4I?t=592
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The only thing possible to be done is to get rid of eternal time Kala, which is compared to Kala Sarpa or the cobra snake, whose bite is always lethal."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that only those in the mode of goodness can guide others to take shelter of the Supreme Lord, the only true alternative for protection, and that the influence of time (kala) is unavoidable, but can be overcome through bhakti yoga.

Therefore, a person who is passionate and ignorant cannot direct his activities on the right path. So, rajasic and tamasic people, they can never be doing things right. They will always make mistakes, and they will suffer. Only the man in the quality of goodness can help to a certain extent. So, a man in the quality of goodness can see things properly and can guide how one can actually take shelter of the supreme lord, only. That is the only alternative for protection. There is no other alternative. Most persons are passionate and ignorant, and therefore their plans and projects can hardly do any good to others. So many plans are there for protection, but they don't work the way somebody desires, because the supreme lord, the supreme will, is ultimately going to override all our plans. Above the modes of nature is eternal time, which is called Kala, which is called Kala because it changes the shape of everything in the material world. Kala is changing everything, and that is forced change. It is not something which we can reverse or we can avoid, the influence of Kala. Even if we are able to do something temporarily beneficial, time will see that the good project is frustrated in the course of time. Kala will ensure that. The destruction will come at the right time. For the time being we may say maintenance, or we may say protection, or we may say some safety, but ultimately, there is no eternal safe position in this material world. No. So Srila Prabhupada says, "The only thing possible to be done is to get rid of eternal time Kala, which is compared to Kala Sarpa or the cobra snake, whose bite is always lethal." Lethal means it's constant. Lethal means it's going to end our life in this body. So, when death comes, there is no remedy for avoiding death except bhakti yoga. That Prabhupada will explain. No one can be saved from the bite of a cobra. The best remedy for getting out of the clutches of cobra, like Kala, or its integrity, the modes of nature, is bhakti yoga.

---
### bhakti yoga, transcendence, preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7mH4uKshn4I?t=775
**Verse References:** BG 14.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The highest perfectional project of philanthropic activities is to engage everyone in the act of preaching bhakti yoga all over the world."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing that bhakti yoga, as recommended in the Bhagavad Gita, is the only way to transcend the modes of material nature and become immune to death, and that preaching bhakti yoga is the highest form of philanthropy.

And this is recommended in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 14th chapter, 26th verse. Maam chayo avyabhicharena bhakti yogena sevate. Sagunan samatityaitaan brahma bhuvaya ayakalpate. One who renders pure devotional service to Krishna, unfailing in all circumstances, transcends the modes of material nature, comes to the spiritual platform. On the spiritual platform, one is immune to death and immune to the influence of Kala, Karma, Guna, everything. One's actions become completely spiritual on the spiritual platform for those who are engaged in pure devotional service. So the highest perfectional project of philanthropic activities is to engage everyone in the act of preaching bhakti yoga all over the world. So you should practice bhakti yoga, pure devotional service, and you should preach this bhakti yoga to people who are not aware of bhakti yoga. And this alone can save the people from the control of Maya or material nature, represented by Kala, Karma, and Guna. So this is very, very clear. So we should practice bhakti while doing our duties. We should practice bhakti yoga and in that way become free from the effect of Kala, Karma, Guna. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.13.47 | 24-10-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### material nature, subsistence, misunderstanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=47
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Very important law of material nature that's working universally is explained by Sri Narada Muni to Yudhishthira and to all of us."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse about one living being being food for another and addresses the common misunderstanding used to justify eating meat, stating that this argument is wrong.

In this session, we will be discussing the forty-seventh verse of the thirteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Ahastāni sahastānām apadāni chatuṣpadām falgūṇī tatra mahatām jīvo jīvasya jīvanam. Very important verse. The meaning of this verse is those who are devoid of hands are prey for those who have hands. Those devoid of legs are prey for the four-legged. The weak are the subsistence of the strong, and the general rule holds that one living being is food for another living being. So, a very important law of material nature that's working universally is explained by Sri Narada Muni to Yudhishthira and to all of us. Jīva jīvasya jīvanam. The food for our subsistence according to the law of material nature is that one living being is food for another. So, there are so many misunderstandings that are possible by simply considering anyway one living being is food for another. So what is wrong in killing animals for food? This is the argument. Many people will give this argument. Those who are meat eaters, they give this argument. But that argument is wrong as will be pointed out by Prabhupāda in the purport.

---
### maya, suffering, divine will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=142
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living beings who have come to this material world against the will of the supreme being are under the control of a supreme power called maya shakti, the deputed agent of the lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of purports for understanding scripture, the necessity of consuming living food, and the role of maya shakti in causing suffering in the material world, highlighting the categories of weak, stronger, and strongest beings.

Purports are important because they help us understand in the proper way the particular statement of the scripture. What Narada Muni is telling is definitely true. For our subsistence, we cannot eat dead objects. But we still have to use our good sense what kind of food we should, we are permitted to take in terms of jīva jīvasya jīvanam. So, Srila Prabhupāda says in the purport, a systematic law of subsistence in the struggle for existence is there by the supreme will and there is no escape for anyone by any amount of planning. So, you try to protect any poor helpless creature. Ultimately, when death comes, you cannot save the body. This is the law. The living beings who have come to this material world against the will of the supreme being are under the control of a supreme power called maya shakti, the deputed agent of the lord. And this daivimaya means divine power is meant to pinch the conditioned souls by threefold miseries, one of which is explained here in this verse. We are always under the threefold miseries, tapatraya, which is the one that is mentioned here, the weak are the subsistence of the strong. No one is strong enough to protect himself from the power of the supreme being. And by the will of the lord, there are systematic categories of the weak, the stronger and the strongest. All around you, you can see this.

---
### scripture, human responsibility, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although the law states that a human being must subsist on another living being, there is also the law of good sense, for the human being is meant to obey the laws of the scriptures."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the law of good sense for human beings, emphasizing obedience to scriptures, self-realization, and offering food to Krishna to avoid sinful reactions (papa) and suffering.

There is nothing to be lamented if a tiger eats a weaker animal. For its food, a tiger will kill another weaker animal and eat its flesh. Although the law states that a human being must subsist on another living being, there is also the law of good sense. For the human being is meant to obey the laws of the scriptures. For the human beings, there is something extra above the fundamental laws of jivo, jivasya, jivanam. We have to subsist on other living beings, but that doesn't mean that we violate the laws of the scriptures. So we have to live by eating some other living thing, but we also have to follow the laws of scriptures. That is the law of good sense for the human being. The human being is meant for self-realization. And for that purpose, he is not to eat anything which is not good. He is not first offered to the lord. This is the law of scripture. It is explained in the Bhagavad Gita. Whatever you eat, you have to first offer to Krishna. And only then you are free from reactions. Otherwise, you are going to suffer severe reactions. And what are those reactions? That reaction is called papa. Sinful reaction. Sinful reaction is severe suffering, which is enforced by material nature.

---
### prasada, devotion, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=390
**Verse References:** BG 9.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You should eat only what is offered to Krishna. Only then you can be free from reactions and you can progress in bhakti, to get out of the clutches of maya."
**Summary:** This segment explains that one should only eat food offered to Krishna (prasada) to avoid reactions and progress in bhakti, specifying that Krishna accepts vegetables, fruits, leaves, grains, and milk.

Nobody can go against it. So, Srila Prabhupada explains, the lord accepts from his devotee all kinds of food, preparations made of vegetables, fruits, leaves, and grains. Fruits, leaves, and milk in different varieties can be offered to the lord. And after the lord accepts, the food is offered to him. The devotee can partake of the prasada, by which all suffering in the struggle for existence will be gradually mitigated. This is confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita, 9.26. Patram pushpam phalam toyam yome bhaktya prayarchati. What Krishna accepts has been told in the Gita. This is the law of scripture. You should eat only what is offered to Krishna. Only then you can be free from reactions. And you can progress in bhakti, to get out of the clutches of maya. No more to suffer any material existence. But, you have to only offer Krishna varieties of foodstuff, which is prepared from patram pushpam phalam toyam. In this category, vegetarian, not non-vegetarian.

---
### scriptural injunctions, regulation, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=474
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For them, the injunctions are not meant to encourage the eating of animal flesh, no, but to restrict them by regulated principles, to minimize the harm done to animals."
**Summary:** This segment discusses scriptural injunctions for meat-eaters, stating that while they cannot offer flesh directly to Krishna, they can offer it to an agent of the Lord under specific conditions, with the ultimate aim of restricting and minimizing animal killing.

Even those who are accustomed to eat animals can offer foodstuff. Now, this is something that is explained in the scriptures, people do not know. They can eat animal flesh, not by offering to the lord directly, not by offering to Krishna. No, Krishna does not accept flesh. But, they can offer to an agent of the lord according to the scriptures, according to the scriptural direction, under certain conditions of religious rights. So, there are specific rules, what they can offer, to whom they can offer, when they can offer, how they have to offer. All this is given in the shastra. So, injunctions of the scriptures, especially for these meat eaters, who want to eat animal flesh. For them, the injunctions are not meant to encourage the eating of animal flesh. No. But to restrict them by regulated principles, to minimize the harm done to animals. The killing of animals is to be restricted. That's the ultimate aim. And put some sense into these people's head, that you should completely avoid animal killing because you can subsist, and not only subsist, you can relish, and lead a healthy life simply by eating vegetarian foods of offer to the lord and partaking prasad. So, to put this sense into the head of the foolish people, who simply, to satisfy their tongue, they indiscriminately kill animals and eat animal flesh. For them to be saved from severe reactions, very, very sinful, severe reactions.

---
### divine arrangement, subsistence, prasada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=595
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The lord arranges for the elephant, it requires 500 kg of food at a time in one day, and the ant requires a small grain of sugar, or some small grain of stuff, particular foodstuff. So, the lord provides ant its small grain, and the lord provides the elephant its 500 kg of food."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the Lord provides for all living beings, regardless of their size or needs, and that one should not worry about subsistence, using the example of animals in the African jungle and advising Maharaj Yudhishtira not to worry about his uncles in the forest.

The scriptures are guiding and regulating such animal killing. So, further Srila Prabhupada says, the living being is a source of subsistence for other stronger living beings. No one should be very anxious for a subsistence in any circumstances, because there are living beings everywhere, and no living being starves for want of food at any place. See, if I am placed in a particular situation by the higher arrangement of the supreme lord, then I will be provided by the lord whatever I require for my subsistence. Now, how that is going to happen is mystical. Like how do, Prabhupada explains this very nicely in another place, how do so many millions of living entities subsist in the African jungle? Of all the matter, any jungle. How do they subsist? The lord arranges for the elephant, it requires 500 kg of food at a time in one day, and the ant requires a small grain of sugar, or some small grain of stuff, particular foodstuff. So, the lord provides ant its small grain, and the lord provides the elephant its 500 kg of food. So, the lord is arranged. So, you don't have to be anxious how elephants will survive, how will they get their food, or how an ant will survive. No. Just like in the African jungle, there is no scarcity of food. So, therefore, the conclusion is Maharaj Yudhishtira has advised by Narada not to worry about his uncle's suffering for want of food, for they could live on vegetables available in the jungles as prasada of the supreme lord.

---

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment discusses how the Lord will provide prasada in the form of vegetables and fruits in the forest for those seeking self-realization, highlighting the abundance of food in the Himalayan region for spiritualists and emphasizing that exploitation of weaker beings is a natural law that can only be checked by spiritual awakening.

And thus realize the path of salvation. So, Narada is going to tell, your uncles have gone to the forest for the sake of self-realization. And how will they subsist without your providing them their food. The lord will provide prasada in the form of vegetables or fruits in the forest. Srila Prabhupada says about Himalayan region, the forest in the Himalayan region, there is plenty of fruits for spiritualists particularly, plenty of fruits available, and roots are there, berries are there, and vegetation is there, which different seasons, different things are available for subsistence of the devotees, the yogis, the transcendentalists. They don't have to kill any animal, nothing. And the lord's protection is also there for a spiritualist, for a transcendentalist, who is engaged in meditation or spiritual practice in the jungle. Prabhupada explains that also. So, that is the higher arrangement. Now, the exploitation of the weaker living being by the stronger is a natural law of existence. That is natural law.

### spiritual sense, peace, vegetarianism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X0X7eEymoC8?t=880
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only a human being should not kill another animal or another creature, whether insect or bird or anything. Human being should subsist on food grains and vegetables and fruits and milk."
**Summary:** This segment concludes that only humans can check the tendency to exploit weaker beings through spiritual practice, advocating for a vegetarian diet offered to Krishna to promote peace and avoid karmic reactions.

A tiger will kill another living being, but only when the tiger is hungry, and only as much as it requires. The tiger lives in the jungle, not that it is always killing some other weaker animal. No. Only when it is hungry, and that also only one animal, and that also that flesh will last for it for several days or weeks. I don't know how much time. But it, till that flesh is there, the tiger will not kill another animal. So, this is the law of existence. There is no possibility of checking this tendency of a stronger living being devouring a weaker living being for its food by any artificial means under material conditions. It can be checked only by awakening the spiritual sense of the human being, by practice of spiritual regulation. Only a human being should not kill another animal or another creature, whether insect or bird or anything. Human being should subsist on food grains and vegetables and fruits and milk. But this sense is possible to be awakened by spiritual practice. So, if a man kills another animal, then how can he expect peace in the society. He will also be killed by the laws of nature. And there are many instances of this explained, which I don't have time to go into. Some other time I will explain. So, human being should not kill another animal. They should only subsist on food grains, vegetables, fruits and milk, by offering that to Krishna and partaking prasad. So, this is the conclusion. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
