### Intimacy, Tolerance, Friendship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IYLnVLjobeg?t=151
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.19
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even in those hours when his value was minimized, he, being the supreme soul, used to tolerate all those utterings of mine, excusing me exactly as a true friend excuses his true friend, or a father excuses his son."
**Summary:** Arjuna describes the intimate and forgiving nature of his relationship with Krishna, emphasizing Krishna's tolerance and understanding even when Arjuna was critical.

The 19th verse: Shayasana Atana Vikathana Bhojana Adishu Aikyad Vyasya Rutavan Iti Vipralabdhaha Sakhyu Sakheva Pitthana Itruvatthana Yasyasarvam Sehe Mahan Mahitaya Kumater Aghamme. Yet another verse spoken by Arjuna remembering Krishna's intimate dealings with Arjuna. So the translation of this 19th verse: Generally both of us used to live together and sleep, sit, and loiter together, and at the time of advertising oneself for acts of chivalry, sometimes, if there were any irregularity, I used to reproach him by saying, "My friend, you are very truthful." Even in those hours when his value was minimized, he, being the supreme soul, used to tolerate all those utterings of mine, excusing me exactly as a true friend excuses his true friend, or a father excuses his son.

---
### Mercy, Devotion, Krishna's nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IYLnVLjobeg?t=231
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is very, very merciful. Even to ordinary beings like us, because even though we have almost totally forgotten him, he never forgets us."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's immense mercy and compassion towards all beings, especially his devotees, highlighting his constant presence and guidance.

This brings out very clearly how Krishna is very, very kind to his pure devotees. Krishna is worshipped as He Krishna Karunasindho. Karunasindho means he is the ocean of mercy. Krishna is very, very merciful, even to ordinary beings like us. How can we understand this? Because even though we have almost totally forgotten him, he never forgets us, and personally he is present in each of our hearts as the Paramatma. He is sitting there, guiding us, providing us, fulfilling our desires, acting as our topmost well-wisher, and instructing us how we can go back to him for eternal life full of bliss and knowledge, in our eternal loving relationship with him. He is making so many arrangements in this material world. So certainly Krishna is rightly, is rightly addressed in this prayer as He Krishna Karunasindho, Dinabandho Jagatpate. He is the Supreme Lord, the Master, the Supreme Master, but still he is Dinabandho. He is the friend of the poor, and the weak, and the fallen, and the downtrodden. He is the friend. He is the true friend, because he is an ocean of mercy. He is the ocean of compassion, ocean of mercy for all such souls in this material world. But when it comes to Krishna's dealings with his pure devotees, he is even more merciful to them. Even more merciful. As it is, he is very, very merciful to everyone, but he is even more merciful to his pure devotees.

---
### Devotion, Intimacy with God, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IYLnVLjobeg?t=376
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna relishes more the dealings of his intimate devotees when they regard him as their equal or their subordinate and even sometimes reproach him in their dealings. Krishna likes that."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that Krishna values the intimate and sometimes even reproachful dealings of his pure devotees more than formal Vedic hymns.

Now in the purport to this 19th verse, Srila Prabhupada says, a brief purport: "Since the Supreme Lord, Sri Krishna, is all perfect, his transcendental pastimes with his pure devotees never lack anything in any respect either as a friend, son, or lover. The Lord relishes the reproaches of friends, parents, of fiancés, more than the Vedic hymns offered to him by great learned scholars and religionists in an official fashion." Here is a very important point. Krishna relishes more the dealings of his intimate devotees when they regard him as their equal or their subordinate and even sometimes reproach him in their dealings. Krishna likes that. Krishna relishes that more. Those are the words used by Prabhupada here. He relishes the reproaches of friends, parents, or fiancés more than the Vedic hymns. The Vedic hymns are, of course, always glorifying Krishna as the Supreme in all respects, as the Supreme Controller, Supreme Master, Supreme Proprietor, Supremely opulent, Supremely knowledgeable, in every way he is the Supreme in every respect. But, when somebody glorifies Krishna by chanting those Vedic hymns that just glorify his greatness, Krishna doesn't relish that as much as he relishes the dealings with his pure devotees in which they regard him as their intimate friend or intimate son or intimate lover.

---
### Separation, Divine Plan, Arjuna's Grief
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IYLnVLjobeg?t=507
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Emperor, now I am separated from my friend and dear most well-wisher, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and therefore my heart appears to be void of everything."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 20th verse, focusing on Arjuna's feelings of separation from Krishna and the bewildering incident where Arjuna was defeated and the wives of Krishna were taken.

The 20th verse: Soham rupendra rahita purushottamena Sakhyapriyena surdha hridayena shunyaha Advanyuru krama parigraham anga rakshan Gopair asadbhir abaleva Finirjitoh smi. The meaning of this verse is, "O Emperor, now I am separated from my friend and dear most well-wisher, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and therefore my heart appears to be void of everything. In his absence, I have been defeated by a number of infidel, and I have heard men while I was guarding the bodies of all the wives of Krishna." After Krishna's disappearance this was an incident that took place that Arjuna was taking charge of protecting the wives of Krishna and something happened that is very bewildering when we hear that some infidel, coward men, they defeated Arjuna and actually kidnapped the wives of Krishna. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains this point. The important point in this verse is how it was possible that Arjuna could be defeated by a gang of ignoble, coward men and how such mundane, coward men could touch the bodies of the wives of Lord Krishna who are under the protection of Arjuna.

---
### Divine Arrangement, Curses and Blessings, Krishna's Lila
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IYLnVLjobeg?t=621
**Verse References:** Vishnu Purana, Brahma Purana
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In order to keep the words of the great Muni, the Lord himself kidnapped his wives from the protection of Arjuna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the apparent contradiction of Arjuna's defeat by recounting a story from the Vishnu and Brahma Puranas involving a curse and subsequent blessing related to the wives of Krishna.

So Srila Vishwanachakravarti Thakur, one of our previous Acharyas, has justified the contradiction by research in the Vishnu Purana and Brahma Purana. This is explained in two other Puranas. In these Puranas, it is said that once the fair denizens of heaven pleased Ashtavakra Muni by their service and were blessed by the Muni to have the Supreme Lord as their husband. Ashtavakra Muni was curved in eight joints of his body and thus he used to move in a peculiar curved manner. The daughters of the demigods could not check their laughter upon seeing the movements of the Muni, Ashtavakra Muni, and the Muni being angry at them cursed them that they would be kidnapped by rogues even if they would get the Lord as their husband. Later on, the girls again satisfied the Muni by their prayers and the Muni blessed them that they would regain their husband even after being robbed by the rogues. So, in order to keep the words of the great Muni, the Lord himself kidnapped his wives from the protection of Arjuna.

---
### Krishna's Plan, Entourage, Return to Godhead
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IYLnVLjobeg?t=791
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna wants back all his entourage, but he is there because he wants back all of his entourage."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating Krishna's desire to bring back all his associates to the spiritual realm, explaining that the events surrounding the kidnapping were part of his divine plan.

So, Krishna, in order to keep the words of this Ashtavakra Muni intact, he himself disguised as some ignoble covered men and kidnapped his own wives even though they were under the protection of Arjuna. And after being kidnapped by Krishna himself, disguised as ignoble covered men, Krishna later on revealed to them that I am your husband. So, it is described here that after the departure of Lord Krishna, he wants back all his entourage. Krishna wants back all his entourage, but he is there because he wants back all of his entourage. So, he made up his mind for that purpose, for his own sake. And his plan is that people will not be returned to the same state as the ones who have lived all these years in his personal abode, and he calls them under different circumstances as part of this past times, in the ignoble covered men defeating Arjuna and kidnapping the wives of Krishna. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.21 - 24 | 10-12-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's Power, Devotee Empowerment, Detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IEVtBnCURC0?t=45
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the absence of Lord Krishna, all those empowerments are no longer valid because Krishna has withdrawn them."
**Summary:** Arjuna's realization of lost power after Krishna's departure is explained through analogies of clarified butter on ashes, magic wand money, and seeds on barren land, emphasizing that powers are for Krishna's pastimes and are withdrawn when no longer needed.

In this session, we will be discussing four verses: verse numbers twenty-one, twenty-two, twenty-three, and twenty-four of the fifteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The twenty-first verse is as follows. The meaning of this verse is: "I have the very same Gandiva bow, the same arrows, the same chariot drawn by the same horses. I have the same horses, and I am the same Arjuna to whom all the kings offer their due respects, but in the absence of Lord Krishna, all of them at a moment's notice have become null and void. It is exactly like offering clarified butter on ashes, accumulating money with a magic wand, or sowing seeds on barren land." These words are very significant, explaining to us that as long as it is required for Krishna's pastimes, He empowers His devotees with extraordinary power. But these powers given by Krishna are not required for the devotees to go back home to Godhead. They were only meant for Krishna to enact his pastimes. So after Krishna's pastimes have been bound up, the devotees do experience that they do not have the same powers they had when Krishna was present. Like Arjuna, he is having the same Gandiva bow, the same chariot drawn by the same horses, he's the same Arjuna, but now he is bereft of all those powers that he had. With the Gandiva bow in his hand, he could defeat many, many powerful Kshatriyas. Now, when he tried to protect the wives of Krishna, some gnomel cavaad men easily defeated him and kidnapped him, so his Gandiva bow was ineffective. So Arjuna is recollecting that in the absence of Lord Krishna, all of them have become null and void. Now Arjuna is giving three examples of how they have become null and void. It is like offering clarified butter on ashes. When you pour clarified butter on it, it is the beginning of the purification process. If you put butter into a blazing fire and there is sufficient fuel, then the fire will blaze even more strongly. But you pour any amount of ghee into ashes, there will be no fire. Another example given here is accumulating money with a magic wand. Sometimes magicians display some magic. They have got something called a magic wand, like a stick. They just move the stick, and suddenly in their hand there will be so much money, but that money is not real money. It is an optical illusion, it is a magical effect for entertaining some audience. There is no real money there. You cannot get money by just waving one stick in a magic show. There is no real money there. Similarly, sowing seeds on a barren land is useless. Those seeds won't fructify, they won't grow, they won't germinate. The land has to be fertile. Then only sowing good seeds will have some effect. Then only sowing good seeds will have some benefit. Similarly, in the absence of Lord Krishna, all those empowerments are no longer valid because Krishna has withdrawn them.

---
### Dependence on Krishna, Borrowed Plumes, Futility of Material Advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IEVtBnCURC0?t=350
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Material civilization without the blessings of the Lord is child's play only."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's explanation emphasizes that all energies come from Krishna, using the analogy of a powerhouse and light bulbs to illustrate dependence on the Supreme Source, and warns against the futility of material advancement without the Lord's blessings.

So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, "As we have discussed more than once, one should not be puffed up by borrowed plumes. All energies and powers are derived from the Supreme Source, Lord Krishna, and they act as long as He desires, and all of them cease to function as soon as He withdraws." Prabhupada gives an example to help us understand. All electrical energies are received from the powerhouse, and as soon as the powerhouse stops supplying energy, the bulbs are of no use. There are so many arrangements for lighting up a big palace or a big mansion, but as long as there is power supply from the electrical source, the powerhouse, these bulbs will glow very nicely, but as soon as the powerhouse stops supplying energy, the bulbs are of no use. In a moment's time, such energies can be generated or withdrawn by the Supreme Will of the Lord. Material civilization without the blessings of the Lord is child's play only. As long as the parents allow the child to play, it is all right. As soon as the parents withdraw, the child has to stop." Prabhupada is giving an example to help us understand. Human civilization and all activities thereof must be dovetailed with the supreme blessing of the Lord, and without this blessing, all advancement of human civilization is like decoration on a dead body. It is said here that a dead civilization and its activities are something like clarified butter on ashes, the accumulation of money by a magic wand, and the sowing of seeds on a barren land. The blessings of the Lord's blessings, all our attempts at independent existence in this material world are futile.

---
### Divine Will, Intoxication, Destruction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IEVtBnCURC0?t=484
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Factually this is all due to the supreme will of the Lord, the personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** Arjuna recounts the demise of the Yadus in Dwarka due to a brahmana's curse, leading to their intoxication and mutual destruction, attributing it all to the supreme will of the Lord, who sometimes allows people to kill or protect each other.

Verses 22 and 23, they are given together. I will read both of them, and then we will hear the translation. Rajamstvayanuprishtanam surdamna suratpure viprashapavimudhanam nignatam mushtibhir mithah varunim madhirampitva madonmathita cetasam ajanatam ivaunyonyam chatupanchavasyeshitah. The meaning of these two verses is, "O King, since you have asked me about our friends and relatives in the city of Dwarka, I inform you that all of them were cursed by the brahmanas, and as a result, they all became intoxicated with wine made of putrefied rice, and fought among themselves with sticks, not even recognizing one another. Now all but four or five of them are dead and gone." Krishna had, sorry, Yudhishthira had sent Arjuna to inquire about the well-being of the Yadus. So now Arjuna is bringing the news, after Krishna's disappearance, all of them, except for four or five, are dead and gone. And how did they die? That is also very bewildering. They fought with each other after becoming intoxicated and killed each other. Twenty-fourth verse, prayenaitad bhagavata ishwarasya vicheshtitam mitho nignanti bhutani bhavayanti chayan mithah. The translation of this twenty-fourth verse, "Factually this is all due to the supreme will of the Lord, the personality of Godhead. Sometimes people kill one another, and at other times, they protect one another." So Arjuna is explaining that as a devotee he knows, the supreme will of the supreme personality of Godhead always prevails, and by his will, sometimes people may kill each other, and at other times, by his will, people may protect each other.

---
### Divine Control, Law of Nature, Free Will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IEVtBnCURC0?t=645
**Verse References:** BG 9.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Not a blade of grass moves without the will of the Lord."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's purport refutes the atheistic view of the world operating solely on the law of survival of the fittest, asserting that the Supreme Lord directs nature, and even peace and upheaval are due to His will, emphasizing His complete control and sanction over every action.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, "According to the anthropologists, there is nature's law of struggle for existence and survival of the fittest." One class of philosophers in this world, they are mostly atheists, and they say that this world is working according to the law of struggle for existence and survival of the fittest. But Srila Prabhupada says, "They do not know that behind the law of nature is the supreme direction of the supreme personality of Godhead." That they do not know, they cannot see, they cannot understand. They do not know how nature is working. In the Bhagavad Gita, Srila Prabhupada says, "It is confirmed that the law of nature is executed under the direction of the Lord." What is the verse? Mayadhyakshena prakritihi suyatesa characharam. Material nature is working perfectly according to the direction of Krishna. Whenever therefore there is peace in the world, it must be known that it is due to the goodwill of the Lord, and whenever there is upheaval in the world, it is also due to the supreme will of the Lord. Both peace as well as upheaval, battles or wars, both are according to the supreme will of the Supreme Lord. Not a blade of grass moves without the will of the Lord. Tenavina trinam apina chalati. Even a blade of grass won't move without the sanction of the Lord. Completely he is in perfect control because he is Paramatman everywhere, everyone's heart, and every single move by every living entity has to be sanctioned by the Lord. Otherwise, the living entity is unable to act or move.

---
### Dharma, Krishna Consciousness, Peace and Prosperity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IEVtBnCURC0?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The surest way to the path of peace, therefore, is dovetailing everything to the established rule of the Lord."
**Summary:** Disobedience to the Lord's rules leads to conflict, while following Dharma, the code of conduct given by Krishna, brings peace, prosperity, and harmony, as exemplified by the reigns of Yudhishthira Maharaj, Ramachandra, and Pariksit Maharaj, and can be observed in Krishna conscious communities today.

Whenever therefore, Srila Prabhupada says further, "There is disobedience of the established rules enacted by the Lord, there is war between men and nations." So there is a reason why Krishna sometimes wills that there be some fighting or battle between men or between nations. Why? Because there is disobedience to the established rules enacted by the Lord. Dharmam tu sakshat bhagavat pranitam. Dharma means the code of conduct given by God personally for all of us, especially for the human beings. So we have to follow those rules of Dharma given by Krishna. Then everything will be fine. There will be peace, there will be prosperity, there will be plenty of supply. There will be very cordial relations between people. Everything will be very, very nice, just like when Yudhishthira Maharaj was ruling, when Ramachandra was ruling, when Pariksit Maharaj was ruling. There was peace, there was prosperity, there was plenty of all supplies. People were completely satisfied. They were happy in all respects. So that is not a utopian idea. It's a fact. You can observe even now in places where there is predominantly Krishna consciousness, God consciousness. People live very nicely, very nicely in communities. You can visit some of these communities if you are not conversant with this fact and see for yourself how people live very nicely in such communities. So therefore Prabhupada concludes by telling, "The surest way to the path of peace, therefore, is dovetailing everything to the established rule of the Lord." So the rule of the Lord is everything we should do in Krishna consciousness. Everything we should offer to Krishna. Then certainly there will be peace, prosperity, and everything will be nice. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.15.25-26) which describe how the stronger Yadu members eliminated the weaker ones, comparing it to the natural order in the ocean.

In this session we will be discussing from two verses which are given together, 25 and 26 of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Jalau Kasam Jale Yadvan Mahanto Adanti Aniyasaha Durbalan Balino Rajan Mahanto Balino Mithaha Evam Balishthair Yadubhir Mahadhir Itaran Vibhu Yadun Yadubhir Anyonyam Bhubharan Sanjaharaha. The meaning of these two verses is given as follows: O King, as in the ocean the bigger and stronger aquatic swallow up the smaller and weaker ones, so also the supreme personality of Godhead to lighten the burden of the earth has engaged the stronger Yadu to kill the weaker and the bigger Yadu to kill the smaller.

---
### material existence, desire, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wMc6qwXZeXw?t=123
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This material world is a place for all those souls who belong to God, who belong to the spiritual world, but are forgetful of Krishna due to their desire to independently enjoy and their desire to lord over material nature."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that the material world is a place for souls who desire independent enjoyment and to lord over material nature, leading to the struggle for existence.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, what does this mean? Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, in the material world, the struggle for existence and survival of the fittest are laws because in the material world there is disparity between conditioned souls due to everyone's desire to lord it over the material resources. This is a very, very important point we have to understand. This material world is a place for all those souls who belong to God, who belong to the spiritual world, but are forgetful of Krishna due to their desire to independently enjoy and their desire to lord over material nature. Two very, very specific desires are the cause of our material existence. What are those two desires?

---
### independent enjoyment, illusion, disparity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wMc6qwXZeXw?t=191
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The illusion energy is working to cover the real knowledge of who we are, the reality of Krishna being the maintainer, Krishna being the protector."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the two desires that cause material existence: independent enjoyment and the desire to lord over material nature, emphasizing that these desires are rooted in illusion.

The desire to independently enjoy, independent of Krishna because in the spiritual world everyone is blissfully happy in association with Krishna. But if anybody wants independent enjoyment it is not possible in the spiritual world. Then the lord has created this material world and in the material world under illusion one may think I am independent, though we can never be actually independent because we are very, very tiny spirit souls. We cannot be independent. We cannot be independent. But we may get the feeling that I am independent under illusion and that's why the illusion energy is working to cover the real knowledge of who we are, the reality of Krishna being the maintainer, Krishna being the protector. The illusion energy is covering our vision of the reality. And under illusion I am thinking I am independent. Each person is thinking like that. And also not only that we desire independent enjoyment, we also desire to lord over material nature. And not only I desire, everyone desires like that.

### illusion, conditioned life, three gunas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wMc6qwXZeXw?t=279
**Verse References:** BG 14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the desire to be the lord creates disparity and illusion, leading to the laws of struggle for existence. He then introduces the concept of conditioned life and the influence of the three gunas (modes of material nature).

So, therefore there is disparity. How can each one think I want to be the lord? There can only be one lord. There cannot be many lords. So, therefore the reality is that under illusion somebody thinks I am the boss, I am the lord, I am the master, I am the enjoyer. Under illusion only. And that is created, that illusion is created because of which there appear to be laws which are like struggle for existence and survival of the fittest. So, Prabhupada further explains this very mentality of lording it over material nature is a root cause of conditioned life. What is the meaning of conditioned life? Conditioned life means we are subject to the conditions of material nature which are conditions produced by interaction of the three gunas. The three modes of material nature. They are always interacting and producing different circumstances, conditions, situations. Satvam rajas tamaiti gunas prakriti sambhavaha. This is explained in the 14th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. Generally people know that material matter or material energy or material nature means

material nature not only consists of eight elements it also consists of three gunas. This is secret that is revealed in the Bhagavad Gita by lord Krishna. the five elements or the eight elements. Five gross elements earth, water, fire, air, sky three subtle elements mind, intelligence, ego. But they do not know material nature consists of three gunas. Satva guna, raja guna, tamo guna and these three gunas are always interacting with each other producing different conditions. And under those conditions everyone is living. All those who are under the laws of nature. They are subject to the conditions imposed upon them by the three gunas interacting and producing so many changes. Continuously material nature is imposing different conditions. Adibhutam charo bhavaha. This is also explained in the 8th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. This material nature is constantly changing in a state of flux. Always. As long as material nature is active. As long as this created world is maintained constantly in a state of flux. So therefore we are all subject to these conditions. As long as we don't surrender to Krishna. As long as we don't take to devotional service. As long as we don't prepare ourselves to go back to Godhead. We are subject to these conditions. So that situation under which everyone is forced by material nature is called conditioned life. **Summary:** The speaker explains that the illusory energy creates disparity by creating stronger and weaker beings in every species. He contrasts this with the spiritual world, where there is no struggle for existence.

the illusory energy of the lord has created a disparity between conditioned living beings by creating the stronger and the weaker in every species of life. If you carefully observe every species there are many many living entities of that particular species. Among them there are stronger and weaker. That is because the mentality of lording it over others always exists in conditioned souls. Sosri Laprapad further explains the mentality of lording it over material nature and creation has naturally created a disparity. To facilitate the living entities lording over there are laws of karma working. And there is naturally a disparity. Some are stronger some are weaker in each species. And therefore there is the law of struggle for existence. Now Sosri Laprapad contrasts this with the spiritual world. He says in the spiritual world there is no such struggle for existence conspicuous by its absence in the spiritual world. This law of struggle for existence. There is no struggle in the spiritual world at all. Nobody has to work. Nobody has to earn his livelihood. No. Everybody is blissful.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.27 | 13-12-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Bhagavad Gita, Timeless Wisdom, Relieving Suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=49
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Those instructions are meant for relieving the burning heart in all circumstances of time and space."
**Summary:** Arjuna remembers Krishna's instructions in the Bhagavad Gita as a means to relieve suffering in all times and places, highlighting the Gita's timeless wisdom.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 27th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Desha Kalaartha Yuktaani Hrithapa Upashamanicha Haranti Smarata Chittam Govinda Abhitaanime. The meaning of this verse is: Now, I am attracting the attention of all of you to those instructions imparted to me by the personality of Godhead Govinda because they are impregnated with instructions for relieving the burning heart in all circumstances of time and space. Arjuna is speaking to Yudhishthira, and he is taking shelter of the impregnated heart, the instructions imparted by Krishna to him, the famous Bhagavad Gita. The instructions of Krishna given in the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, now Arjuna is remembering them, and he is telling those instructions are meant for relieving the burning heart in all circumstances of time and space. So, the timeless wisdom of the Bhagavad Gita is meant for all people in all places at all times.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Vedic Wisdom, Accessibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=150
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The Bhagavad Gita being spoken by the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the essence of all Vedic wisdom."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that the Bhagavad Gita is the essence of Vedic wisdom, condensed for those with limited time, and presented within the Mahabharata for the less intelligent.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada very nicely explains the gist of the instructions given by Krishna in Bhagavad Gita. Srila Prabhupada says, "Herein Arjuna refers to the instruction of the Bhagavad Gita, which was imparted to him by the Lord of the Bhagavad Gita on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. The Lord left behind him the instructions of the Bhagavad Gita not for the benefit of Arjuna alone, but also for all time and in all lands." The Bhagavad Gita being spoken by the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the essence of all Vedic wisdom. Entire Vedas have been condensed in just 700 verses in the Bhagavad Gita. It is nicely presented by the Lord himself for all who have very little time to go through the vast Vedic literatures like the Upanishads, Puranas, and Vedanta Sutras. It is put within the study of the great historical epic Mahabharata, which was especially prepared for the less intelligent class, namely the women, the laborers, and those who are worthless descendants of the Brahmana, Kshatriyas, and higher sections of the Vaishyas.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Guidance, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=248
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The Bhagavad Gita can be consulted in all critical times, not only for solace, for all kinds of mental agonies, but also for the way out of great entanglements."
**Summary:** Arjuna's remembrance of the Bhagavad Gita after Krishna's departure teaches us to consult it in critical times for solace and solutions, even though Arjuna, as a pure devotee, doesn't inherently need it.

The problem which arose in the heart of Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra was solved by the teachings of the Bhagavad Gita. Again, after the departure of the Lord from the vision of earthly people, when Arjuna was face to face with being vanquished in his acquired power and prominence, he wanted again to remember the great teachings of the Bhagavad Gita just to teach all concerned that the Bhagavad Gita can be consulted in all critical times, not only for solace, for all kinds of mental agonies, but also for the way out of great entanglements which may embarrass one in some critical hour. Actually, Arjuna is a pure devotee. There is no need for him to specifically consult some specific literature. His personification of the ideal devotee, ideal person who follows all the instructions of Krishna, but he is teaching us through his example how whenever we have any difficulty, we can rely on the teachings given by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Sound Representation, Krishna's Presence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=346
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "There is no difference between the sound representation of the Lord and the Lord himself."
**Summary:** The Bhagavad Gita is presented as a sound representation of Krishna, allowing one to derive the same benefit as Arjuna did in Krishna's personal presence, even though Krishna is not physically visible.

Everyone can take the instructions of the Lord. So, Srila Prabhupada says further, "The merciful Lord left behind him the great teachings of the Bhagavad Gita so that one can take the instructions of the Lord even when he is not visible to material eyesight." Krishna just didn't speak for his time when he was present. He has spoken this for posterity also. Material senses cannot have any estimation of the Supreme Lord, but by his inconceivable power, the Lord can incarnate himself to the sense perception of the conditioned souls in a suitable manner through the agency of matter, which is also another form of the Lord's manifested energy. Lord's manifested energy. Lord is completely spiritual, but he can be present in a material medium by his inconceivable power. Thus, the Bhagavad Gita or any authentic scriptural sound representation of the Lord is also the incarnation of the Lord. Krishna incarnated 5000 years back in his original form, transcendental form, but Krishna is present also in his sound representation in the form of Bhagavad Gita. Therefore, Srila Prabhupada says there is no difference between the sound representation of the Lord and the Lord himself. Krishna is identical. Krishna is identical with the Bhagavad Gita. One can derive the same benefit from the Bhagavad Gita as Arjuna did in the personal presence of the Lord.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Knowledge, Five Factors
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=478
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "In the Bhagavad Gita, there are five important factors of knowledge."
**Summary:** The Bhagavad Gita contains five key factors of knowledge: the Supreme Lord, the living being, nature, time and space, and the process of activity, which are explained using Sanskrit terms and English equivalents.

So, in the Bhagavad Gita, there are five important factors of knowledge, and they are number one, the Supreme Lord; number two, the living being; number three, nature; number four, time and space; number five, the process of activity. So, Ishwara, Jeeva, Prakriti, Kala, Karma, these are the Sanskrit terms. Srila Prabhupada has given the English equivalents: the Supreme Lord, the living being, nature, time, and the process of activity, Karma.

---
### Supreme Lord, Living Being, Material Nature, Karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=534
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We are a very, very, very, very, very tiny part of the Lord."
**Summary:** The Supreme Lord and the living being are qualitatively one, but the Lord is the whole, and we are tiny, inseparable parts. Material nature is inert, influenced by three modes, while time is eternal. Karma can either entrap or liberate us.

Out of these, the Supreme Lord and the living being are qualitatively one. The Supreme Lord is completely spiritual, and we are also, each one of us, we are spiritual beings. The difference between the two, still there is a difference between the Supreme Lord and each one of us. What is the difference? The difference between the two has been analyzed as the difference between the whole and the part and parcel. We are a very, very, very, very, very tiny part of the Lord, and parcel means we are inseparable. We cannot be separated from the Lord. We have an eternal connection, eternal relationship. Nature is inert matter, material nature is inert matter, displaying the interaction of the three different modes. Material nature is material energy. Energy means there is some force, and that force is the influence of the three gunas while they interact with each other. Eternal time and unlimited space are considered to be beyond the existence of the material nature. Eternal time, time is eternal, and unlimited space, space, are considered to be beyond the existence of the material nature. Now, what about the karma or activities? Activities of the living being are different varieties of aptitudes which can entrap or liberate the living being within and without material nature. We do some type of activities by which we become more and more entangled in this material nature. We can do some other types of activities in this material world which will liberate us from the entanglement. So, one set of activities will get us out, and the usual normal activities which most people do simply entangles them more and more.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Srimad Bhagavatam, Karma, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a8fDiJL6fa8?t=686
**Verse References:** BG 4.0
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavad Gita is the preliminary study of Srimad Bhagavatam."
**Summary:** The Bhagavad Gita concisely discusses these subjects, which are elaborated in the Srimad Bhagavatam. The Gita is a preliminary study for the Bhagavatam. Karma binds, while akarma liberates, illustrated by the difference between eating ordinary food and prasadam.

All these subject matters are discussed concisely in the Bhagavad Gita, and later, these subject matters are elaborated in the Bhagavatam for further enlightenment. Many times, Srila Prabhupada says Bhagavad Gita is the preliminary study of Srimad Bhagavatam. So, the topics of the Gita are described in the Bhagavatam in great detail. Now, Srila Prabhupada summarizes the Bhagavad Gita that out of the five subjects, the Supreme Lord, the living entity, nature and time, and space are eternal, but the living entity, nature, and time are under the direction of the Supreme Lord, who is absolute and completely independent of any other control. The Supreme Lord is supremely independent, completely independent, and we ourselves, each one of us, the living entity, and the material nature, and time, kala, all the three are under the control, complete control of the Supreme Lord. Material activity of the living being is beginningless, anadi karma phale padhi bhava navajale. Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur describes we cannot trace the beginning of our material activity in this material world, but it can be rectified by transferring into the spiritual quality. We can spiritualize our activities, our ordinary activities, and then the material reactions will stop. This is exactly the subject matter, the fourth chapter, karma and akarma. Karma means that which binds us. Akarma means that which liberates us. Just like you eat ordinary food, you become more and more bound up. You eat prasadam, you become liberated. The same activity of eating, but depending on whether you eat ordinary food or you eat prasadam, Krishna prasadam, there will be a difference in the effect of such eating, the action of eating.

---

We are devotee of Krishna. We are meant to serve Krishna eternally. ### meditation, bhakti yoga, ashtanga yoga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=50
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Since the Lord is absolute, deep meditation upon him is as good as yogic trance."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of verse 28, highlighting that meditating on Krishna is equivalent to yogic trance and superior to Ashtanga Yoga.

The 28th verse is as follows. Suta Vacha. Now Suta Goswami is narrating this entire incident to the sage of Sunayamisharanya. Now he is speaking. Evam Chintayato Jishno Krishna Pada Saro Ruham Sauhardena Atigardhena Shanta Asid Vimalamati Hi. The meaning of this verse is Suta Goswami said, thus being deeply absorbed in thinking of the instructions of the Lord, which were imparted in the great intimacy of friendship, and in thinking of his lotus feet, Arjuna's mind became pacified and free from all material contamination. So in the purpose Srila Prabhupada explains, since the Lord is absolute, deep meditation upon him is as good as yogic trance. The highest perfection of Ashtanga Yoga is Samadhi, total absorption in meditation on the Lord. And simply a devotee deeply thinks about the Supreme Lord, Krishna. He is immediately in Samadhi. See the difference between Bhakti and Ashtanga Yoga. Bhakti Yoga and Ashtanga Yoga.

### instructions, duty, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=137
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord's appearance within the mind of the devotee can at once drive away the miserable material defects."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Arjuna's contemplation of Krishna's instructions on the battlefield of Kurukshetra and how these instructions purify the mind from material contamination, comparing Krishna to the sun that dispels darkness.

The Lord is non-different from his name, form, quality, pastimes, entourage and specific actions. Arjuna began to think of the Lord's instructions on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. He just began to think, what is it that Krishna told me when I was confused and I was searching for an answer to what is my duty under the circumstances, when I am facing my own near and dear ones as the enemy on the opposite side. I have to kill them. My respectable Supreme Lord, my superiors, my dear relatives. How can I kill them? So this duty. He was confused about his duty. And he asked Krishna to instruct him. Shishya Steham Shadhi Maam Tvaam Prapannam. So now, Arjuna is beginning to think of those instructions after Krishna's disappearance, when he is feeling that separation from Krishna. Now he is thinking of those instructions. Now, Srila Prabhupada says, only those instructions which Krishna gave on the battlefield of Kurukshetra began to eliminate the tinges of material contamination in the mind of Arjuna. How does it work? Srila Prabhupada says, the Lord is like the sun. The sun's appearance means immediate dissipation of darkness or ignorance. And the Lord's appearance within the mind of the devotee can at once drive away the miserable material defects. This is a good instruction for us.

### remembrance, chanting, separation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=256
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Feelings of separation are also sources of transcendental bliss for a devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker advises remembering the Lord's name, form, qualities, and instructions to dispel ignorance and confusion, emphasizing the power of chanting Hare Krishna and the transcendental bliss derived even from the feeling of separation from Krishna.

Whenever we feel that there is some ignorance, or some darkness, or some confusion, or some lack of direction, simply we have to remember the Lord. Remember the Lord's name. Remember the Lord's form. Remember the Lord's qualities. Remember the Lord's instructions in the Bhagavad Gita. Remember the Lord's pastimes described in the Bhagavatam. And immediately the darkness will be dissipated. Lord Chaitanya has therefore recommended constant chanting of the name of the Lord for protection from all contamination of the material world. Hare Nama Hare Nama Hare Namaiva Kevalam. This is a recommendation of Lord Chaitanya. Kalao Nastyaiva Nastyaiva Nastyaiva Gatiranyatam. Kaliva, there is no other remedy. Only one remedy. Constant chanting of the holy name, form, qualities, pastimes, etc. of the Supreme Lord Krishna. The feeling of separation from the Lord is undoubtedly painful to the devotee, but because it is in connection with the Lord, it has a specific transcendental effect which pacifies the heart. The feeling of separation from Krishna is actually soothing for the heart, even though it is actually a kind of painful situation for a devotee. But even that has got a spiritual effect of pacifying, soothing the heart. Feelings of separation are also transcendental bliss. Sorry. Feelings of separation are also sources of transcendental bliss for a devotee.

### transcendental bliss, separation, material vs spiritual
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=378
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Separation from the Lord is a source of transcendental bliss of a different kind."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the transcendental bliss derived from separation from Krishna with the pain of material separation, emphasizing the unique spiritual dimension of the devotee's experience.

Of course, when he is in association with the Lord, he is very, very happy, very pleased. It's blissful. But feeling of separation is also a source of transcendental bliss of a different kind. And they are never comparable to contaminated material feelings of separation. In this material world, we also sometimes feel that there is some person who is our near and dear one. When we are separated from them, we feel the pain of separation. But what pain of separation Arjuna is feeling now after separation from Krishna, after Krishna's disappearance from this material world, is not the same as our material feelings of separation from some material relationship. They are not the same. In this material world, when there is separation, we feel pain. And we lament about it. But in the spiritual atmosphere, in the spiritual dimension of existence, separation from the Lord is a source of transcendental bliss of a different kind. It's a variety of bliss.

---
### devotion, material desires, spiritual identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=475
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our spiritual identity is, we are eternally meant to serve Krishna, lovingly."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain verse 29, stating that Arjuna's remembrance of Krishna's lotus feet increased his devotion and eliminated trash in his thoughts, which are material desires that obscure our spiritual identity.

Twenty-ninth verse. Vasudeva Angri Anudhyana Paribhramhita Ramhasa Bhaktya Nirmati Tashyesha Kashaya Dishano Arjunaha. The meaning of this verse is, Arjuna's constant remembrance of the lotus feet of Lord Shri Krishna rapidly increased his devotion and as a result, all the trash in his thoughts subsided. In the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, material desires in the mind are the trash of material contamination. If we have material desires in our mind, they are nothing but garbage, which is somehow sitting in our heart or consciousness. And Srila Prabhupada explains, by such contamination, the living being is faced with so many compatible and incompatible things. Different kinds of material desires bring about different kinds of compatible and incompatible things that discourage the very existence of spiritual identity. Our spiritual identity is, we are eternally meant to serve Krishna, lovingly.

---
### devotional service, material desires, duality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=579
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In this material world, there is nothing to like or dislike. Everything is simply a source of misery."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the bliss of devotional service with the distress caused by material desires, highlighting the duality of likes and dislikes that characterize material existence and obscure our true spiritual nature.

In such service, there is no distress, there is no suffering, there is no pain, there is no ignorance, there is no illusion, there is no misery, none of those things. But, when there is material desire in the heart, then there is so much of incompatible and compatible feelings, and those are opposed to our spiritual identity. They are opposed to devotional service. Birth after birth, the conditioned soul is entrapped with so many pleasing and displeasing elements. Itchadvesha. Samuthena dvanva mohena bharata. We are born always with some likes and dislikes. And they are always confusing for us. Because, whole life in a particular body, we are simply chasing something which we think we like. And, we are trying to avoid something which we dislike. But, in this material world, there is nothing to like or dislike. Everything is simply a source of misery. Dukkhalayam, ashashvatam. So, there is nothing to really aspire for. There is nothing which we think if we avoid we will be happy. No. Material existence is not meant for us at all.

---
### spiritual life, transcendence, material desires
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Don't chase these material desires. They are all worthless."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that spiritual life in association with Krishna is the true purpose of our existence, contrasting it with the false and temporary nature of material desires and their reactions.

We are meant for spiritual life in association with Krishna, in devotional service to him. And that is total bliss. There is nothing compatible or incompatible in that spiritual existence. So, all these pleasing and displeasing elements, in material existence, are false and temporary. They accumulate due to our reactions to material desires. How do they get accumulated? Because of the reactions of the material desires. But, when we get in touch with the transcendental Lord in his variegated energies by devotional service, his spiritual energy, devotional service is always under the protection of the devotional energy, spiritual energy. Then, when we touch Hare Krishna, when I say Hare Krishna, I am touching the transcendental sound, spiritual sound of Krishna's name. And by that, what happens? The naked forms of all material desires become manifest. I can clearly see the nature of these material desires which are there in the heart. And the intelligence of the living being is specified in its true color. The intelligence will begin working. Vyavasaya Atmika Buddhi. It will reveal to us, don't chase these material desires. They are all worthless.

---
### bhagavad gita, transcendence, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EQgYwvsGlKQ?t=801
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even any one instruction of Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita, you remember, immediately all your confusion, your ignorance, your illusion, your likes, dislikes, material likes and dislikes, everything will be dissipated."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by illustrating how remembering Krishna's instructions in the Bhagavad Gita can instantly dispel material contamination and lead to transcendence, using the analogy of a spark of light dispelling darkness.

Don't chase these material desires. As soon as Arjuna turned his attention towards the instructions of the Lord, as they are inculcated in the Bhagavad Gita, his true color of eternal association with the Lord became manifest. Arjuna began to feel the effect of associating with the Lord even through his instructions, transcendental instructions in the Bhagavad Gita, in the form of Bhagavad Gita. And thus he felt freed from all material contaminations. Immediately all material contamination is dissipated. It is cleansed away. Instantaneous cleansing. It's exactly like you are sitting in abject darkness. Somebody brings in a tiny spark of light. Immediately darkness dispels. Immediately. So like that, even any one instruction of Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita, you remember, immediately all your confusion, your ignorance, your illusion, your likes, dislikes, material likes and dislikes, everything will be dissipated. And you simply become situated in transcendence, beyond all material illusion, beyond material duality, beyond material confusion, everything beyond matter. That is spiritual existence. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.30 | 16-12-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### conditioned soul, gunas, material existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oWz8iip5bfI?t=32
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When you elevate your consciousness to the spiritual platform, you are above the influence, you are freed from the influence of the three gunas."
**Summary:** This segment defines a conditioned soul as one influenced by the three gunas and contrasts it with spiritual existence, which transcends these influences.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Chayiva Narottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tatho Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 30th verse of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, because of the Lord's pastimes and activities, and because of his absence, it appeared that Arjuna forgot the instructions left by the personality of Godhead. But factually, this was not the case, and again he became Lord of his senses. So, Srila Prabhupada explains what does this mean. A conditioned soul, I have explained what is conditioned soul in a previous session. Conditioned soul means one who is subject to material conditions enforced by the interaction between the body and the soul. So, we have the combination of the three gunas. Three gunas means three kinds of influences: Sattvic influence, Rajasic influence, Tamsic influence on our consciousness in material existence. So, we are called conditioned soul. So, we behave, we act, we think, we feel, we have got the drive to work according to the combination of the influence of these three gunas. But that is material existence. When you elevate your consciousness to the spiritual platform, you are above the influence, you are freed from the influence of the three gunas. You simply act spiritually.

---
### eternal time, internal potency, transcendental activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oWz8iip5bfI?t=159
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual activities are not at all like material activities. They have nothing to do with the three gunas. They have nothing to do with Kala, material time."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the Lord is not influenced by time or the gunas, making His activities transcendental and distinct from ordinary material actions.

So, here Srila Prabhupada begins his purport by telling, a conditioned soul is enwrapped, enwrapped means completely covered, in his fructive activities by the force of eternal time. Kala, through the influence of the gunas, forces us to act in a way that is according to the past karma. But the Supreme Lord, when he incarnates on the earth, is not influenced by Kala. Kala, nor is the Supreme Lord influenced, by the material conception of past, present, and future. Neither eternal time in the material world, nor the conception of past, present, and future, is influencing the Lord. So, therefore, the activities of the Lord, which may appear like ordinary activities of this material world, are not ordinary. The activities of the Lord, are eternal, sanātana, and they are manifestations of his ātma māyā, or internal potency. Our ordinary activities are due to the interaction of the three gunas. Prakṛte kriyamānāni gunai karmāni sarvaśaha ahankāra vimūdātmā kartāḥ mitimanyate. Whatever material activities are happening, they are not the activities of the Lord, but the activities of the Lord, and the activities of the Lord, and the activities of the Lord, and the activities of the Lord, and the activities of the Lord. They are carried out by the three gunas, but I think I am the doer under the influence of illusion. But spiritual activities are not at all like material activities. They have nothing to do with the three gunas. They have nothing to do with Kala, material time. They are completely transcendental to material time and material modes. And therefore they are called under the internal potency or spiritual potency or spiritual energy, divine energy, transcendental energy, more than divine, transcendental energy.

---
### Krishna's pastimes, transcendence, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oWz8iip5bfI?t=289
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is transcendence himself. And all of Krishna's activities are transcendental activities."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the spiritual nature of Krishna's pastimes, even seemingly ordinary ones like crawling, and how they are perceived by devotees.

All pastimes or activities of the Lord are spiritual in nature. All activities of the Supreme Lord are spiritual by nature. Even stealing the butter, even crawling in the courtyard of Mother Yashoda like an ordinary child. All those activities are completely transcendental. They are not ordinary activities. Therefore Shukadeva Goswami, he is speaking to all the assembled saintly persons and to Pariksit Maharaj. And Shukadeva Goswami is speaking very very gravely about transcendental subject matter, about spiritual subject matter. Trying to enlighten Pariksit and through Pariksit all the other saintly persons. But he is describing Krishna's childhood pastimes. And everybody is seriously sitting and hearing. And Shukadeva Goswami is telling, Krishna was crawling on the floor and Mother Yashoda was enjoying seeing her beloved child very beautifully decorated with nice ornaments and nice dress and nice beautiful smile on Krishna's face. So what is all this got to do with transcendence? Krishna is transcendence himself. And all of Krishna's activities are transcendental activities. How are they different? Ordinary child gives pleasure to its mother by its movements, by its crawling, by its being decorated by the mother. Here Mother Yashoda is also similarly apparently decorating a small child and taking pleasure in the child crawling, smiling, playing. What's the difference? So that is explained by Shukadeva Goswami in the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam. Ittham satam brahma-sukhanu bhutya dasyaṁgatānām para-daivatena māyāśritānām naradārakena sākaṁ vijagru kṛta-punya-punjā. Krishna is no ordinary child. He is the same supreme Brahman who is the object of worship by all devotees who have accepted him as their master. And he is the same object of meditation, of worship or the destination of the impersonalists who are after the brahma-jyoti, Brahman effulgence, that is his own bodily effulgence. So that Krishna is appearing as a beloved child of Nanda and Yashoda, simply for the sake of pastimes, that's all.

---
### transcendental protection, Krishna's mission, devotee's role
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oWz8iip5bfI?t=498
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna wants his pure devotees to rule the world. And Krishna has arranged this battle simply for eliminating all the unnecessary burden in the form of demoniac forces on this earth planet."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the transcendental nature of activities associated with Krishna, such as the battle of Kurukshetra, and emphasizes Krishna's mission for devotees to rule and guide others towards Krishna consciousness.

So they are completely transcendental, discussing them, hearing about them, describing them, participating or being a spectator of such activities is completely spiritual. There is no tinge of any material guna or material reactions, nothing. It so appeared that Arjuna and the Lord were engaged in the battle of Kurukshetra as the other party was also engaged. But factually, the Lord was executing his mission of incarnation and association with his eternal friend Arjuna. Like so many battles are fought. This battle of Kurukshetra was another battle between the Pandavas and Kauravas. And the whole world was divided, all the kings were divided into two parties. Some took the side of Pandavas, others took the side of Kauravas and they were fighting. Whoever wins the battle will rule the world. No, it is not as simple as that. Krishna being present on the battlefield of Kurukshetra and his pure devotees, the Pandavas, under the complete transcendental protection of Krishna, they are present on the battlefield. They are executing Krishna's mission. Krishna wants his pure devotees to rule the world. And Krishna has arranged this battle simply for eliminating all the unnecessary burden in the form of demoniac forces on this earth planet. He has arranged this battle. They will all be killed and Yudhishthira Maharaj and the other Pandavas, they will come out victorious by Krishna's plan, Krishna's arrangement, Krishna's transcendental mission in this material world. Pure devotees should rule this world. Pure devotees should direct the people in general towards Krishna consciousness.

---
### Krishna consciousness, devotion, eternal truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oWz8iip5bfI?t=650
**Verse References:** BG 18.65
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Always think of me, Krishna says. The mind should not forget him."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Arjuna's actions were aligned with Krishna's mission, leading to his transcendental position, and emphasizes the importance of constantly thinking of Krishna as instructed in the Bhagavad Gita.

Therefore, such apparently material activities of Arjuna did not drive him away from his transcendental position, but on the contrary revived his consciousness of the songs of the Lord as he sang them personally. Krishna, whatever instructions Krishna gave on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, those instructions Arjuna is trying to recollect. And he is remembering the activities of the Lord also in connection with that battle. And simply by remembering them, Arjuna is re-established in his transcendental position as the eternal friend of Krishna, as the associate of Krishna, as the participant in Krishna's Leela. So all illusion, ignorance are absent in such thoughts. Thinking of Krishna is different from thinking of ordinary subject matter. Big, big difference. The revival of consciousness, the way Arjuna's consciousness is revived simply by thinking of Krishna's instructions, is assured by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 18th chapter, 65th verse. What does Krishna say? What is the meaning of this verse in the Bhagavad Gita? One should think of the Lord always. Always think of me, Krishna says. The mind should not forget him. One should become a devotee of the Lord. And offer obeisances unto him. Devotee means he bows down before the Lord. One who lives in that fashion becomes undoubtedly endowed with the blessing of the Lord. Guaranteed to get blessing of the Lord if you live as a devotee, constantly thinking of Krishna, bowing down before him. And in this way, by the blessing of the Lord, what happens? One achieves the shelter of Krishna's lotus feet. Get complete shelter at his lotus feet. Completely protected. Always safe under his protection. There is nothing to doubt about this eternal truth. What is told in the Bhagavad Gita is eternal truth for all times, for all people, in all circumstances, in all places. Eternal truth.

---
### Krishna's mission, bhakti, back to Godhead
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oWz8iip5bfI?t=850
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The mission of the Lord is that we revive our lost or forgotten relationship with Krishna through the activities of bhakti."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing the importance of aligning one's life with Krishna's mission, which involves reviving our relationship with Him through bhakti, ultimately leading to going back to Godhead.

Because Arjuna was his confidential friend, the secret was disclosed to him. In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna tells Arjuna, You are my most dear devotee, situated in a friendly relationship. Therefore, I am disclosing the most confidential knowledge. Arjuna had no desire to fight with his relatives. But he fought for the mission of the Supreme Lord Krishna. So Arjuna's fighting is also participating in the mission of Krishna. So Arjuna was always engaged in the execution of Krishna's mission only. And therefore, after the Lord's departure, he remained in the same transcendental position, even though it appeared like he forgot all the instructions of the Bhagavad Gita. One should therefore adjust the activities of life in pace with the mission of the Lord. And by doing this, one is sure to return back home, back to Godhead. So what is the mission of the Lord now in this material world? The mission of the Lord is that we revive our lost or forgotten relationship with Krishna through the activities of bhakti. And in this way, we prepare ourselves to go back to Godhead. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### Krishna's presence, transcendental presence, energies
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jmV-Mn6PQCk?t=573
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is always present in the form of his instructions."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is always present through his instructions, form, pastimes, and qualities, and that a devotee never feels His absence when Krishna conscious.

So, therefore, the instructions of the Bhagavad Gita eradicate all kinds of material conceptions. So, Arjuna could realize that the Lord was still present before him by his instruction, by his form, by his pastimes, by his qualities and everything else related to him. Consider this, that Krishna is always present in the form of his instructions. Eternally, those instructions are true. So, if Krishna is present, where is the cause for lamentation that Krishna passed away, Krishna disappeared, Krishna went away to his personal abode? No, Krishna is present. He is present in one form, yet another form, yet another form, unlimited form. Unlimited forms. Krishna is always present. So, in a sense, the devotee never feels absence of Krishna if a devotee is Krishna conscious. So, Arjuna could realize that Lord Krishna, his friend, was still present before him by his transcendental presence in different non-dual energies. All energies are Krishna's. So, in the form of energy, Krishna is present. And there is no question of attainment of the association of the Lord by another change of body under the influence of time and space. Just like sometimes people ask that I lost my father.

---
### material form, spirit soul, spiritual identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jmV-Mn6PQCk?t=678
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The spirit soul has got spiritual identity, which is eternal."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the common question of reuniting with deceased loved ones, clarifying that the material body is temporary and the spirit soul has an eternal, spiritual identity.

And you say my father has gone to some other body. Can I actually again associate with my father, whom I am missing, by myself taking another body like he has taken? Because our conception of existence in the material world is simply based on body. That's all. But the truth is, this body is simply some material form. Temporary material form. It has nothing to do with the real person, the spirit soul. The spirit soul has got spiritual identity, which is eternal. The material form is simply a dress, a covering, a form, a material object. It has no life by itself. Material form has no life by itself. Whereas the spiritual identity is ever-present. Eternally present. In eternal platform. Spiritual platform. In spiritual form. In spiritual relationship with Krishna. Eternally present.

---
### absolute knowledge, devotional service, Krishna's presence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jmV-Mn6PQCk?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One can be in association with the Lord constantly, even in this present life."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that through absolute knowledge and devotional service, one can constantly be in association with the Lord, even in this life, by hearing, chanting, thinking of, and worshipping Him.

So therefore, Prabhupada concludes by telling, by attainment of absolute knowledge, one can be in association with the Lord constantly, even in this present life. How? Simply by hearing, chanting, thinking of and worshipping the Supreme Lord. What is true for Arjuna, is true for everyone at all times. Even now, if we chant constantly Krishna's holy name, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, we should understand and should realize this fact, that Krishna is appearing in the form of that holy name, transcendental sound on my tongue. And if I hear, Krishna is entering, as the transcendental sound within, through my ears, and He can enter my heart and touch my soul. Touch me. He means my soul. And then, I immediately feel the loving presence of Krishna. The soothing touch of Krishna. And this is a fact.

---
### transcendental truth, devotional service, revelation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jmV-Mn6PQCk?t=872
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Absolute Lord, will reveal Himself to you, within your heart, and you will never feel His absence under any circumstances."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the experience of Krishna's presence is not mere sentiment but a transcendental truth, attainable through devotional service, leading to the revelation of the Absolute Lord within one's heart.

This is not some kind of sentiment, or some material adjustment in the mind. No. It is a transcendental truth. One can see Him, one can feel His presence, even in this present life, simply by understanding the Advayagyana Lord, or the Absolute Lord, through the process of devotional service. You perform devotional service, beginning with hearing and chanting, about Krishna, and then, the Advayagyana Lord, the Absolute Lord, will reveal Himself to you, within your heart, and you will never feel His absence under any circumstances. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
