### Bhagavatam, detachment, renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=40
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.32
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Yudhishthira decided to go back home, back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam (1.15.32) which describes Yudhishthira's decision to retire from worldly life and return to Godhead upon hearing of Krishna's departure and the end of the Yadu dynasty.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udeerayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the book of Bhagavad Gita, from the 32nd verse of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. This chapter is describing the Pandavas retire timely. Nishamya Bhagavan Margam Samstham Yadukulasya Cha Swapathaya Matim Chakrae Nibhratatma Yudhishthiraha. The meaning of this 32nd verse is upon hearing of Lord Krishna's returning to his abode, and upon understanding the end of the Yadu dynasty's earthly manifestation, Maharaj Yudhishthira decided to go back home, back to Godhead.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, instruction, application
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=101
**Verse References:** BG
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavad Gita was not just spoken for Arjuna alone. It is spoken for everyone or anyone who wants to actually go back home, back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Yudhishthira, like Arjuna, turned to the Bhagavad Gita for guidance after Krishna's departure, emphasizing that the Gita's teachings are universal and meant for anyone seeking to return to Godhead.

Shrila Prabhupada explains in the purport, Maharaj Yudhishthira also turned his attention to the instructions of the Bhagavad Gita after hearing about the Lord's departure from the vision of earthly people. In the earlier verse, it was described, Arjuna turned towards the instructions of Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita. Arjuna was personally instructed by the Lord in the subject matter of the Bhagavad Gita, and that was during the battle, just before the beginning of the battle of Kurukshetra. But now, after the Lord's departure, when Arjuna was heartbroken, then in order to pacify himself, he recollected and remembered and applied the same instructions of Bhagavad Gita, which Krishna had spoken before the battle of Kurukshetra. Now, this verse describes, Maharaj Yudhishthira, the elder brother of Arjuna, also turned his attention to the instructions of Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita. You see, so Bhagavad Gita was not just spoken for Arjuna alone.

---
### transcendental, appearance, disappearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's appearance and disappearance is totally transcendental. It is not like the appearance or the birth and death of conditioned souls like us."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the transcendental nature of Krishna's appearance and disappearance, contrasting it with the forced birth and death of conditioned souls, and emphasizing Krishna's complete independence and control over these events.

It is spoken for everyone or anyone who wants to actually go back home, back to Godhead. According to Krishna's instructions, we understand we belong to Krishna, we are meant to actually prepare ourselves to go back to Krishna in his personal abode. And perfect instructions are given by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita. So Srila Prabhupada continues, he began to deliberate on the Lord's way of appearance and departure. So Krishna's appearance and disappearance is totally transcendental. It is not like the appearance or the birth and death of conditioned souls like us. Srila Prabhupada says further, the mission of the Lord's appearance and disappearance in the mortal universe is completely dependent on his supreme will. He is not forced to appear or disappear by any superior energy, as the living beings appear and disappear, being forced by the laws of nature. Our birth and death is forced upon us. We don't choose to either take birth or die. We don't choose. But Krishna's appearance and disappearance looks like the birth and death of ordinary living beings, but it is entirely a transcendental pastime of the Supreme Lord. It is completely dependent on his supreme will. He is completely independent and exactly as he wants it, it will happen.

---
### incarnation, spiritual world, forms of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=312
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is present in many many many many many places in many different forms and he appears and disappears without disturbing the other forms of appearance in other places."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the simultaneous presence of the Lord in various forms and places, both in the spiritual and material worlds, highlighting His expansions and incarnations to serve the desires of His devotees.

Srila Prabhupada continues, whenever the Lord likes, he can appear himself anywhere and everywhere without disturbing his appearance and disappearance in any other place. So simultaneously, the Lord is present in many many many many many places in many different forms and he appears and disappears without disturbing the other forms of appearance in other places. So simultaneously, he is in the spiritual world. In the spiritual world also, he is present in many many forms. His original form is Krishna, Shamasundara, and he is present in the spiritual world, in different forms according to the devotees' desire to serve him. And he expands for the sake of devotees as Balarama, as Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha, Kesava, Narayana, Madhava, Govinda, Madhusudana, so many forms. Each of these forms particularly the Narayana forms are predominators of the spiritual planets, the Vaikuntha Lokas. And in those Lokas, they have their devotees and they are receiving the service and reciprocating with the devotees. Similarly, Krishna also, in his personal abode, Goloka Vrindavana, he is reciprocating with his devotees. The devotees who are serving him out of love. So this is the truth about the personality of Godhead.

---
### material world, incarnations, Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=453
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "While he is present in the form of Maha Vishnu, Garbhodakshayi Vishnu, Shirodakshayi Vishnu, Paramatma, he also appears in the form of incarnations, past time incarnations, Leela Avatara."
**Summary:** The speaker details the Lord's presence in the material world through various Vishnu expansions (Maha Vishnu, Garbhodakshayi Vishnu, Shirodakshayi Vishnu, Paramatma) and incarnations (avatars) like Rama, Narasimha, and Krishna.

Now, not only in the spiritual world, he is also present in the material world. Which is the first incarnation in the material world? It is the incarnation of the Supreme Lord as Maha Vishnu at the beginning of the creation. Then, Maha Vishnu expands as Garbhodakshayi Vishnu to enter into each universe which he himself generates by his spiritual potency. And, that Garbhodakshayi Vishnu further expands as Shirodakshayi Vishnu, the maintainer Vishnu. And, this maintainer Vishnu further expands as Paramatma in the heart of every living being and enters into everything, every spiritual material atom, every atom of material manifestation, matter, material objects. And, he completely controls everything in existence in the material world in that way. Now, while he is present in the form of Maha Vishnu, Garbhodakshayi Vishnu, Shirodakshayi Vishnu, Paramatma, he also appears in the form of incarnations, past time incarnations, Leela Avatara. He is also present as Guna Avatara, as Brahma, as Shiva. He is present in the form of Shakya Vesha Avatara. Empowered incarnations. He is present as or rather he appears in so many different past time incarnations, Leela Avatara, Rama, Narasimha, Varaha, Krishna, Balarama in the material world.

---
### unity, simultaneous presence, analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=581
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "They appear as if they are independent of each other. But it is the same Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the unity of the Supreme Lord despite His diverse forms and incarnations, using the analogy of the sun's simultaneous presence in different parts of the world to illustrate this concept.

Now, this does not disturb or in any way affect any of the other forms or incarnations of the Lord. They appear as if they are independent of each other. But it is the same Supreme Lord. Krishna, Rama in the spiritual world, in the material world, as Mahavishnu, as Garbhodakshayi Vishnu are all the same person, the same Supreme Personality of Godhead. So that is being explained by Srila Prabhupada here. He is like the sun. The sun appears and disappears on its own accord at any place without disturbing his presence in other places. Just like now, at this time, it is 7.30 pm. So, the sun has set for our vision in the place we are now. I am in Bangalore. So, the sun has set now. And, the sun simultaneously is rising somewhere. Simultaneously, the sun is overhead somewhere else, some other part of the world. So, the appearance and disappearance of the sun, the rising and setting of the sun relative to my vision in one place on the earth has nothing to do with, is not disturbing the sun's presence in other parts of the world. So, this is an example that is given by Srila Prabhupada to explain the Lord's appearance and disappearance is undisturbed.

---
### liberation, transcendental knowledge, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=694
**Verse References:** BG 4.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Anyone who factually understands the transcendental appearance and disappearance of the Lord by his inconceivable energy becomes liberated from the laws of birth and death."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the sun analogy and quotes the Bhagavad Gita to explain that understanding the transcendental nature of the Lord's appearance and disappearance leads to liberation from the cycle of birth and death.

His appearance and disappearance in other parts of the world or in other places in other times is undisturbed. So, Srila Prabhupada explains the sun example further. The sun appears in the morning in India without disappearing from the western hemisphere. The sun is present everywhere and anywhere all over the solar system. But, it so appears that in a particular place the sun appears in the morning and also disappears at some fixed time in the evening. The time limitation even of the sun is of no concern. And so, what to speak of the Supreme Lord who is a creator and controller of the sun. If the sun itself is independently appearing and disappearing in different parts of the universe, then what to speak of the Supreme Lord? He can appear and disappear anywhere according to his own sweet will. Therefore, in the Bhagavad Gita, it is stated that anyone who factually understands the transcendental appearance and disappearance of the Lord by his inconceivable energy becomes liberated from the laws of birth and death and is placed in the eternal spiritual sky where the Vaikuntha planets are. Janma karma chame divyam evam yo naithi mamethi sorjuna punar janma naithi. Understanding the transcendental nature of Krishna's appearance and activities.

---
### realization, liberation, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7MX47dsScHo?t=804
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All our problems are solved without any separate endeavor simply by becoming Krishna conscious and returning to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of hearing from authentic sources, realizing the transcendental nature of Krishna, and becoming Krishna conscious to achieve liberation and return to the spiritual realm.

Janma karma chame divyam. Even though they look like ordinary human beings birth and actions but they are different entirely different. This is what we have to hear from the authentic scriptures from the Acharyas from Srila Prabhupada to hear and understand and become fixed up in this understanding in the realization and that will clear away all misconceptions about our own self. And then when we have the proper understanding properly situated without any misconception then we are eligible to become liberated from material influence material and that is called ultimate liberation when we are placed in the eternal spiritual sky in one of the Vaikuntha planets where we belong actually. There that means in the Vaikuntha planets such liberated persons can eternally be liberated from material existence. So all our problems are solved without any separate endeavor simply by becoming Krishna conscious and returning to Krishna. I will stop here. Thank you very much.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.33 | 19-12-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Mahabharata, Srimad Bhagavatam, Devotee's perspective
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But Mahabharata may not help us understand the exact position of especially the devotees of Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how the Srimad Bhagavatam provides a deeper understanding of the devotees of Krishna compared to the Mahabharata.

Because the descriptions in the Mahabharata about the different difficulties and troubles Kunti went through is kind of bewildering to people that such a great devotee had to undergo so much tribulation in this world. So, it is not at all a tribulation for the devotee, when the devotee is participating in the pastimes of the Lord in a particular role, according to the will of the Lord. So, if we have to understand the real purport of the Mahabharata, then we have to hear that or understand that from the Srimad Bhagavatam. In the Garuda Purana, it is said, Srimad Bhagavatam is Artho-yam Brahma Sutranam is the explanation of the Vedanta Sutras, is a natural commentary on the Vedanta Sutras by the self-same author, Vyasadeva. And Bharata-artha-minirnayam, it is also giving the purport of the Mahabharata, Bharata-artha, referring to the Mahabharata. The Mahabharata describes so many historical incidents and also describes Krishna's pastimes related to those historical incidents, in connection with those historical incidents. But Mahabharata may not help us understand the exact position of especially the devotees of Krishna.

---
### Kunti, Tribulation, Mahabharata
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=275
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It appears like Kunti was very, very, very, very unfortunate personality simply undergoing one misery after another."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates Kunti's life story as described in the Mahabharata, highlighting the many difficulties and tribulations she faced from her youth to raising the Pandavas.

So, you understand the position of the devotees of Krishna from the Bhagavatam, particularly the incidents in Mahabharata. Just like, to give an example, in the Mahabharata, there is a description that Kunti was feeling very, very sorry that she had to undergo so many tribulations just because of her position in her parents' house, even as an unmarried young girl. She was blessed by Durvasamuni to be able to call upon any demigod and conceive a child through that demigod. Now, she was a young girl. She just happened to try out chanting that mantra, which Durvasamuni had given her. And she is not yet married. She called upon the sun god. And then sun god appeared and said, Now you will conceive a child, but your virginity will be intact. So, she had a child. And she didn't know how to. She is unmarried. She never wanted to have a child. She was so young. So, anyway, with a very heavy heart, she just abandoned the child. And that was Karna. And then later on she got married to Pandu. And Pandu died an untimely death. Then she was left behind with five sons of Pandu, the Pandavas. And she was the widow mother of these five sons, Pandu's widow. And they were deprived of the kingdom after that. And so many tribulations, one after another, because Duryodhana had some enmity against the Pandavas. So, Duryodhana was giving trouble to the Pandavas. And Kunti, she also had to suffer, because she is not independent. To do anything separately she is with her sons and whatever the sons whatever difficulty the sons undergo she is also a part of that so it appears like Kunti was very, very, very, very unfortunate personality simply undergoing one misery after another. So in the Mahabharata there is a description that she is lamenting her miseries

---
### Kunti, Bhagavatam, Devotional Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So she is not at all lamenting about those difficulties rather she is welcoming them so this perspective is the speciality of the confidential knowledge in the Bhagavatam that is given to us"
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the Mahabharata's portrayal of Kunti's lamentation with the Bhagavatam's depiction of her offering prayers and welcoming difficulties as opportunities to remember Krishna.

But in the Bhagavatam we see Kunti offering prayers to Krishna and telling my dear Lord Krishna whenever we had difficulty you are always there to save us from calamities and solve our problems. And I wish that those difficulties or problems come again. Again and again because simply by remembering you when we are in difficulty because we are devotees we are dependent on you we are able to completely get rid of the material bondage we are assured of ultimate liberation simply by thinking of you remembering you by praying to you. So she is not at all lamenting about those difficulties rather she is welcoming them so this perspective is the speciality of the confidential knowledge in the Bhagavatam that is given to us similarly here it's being explained when she heard that Krishna has gone back to his personal abode and the Yadu dynasty also has ended their earthly manifestation then she decided.

---
### Devotional Service, Krishna's Lila, Eternal Association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=569
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So pure devotees only business is devotional service that's all."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Kunti, upon hearing of Krishna's departure, fully engaged in devotional service, emphasizing her eternal association with Krishna and the nature of pure devotees.

To completely engage herself in pure devotional service focus her attention completely on Krishna as a pure devotee it's natural for her to do that and she gained release from the course of material existence finished her material existence is over. She had come for participating Krishna's Lila and it's over elsewhere it is described she is the eternal associate of Krishna in the material world for his earthly pastimes. For his material pastimes in the material world so Srila Prabhupada says in the purport the setting of the sun does not mean the end of the sun it means that the sun is out of our sight similarly the end of the mission of the Lord on a particular planet or universe only means that he is out of our sight the end of the Yadu dynasty also does not mean that it is annihilating the Lord. It disappears along with the Lord out of our sight both the Lord and his pure devotees they appear and disappear apparent to our vision they are all eternally present eternally present in the spiritual world eternally present in the spiritual world they are never absent in the spiritual world. But they also appear in the material world as required for the Lord's pastime in the material world for our sake as Maharaj Yudhishtira decided to prepare to go back to Godhead so also Kunti decided because both are pure devotees of the Lord so pure devotees only business is devotional service that's all.

---
### Devotional Service, Bhagavad Gita, Krishna's Instructions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=689
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Always be engaged in my devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of constant engagement in devotional service, referencing Krishna's instructions in the Bhagavad Gita.

Whether in this situation or that situation whether in distress or in happiness whether in the presence of the Lord or in the apparent absence of the Lord in all circumstances at all times in all situations in all circumstances always they are engaged in pure devotional service and that's what Krishna instructs in the Bhagavad Gita. Always be engaged in my devotional service. Man mana bhava madbhakto madhyaji mam namaskuru. Several places in the Bhagavad Gita Krishna says always engage in my devotional service in different ways. tasmat sarveshu kaaleshu maa manusmara yudhya chak. You always engage in my devotional service. You always think of me. You always remember me. Always worship me. Always discuss about me. Katayantas chamam nityam. So like this the pure devotees are teaching us to always engage in pure devotional service. So Srila Prabhupada further says

---
### Devotional Service, Going Back to Godhead, Passport
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=778
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Going back to Godhead is guaranteed for pure devotees who are fully engaged in transcendental devotional service of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of a passport to explain how devotional service guarantees one's return to Godhead, emphasizing that death is not the end for devotees.

Thus she fully engaged herself in the transcendental devotional service of the Lord which guarantees one a passport for going back to Godhead after quitting this present material body. See passport means as we are citizens of India the Indian government gives us this document called passport which means our travel in foreign countries may be without any hindrance. So without any hindrance we may be allowed to travel in other countries based on our bona fides of being citizens of this country. So going back to Godhead is guaranteed for pure devotees who are fully engaged in transcendental devotional service of the Lord. When they quit this material world or this place the earthly planet it doesn't mean that everything is finished for them. No. They are simply quitting this place to go to the next place where the Lord wants them to go. And they are always guided by the Lord. Even while in this world they are guided by the Lord and even their travel or their transfer from this world to another place where the Lord's pastimes are going on is also guided by the Lord. So they are not destroyed in any way. Their devotional service does not come to an end.

---
### Spiritualization, Devotional Service, Abode of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVgdAvQhC30?t=921
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The beginning of devotional service to the Lord is the beginning of spiritualizing the present body."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how devotional service spiritualizes the body and consciousness, leading to a detachment from material existence and ultimately allowing one to reach the abode of the Lord.

It is always continuously going on. The beginning of devotional service to the Lord is the beginning of spiritualizing the present body. See we have a material body when we take birth in this material world. And that is due to our own karma, our material activities. But when we begin spiritual life it means that we want to end this material existence and we want to reinstate ourselves in our spiritual position as eternal devotees of Krishna in His personable. So spiritualizing this present body begins when we begin pure devotional service. And thus an unalloyed devotee of the Lord loses all material contact in the present body. As we go on spiritualizing this present body means we spiritualize our consciousness. Then our contact with this body, even though I am in this body, the contact with the body is lost in proportion to our development of our spiritual consciousness. The abode of the Lord is not a myth as is thought by the unbelievers or ignorant people. But one cannot reach there by any material means. One can go there after quitting the present body only if one has prepared oneself by practicing devotional service. And Kunti did that and she went back to God. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### invocation, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha"

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. ### transcendental activities, Krishna's actions, Yadu dynasty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This verse is an explanation of the difficult to understand actions of the Lord Shri Krishna and his pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 34th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the seemingly incomprehensible actions of Lord Krishna and the Yadu dynasty, highlighting the transcendental nature of their activities.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 34th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Jaya Harad Bhuvobharam Tam Tanum Vijahav Ajah Kantakam Kantakenewa Dwayam Chapi Shituhu Samam. The meaning of this verse is: The supreme unborn Lord Shri Krishna caused the members of the Yadu dynasty to relinquish their bodies, and thus he relinquished their bodies, to relieve the burden of the world. This action was like picking out a thorn with a thorn, though both are the same to the controller. Now this verse is an explanation of the difficult to understand actions of the Lord Shri Krishna and his pure devotees.

### transcendental activities, devotee perception, Yadu dynasty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Many places it is said all the Lord's activities or the pure devotees activities in this world are completely transcendental, but these transcendental activities are not always easy to understand."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the transcendental nature of the Lord's and devotees' activities, acknowledging their difficulty to comprehend, particularly the Yadu dynasty's relinquishing of their bodies through internal conflict, and introduces Srila Prabhupada's purport for further explanation.

Many places it is said all the Lord's activities or the pure devotees activities in this world are completely transcendental. But these transcendental activities are not always easy to understand. Some of them are very difficult to understand, especially the members of the Yadu dynasty. How did they relinquish their bodies? How did they give up their bodies fighting with each other, killing each other, getting intoxicated? When fighting with each other, how can this be understood as a transcendental activity of the pure devotees, the Yadavas, the members of the Yadu dynasty, the associates of Krishna? How can we understand? So that is explained in this verse. So let's hear Srila Prabhupada's purport which will wonderfully, very nicely explain this particular verse.

### Srila Prabhupada's purport, Vishwanatha Chakravarti Thakur, devotee perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=182
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Vishwanacharya Chakravarti Thakur explains that to give them relief from this mental agony Suta Goswami assured them that they will not suffer the pain of intoxication."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Srila Prabhupada's purport, referencing Vishwanatha Chakravarti Thakur's explanation of the sages of Naimisharanya's discomfort with the Yadu's demise, and Suta Goswami's assurance that they did not suffer the pain of intoxication.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada says Srila Vishwanacharya Chakravarti Thakur, who is one of the previous Acharyas, he suggests that the rishis like Shaunaka and others who are hearing Srimad Bhagavatam from Suta Goswami at Naimisharanya were not happy to hear about the Yadus dying in the madness of intoxication. The sages of Naimisharanya, they are devotees. Now they're hearing about other devotees and the Yadus as devotees, are they really fighting in the madness of intoxication and dying due to that fighting? So that is not very pleasing to hear for them. So here Srila Vishwanacharya Chakravarti Thakur explains that to give them relief from this mental agony Suta Goswami assured them that they will not suffer the pain of intoxication.

---
### Yadu dynasty, burden of the world, divine arrangement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=260
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord made this arrangement that they kill each other simply to give up their material bodies, that's all."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Yadu dynasty appeared to assist the Lord in reducing the burden of the world, and after their role was complete, the Lord arranged for them to relinquish their bodies through internal conflict to return to their original spiritual position.

This courage of the Yadus dynasty to relinquish their bodies by which they had to take away the burden of the world. So this is a very confidential understanding. The Yadu dynasty members had appeared in the satchar traveling in satchar going through the satchar editing. These must be the people who had come for knowledges in this world in order to assist the Lord in his leela of reducing the burden of this world, which is overburdened by asuras, by demons, and to reduce that burden of the earth planet. The Lord came along with his associates, the members of the Yadu dynasty. Now after the Lord had annihilated these demons, the Yadu dynasty itself remained in this material world and they could not be annihilated by anyone else. After their work was over, after their role was completed of assisting the Lord in this material world to reduce the burden of the world, then they are no longer required here. So for them to return to their original position in the spiritual world, the Lord made this arrangement that they kill each other simply to give up their material bodies, that's all.

### demigods, divine mission, intoxication
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=381
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's not their Karma. It is the arrangement of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker continues with Srila Prabhupada's explanation, stating that the Lord called for confidential demigods to appear in the Yadu family to serve in his mission of eradicating the burdens of the world, and after the mission was fulfilled, they relinquished their bodies by fighting amongst themselves in the madness of intoxication, which was arranged by the Lord, not due to their karma.

Srila Prabhupada further explains this point: the Lord and his eternal associates appeared on earth to help the administrative demigods in eradicating the burdens of the world and to remove the burden of the world from the of the world. He therefore called for some of the confidential demigods to appear in the Yadu family and serve him in his great mission. After the mission was fulfilled, the demigods by the will of the Lord relinquished their corporeal bodies by fighting amongst themselves in the madness of intoxication. Their material bodies anyway they have to give up after their work is over. Everybody gives up, but how they give up? In their case it is by the arrangement of the Supreme Lord. It's not their choice. It's not their Karma. It is the arrangement of the Supreme Lord. So it is the will of the Lord. The demigods are accustomed to drinking the Somarasa beverage and therefore the drinking of wine and intoxication are not unknown to them.

### equality, Lord's perspective, part and parcel
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=513
**Verse References:** BG 4, BG 6.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For the Supreme Lord both the Asuras and the demigods are equal. Why? Both of them are his part and parcels."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that from the Lord's perspective, both asuras and demigods are equal because they are all His part and parcel, referencing verses from the Bhagavad-gita to support this point.

For the Supreme Lord both the Asuras and the demigods are equal. Why? Both of them are his part and parcels. All living beings in the entire creation are part and parcel of the Lord. Mamaivamso jivaloke jivabhutasanatanah. Mama amsa, they are my part. In the fourth chapter Krishna also says yena bhutanee aseshani drakshasi atmani ataumayee in the 35th of the sixth chapter that every living being is my part and parcel, it's mine, belongs to me, is in me, Krishna says in the fourth chapter the Bhagavad-gita. So nobody is independent in their entire being. The Bhagavad Gita asks the personality to be independent of the lord, nobody is separate from the lord means nobody is unrelated to the lord, everybody is related, connected as lord's own. So that some of them are asuras and others are devatas does not mean that the asuras don't belong to krishna and the devatas alone belong to krishna.

### obedience, devatas vs asuras, thorn analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=601
**Verse References:** BG 9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The difference is however between the demigods and the demons or the devatas and asuras the demigods are obedient to the lord whereas the asuras are not obedient to the lord."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies the difference between demigods and asuras, highlighting that demigods are obedient to the Lord while asuras are not, and explains the analogy of removing a thorn with another thorn.

No, both of them belong to krishna so for the supreme lord both are equal, same thing krishna says in bhagavad gita the ninth chapter. The difference is however between the demigods and the demons or the devatas and asuras the demigods are obedient to the lord whereas the asuras are not obedient to the lord. Therefore the example of picking out a thorn by another thorn is quite befitting. The example that is given here is explained also by shrina pravpa what is this example one thorn which causes pinpricks on the leg of the lord is certainly disturbing to the lord and the other thorn which takes out the disturbing elements certainly gives service to the lord.

---
### thorn analogy, practical example, agricultural context
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=669
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "To remove that thorn sometimes may not be possible just by pulling it out because it is gone little deep inside so then the person to remove that thorn takes another thorn and uses that second thorn to actually clear the skin in the area where the thorn is pricked and thereby making it easy to pull out the thorn with the second thorn the first thorn."
**Summary:** The speaker provides a practical explanation of the thorn analogy, describing how in agricultural settings, a second thorn is used to clear the skin around a deeply embedded thorn to facilitate its removal.

To understand this it's very common thing in in agricultural fields when somebody is walking barefoot and let us say because they're walking barefoot a thorn pricks the leg, the bottom of the foot, the sole of the foot, then to remove that thorn sometimes may not be possible just by pulling it out because it is gone little deep inside so then the person to remove that thorn takes another thorn and uses that second thorn to actually clear the skin in the area where the thorn is pricked and thereby making it easy to pull out the thorn with the second thorn the first thorn. So this may not be very much an experience of the city people who never have to walk barefoot who never have experience of a thorn pricking their leg but this is the most appropriate example in this instance.

---
### devatas vs asuras, Lord's protection, impartiality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vyIYYoER6FU?t=771
**Verse References:** BG 16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord's mission is always to protect the devatas and destroy the asuras and to do good to both of them."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that while all living beings are part and parcel of the Lord, those who are disobedient are considered asuras, while voluntary servitors are devatas, and the Lord's mission is to protect the devatas and destroy the asuras, ultimately doing good to both by assuring them contact with Him and liberation, emphasizing His impartiality.

So Prabhupada explains although every living being is a part of the city as long as they owned some of the parts and parcel of the Lord still one who is a pin prick to the Lord is called an hasura who is disobedient and unnecessarily gives trouble to others and one who is a voluntary servitor of the Lord is called a devata or demigod. Both are parts and parcels of the lord, both belong to the lord but still one is called an asura another one is called the devata. In the material world, the devatas and asuras are always contending. There is competition between them and the devatas are always saved from the hands of the asuras by the Lord. Both of them are under the control of the Lord. Both the devatas and asuras, both. The world is full of two kinds of living beings. Krishna explains this in the 16th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. So, there are two kinds of created beings, the devatas and asuras. The Lord's mission is always to protect the devatas and destroy the asuras and to do good to both of them. The Lord does. Good to both of them. Because whether he kills the asuras or protects the devatas, both are delivered by the Lord. The contact with the Lord is assured, guaranteed liberation for whoever comes in contact, whichever way. So, Lord does good to both. He is not partial to the devatas. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### impersonalism, voidism, Prabhupada's mission
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GOUTfsI43aY?t=89
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada has come, being empowered by the supreme Lord for the sake of delivering especially the western countries which are filled with this impersonalism and voidism."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Srila Prabhupada elaborates on the verse, contrasting Vaishnava philosophy with impersonalism (Maya vada) and voidism (Shunya vada), and highlighting Prabhupada's mission to deliver the Western countries from these philosophies.

The way the Lord appears and disappears in this material world in different forms is very nicely explained by Shri Lasuta Goswami in this particular verse. Shri La Prabhupada elaborates in the purport: The Supreme Lord, personality of Godhead, is neither impersonal nor formless. Shri La Prabhupada elaborates in the purport two theories: Maya vada and Shunya vada. And the prayer we offer to Shri La Prabhupada, Nirvishesha Shunya Vadi Pashchakya Deshat Aryane, Nirvishesha Shunya Vadi Pashchakya Deshat Aryane. Prabhupada has come, being empowered by the supreme Lord for the sake of delivering especially the western countries which are filled with this impersonalism and voidism. So impersonal philosophy is that God is impersonal, God has no transcendental personality, no personality, and the widest philosophy is God is void, shunya, formless, no form at all.

### spiritual body, eternity, Brahma Samhita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GOUTfsI43aY?t=171
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's form or body is never material, though it may appear like that, it may appear like that, but it is not material, it is completely spiritual."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the material body with Krishna's spiritual body, emphasizing that Krishna's body is non-different from Himself and is an embodiment of eternity, knowledge, and bliss, as described in the Brahma Samhita. He quotes the Brahad-Vaishnava Tantra, stating that anyone who considers Krishna's form to be material should be ostracized.

So the Vaishnavas based on the authentic scriptures explain and understand and realize the fact that the Supreme Lord Personality of Godhead is not impersonal, he is not formless, but his body is non different from him. Our body is different from our self, Krishna's form or body is not different from his self. Therefore he is known as an embodiment, as the embodiment of eternity, knowledge and bliss. This is the description of the Brahma Samhita. In the Brahad-Vaishnava Tantra, it is clearly mentioned that anyone who considers the form of Lord Krishna to be made of material energy must be ostracized by all means. What's the meaning of this? That Krishna's form. Krishna's form or body is never material, though it may appear like that, it may appear like that, but it is not material, it is completely spiritual. That means in that spiritual form there is no deterioration, there is no action of the gunas, there is no death, there is no birth, there is no death. There is no death. There is no birth. There is no death. There is no death. No birth. There is no disease. There is no pain. There is no blood. There is no flesh. There is no bones. There is no matter. So, how to understand this?

### pastimes, divine manifestation, spiritual form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GOUTfsI43aY?t=298
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He is what he is irrespective of what form he displays, this is the point."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the apparent changes in Krishna's body during His pastimes, explaining that these are displays suitable for the pastime and do not affect His eternal, spiritual form. He emphasizes that Krishna is what He is, regardless of the form He displays.

Because when Krishna performs his pastimes, he appears to take birth as a small infant, son of Devaki, then later son of mother Yashoda and then he appears to grow, he appears to grow from a small infant like ordinarily any child will grow up but if there are changes in the body and the spiritual body is eternal without any change then how do we understand that in spite of such changes, apparent changes, the Lord's body is completely spiritual, changeless without deterioration, how do we understand this? So that is explained that for the sake of his pastimes he displays different kinds of forms suitable for the pastime, suitable for the past. So the pastime requires Krishna to display his form as a small child of mother Yashoda, he displays that form. If it requires that he display the form of four-handed Vishnu in front of Devaki and Vasudeva to convince them that he is a supreme lord who has appeared as the son, he displays that form. That's all, he is what he is irrespective of what form he displays, this is the point.

### eternal forms, fish incarnation, simultaneous manifestation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GOUTfsI43aY?t=674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord is not fish, neither is entering a fish body, he is coming in his original spiritual form as a fish, that is just exhibited."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the magician analogy, emphasizing that the Lord has eternal forms and that the fish incarnation is one of them, exhibited within this universe. He explains that the Lord is not entering a fish body but manifesting His original spiritual form as a fish, and that these pastimes are continuously going on in different universes.

So, it is all appearances, they are false shows. Similarly, just as the magician himself is neither burnt to ashes nor cut to pieces, nor is he dead or unconscious at any stage of his magical demonstration. Similarly, the Lord has his eternal forms of unlimited variety, of which the fish incarnation was exhibited within this universe, which was exhibited in this universe is also one of them. So, Lord is not fish, neither is entering a fish body, he is coming in his original spiritual form as a fish, that is just exhibited. Because there are innumerable universes, somewhere or other the fish incarnation must be manifesting his pastimes without cessation. So, just like Krishna appeared 5000 years back in this material world. So, when he appeared, the next moment Krishna is appearing in another universe as a small child. Meantime, in this universe what is happening, he appears to be grown up, he has been transferred to Mother Yashoda's house from Devaki's lap in the prison. So, in the other universe, after little time where he has appeared, he will again appear in another universe and in that other universe he will be transferred. So, his pastimes are continuously going on, it appears like here some changes are happening, but actually his Leela is going on, he is not changing.

The idea is that the Lord does not create the fish incarnation, he eternally has such a form and appearance or disappearance of such an incarnation serves particular purposes. ### impersonalists, lack of intelligence, yoga maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GOUTfsI43aY?t=873
**Verse References:** BG 7.24, BG 7.25
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The impersonalists think that I have no form. They think that I am formless."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita (7.24-25), stating that impersonalists think the Lord has no form and that they lack sufficient intelligence to understand His eternal nature and energies. He explains that the Lord reserves the power of not being exposed to non-devotees through His yoga maya potency.

These are not temporary forms. In the Bhagavad Gita, in 7th chapter, 24th and 25th verses, the Lord says, the impersonalists think that I have no form. They think that I am formless. And they also have a theory that I accept a form to serve a purpose because of which I am now manifested as a human being. But such speculators are without sharp intelligence. Avyaktam vyaktimapannam manyantemam abuddhaya manyante. They think like that because they lack sufficient intelligence. Though they may be good scholars in Vedic literature they are practically ignorant of my inconceivable energies and my eternal forms of personality param bhavam ajananto they do not know my eternal nature, my supreme eternal form and his energies. Krishna's energy they don't know the reason is that I reserve the power of not being exposed to non devotees by my mystic curtain naham prakasha sarvasya yoga maya sama vrittaha I am covered by my yoga maya potency therefore I am not manifest today. I will stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

Om Agyanati Mirandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurunmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namo Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 36th verse and the 37th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam.

---
### Kali Yuga, Krishna's departure, spiritual form
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=55
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the personality of Godhead Lord Krishna left this earthly planet in its self-same form, from that very day, Kali, who had already partially appeared, became fully manifest to create inauspicious conditions for those who are endowed with the power of the Lord, with a poor fund of knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 36th verse, explaining that Kali's full manifestation began with Krishna's departure in His original form, creating inauspicious conditions for those lacking knowledge.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 36th verse and the 37th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 36th verse first. The meaning of this verse is: When the personality of Godhead Lord Krishna left this earthly planet in its self-same form, from that very day, Kali, who had already partially appeared, became fully manifest to create inauspicious conditions for those who are endowed with the power of the Lord, with a poor fund of knowledge. So Krishna's departure from this world to his personal abode in his self-same form, not change of body. This difference has to be noted.

---
### eternal form, spiritual reality, material illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=119
**Verse References:** BG 2.22, SB 1.15.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything material is temporary, and everything spiritual is permanent, eternal, changeless."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the distinction between Krishna's eternal spiritual form and temporary material manifestations, explaining that Kali's influence is limited as long as Krishna is present.

In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says death means change of body. Now here, Krishna is departing to his personal abode in the self-same form. He never changes his spiritual form. So eternally he is the Lord, but eternally he has his form. Eternally he has everything. He has his energies. He has his paraphernalia. He has his name. He has his qualities. He has his activities. Everything eternal. So this distinction should be understood between material name, material form, material paraphernalia, material activities, material possessions, material manifestation. Everything is temporary. Everything material is temporary. And everything spiritual is permanent, eternal, changeless. So as long as Krishna was present, Kali could not manifest its actual characteristic of inauspicious conditions. But when Krishna left this earthly planet, from that very day, Kali manifested, became fully manifest. And the full manifestation means creating inauspicious conditions, creating inauspicious conditions for those who are endowed with a poor fund of knowledge. So what is the meaning of this? Creating inauspicious conditions for those who are endowed with a poor fund of knowledge.

---
### God consciousness, protection, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=270
**Verse References:** BG 14.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One can neutralize the effects of Kali by keeping oneself fully under the supreme care of the personality of Godhead; actually Krishna is our only protector, only maintainer, and we should take complete shelter of Krishna through the proper method."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Prabhupada, explaining that those fully developed in God consciousness are unaffected by Kali and that taking shelter of Krishna is the only way to neutralize Kali's influence.

Prabhupada explains in the purport: The influence of Kali can be enforced only upon those who are not fully developed in God consciousness. So, Kali or for that matter even the material modes of nature cannot act upon those who are fully developed in God consciousness. Krishna says this in the Bhagavad Gita: those who are engaged in pure devotional service in an unfailing manner, they are always on the spiritual platform, Brahma Bhuyaya Kalpate, Guna Tita, they are above the three modes, above the influence of the three modes. Above the influence of the three modes also means above the influence of Kali because Kali acts in the material atmosphere through the three gunas. So Srila Prabhupada says further, one can neutralize the effects of Kali by keeping oneself fully under the supreme care of the personality of Godhead. Actually Krishna is our only protector, only maintainer, and we should take complete shelter of Krishna through the proper method.

---
### spiritual reality, illusory energy, Krishna's presence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=375
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has disappeared from our vision, but Krishna is eternally present in his spiritual abode."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material methods with spiritual reality, emphasizing Krishna's eternal presence and protection, and reiterating that Kali's influence was absent during Krishna's presence.

Our material method will not work because Krishna is not a material personality. This material personality is a concoction, is a creation of the illusory energy, whereas Krishna's personality is beyond all material manifestations, beyond illusion, is a sent person reality, eternal reality. So take shelter of Krishna, who can give you permanent eternal protection, who always does give permanent eternal protection to everyone except those who turn away from him and are in the clutches of the illusory energy. Except for them, everyone else is sheltered by Krishna. That's what Prabhupada is telling here. The age of Kali ensued just after the battle of Kurukshetra, but it could not exert its influence because of the presence of the Lord, because of the presence of Lord Krishna. The Lord however left this earthly planet in his own transcendental body, self-same form, that's what is said in this verse, his own transcendental body.

---
### Kali Yuga, omens, departure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=475
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.36, SB 1.15.37
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as he left, the symptoms of Kali Yuga as were envisioned by Maharaj Yudhishthira prior to Arjuna's arrival from Dwaraka began to manifest."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the symptoms of Kali Yuga began to manifest immediately after Krishna's departure, as foreseen by Maharaj Yudhishthira, and uses the analogy of the sun setting to illustrate Krishna's disappearance from our vision.

As soon as he left, the symptoms of Kali Yuga as were envisioned by Maharaj Yudhishthira prior to Arjuna's arrival from Dwaraka began to manifest, and Maharaj Yudhishthira rightly conjectured on the departure of the Lord from the earth even for Arjuna could come back with the actual news of the departure of Krishna from Dwaraka. Yudhishthira Maharaj seeing the ill omens conjectured that these ill omens are indicating Krishna's departure from this world. As we have already explained, the Lord left our sight just as when the sun sets, it is out of our sight. Nothing happens to the sun when it sets. It is just disappeared from our vision, our sight, that's all. Same thing, Krishna has disappeared from our vision, but Krishna is eternally present in his spiritual abode. The 37th verse: yudhishthira stat parisarpanam budhaha purecha rashtrecha grihe tathatmani vibhavya lobhan jihma himsa nadi adharma chakram gamanaya paryadhat. The meaning of this verse, 37th verse.

### Kali Yuga, greed, falsehood
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=563
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains the 37th verse, highlighting Yudhishthira's understanding of Kali's influence, characterized by greed, falsehood, and other negative qualities, and the long duration of Kali Yuga.

Maharaj Yudhishthira was intelligent enough to understand the influence of the age of Kali characterized by increasing avarice, avarice means greed, falsehoods. The influence of the age of Kali began manifesting and from authentic scriptures it is learned that the age of Kali still to run on for 427,000 years. If I were to put it in the form of lakhs the total duration of Kali is 4,32,000 out of which 5,000 if you deduct what remains this balance is 4,27,000 years. So, the symptoms of the Kali Yuga as mentioned above namely avarice, greed, avarice means greed, falsehood, diplomacy, diplomacy means duplicitous behavior, cheating, nepotism, violence and all such things are already involved in the Kali Yuga.

### civilization, godlessness, divine qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bQqWaOoslBc?t=675
**Verse References:** BG 16.1-3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts godless, uncivilized people with God-conscious individuals who possess divine qualities, referencing the Bhagavad Gita's description of divine characteristics.

And no one can imagine what is going to happen gradually with further increase of the influence of Kali till the day of annihilation. We have already come to know that the influence of the age of Kali is meant for godless so-called civilized man. They call themselves civilized but they are not civilized. They are godless. So, therefore they are uncivilized. God conscious or godly people are always civilized, are always properly behaving, properly acting, properly situated, have all the good qualities that are to be found in demigods. Yesyasti Bhakti Bhagavati Akinchana Sarvair Gunai Stratra Samasate Suraha. In fact, demigod means divine nature. So, divine nature means god conscious people, godly character as defined in the scriptures. This is not somebody's concoction. It is a definite description in the 16th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. The first three verses describe divine qualities, divine characteristics. Yesyasti Bhakti Bhagavati Akinchana Sarvair Gunai Stratra Samasate Suraha. Yesyasti Bhakti Bhagavati Akinchana Sarvair Gunai Stratra Samasate Suraha. And you can see each one of them is good quality, good qualification, good behavior.

Yudhishthir is a pure devotee so he doesn't have to actually do any preparation he is always God conscious so but he wants to teach us before death comes one must retire from active household life or one must retire from material activities even if one is not a householder even a brahmachari should retire from all material activities and prepare himself to go back home back to God so Yudhishthir Mahal setting the example for us the Pandavas are eternal companions of the Lord and therefore they are more interested in the company of the Lord than anything else the eternal companions of the Lord always accompany the Lord in his mission in the material world that's the meaning of eternal companions in this context. ## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.38 | 24-12-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Kingship, Administration, Responsibilities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XY4bYVfQ9Y8?t=50
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The king had to essentially ensure that all the necessities were provided for the citizens, so that the citizens can actually become responsible for preparing themselves to go back to Godhead, after having achieved or after having received a human form of life."
**Summary:** This segment describes Yudhishthira Maharaj handing over the kingdom to Parikshit Maharaj, emphasizing the king's responsibility to provide for the citizens' material and spiritual well-being, enabling them to achieve the ultimate goal of returning to Godhead.

In this session, we will be discussing the 38th verse of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this 38th verse is: Thereafter, in the capital of Hastinapura, he enthroned his grandson, who was trained and equally qualified as the emperor and master of all land bordered by the seas. So here is the description of Yudhishthira Maharaj retiring from active household life, and he is handing over charge of ruling the world to his trained and qualified grandson, Parikshit Maharaj. Parikshit Maharaj was the only surviving descendant of the Pandava family of the Kuru dynasty. Everyone else was killed in the battle of Kurukshetra. So Yudhishthira Maharaj handed over charge to Parikshit Maharaj, and he handed over charge as the emperor and master of all lands bordered by the seas, the whole world. He handed over charge of ruling the entire world to Parikshit Maharaj. Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, the total land on the earth bordered by the seas was under the subjugation of the king of the Kuru dynasty, the king of Hastinapura. This is the arrangement. The emperor of the world ruled from the place called Hastinapura. Hastinapura is modern day Delhi. Elsewhere, Srila Prabhupada explains, so the capital was always in Hastinapura of the rulers of the entire world. There was subordination. There were subordinate kings, and they had their smaller jurisdiction of province or state or some small kingdom, but they were always subordinate to the emperor. So that was actually the system, that there were different territorial kings who were protected by the emperor against any invasion by rogues and thieves and dacoits, etc. So they used to rule the citizens very nicely, according to the scriptural directions, according to the kshatriya dharma, the perfect system of administration, given in the perfect scriptures, coming from the perfect source, the supreme personality of Godhead. So Srila Prabhupada further says, Maharaja Yudhishthira trained his grandson, Maharaj Pariksit, who was equally qualified in state administration, in terms of the king's obligation to the citizens. Earlier, Srila Prabhupada elaborately explained what were the obligations of the king to the citizens. The king had to essentially ensure that all the necessities were provided for the citizens, so that the citizens can actually become responsible for preparing themselves to go back to Godhead, after having achieved or after having received a human form of life. 

---
### Human Life, Responsibility, Spiritual Guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XY4bYVfQ9Y8?t=276
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Human form of life means a life of responsibility, and the king ensures that people take advantage of this human form of life by becoming very responsible, not that they are busy simply earning money or busy with some material activities, busy with some sense gratification, all under the influence of the illusory energy for some temporary effects."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the responsibility that comes with human life, contrasting it with mere material pursuits and sense gratification. It highlights the importance of distinguishing between the temporary and permanent, material and spiritual, and the need for guidance from a bonafide spiritual master to understand these distinctions.

So human form of life means a life of responsibility, and the king ensures that people take advantage of this human form of life by becoming very responsible, not that they are busy simply earning money or busy with some material activities, busy with some sense gratification, all under the influence of the illusory energy for some temporary effects. No. Human being means should understand the difference between the temporary and permanent, material and spiritual, body and soul, matter and spirit. Clear difference or distinction should be made, and the human being as the developed consciousness or intelligence or brain substance to actually be able to do this under proper guidance. Therefore, scriptures say approach a bonafide spiritual master who is self-realized and who has seen the truth, who is conversant with the subject spiritual subject matter by having properly studied it under another bonafide spiritual master in discipline succession coming from Krishna. These things are all very clearly explained.

---
### Material World, Sense Gratification, Desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XY4bYVfQ9Y8?t=449
**Verse References:** BG 15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This material world is like vishaya sagara, an ocean of sense objects. Vishaya means something enjoyable by the senses."
**Summary:** This segment describes the material world as an ocean of sense objects (vishaya sagara), where different creatures are attracted to different forms of enjoyment based on their acquired senses and desires. It explains that the type of body one gets is determined by one's conception of enjoyment.

These are creature depending on the type of senses they have acquired the material senses there are sense objects and to give an example for a dog a male dog the form of the female dog is very attractive is very attractive for a male deer the form of the female deer is very attractive these type of illusory attractions are plenty in this material world. So one of our acharyas, Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, says this material world is like vishaya sagara, an ocean of sense objects. Vishaya means something enjoyable by the senses. For the eyes, it is all what we say as beautiful form, not form, forms, beautiful forms. For the ears, it is sweet sounds. For the tongue, it is palatable, tasty edibles. And as you know, different creatures have different things they consider as edible. For a hog, stool is very much relaxable and is an edible. It eats too. It eats rubbish. Camels eat thorns. And there are different creatures with different kinds of a sense of taste according to tongue they have acquired, according to their desire to enjoy eating or seeing or smelling or touching or hearing. This all simply centered around a concoction in the mind to enjoy materially in a particular way. The business of the living being in this material world, having acquired this material senses, centered around a material mind with a certain conception of enjoyment. Why these different types of bodies, different souls are getting in this material world? Because there are different conceptions of enjoyment. If I want to enjoy like a dog, I should get a dog's body. If I want to enjoy like a cat, I should get a cat's body. If I want to enjoy like a tiger, I should get a tiger's body. This is the whole principle of getting a material body.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Guidance, Material Existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XY4bYVfQ9Y8?t=694
**Verse References:** BG 15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Unless we are guided by a God-conscious king, administrator, ruler, unless we approach a bona fide spiritual master after receiving such guidance, as to real goal of human life is to go back home, back to Godhead, to get out of this material existence, which is temporary and filled with miseries."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of studying the 15th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, which explains the process of acquiring material bodies based on desires. It stresses the need for guidance from a God-conscious leader and a bona fide spiritual master to understand the true goal of human life: to escape the temporary, miserable material existence and return to Godhead.

This is very nicely explained in the 15th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. Please read or study the 15th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita very systematically. Krishna explains this. I just quoted two verses, but it is very elaborately, nicely explained by Prabhupada in the translation and the purports of these verses in the 15th chapter. So, unless we are guided by a God-conscious king, administrator, ruler, unless we approach a bona fide spiritual master after receiving such guidance, as to real goal of human life is to go back home, back to Godhead, to get out of this material existence, which is temporary and filled with miseries, and go to that place where there are no miseries, which is full of bliss, which is permanent, eternal, abode full of bliss and knowledge. So, unless we receive proper guidance, we will waste our time. We will waste our time. We will waste our time. We will waste our time. We will waste our time. We will waste our time. We will waste our time. We will waste our time and our life only in pursuit of some temporary sense pleasures, which are fleeting.

---
### Administration, Happiness, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XY4bYVfQ9Y8?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Even today in this condition there can be spiritual emolument and God consciousness if people follow this this program of Krishna consciousness simply by beginning with chanting Hare Krishna or chanting God's name there can be God consciousness awakened in everyone."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the training of Parikshit Maharaj in state administration, focusing on ensuring the happiness of citizens through ample natural resources and freedom from various miseries. It contrasts this ideal with the current tense situation between rulers and the ruled, but suggests that even now, spiritual upliftment and God consciousness are possible through the practice of Krishna consciousness, particularly by chanting Hare Krishna.

So, it is specifically told Maharaj Pariksit was trained in state administration in terms of the king's obligation to the citizens. Thus, Pariksit Maharaj was enthroned on the seat of Maharaj Yudhishthira prior to Yudhishthira's departure back to Godhead. Now, concerning Maharaj Pariksit, one word is used, Vinayinam. The reason is, this Vinayinam means properly trained. He was trained to actually ensure the happiness of the citizens, which was due to the king's obligation to the citizens, which was due to ample production of natural produce, such as grains, fruits, milk, herbs, valuable stones, minerals, and everything that the people needed. They were even free from bodily miseries, anxieties of mind, disturbances caused by natural phenomena, and other living beings. So, this was the standard of administration when Yudhishthira Maharaj ruling, Pariksit Maharaj ruling. This was the standard, because everyone was happy in all respects, there was no resentment. Although there were sometimes battles between kings, different state kings, for political reasons and supremacy. So, people were trained to attain the highest goal of life, and therefore, they were enlightened enough not to quarrel over some trivial material things. So, the influence of the Indian people was not enough. They were trained to achieve the highest goal of life. of Kali gradually infiltrated the good qualities of both the kings and the citizens and therefore presently we can see there is a tense situation developed between the rulers and the ruled between the kings and the citizens or between the administrators and the and and the citizens but even today in this condition there can be spiritual emolument and God consciousness if people follow this this program of Krishna consciousness simply by beginning with chanting Hare Krishna or chanting God's name there can be God consciousness awakened in everyone and there can be the kingdom of God the atmosphere of the kingdom of God even on this earth planet today now by awakening the God consciousness through simply chanting Hare Krishna I'll stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

### renunciation, leadership, scriptural explanation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WKmaDjOytHY?t=47
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.39
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Afterwards Maharaj Yudhishthira performed a Prajapatiya sacrifice and placed in himself the fire for quitting household life."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains the meaning of Srimad Bhagavatam 1.15.39, focusing on Yudhishthira's actions of appointing Vajra as king and preparing for renunciation.

Mathurayam Tatha Vajram Shura Sena Patim Tathaha Prajapatyam Nirupyeshtim Agneen Apibad Ishwaraha. The meaning of this 39th verse is Then he posted Vajra, the son of Aniruddha. Aniruddha was the grandson of Lord Krishna at Mathura. That means Vajra who was the great grandson of Krishna was posted by Yudhishthira at Mathura as the king of Shura Sena. Afterwards Maharaj Yudhishthira performed a Prajapatiya sacrifice and placed in himself the fire for quitting household life. He prepared himself to quit household life after responsibly handing over charge to Parvatiya and one person who we have mentioned.

So one must quit household life and become detached, renounced from all household life attachments, from all family attachments, from all material attachments. **Summary:** The speaker further elaborates on the Varnashrama system, emphasizing that it is meant for everyone, regardless of their occupation, and that spiritual advancement is possible for all humans due to their developed intelligence and consciousness.

The scientific system of Varnashrama dharma divides the human life into four divisions of occupation and four orders of life. So, Srila Prabhupada elaborates what I just explained. The four orders of life as Brahmachari, Grhastha, Vanaprastha and Sanyasi are to be followed by all irrespective of the occupational division. If somebody is serious about going back home, back to Godhead, they should follow these divisions that are made in the ashrama system. Brahmachari means student life. And Grhastha means householder life. And Vanaprastha means retired life. And Sanyasa means renounced or detached life. So, these stages are natural and very scientific and progressive for somebody whose ultimate aim of life is to go back to Godhead at the end of this life. Before quitting the body, one has to prepare. Now, this goal is for every human being. Because human being has developed developed intelligence, developed consciousness, developed understanding, developed brain. That is the meaning of human life.

In this session, we will be discussing the 40th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Visrijya Tatra Tatsarvam Dukula Valayadikam Nirmamo Nirahankaraha Sanchinna Ashesha Bandhanaha. Now this verse describes how Maharaj Yudhishtira developed detachment from all household affairs. So the translation of this 40th verse: Maharaj Yudhishtira at once relinquished all his garments, belt, and ornaments of the royal order and became completely disinterested and unattached to everything. Everything in the household life.
