### purification, material contamination, back to Godhead
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gCUZ9dvh6rw?t=101
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To become purified of material contamination is the necessary qualification for becoming one of the associates of the Lord."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's purport is discussed, emphasizing the necessity of purification from material contamination to return to Godhead and attain one's original position as an associate of the Lord.

Nirmamo Nirahankara. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains: To become purified of material contamination is the necessary qualification for becoming one of the associates of the Lord. What's the meaning of going back home, back to Godhead? We all belong to the Lord as his associate. So for reinstating ourselves in our original position as the associate of the Lord Krishna, we have to acquire the necessary qualification, and that qualification is to become purified of material contamination. This association with the material atmosphere or material nature has resulted in our developing different kinds of attachment in our heart towards so many material things, both gross and subtle. Grossly, we are attached to material objects, my car, my house, my table, my chair, my furniture, my clothes, my ornaments, and certainly we are attached to social status, some honor in society, some respectability in some material hierarchy. All these kinds of gross and subtle material attachments we have to become purified of. All such contamination should become detached. Materially, no one can become an associate of the Lord or can go back to Godhead without such purification. This is absolutely required, essential for everyone.

---
### renunciation, vanashrama dharma, simplicity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gCUZ9dvh6rw?t=230
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The saffron loincloth of a sannyasi indicates freedom from all attractive material garments."
**Summary:** The discussion extends to the concept of renunciation, particularly within the vanashrama dharma, highlighting the significance of simple clothing and detachment from attractive material garments, as exemplified by Maharaj Yudhishtira.

Maharaj Yudhishthira, therefore, to become spiritually pure, spiritually pure means free from all material contamination. This spiritual purity at once gave up his royal opulence, relinquishing his royal dress and garments. So Srila Prabhupada explains one specific aspect of renunciation in the vanashrama dharma. Prabhupada says the kasha or saffron loincloth of a sannyasi indicates freedom from all attractive material garments. Sympathetically, when one takes sanyasa, then one receives a saffron loincloth. Saffron processed clothes and text. But that is actually indicative of something, not that everybody has to receive a saffron copium. No, it is indicative of freedom from all attractive material garments. We adore the body so much, or we are attached to the body so much. We decorate this body in so many ways, particularly the garments and, of course, the ornaments, garments and the ornaments decorating this body. So we have to give up those garments and ornaments and accept some simple clothing. And what did he do? The details will be explained. He actually gave up his royal dress and garments, and the next verse will tell that he dressed himself in some rejected, discarded, torn, old garments simply to cover the body, some simple cloth, any piece of cloth available which is not at all attractive, which is not at all fashionable.

### detachment, designations, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gCUZ9dvh6rw?t=373
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To become free from all designations is the way to actually practice bhakti and advance in Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes detachment from material designations, explaining that true bhakti involves freedom from identifying with family, society, country, occupation, wealth, and other worldly positions.

So he thus changed his dress accordingly. He became disinterested in his kingdom and family and thus became free from all material contamination or material designation. He had ruled as the emperor of the world, and in that position as the emperor of the world, he had his royal dress, he had his royal paraphernalia, he had his royal throne, he had his royal insignia, but it was required for ruling as the emperor of the world. But when he handed over charge, he no longer was keeping any of those garments or ornaments or any of the paraphernalia. Everything he gave up. In this way, he became detached from all those things. Also, it is important to understand becoming free from material contamination also means becoming free from material designations. The definition of bhakti is very interesting. One of the definitions of bhakti: sarva upadhi vinir muktam. Bhakti means sarva upadhi vinir muktam. Upadhi means designation. To become free from all designations is the way to actually practice bhakti and advance in Krishna consciousness. So Srila Prabhupada explains here, people are generally attached to various kinds of designations, the designations of family, society, country, occupation, wealth, position, and many others. We have different roles in our normal life, householder life, or family life with different roles. I am the head of some family. I am an important member of some society, or I am having a position in the country, in the political administration or occupation. I may be an expert technocrat or expert skilled artisan or some very, very expert painter or whatever, or designation of wealth. Oh, he is a trillionaire, he is a billionaire, he is in Fortune 500 list, or in British society, they used to get this designation as lord and earl upon paying some huge amount of money to the government to get such titles.

---
### designations, material impurity, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gCUZ9dvh6rw?t=577
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as one is attached to today's material world, such designations is considered materially impure; this is the material contamination, this is the material impurity."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how attachment to designations leads to material impurity and bondage, referencing Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's teachings on transcending social and occupational identities to realize one's true self.

So many of these are the definitions of bhakti, sarva upadhi vinir muktam bhakti sarva upadhi. But too many such institutions are there which have hard according to the the wealth one possess or one can pay for getting such designations, titles. So all these are designations which are the cause for our attachment and bondage in this material world. As long as one is attached to today's material world, such designations is considered materially impure. This is the material contamination. This is the material impurity. I am having designations. The so-called leaders of men in the modern age are attached by national consciousness, but they do not know that such false consciousness is also another designation of the materially conditioned soul. One has to relinquish such designations before one can become eligible to go back to Godhead. All designations will become free. Then somebody might say that in the renounced order of life, somebody calls you Maharaj, addresses you as sannyasi, that's also a designation. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu teaches in this regard how even though for the sake of renouncing, we accept the renounced order formally, but you can become detached even without formally accepting the renounced order, and you should understand that to renounce means to renounce even these designations and titles within the heart. What does Chaitanya Mahaprabhu teach us? Naham vipro na cha narapati na pi vaisho na shudro naham varni na cha grahapati no vanastho yatirva. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, if you ask me who am I, I am not a Brahmana, I am not a Kshatriya, I am not a Vaishya, I am not a Shudra, I am not a Brahmachari, I am not a grahastha, I am not a vanaprasad, I am not a sannyasi. So what does he mean by this? He means that we should not be having any designations and the associated respect and honor and kind of this thing in the society, honor in the society for every position within our heart. We should not have any of those expectations or any of those attachments or any of those false egos. We should not have any of those. Then if Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, I am not a Brahmin, I am not a Kshatriya, I am not a Vaishya, I am not a Shudra, I am not a Brahmachari, Ghrastha, Vana, Prasad, Sanyasin. What are you?

### devotion, servant, sannyasa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gCUZ9dvh6rw?t=793
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker defines the true identity as a servant of Krishna, emphasizing the loving, devotional relationship and clarifying the role of a sannyasi as a guide offering spiritual knowledge without expecting anything in return, using the analogy of a policeman's uniform.

Kintu Prodhyan Nikhil Paramananda Poonamrata Abder Gopi Bhartuhu Pada Kamalayor Dasa Dasa Anudasaha. I identify myself only as a servant of the servant of the servant of the servant of the Supreme Lord Krishna who is the protector and maintainer of the Gopis. So this is our constitutional position, this is our eternal position, this is our actual position. Everyone, every living being, we are eternally Krishna's servants or Krishna's devotees. Now don't misunderstand the word servant. Servant means devotee. Devotee means devoted to serving Krishna out of love, that kind of servant, loving, devotional servant. And that's a very sweet relationship between each one of us and Krishna, eternal relationship. So that is our actual position, and that is not a designation. That is what we are at the core of our heart. That's what we are. These material designations are external impositions on that eternal position. So that gets covered. I forget I am a devotee, and then I think in terms of the body or in terms of the social position in the society. So actually, even though I have accepted sannyas and people, they recognize me as a sannyasi, that's only for the sake of social interaction so that people understand that here is a sannyasi means here is a person whom I can approach for any spiritual guidance, any spiritual clarification, any spiritual knowledge without the sannyasi expecting anything in return. Free of charge. Without. Because sannyasi is renounced, he does not require anything. So he will not charge you anything. He will not ask anything from you. His life is dedicated for simply disseminating this knowledge, this practice, this understanding, this important valuable lesson, this goal of life. That's why the dress. It's like a policeman performs his duty very nicely with a uniform. The policeman actually, as far as skill is concerned, his activity is concerned, he can do without uniform, but for people to be able to easily approach him for traffic guidance and in driving on the road. So with a uniform, he can perform very easily his job. Similarly, a sannyasi with this uniform treat me with honor. No. Sanyasi, if he thinks like that, he's actually fallen. He's not qualified. He's not qualified. This is very important. So, I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### material conception of life, spiritual identity, relationship with Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jl8aAhNIRtw?t=142
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because the fact is, as spirit soul, I don't belong to this world. I belong to Krishna. I belong to Krishnaloka."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the material conception of life as falsely identifying with the body and forgetting one's true spiritual identity and relationship with Krishna.

Maharaja Yudhishthira, like his brother Arjuna, began to concentrate and gradually became freed from all material bondage. So, becoming free from material bondage means becoming free from the material conception of life. What is the material conception of life? Actually, I am spirit soul, or you are spirit soul. Each person is spirit soul, but I wrongly think I am this body. I wrongly think I belong to this world, because the fact is, as spirit soul, I don't belong to this world. I belong to Krishna. I belong to Krishnaloka. I have a permanent, eternal, loving relationship with Krishna, but in my present condition, I have forgotten Krishna. I have forgotten that relationship. I have forgotten who I am. I have forgotten who is Krishna. I have forgotten what is my relationship with Krishna. I have forgotten the spiritual world, and by association with this material world, I identify I belong to this world. Because I have taken birth in a certain family, I identify I belong to this family. Now, this family itself, consisting of the family members, my mother, my father, my brothers, my sisters, my aunts, my uncles, my neighbors, my friends, my well-wishers, all this is developed around a false conception that I am the body.

---
### detachment, relationships, spiritual practice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jl8aAhNIRtw?t=272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is to understand in the correct way what is the relative importance of this body of mine, the relationships I have with my understanding as I am the body, with my parents, with my... Not to simply negate, but to understand the relative importance."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that detachment doesn't mean destroying relationships but understanding their relative importance in light of one's spiritual identity.

You can easily understand. Without this body, is there any meaning to relationships? You just think about it. Without the body, there is no meaning to a relationship. So, all these relationships, my father, my mother, my family, or my friend, my neighbor, my relatives, everything is based on my conception that I am thinking I am this body. So, to become free from material bondage means to become freed from the wrong conception of the self, of who I am. And that is made possible by different methods of spiritual practice. Now, don't think that becoming free from material bondage is to completely destroy all our relationships, our responsibilities, our duties, our affection. No, it's not that. Please don't misunderstand that way. It is to understand in the correct way what is the relative importance of this body of mine, the relationships I have with my understanding as I am the body, with my parents, with my... Not to simply negate, but to understand the relative importance.

---
### importance of human form, death, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jl8aAhNIRtw?t=398
**Verse References:** BG 2.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only a devotee, at the time of death, because he understands I am not the body, he is also able to understand now I am leaving the body."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of the human form for spiritual realization, discusses death as leaving the body, and highlights the devotee's understanding at the time of death and the continuation of devotional service.

There is a certain importance for this human form of body, because only in the human form of body, it is possible for me as spirit soul to hear, understand, I am not this body. So, the body is also important. In my present condition, body is important, but it doesn't have absolute importance because it's temporal. It's going to perish. It's not going to remain with me forever. So, before it perishes, I should actually come out of this misconception that I am the body. Otherwise, what will happen? Supposing I don't come out of the misconception, then I have to face death, which means I am forced to leave this body. What is death? I don't die. As spirit soul, I don't die. This you have heard many times in the Bhagavad Gita. It's clearly explained. Only a devotee, at the time of death, because he understands I am not the body, he is also able to understand now I am leaving the body. And depending on Krishna, if I leave the body, then Krishna will guide me and take me to my next body and I continue my devotional service in my next body, provided I was engaged in devotional service in my previous body and I have cultivated the desire to continue my devotional service even after quitting the body. So that's the devotional process. Now, as far as Yudhishthira is concerned, there is a technical difference in the process Yudhishthira Maharaj is actually employing for becoming detached.

---
### sense control, mind control, spiritual senses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jl8aAhNIRtw?t=533
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All our senses are centered about the mind. It is the mind which directs the senses."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Yudhishthira Maharaj's process of detachment, focusing on controlling the senses by amalgamating them into the mind and replacing material understanding with spiritual understanding.

Let's hear what is Yudhishthira Maharaj doing. First he concentrated all the actions of the senses and amalgamated them into the mind. What is the meaning of this? All our senses are centered about the mind. It is the mind which directs the senses. You have heard this earlier. So if the mind is directing the senses, if the misconception around this body and based on the bodily actions of the senses, I try to derive happiness, I try to avoid suffering, I try to gather knowledge, I try to use my intelligence, etc, etc. So many things. Now, this has to be replaced with spiritual understanding of my spiritual self, spiritual intelligence, spiritual mind, spiritual senses, everything spiritual, which is inside. Not these material senses, material body, material mind, material intelligence. No, not this. This is a covering for the real mind, the spiritual mind, the spiritual senses, the spiritual intelligence, the spiritual self. It's a covering for all that. So to actually uncover, to uncover, to remove the covering, to become free from the covering is possible, first of all, by concentrating all the actions of the senses and amalgamating means gathering them and mixing them with the mind, mixing them with the mind, so that these senses I no longer identify as my senses, these material senses.

---
### spiritual senses, devotional service, mind's role
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jl8aAhNIRtw?t=684
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He turned his mind towards the transcendental service of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that amalgamating senses doesn't mean becoming senseless, but rather using spiritual senses. He connects this to devotional service, emphasizing turning the mind towards transcendental service.

Then if I don't identify these material senses as my senses, then I become senseless? No! My spiritual senses are there, and I am using my spiritual senses through these material senses in my material condition. So stop using your spiritual senses through your material senses. That's what Yudhishthira is doing. That's what Yudhishthira is doing. So he doesn't want to anymore use his spiritual senses through his material senses. He doesn't want to use that. Similarly, so what does this mean in terms of devotional service? Prabhupada says, or in other words, he turned his mind towards the transcendental service of the Lord. He turned his mind towards the transcendental service of the Lord. He prayed that since all material activities are performed by the mind in terms of actions and reactions and reactions of the material senses, and since he was going back to Godhead, the mind would wind up its material activities and be turned towards the transcendental service of the Lord. See, there is the gross body and the subtle body. The gross body consists of this gross senses of action and senses of perception, karmendya and jnanindya. The subtle body consists of the mind, material mind, material intelligence and material ego. So, when this material mind is turned towards transcendental service of the Lord, then the material mind will no longer be engaged in any material activities or material perceptions, material objects, nothing, because now Yudhishthira has nothing to do with the material world. He is handed over charge. He is handed over charge to his grandson and to Vajra, Krishna's great-grandson, all the charge of ruling. And he is prepared to quit home after placing the fire of renunciation in his heart, deciding to become completely renounced, preparing for going back to Godhead. Why all this? Preparing for going back to Godhead, quitting this body. So before he quits the body, the attachments have to be cut off. All the links have to be cut off, which were established when he entered the body. So how they cut off the link? By spiritualizing this material mind, by engaging it in transcendental service. And that's exactly what we are doing every day.

---
### chanting, spiritualization of mind, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jl8aAhNIRtw?t=855
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That way the mind becomes spiritualized. And when the mind is spiritualized, there is no longer any need for me, a spirit soul, to utilize these material senses for any material activities."
**Summary:** The speaker connects the discussion to daily practice, emphasizing chanting Hare Krishna mindfully to spiritualize the mind and reduce the need for material activities.

What are we doing? We're chanting Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, but you have to involve the mind in chanting Hare Krishna. Here, using the mind, mindfully chant, attentively hear, mindfully hear. So that way the mind turns towards the transcendental service of the Lord. The Harinama Japa, Harinama Kirtana, Hare, devotional service, Krishna Seva. That way the mind becomes spiritualized. And when the mind is spiritualized, there is no longer any need for me, a spirit soul, to utilize these material senses for any material activities. Even if I use the material senses, I'll use it only for devotional service. There was no longer any need for material activities, because he decided to stop all material activities. So I will stop here. We'll discuss this same verse more in the next session. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

So you prepare yourself to go back to God and give up this material coverings. Now giving up the material coverings is not as easy as trying to give up this covering that is a piece of cloth. I just remove this and just discard it. Now the difficulty with this material covering called the gross body and the subtle body is that the gross gross body to some extent is easy to understand what is this gross body but the difficulty is my thinking is without the gross body I am unable to work, I am unable to act, I am unable to live. Just like somebody unfortunately has got some problem because of which the hand is amputated, without the hand the person is handicapped so much of difficulty in doing ordinary things. I don't know how to use my spiritual hand to hold material objects. ### mind control, devotional service, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LI_x8kx2fbA?t=535
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The activities of the mind can be changed from matter to devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the activities of the mind are a reflection of the soul, and that the mind should not be stopped, but rather its activities should be changed from material to devotional service, purifying it from the contamination of repeated births and deaths.

In this way, the senses, one is able to actually become free from the material senses. This is the outermost layer of covering, this gross bodily covering. Then, actually, the activities of the mind cannot be stopped, for they are the reflection of the eternal soul. Actually, the material mind, we want to stop all material thinking, but that does not mean totally stop all activities of this mind. The present mind that we have. No. Because, it is the desire of the soul that is expressed through the mind. The activities of the soul are expressed through the material mind. So, therefore, Prabhupada says, the activities of the mind are reflection of the eternal soul. I am spirit. I am soul. I am eternal soul. So, the soul in the material world is reflected through the mind. Therefore, what has to be done is, the activities of the mind can be changed from matter to devotional service. So, there is a need to engage the mind differently. That's all. So, the material color of the mind is changed when one washes it from contaminations of life breathing, and thereby frees it from the contamination of repeated births and deaths.

---
### spiritual practice, mind control, birth and death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LI_x8kx2fbA?t=664
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Begin with spiritual life. Let's say, just chant 108 times Hare Krishna every day."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of purifying the mind to stop the cycle of birth and death, suggesting starting with simple spiritual practices like chanting Hare Krishna daily and gradually increasing spiritual activities while reducing material engagements.

If you are having idea of a continuing material existence, then, when this body, the term of life, in this body is over, if I have to continue material existence, I have to get another material body. That's called repetition of birth and death. So, to purify this mind means to stop this repetition of birth and death. That means to end all activities of the mind. All plans for continuing material existence or rather, change material existence to spiritual existence. Now, Srila Prabhupada says, one has to situate oneself in pure spiritual life. Therefore, we should practice. Therefore, we should practice. Begin with spiritual life. Let's say, just chant 108 times Hare Krishna every day. It just takes about 10 minutes. So, out of 24 hours in a day, 10 minutes at least, perform spiritual activity, whereby 10 minutes you are not engaging the mind in material activity. The mind becomes engaged in spiritual activity. So, this is a very important point. And gradually go on increasing the spiritual activity, thereby reducing the engagement of the mind in material activities. And by the time you retire from all your material responsibilities, you should have completely changed all your engagements of the mind from material to spiritual. It means go on increasing your spiritual activities by which you engage the mind in spiritual life.

---
### sadhana bhakti, association, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LI_x8kx2fbA?t=799
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When the mind is purified, and that means the mind is constantly engaged in the service of the Lotus Feet of the Lord, then at the time of death, the mind will not produce another material body, but the mind will actually enable the soul to go back home, back to God."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Sadhana Bhakti as a program to increase devotional service through body, words, and mind, suggesting various activities like chanting, singing, offering food, reading scriptures, and associating with dedicated devotees to purify the mind and enable the soul to return to Godhead at the time of death.

Therefore, the program of Sadhana Bhakti is how to increase the activities that we perform through the body, words and mind, more and more in devotional service. So, there are 64 principles of Sadhana Bhakti, different varieties of engagement in spiritual life. Why 64? Why so many? Because in different ways, we are engaging our body, words and mind in material activities. So, if we have to spiritualize our engagement of the mind, we have to find different varieties of engagement for the mind through the body, through the words and through the mind. So, therefore, there are 64 activities. And one by one by one, we should start adding those activities to our daily routine. Just like chanting Hare Krishna, then singing Hare Krishna, then offering food to Krishna, either a picture of Krishna or a deity of Krishna, accepting Prasadam after offering food, then reading the spiritual literature, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, hearing about Krishna's pastimes, like that. So many different engagements are possible and increase the engagements. Now, how do the engagements become more and more? When you associate with devotees who are already engaged in such spiritual activities, especially the devotees who are dedicated for Krishna Consciousness. Throughout the day, they are engaged in varieties of activities simply meant for engaging the mind in Krishna Consciousness. So, you also associate, you learn, you become interested, you become attracted. You also get some practical tips about how to manage your material duties, material responsibilities while minimizing the material engagement of the mind. You learn from the devotees. And in this way, the mind becomes purified. When the mind is purified, and that means the mind is constantly engaged in the service of the Lotus Feet of the Lord, then at the time of death, the mind will not produce another material body, but the mind will actually enable the soul to go back home, back to God. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.42 | 30-12-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### detachment, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.42
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thus annihilating the gross body of five elements into the three qualitative modes of material nature, he merged them in one science."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse to be discussed, explaining that it describes Yudhishthira Maharaj's process of freeing himself from material attachments by merging the gross body into the three modes of material nature and then into the self, Brahman.

In this session, we will be discussing the 42nd verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tritve Hutvacha Panchatvam Tachayi Katve Ajuhon Munihi Sarvam Atmani Aju Habid Brahmani Atmanam Avyaye. The meaning of this verse is, thus annihilating the gross body of five elements into the three qualitative modes of material nature, he merged them in one science, and then absorbed that science in the self, Brahman, which is inexhaustible in all circumstances.

---
### material energy, identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=86
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the basic understanding is, I am not this body."
**Summary:** This segment explains Srila Prabhupada's precepts on understanding Yudhishthira Maharaj's process, emphasizing that everything in the material world is a product of the Mahat-Tatva Avyakta and that the living entity is distinct from the material body.

Now, this is a highly technical description of how Yudhishthira Maharaj freed himself from all material attachments, but Srila Prabhupada explains very nicely in the precepts how to understand this process that Yudhishthira Maharaj is applying. So in the precepts, Srila Prabhupada says, all that is manifested in the material world is a product of the Mahat-Tatva Avyakta, and things that are visible in our material vision are nothing but combinations and permutations of such variegated material products. So the basic understanding is, I am not this body, and this body is one of the many different products of the material elements, which are generated at the beginning of the creation out of the material energy called Mahat-Tatva Avyakta.

---
### Mahat-Tatva, material elements
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=176
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whatever we see in our material vision is nothing but the combination of different material objects or products."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the activation of material energy (Avyakta to Mahat-Tatva) by Mahavishnu's glance, leading to the creation of material elements and the variegated material universe, emphasizing the distinction between the living entity and these material products.

The material energy in its dormant condition, inactive state, is Avyakta, and when it is activated at the beginning of creation by the glance of Mahavishnu, it becomes Mahat-Tatva, the state in which the material energy becomes activated. Activated means the three gunas start working, and those three gunas, they interact with the Mahat-Tatva and produce so many material elements, and those material elements are combined to form this variegated material universe, different products, different material elements, different objects, and different bodies, everything. So now, whatever we see in our material vision is nothing but the combination of different material objects or products, but the living entity is different from such material products. They are completely different from the body, from the mind, from the intelligence, from the false ego, from the material objects, varieties of material objects.

---
### forgetfulness, false ego, sense enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=278
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is due to the living entity's forgetfulness of his eternal nature as eternal servitor of the Lord, and his false conception of being a so-called Lord, the Lord of material nature, that he is obliged to enter into the existence of false sense enjoyment."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the root cause of material existence and false sense enjoyment is the living entity's forgetfulness of their eternal nature as a servant of the Lord and the false desire to be the Lord of material nature.

Further, Srila Prabhupada says, it is due to the living entity's forgetfulness of his eternal nature as eternal servitor of the Lord, and his false conception of being a so-called Lord, the Lord of material nature, that he is obliged to enter into the existence of false sense enjoyment. The very fundamental statement Prabhupada is making here to help us understand why this material existence, why this false sense enjoyment. It is because of our forgetfulness of our eternal nature. I am eternal. I am an eternal spirit soul. And as eternal spirit soul, I am eternal servant of Krishna, or servitor of Krishna, or devotee of Krishna, eternally. And also, not only I forget my eternal position as eternal devotee of Krishna, but I also have got a wrong conception that I want to be the Lord of material nature. I want to be the Lord. There is only one Lord, that is Krishna. Material nature, spiritual nature, living entities, everyone, everything. Krishna is the only Lord. Ekala, Eeshwara, Krishna. Nobody else is Lord. All others are very, very, very tiny, insignificant living entities. So this tiny living entity simply, foolishly desires, says, I want to be the Lord of material nature. Then you are forced to enter into the existence of false sense enjoyment.

---
### material energy, mind, gross body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=398
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our actual mind is materially affected because of which there is a need for so many different material products to be generated to give us the facility for the mind to be materially affected."
**Summary:** This segment continues Prabhupada's explanation, stating that the generation of material energies affects the mind, leading to the creation of the gross body of five elements, which is unnecessary for spiritual life but necessary for material engagement.

Why false? Because it is a concoction for your sake. It is temporarily created and enabled. So, Prabhupada next says, thus, a concomitant generation of material energies is a principal cause of the minds being materially affected. Our actual mind is materially affected because of which there is a need for so many different material products to be generated to give us the facility for the mind to be materially affected. Thus, the gross body of five elements is produced. Birth means what? A gross body of five elements, combination of five elements, is produced in the mother's womb. Actually, there is no need of this gross body if we are not having any material engagement. For spiritual life, there is no need, but our present condition is material life, so for that, this is necessary.

---
### detachment, three gunas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=494
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Yudhishthira reversed the action and merged the five elements of the body in the three modes of material nature."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Yudhishthira Maharaj reversed the process of creation by merging the five elements of the body back into the three modes of material nature, extinguishing the qualitative distinctions of the body.

So Prabhupada explains, Maharaj Yudhishthira reversed the action and merged the five elements of the body in the three modes of material nature. The beginning of creation, the three modes of material nature combined, interacted, and produced the different bodies. Now, this body which is generated by the combination of the three gunas, interaction, it has to be reversed so that the body will go back to the three gunas. The qualitative distinction of the body as being good, bad, or mediocre is extinguished.

---
### detachment, spiritual sky
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=550
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When one is thus inclined to become an associate of the Supreme Lord, the Personality of Godhead, in one of the innumerable planets of the spiritual sky, especially in Goloka Vrindavana, one has to think always that he is different from the material energy."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the irrelevance of considering the body as good, bad, or mediocre and emphasizes the need to think of oneself as separate from the material energy to become an associate of the Lord in the spiritual sky, particularly in Goloka Vrindavana.

So, we know that the body sometimes when it is in a healthy condition, a very nice body. The same body when it becomes diseased and gives us pain or suffering, or I don't like this body. Or identifying the body with some possessions, I have so many different varieties of things to enjoy, a very nice body. But if I don't have many things that I desire to enjoy with, then this body is not a very nice body. Like that, we have our own conception of body being good, bad, or mediocre, but all these are irrelevant, these distinctions between different types of bodies, when the body itself, we think is unnecessary, un-artha, superfluous. It is unwanted. When the body is unwanted, then the body becomes merged in the material energy. That means body means the elements, body is transformed into the elements, and the elements become, go to the reservoir of all the elements. When one is thus inclined to become an associate of the Supreme Lord, the Personality of Godhead, in one of the innumerable planets of the spiritual sky, especially in Goloka Vrindavana, one has to think always that he is different from the material energy. The whole thinking process has to completely change, no more thinking in terms of material body, material enjoyment, material activities, material relationship, none of those.

---
### pure spirit, spiritual training
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=682
**Verse References:** —

So one has to always think that I am different from the material energy, I am completely spiritual. He has nothing to do with the material energy and therefore he has to realize himself as pure spirit. Spirit is always pure but I think otherwise. So that thinking has to change, has to completely be absorbed in the thought of I am pure spirit. And as pure spirit I am qualitatively equal with the Supreme Brahman or the Parameshwara or the Supreme Lord, Krishna. ### detachment, freedom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=752
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This sense of freedom from material relations as well as freedom from the material engagement of the gross and subtle encirclement makes one free to act as a servant of the Lord even though one is in the material world."
**Summary:** This segment uses the example of Yudhishthira Maharaj to illustrate how detachment from material responsibilities and a sense of freedom from material relations allows one to act as a servant of the Lord even while still in the material world.

Maharaja Yudhishthira after distributing his kingdom to Pariksit and Vajra, did not think himself the emperor of the world or head of the Kuru dynasty. See when he gave away charge, he is no longer thinking I am the emperor or I have got to do anything with ruling the kingdom, because training, handing charge, everything is over. Pariksit is fully ready to take over, and he is taken over also when Yudhishthira Maharaj is handed over. Now Yudhishthira Maharaj is not going to worry or be concerned how he is going to rule, what if he makes a mistake, what if he wants advice, nothing, because his job is over, his business is over, he is only thinking of going back to Godhead. This sense of freedom from material relations as well as freedom from the material engagement of the gross and subtle encirclement makes one free to act as a servant of the Lord even though one is in the material world.

---
### liberation, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E5wZZzSp9N0?t=831
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is complete freedom to act as servant of Krishna, devotee of Krishna and for that the spiritual senses, the spiritual mind, the spiritual intelligence is very much required and is there as the devotee, as a pure devotee."
**Summary:** This segment explains that liberation from material entanglement can be achieved even before death by handing over material duties and engaging fully in devotional service, allowing one to act as a servant of Krishna with spiritual senses, mind, and intelligence, reaching the liberated stage of Jeevan Mukta.

Now it is not that exactly when death comes and obviously I have to leave this body, only then I become free from this body. No, even before death comes. If I hand over charge of all my material duties and responsibilities to someone else, a younger person, then by engaging completely in devotional service, I get a freedom from all material relationships. All material relationships I get freedom, no more material relationships. Then freedom from the entanglement or imprisonment in the material gross body of the five elements. The body is there but I have no need for this body, neither I am concerned with any activities of this body, nor the activities of the material mind or the material intelligence, not required. Though they are there, before death they are there, but I don't require to use them, I don't require to engage them, no. So this is complete freedom to act as servant of Krishna, devotee of Krishna and for that the spiritual senses, the spiritual mind, the spiritual intelligence is very much required and is there as the devotee, as a pure devotee. It is there. So this is called liberated stage, even in the material world, Jeevan Mukta. And Yudhishthira Maharaj actually attained that. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Chiravasa Niraharo Baddhavan Mukta Murdha Jaha Darsayan Atmano Rupam Jadonmatta Pishachavat Anavekshamano Niragad Ashranvan Badhiro Yatha. ### devotion, Varnashrama Dharma, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tgOACsCIok4?t=374
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.44
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For self-realization, for going back to Godhead, this is a necessary step."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces and translates verse 44, discussing Yudhishthira's journey north and Srila Prabhupada's explanation of the Varnashrama Dharma system and the necessity of renunciation for self-realization.

44th verse. 44th verse. 44th verse. Translation is as follows the 44th verse. Você empezou go para, towards the north, treading the path accepted by his forefathers and great men, to devote himself completely to the thought of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. And he lived in that way wherever he went. So in the Purport Shrila Prabhupada says, it is understood from this verse that Maharaja Yudhishthira followed in the footsteps of his forefathers and the great devotees of the Lord. We have discussed many times before that the system of Varnashrama Dharma, as it was strictly followed by the inhabitants of the world, specifically by those who inhabited the Aryavarta province of the world, emphasizes the importance of leaving all household connections at a certain stage of life. Aryavarta means the tract of land which is now called India, between the Himalayas and the Indian Ocean. This tract of land, India, what we now call India. So this is Aryavarta. And people who inhabit, live in this place, their culture was in the past to leave all household connections at a certain stage of life to completely free oneself from material entanglement, prepare oneself for going back to Godhead. The training and education was so important and thus a respectable person like Maharaja Yudhishthira had to leave all family connection for self-realization and going back to Godhead. Now somebody may wonder, Yudhishthira was such a respectable person as the emperor of the world, he is dressing in tone clothing and he is acting as a madman. So what's gone wrong with this person? Why is he doing this? Why is he doing that? If somebody recognizes him, oh he is the same person who was the emperor, now he is like a mad urchin. What has happened to him? Something has gone wrong with his mind, he has become deranged, mentally deranged. No, it is necessary for becoming free from all material entanglement. For self-realization, for going back to Godhead, this is a necessary step.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.45-46 | 01-01-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Kali Yuga, irreligion, following the example of elders
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=33
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.45
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The younger brothers of Maharaja Yudhishthira observed that the age of Kali had already arrived throughout the world, and that the citizens of the kingdom were already affected by irreligious practice."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 45th verse of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that Yudhishthira's younger brothers decided to retire from household life due to the influence of Kali Yuga and the increase of irreligious practices.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Chai Vanarottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from two verses, 45 and 46 of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 45th verse is as follows, Sarvetam Anunir Jagmoham, Bhraatara Ha Krita Nishchayaha, Kalina Adharma Mitrena, Drishtvas Prishta Praja Bhuvii. The meaning of this 45th verse: The younger brothers of Maharaja Yudhishthira observed that the age of Kali had already arrived throughout the world, and that the citizens of the kingdom were already affected by irreligious practice. Therefore they decided to follow in the footsteps of their elder brother.

---
### Gurukul, spiritual education, ultimate goal of life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=109
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Material knowledge was meant for their livelihood, and spiritual knowledge was meant for their ultimate goal of life."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing the importance of spiritual training in ancient times, where individuals were educated in gurukuls to understand the ultimate goal of life, contrasting it with modern education that primarily focuses on material well-being.

So after having described that, Yudhishthira Maharaj retired from household life, the younger brothers also followed the elder brother, they also retired from their respective household lives. So in the purport Shri Lapa Prabhupada says, the younger brothers of Maharaj Yudhishthira were already obedient followers of the great emperor, and they had sufficiently been trained to know the ultimate goal of life. So in the former times, the brahmanas, the kshatriyas and the vaisyas, the upper three sections of the society, they received systematic training in a gurukul from the teacher. The teacher would teach about spiritual and material knowledge. Material knowledge was meant for their livelihood, and spiritual knowledge was meant for their ultimate goal of life. So this ultimate goal of life is very important. Even nowadays there is training, there is education, but only in terms of how to keep this body fit for living in this world. There is no training for understanding that we are spirit souls, we belong to the spiritual world, we have to prepare for stopping the cycle of birth and death, we should prepare for going to the spiritual world. This is not taught in the regular schools and colleges. But formerly, all of them used to be trained in this. So not only the brahmanas, kshatriyas and vaisyas, even the shudras, though there was no training in a gurukul, in their homes, they would be following the elders and the general population was guided by the brahmanas as to what are their duties, material duties, spiritual duties. Everyone had to perform their material and spiritual duties as guided by the brahmanas, who were strictly following the directions of the shastras given by the Supreme Lord Krishna for the whole civilized human society. So everyone knew the ultimate goal of life.

---
### spiritual knowledge, chanting, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=300
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply to render devotional service to Lord Krishna. That's all. It's very simple."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the spiritual awareness of even illiterate people in Vedic culture with the ignorance of modern educated individuals regarding the nature of the soul and the goal of life, emphasizing that the spiritual duty of everyone is to render devotional service to Lord Krishna, simplified in this age by chanting Hare Krishna.

Srila Prabhupada many times says that in India, in the Vedic culture, even the most illiterate person in a remote part of any village will know that I am not this body, I am spirit soul. Will know that I am eternal. Will know that my goal is to get out of the cycle of birth and death. Will know that he has to actually perform his material and spiritual duties in order to get out of the entanglement of material existence. Everybody knows, everyone. And today there are so many so-called educated philosophers, scholars, scientists, but they do not know I am not the body, I am spirit soul. They do not know that. They do not know about material entanglement. See the contrast. So here it is clearly said the younger brothers of Maharaja Yudhishthira were already obedient followers of the great emperor and had sufficiently been trained to know the ultimate goal of life. Therefore they decidedly followed their eldest brother in rendering devotional service to Lord Krishna. So what is the spiritual duty of everyone? Simply to render devotional service to Lord Krishna. That's all. It's very simple. And it has been further simplified in this age by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu that to render devotional service to Lord Krishna means to simply chant Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. This can be done by anybody. It doesn't require any qualification. It is not difficult. It is very very easy.

---
### retirement, self-realization, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=424
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Here is a decision by authorities like the Pandavas. All of them engage themselves in favorably culturing the devotional service of the Lord, the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the Vedic principle of retiring from family life after half of one's life is finished to engage in self-realization, contrasting the potential bewilderment of retired individuals who find spiritual practices boring with the Pandavas' example of continuing devotional service with greater emphasis after retirement.

So Sri La Pravapada further says according to the principles of Sanatana Dharma one must retire from family life after half the duration of life is finished. Panchashodram vanam vrajayet. This is the Vedic injunction. And must engage himself in self-realization. You see. Engage oneself in self-realization in spiritual activities. But the question of engaging oneself is not always decided. What will they do after becoming retired? So Sri La Pravapada says sometimes retired men are bewildered about how to engage themselves for the last days of life. They don't know what to do. They sometimes think it is very boring to sit and chant all the time or to sit and read some scriptures or do some Deity worship. Oh it's very boring. So see the contrast here. Sri La Pravapada says here is a decision by authorities like the Pandavas. All of them engage themselves in favorably culturing the devotional service of the Lord, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. You see. They had trained, they had been trained and they were engaged before retiring, they were engaged in devotional service. So they continued their devotional service with greater emphasis because they are freed from material responsibilities. So further Sri La Pravapada says according to Swami Shridhara, he is the original commentator on the Srimad Bhagavatam, Dharma, Artha, Kama and Moksha or fruitive activities, philosophical speculations and salvation as conceived by several persons are not the ultimate goal of life.

---
### ultimate goal of life, Krishna consciousness, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=559
**Verse References:** BG 18.64
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the ultimate goal of life."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that even Moksha is not the ultimate goal of life, as it is often pursued by those unaware of the true goal, which is Krishna, as indicated in the Bhagavad Gita, highlighting the Pandavas' intelligence in following this principle without hesitation.

See this is something we have to carefully understand. Why is it being said that Moksha, even Moksha is not the ultimate goal of life. Why? Because Sri La Pravapada says they are more or less practiced by persons who have no information of the ultimate goal of life. And what is that ultimate goal of life? It is not Moksha. The ultimate goal of life is already indicated by the Lord himself in the Bhagavad Gita in the 18th chapter 64th verse. And the Pandavas were intelligent enough to follow it without hesitation. Krishna is the ultimate goal of life. Maamey vaisyasi, Maa manusmara, Madhyaji, Maam namaskuru, Bhavamad bhakto, Matkarmakrth, Matparamo. See all the Bhagavad Gita statements clearly indicate Krishna to be the ultimate goal. Krishna to be the ultimate goal. Krishna to be the ultimate goal. Krishna to be the ultimate goal.

### pure devotees, pious deeds, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=629
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.46, BG 7.28
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They all had performed all the principles of religion and as a result rightly decided that the lotus feet of the Lord Sri Krishna are the supreme goal of all."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 46th verse, explaining that the Pandavas, having performed all religious principles, rightly decided that the lotus feet of Lord Krishna are the supreme goal, meditating upon them without interruption. He then quotes Bhagavad Gita 7.28, stating that only those who have performed pious deeds in previous lives and are free from impious acts can concentrate on Krishna's lotus feet, emphasizing that the Pandavas were pure devotees who had always performed devotional service.

The 46th verse te sadhukrtha sarvartha nyatavya atyantikam atmanah manasa dharayam asuhu vaikuntha charanam bujam. The translation of this 46th verse: They all had performed all the principles of religion and as a result rightly decided that the lotus feet of the Lord Sri Krishna are the supreme goal of all. Therefore they meditated upon his feet without interruption. You see the ultimate goal of life is clearly indicated here. In the Purpose Srila Prabhupada says in the Bhagavad Gita 7th chapter 28th verse the Lord says that only those who have done pious deeds in previous lives and have become freed from the results of all impious acts can concentrate upon the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna. Yesham tu antagatam papam jananam punyakarmanam te dvandva mohanir mukta bhajan temam. Krishna is speaking that bhajan temam dhradam pratah. The Pandavas not only in this life but also in their previous lives had always performed the supreme pious work. What is the supreme pious work? Devotional service. And thus they are ever free from all the reactions of impious work. The Pandavas are pure devotees of Krishna. So there is no question of their engaging in any impious activities, sinful activities.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotees never do any sinful activity."
**Summary:** The speaker uses Arjuna's hesitation to kill his kinsmen in the battle as an example of a pure devotee's inherent aversion to sinful activities, even in a just cause, highlighting their kindness and soft-heartedness, and emphasizing that pure devotees are naturally endowed with all good qualities.

This is very evident that Arjuna was hesitating to kill his kinsmen even though they were so unjust. They were so corrupt. They troubled the Pandavas in so many ways, the Kauravas. But Arjuna hesitated to kill them. Because Arjuna is the pure devotee. Pure devotees never do any sinful activity. If there is even the slightest doubt whether they are acting sinfully or not, they refrain if they even have the slightest doubt that they may be indulging in sin. So Arjuna, even though he knew that this was a fair battle for a just cause, still, when it came to actually killing his own kinsmen, he hesitated. And he told Krishna, I cannot kill my kinsmen. So Srila Prabhupada says, that is because he is a pure devotee of the Lord. He is kind and soft-hearted. A pure devotee of the Lord is naturally endowed with all good qualities.

---
### devotional service, surrender, Vaikuntha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/_H6ZPOVQCZs?t=830
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only those who are free from all material contamination can accept the lotus feet of the Lord in the spiritual sky."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by quoting Srila Vishvanatha Chakravarti Thakura, explaining that dharma, artha, kama, and moksha are intermediate steps towards complete surrender to Krishna and devotional service. He emphasizes that only those free from material contamination can access the lotus feet of the Lord in the spiritual sky of Vaikuntha, highlighting the Pandavas' unwavering focus on Krishna's lotus feet in the spiritual world.

So, in this case, Sree Sri Prabhupada further says here, it is quite reasonable therefore that they concentrated their minds upon the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna. And Sri La Vishwa na Chakravarti Thakur, one of our previous acharyas, he writes in his commentary on the Bhagavatam, that dharma, artha, kama and moksha principles are accepted by persons from the results of impious action the pure devotees never have these goals the scriptures explain these are intermediate steps to come to the point of complete surrender to Krishna and become firmly fixed up in devotional service to lotus feet of Lord Krishna only those who are free from all material contamination can accept the lotus feet of the Lord in the spiritual sky the Vaikuntha world is situated far beyond the material sky and the material sky is under the management of Durga Devi but the Vaikuntha world is managed by the personal energy of the Lord yoga mind Naya Subhadra Devi so the Pandavas being pure devotees they fixed their attention completely on the lotus feet of Krishna in the spiritual world I'll stop here thank you very much Hare Krishna.

---

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.47-48 | 03-01-2023
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotional service, liberation, spiritual body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=34
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.47-48
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This abode of the Lord Sri Krishna, known as Goloka Vrindavana, cannot be attained by persons who are absorbed in the material conception of life."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces verses 47 and 48 of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that pure devotees attain the spiritual sky and the abode of Lord Krishna, Goloka Vrindavana, even in their current bodies, due to their constant devotional remembrance.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naramchaiva Narottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from two verses 47 and 48 of the 15th chapter of the first canto Srimad Bhagavatam. Tadhyana Udriktaya Bhaktiya Vishuddha Dhisana Pare Tasmin Narayana Pade Ekanta Matayogatim Avapur Duravapamte Asadbhir Vishayatmabhihi Vidhutakalmashasthanam Virajena Atmanayivahee. The meaning of these two verses is given together: Thus by pure consciousness, due to constant devotional remembrance, they attain the spiritual sky, which is ruled over by the supreme Narayana, Lord Krishna. This is attained only by those who meditate upon the one supreme Lord without deviation. This abode of the Lord Sri Krishna, known as Goloka Vrindavana, cannot be attained by persons who are absorbed in the material conception of life. One of us, being completely washed of all material contamination, attained that abode, in their very same bodies.

---
### Pandavas, spiritual body, transcendence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=141
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Pandavas went back to Godhead in the self-same body."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the significance of the Pandavas returning to Godhead in their physical bodies due to their purity, contrasting this with the general process of leaving the material body at death, and then quotes Srila Jiva Goswami on how one freed from the modes of material nature can attain perfection without changing the body.

This is very significant that the Pandavas went back to Godhead in the self-same body. Generally, at the end of life, we quit this body. We leave behind this material body. Even those who go back to Godhead, generally, leave the material body behind. But the Pandavas, in the self-same body, they went back to Godhead because they were purified of all material contamination by constant devotional remembrance of Krishna. This is the situation of the pure devotees, constantly thinking of Krishna, constantly engaged in Krishna's service, pure devotional service, no tinge of any material sense gratification in any of their activities. Such devotees' bodies are all spiritual. They are not material. So, Srila Prabhupada explains this point in the purport. According to Srila Jiva Goswami, a person freed from the three modes of material qualities, namely goodness, passion and ignorance, and situated in transcendence, can reach the highest perfection of life without change of body.

---
### material modes, devotional service, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=277
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who is engaged in pure devotional service, without deviation, such a person is completely free from the influence of all the three gunas."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Jiva Goswami's statement, explaining how being influenced by the material modes binds us to the world, but engaging in pure devotional service frees one from these modes, and then quotes Sanatana Goswami on how anyone can attain the perfection of a twice-born brahmana through spiritual discipline under a bona fide spiritual master.

So generally we are engaged in one of the three gunas because we are influenced by the gunas, the material modes. So our actions are either sattvic, rajasic or tamasic, or a combination of one or more of these qualities. So we have material activities and material reactions, which bind us to this world. Now those who are freed from these three qualities, because they are constantly engaged in the pure devotional service, Krishna explains this in the Bhagavad Gita, as Srila Prabhupada will quote later, this very verse, that one who is engaged in pure devotional service, without deviation, such a person is completely free from the influence of all the three gunas. All actions of such a person are completely transcendental. And further, Srila Prabhupada quotes Sanatana Goswami. What does he say? Srila Sanatana Goswami in his Haribhakti Vilas says, that a person, whatever he may be, can attain the perfection of a twice-born brahmana by undergoing the spiritual disciplinary actions under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master.

---
### spiritual master, purification, transformation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=383
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Just like the bell-metal can be turned into gold by chemical manipulation...similarly, any person from any material background...if he undergoes the spiritual disciplinary actions under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master, he can attain the perfection of a twice-born brahmana."
**Summary:** The speaker recites a verse from Sanatana Goswami's Haribhakti Vilas, comparing the transformation of bell-metal into gold through alchemy to the purification of a person from any background into a twice-born brahmana through spiritual discipline under a spiritual master.

yatha kanchanatam yati kamsyam rasa-vidhanatah tatha deeksha-vidhanena vijatvam jayate nirunam rasa-vidhana and deeksha-vidhana. This is a comparison made by Srila Sanatana Goswami, that just like the bell-metal can be turned into gold by chemical manipulation, by treating it with an alchemical process using mercury, rasa-vidhana. Similarly, any person from any material background, how much ever contaminated he may be, if he undergoes the spiritual disciplinary actions under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master, he can attain the perfection of a twice-born brahmana.

---
### sadhana bhakti, spiritual master, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=476
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One cannot independently do anything in spiritual life, no. You require the proper guidance of a bona fide spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of sadhana bhakti under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master, highlighting that spiritual progress is impossible independently and that strict adherence to rules and regulations leads to purification and the perfection of becoming a devotee.

vijatvam jayate nirunam. So, that process is described as sadhana bhakti, sadhana bhakti. And, it is given in the scriptures, anyone who adopts the process of sadhana bhakti under proper guidance of a bona fide spiritual master. One cannot independently do anything in spiritual life, no. You require the proper guidance of a bona fide spiritual master. And under such expert guidance, strictly following the rules and regulations and the process, one becomes purified. And that purification results in one becoming a perfect twice-born brahmana. Perfection of a brahmana is to become a devotee. So one becomes a devotee. So Srila Prabhupada says, it is therefore the actual guidance that matters in the process of becoming a brahmana, even without change of body, or in going back to Godhead without change of body. It is the actual guidance of an expert and engaging properly in the activities of sadhana bhakti.

---
### spiritual body, devotional service, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotional service is only possible on the Brahman platform, on the spiritual platform."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Srila Jiva Goswami affirms the truth that there is no need to change the body to go back to Godhead after executing pure devotional service, and that the Bhagavad Gita confirms that anyone who systematically executes pure devotional service without deviation can attain Brahman perfection, which is only possible on the spiritual platform, leading to direct access to the spiritual world, specifically Goloka Vrindavana, without changing the body.

Now, Srila Jeeva Goswami says here that the use of the word 'he' in this connection positively affirms this truth. What is the word? In the verse, virajena atmana eva he. This word 'he', ending word of the 48th verse, that affirms positively this truth that there is no need to change body if one has to go back to Godhead after having executed pure devotional service. So this fact is affirmed here. And the Bhagavad Gita confirms this that anyone who systematically executes pure devotional service without deviation can attain this perfection of Brahman. Brahman perfection needs to be situated on the spiritual platform above the three gunas. And on the Brahman platform then one engages in pure devotional service. Pure devotional service is only possible on the Brahman platform, on the spiritual platform. Then one becomes completely perfect. Becomes completely perfect. And such a person actually is eligible to directly go to the spiritual world and that also the supreme planet, Koloka Vrindavana, without change of body.

---
### Krishna's body, spiritual form, pure devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna returned to his own abode without a change of body because Lord's body is completely transcendental."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Lord Krishna returns to his abode without changing his body because his body is transcendental and never deteriorates, contrasting this with material bodies that change and must be quit at death, and then states that pure devotees, constantly engaged in devotional service, also do not change their bodies because their bodies are spiritualized.

Now this has already been explained by Srila Prabhupada in an earlier verse where it was said Lord Krishna returned to his own abode without a change of body. Because Lord's body is completely transcendental. Even when he comes to this world he comes in his original spiritual form. Ajopisan avyayatma. Avyayatma means Krishna never changes his body. His body never deteriorates. His body is always spiritual. Material bodies are deteriorating. They change. And we have to quit this body at the time of death or at the end of this life or when we are transferred to another body. We have to quit this body. But Krishna never accepts any material body. He never comes in touch with matter. He is always situated in his eternal spiritual form, spiritual body, which may be of different forms according to the need. But all of them are the same spirit, same supreme Lord without any change. The same supreme Lord. So when Lord Krishna returns to his abode he returns in his personal form, the same supreme spiritual form. There is no change of body. Similarly, pure devotees simply because they are engaged in pure devotional service always, constantly, 24 hours a day. They don't change their body because their body is a spiritual one, spiritualized by pure devotional service.

---
### chanting, spiritualization, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ziIK8i18pGg?t=796
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Our body becomes spiritualized by constantly engaging in chanting and hearing the holy name."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that even practicing constant chanting of Hare Krishna can spiritualize our bodies, comparing it to iron becoming fire-like in contact with fire, and emphasizes that our material body can also become spiritualized through engagement in pure devotional service.

Spiritualized by pure devotional service. So, even if we practice constant chanting of Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, then we don't have to change our body. Our body becomes spiritualized by constantly engaging in chanting and hearing the holy name. Just like iron in contact with fire becomes red hot and fire-like. Similarly, of course this is a crude example, but in the case of our own material body, that we have now, it also becomes spiritualized by our engagement in devotional service, pure devotional service. So I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
