## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.49 | 04-01-2023
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### invocation, introduction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura, while on pilgrimage, left his body at Prabhasa because he was absorbed in thought of Lord Krishna."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and an introduction to the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam that will be discussed, focusing on Vidura leaving his body absorbed in Krishna.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chaksharunmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Srivasa Adi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 49th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Vidropi Parityajya Prabhase Deham Atmanaha Krishna Aveshenata Chittaha Pitrabhir Svakshayam Yayau. The meaning of this verse is, Vidura, while on pilgrimage, left his body at Prabhasa because he was absorbed in thought of Lord Krishna, he was received by the denizens of Pitraloka planet, where he returned to his original post.

---
### devotion, hierarchy, demigods
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=108
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The difference between the Pandavas and Vidura is that the Pandavas are eternal associates of the Lord, the Personality of Godhead, whereas Vidura is one of the administrative demigods in charge of the Pitraloka planet, and is known as Yama-Raj."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the Pandavas as eternal associates of Krishna with Vidura, who is identified as an incarnation of Yama-Raj, a pure devotee administering justice in Pitraloka.

So now, after having described the Pandavas, they returned to Krishna's personal abode. Now the description is being given of Vidura. Earlier we had heard how Vidura instructed Dhritarashtra to prepare himself for quitting the body and attaining self-realization. So now Vidura himself, he was constantly absorbed in thought of Krishna. Therefore, when he left his body, he went back to his original post in Pitraloka. So Srila Prabhupada explains this point in the purport. The difference between the Pandavas and Vidura is that the Pandavas are eternal associates of the Lord, the Personality of Godhead, whereas Vidura is one of the administrative demigods in charge of the Pitraloka planet, and is known as Yama-Raj. Vidura is an incarnation of Yama-Raj. Yama-Raj is always a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. Even in his Pitraloka planet, he is always in the position of being a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord. So his devotion and service is eternal. He also has got a responsibility of punishing the sinful people, extremely sinful people in the post of Yama-Raj.

---
### devotion, karma, fear
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=207
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are very very sinful, they are punished by Yama-Raj by being sent to hellish planets for punishment."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Yama-Raj's role in punishing the sinful and sending them to hellish planets, contrasting this with his role as a cordial friend to devotees of the Lord.

So he is not like the other devatas who have got a desire for material enjoyment and therefore they occupy a post. No. Yama-Raj is not like that. Vidura is not like that. So Srila Prabhupada says about Vidura as Yama-Raj, men are afraid of Yama-Raj because it is he only who awards punishment to the miscreants of the material world. Those who are very very sinful, they are punished by Yama-Raj by being sent to hellish planets for punishment. Hellish planets are not places of inhabitation for any kind. They are not places of inhabitation for any condition. So it is exclusively meant for giving punishment to extremely sinful persons. So these miscreants, sinful people, they are afraid of Yama-Raj. But those who are devotees of the Lord have nothing to fear from him because he himself is a devotee. Yama-Raj is devotee, pure devotee. So other devotees, pure devotees of the Lord have nothing to fear from Yama-Raj. To the devotees, Yama-Raj is a cordial friend. But to the non-devotees, he is fear personified. He is doing his duty of punishing the non-devotees who are engaged in grievous sinful activities. As we have already discussed, it is understood that Yama-Raj was cursed by Manduka Muni to be degraded as a Shudra.

---
### karma, devotion, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=322
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though he is a pure devotee, he accepted the curse and therefore he had to take birth as Vidura."
**Summary:** The segment explains the curse that led Yama-Raj to be born as Vidura, the son of a maidservant, and emphasizes that despite this, Vidura lived a pious life dedicated to devotional service.

Once Yama-Raj made a mistake in the past that this Manduka Muni who did some mistake when he was a small boy, he was punished later on for the mistake innocently committed. So Manduka Muni became angry and he cursed Yama-Raj to take birth as the son of a Shudrani, a maidservant, Dasiputra. So that curse, even though he is a pure devotee, he accepted the curse and therefore he had to take birth as Vidura, but as the son of a maidservant. So he was, Yama-Raj was born as Vidura, considered as a Shudra. Therefore Vidura was an incarnation of Yama-Raj because he is cursed to take birth as a Shudra. As an eternal servitor of the Lord, he displayed his devotional activities very ardently and lived a life of a pious man. Devotees always live a pious life. They don't commit sinful activities. This is the wonderful nature or characteristic of a devotee, even in this material world, even if they are harassed, even if they are insulted, even if they are cheated, even if the others try to mislead them, they are fixed in devotional service. They don't get misled.

---
### devotion, salvation, instruction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=454
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura lived a life of a pious man so much so that a materialistic man like Dhritarashtra also got salvation by his instruction."
**Summary:** This section highlights Vidura's pious life and his ability to guide even a materialistic person like Dhritarashtra to salvation, emphasizing the power of devotional service.

On the other hand, it is described here, such pure devotees as Vidura, about them Prabhupada is telling, that Vidura lived a life of a pious man so much so that a materialistic man like Dhritarashtra also got salvation by his instruction. Dhritarashtra was very materialistic, but even Dhritarashtra got salvation or moksha by the instructions of Vidura. What is the connection between Vidura and Dhritarashtra? Both are born as sons of Vichitravirya, but Vidura was born as a son of a maid servant of one of the queens of Vichitravirya, and Dhritarashtra was born as the son of the queen of Vichitravirya. So Dhritarashtra had a share in the inheritance of the kingdom, but Vidura had no share. So Vidura accepted his position as the Shudra, but Dhritarashtra as a Kshatriya, he became the caretaker king of the entire world. So Dhritarashtra was very materialistic, but by the instruction of Vidura, he was able to get moksha, salvation.

---
### devotion, purification, remembrance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=580
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vidura by his pious activities in the devotional service of the Lord, was able to always remember the lotus feet of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Vidura's constant remembrance of the Lord's lotus feet, achieved through devotional service, purified him from any perceived contamination due to his birth as a Shudra.

So therefore Prabhupada says, Vidura by his pious activities in the devotional service of the Lord, was able to always remember the lotus feet of the Lord. So remembering constantly the lotus feet of the Lord is a devotional service, and that is possible by constant engagement in devotional activities. This remembrance is not some idle meditation or lazily sitting in the excuse of doing meditation and actually sleeping. No, it's not that. Now by constantly remembering the lotus feet of the Lord, Vidura became washed of all contamination of a Shudra-born life. Actually as a pure devotee there is no contamination, but even if there were any contamination due to taking birth as a Shudra and engaging in such Shudra activities, still he became free from all contamination by his constant remembrance of the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord.

---
### devotion, demigods, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=668
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The demigods are also associates of the Lord without personal touch whereas the direct associates of the Lord are in constant personal touch with him."
**Summary:** This segment differentiates between direct associates of the Lord, like the Pandavas, who have constant personal interaction, and demigods like Yama-Raj, who are also associates but without the same level of personal connection.

In many places in scripture it is said one who constantly thinks of Krishna becomes fully purified. At the end he was received again by the denizens of Pitraloka and posted in his original position. He came from Pitraloka, he went back to Pitraloka. So Srila Prabhupada says the demigods are also associates of the Lord without personal touch whereas the direct associates of the Lord are in constant personal touch with him. What is the difference between Pandavas and Vidura? Pandavas are in constant touch with the Supreme Lord as his direct associates. The demigods like Yamaraj they are also associates of the Lord but they don't have that personal touch. They participate in the Lord's pastimes when they are given an opportunity because Lord's pastimes are an internal potency.

---
### devotion, incarnation, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=740
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord and his personal associates incarnate in many universes without cessation."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Lord and his associates continuously incarnate in many universes, emphasizing the importance of constant engagement in devotional activities.

So unless they are given an opportunity they have to engage in such devotional activities as constantly remembering the Lord, constantly hearing the glories of the Lord from the Bhagavatam, constantly chanting and hearing the Holy Name, there is constant association, constant engagement in devotional service. So Srila Prabhupada says further the Lord and his personal associates incarnate in many universes without cessation. Without cessation, no stopping. No stoppage. They continuously incarnate in many many universes. Now the difference is between the Lord and his associates is the Lord remembers all of his previous incarnations whereas the associates forget due to their being very minute parts and parcels of the Lord.

---
### incarnation, memory, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YrmgHs-Vaik?t=820
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord remembers all his previous incarnations. I remember all of them but you do not."
**Summary:** This final segment illustrates the difference in memory between the Lord and his associates, using the example of Krishna and Arjuna, and concludes with a thank you and Hare Krishna.

We are very very tiny parts and parcels of the Lord. Just like Arjuna, Krishna tells Arjuna many many births you and I have taken in the past. You and I have taken many many births in the past. Now the Lord remembers all his previous incarnations. I remember all of them but you do not. So Prabhupada explains Arjuna is a pure devotee. Arjuna is a constant companion of the Lord, but he cannot remember his past appearances with the Lord. He can't remember. That Lord only knows. Lord only can remember because the Lord is supreme Lord, unlimited Lord. He has unlimited memory. We are very very tiny part so we have limited memory. Therefore this difference always exists. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

Om Agyana Timiran Dhasya Gyananjali Shalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Vanarottamam. Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 5th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st Canticle.

### separation, divine power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xqkkv2LgURE?t=54
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna said, O King, the Supreme Personality of Godhead Hari, who treated me exactly like an intimate friend, has left me alone. Thus my astounding power, which astonished even the demigods, is no longer with me."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains Srimad Bhagavatam 1.15.5, focusing on Arjuna's feelings of separation from Krishna and the loss of his divine power after Krishna's departure.

The meaning of this verse is, Arjuna said, O King, the Supreme Personality of Godhead Hari, who treated me exactly like an intimate friend, has left me alone. Thus my astounding power, which astonished even the demigods, is no longer with me. So immediately Arjuna's response is that the Supreme Lord has returned to his abode and Arjuna is feeling separation from that dear most Lord Krishna. And the immediate response of Arjuna is that Arjuna's astounding power, which he displayed in the presence of Krishna, is no longer with Arjuna. And that power was astounding even to the greatest of demigods in the heavenly planets. And that power is no longer with Arjuna.

### opulence, divine energy, Krishna

**Verse References:** BG 10.41
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anyone specifically powerful and opulent in wealth, strength, beauty, knowledge, and all that is materially desirable, is to be considered but a product of an insignificant portion of the complete whole of my energy."
**Summary:** The speaker references Bhagavad Gita 10.41, explaining that all opulence, strength, and beauty are derived from Krishna's energy, as explained by Srila Prabhupada.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains this in the purport very nicely. What is the significance of this statement by Arjuna? Srila Prabhupada explains on the basis of what is said in the Bhagavad Gita by Lord Krishna. Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, In the Bhagavad Gita, in the 10th chapter, the 10th chapter of Bhagavad Gita is entitled The Opulence of the Absolute. What is the meaning of that? The opulences of Krishna, which are displayed by him in the material world. So those opulences are described in the 10th chapter, specifically on the request of Arjuna in the Bhagavad Gita. So, one such verse, 41st verse, in the 10th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna says, Anyone specifically powerful and opulent in wealth, strength, beauty, knowledge, and all that is materially desirable, is to be considered but a product of an insignificant portion of the complete whole of my energy, my energy, everything, this Krishna statement in the Bhagavad Gita, the 41st verse and 10th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita.

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept that all forms of wealth, beauty, strength, and even the material body are derived from Krishna's energy, emphasizing the dependence of all beings on Krishna.

So, what does it mean? Anybody may have some wealth, or they may have extraordinary beauty, or strength, or knowledge, or any possession, anything glorious, anything magnificent, wonderful anything astounding but that is only Krishna's own energy which is a tiny fragment of that energy is being displayed so this is to be understood all wealth all strength all knowledge all beauty is actually Krishna's own energy in different forms in different varieties Krishna's own beauty each one of us if we have some beauty it is a tiny portion of Krishna's total beauty. Even for a matter this material body that we have which is made up of the five elements earth water fire air and sky this material body is also a tiny portion of the earth water fire air and sky which is Krishna's material energy from that a small portion is taken combined and this body is formed so the body is also part of Krishna's material energy. I a spirit soul I am also part of Krishna's marginal energy so everything is Krishna's own energy every one is Krishna's own energy this is very carefully understood.

### devotees, empowerment, mission
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xqkkv2LgURE?t=355
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the Lord descends on the earth along with his eternal, ever-liberated associates, he not only displays the divine energy possessed by himself, but also empowers his associate devotees with the required energy to execute his mission of incarnation."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna empowers his devotees with the necessary qualities to participate in his mission when he incarnates on Earth, while Krishna himself comes with the totality of his opulences.

Therefore, Srila Prabhupada says, no one therefore can be independently powerful in any measure without being endowed by the Lord. So if I have any wealth, it is bestowed upon me by Krishna. Now, if somebody else has more wealth or less wealth than me, even they are having that greater wealth or lesser wealth bestowed by Krishna only. Now this is irrespective of somebody's being a devotee or a non-devotee. Independent of that, everyone's wealth actually is a portion of Krishna's own wealth. Everyone's strength, everyone's beauty, everyone's knowledge. It is a portion of Krishna's knowledge. Now further, Srila Prabhupada explains, when the Lord descends on the earth along with his eternal, ever-liberated associates, he not only displays the divine energy possessed by himself, but also empowers his associate devotees with the required energy to execute his mission of incarnation. Lord Krishna comes. Lord Krishna comes to this world as incarnation. He comes with his associates, eternal associates from the spiritual world. Now he comes with his energies, with his power, with his wealth, with his beauty. His devotees also, they come here and they are bestowed with required wealth, strength, beauty, knowledge, etc. by the Lord in order for them, so, to participate in Krishna's mission in this material world. So, they are endowed with the required, necessary wealth, strength, etc. And Krishna comes, of course, with his totality of his wealth, strength, knowledge, beauty, etc.

**Summary:** Referencing Bhagavad Gita 4.5, the speaker discusses the difference between Krishna and his eternal associates regarding memory and knowledge, highlighting Krishna's unlimited memory compared to the limited memory of even liberated souls like Arjuna.

Now, in the same Bhagavad Gita, in the fourth chapter, fifth verse, it is stated, The Lord and his eternal associates descend on the earth many times but the Lord remembers all the different roles of incarnations whereas the associates by his supreme will forget them. Now in the fourth chapter Krishna explains to Arjuna both you and I have come to this earth many many times in the past I remember all those previous appearances but you have forgotten them so it is a fact that the eternal associates of Krishna like Arjuna whenever Krishna incarnates Arjuna also incarnates with Krishna but Arjuna cannot remember in his present life he cannot remember his past lives but Krishna remembers everything all his past appearances everything Krishna's incarnation and even Krishna remembers with whom he has appeared in which incarnation that also remembers because Krishna's memory is unlimited our memory is limited Krishna's knowledge is unlimited our knowledge is limited like that so Krishna remembers everything we don't remember everything that difference eternally exists between us.

### limitations, divine will, withdrawal
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xqkkv2LgURE?t=588

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the eternal difference between the limited jiva and the unlimited Supreme Lord, even for liberated souls. He explains that Krishna withdraws powers bestowed upon devotees like Arjuna once their mission is complete.

Each one of us all the Lingv Gate dies and so it is for that release and the life of us on Earth and the Supreme Lot so Krishna is always the Supreme Lord as much as all of us are subуется to ob Arms and we are always limited or finite and we can't for even be in material terms This difference in the unmanageable, unlimited Jeva exist in this this height completely Wayne, Even in the case of pure devotees, even in the case of liberated souls, and definitely so in the case of conditioned souls or beings, living beings who are entangled in this material world. In all cases, all circumstances, all situations, all positions, every jiva, liberated jivas, all souls, all living beings, they are always limited and Krishna is always unlimited. Now further Srila Prabhupada says, the Lord takes away with him all his associates when he disappears from the earth. The power and energy which were bestowed upon Arjuna were required for fulfillment of the mission of the Lord. But when his mission was fulfilled, the emergency powers were withdrawn from Arjuna. Arjuna was bestowed some extraordinary powers by Krishna, but when the mission was over, Krishna withdrew the powers of Arjuna. Why? Because Prabhupada says, because the astounding powers of Arjuna, which were astonishing even to the denizens of heaven or the demigods, were no longer required.

### humility, service, dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xqkkv2LgURE?t=704
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The lesson is therefore that no one should be puffed up for his powers borrowed from the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that Arjuna's powers were withdrawn because they were not needed for his return to Godhead. He emphasizes that all powers are borrowed from Krishna and should be used in His service, not for personal pride.

As they were not meant for going back home, back to Godhead. So now what Arjuna requires to do is to go back to Godhead, because that's where he actually is eternally present. He had come when Krishna came along with Krishna for the sake of participating Krishna's pastimes. So when Krishna's pastimes are wound up, then Arjuna's powers are gone. Then Arjuna's power is also withdrawn by Krishna. It is Krishna only who gave Arjuna the power, extraordinary power. And Krishna only withdraws it when the power is no longer required by Arjuna. So that extraordinary power is not required for Arjuna to go back home, back to Godhead. If endowment of powers and withdrawal of powers by the Lord are possible even for a great devotee like Arjuna, or even for the demigods in heaven, then what to speak of ordinary living beings who are but fixed compared to such great souls, even any person in this world, however great he or she maybe. They display certain extraordinary power or knowledge or strength or skill or wealth because Krishna has bestowed it upon them. And when Krishna withdraws or takes away what is best for them, then they will be unable to possess them. They will be unable to use them. They will feel the absence of such extraordinary power or wealth when Krishna withdraws. So Srila Prabhupada concludes by telling, the lesson is therefore that no one should be puffed up for his powers borrowed from the Lord. Whatever anyone may have, they are actually borrowed from Krishna. And don't be proud about the borrowed power thinking.

---
### service, bewilderment, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xqkkv2LgURE?t=861
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The best use of such power and opulence is to engage them in the service of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the importance of using one's wealth, power, and skills in Krishna's service to avoid bewilderment and ensure that these gifts are not withdrawn. He concludes with a call to humility and devotion.

Which means that nobody's independent. Independently, nobody's powerful. The same man should rather feel obliged to the Lord for such benefactions and must utilize such power for the service of the Lord. Such power can be withdrawn at any time by the Lord. So the best use of such power and opulence is to engage them in the service of the Lord. So if you have got some extraordinary wealth or whatever wealth you have in your possession, it is best utilized in the service of Krishna. If you have any power, if you have any skill, if you have any knowledge, if you have any strength, please use it in Krishna's service. Otherwise, if you use it for something else, you will be completely bewildered. You will be baffled in your attempts to use it some other way. Because it will be withdrawn when it is no longer required by the Lord who bestows it. So don't be proud. Don't use it for anything other than the Lord's service. That's a lesson to be learned. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### separation from Krishna, loving relationship with Krishna, forgetfulness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AqTAbLOhPOs?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a living being, there is no one dearer than the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse to be discussed, focusing on Arjuna's feeling of separation from Krishna and emphasizing that every living being has a natural loving relationship with Krishna, which is often forgotten.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 6th verse of the 15th chapter. The meaning of this verse is, "I have just lost him whose separation for a moment would render all the universes unfavorable and void like bodies without life." Arjuna is further describing to Yudhishthira the feeling that he is having after having lost the association of Krishna. So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport that, factually, for a living being, there is no one dearer than the Lord. For any living being, not only for devotees, for every living being, who is the dearest person? It is Lord Krishna. So, which means that we should understand, if we are not experiencing that loving relationship with Krishna right now, in our present condition, it is because we have forgotten Krishna, somehow. We don't know exactly how. We cannot trace back, how did I lose my dear most friend, my relationship with my dear most friend Krishna, how did I lose? We cannot trace it. So, we have to accept on the authority of the scriptures that we have lost our dear most friend. We have lost Krishna. We have lost the relationship with our dear most friend Krishna, dear most person. So, Srila Prabhupada explains the fact of the relationship of every living being with Krishna.

---
### Svamsa, Vibhinamsa, expansions of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AqTAbLOhPOs?t=200
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna has got His own expansions, His personal expansions."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concepts of Svamsa and Vibhinamsa, describing the different types of expansions of Lord Krishna, including His personal expansions (Svamsa) and the living beings as expansions of His energy (Vibhinamsa).

Prabhupada says, the Lord expands himself by innumerable parts and parcels as Svamsa and Vibhinamsa. I will explain what this means. Svamsa. Lord Krishna has got His own expansions, His personal expansions. He himself expands in forms called Svamsa. His own personal expansion. Just like Krishna expands as Balaram, Narayana, Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha, Ram, Sankarsana, Vibhinamsa, Svamsa. Lord Krishna has got His own personal expansions. Lord Krishna has got His own personal expansions. Lord Krishna has got His own personal expansions. Lord Krishna has got His own personal expansion. Like Adha, Rama, Narasimha, Varaha, Vamana. Like that He expands in so many forms of incarnation, of different expansions in the spiritual world, incarnations in the material world. He expands Himself. His own Self. That means all these Swamsa expansions or incarnations are actually Lord Krishna himself. So, Swamsa is equal in power with Lord Krishna himself. So, Krishna only is present in another form as Rama, Narasimha, Varaha. And Krishna expands himself in another kind of expansion called Vibhinamsa. Vibhinamsa is the expansion of Krishna as the living beings. That is an expansion of Krishna's energy. Expansion of Krishna's energy. So, those expansions are very, very, very, very, very, very tiny. They don't have equal power as that of Krishna. The expansion called Vibhinamsa, which is the living beings, they don't have the same power as Krishna. They have a very, very, very, very, very, very, very tiny fraction, fragmental portion of Krishna's energy, Krishna's power, Krishna's wealth, Krishna's beauty, Krishna's strength, Krishna's knowledge, very tiny bit of that. That is what the Jivas have as expansions of Krishna. They are called Vibhinamsa.

---
### Paramatma, Jivatma, relationship between soul and body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AqTAbLOhPOs?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without the living being, the material body has no value."
**Summary:** The speaker further explains the concept of Paramatma as the Swamsa part of the Lord, seated in everyone's heart, and contrasts it with the Vibhinamsa parts, the living beings. He emphasizes the importance of the soul within the body and how the body becomes useless without the soul.

So, Srila Prabhupada says further in the purport, Paramatma is the Swamsa part of the Lord. Who is Paramatma? The Lord seated on everybody's heart in this material world. Paramatma is the Swamsa part of the Lord. Who is Paramatma? The Lord seated on everybody's heart in this material world. Paramatma. He is the Swamsa part. That means he has full power as Krishna. Whereas the Vibhinamsa parts are the living beings. Vibhinamsa parts, the living beings have got every, very, very, very less power. So, the English terms that are used by Prabhupada, we are infinitesimal part of Krishna and the incarnations of Krishna are infinite incarnations of Krishna. So, infinite and infinitesimal. Unlimited, limited. Param Brahma and Brahma. So, the difference always exists, eternally exists in all conditions. The living being is always tiny, limited. The Lord, including all his incarnations of his personal self, are always unlimited, always full with all power, all strength, all beauty, all knowledge, etc. Therefore, all those incarnations are called Bhagavan, Bhagavan Rama, Bhagavan Varaha, Bhagavan Narayana, Bhagavan Krishna. Therefore, whenever Krishna speaks in the Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavan Narayana, Bhagavan Krishna. Therefore, whenever Krishna speaks in the Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavan Narayana, Bhagavan Krishna. mijita-vedavyasa records it as Shree Bhagowanu Aacha. When the Lord incarnated as Kapila, then the description in the Bhagavatam is, Shree Bhagawanu Aacha. When Kapila speaks, the Samkhya philosophy explains. So, that is recorded by Vyasadeva in the Bhagavata, the Bhagavatam as Shri Bhagavanu Acha. So, this is important to understand. As the living being is the important factor of the material body, now all living beings, the important factor is the spirit soul, the living entity, the living being. Without the living being, the material body has no value. We know what happens when we say a person died. What happens? The living being, the spirit soul, has left the body. The body is a lump of matter, flesh and bones and skin and all that material covering. That's all that is left. Then we immediately dispose it off. Because the person is not there. There is no life. There is no person.

---
### Paramatma, Brahman, Krishna as the source
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AqTAbLOhPOs?t=573
**Verse References:** BG 9.4, BS 5.40
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the Supreme Personality of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the relationship between Paramatma, Brahman, and Krishna, emphasizing that Krishna is the source of both. He clarifies that Brahman and Paramatma have no independent existence without Krishna.

So, therefore, in a living person, the important factor is the soul inside, the living being inside, not the body. Similarly, without Paramatma, the living being has no status quo. Now, the soul also in the same way. The body has its existence based on the Paramatma within the heart of the living being, within the heart of the soul. It is only because of Paramatma's presence in the heart of the soul that the soul is able to utilize the body and do some activity while in the body. Without the presence of Paramatma we are completely helpless, we are totally lost, we are incapacitated. So Paramatma enters to facilitate our life in this body. Now further Srila Prabhupada says Brahman or Paramatma has no locus standing without the Supreme Lord Krishna. Now Krishna the Supreme Personality of the Supreme Lord Krishna is the Supreme Personality of the Supreme Lord. You've got it? He expands as Paramatma, He expands as Brahman. Now both Brahman and Paramatma expansion of Krishna, they have no independent or separate existence without Krishna. Krishna only expands as Paramatma. Krishna's bodily the effulgence only is Brahman or the impersonal feature or the energies. In different ways this Brahman is explained in the scriptures, which is one of the features of the Supreme Lord Krishna, the Absolute Truth. So that feature is a subordinate feature to Bhagavan, Krishna. Krishna says this in the Bhagavad Gita, brahmanohi pratishta aham. I am the basis of the Brahman, impersonal Brahman. Elsewhere in the Brahma Samhita it is said, yasya prabha prabhavato jagadandakoti. This entire jagadandakoti, the crores of universes, they are a rest. They are resting in the Brahman effulgence, which is the bodily luster of the Supreme Lord Krishna, yasya prabha. His bodily effulgence is the Brahman. And in that Brahman all the universes are resting, they are sustained. So that is explained also. This is all explained thoroughly in the Bhagavad Gita.

---
### interconnectedness, Krishna as the source, Jivatma

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the source of the Paramatma. Paramatma is the source of the Jivatma. Jivatma is the source of the body."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating the interconnectedness of everything, emphasizing that Krishna is the ultimate source of Paramatma, Jivatma, and the material body, and that nothing exists independently of Him.

They are all interrelated. They are linked with one another or independent factors. It appears like each one is existing independently, but actually none of them are independent of Krishna. So Krishna is the source of the Paramatma. Paramatma is the source of the Jivatma. Jivatma is the source of the body. I am just talking only of Jivatma and so there is no other source of the Paramatma. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Arjuna, Draupadi, Krishna's grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/F_UAXG2kTQE?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.15.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only by his merciful strength was I able to vanquish all the lusty princes assembled at the palace of King Drupada for the selection of the bridegroom; with my bow and arrow, I could pierce the fish target and thereby gain the hand of Draupadi."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 7th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Arjuna's remembrance of Draupadi's Swayamvara and how he was empowered by Lord Krishna.

In this session we will be discussing from the 7th verse of the 15th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Yad Samshrayat Drupadageham Upagatanam Ragnam Swayamvara Mukhe Smara Durmadanam Tejo Hritham Kalu Mayabihadascha Matsyaha Sajji Krtena Dhanusha Adhigata Cha Krishna. The meaning of this 7th verse is as follows: Only by his merciful strength was I able to vanquish all the lusty princes assembled at the palace of King Drupada for the selection of the bridegroom; with my bow and arrow, I could pierce the fish target and thereby gain the hand of Draupadi. Arjuna is remembering all the different incidents in the book of Draupadi in the past, in the association of the Lord, how he was specially empowered by Lord Krishna to do something, many things, very very extraordinary, even amazing to the demigods. So Srila Prabhupada explains this in the purport what is amazing about Arjuna winning the hand of Draupadi in that Swayamvara ceremony, Draupadi Swayamvara, how did Arjuna actually defeat the other princes, how was he able to pass the test which Drupada had actually arranged to select the proper bridegroom for Draupadi. So Srila Prabhupada explains that in the purport.

---
### Draupadi's beauty, Drupada's desire, Arjuna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/F_UAXG2kTQE?t=173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Drupada Maharaj decided to hand over his daughter to Arjuna only; this was Drupada's desire."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that Draupadi was the most beautiful princess, desired by many. Drupada specifically wanted Arjuna to marry her and contrived a plan to make it happen.

Srila Prabhupada says Draupadi was the most beautiful daughter of King Drupada, and when she was a young girl, almost all the princess desired her hand. The Kshatriyas, they want to marry the most beautiful princess of their time in their neighborhood. So it was well known in the different parts of this Bharat Varsha during the time of Draupadi that this daughter of Drupada, she was the most beautiful princess of her time. And when Drupada announced that she is going to be married to the prince who can actually successfully pass a test, then so many princess came. This was the time when Duryodhana was plotting to kill the Pandavas, and Duryodhana along with the support and the encouragement by his father Dhritarashtra, Duryodhana built a palace, separate palace for the Pandavas in another place, and that palace was made of combined combustible materials, shellac, and the Pandavas were told by Dhritarashtra, you don't have to share the palace in Hastinapura with the Kauravas, you go and stay separately in a beautiful palace that I have specially arranged to be built for you, and you stay there comfortably. Now Pandavas, they came to know through some secret messages, basically from Vidura, the Pandavas came to know that this was a plot to kill them by setting fire after they went to that new palace and in that way burn them alive. **Summary:** The speaker describes the difficult test set up by Drupada for Draupadi's Swayamvara: piercing the eye of a fish hanging behind a wheel, using only its reflection in a pot of water.

There was a fish hanging on the inner roof of the house under the protection of a wheel. There is a fish hanging on the inner roof of the house and that underneath the fish there was a wheel. The condition was that out of the princely order, the contending prince must be able to pierce the fishes eyes through the wheel of protection. The eye of the fish has to be pierced by the prince who shoots an arrow, that eye has to be pierced through the wheel and no one will be allowed to look up at the target. They can't directly look up and aim the arrow at the eye of the fish. On the ground there was a water pot in which the target and wheel were reflected. The target which is the eye of the fish and the wheel were reflected in the water pot and they can only look down into the reflection and one has to fix his aim towards the target by looking at the trembling water in the pot. The water is not steady, the target is steady, but the water is shaking is trembling and the water is not steady. The water is not steady, the water is not steady because the water is not steady. The water is not...

But Draupadi tactfully avoided Karna as the rival of Arjuna and she expressed her desires through her brother Drishtadyumna that she was unable to accept anyone who was less than a Kshatriya. So Karna was also there. Karna came forward, but Draupadi expressed through Drishtadyumna that she will not marry anybody who is less than a Kshatriya. Karna was known as the son of a carpenter or a shudra and in those days, the Vaishyas and the Shudras are considered to be less important than the Kshatriyas. So Draupadi... Draupadi avoided Karna by this method. Karna was known as the son of a shudra, so he was called a shudra. So Draupadi said, I don't want to marry a shudra or a Vaishya. I want to marry a Kshatriya.

### Arjuna, Brahmana, Swayamvara

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** Arjuna, disguised as a poor brahmana, was present at the Swayamvara. After all the Kshatriya princes failed, he stepped forward and successfully pierced the target, astonishing everyone.

When Arjuna, who was also present in that assembly, he was disguised as a poor brahmana. When the Pandavas escaped from that shellac house when it was set on fire, they actually came away to the forest and in the forest they were disguised because they were still not clear how they are going to reveal to everyone that they have escaped and how will they protect themselves against the attack of Duryodhana and the other Kauravas who are always thinking of how to kill the Pandavas. So how to protect themselves and how to reveal to the others that they are alive. So they were disguising themselves. So in this Swayamvara ceremony they came disguised as poor brahmanas. Arjuna was disguised as poor brahmana. So Arjuna, when none of the other princes were able to pierce the target, who were Kshatriyas, they all failed to pierce the target. It was not possible for them. Then, Arjuna came forward, though he was dressed as a poor brahmana, because a brahmana is superior to a Kshatriya. So he came forward and said, let me try, even though I am not a Kshatriya, let me try if I am permitted to. So, Drupada, Draupadi and Drishtadyumna all were very curious. Who is this brahmana who is going to try? Let us see. When everyone else has failed, whether this brahmana will succeed. Let us see. So he was allowed. Then Arjuna, actually in the dress of a poor brahmana, he pierced the difficult target and everyone was astonished.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.15.8 | 29-11-2022
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Pandavas, Krishna-Arjuna relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Wb3bLWXpbLU?t=68
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna had a wonderful friendship with Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the context of the chapter, focusing on the Pandavas' retirement, Arjuna's grief over Krishna's departure, and the deep friendship between Arjuna and Krishna.

In this session, we will be discussing the 8th verse of the 15th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter is entitled 'The Pandavas retire timely.' The events leading to the Pandavas retiring from their kingly duties are being described in this chapter, primarily because the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Krishna, wound up his pastimes on this earth planet and returned to his own personal abode. That was the reason why the Pandavas decided also to retire from their royal duties, and they handed over charge of ruling this earth planet to their grandson, Parikshita, and they themselves went on Mahaprasthana, as we described in this chapter. So here, the description so far has been how Yudhishthira is anxious to know why Arjuna has returned from Dwaraka and is very much aggrieved. Yudhishthira asked so many questions as to the cause for Arjuna's grief, and when Arjuna did not reply, Yudhishthira guessed that it is only because Krishna has left the planet and Arjuna is feeling the intense separation. He has become his dearest friend. So, Arjuna is now recollecting the different incidents in the presence of the Supreme Lord. How Arjuna had a very nice, wonderful companionship with the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Arjuna had a wonderful friendship with Krishna. So he is describing to Yudhishthira.

---
### Krishna's grace, Mahabharata, Devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Wb3bLWXpbLU?t=225
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavatam is summarizing those incidents to teach us a very important principle of devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment explains the meaning of the verse, highlighting Arjuna's achievements through Krishna's grace, including conquering Indra and saving Maya Danava, and emphasizes the importance of devotional service as taught by Srila Prabhupada.

The meaning of this verse is, because he was near me—this is Arjuna referring to Krishna—because Krishna was near Arjuna, it was possible for me to conquer with great dexterity the powerful king of heaven, Indra Deva, along with his demigod associates. And thus enabled the fire god to devastate the Khandava forest. And only by his grace was the demon named Maya saved from the blazing Khandava forest. And thus we could build our assembly house of wonderful architectural workmanship where all the princes assembled during the performance of Rajasuya Yajna and paid you tributes. This is an incident from the Mahabharata that is being cited by Arjuna. In Mahabharata, a lot of details are given, but Bhagavatam is summarizing those incidents to teach us a very important principle of devotional service. Srila Prabhupada very nicely elaborates and brings out the important principle of devotional service at the end of this purport. So the purport by Srila Prabhupada explains this particular verse very nicely.

---
### Maya Danava, Arjuna's compassion, Assembly house
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Wb3bLWXpbLU?t=352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna is such a wonderful devotee that when somebody asks him for help, he never refuses."
**Summary:** This segment details the story of Maya Danava seeking protection from Arjuna during the Khandava forest fire, Arjuna's compassionate nature in saving him, and Maya Danava's reciprocation by building a magnificent assembly house for the Pandavas.

The demon Maya Dhanava was an inhabitant of the forest Khandava. And when the Khandava forest was set on fire, he asked protection from Arjuna. Even though he is a powerful demon, when there was a danger to his life because the very place of his residence was completely ablaze with fire, then he, this Maya Dhanava, asked for protection from Arjuna. And what did Arjuna do? Arjuna is such a wonderful devotee that when somebody asks him for help, he never refuses. That is the quality of a devotee. Arjuna saved this Maya Dhanava's life, and as a result of his life being saved, this Maya Dhanava felt obliged to Arjuna. So what did Maya Dhanava do? He reciprocated by building a wonderful assembly house for the Pandavas. Now this assembly house attracted the extraordinary attention of all state princes. This Maya Dhanava was expert in architectural science, so he could build beautiful buildings. So under the direction of Krishna, Maya Dhanava actually built a very nice assembly house for the Pandavas. And this was such a nice assembly house, it attracted the extraordinary attention of all state princes. They felt the supernatural power of the Pandavas, and thus, without grudge, all of them submitted and paid tributes to the emperor. This was during the Rajasuya sacrifice that Yudhishthira actually performed after this assembly house was built.

---
### Yudhishthira, Krishna's desire, Rajasuya Yajna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Wb3bLWXpbLU?t=526
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It was actually Krishna's desire that a pure devotee rule the world and lead people on the path of devotional service to achieve the ultimate liberation."
**Summary:** This segment explains Yudhishthira's motivations for performing the Rajasuya Yajna, emphasizing his desire to glorify Krishna and Krishna's desire for a pure devotee to rule the world, leading people to devotional service and liberation.

Now Yudhishthira himself is a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Krishna. So he was not interested in the power of the Pandavas. He was interested in performing his Rajasuya Jnana for his own sake. Generally, a king declares himself as the emperor of the world, and he offers a challenge to those who do not want to be subordinate to him, that they fight and defeat the emperor or the would-be emperor. If they do not want to be under his subordination. So Yudhishthira was not very much interested in himself becoming the emperor. It was actually Krishna's desire that a pure devotee rule the world and lead people on the path of devotional service to achieve the ultimate liberation. Now, Yudhishthira himself, he wanted to glorify Krishna. And in order to glorify Krishna, he wanted an assembly of all the state princes. And when they assembled, in that assembly, Yudhishthira worshiped Krishna as the foremost among all the personalities in the entire creation, because Yudhishthira's yajna was attended to by even the devatas. So in the presence of all great kings and devatas and saintly persons, in that assembly, Yudhishthira wanted to glorify Krishna. So Yudhishthira would be the first appearant as the guest. So this was just a demonstration of the great Yudhishthira as Yudhishthira, the great king. So Yudhishthira, the great king is the first aware of this opportunity. One important event that took place is that Raja Suya ageyam. In the central palace of the Raja Suya Yajna Fort is alioor and knocked down this great temple, a true sign of great schedules, he taking his集 for us. by Arjuna, he constructed this wonderful assembly house which was used to perform the Rajasuya sacrifice.

---
### Demons, Material scientists, Divine vs. Demoniac nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Wb3bLWXpbLU?t=689
**Verse References:** BG 16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The modern demons are the harmful material scientists."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the nature of demons, comparing them to harmful material scientists who create disturbances in society with their inventions, contrasting them with those of divine nature who use their intelligence for constructive purposes.

Then Srila Prabhupada says, the demons possess wonderful and supernatural powers to create material wonders, but they are always disturbing elements to the society. That is a demoniac nature. There are people in this world, Dvabhuta Sarge Lokesmin, Daiva Asura Evachara. In the 16th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna explains there are two kinds of created beings: some with divine qualities and some with demoniac qualities. Daiva Asura. So these demons, they have some supernatural powers, supernatural powers to create material wonders, but they are disturbing elements because of their demoniac nature. That is why they are called demons. The modern demons are the harmful material scientists. In our age also now, there are demons. Who are the demons? The harmful material scientists, because they create some material wonders for disturbance in the society. And Prabhupada gives the example here. For example, the creation of nuclear weapons has caused some panic in human society. So much of nuclear weapons are there all over the world in different countries. So the intelligence of a demoniac natured person is always used for destructive purposes, and the intelligence of a divine natured person is used for constructive purposes. So there is a difference between the divine nature and the demoniac nature. Therefore Prabhupada says they create disturbance in society. Always they are disturbing the people in general.

---
### Maya's surrender, Devotee's mercy, Pleasing the devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Wb3bLWXpbLU?t=821
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is more important to please the devotee of the Lord, even more than trying to please the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Maya Danava, chased by fire and Krishna's Sudarshan Chakra, took shelter of Arjuna, highlighting the mercy of a devotee and the importance of pleasing the Lord's devotee, as the Lord is automatically pleased when His devotee is pleased.

So Maya was also a materialist like that, but he knew the art of creating wonderful things. And yet, when Lord Krishna wanted to kill him. So Maya Dhanava was chased both by the fire and by the wheel of Lord Krishna. Krishna wanted to eliminate this demon by using Sudarshan Chakra. And this Maya Dhanava was also in danger because of the fire in the Khandava forest, which was his place of residence. So he was chased both by the fire and the Sudarshan Chakra of Lord Krishna. Then what did Maya Dhanava do? He simply took shelter of Arjuna, Arjuna who is a great devotee. Now when Arjuna saw that Maya Dhanava's life is in danger, Arjuna saved him from the wrath of the fire of Lord Sri Krishna. So it is described devotees are more merciful than the Lord. So by the mercy of a devotee, even the Lord will pardon someone who is an offender. So when somebody is given shelter by a devotee, they are actually blessed by the Lord. They are actually benedicted by the Lord. So therefore it is more important to please the devotee of the Lord, pure devotee of the Lord, even more than trying to please the Lord. And the Lord is automatically pleased when we please his pure devotee. Asya Prasada Bhagavata Prasada. So this is the moral to be learnt from this incident. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Jarasandha, birth story, demons
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq9HxmlW0XA?t=146
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And thus, the child was surnamed Jarasandha or one who was joined by Jara, the she-demon."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Jarasandha's birth, including his father's desire for a son, the blessing of a Rishi, the two parts of the child, the she-demon Jara joining them, and how he got his name.

Prasurtyamantra was a very powerful king of Magada and the history of his birth and activities was also very interesting. His father, King Brihadratha was also a very prosperous and powerful king of Magada but he had no son although he married two daughters of the king of Kashi. Being disappointed in not getting son from either of the two queens the king along with his wives left Kashi. Being disappointed in not getting son from either of the two queens the king along with his wives left Kashi home to live in the forest for austerities but in the forest he was blessed by one great Rishi to have his son and that Rishi gave him one mango to be eaten by the Queen's the Queen's did so and were very soon pregnant the King was very happy to see the Queen's bearing children but when the right time approached the Queen's delivered one child in two parts one from each of the Queen's wombs the two parts were thrown in the forest where a great she demon used to live and she was glad to have some delicate alnya flesh and blood from the newly born child. Out of curiosity, she joined the two parts and the child became complete and regained life. The king was in a haste. He thought the queens have delivered two parts which are not complete. He thought they are useless. Both the queens have delivered something which is incomplete. So, they simply discarded the parts. But when the she demon joined the two parts, they became complete and regained life. The child became complete and regained life. Because the child was born due to the blessing of the rishi in the forest. The rishi blessed that the king have only one child through the two queens, one child. Becoming common, the class 4 queen came out. So therefore, the she demon was known as jara', that was her name who lived in that forest where the king had retired, and being compassionate on the childless king, she went to the king and presented him with the nice child, instead of She presented the king with the child. The king was very pleased with the she-demon and wanted to reward her according to her desire. The she-demon expressed her desire that the child be named after her. And thus, the child was surnamed Jarasandha or one who was joined by Jara, the she-demon. Sandha means to join. So, this child got the name Jarasandha.

---
### demoniac qualities, Lord Shiva, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq9HxmlW0XA?t=378
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, this child was born with demoniac qualities."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Jarasandha's demoniac nature from birth, his devotion to Lord Shiva, and how demons often worship Shiva for material benedictions, giving Ravana as another example.

So, it is also explained here that this Jarasandha was born as one of the children of the king. He was also one of the parts and parcels of the demon Viprachitti. So, he was also a demon, this Jarasandha, as a part and parcel of another big demon, Viprachitti. The saint by whose benedictions the queens bowed, the child was called Chandrakaushika, who foretold of the child before his father, Brihadratha. Now, this child, since he possessed demoniac qualities from birth, this is what the Bhagavad Gita explains, by birth, somebody is born with divine qualities, somebody else is born with demoniac qualities. So, this child was born with demoniac qualities. Naturally, he became a great devotee of Lord Shiva. Now, Shiva, who is Shiva? Shiva is the lord of all ghostly and demoniac men. Because Shiva is very easily pleased, these demons, they try to appease Shiva and get some material benedictions from him. And Shiva is very powerful. So, he bestows material benedictions very easily. He is easily pleased. So, therefore, you can find many demons are devotees of Lord Shiva. So, Srila Prabhupada gives the example here. Ravana was a great devotee of Lord Shiva and so also was King Jarasandha.

---
### sacrifice, Kamsa, Krishna, enemy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq9HxmlW0XA?t=502
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, Jarasandha became very angry against Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Jarasandha's practice of sacrificing kings to Lord Mahabhairava, his relation to Kamsa, and how Kamsa's death at the hands of Krishna made Jarasandha an enemy of Krishna.

Jarasandha also was a devotee of Lord Shiva. This Jarasandha used to sacrifice all the demons, all arrested kings before Lord Mahabhairava or Shiva. And by his military power, this Jarasandha defeated many small kings and arrested them to butcher before Mahabhairava. This is offering as a tribute to Mahabhairava, another form of Shiva. So, Srila Prabhupada also says here, that, there are many devotees of Lord Mahabhairava or Kalabhairava in the province of Bihar. This was called formerly Magadha. Jarasandha was a relative of Kamsa, the maternal uncle of Krishna. And therefore, after Kamsa's death, King Jarasandha became a great enemy of Krishna. Why? Because, Jarasandha's daughters were married to Kamsa. So, when Kamsa was killed by Krishna, Jarasandha's daughters became widows. After becoming widows, they went back to their father's house, Jarasandha's house. And Jarasandha was very unhappy that his daughters became widows, and he thought the cause of their becoming widows, is Krishna. He never considered that Kamsa was doing all kinds of atrocious activities. Kamsa harassed Vasudeva and Devaki so much. So, Krishna in order to actually give protection to Vasudeva and Devaki, who are devotees, he had to kill this Kamsa. So, Jarasandha became very angry against Krishna. So, Jarasandha became enemy.

---
### Krishna's plan, Bhima, Yudhishthira, tribute
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq9HxmlW0XA?t=652
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the real cause for all these happenings."
**Summary:** The speaker details Krishna's plan to have Bhima kill Jarasandha, the release of the imprisoned kings, and their subsequent tribute to Yudhishthira, emphasizing Krishna's role as the prime cause of these events.

Actually, Lord Krishna wanted to kill Jarasandha, but he also wanted that those who served as military men for Jarasandha might not be killed. Therefore, a plan was adopted to kill this Jarasandha by Lord Krishna. What is the plan? Krishna, Bhima and... Krishna, Bhima and... Vayu... So what is the plan? Krishna, Bhima and Arjuna Krishna and Bhima and Arjuna Krishna and Bhima and Arjuna They disguised themselves as poor brahmanas and went to Jarasandha and begged charity from him. Jarasandha never refused charity to any brahmana. So, when Krishna, Bhima and Arjuna asked Jarasandha for the facility of fighting him, to settle that Jarasandha would fight with Bhima only. So, all of them were guests and combatants of Jarasandha. So, Bhima and Jarasandha fought every day for several days. And Bhima became very disappointed that he could not kill Jarasandha, could not defeat or kill Jarasandha. Because both were very powerful. Then Krishna gave hints that, he gave hints to Bhima about Jarasandha being joined together as an infant. Two parts of a child were joined together. That's why Jarasandha was born. So, Bhima dissected him again and killed him. So, all the kings who were detained in the concentration camp to be offered as Bali in front of Mahabhairava, they were released by Bhima. Now, when the kings felt obliged to the Pandavas, they paid tribute to King Yudhishthira. In this way, Arjuna is recollecting how by Krishna's mercy, Bhima was able to kill Jarasandha. And because the kings who were arrested by Jarasandha were released by Bhima, after Bhima killed Jarasandha, so the released kings, they paid tributes to Yudhishthira in the Rajasuya Yajna. So, Krishna is the real cause for all these happenings. Though Krishna remains in the background, he is the prime cause for all these incidents. This is the way of the Lord's pastimes in this world. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### leadership, Vedic knowledge, governance

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts modern democracy with the responsible leadership of kings like Pariksit, who followed Vedic instructions, highlighting the flaws of laws made by imperfect people.

We have a system of democracy where we elect a particular person to be our leader and executive head and carry out the governance. But Srila Prabhupada says these great kings of the former times like Maharaj Pariksit, Maharaj Yudhishthira and such kings, they were very, very responsible because, why are they described as very responsible here? Because they obliged the great authorities by following their instructions left in Vedic literatures. They are great authorities. They are themselves first class leaders because they follow the proper authority and thereby they are able to give perfect guidance to the common people. There was no need for impractical fools to enact daily a new legislative bill and to conveniently alter it again and again to serve some purpose. See, this is important. In the modern system of administration, we see that the laws are made by imperfect people. So therefore, what is working for one time or one situation may not be working for another time with changing times. The laws have to be amended but Srila Prabhupada says the rules and regulations were already set forth by great sages like Manu, Yajnavalkya, Parashara and other liberated sages and the enactments were all suitable for all ages in all places. See the wonderful thing about the Dharma Shastras.

---
### Vedic knowledge, governance, ethics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SrpX4ROpiCY?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The state would get the best advice without expenditure."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the Dharma Shastras and the council of advisors in Vedic times, emphasizing their selfless service and equal treatment of all beings.

There is Parashara. There is Parashara Smriti. There is Manu Smriti. Yajnavalkya Smriti. And these are instructions given for different classes of human beings including the kings. How they should rule over the citizens? Perfect instructions because they are given by liberated sages. They are not conditioned. They are not having the defects of a conditioned soul. So when they give some advice or instruction or direction, they are perfect. They are good for all times, they are good for all places, all kinds of people. Therefore the rules and regulations were standard and without flaw or defect. No defect, no mistake, no limitation of time or place. Kings like Maharaj Pariksit had their council of advisors. And all the members of that council were either great sages or brahmanas of the first order. So who constituted this council of advisors of Parikshit Maharaj? All great sages, liberated souls. They did not accept any salary, nor had they any need for such salaries. These brahmanas, they were not paid by the king. Unlike modern day advisors, the IAS officers or the council of ministers, everyone is paid. Of course they need to take some salary, because they don't have any other means of subsistence. But in the former times, the brahmanas would give advice, they would not accept any salary. The state would get the best advice without expenditure. You see, this is the wonderful system in the Vedic times. The state would get the best advice because they are the topmost people, intelligent people, well versed in chastity. And they would not charge any fees. They would not take any salary. So without any expenditure, the state is getting the best advice. Now these kings, they were themselves samadarshi, equal to everyone, both man and animal. Sorry, the brahmanas. They were equal to both man and animal. This is important in the modern context. They would not advise the king to give. They would not give protection to man and instruct him to kill the poor animals. That is not the way to run the state administration. That human beings, they deserve protection. But animals, they can be sent to the slaughterhouse. Now, such council members were not fools or representatives to compose a fool's paradise.

No. There is no internal dwelling. They were all self-realized souls. And they knew perfectly well how all living being in this state would be happy, both in this life and the next. Both in this life and the next. People can be happy in this life, as well as in the next, in the future, also. **Summary:** The lecture begins with Sanskrit invocations and introduces the topic: Parikṣit Mahārāja's response to the onset of Kali Yuga as described in Srimad Bhagavatam 1.16.10.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyanan Janishalakaya Chaksharan Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pachatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadhadhara Shri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 10th verse of the 16th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Sūta Vāca Yadā Parikṣit Kuru Jāngale Avasat Kalim Praviṣṭam Nija Chakra Vartite Nishamya Vārtām Anathipriyāṃ Tatah Sharāsanam Samyuga Saundirādade. The meaning of this verse is Sūta Gosvāmī said while Mahārāja Parikṣit was residing in the capital of the Kuru Empire. The symptoms of the age of Kali began to infiltrate within the jurisdiction of his state. When he learned about this, he did not think the matter very palatable. This did, however, give him a chance to fight. He took up his bow and arrows and prepared himself for military activities. So, as the title of this chapter indicates, how Parikṣit received his military service. He received the age of Kali. So, the appearance of Parikṣit was at the end of Dwapar Yuga and the beginning of Kali Yuga.

**Summary:** The discussion focuses on the characteristics of Kali Yuga, its beginning after Krishna's departure, and how it influenced Yudhishthira Mahārāja to retire and hand over the kingdom to Parikṣit.

And the age of Kali is symptomized, as Srila Prabhupāda will explain in this purport, by so many sinful activities. Kali is called Adharma Bandhu, friend of irreligion. So, irreligion is prominent in Kali Yuga. So, therefore, this Kali Yuga began when Parikṣit Mahārāja was ruling. Actually, Kali Yuga began when Krishna departed for his personal abode after winding up his pastimes. But, still, as long as Krishna was there, sorry, Kali Yuga began when the battle of Kurukshetra ended. But, as long as Krishna was present, Kali Yuga could not exert its influence on the people. As soon as Krishna departed from this world, after winding up his pastimes, the influence of Kali began to affect the people of this earth planet. So, it is said here, Yudhishthira Mahārāja, he decided to retire from his kingly duties when he learnt about the disappearance of Lord Krishna. And he handed over to Krishna. And he handed over to Krishna. And he handed over to Krishna.

And he handed over the charge to Mahārāja Parikṣit to rule the entire world. Now, the description is being given that Parikṣit Mahārāja performed Ashwamedha Yajña and he was ruling. So, it is described here, while he was residing in his capital, the symptoms of the age of Kali began to infiltrate within the jurisdiction of his state. And he was not very happy about it, because the symptoms of the age of Kali are all indicative of people taking to sinful activities. Parikṣit being an ideal representative of the Supreme Lord, he protects his citizens from sinful activities, from engaging in sinful activities, from engaging in indulging in sinful activities, from engaging in indulging in sinful activities, from engaging in indulging in sinful activities. Because a king rules the kingdom, not only for providing the people with their material needs, or arranging for their material necessities, but more importantly, a king is responsible for people to understand the real ultimate goal of life as human beings, that is self-realization, that is self-realization. And he arranges for their preparation to go back home, back to Godhead, by having spiritual culture disseminated in the entire kingdom. So when Parikṣit learnt about the symptoms of the age of Kali infiltrating his kingdom, then he decided to deal with the matter by by by taking up his bow and arrows and prepared himself for military activities.

### ideal administration, symptoms of Kali, quarrel

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This section explains Parikṣit's perfect administration and how he planned to address the infiltration of Kali's symptoms, which are identified as illicit sex, meat-eating, intoxication, and gambling, leading to quarrel.

He decided to go and examine where exactly are these symptoms very prominent, in which part of the kingdom. So Śrīla Prabhupāda says in the purport, the state administration of Mahārāja Parīkṣit was so perfect that he was sitting in his capital peacefully. He was such an able administrator that all the subordinate kings were paying the taxes properly and carrying out their duties in their own respective smaller zones very nicely. And they had the full protection of Parīkṣit himself and his army. So everything was going on nicely, but he got the news that the symptoms of the age of Kali had already infiltrated into the jurisdiction of his state and he did not like this news. So Śrīla Prabhupāda now explains what are the symptoms of the age of Kali. This is very important for us to understand. We are all living in Kali Yuga. What are the symptoms of the age of Kali? They are four. Four symptoms of the age of Kali, particularly prominent. What are they? The first one is illicit connection with women. The second one is indulgence in meat eating. The third one is intoxication. And the fourth one is taking pleasure in gambling. Over and above this, it is mentioned here, the age of Kali literally means the age of quarrel. Kali means, it comes from the word Kalaha. Kalaha means quarrel.

---
### sinful activities, degradation, influence of Kali
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/O3d4aVn7Uyc?t=476
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The lecture connects the four symptoms of Kali to quarrels and explains how Parikṣit wanted to prevent these activities. It uses the example of changing vegetarian/non-vegetarian ratios in India to illustrate the increasing influence of Kali.

And the above mentioned four symptoms in human society are the root causes for all kinds of quarrel. You see how these four symptoms of the age of Kali are linked up to so many quarrels taking place in Kali Yuga over trifles. So Maharaj Parikshit heard that some of the people of the state had already taken to those symptoms. And he wanted to take immediate steps against such causes of unrest. That means, as long as people are not sinful with regard to these four sinful activities, particularly meat eating, intoxication, gambling, and gambling, intoxication, gambling, and illicit sex. As long as people don't take to these four sinful activities as a routine, it's not that in early ages, nobody was eating meat, no. Meat eating was under heavy restriction. And that also some real, real low-class people, they would indulge in meat eating or intoxication, gambling or illicit sex very, very, very, very restrictedly. So it is not prevalent in human society in a big way. Nowadays you see the symptoms of Kali that it is prevalent amongst so many people. Just to give you an idea how as Kali progresses, these sinful activities become more and more prominent among the mass of people. When India got independence, this is the statistics I am giving from some census data that was published recently. When India got independence in 1947, overall population of the whole country, the percentage of vegetarians was 75% and non-vegetarians was 25%. Sometime in 2010, the census was again done and they said, according to the census, I'll survey, the percentage of people who are non-vegetarians had jumped up to 75%. And percentage of people who are still vegetarians in 2010 was 25%. So you see, just tripled the number of non-vegetarians. So, this is the influence of the age of Kali, how the mass of people take to sinful activities, that means they unrestrictedly indulge in sinful activities. That's the meaning. Unrestrictedly they indulge in sinful activities. Unrestrictedly they indulge in sinful activities. Unrestrictedly they indulge in sinful activities.

### ideal king, public life, advertisements
