### Kali Yuga, suffering, Dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vSAp_aN0IGs?t=54
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So different questions raised by Dharma are indicative of the different ways people suffer in Kali Yuga due to this age."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces Srimad Bhagavatam 1.16.20, explaining Dharma's lament to Mother Earth about the suffering in Kali Yuga due to loss of religious principles, exploitation, lack of sacrifices, and famine.

So, this session we will be discussing part one, the first half of the purport of this verse, as Prabhupada explains this elaborately, it is a very important verse. So, the 20th verse Padair Nyunam Shochasi Maikapadam Atmanam Vavrushalair Bhokshamanam Aaho Suradeen Hruta Yajna Bhaagaan Praja Uthasvin Maghavatyavarshati. Further, here is a description of the conditions in this Kalyuga of people in general, in various ways they suffer, in so many ways. Okay. So, the meaning of this 20th verse is as follows, Dharma is speaking to Mother Earth, Dharma in the form of a bull is speaking, addressing Mother Earth in the form of a cow. So, what is Dharma telling the cow? I have lost my three legs. I have lost my three legs. I have lost my three legs. I have lost my three legs. I have lost my four legs. I have lost my five legs. I have lost my five legs. I have lost my six legs. I have lost my seven legs. So, if you have lost your six legs, you are now standing on one leg only. Are you lamenting for my state of existence? Or, are you in great anxiety because henceforward the unlawful meat eaters will exploit you? Or, are you in a sorry plight because the demigods are now bereft of their share of sacrificial offerings? Because no sacrifices are being performed of being performed at present or are you grieving for living beings because of their sufferings due to famine and drought. So different questions raised by Dharma are indicative of the different ways people suffer in Kali Yuga due to this age.

---

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Kali Yuga, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1fkCQwytcwM?t=49
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I have lost my three legs and I am now standing on one only. Are you lamenting?"
**Summary:** The speaker reviews the previous session and restates the translation of the verse under discussion, which focuses on the lamentation of the earth due to the decline of dharma in Kali Yuga.

In the previous session we discussed a small portion of the purport. Now we will continue to read the purport further because it is a very big purport. So just to refresh your memory about the verse itself, the translation of the verse I will read. I have lost my three legs and I am now standing on one only. Are you lamenting? Are you grieving for my state of existence? Or are you in great anxiety because henceforward the unlawful meat eaters will exploit you? Or are you in a sorry plight because the demigods are now bereft of their share of sacrificial offerings because no sacrifices are being performed at present? Or are you grieving for the living beings because of their sufferings due to famine and drought?

### atheism, Kali Yuga, false religion

**Verse References:** BG 18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the age of Kali, godless civilizations will create so many so-called religious societies in which the personality of godhead will be directly or indirectly defied."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the purport, explaining how godless civilizations in Kali Yuga create religious societies that defy the Personality of Godhead, Krishna, either directly through atheism or indirectly by accepting bogus incarnations.

So one by one we will discuss. In the purport which I have read already in the previous session, I will continue further. In the age of Kali, godless civilizations will create so many so-called religious societies in which the personality of godhead will be directly or indirectly defied. Basically people are godless. But externally they want to make a show of religion. Some people openly say I am atheistic, I don't believe in god. But there are other godless people who actually are faithless when it comes to god. But they will profess to follow some so-called religion. I don't believe Krishna is god. Then who do you think is god? Some bogus incarnation, I accept him as god. Because they want to deny or defy Krishna, Krishna's authority. And why will they accept somebody else as god? Because they are getting some sense gratification out of that person. Prabhupada gives a very nice example. In the Bhagavad Gita, in the 18th chapter it is mentioned, those in the mode of passion they worship and those in the mode of passion, human beings, who are actually bewildered by the material energy, influence of the material energy, illusory energy, they worship some powerful person as god. They don't worship real god. Krishna they won't worship. But they worship some powerful person as god.

So this is another class of people. They say there is God, but God is no form. God has no eyes, God has no hands, God has no legs, God has no ears, God has no hands, God is crippled, God is blind. This is their God. This is actually not the truth. God certainly has got a very, very, very beautiful transcendental form, indestructible. That form cannot be destroyed, doesn't deteriorate, has no material limitations. God's form is spiritual, transcendental. So when the scriptures say, God doesn't have a form, it is to be understood that God does not have a material form like I and you have. That's what the scriptures are describing. God as having no material form. Nirakar. Nirakar. Nirakar Brahman. Nirakar Brahman means Brahman who is completely transcendental, has no material eyes, has no material hands, but Brahman has got transcendental form, transcendental eyes, transcendental hands, transcendental legs, transcendental ears, transcendental body.

### interpretation, standard, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1fkCQwytcwM?t=623
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of physics and other sciences to illustrate that, unlike those fields with standard interpretations, people often believe they can interpret the Bhagavad Gita in their own way, which is incorrect.

This is godless civilization. Godless civilization. Only when it comes to understanding Bhagavad Gita, people will think, oh each person can have his or her own understanding. Do you say that for a physics, book of physics, book of chemistry, book of science, will you say? Oh you can have your own interpretation. You can have your own understanding. You can have your own meaning. Newton's laws of physics. Will each person interpret Newton's laws in his or her own way? In the physics field? No, no way. It is standard. Whichever university you go, whichever school you go, whichever college you go, whichever scholar you go, the scholar or the school or the college or the university cannot give their own definition of Newton's laws of motion in physics. Anywhere you go, it is the same. This is standard. Similarly, Bhagavad Gita, there is a standard. You cannot have your own interpretation or somebody else cannot have their own interpretation.

Krishna says, I am the source of everything. Everything emanates from me. And the symptoms of one who is in knowledge of me is that they are engaged in pure devotional service to me, knowing me as the ultimate cause of all causes. Clearly, this is told in the Bhagavad Gita, 10th chapter, 8th verse, aham sarvasya prabhava. Who is speaking? Krishna is speaking. The Supreme Personality of God is speaking. Bhagavan. Vacha. Bhagavan is telling, Aham. I. Person is speaking. And person is telling, I. But one other translator, he is translating, Oh, you should not worship the person Krishna. You should worship something inside Krishna. Where is it said and how is he understanding something else inside Krishna is there? As if Krishna's body is like our body? In our case, inside this body I am seated as spirit soul. You think Krishna also has got one material body and in that body Krishna has the spirit, Supreme Spirit is sitting there?

They will make the world uninhabitable for the saner section of people. There are some sensible people who know who is real God and they live according to some moral principles. They follow some religious principles, genuine religious principles that we should not tell lies. We should not hurt others. We should not kill the innocent animals. When you got plenty of supply of fruits and vegetables and grains, so there are saner people. Now for these saner people, the faithless, godless atheists make this world a hellish place. ### Kali Yuga, Dharma, Mother Earth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZA63aW6POnI?t=56
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Are you lamenting for my state of existence? Or are you in great anxiety because henceforward the unlawful meat eaters will exploit you?"
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of the session, which is a continuation of the discussion on the 20th verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Dharma in the form of a bull and Mother Earth in the form of a cow, and the symptoms of Kali Yuga.

In this session, we will continue to discuss from the 20th verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We already have discussed this verse in two previous sessions. So, this is part 3 of the same verse. The 3rd session will be discussing the remaining portion of the purport because it is a very big purport. Just to refresh your memory, this 20th verse: I have lost my three legs and I am now standing on one only. Are you lamenting for my state of existence? Or are you in great anxiety because henceforward the unlawful meat eaters will exploit you? Or are you in a sorry plight because the demigods are now bereft of their share of sacrificial life? Because no sacrifices are being performed at present? Or are you grieving for living beings because of their sufferings due to famine and drought? These are the various questions raised by Dharma in the form of a bull to Mother Earth in the form of a cow. So, this is actually describing the symptoms of the Kali Yuga. Of course, the Dharma in the form of a bull, the symbolic representation of Dharma in the form of a bull, and Mother Earth symbolically represented by the cow, form of a cow, shape of a cow. So, Srila Prabhupada describes these things in the purport in a great detail.

### faith, knowledge, blind faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZA63aW6POnI?t=174
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Real faith is completely based on perfect knowledge, is based on reality, is based on knowledge, is based on truth and facts."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the gradations of human beings in terms of faith in the Supreme Personality of Godhead, differentiating between real faith and blind faith, emphasizing that real faith is based on perfect knowledge and reality, using the example of knowing about China without having visited it.

There are gradations of human beings in terms of proportionate faith in the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Actually, Bhakti means 100% firm faith in Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, in real God. That is Bhakti. But this faith should not be mistaken to be something like blind faith. Now, what's the difference between real faith and blind faith? Is there a difference? Yes, there is a difference. What's the difference? Now, blind faith is not having the foundation of proper knowledge. Proper facts is not having the foundation of reality. That's called blind faith. Now, real faith is completely based on perfect knowledge, is based on reality, is based on knowledge, is based on truth and facts. For example, if I have not seen, let us say, I have not seen China, I never been to China, I never seen China, the country called China. But then, my faith that there is a correct knowledge, there is another country called China, people are living there, and it is another country as much as our present day India is a country. It's one of the many countries in the world. And they have their own culture, they have their own kind of living conditions, they have their mountains, they have their plains, they have their lands, they have their rivers, they grow some different food items like we grow. So, this faith is not blind faith. Why? Because it is based on the solid foundation of knowledge, proper knowledge from authoritative sources have gathered this fact that there is another country called China, even though I have not seen it, I have not actually visited that place. I can visit if I want to, but I don't have to necessarily visit that place in order to actually know for certain and be 100% sure that there is a country called China. You understand? So this understanding and the firm faith is based on solid foundation of perfect knowledge. So I don't have any reason to doubt, though I have not seen the place called China, I have no reason to doubt that there is a place called China which is really existing.

---
### Krishna, spiritual form, authorized sources
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZA63aW6POnI?t=398

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains how a devotee can have faith in Krishna even without seeing Him with material eyes, emphasizing that Krishna has a transcendental form and that knowledge about Him comes from authorized scriptures and spiritual teachers.

Similarly with God Krishna, now a devotee of Krishna knows Krishna actually is real, is the real person, is the Supreme Lord. How does he know? How does he have that faith even though he may not have seen Krishna in the sense of seeing with these eyes a person that we know in this world? It's not like that. Seeing Krishna is not possible to see with these eyes because Krishna is not a person who has got a material form. Krishna has no material form. Krishna has only transcendental form, spiritual form. These eyes cannot see spiritual form. Therefore, we have to know about Krishna, we have to hear from the perfect authority, perfect source, and that perfect source are the authentic scriptures like Srimad Bhagavatam, Srimad Bhagavad Gita, the authorized devotees, the Acharyas, Ramanujacharya, Madhvacharya, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Srila Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. All these are authorized perfectly perfect sources, authorized sources for hearing about Krishna, knowing about Krishna, establishing that firm faith simply by hearing Prabhupada and practicing Krishna consciousness. Of course, the hearing leads to the practice, leads to a certain transformation of heart which is what establishes that firm faith. So try to understand the difference between Shraddha which is a Sanskrit word for real faith and ordinary belief systems.

### transcendental faith, Varnashrama, gradations of faith

**Key Quote:** "As first class faithful men are the Vaishnavas and the Brahmanas. Qualified ones."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the session by explaining that the real faith Prabhupada refers to is transcendental faith, unrelated to material sentiments, beliefs, knowledge, experience, or relationships, and discusses the gradations of faith in the Varnashrama society.

So this is explained by Prabhupada here. As first class faithful men are the Vaishnavas and the Brahmanas. Qualified ones. The second class faith are those qualified Kshatriyas. The third class faith are those for qualified Vaishyas. And the fourth class faith are the qualified Shudras. So this is the proportionate faith in the Supreme Personality of Godhead in the gradations of human beings in the Varnashrama society. So I'll stop here. We'll continue this in the next session. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the nature of real faith (Shraddha) as a transformation of the heart through purification via devotional service, contrasting it with superficial belief. He then discusses the degradation of human instinct in Kali Yuga due to sinful activities, particularly meat-eating, intoxication, gambling, and illicit sex.

So till one actually has got that Shraddha, the real faith in Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, there is no question of Bhakti at all. So what passes off as Bhakti or devotion is some sentiment, some blind faith, some belief system. Like sometimes people ask, do you believe in God? One person may ask another person. The other person may say, yeah, yeah, I believe in God. Somebody else may say, I don't believe in God. So this belief system is not what we are talking about here. When you say faith, this is different. This is completely, it is coming from the core of the heart. It's a transformation of the heart by purification through the authorized process of executing Bhakti, devotional service. So it is not just a mental state or it's not an intellectual exercise. It is actually a transformation of the heart by purification. Heart means here we are talking of consciousness, which is a symptom of the soul, conscious symptom of the soul. So let me continue the purport. The degradation of the human instinct begins from the mlechas and the Chandala state of life is the last word in human degradation. Why is Prabhupada telling this? He's telling in Kali Yuga, the human beings degrade, become degraded in their character, in their consciousness due to the evil influence of the age, the Kali Yuga. And what is the evil influence of Kali Yuga? There is widespread sinful activities, particularly four kinds of sinful activities characterizing this Kali Yuga: meat eating, intoxication, gambling, illicit sex. So these four sinful activities are prominent in Kali Yuga.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

He explains that a Mlecha, someone who doesn't follow Vedic principles, can transform into a devotee through spiritual education and association with transcendentalists.

And if somebody practices bhakti, then their qualifications can change. Just like a child is born ignorant, but you send the child to school and the child gets trained in mathematics, then it becomes knowledgeable in mathematics. So it's no longer ignorant of mathematics. So ignorant person becomes a knowledgeable person by training, by education, by culture. Same way, a mlecha means a person who is not following a Maisha who is following the Maekas, but they call such a religious the Vedic principles uncultured, uncivilized according to the Vedic standard, not having cleanliness, not having good habits, not does not know how to show respect to elders. Such a person by training, by education, spiritual education, by association with transcendentalists can become completely a changed person, can become a nice devotee, can become a wise no ma, can become a first-class human being possible. So it's not that permanently somebody is labeled as malecha Sudra. No, never. That is not the orderly medical system that is the principle of a person. This is the principle of spiritual education authorized Vedic tradition or Vedic culture.

Your forefathers are first class qualified Brahmin because of which you have a good possibility of getting trained up in first class behavior, first class qualities. Yes. ### varnashrama, qualification, association, bhakti

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker reiterates that designations are based on qualification and effort, not birthright. He gives examples of how the son of a Vaishnava can become a Mlecha through bad association, while the son of a Chandala can become a first-class Vaishnava through association and devotion to Krishna.

There is no question of birthright or community. No. That is not the way. One can acquire the respective number of birthrights by birthright. One can acquire the respective number of birthrights by qualification by one's own efforts and thus the son of a Vaishnava can become a Mleksha born in a nice Vaishnava family but by bad association by not following the principles of Vaishnava conduct one becomes a Mleksha or the son of a Chandala can become more than a Brahmana can become first-class Vaishnava all in terms of their association and intimate relation with the Supreme Lord. Vaishnava means one who connects with Krishna through the process of bhakti devotional service bhakti yoga and then when the connection is established by qualification by practice by association by behavior by culture then even though a person by birth was not having any good qualities or any good behavior can become qualified can exhibit all the good qualities by training, by culture, by association develop that relationship and become a first-class Vaishnava.

### choice, regulation, diet, kali yuga, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QaGIkXMZFUo?t=908
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is consideration of what a person should eat. Every living being by arrangement, higher arrangement has got designated food."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the common justification that eating is a matter of personal choice, arguing that there are considerations of what one should eat. He explains that every living being has designated food by higher arrangement, and that while humans have some freedom, it is restricted to vegetables, fruits, grains, dahl, and cow's milk. He reiterates the scriptural restrictions for meat eating and concludes that people in Kali Yuga invite suffering by ignoring these restrictions.

Thank you they are very good but just to have proper guidance, they say it is my choice which kind of flesh I eat, eating is my choice. You like to eat vegetables, you eat vegetables. I like to eat them vegetables and some meat also or somebody else likes to eat fish. Somebody else likes to eat eggs. Astrology. So whatever one's choice one may eat, this is not correct understanding. There is consideration of what a person should eat. Every living being by arrangement, higher arrangement has got designated food. Just like a cow will never eat any flesh. A tiger will never eat grass. See that designated food for tiger is the flesh of certain animals. Designated food for cow is grass. So this is by higher arrangement in nature. But among human beings there is no such thing. There is a certain freedom given that you may eat different kinds of edibles. But that is restricted to vegetables, fruits, grains, dahl, cow's milk like this. It is restricted to that for human beings. Designated food for human beings. Now if somebody thinks that I will eat what I like to eat or I will eat what is good for my health. Somebody may say for you vegetable may be okay, for me vegetable is not enough to be healthy, to develop good strength. I require to eat some non-vegetarian food. So that may be some consideration some people may have. But if you have to eat meat there is restriction by which if you follow this restriction only goat flesh, only once a month, only after sacrificing the goat to Kali. Only to Kali, not to any Devata, not to any, not to Krishna, not to Vishnu, no. And it must be sacrificed. There is a procedure for sacrificing according to Shastra. ## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.16.20 part 4 | 27-01-2023
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Kali Yuga, sinful activities, sacrifice (yajna)
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9D30TKdo_xk?t=48
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, therefore, those who are engaged in sense enjoyment, material sense enjoyment, which is majority of the population, they have to perform yajna in order to become free from sinful reactions."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the degradation in Kali Yuga due to godlessness and faithlessness, emphasizing the importance of performing yajna to become free from sinful reactions arising from sense enjoyment.

In this session, we will continue to discuss from the 20th verse of the 16th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is the fifth part of the discussion on this verse from the purport. This is a very big purport. So we were discussing about the degradation in Kali Yuga due to people becoming godless and they become faithless, and they are unrestricted in meat eating, and they don't follow the Vedic principles because they think they can't believe or they are superstitious, various reasons they get like that. Prabhupada says that people in this age, they suffer in Kali Yuga because they don't perform any sacrifice, yajna. So, Prabhupada says the Mleccha population will care very little for performance sacrifices, although performance of sacrifice is essential for persons who are materially engaged in sense enjoyment. What is the reason for all suffering in this material world? It is our sinful activities. What are sinful activities? For the sake of satisfying my senses, I don't care for any restrictions or regulations or rules that are actually enforced in this material world. I don't care for them, or either I'm ignorant, or even if I have knowledge, I don't bother about it. So, therefore, those who are engaged in sense enjoyment, material sense enjoyment, which is majority of the population, they have to perform yajna in order to become free from sinful reactions.

---
### karma, sacrifice (yajna), Varnashrama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9D30TKdo_xk?t=192
**Verse References:** BG 3.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to avoid suffering, you want to get free from the future reactions, then now you stop all sinful activities, follow the injunctions of the scriptures, and perform work as yajna."
**Summary:** This segment explains that performing work as a sacrifice, as directed in scriptures, prevents sinful reactions, and introduces the concept of Varnashrama as a system for civilized human beings to maintain themselves and earn their livelihood while following scriptural restrictions.

So, in the Bhagavad Gita, it is said, if work is done as sacrifice, as it is directed in the authentic scriptures, then you don't become bound up by sinful reactions. You don't become bound up by reactions. Otherwise, those who don't perform yajna, but keep on doing yajna, you don't become bound up by sinful reactions. You don't become bound up by yajna. If a mortal body and one plant tries to stop their yajna, it needs to do something or another or he should not do something involving it. So the eternal behavior, to be a material intention I am trying to send to you, the ultimate, to be the ultimate Thinking and religion is somewhere where he is doing some work for their different purposes. They become bound up more and more and more and more and get entangled to material existence and are forced to suffer against their wish. Suffering material reaction. It is forced on somebody who is not performing yajna. So, Bhagavad Gita says, you want to avoid suffering, you want to get free from the future reactions, then now you stop all sinful activities, follow the injunctions of the scriptures, and perform work as yajna. So, Srila Prabhupada says that this world is created in such a way that for maintaining the living beings, there is the system of working according to one's guna and karma. Guna, karma, Varnashrama. Varnashrama is the way of classifying the civilized human beings into four social orders, and four spiritual orders. And thereby they do the appropriate kind of work for maintaining them off earning their livelihood. And, they also follow the restrictions of the scriptures in the work they do.

---
### scriptural guidance, purification, vegetarianism, sacrifice (yajna)
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9D30TKdo_xk?t=339
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotees always offer food for sacrifice called naivedya, offering to Krishna and then partaking the remnants as prasadam, Krishna prasadam. So by eating Krishna prasadam, you are purified of the old reactions."
**Summary:** This segment explains how scriptures guide all aspects of life, leading to purification and freedom from sinful reactions, and emphasizes the importance of vegetarianism and offering food to Krishna as yajna, consuming the remnants as prasadam for purification.

What they eat, how they dress, how they interact with other fellow human beings and what is their ultimate goal, what should be their ultimate goal in life. Everything is directed, is regulated, is instructed in the scriptures. So then they become free from the sinful reactions of the past. They don't accumulate any fresh reactions and they become purified. For example, we have to eat to survive, but eat only vegetarian food. That also, after preparing the vegetarian food, it has to be offered. Offered to Krishna as yajna, sacrifice. And then yajna shishta asinaha santaha. Devotees always offer food for sacrifice called naivedya. Offering to Krishna and then partaking the remnants as prasadam, Krishna prasadam. So by eating Krishna prasadam, you are purified of the old reactions. And you don't get any fresh reactions. Rather you develop your bhakti. So this is the way of performing yajna in the matter of eating, worshipping Krishna and offering him foodstuff which we have cooked, only vegetarian foodstuff. And that also cooked without any contamination in a pure way, cooking in a pure way. So that is now a very important point.

### sacrifice (yajna), karma, environmental balance, animal welfare
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9D30TKdo_xk?t=450
**Verse References:** BG 3.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But people must know that there is severe sinful reactions for cow killing. For unnecessarily killing even a chicken, unnecessarily killing. When there is plenty of food grains, after all the same grains they are using to feed the livestock. Why don't you eat the grain?"
**Summary:** This segment connects sacrifice to proper rainfall and agricultural production, contrasting it with the Mleccha's misuse of resources by feeding grains to livestock and then slaughtering them, highlighting the sinful reactions of cow killing and the importance of spiritual culture.

It is said that there will be sufficient grains, agricultural produce, if there are proper rains. And proper rainfall is dependent on performing sacrifice. So if people perform sacrifice, there will be cause. It says parjanyad annasambhavaha in the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna says this. Parjanya means rain. Yajna bhavati parjanya. Rainfall, proper rainfall, not too much, not too little. Proper rainfall is possible only by performance of yajna. If people perform yajna, there will be proper rainfall. If there is proper rainfall, there will be proper production of grains, vegetables, fruits, etc. And then there will be plenty of grains, food, all edibles for the human beings and the animals also. So the mlechas, they don't grow proper food grains. Or if they grow food grains, they use that to feed the livestock. They feed the cows. They feed the chickens. And then they slaughter them for their own food. They eat chicken flesh or cow flesh. Instead of growing some grains and vegetables and themselves eating and also leaving the remnants for the other creatures like chickens and cows, instead of that, they misuse their superior position in society. The dumb animals, the innocent animals, they are killed unnecessarily. So this is the anomaly in Kalyuga. But people must know that there is severe sinful reactions for cow killing. For unnecessarily killing even a chicken, unnecessarily killing. When there is plenty of food grains, after all the same grains they are using to feed the livestock. Why don't you eat the grain? Instead of feeding the chicken or the cow and then killing that poor animal and then eating the flesh. Why do you do this? So therefore, by spiritual culture, by spiritual education by spiritual training a person can actually change his or her life and become peaceful become happy become free from sinful reaction become free from suffering and have a very bright future all that is possible.

### bhakti yoga, sacrifice (yajna), chanting, prasadam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9D30TKdo_xk?t=652
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, that is what is being recommended here by Prabhupada that Lord Chaitanya, he recommended only one type of Yagna, that is Sankirtanayanya, chanting Hare Krishna, hearing the Bhagavatam, offering nice vegetarian foodstuff to Krishna and partaking the remnants, Krishna Prasadam."
**Summary:** This segment explains that sacrifice means pleasing Vishnu through bhakti yoga, particularly chanting and hearing the holy name of Krishna, worshipping Krishna, and that Lord Chaitanya recommended Sankirtanayanya, which includes chanting Hare Krishna, hearing the Bhagavatam, and offering vegetarian food to Krishna.

So sacrifice means to please Vishnu and how to please Vishnu by practice of bhakti yoga the most feasible type of sacrifice packet india the the sacrifice of chanting and hearing the holy name of krishna the sacrifice of worshipping Krishna, Deity of Krishna or picture of the Deity of Krishna. All these methods of practicing Bhakti are called different varieties of sacrifices performed in Bhakti Yoga. Shravanam, Kirtanam, Smaranam, Padasevanam, all these are sacrifices performed by Bhakti Yogis. When we chant Hare Krishna, we are performing sacrifice to Vishnu called the Kirtana sacrifice, Sankirtana sacrifice, Sankirtanayanya. When we hear the Bhagavatam, we are performing Shravanam sacrifice, Shravanam sacrifice, hearing. Hearing what? About Vishnu, about Vishnu's devotees, about Vishnu's glorious activities like that. So, this is the sacrifice that is most recommended. It is simple, it is effective, it is very very powerful. And easily performed by anyone, any human being without any previous qualification. Therefore, that is what is being recommended here by Prabhupada that Lord Chaitanya, he recommended only one type of Yagna, that is Sankirtanayanya, chanting Hare Krishna, hearing the Bhagavatam, offering nice vegetarian foodstuff to Krishna and partaking the remnants, Krishna Prasadam.

---
### chanting, prasadam, purification, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9D30TKdo_xk?t=770
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This Krishna consciousness is the way for actually becoming free from all sinful activities, purifying our old sinful reactions and ushering in peace and prosperity for ourselves and everyone else."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the accessibility and benefits of chanting Hare Krishna and partaking of prasadam, explaining the process of offering food to Krishna and highlighting how Krishna consciousness leads to purification, freedom from sinful reactions, and peace and prosperity.

This kind of Sankirtanayanya will benefit everyone in society. It is feasible, it is practical, it is easy to perform. It does not require any qualification. But if instead of chanting Hare Krishna, somebody thinks I will chant Gayatri Mantra, that requires qualification. Only a qualified Brahmana can chant Gayatri Mantra and derive the spiritual benefit of chanting. If you chant Hare Krishna, you get the benefit regardless of what your qualification may be. It does not demand any qualification. Chanting Hare Krishna does not demand any qualification. So, therefore, please chant Hare Krishna. Do not chant any other mantra. And do not eat simply anything you like. Eat only vegetarian food after offering that to Krishna. How do you offer to Krishna? You just prepare without tasting and adding the proper ingredients, the vegetarian ingredients. And then that has to be the prepared portion. A portion of the prepared food has to be kept in a particular bowl or a plate in front of a picture or deity of Krishna. And then you pray to Krishna, please accept this offering. You can just say this in English or your own language. Better still, you pray to Krishna to accept the offering by chanting the Hare Krishna Mantra three times. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. Three times you chant. And then leave that for five minutes in front of the picture or the deity of Krishna. And after five minutes, you take it out and you mix it with whatever you have cooked. And then that becomes prasadam, remnants of what is offered to Krishna. That becomes prasadam. That prasadam you partake and you are completely purified by taking that prasadam and you are also freed from, you don't get any fresh sinful reactions. That is the way. So, this Krishna consciousness is the way for actually becoming free from all sinful activities, purifying our old sinful reactions and ushering in peace and prosperity for ourselves and everyone else. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### suffering, Kali Yuga, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ebbdi4RmtxA?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mother earth is feeling for the people of this earth planet who are suffering in this Kali Yuga in different ways. So, dharma is questioning mother earth."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 21st verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, where Dharma, in the form of a bull, questions Mother Earth, in the form of a cow, about the suffering in Kali Yuga.

In this session we will be discussing from the 21st verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this verse is, are you feeling compunction for the unhappy women and children who are left forlorn by unscrupulous persons, or are you unhappy because the goddess of learning is being handled by brahmanas addicted to acts against the principles of religion, or are you sorry to see that the brahmanas have taken shelter of administrative powers, their families, but do not respect brahmanical culture. So this verse is a continuation of dharma questioning mother earth. Dharma in the shape of a bull is asking mother earth in the shape of a cow. Three more questions are raised here. All these are symptoms of suffering of the people in Kali Yuga. Mother earth is feeling for the people of this earth planet who are suffering in this Kali Yuga in different ways. So, dharma is questioning mother earth.

---
### compunction, brahmanas, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ebbdi4RmtxA?t=162
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now, these brahmanas, if they don't have the knowledge, they don't have the knowledge to follow the principles of religion, the dharmic principles, then they become disqualified as brahmanas."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of 'compunction' and elaborates on the first two questions posed in the verse, focusing on the plight of women and children and the degradation of brahmanas who misuse knowledge and disregard religious principles.

Are you feeling compunction? What is the meaning of compunction? Compunction means are you feeling sad or sorry for the unhappy women and children who are left forlorn. Forlorn means they are completely deserted. They are left alone to suffer on their own by unscrupulous persons. Prabhupada will explain how this is happening in the purport, but I am just going through the verse itself. This is the first question. Then the next question being asked by dharma in the same verse, in these 21st verses. Or are you unhappy because the goddess of learning is being handled by brahmanas addicted to acts against the principles of religion? That means knowledge is in the hands of brahmanas. They learn and they disseminate that knowledge to the people in society. Now, these brahmanas, if they don't have the knowledge, they don't have the knowledge to follow the principles of religion, the dharmic principles, then they become disqualified as brahmanas. Then they are no longer able to properly either learn or properly disseminate or set a right example or teach people the right values, right principles. They are unable to do that.

---
### brahmanas, administration, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ebbdi4RmtxA?t=256
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These brahmanas, then knowledgeable or learned people, they work for the government, and they are not treated properly by the government. They are forced to commit sinful activities in spite of having knowledge because they are dependent for their livelihood upon the government's salary."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the third question, addressing the situation where brahmanas are employed by administrative bodies that do not respect brahmanical culture, forcing them to compromise their principles for livelihood.

We don't have qualified brahmanas. We have teachers, but they are not qualified brahmanas. They don't follow principles. Then the third question is, or are you sorry to see that the brahmanas have taken shelter of administrative families that do not respect brahmanical culture? These brahmanas, then knowledgeable or learned people, they work for the government. And they are not treated properly by the government. They are forced to commit sinful activities in spite of having knowledge because they are dependent for their livelihood upon the government's salary. So, they cannot say, I won't do something that they are asked to do. So, they will do it, even though they know it's wrong, it is bad, it is sinful, it is going to bring about lot of misery, suffering for the people. Still, they cannot avoid, they cannot refuse, they cannot instruct the administrators that this is not correct, what you are doing. They cannot do that. So, this is a sorry plight of the people in this Kali Yuga due to the degradation or the bad, evil influence of Kali.

---
### Kali Yuga, protection, women, children, brahmanas, cows
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ebbdi4RmtxA?t=337
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the age of Kali, the women and the children along with brahmanas and cows will be grossly neglected and left unprotected."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on the neglect of women, children, brahmanas, and cows in Kali Yuga, emphasizing the scriptural injunction that these five categories should be protected by the government and respective guardians.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, in the age of Kali, the women and the children along with brahmanas and cows will be grossly neglected and left unprotected. This is Kali Yuga symptom. In the scriptures it is said very clearly, in a civilized society five categories of people deserve to be protected by the government and by the respective guardians, respective leaders, or respective protectors. The women had to be protected by men, especially in the young age by the father, a girl has to be protected in the middle age by her husband, and in old age by her son or another guardian family guardian. So this is the protection that shastras say must be given to women. What happens if they are not given protection? Then they are exploited by unscrupulous ruffians, rogues, and uncultured mlechas, yavanas, gundas, drunkards, meat-eaters, womanizers, woman hunters, prostitute hunters. Women are exploited therefore women should be protected but in the age of Kali there is no protection. Children definitely require protection. Children should be protected by the state government and children should also be protected by the parents. In case the parents, the child doesn't have parents because it's an orphan, parents died or separated from parents then the state has to give protection to such children, orphans. So children require protection again they will be exploited, they will be uncared for, there will not be proper upbringing, there will be no proper development of the child for a good future etc.

---
### brahmanas, cows, protection, society

**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain why brahmanas and cows require special protection, highlighting the brahmanas' role in spiritual guidance and the cow's provision of nutritious milk essential for human well-being.

Now brahmanas, they require protection because they don't have time for earning their livelihoods. They don't have time for earning their livelihood by doing some work. They are supposed to be busy with worshipping God and learning the scriptures and disseminating that knowledge in society free of charge to everyone else who is ignorant or who doesn't have the aptitude to learn this by the system of gurukula. So brahmanas have got a very irresponsible task and brahmanas they are not strong enough to defend themselves against any attack. They have to be protected by the Kshatriyas government. Government has to give protection law and order situation has to be maintained by the government so brahmanas specially require protection for the welfare of the entire society. Cows cows require special protection because cow is the only animal that gives the human society what is called as cow's milk which is a miracle food which contains the aggregate of all nutritional values required for a human being it's required for our subsistence it's required for our development and growth and development of finer brain tissues to understand spiritual subject matter we discuss this many times earlier so cow should be specially protected so in Kali Yuga there is no protect proper protection for these four categories of persons they'd be grossly neglected and they left unprotected in this age of.

### Kali Yuga, women, protection, marriage, exploitation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ebbdi4RmtxA?t=597
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the lack of protection for women and children in Kali Yuga, particularly in Western countries, citing broken families and the dangers of women seeking independence outside of marriage.

Kali illicit connection with women will render many women and children uncared for. You can see this specially in the western countries in America. So many women, because somehow they get married or even if they are not married, they have their living partner. Then they have a child and the person who is supposed to be the father of the child that is born, he just walks out due to divorce or separation or broken family. Relationship, whatever, broken marriage. And then the child and the mother are left unprotected, uncared for. That's what is being mentioned here. And the numbers are increasing as the years go by. Numbers are increasing. And one more problem in this Kali is present age is circumstantial. The women will try to become into the relationship independent of the protection of marriage. Sorry, independent protection of men through marriage. They want to be independent. This independent is dangerous for the woman. Why? Because then she is prone to be exploited by rogues, ruffians. As long as they are married and there is a proper family unit where they are married, they are given proper protection, they are guided and they are cared for. Women will be exploited. They will be exploited. That's what we see everywhere around us now.

### brahmanas, education, morality, administrators, degradation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ebbdi4RmtxA?t=715
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker discusses the degradation of brahmanas who acquire modern education but neglect moral and religious principles, and the failure of secular administrators to provide proper guidance, leading to societal degradation.

The brahmanas, they are traditionally intelligent people who can pick up knowledge, who can learn, who can teach. But in Kali Yuga, they will acquire modern education, but as far as moral and religious principles are concerned, they don't care for that. They shall be most fallen in terms of following good moral principles. Very intelligent, very educated, very learned, but as far as morality is concerned, very, very poor, very bad. So, education, education and bad character don't go together, normally, but in Kali Yuga, they will run parallel. The person is very intelligent, very educated, very learned, but as for don't ask what is his character, character is worst character, because they don't care to follow the principles, moral principles, religious principles, they don't care to follow. And, what about the administrators? They call themselves a secular. Because of it, they don't follow any of the eternal Vedic principles. So, they say we don't believe. And, they have their own so-called system of administration. They don't follow the standard authorities, Manusamhita, etc. So, they have some concocted system of governance. And, they will actually not give protection to the genuine brahmanas, to followers of real dharma, because of which, there will be so much of degradation in society, because people don't get proper guidance from a qualified brahmana, they become degraded. The innocent people, the common people become degraded. And, society will actually become very, very, very, very fallen.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurunmeelitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. The 22nd verse is as follows: Kim Chhatra Bandhun Kalinopa Srishtan Rashtrani Vaatair Avaropitani Itas Tato Vasana Pana Vasaha Snana Javayon Mukha Jeevalokam.

### Kali Yuga, degradation, administration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4I_VHDnDhlI?t=116
**Verse References:** BG 4.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The administrators are being particularly influenced by the influence of this age of Kali."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the verse, focusing on the degraded condition of administrators and the general populace in the age of Kali, leading to disorder in state affairs.

The meaning of this verse is: The so-called administrators are now bewildered by the influence of this age of Kali and thus they have put all state affairs into disorder. Are you now lamenting this disorder? Now the general populace does not follow the rules and regulations for eating, sleeping, drinking, mating etc., and they are inclined to perform such any business, whether they are staying in their inner community that they have been advised by God, how to behave in this global and they have too much to stay in them, to live anyway. How can one save himself? How to take care of one's life, every life in the world? Cow because mother earth was very much agreed and dharma is trying to find the cause for her grief, her condition so different questions are being raised which are basically indicative of the degraded condition of the Kali Yuga, the present age. So here the administrators are being particularly mentioned as biblical by the influence of this age of Kali. What are the influence of age of Kali? Adharma Bandhu. Kali is described as Adharma Bandhu, friend of irreligion, so irreligion becomes prominent. Same thing in the Bhagavad Gita Krishna says, yada yadahi dharmasya gladi bhavati bharata dharmasya gladi means the religion is going down and down and down people are not following religious principles abhyutthanam adharmasya religion becomes very prominent and that is exactly what Kali does, makes irreligion very prominent. So the administrators they don't follow religious principles, they themselves are religious and the people they have to administer are also irreligious so all the State affairs are in disorder. There is no proper law and order situation prevailing, which was much better in the earlier ages. There were very strong administrators, the kings, and they had excellent administration. So things were very much well maintained. But now, due to the influence of Dejav Kali, there is disorder in the state affairs.

### Krishna's presence, burden of earth, transcendental activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=50
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as Krishna was present, there was no possibility of any evil influence of any sort."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces and explains the 23rd verse of the 16th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the earth's grief after Krishna's departure and the absence of His transcendental activities.

In this session we will be discussing from two verses, the 23rd and the 24th verses of the 16th chapter of the Shri Advaitha Gadadhara. This is the 16th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. The 23rd verse first. Yadva Amba Te Bhuri Bharaavatara Krataavatarasya Hare Dharitri Antarhitasya Smarati Vishrishta Karmaani Nirvana Vilambitani. The meaning of this 23rd verse is O Mother Earth, the Supreme Personality of Godhead Hari incarnated Himself as Lord Shri Krishna just to unload Your heavy burden. All His activities here are transcendental and they cement the path of liberation. You are now bereft of His presence. You are probably now thinking of those activities and feeling sorry in their absence. So, one more reason for Mother Earth to be aggrieved, feeling that grief is because the Kali Yuga began after Krishna's disappearance, after Krishna wound up His pastimes on the earth. So, as long as Krishna was present, there was no possibility of any evil influence of any sort. And not only that, the Supreme Lord Himself incarnated to reduce the burden of the earth, which was overburdened by demoniac persons posing as kings. And as long as Krishna was present, His activities were all completely transcendental. And because of that, there was no possibility of any bad influence. So, in the absence of Krishna, those transcendental activities are not there anymore. And maybe, Dharma is asking Mother Earth, maybe you are feeling sorry in the absence of Krishna and His transcendental activities.

---
### liberation, devotional service, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=199
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The activities of the Lord, which are the essence of all activities of Bhakti, they are more relishable than the pleasure derived from liberation."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining that the pleasure derived from pure devotional service is far greater than the pleasure of liberation, making devotees uninterested in the four Purusharthas.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani, nirvana vilambitani. That is explained specially by Srila Prabhupada in the purport. What does it mean? In the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, the activities of the Lord include liberation, but they are more relishable than the pleasure derived from nirvana or liberation. This is a very important point. In bhakti, liberation is a secondary benefit or a secondary result of practicing Bhakti. In fact, those seriously practicing pure devotional service are not at all interested in liberation, are not interested in any of the four Purusharthas, not interested in dharma, artha, kama, moksha. Why? Because the activities of the Lord, which are the essence of all activities of Bhakti, they are more relishable than the pleasure derived from liberation. Millions and billions of times more relishable. The description is given in the Bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu, Nectar of Devotion, this book, written by Srila Rupa Goswami, describing the characteristics of pure devotional service. Srila Rupa Goswami says, quoting authentic Vedic references, he says, the pleasure derived from liberation is so very insignificant as compared to the pleasure derived from pure devotional service, that even one drop of pleasure of pure devotional service cannot be compared with an ocean of pleasure that may be derived from liberation. The two cannot be compared at all. That means, the pleasure derived from pure devotional service is billions upon billions of times greater than the pleasure of liberation. So therefore, devotees who are seriously practicing in knowledge the system of pure devotional service, they are not at all interested in liberation.

---
### nirvana vilambitani, devotional service, Krishna's activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=373
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by remembering such activities of the Lord, one can defy the pleasure derived from nirvana and reach the transcendental abode of the Lord to associate with Him and eternally engage in His blissful loving service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of 'nirvana vilambitani' according to previous acharyas, emphasizing that pure devotional service minimizes the value of liberation and that remembering Krishna's activities leads to eternal service in Krishnaloka.

So, according to Srila Jeeva Goswami and Vishwanath Chakraborty, two previous acharyas, the word used here is nirvana vilambitani. What's the meaning of this? These two terms? That which minimizes the value of liberation. Pure devotional service is such that it minimizes the value of liberation. It makes it insignificant. To attain nirvana, liberation, one has to undergo a severe type of tapasya or austerity. But the Lord is so great, so merciful, that He incarnates to diminish the burden of the earth. Krishna comes down, He definitely removes the burden of the earth. Simply by remembering such activities of the Lord, one can defy the pleasure derived from nirvana and reach the transcendental abode of the Lord to associate with Him and eternally engage in His blissful loving service. So Krishna comes to attract us by His activities. And if we become attracted, we straight go to Krishnaloka, after quoting this body, and there we are eternally engaged in the wonderful loving service of Krishna, which is many, many, many billions of times more relishable than the pleasure derived from nirvana.

---
### influence of time, earth's condition, Krishna's disappearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=465
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.24
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The powerful influence of time which conquers the most powerful."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces and explains the 24th verse, focusing on the deteriorated condition of the earth after Krishna's disappearance and attributing it to the powerful influence of time (kala).

The 24th verse, idam mamachakshva tavadhi moolam vasundhare yena vikarshitasi kalena vate balinam baliyasa surarchitam kim hrutam amba saubhagam. The meaning of this verse is, Mother, you are the reservoir of all riches. Please inform me of the root cause of your tribulations by which you have been reduced to such a weak state. I think that the powerful influence of time which conquers the most powerful might have forcibly taken away all your fortune which is adored even by the demigods. So dharma is making a reference to the condition of the earth when Krishna was present here. And the present condition of the earth after Krishna's disappearance. So there is a big difference in the two, between the two. So why has the earth been reduced to such a weak state after the disappearance of Krishna? So it is mentioned here in the verse itself, the powerful influence of time which conquers the most powerful. Kala.

---
### kala, earth's opulence, Krishna's will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=563
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By the will of the Lord, the whole earth can at once be changed."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how time deteriorates everything and how Krishna's presence enriches the earth, while His will can change the earth's condition instantly.

Kala deteriorates everything that is created in the material world. That's a fact. Including our own body and all the other things in the surrounding atmosphere. They go on gradually reducing in strength, reducing in potency, reducing in their vitality. So it goes on reducing, reducing, reducing, reducing, reducing. So that is very much experienced by us now. So Srila Prabhupada says, by the grace of the Lord, each and every planet is created fully equipped. Earth is fully equipped for maintenance of the inhabitants of this earth planet. But when the Lord comes, Krishna comes to this earth planet, the earth becomes enriched with so many different opulences that even the devatas who have much better conditions of living in the heaven as compared to earth, they become impressed by the extraordinary opulence on this earth planet. So Srila Prabhupada says, we should always remember that by the will of the Lord, the whole earth can at once be changed. Either the earth is lacking resources, then by Krishna's will, earth can become enriched to a great extent. Or if earth is having lot of opulences, then by the will of the Lord, all those opulences can be lost in a fraction of a moment.

---
### dependence on Krishna, material existence, independence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=697
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The problem with material existence is people think they are independent. While the truth is we are entirely dependent on the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes our complete dependence on Krishna in all conditions of existence and contrasts this with the foolish notion of independence prevalent in the material world.

So Srila Prabhupada says, therefore no one should consider himself to be self-sufficient or independent of the Lord. The problem with material existence is people think they are independent. While the truth is we are entirely dependent on the Supreme Lord. In all conditions of existence, in the spiritual world, in the material world, in the liberated state, in the conditioned state, any condition, we are completely dependent on the Lord. Fully dependent. And the Lord being the actual maintainer, protector, guardian, well-wisher of everyone, He happily maintains us. He doesn't have to exert himself, He is full of all opulences. So He does maintain us. But if we foolishly think why should I depend on Krishna? I want to be independent. This is exactly the mentality of people in this world, in this material world. That they somehow mistakenly think that they can be independent and they turn their face away from the Supreme Lord in the spiritual world. They are placed in the material world. And in the material world the laws work by which they don't get everything they really need very freely. They have to work and they have to get their needs according to the work they do and according to what they deserve and desire. Both are considered. Both are considered.

---
### material nature, Krishna's control, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RAcwjLTKUlQ?t=814
**Verse References:** BG 9.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This material nature is completely controlled by Krishna under His superintendence everything is working, everything is happening."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that material nature is controlled by Krishna, and the illusion of independence leads to suffering, emphasizing the importance of recognizing our dependence on the Lord.

And foolishly people think that this material nature is meant for our exploitation or can be brought under our control. No. From the Bhagavad Gita we get the information, the knowledge. mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛteḥ sūyate sa-carācaram. This material nature is completely controlled by Krishna under His superintendence everything is working, everything is happening. He's got many agents to maintain everything. He doesn't have to exert himself. He directs. So, material nature is not independent. This earth planet is not independent. The conditions on this earth planet are not simply because of some human endeavor or effort. No. So, it is completely dependent on the Lord. So, nobody should think I am self-sufficient. I am independent. Nobody should think like that. Because that's the truth. That's the reality. If they somehow think like that and live like that then under illusion they will actually have to suffer so many bad consequences of such a wrong thinking that I am independent. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Kṛṣṇa.

### dharma, mother earth, scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3FqXkXZLsK8?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Once you too were maintained by your four legs and you increased happiness all over the universe by the mercy of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses to be discussed (SB 1.16.25-30), focusing on the dialogue between Mother Earth (as a cow) and Dharma (as a bull), and reads verse 25 with its meaning.

In this session we will be discussing from 5 verses 25 to 30, actually 6 verses 25 to 30. So I will read these verses and then we will discuss little by little. This discussion will spread over several sessions; it is not possible to complete this in one session. So the 25th verse: Dharani Uvacha. Dharani Uvacha. Dharani here refers to mother earth. Mother earth is now going to reply to dharma. Mother earth is in the form of a cow and dharma is in the shape of a bull so the cow will reply to the bull. Dharani Uvacha: Bhavani Veda tatsarvam yaman dharma anuprichhsi chatur bhir-vartase yena paday lokasukha vahayi. Meaning of this verse is the earthly deity in the form of a cow thus replied to the personality of religious principles in the form of a bull. The reply was: O Dharma, whatever you have inquired from me shall be known to you. I shall try to reply to all those questions. Once you too were maintained by your four legs and you increased happiness all over the universe by the mercy of the Lord. This is the initial reply given by Mother Earth to Dharma.

---
### dharma, yugas, time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3FqXkXZLsK8?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Kali Yuga the religious principles are reduced to one-fourth means seventy-five percent is lost and this twenty-five percent also it will go on reducing reducing reducing as Kali Yuga progresses and towards the end of Kali Yuga devastation takes place because all the religious principles have been reduced to zero practically nobody follows anything so that is when there is devastation the destruction mass destruction"
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's purport is discussed, explaining that Dharma is established by the Lord and enforced by Yama. The discussion covers the four Yugas (Satya, Treta, Dwapara, Kali) and the gradual decline of Dharma in each age, leading to devastation at the end of Kali Yuga.

So Srila Prabhupada comments on this. He says in the purport: The principles of religion are laid down by the Lord himself, Dharmaṃ tu sākṣād bhagavat pranītam, and the executor of such laws is Dharma Rāja or Yama Rāja. Yama is a very powerful Devata. He is responsible for enforcing the laws of Dharma given by God. Such principles work fully in the age of Satya Yuga. There are four cycles of four ages that rotate like calendar months. So the first Yuga is called Satya Yuga and then Treta Yuga and then Dvapar Yuga and then Kala Yuga and after Kala Yuga again Satya Yuga begins. So in Satya Yuga, the first of the four ages, the principles of Dharma are fully followed by the people in general this earth planet. In the Treta Yuga they are reduced by a fraction of one-fourth. That means by the time Treta Yuga starts, Satya Yuga is over. Then the irreligion creeps in to the society and one-fourth of the religious principles are lost. In the Dwapar Yuga, the third Yuga, they are reduced to one-half. Irreligion has increased and religion keeps on decreasing. So irreligion has become half and religion is only the other half. And in Kali Yuga the reduced to one-fourth gradually diminishing to the zero point and then devastation takes place so Kali Yuga is the lowest of the four yugas but in Kali Yuga the religious principles are reduced to one-fourth means seventy-five percent is lost and this twenty-five percent also it will go on reducing reducing reducing as Kali Yuga progresses and towards the end of Kali Yuga devastation takes place because all the religious principles have been reduced to zero practically nobody follows anything so that is when there is devastation the destruction mass destruction.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.16.25 - 30 Part 2 | 01-02-2023

### invocation, introduction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In this session we continue to discuss from the verses 25 to 30 of the 16th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam."

### qualities of God, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=63
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nobody can estimate how many good qualities the Lord has."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of the Supreme Lord's unlimited good qualities, contrasting them with the lack of such qualities in Kali Yuga, and mentions that 40 of these qualities will be enumerated.

Now we heard how the Supreme Lord is full of wonderful qualities of which some 40 qualities have been listed by Mother Earth when she was questioned as to the cause for her grief in this Kali Yuga. So people in general lack these good qualities in Kali Yuga, whereas the Supreme Lord and Mother Earth, they always are having good qualities. So here the description is given by Srila Prabhupada in the purport that nobody can estimate how many good qualities the Lord has. So in these verses, 5 verses, 40 of the unlimited number of qualities the Lord has, 40 of the unlimited number of qualities the Lord has, are being enumerated.

### Vishnu's prowess, limitations of science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=150
**Verse References:** SB 2.7.40
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the scientist who might have counted the particles of the atoms of the universe cannot do so."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the impossibility of fully describing Vishnu's prowess, even for scientists who can count the atoms of the universe, referencing a verse from the Bhagavatam.

We will try to discuss some of them. So before that, it is described in another place in the Bhagavatam. It says in the 40th verse of the 7th chapter of the second canto of Bhagavatam, it is said, who can describe completely the prowess of Vishnu? Who can describe completely the prowess of Vishnu? By asking this question, it is understood that nobody can describe completely the prowess of Vishnu. Even the scientist who might have counted the particles of the atoms of the universe cannot do so. Is it a small thing? For the scientist the achievement is to have discovered the atom, the tiniest particle of matter. But even if a scientist can count by scientific advancement the particles of atoms in the whole universe, still such a scientist cannot actually estimate how many qualities the Supreme Lord has.

### Trivikrama, Vamandeva, prowess of Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=233
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is the prowess of Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Vamandeva (Trivikrama) tricking Bali Maharaj and expanding his leg to pierce the universe, illustrating the immeasurable prowess of Vishnu.

Now an example is given here that only in his form as Trivikrama, the Supreme Lord, he moved his leg effortlessly beyond the topmost planet Satyaloka up to the neutral state of the three modes of material nature. The Lord, as Vamandeva or Trivikrama, he tricked Bali Maharaj into giving him charity of three paces of land. And when he actually wanted to measure three paces of land, he said, according to my measurement. So it was agreed that according to this small boy's measurement it will be awarded. But actually the boy started growing and growing and growing and growing, and he extended his leg, one of his legs, to pierce the coverings of the universe and touch the shell of the universe and make a hole there by which the water from the causal ocean, the spiritual water, started coming to this world. So this is the prowess of Vishnu. One prowess of Vishnu being described here. So actually nobody can estimate how much, how many qualities wonderful qualities Vishnu has got.
