### forty qualities, truthfulness, cleanliness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=348
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Internal cleanliness is cleanliness of the consciousness, and that is possible by the appropriate method of chanting Hare Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to discuss the forty qualities of the Lord, focusing on truthfulness and cleanliness, emphasizing the importance of both external and internal cleanliness, with internal cleanliness achieved through chanting Hare Krishna.

Now these forty qualities mentioned here in these five verses, Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, the qualities will be divided into many subheadings. So I'll just read the qualities and then some of them are elaborated in the purport. We will discuss them in a little more detail. So these forty qualities that are listed here are to be found completely in the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna. The first quality is truthfulness, Satyam. Truthfulness is a virtue that is absolutely essential for everyone. Then only we can, we can be free from sinful reactions. Anybody who tells lies, they are punishable by the laws of God, which are always working. So one should not tell lies, one should tell the truth, and that is called truthfulness. People who follow this, this, being truthful, this quality, they are respected in society, they are held in great esteem like that, truthfulness. Second is cleanliness. Cleanliness means not only external cleanliness, which all of us know, we take a bath, we wash our clothes, we keep our surroundings clean, etc. Not only that external cleanliness, but also internal cleanliness. This is very very important. Internal cleanliness is cleanliness of the consciousness, and that is possible by the appropriate method of chanting Hare Krishna. We can cleanse the the the the the the heart by chanting Hare Krishna.

---
### intolerance of unhappiness, Daya, Jiva Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=494
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Daya refers to not able to tolerate another's unhappiness."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the third quality, 'Daya' or intolerance of another's unhappiness, explaining Srila Jiva Goswami's subdivision of this quality into protection of surrendered souls and well wishes for devotees.

And by executing pure devotional service, our heart becomes cleansed. Otherwise the heart is filled with so many material contamination by our long association with this material nature from time immemorial. So the third quality described here is intolerance of another's unhappiness. See very interesting. The Sanskrit for that third quality being mentioned in this list is Daya. Daya literally means one of the meanings is kindness, but here specifically Daya refers to not able to tolerate another's unhappiness, intolerance of another's unhappiness. So about Daya, Daya refers to not able to tolerate another's unhappiness. So about this quality, Srila Prabhupada is telling in the purport, quoting Srila Jiva Goswami, one of the previous Acharyas. Srila Jiva Goswami says that this quality can be subdivided into two divisions, subdivisions. The first one is protection of the surrendered souls, and the second one is well wishes for the devotees. Protection of the surrendered souls and well wishes for the devotees are two subdivisions of the third quality called intolerance of another's unhappiness.

---
### surrender, protection, well-wishing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=595
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna says don't fear, don't worry about some sinful reactions from the past, I will free you from all those reactions, you simply surrender to me."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the Lord's intolerance of suffering, explaining how He protects surrendered souls and acts as the topmost well-wisher, referencing Krishna's assurance in the Bhagavad Gita to protect those who surrender.

The Lord cannot tolerate our suffering. We are unhappy because of so many sinful reactions that we have to undergo, suffering. And the Lord is not happy about it. So how does he exercise his intolerance? He protects the surrendered souls. He protects the surrendered souls because of which they don't suffer the reactions in his material world. And second is well wishes for the devotees. Krishna is the topmost well wisher of all the devotees. Actually Krishna is well wisher of every living being, but particularly devotees of single doubt, those who are surrendered to Krishna, those who are engaged in in serving Krishna through devotion, they are particularly maintained by the Lord in such a way that the Lord acts as their topmost well wisher. So these two are indicative of the Lord's intolerance towards another's unhappiness. Now Srila Prabhupada explains why is it said protection of the surrendered souls? Why not protect the unsurrendered souls also? Why not protect everybody? Why is the Lord selective about whom he protects? Prabhupada explains elaborately in the purport. Prabhupada says to understand this we have to remember in the Bhagavad Gita the Lord states that he wants every soul to surrender unto him only, and he assures everyone that if one does so, Krishna will give protection from the reactions of all sins. Krishna says don't fear, don't worry about some sinful reactions from the past, I will free you from all those reactions, you simply surrender to me. You leave it to me. You surrender to me and leave it to me. I will take care. Take care means I will free you from all sinful reactions. So he gives assurance.

---
### surrender, independence, misconceptions about God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=742
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because they don't surrender and they want to be independent, Krishna does not interfere in their life."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why the Lord particularly protects surrendered souls, noting that many people in the material world do not surrender due to misconceptions about God and a desire for independence, leading to a lack of specific protection.

But there are people who are not surrendered to the Lord in this material world. In fact majority of people are not surrendered. They don't want to surrender to Krishna, either because they think themselves independent or they think God is another ordinary person of this world, or they think that they can compete with God. Even if he is powerful they can compete with him. They think God's position as powerful personality if at all they know something about God is a relative position. Just like we see people around us, somebody is more powerful than us, somebody is less powerful than us. So they think like that God may be another more powerful person than me, but I can eventually by working my way to become more powerful, I can become as powerful as God. I can become more powerful than him. So these are all some of the misconceptions because of which people don't surrender to Krishna, don't surrender to God. Then because they don't surrender and they want to be independent, Krishna does not interfere in their life, and therefore they are not particularly protected by the Lord.

---
### general protection, laws of karma, devotee maintenance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ywd495Suhz4?t=844
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotees Krishna maintains completely perfectly in all respects."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between the general protection Krishna provides to everyone and the complete maintenance He offers to devotees, explaining that non-devotees are provided for according to the laws of karma due to their desire to exploit and lord over others.

Though in general Krishna protects everybody. That means what is this general protection of everyone, even the unsurrendered people? It is that Krishna only provides for them for their maintenance, even in the material world. In the material world, in the spiritual world, everywhere Krishna is the only maintainer of everyone else. His maintenance is different of the devotees and non-devotees. Devotees Krishna maintains completely perfectly in all respects, 100% maintenance. All desires are fulfilled. They are completely blissful under shelter of Krishna directly. The living beings in the material world who are not surrendered to Krishna, they are provided for by the Lord according to certain laws of karma, laws of karma. Because why the laws of karma? Because they are not surrendered and they have a tendency to exploit others, to lord over others, to ill-treat others like that. So they are not provided like the devotees are provided. So this difference exists, and we will discuss in the next session how this difference comes about. We will discuss in the next session. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

These are a series of verses in which 40 different qualities of the Supreme Lord, the transcendental qualities of the Supreme Lord, are mentioned, listed by the earthly deity Mother Earth in the form of a cow while replying to the questions of dharma, the personality of religious principles in the form of a bull. ### transcendental qualities, unlimited nature of God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kwxD9Tz3e8w?t=139
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is not possible to count the number of transcendental qualities the Lord possesses."
**Summary:** The speaker refers to Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing the unlimited nature of the Lord's transcendental qualities, comparing them to the number of atoms in the universe to illustrate their infiniteness.

So Srila Prabhupada writes a very elaborate purport for these verses in the Srimad Bhagavatam. And we have already finished discussing in the past two sessions, part one and part two, some of the qualities described here, particularly the first point that Prabhupada makes in this purport to these set of verses. He says that it is not possible to count the number of transcendental qualities the Lord possesses. Even if one were to compare this number to the number of atoms, one can obtain by smashing the entire universe into small particles of dust. Still you cannot compare that number of atoms in the universe, total number of atoms, to the number of transcendental qualities possessed by the Lord. Which means that the number of transcendental qualities of the Lord are unlimited. Whereas the number of atoms in this universe is a finite number, is not unlimited. It is not unlimited.

### compassion, Paramatma, living entities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kwxD9Tz3e8w?t=345
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He cares for every one of us and his compassion is so much that he personally comes to this world in the form of three Vishnus and incarnates as three Vishnus."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the relationship between the Lord and living entities, emphasizing the Lord's compassion, which leads Him to incarnate and expand as Paramatma to guide and fulfill the desires of every living being.

So, since we are a very, very insignificant part of the Lord, we have some qualities of the Lord in a very, very minute quantity. So, we should remember, Lord is completely spiritual, we are also completely spiritual beings. But the Lord is unlimited personality and we are always limited. Unlimited transcendental qualities we have and he has those qualities to an unlimited extent. Just like the prayer we offer to Krishna, we say, Hey Krishna, Karuna, Sindho, you are an ocean of compassion. Now, comparison to the ocean means that he has got unlimited compassion. And what is the proof? That even though there are so many millions and billions and trillions of living entities who are almost completely forgetful of the Lord in this world, still the Lord out of compassion for each one of them, because they are insignificant. They are many and they are insignificant, but still, as far as Krishna is concerned, every living being, he says, is my part and parcel. Cannot be separated from me, Krishna says. So, he cares for every one of us and his compassion is so much that he personally comes to this world in the form of three Vishnus and incarnates as three Vishnus. The Mahavishnu, the Padmanabha Vishnu and the maintainer Vishnu. Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, you would have heard these three names. So, that maintainer Vishnu. That maintainer Vishnu expands as Paramatma and enters the heart of every living being. Why? Why does he do so?

### material desires, spiritual pleasure, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kwxD9Tz3e8w?t=495
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the material desires are the cause of our suffering."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Lord facilitates the desires of living beings, even those that lead to suffering, because of their stubborn insistence on material enjoyment, contrasting this with the spiritual pleasure and existence that is their true nature.

In order to actually direct and satisfy the desires and give the intelligence and make the living entity, the Jeeva, in a particular body for the Jeeva to live in that body and to utilize this bodily functions, the Lord is facilitating as Paramatma. So, that is his kindness. He fulfills the desires of the living being. Even though those desires are not really very conducive for the actual happiness of the living being, still, because we are very, very adamant, we are stubborn, we want to fulfilment of our material desires. Even though we are not meant to live in the material world, we are not meant to have material body, we are not meant for material enjoyment, we are not meant for that. Because we are spirit soul. So, we are meant for spiritual pleasure, we are meant for spiritual existence, we are meant for spiritual body. We are meant for spiritual happiness. And that is wonderful. That is very, very nice. That is everlasting. But anyway, as long as we desire and we are stubborn, we desire material enjoyment and we are stubborn about it, we don't want to give up. Even though, in all the scriptures it is said, if you give up these material desires, then immediately you become free from so much of suffering. All the material desires are the cause of our suffering. And the cause of our suffering and the cause of our material existence are the cause of so many problems and complications in our life. But still, if we are very stubborn, and we are about to give up all our catacombs, and we are about to give up our service, we must, we have to give up everything. That is, if I don't give up, they will come after me. If we don't give up the service, the service will not come after us. If we don't give up everything, the service will not come after us. That is the only way. So, we have to fear our universe sitting next to the particular soul who has got that body so he is actually fulfilling the desires.

### surrender, protection, material miseries

No discrimination but there are people who are unable to take advantage of the sunshine. Similarly Prabhupada says the Lord says that surrendering unto him is the guarantee for all protection from him he will give protection. You just surrender to him and he will guarantee all protection. But unfortunate persons are unable to accept this proposition. There are some people who won't surrender to the Lord. In the Bhagavad Gita in the seventh chapter it is described, there are four kinds of people who surrender to Krishna and there are four other kinds of people who don't surrender to Krishna. So the ones who don't surrender to Krishna, they don't get his personal protection. And therefore, because they don't have his personal protection, such people who don't surrender to Krishna suffer from all material miseries which are there in the material world. Material world means material existence means miseries.

### material enjoyment, spiritual world, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/kwxD9Tz3e8w?t=930
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure happiness is only in the spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that the desire for material enjoyment leads to suffering, while true happiness lies in the spiritual world, and that surrendering to God is the path to freedom from suffering.

Means they don't completely accept and for this the Lord is never to be blamed. We have foolishly come to this material world desiring material enjoyment. There is no material enjoyment in the spiritual world and we as spiritual beings, we belong to the spiritual world. But then we desire material enjoyment. Then we insist on that. We are stubborn about it. Then the Lord makes arrangement for us to come to this material world because that's material enjoyment is possible only in the material world. Now the material world, the characteristic is such that it is filled with miseries. There is no pure happiness in this material world. Pure happiness is only in the spiritual world. Spiritual world there is no distress. There is no unhappiness. There is no misery. There is no old age. There is no disease. There is no death. So that is spiritual world. So having come here, now that we may realize in the human form that I am suffering even though I don't want to suffer miseries. And we are also trying to avoid suffering. You have not been able to suffer this suffering in so many ways, our own ways but when one comes in touch with Shastra scriptures one understands that if we surrender to God then God will free us from this suffering but people don't surrender then they have to blame. They have to suffer. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### protection, devotion, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/R6BYi1xzKfs?t=185
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna took up one wheel of a chariot and rushed towards Bhishma to stop Bhishma from attacking Arjuna."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Krishna breaking his promise not to take up weapons during the Kurukshetra war to protect Arjuna from Bhishma, illustrating Krishna's commitment to his devotees.

All circumstances. There are many examples given. Just like Arjuna's life was in danger on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. And Krishna will protect Arjuna under all circumstances. So once it so happened that Bhishma was fighting on the side of Duryodhana, he was attacking Arjuna and Arjuna's life was in danger. Bhishma is much, much more powerful and expert in fighting than Arjuna, definitely. Even though Arjuna is a great warrior. But still, Bhishma is experienced. He is very, very expert. And he has fought many battles before. He is much, much, much, much more experienced than Arjuna. So Bhishma was fighting very, very, very valiantly. And very bravely, he was attacking Arjuna. Expertly shooting arrows. And it appeared like Arjuna's life was in danger. Arjuna was going to get completely destroyed. So Krishna, in order to protect Arjuna, he personally took up a weapon, a wheel of a chariot, and went to stop Bhishma from attacking Arjuna. Now, in doing so, Krishna, Krishna, Krishna had actually is breaking his promise. He had promised that he will not take any weapon. He will not fight in the battle. He will not take any weapon. Though he will drive Arjuna's chariot and give Arjuna some strategic directions, how to fight and how to conquer, etc. But Krishna said he will never fight. He will not take any weapons. As long as he is in the battlefield, he will never do that. So, but when Arjuna's life was in danger, Krishna took up one wheel of a chariot and rushed towards Bhishma, got down from the chariot of Arjuna, rushed towards Bhishma to stop Bhishma from attacking Arjuna. And what happened? Bhishma said, Krishna is coming to kill me. What better death can I have? So, Bhishma simply surrendered to Krishna. He stopped attacking Arjuna. He stopped. Arjuna's life was saved. For the moment, Arjuna's life was saved.

---
### surrender, duty, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/R6BYi1xzKfs?t=357
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is the duty of the pure devotee to be fixed in the discharge of the duty entrusted to him by the Lord or the Lord's bona fide representative, the spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story by explaining that Krishna did not attack Bhishma after he surrendered and emphasizes the importance of devotees fulfilling their duties as instructed by the Lord or their spiritual master, with the assurance of divine protection.

And after that, when Bhishma surrendered to Krishna, Krishna actually did not attack Bhishma at all. Krishna's purpose is served. Arjuna is saved. And Bhishma is also Krishna's devotee. So, when he surrenders, that means, he is not going to fight anymore. So, when Bhishma surrendered, seeing Krishna is coming to kill me, oh, what better opportunity for me to die than at the hands of the Supreme Lord. Bhishma is a devotee, he knows. So, Krishna never went ahead, did not go ahead to kill Bhishma. He himself also stopped. Because Kshatriya code of conduct is, if somebody surrenders, they don't attack, they don't kill. So, Bhishma is surrendering. So, Krishna said, okay, I am not going to attack you, I am not going to kill you. And then, after that, Bhishma never fought again. He said, enough fighting, enough fighting I have done. Now that I have surrendered, I am not going to fight anymore. So, Arjuna's life was saved. Arjuna's life was saved by Krishna, even at the cost of Krishna breaking his promise. So, when Prabhupada says here, where Krishna will never deviate from his word of honor, he will execute his promise under all circumstances. Therefore, Prabhupada says, it is the duty of the pure devotee to be fixed in the discharge of the duty entrusted to him by the Lord or the Lord's bona fide representative, the spiritual master. Guru or Krishna, their instruction we have to follow under all circumstances.

---
### responsibility, devotion, energies of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/R6BYi1xzKfs?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has no responsibility because all his work is done by his different appointed energies."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to discussing the unique nature of Krishna's responsibility, explaining that Krishna has no responsibility because all his work is carried out by his energies.

And we should be assured that while we are engaged in serving the Supreme Lord or the spiritual master, we have full protection of the Supreme Lord. And we have full guarantee of safety. Now, as far as the responsibility of the Lord is concerned, Srila Prabhupada says, that responsibility of the Lord is also unique. We all know what responsibility is. That means we have got certain duties and we have to carry out those duties as far as possible under all circumstances. We carry out the duties. Of course, we are tiny living beings, so sometimes it may be not possible to do our duty. Except such circumstances where we cannot do our duty, we will otherwise, a dutiful person will always, a responsible person will always do his or her duties. But when it comes to Krishna, whatever Krishna's responsibility, so Prabhupada says it is unique. What is the meaning of the uniqueness of Krishna's responsibility? Actually, Krishna has no responsibility. What does it mean? Prabhupada says, Krishna has no responsibility because all his work is done by his different appointed energies.

---
### energies of Krishna, pastimes, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/R6BYi1xzKfs?t=572
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Still, he accepts voluntary responsibilities in displaying different roles in his transcendental pastimes."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's energies and explains that despite not having any inherent responsibility, Krishna voluntarily engages in transcendental pastimes with his devotees.

He has many energies. parashya shaktir vividhaiva shruyate svabhaviki jnana balakriyacha. The statement in the Upanishads. So, Krishna has got many, many energies and those energies are simply carrying out whatever he desires to be done according to his will. They carry out whatever he desires. That should happen. So, he has nothing to do. Therefore, the Upanishads say, natasya karyam karanam ca vidyate. He has no responsibility. He has no duty. He has no work. So, does it mean he is inactive? If he has no work to do, he has no responsibility because all his responsibilities and duties are carried out by his energies? No. Still, he accepts voluntary responsibilities in displaying different roles in his transcendental pastimes. See, Krishna has nothing to do but doesn't mean he is inactive. He is active in performing his lila, his transcendental pastimes, divya lila. And those transcendental pastimes are always performed in his association with his pure devotees. And he accepts different roles in those pastimes.

---
### pastimes, duty, cow protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/R6BYi1xzKfs?t=669
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna in his transcendental pastime of cowherding in Vrindavan when he appeared 5000 years back, he used to take 108 groups of cows for cowherding after he grew up."
**Summary:** The speaker provides the example of Krishna as a cowherd boy in Vrindavan, highlighting his dutifulness in taking care of the cows and connecting the number 108 to the number of cow groups Krishna tended.

Prabhupada gives an example. As a boy in Vrindavan, he was playing the part of a cowboy, Gopalam. As a son of Nanda Maharaj, he discharged responsibility perfectly. He would dutifully go cowherding in the forest, take the cows for grazing, and he would go with his friends and he would bring back the cows after they finished grazing in the evening before sunset. So he would supervise the milking of the cows and he would ensure that the cows have all properly returned. He would count the groups of cows. There were 108 groups of cows Krishna was taking care of. It's very interesting, this number 108. We chant 108 times the Hare Krishna mantra in one mala, one round we say. The beads which we use for chanting japa, counting and chanting japa, there are 108 beads. So where this number 108 is coming from? One of the reasons this 108 number is fixed is because Krishna in his transcendental pastime of cowherding in Vrindavan when he appeared 5000 years back, he used to take 108 groups of cows for cowherding after he grew up. And when Krishna returns with those cows, he will count the number of groups that have come back when each group he calls and they come near Krishna and Krishna directs them to enter their particular that, what do you say, cow shelter, cow pen, the particular designated area where the cows will stay. So he would count the groups, 108 groups he used to take and ensure that all the groups of cows have come back. It was a huge number. So he would count. And for that Krishna had a string of 108 pearls, a mala made of 108 pearls and he would count on them how many have come back. So when all the 108 have been counted, then yes, all the cows have come back. So he would do, my point is that he would do his duty responsibly as a son of Nanda Maharaj, who was a cowherd, a king of the cowherds.

## Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | SB 1.16.25 - 30 Part 6 | 06-02-2023
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's beauty, Srivatsa, Madana Mohana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sqNlbW2jUWg?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So how much more beautiful Krishna should be to attract even Radharani, who is the most beautiful creation of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment describes the unique qualities of Krishna's beauty, including the Srivatsa mark and his ability to attract even Radharani and Madana.

We are discussing the various qualities of the Supreme Lord, the transcendental qualities, from Srimad Bhagavatam 1.16.25-30, part 6. Srila Prabhupada explains the quality of the Lord's beauty. As far as the beauty of the Lord is concerned, he has some special features that distinguish him from all other living beings. Krishna, when he appears in his original form, human-like form, Manushin Tanumashrit, it's not a human form, it's not a material form. It looks like a material form of the human being. So he has some special features by which you can recognize him as completely different from all the other humans. He has some special features, just like Krishna has got a mark on his chest called Srivatsa. Srivatsa. It's the hoof print of a calf. Srivatsa. Vatsa means calf, on the chest, which is the residing place of Sri, Lakshmi. Lakshmi is residing on the bosom of the Lord, the chest of the Lord. So he's also called Srinivasa. The Lord, Srivatsa. The Lord, Srivatsa. Srivatsa is the one who, that is the place where Srinivasa resides on his chest. So that place where Lakshmi resides, that place there is one mark called Srivatsa. That Srivatsa is only there on the chest of the Lord. Only the Lord has that marking. Nobody else can have that marking. So like this he has got some special features that distinguish him from everyone else. Over and above those special features which distinguish him from all other living beings, he has some special attractive beautiful features regarding his beauty, by which he attracts the mind of even Radharani, who is the supermost beautiful creation of the Lord. Radharani is very, very, very, very, very, very beautiful, most beautiful among everyone else other than the Lord. So she is most beautiful. And Krishna, he is so beautiful, he attracts even Radharani. So how much more beautiful Krishna should be to attract even Radharani, who is the most beautiful creation of the Lord. Therefore, he is known as Madana Mohana, one who attracts the mind of even Cupid, Madana. Madana is the most beautiful personality in this universe among the created beings of the material world. So Krishna attracts even Madana, Madana Mohana, he captivates the mind of Madana. So he is millions and billions of times more beautiful than Madana, that is described in the Brahma Samhita. So Krishna attracts even Madana, Madana Mohana, he is known as Madana Mohana.

---
### Transcendental qualities, Mystic powers, Jiva Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sqNlbW2jUWg?t=294
**Verse References:** BG 18.77
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the one who is the master of all mystic powers Yogeshwara."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Srila Jiva Goswami's analysis of Krishna's transcendental qualities, emphasizing his omnipotence, unlimited mystic powers (Yogeshwara), and the fact that he bestows mystic powers on others.

Now, Srila Jiva Goswami Prabhu, who is one of the previous Acharyas, he has scrutinizingly analyzed other transcendental qualities of the human being. He has studied the qualities of the Lord, and he says affirmatively that Lord Sri Krishna is the absolute supreme personality of God, Param Brahman. How do we ascertain somebody is God? Because he looks like another created being, but he is not. But how do we ascertain he is God? By his qualities. So, his analyzed the transcendental qualities displayed by the Lord when he incarnates, Srila Jiva Goswami Prabhu has concluded that the Lord is, Krishna is the Supreme Lord, absolute supreme personality of God. So, Srila Jiva Goswami explains that Omnipotent by his inconceivable energies. Omnipotent means he has got all kinds of powers, because he has so many energies. And he is the Yogeshwara or the supreme master of all mystic powers. There are so many mystic powers. But a yogi who is practicing Ashtanga Yoga can have only Ashta Siddhi, eight major mystic powers, eight minor mystic powers, and four auxiliary mystic powers. Elsewhere, Prabhupada explains. Totally, different types of mystic powers are twenty. Eight major, eight minor, and four subsidiary. So, Krishna has unlimited mystic powers. So, nobody else can have unlimited mystic powers. They can have some limited mystic powers. Eight at the most. Or some rare cases, ten. But Krishna has unlimited mystic powers. Not only that, he is the one who bestows mystic power on somebody who desires to have it. Devotees never want any mystic powers. Devotees don't want any favor from the Lord. They don't desire for it. They don't aspire for it. They never want it. So that is Lord's greatness. Krishna is the one who is the master of all mystic powers Yogeshwara. In the Bhagavad-gita it is said in the 18th chapter last words yatra Yogeshwara Krishna yatra partho dhanur dharah Krishna is addressed as Yogeshwara by Sanjaya Yogeshwara Krishna so he Krishna is the master of all mystic powers unlimited powers so because he is the Yogeshwara.

---
### Spiritual form, Ignorance, Transcendental knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sqNlbW2jUWg?t=518
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna only will dispel the darkness by giving them the enlightenment in transcendental knowledge."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts Krishna's eternal, spiritual form of eternity, bliss, and knowledge with our material form, which is filled with ignorance, and explains that only Krishna can dispel this ignorance through transcendental knowledge.

Prabhupada says his eternal form is spiritual, a combination of eternity, bliss, and knowledge. Our form is spiritual, a combination of eternity, bliss, and knowledge. Our material form, however a person may be learned, however intelligent he may be, but our form is basically a form of ignorance. Material form is filled with ignorance, and this ignorance even if we try to learn whole life, this ignorance cannot be eradicated by that acquisition of material knowledge. Material knowledge does not counteract the ignorance due to our accepting a material body. It's only by the supreme Lord's blessing that we get his mercy to eradicate the ignorance within our heart. When Krishna shines the divine knowledge, transcendental knowledge within our heart, he says this in the Bhagavad Gita. tesham eva anukampartham aham ajnanajam tamaha nashayami atmabhavastho jnana deepena bhāsvat. So, Krishna dispels the darkness of ignorance in every living entity who so desires. Krishna only will dispel the darkness by giving them the enlightenment in transcendental knowledge. So, I will...

### Dynamic knowledge, Krishna's beauty, Unlimited qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sqNlbW2jUWg?t=642
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "His beauty is... He looks ever fresh, and newer and newer beauties are every moment being displayed by him this is called dynamic quality of beauty dynamic ever increasing ever fresh beauty Krishna displays."
**Summary:** This segment describes Krishna's knowledge as dynamic and unfathomable, and his beauty as ever-fresh and ever-increasing, attracting the residents of Dwarka. It also emphasizes that living entities can never equal or excel the Lord's unlimited qualities.

I will continue to explain the nature of this knowledge that Krishna possesses. Krishna's knowledge is not static. It is of dynamic nature. The knowledge of the Lord is unfathomable. Nobody can estimate how much knowledge the Lord has got. And it is dynamic means according to the situation, time, place, circumstances, he can actually apply knowledge suitably in different situations. His knowledge is dynamic. His knowledge is dynamic. Dynamic knowledge. Dynamic knowledge. Now, regarding his beauty. Shri Lasuta Goswami says in the Bhagavatam that even though Krishna was observed by the citizens of Dwarka every day, they were always increasingly anxious to see him again and again. Shri Lasuta Goswami says... So, what is the nature of his beauty? His beauty is... He looks ever fresh, and newer and newer beauties are every moment being displayed by him this is called dynamic quality of beauty dynamic ever increasing ever fresh beauty Krishna displays so the residents of Dwaraka every day they would want to see Krishna's beauty and to be attracted more and more and more increased attraction increased appreciation of Krishna's beauty and they become there's no saturation also so this saturation in transcendental qualities of the Lord there's no saturation so even though those devotees who appreciate the Lord's beauty every day but they cannot attain by any amount of work the equality with the Lord in his beauty not possible that's not possible so none of the qualities even though the living it is put possessed in small quantity the qualities of the Lord they can never equal him or excel in those qualities by their efforts different endeavors that's never possible so the Lord is always the Lord eternally is the Lord unlimited qualities unlimited number of qualities and unlimited extent unlimited quantity of those each quality and the living entities have some of the qualities which the Lord has and they possess in very very very very very insignificant quantity but that insignificant quantity of whatever good qualities we have is sufficient more than sufficient for us to be happy to be blissful to be situated always in transcendent etc it's enough we don't have to strive for more qualities or greater quantity of those qualities we don't have to strive for any of those qualities.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjan Shalakaya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. ### sacrifice, kingship, demigods
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.3
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Parikshit, after having selected Kripacharya for guidance as his spiritual master, performed three horse sacrifices on the banks of the Ganges."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam describing Maharaj Parikshit's performance of three horse sacrifices on the banks of the Ganges, guided by Kripacharya.

In this session we will be discussing from the 3rd verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Aajahara Ashwamedham Streen Gangayam Bhuridakshinan Sharadvatam Gurum Krutva Deva Yatrakshi Gocharaha. The meaning of this verse is Maharaj Parikshit, after having selected Kripacharya for guidance as his spiritual master, performed three horse sacrifices on the banks of the Ganges. These were executed with sufficient care, with sufficient rewards for the attendants.

---
### demigods, interplanetary travel, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=91
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It appears from this verse that interplanetary travel by the denizens of higher planets is easy."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada's purport, highlighting the ease of interplanetary travel for demigods and their attendance at sacrifices performed by influential kings like Maharaj Parikshit, where they were even visible to common people.

And at these sacrifices, even the common man could see demigods. So in the purport Shri La Prabhupada says, it appears from this verse that interplanetary travel by the denizens of higher planets is easy. We may have difficulty, or rather, we definitely do not have difficulty, we do have difficulty in travelling to other planets. But for the inhabitants of the upper planets to come to this earth planet, there is no difficulty. Further Shri La Prabhupada says, in many statements in Bhagavatam, we have observed that the demigods from heaven used to visit this earth to attend sacrifices performed by influential kings and emperors. Herein also we find that during the time of the horse sacrifice ceremony of Maharaj Parikshit, the demigods from other planets were visible even to the common man due to the sacrificial ceremony.

---
### demigods, devotees, respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=161
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee, a first class devotee, is respected and honored everywhere in the whole universe."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that demigods live in upper planets and have subtle bodies, rarely visiting Earth except when invited by pure devotees like Parikshit Maharaj, who are respected throughout the universe.

You should remember the devatas, they live in upper planets. They don't have gross bodies like we have, we human beings have on the earth. They have subtle bodies. And in their subtle body, they are not visible even if they come to the earth planet. First of all, they don't come to the earth planet very often. There is no reason to come here. Only under exceptional situations or circumstances like being invited by a first class devotee, Mahabhagavata Parikshit Maharaj, he invites the devatas to come for the sacrifice. Then they accept the invitation very graciously because of the stature of Parikshit Maharaj. A pure devotee, a first class devotee, is respected and honored everywhere in the whole universe. By all the great sages, by all great devatas, by all great jnanis, yogis, tapasvis, everyone, by realized souls, everyone honors and respects a pure devotee of Krishna.

---
### devotees, demigods, Krishna, visibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=244
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, the demigods also become visible to the common man by their own grace."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the high position of pure devotees and explains that demigods, like Lord Krishna, are not generally visible but can choose to reveal themselves through their own grace.

That is the position of the pure devotee. So please try to understand what is being described here. That common men could see devatas in the sacrifice that was organized by Parikshit Maharaj. So Srila Prabhupada explains that demigods are not generally visible to common men even when they come to this earth. As Lord Krishna is not visible to common men, but as the Lord, Supreme Lord Krishna, by His causeless mercy, descends to be visible to the common man, Krishna comes down from His spiritual abode, personal abode, that's called avatara, to descend, to come down, and becomes visible. Similarly, the demigods also become visible to the common man by their own grace. If they decide to be visible, they can be visible. If they decide not to be visible, they'll not be visible.

---
### Krishna, demigods, spiritual body, visibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=317
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It was due to the influence of Maharaj Pariksit that the demigods also agreed to be visible."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain that neither Krishna nor the demigods are visible to ordinary people due to their spiritual bodies, but they can become visible by their own will, as the demigods did due to Maharaj Parikshit's influence.

Just like Krishna. He sits in our heart. But unless He gives darshan, we cannot see Him. Or even when Krishna was present on the earth, 5000 years back, only if He decided to become visible for the common man, He would be visible. Otherwise they could not see Him. Because Krishna's form or body is completely spiritual. It's not material like our body. And the devatas, they don't have a gross body. So therefore, neither the devatas nor Krishna are visible to ordinary people, to common people's sight on this earth. If they decide to become visible, they can be seen by common people. All those celestial beings, means those living in heavenly planets, are not visible to naked eyes of the inhabitants of this earth. It was due to the influence of Maharaj Pariksit that the demigods also agreed to be visible.

### sacrifice, kingship, charity, welfare
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=384
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that kings used to spend lavishly on sacrifices for the welfare of their citizens, pleasing the demigods, sages, and learned brahmanas, and distributing charity liberally.

The kings used to spend lavishly during such sacrifices as a cloud distributes rains. So this is very significant. The kings used to organize such sacrifices for the welfare of the entire citizens. Now, in such sacrifices, it was an important aspect of pleasing the devatas, the sages, the brahmanas, learned brahmanas, who direct the king how to perform the sacrifice. Their satisfaction is very very important to get the full benefit of the sacrifice, to derive the benefit. So one of the aspects of the king performing sacrifice successfully is that the king will spend very very liberally and the description given here is by Srila Prabhupada is just like a cloud distributes rains, similarly the kings would spend during such sacrifices to give in charity to all the invitees.

---
### charity, taxes, welfare, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=481
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The result of the sacrifice is all prosperity for the citizens."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of clouds distributing rain to explain how the king's charity during sacrifices is a redistribution of taxes collected from citizens, ultimately benefiting them through prosperity.

Srila Prabhupada explains about cloud distributing rain. A cloud is nothing but another form of water. Or in other words, the waters of the earth transform into clouds. What is a cloud? After all, water evaporated by the sun forms the cloud, which has got water in the form of water vapor. Similarly, and those water vapor when they condense, then the clouds pour down rain. That's the system, arrangement of the Supreme Lord in this material world. Similarly, the charity made by the kings in such sacrifices are but another form of the taxes collected from the citizens. Where will the king spend from? From the taxes he collects from the citizens. And what will he spend for? He will spend for giving back the benefit to the citizens. How will the citizens benefit if the king spends for a sacrifice? The result of the sacrifice is all prosperity for the citizens.

---

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Satisfied citizens will never organize agitation against the king."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the king's liberal distribution of charity during sacrifices, emphasizing that satisfied citizens are less likely to revolt, thus maintaining a stable monarchical state.

All types of prosperity. So the citizens benefit by the king performing sacrifices properly. And the king gives sufficient charity to the deserving. Qualified brahmanas, sages, like that. But, as the rains fall down very lavishly and appear to be more than necessary, the charity made by such kings also seems to be more than what the citizens need. So, they would distribute liberally to everyone present in the sacrifice. Not only to the brahmanas, not only to the sages, but to everyone. Now, this is done according to the direction of the shastra. This is not one's whimsical idea to distribute liberally to everyone. No. It is exactly according to shastra. Now, in the sacrifice, the citizens will be satisfied completely. So, Srila Prabhupada says, satisfied citizens will never organize agitation against the king. And thus, there was no need in changing the monarchical state. The kings would rule keeping all the citizens satisfied by providing all the necessities, by doing proper sacrifices, by guiding the citizens properly towards the ultimate goal of life. In so many ways, the kings were very responsible.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ma8Tz568NBs?t=686

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without such guidance, one cannot make progress in spiritual life."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the necessity of a spiritual master for guidance, even for advanced devotees like Maharaj Parikshit, highlighting that spiritual progress requires guidance from the right authority.

Srila Prabhupada says further, even for a king like Maharaj Pariksit, there was need of a spiritual master for guidance. Of course, Pariksit was trained and he was well-versed in the principles of bhakti. He himself was a first-class devotee. But that does not mean he does not require guidance. He still requires a spiritual master for guidance. Without such guidance, one cannot make progress in spiritual life. Spiritual life means to progress by getting guidance from the right person, from the right authority, from the right source. Whimsically, we cannot select somebody we like.

Or as people say, I believe or I think such and such a person is very good. No. It should not be based on what we like or what we whimsically choose or what we think is correct. No. It has to be guided by, again, proper spiritual authorities. That's why acharya. Acharya means he is identified as the universal teacher. He teaches by his example. His life is spotless, character. He has spotless character. His life is exemplary in every way. Acharya. So like that, one has to accept a spiritual master and take guidance in spiritual matters to make spiritual progress. The spiritual master must be bona fide. And one who wants to have self-realization must approach and take shelter of a bona fide spiritual master to achieve real success. This is very important. Success is possible only if one has got a proper bona fide spiritual master.

**Summary:** The lecture begins with Sanskrit verses and then transitions into a discussion of verse 1.16.31 of the Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Dharini's (Mother Earth's) concern for all beings and the importance of cooperation.

Om Agyana Timiran Dhasya Gyanan Janishalakaya Chaksharun Militam Yena Tasvai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhigaura Bhakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we are discussing from the 31st verse of the 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam: Atmanam Chanu Shochami Bhavantam Chamarottamam Devan Pitrin Rishin Sadhun Sarvan Varanam Stathashraman. The meaning of this 31st verse is as follows: I am thinking about myself and also, O best amongst the demigods, about you as well as about all the demigods, sages, denizens of Pitraloka, devotees of the Lord, and all men obedient to the system of Varana and Ashrama in human society. These are words spoken by Dharini, Mother Earth, in the shape of a cow to Dharma, the personality of religious principles, in the shape of a bull. So, what is Dharini telling? She is thinking about herself and even about dharma, and all the devatas, the rishis, the pitris, the Vaishnavas, the devotees, about all the people, she is thinking. What about their condition, their position?

### Human vs. Animal Life, Dharma, Bodily Needs
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DoviP6-O-cU?t=271
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But there is a fifth activity, and that's called dharma, and without dharma a human being is on the level of animals, living a life on the level of animals."
**Summary:** The lecture contrasts human and animal life, explaining that while both share basic bodily needs (eating, sleeping, defense, mating), humans have a fifth activity: dharma. Without dharma, humans live on the level of animals.

The scriptures describe this that there are four activities which are common for human beings and animals, the demands of the body: ahara nidra bhaya maithunam, the demands of the body, the necessity of food, necessity of sleep or rest in the afternoon and at night also, and the necessity of the body in terms of defense, protecting oneself, and mating, the necessity of sex life for procreation. So these four are common activities between animals and human beings, but there is a fifth activity, and that's called dharma, and without dharma a human being is on the level of animals, living a life on the level of animals. Dharma viheen apadabha, dharma viheen apadabha, dharma viheen apadabha, this is the statement of the Shastra so therefore human life.

**Verse References:** SB 1.16.32, SB 1.16.33

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces verses 32 and 33 of the 16th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Mother Earth's feelings of separation from Lord Krishna after his departure.

In this session, we will be discussing the 32nd and 33rd verses of the 16th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. These two verses are given together. I shall read them first, the two verses, and then we will hear the translation of the two verses given together, and then we will discuss from the purport. This was the 32nd verse, and the 33rd verse. These two verses, the meaning is given together by Srila Prabhupāda, the translation of these two verses. This glance of grace was sought by demigods like Brahma, and for whom they surrendered many a day unto the personality of Godhead. That Lakshmiji gave up her own abode in the forest of lotus flowers and engaged herself in the service of the lotus feet of the Lord. And now Mother Earth is telling, I was endowed with specific powers to supersede the fortune of all the three planetary systems by being decorated with the impressions of the flag, thunderbolt, elephant-driving rod, and lotus flower, which are the signs of the lotus feet of the Lord. But at the end, when I felt I was so fortunate, the Lord left me. So this is a beautiful description of how Mother Earth is feeling separation from the Lord after the Lord's disappearance from the Earth.

### divine grace, material nature, Krishna's presence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M4I2JbNriyc?t=206
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The beauty and opulence of the world can be enhanced by the grace of the Lord and not by any man-made planning."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the significance of Lord Krishna's presence on Earth, emphasizing that the Earth's beauty and opulence are due to the Lord's grace, not human endeavors, and that we often forget the Lord's control over material nature.

Because as long as the Lord was there on the Earth, his lotus feet were constantly in touch with the surface of the Earth planet. And the touch was very special because he would leave behind impressions of the sole of his feet. And they were recognized as the impression of flag, of thunderbolt, of elephant-driving rod, and of lotus flower. Four different signs are given here. Now in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains something very valuable, very important for all of us to understand. He says, the beauty and opulence of the world can be enhanced by the grace of the Lord and not by any man-made planning. We think that by our own efforts at utilizing the resources of nature and controlling material nature in such a way that he is most favorable for our enjoyment here. We forget the actual control of the Supreme Lord over material nature and over us and over everything else that we forget. So when there is the mercy or grace of the Lord, there is no scarcity of anything for the whole material existence and the beauty and opulence of the world can be enhanced significantly by getting the mercy of the Lord, his merciful glance.

---
### Krishna's footprints, Vrindavan, dependence on nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M4I2JbNriyc?t=334
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We simply have to do our duty, as explained in the scriptures, towards the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The lecture describes the beautification of the Earth by Krishna's footprints, the inhabitants of Vrindavan's reverence for them, and the dependence of human civilization on nature's supplies, which are abundant when people are pious and curtailed when sinful.

When the Lord Sri Krishna was present on this earth, the impressions of the special signs of his lotus feet were stamped on the dust. Whenever Krishna used to walk, so he would leave behind the impression of his feet. And especially in Vrindavan, the other inhabitants of Vrindavan were very conscious of Krishna's footprints, that when they are walking in Vrindavan, if there is a footprint of Krishna which is recognized by the symbols of a flag, thunderbolt, elephant-driving rod, or the lotus flower, then they will not disturb that impression. They will walk away from that. So the earth was beautified by these impressions. And as a result of this, what happened? The whole earth was made as perfect as possible. The earth became a wonderful place. The earth became a wonderful place for all its inhabitants. It is described here, in other words, the rivers, the seas, the forests, the hills, and the mines which are the supply agents for the necessities of men and animals, were fully discharging their respective duties. So this is very, very important. We depend on the supplies of nature for our success, for our subsistence, and for our civilization to flourish. But that supply is sometimes curtailed if we become sinful, and if we are pious, that supply is in abundance. So we need not have industrial enterprises to exploit this material nature and try to get more and more and more and more and more of those resources, demanding Romans and freemen to realize that there are many abundant resources for our own enjoyment. We don't have to endeavor or struggle to acquire them. We simply have to do our duty, as explained in the scriptures, towards the Supreme Lord. ### burden of the earth, divine incarnation, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/isc0mIXGvfA?t=47
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mother Earth is addressing the personality of religious principles, Dharma."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami begins explaining verse 34, focusing on Mother Earth's distress due to atheistic kings and how Krishna incarnated to relieve her burden and restore religious principles.

The last 3 verses are from 3 verses 34, 35 and 36. The last 3 verses of this chapter, 16th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavata. So these verses are all given separately. So one verse at a time. The 34th verse. Yovai Mamatibharam Asura Vamsa Raghmyam Akshauhini Shatam Apanudad Atmatantraha Tvam Dustham Oonapadam Atmani Paurushena Sampadayan Yadushu Ramyam Abhibhrad Angam. The meaning of this 34th verse. Mother Earth is addressing the personality of religious principles, Dharma. Dharani is addressing Dharma. O Personality of Religion! I was greatly overburdened. Burdened by the undue military phalanxes arranged by Atheistic Kings. And I was relieved by the grace of the Personality of Godhead. By the grace of the Supreme Lord, all those military phalanxes were destroyed of the Atheistic Kings. They were destroyed. Similarly, you are also in a distressed condition, weakened in your standing strength, and thus he also incarnated by his internal energy in the family of the Yadus to relieve you.

### atheism, kshatriya, misrule
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/isc0mIXGvfA?t=160
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's appearance, one of the reasons for his appearance is to reduce the burden of the Earth which is overburdened by demoniac persons posing as Kshatriyas."
**Summary:** The lecture elaborates on the reasons for Krishna's appearance, emphasizing the burden caused by demoniac individuals posing as rulers without possessing true Kshatriya qualities, leading to chaos and suffering for ordinary people.

So Krishna's appearance, one of the reasons for his appearance is to reduce the burden of the Earth which is overburdened by demoniac persons posing as Kshatriyas. They don't have Kshatriya qualities. To properly rule this Earth planet, or to rule some provinces or states, they don't have the Kshatriya qualities. So they simply pose as if they are independently able to do the administration without any consideration of any principles, whimsically. They take charge. They take charge or control of the whole state or province or country or whatever, and they create havoc in the lives of ordinary people. So, to curtail these people's miscreant activities, the Lord incarnated as Krishna, and he incarnated by his internal energy.

**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami continues explaining Srila Prabhupada's purport, describing the nature of Asuras, their desire for sense gratification at the expense of others, and the Lord's duty to protect devotees and curtail their nefarious activities.

That means his appearance is not like our appearance which is subject to so many material... own spiritual identity as spirit soul we forget that whereas internal energy means there is full enlightenment full knowledge full consciousness so the two are totally different so in the purport Srila Prabhupada says the Asuras want to enjoy life of sense gratification even at the cost of others happiness they don't care for others happiness and they also don't hesitate to give trouble to others who want to live a pious life the Asuras give trouble to such people and they want to enjoy independently they don't care for others and Prabhupada says further in order to fulfill this ambition the Asuras especially atheistic kings or state executive heads try to equip themselves with all kinds of deadly weapons to bring about a war in a peaceful society unnecessarily some demoniac kings they want to declare war and somehow capture power defeat or kill some righteous kings and capture power so these atheistic kings they equipped themselves with so many deadly weapons so such people simply create a condition or situation where there will be war unnecessarily in a peaceful society there is no need for war but they will create situation that there has to be war and mother earth because she considers all the living entities on living in this earth planet as her own children so she feels for their suffering when the Kings oppress the people or the the unwanted asuras they have to be vanquished by the supreme lord only nobody else can actually control them or curtail them or restrict their nefarious activities even the demigods the rishis the all the different inhabitants of the upper planets or even the the powerful persons on this earth or in the lower planets they cannot restrict these asuras so they and the asuras unnecessarily give trouble to the devotees unless they give trouble so therefore there is an absolute necessity of the supreme lord's appearance and he does his duty or his or his seva regardless of whether others are doing or not doing so he appears also for delivering all the others and he simultaneously protects the devotees and kills the demons.

### separation, divine affection, earth's joy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/isc0mIXGvfA?t=535
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is very very very kind and benevolent towards his devotees."
**Summary:** The lecture transitions to verse 35, describing the intense feelings of separation from Krishna and the joy Mother Earth experienced through constant contact with the Lord's lotus feet.

In the 35th verse it is said the meaning of this 35th verse is that who therefore can tolerate the pangs of separation from that supreme personality of godhead he could conquer the gravity and passionate wrath of his sweethearts like satyabhama by his sweet smile of love pleasing glance and hearty appeals krishna is very very very kind and benevolent towards his devotees so for those who render loving service to him krishna is also reciprocating with such devotees and his reciprocation is very very very very attractive further it is said when he traversed my surface that is the earth surface i would be immersed in the dust of his lotus feet And thus would be sumptuously covered with grass, which appeared like hair standing on me out of pleasure. Lord was constantly in touch with the surface of this earth planet. So Mother Earth felt the touch of the Lord's lotus feet on her body.

### separation, divine presence, earth's feelings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/isc0mIXGvfA?t=661
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So no chance of separation for Mother Earth from the contact with the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord Krishna when he was present on the earth."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with verse 35, explaining how the earth felt constant joy from Krishna's presence and the acute sense of separation when He departed, contrasting it with the occasional separations felt by Krishna's queens.

And she was always very much happy and ecstatic with such contact. So it appeared like the grass that was growing whenever the Lord walked on the surface of the earth. Those grass appeared like hair standing on the body of the earth planet due to... excessive joy due to the contact of the Lord's lotus feet with the Mother, with the earth planet. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says there were chances of separation between the Lord and his thousands of queens because of the Lord's being absent from home. So in the daytime the Lord would go to the assembly house in Dwarka. And in the evening He would return and spend time with His family members in His palaces. So when he would go away, they would feel the lord's absence from their home. Thousands of queens, sixteen thousand one hundred and eight queens, But as far as this connection with the earth was concerned, It is different. What is the difference? The Lord would traverse the earth with his lotus feet, and therefore there was no chance of separation. He would always travel on the earth surface. So no chance of separation for Mother Earth from the contact with the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord Krishna when he was present on the earth. So therefore, when the Lord left this earth planet and went back to his personal abode in the spiritual world, Mother Earth's feelings of separation were therefore very, very, very acute, very intense. She felt the separation so much. Because for 125 years the Lord was there constantly. His lotus feet was in touch with the surface of the earth. So she felt the most, the separation from the Lord after he left for his personal abode.

### Pariksit, Kali, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/isc0mIXGvfA?t=812
**Verse References:** SB 1.16.36
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "While the earth and the personality of religion were thus engaged in conversation, the saintly king Pariksit reached the shore of the Saraswati river which flowed towards the east."
**Summary:** The lecture concludes with verse 36, describing King Pariksit's arrival at the scene where Dharma and Mother Earth are conversing, setting the stage for his intervention against the influence of Kali.

The 36th verse, the last verse, tayor evam kathayato prithvi dharmayostada parikshin nama rajarshi praptaprachim sarasvatim The meaning of this last verse, while the earth and the personality of religion were thus engaged in conversation, the saintly king Pariksit reached the shore of the Saraswati river which flowed towards the east. So the description is continuing that Pariksit has reached that spot where the personality of Kali was trying to beat a cow and a bull. So Pariksit has reached there. And then now there will be the personality of Dharma and the personality of Mother Earth were conversing with each other. And Pariksit reached there at that time. Now Pariksit is going to enquire and is going to take action, suitable action against the personality of Kali. We will hear that in the next chapter. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
